PT P
PN KR2101023-B1
TI Automatic building control system for reducing fine dust through predictive control, has control unit to implement mode for controlling ventilation device, when carbon dioxide concentration is greater than or equal to threshold.
AU MYEON J C
AE MYEON J C (MYEO-Individual)
GA 2020325132
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a control unit that implements the ventilation of a building, based on the carbon dioxide concentration information, external fine dust concentration information and internal fine dust concentration information. The first control mode is a mode for controlling the ventilation device to the external ventilation mode, when the carbon dioxide concentration in the internal space is greater than or equal to preset carbon dioxide threshold or when the internal fine dust concentration in the internal space is greater than or equal to preset fine dust threshold. The second control mode is a mode for controlling the ventilation device to the internal circulation mode, when the carbon dioxide concentration in the internal space is greater than or equal to a preset carbon dioxide threshold. The internal air quality is improved through reduction of fine dust, which is changed by the control unit based on the external fine dust concentration.
   USE - Automatic building control system for reducing fine dust through predictive control.
   ADVANTAGE - Reduction of fine dust through predictive control is ensured. The fine dust concentration inside the building is reduced effectively, even when the fine dust concentration outside is high.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for building automatic control method for improving internal air quality through reduction of fine dust.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a graphical view explaining the change in the concentration of external fine dust and the concentration of internal fine dust. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
DC Q74 (Heating, ranges, ventilating (F24)); X27 (Domestic Electric Appliances\n)
MC Q74-A02; Q74-T20; X27-E01B
IP F24F-011/00; F24F-011/63; F24F-110/64; F24F-110/70

PN CN110358594-A; CN210635951-U
TI Device for synthesizing natural gas from hydrogen            production by electrolysis of water and methanation of            carbon dioxide, has gas-liquid separation reactor whsoe            liquid outlet is connected to water tank through            pipe.
AU HU Q
   LIN J
   ZENG Q
   SUN X
   GONG J
   WU J
   ZHANG G
AE TSINGHUA SICHUAN ENERGY INTERNET RES INS (TSIN-Non-standard)
GA 2019888146
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a water tank (1), a heat                exchanger (2), a gas mixer (4), a gas-liquid                separation reactor (7), electrolysis water hydrogen                production unit (3), carbon dioxide generating unit                (5) and a methanation unit (6). The water outlet of                the water tank is connected to electrolysis water                hydrogen production unit through a heat exchanger.                The electrolysis water hydrogen production unit and                the carbon dioxide generation unit are respectively                connected with the gas mixer. The gas mixture                outlet of the gas mixer is connected to the                methanation unit through pipe. The methanation unit                is sequentially connected to the heat exchanger and                the gas-liquid separation reactor. The liquid                outlet of the gas-liquid separation reactor is                connected to the water tank through a pipe.
   USE - Device for synthesizing natural gas from                hydrogen production by electrolysis of water and                methanation of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The energy loss in the electrolysis water                hydrogen production process is reduced, and the                water generated during the reaction process is                reused, so that the preparation cost of hydrogen is                reduced and environmentally-friendly device is                provided.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method                for synthesizing natural gas from hydrogen                production by electrolysis of water and methanation                of carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                device for synthesizing natural gas from hydrogen                production. (Drawing includes non-English language                text)
   Water tank (1)
   Heat exchanger (2)
   Electrolysis water hydrogen production unit                (3)
   Gas mixer (4)
   Carbon dioxide generating unit (5)
   Methanation unit (6)
   Gas-liquid separation reactor (7)

The box body and the humidification box are connected. The box body and the lid are sealed by a silicone sealing strip, and are pressed by a buckle. The energy module and the cover are sealed by a silicone sealing strip and fixed by a buckle. The function module has an air inlet, an air outlet, an oxygen sensor and a carbon dioxide sensor, where air inlet, the air outlet, the oxygen sensor and the carbon dioxide sensor are open to the inside of the box. The controller is respectively connected to the box and the nitrogen through pipes and wires a cylinder and a carbon dioxide cylinder are connected.

PT P
PN CN109589896-A
TI Carbonization reactor, has pressure control valve            controlling carbon dioxide intake, and closing intake            pipe when atmospheric pressure in reaction vessel is            greater than or equal to preset pressure.
AU XIANG C
   DAI S
AE CHILWEE POWER CO LTD (CHWE-C)
GA 201935078M
AB    NOVELTY - The reactor has an air intake device (1)                including a carbon dioxide gas inlet tank (11), an                intake pipe (12) connected to the carbon dioxide                gas inlet tank, and a pressure control valve (13).                The reaction device (2) comprises a reaction kettle                (21), a motor (22), a scraping wall stirring paddle                (23) and a discharge port (24) connected to the                motor. The feeding device (3) comprises an                atomizing nozzle (31), a high-pressure liquid                feeding tube (32), a high-pressure pump (33), and a                feeding tube (34). The pressure control valve                controls the carbon dioxide intake by balancing the                atmospheric pressure in the reaction vessel with                the spring force of the pressure control valve. The                pressure control valve opens the intake pipe, when                the atmospheric pressure in the reaction vessel is                less than a preset pressure. The pressure control                valve closes the intake pipe, when the atmospheric                pressure in the reaction vessel is greater than or                equal to a preset pressure.
   USE - Carbonization reactor.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbonization reactor can stabilize carbon                dioxide gas supply, stabilize production                efficiency, and save production raw                materials.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                carbonization reactor.
   Air intake device (1)
   Reaction device (2)
   Feeding device (3)
   Carbon dioxide gas inlet tank (11)
   Intake pipe (12)
   Pressure control valve (13)
   Reaction kettle (21)
   Motor (22)
   Stirring paddle (23)
   Discharge port (24)
   Atomizing nozzle (31)
   High-pressure liquid feeding tube (32)
   High-pressure pump (33)
   Feeding tube (34)

PT P
PN KR1965263-B1
TI Refrigeration system used for refrigerating vaccine, has carbon dioxide gas controller which is provided for maintaining acidity or osmotic characteristics of object, by suppressing physicochemical reaction of object with air exposure.
AU BEOM L J
   YOUNG K E
   PILL P S
AE MIRAECELLBIO CO LTD (MIRA-Non-standard)
GA 2019307412
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has a cooling module (130) which is provided for generating cold air. A receiving module (120) is provided for receiving the cool air and object. A regulator module (140) is provided for supplying carbon dioxide into the receiving module. A control module (110) is configured for controlling the cooling module and the regulator module. A sensing module is provided for sensing an environment in the receiving module. The cooling module is provided for supplying cool air into the receiving module. The cooling capacity of the cooling module is reduced, if the temperature in the receiving module is lower than the target temperature. The cooling capacity of the cooling module is increased, if the temperature in the receiving module is higher than the target temperature. A carbon dioxide gas controller (112) is provided for maintaining the acidity or osmotic characteristics of the object, by suppressing physicochemical reaction of the object with air exposure.
   USE - Refrigeration system used for refrigerating vaccine, medical injection and drugs. Can also be used for refrigerating vegetable, fruit, meat and fish.
   ADVANTAGE - The desired amount of carbon dioxide is maintained inside the refrigerating chamber. The physicochemical reaction is minimized according to the air exposure of the product. The loss of goods is reduced, by increasing the usable period of the product. The loss of refrigerating temperature and loss of carbon dioxide during opening and closing of refrigerating compartment are minimized.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the refrigeration system.
   Refrigeration system (100)
   Control module (110)
   Carbon dioxide gas controller (112)
   Receiving module (120)
   Cooling module (130)
   Regulator module (140)

PT P
PN CN109081817-A
TI Preparing 1,3-oxazolidin-2-one compound by catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide at normal temperature and pressure using copper(I) iodide/ionic liquid comprises e.g. mixing prop-2-yn-1-ol compound and primary amine compound.
AU ZHAO Y
   QIU J
   WANG J
AE UNIV HENAN NORMAL (UYNR-C)
GA 201901587M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 1,3-oxazolidin-2-one compound (III) by catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide at normal temperature and pressure using copper(I) iodide/ionic liquid, comprises taking prop-2-yn-1-ol compound (I) as a raw material, primary amine compound (II) as solvent, copper(I) iodide and ionic liquid (P4444)(Im) are catalytic systems, and then stirring the reaction under normal temperature and normal pressure in a carbon dioxide atmosphere to obtain (III).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing 1,3-oxazolidin-2-one compound by catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide at normal temperature and pressure using copper(I) iodide/ionic liquid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple process, mild reaction conditions and high in product yield (up to 99%); and utilizes easily available catalyst system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing 1,3-oxazolidin-2-one compound of formula (III) by catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide at normal temperature and pressure using copper(I) iodide/ionic liquid, comprises taking prop-2-yn-1-ol compound of formula (I) as a raw material, primary amine compound of formula (R3NH2)(II) as solvent, copper(I) iodide and ionic liquid (P4444)(Im) are catalytic systems, and then stirring the reaction under normal temperature and normal pressure in a carbon dioxide atmosphere to obtain (III).
   R1 and R2 = 1-4C linear alkyl, branched alkyl or cycloalkyl group;
   R3 = 1-4C linear alkyl, branched alkyl, cycloalkyl group or phenyl group; and
   (Im) in ionic liquid = imidazole anion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio between (I) and (II) is 1:1. The molar ratio between (I) and copper(I) iodide or ionic liquid (P4444)(Im) in the catalytic system is 1:0.1.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R1, and R2 = methyl, ethyl, isobutyl or cyclohexyl; and
   R3 = n-butyl, isobutyl, cyclohexyl or phenyl.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN108625990-A; CN108625990-B
TI Cross-critical carbon dioxide circulating coupling natural gas oxygen rich combustion power generation system, has first part directly arranged in system main unit, and second part arranged in water pump and returned to combustion chamber.
AU CAI L
   XIANG Y
   GUAN Y
   LIU W
   HAN Y
   LIANG Y
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 201882323K
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a natural oxygen-rich combustion gas turbine orderly connected with a combustion chamber, a gas turbine, a waste heat boiler, a heat exchanger, a flue gas distributor, a water distributor and a water pump through a pipeline. The heat exchanger enters flue gas into the flue gas distributor. The water distributor is divided into two parts after liquid water separated by the flue gas distributor flows out from a bottom. The first part is directly arranged in a system main unit. The second part is arranged in the water pump and returned to the combustion chamber as combustion temperature neutralizing medium.
   USE - Cross-critical carbon dioxide circulating coupling natural gas oxygen rich combustion power generation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system adopts a gas-steam combined cycle and a transcritical carbon dioxide cycle coupling mode, and can fully utilize heat energy of the flue gas so as to significantly improve power generation efficiency of the system, and can sequentially capture of carbon dioxide in the flue gas by using liquefied natural gas cold energy, and condensation of the working fluid in the transcritical carbon dioxide cycle reduces power consumption of carbon capture so as to ensure smooth operation of the transcritical cycle, thus achieving efficient use of cold energy and significant improvement in system power generation efficiency.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a circuit block diagram of a cross-critical carbon dioxide circulating coupling natural gas oxygen rich combustion power generation system. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'
DC Q51 (Internal combustion engines, reciprocating engines, rotary engines (F01, F02B,D,F,G,M,N,P)); Q52 (Reaction engines: external combustion; gas turbines; rockets (F01D, F02C, F02K, F02M)); X11 (Power Generation and High Power Machines)

TI System for capturing carbon dioxide in flue gas of power plant by coupling rice husk gasification with sodium silicate, has pipe that is connected to heat exchanger connected back to bottom of fluidization reactor.

   ZHAO C

   LIAO Y

   WU T

GA 201836711S
AB    NOVELTY - The system has primary cyclone separator whose upper output is connected to a secondary cyclone separator (9) having upper output is connected to third heat exchanger (11). A dissolver output is connected to a filter (14) whose output is connected back to rice husk gasification coupled sodium metasilicate preparation furnace (4). A fourth heat exchanger (12) is provided with a pipe connected to first heat exchanger (5) additionally provided with a pipe connected to second heat exchanger (8) connected back to the bottom of the electric field assisted fluidization reactor (7).
   USE - System for capturing carbon dioxide in flue gas of power plant by coupling rice husk gasification with sodium silicate.
   ADVANTAGE - The silicon oxide carrier is formed through pyrolysis activation, and sodium carbonate absorbent is highly-synthesized through sodium carbonate solution to remove the carbon dioxide in the power plant flue gas and convert the carbon dioxide into high quality synthetic gas. The recycled sodium carbonate and silicon-rich aluminum materials are also generated, turning waste into treasure, realizing high-efficiency utilization of solid waste, and saving system decarburization costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for capturing carbon dioxide in flue gas of power plant by coupling rice husk gasification with sodium silicate.

PT P
PN CN107788161-A
TI Microencapsulated tea extract is prepared by pre-treating tea, extracting in water, re-extracting in supercritical carbon dioxide, and microencapsulating as double emulsion structure.
AU XING X
AE PUJIANG ANGBAO BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (PUJI-Non-standard)
GA 201821791L
AB    NOVELTY - Microencapsulated tea extract is prepared by pre-treating tea, extracting in water, re-extracting in supercritical carbon dioxide, and microencapsulating as double emulsion structure.
   USE - Microencapsulated tea extract.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple operation which combines water extraction and supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to simultaneously extract water-soluble and fat-soluble components in the tea with high extraction rate. The method also uses microcapsule technology which reduces surface tension between two phases resulting to stable property.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The pre-treatment of tea is carried out by crushing tea into 100-300-mesh powder, soaking in 10-30 times distilled water for 2-5 hours. The water extraction is carried out by mixing pre-treated tea with 0.001-0.002 time by volume ethanol, heating mixture at 40-50 degrees C for 1-4 hours, filtering, concentrating filtrate to 1/10-1/8 of original volume to 1/8 to obtain water extract, and drying residue to obtain tea powder. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is carried out by subjecting 70-100 g tea powder by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction with carbon dioxide flow rate of 30-40 kg/hour at 30-40 degrees C and 30-40 MPa having first separation temperature of 40-50 degrees C and separation pressure of 8-8.5 MPa, and second separation of 30-35 degrees C and separation pressure of 6-7 MPa for 2-3 hours. The extract is microencapsulated by mixing supercritical carbon dioxide extract and water extract, adding 0.5-0.8 times by volume medium-chain triglycerides and 7-10 times by volume Cremophor EL 35 (RTM: polyoxy 35 castor oil), ultrasonicating mixture at 15-25 kHz and 150-200 W for 5-10 minutes, adding 0.1-0.4 times by volume ethanol solution containing 0.03-0.05 times by volume kojic acid dropwise at dropping speed of 1-2 drops/second, and stirring.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Component: The tea comprises green tea, black tea, yellow tea, white tea, fermented tea, pu'er tea, oolong tea, and/or jasmine tea, preferably mixture of green tea and black tea at 1:0.1-0.3 ratio.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC D03-D03; D03-H01T2B; D05-A04D; A10-E07A; A10-E08A; A12-V01; A12-W05; A12-W09
IP A23F-003/16; A23F-003/18; A61K-036/82; A61K-047/10; A61K-047/14; A61K-047/44; A61K-009/113

PT P
PN CN113772800-A
TI Method for adding carbonic acid using carbonic acid solution adding system, involves, enabling supersaturated carbonic acid to be sprayed into water to be treated, at certain outlet pressure, so that preset pressure difference is ensured between outlet pressure of diffuser and pressure of water.
AU LIN X
AE HAWEI SHANGHAI ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY (HAWE-Non-standard)
GA 2021E8666F
AB    NOVELTY - Method for adding carbonic acid using carbonic acid solution adding system, involves constructing carbonic acid solution adding system which comprises a reaction tank (5), diffuser (6), carbon dioxide gas pipeline (12) and reaction water pipeline (11). A channel for releasing carbonic acid solution, generates certain back pressure and enables supersaturated carbonic acid to be sprayed into water to be treated, at certain outlet pressure, so that pressure difference of more than 2bar is ensured between outlet pressure of diffuser and pressure of water. Carbon dioxide gas is sent into tank after setting temperature and pressure. Reaction water is pressurized by water pump, sent to tank in high-pressure mist form from top of tank. Water quality on-line detector is connected to signal receiver connected to programmable logic controller to process signals, opening size of control valve is controlled thereby controlling adding amount of carbonic acid solution.
   USE - The method is used for adding carbonic acid by using small-sized carbonic acid solution adding system, in fishponds, shrimp ponds, swimming pools, vegetable greenhouses, flower planting, small-scale seawater desalination.
   ADVANTAGE - The method accurately adjusts the water quality, efficiently reduces the generation of bubbles and improve the utilization rate of carbon dioxide gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for adding carbonic acid using small-sized carbonic acid solution adding system, involves several steps. In step (1), a small-sized carbonic acid solution adding system which comprises a reaction tank (5), diffuser (6), carbon dioxide gas pipeline (12) and reaction water pipeline (11), is constructed. A water mist nozzle and carbon dioxide gas nozzle are arranged on top of inner tank of reaction tank. A water pump (4) arranged on reaction water pipeline, has outlet connected to water inlet and water mist nozzle on top of reaction tank through a water pipeline. A pressure regulating valve group (10) is arranged on carbon dioxide gas pipeline and the outlet of pressure regulating valve group is connected to carbon dioxide gas inlet and carbon dioxide gas nozzle on top of reaction tank. The outlet at bottom of reaction tank is connected with inlet of diffuser and a control valve or a flow-limiting orifice plate or a throttling hole which can accurately control flow and pressure, is arranged on carbonic acid solution pipeline between outlet at bottom of reaction tank and inlet of diffuser. The diffuser arranged in water to be treated, is a hollow cylindrical object with one open end and one closed end. A passage for releasing carbonic acid solution is arranged on cylindrical side wall of diffuser and channel for releasing the carbonic acid solution is a small hole or a narrow gap, or a combination of the small hole and the narrow gap. The channel for releasing the carbonic acid solution generates certain back pressure and enables the supersaturated carbonic acid solution to be sprayed into water to be treated at certain outlet pressure, so that pressure difference of more than 2bar is ensured between outlet pressure of diffuser and pressure of water to be treated. The diffuser is arranged in the raw water or water pipeline which flows by water and is to be treated, is positioned at the upstream of the water flow, and a passage for releasing carbonic acid solution is arranged on one half side wall of diffuser having one side provided with passage for releasing carbonic acid solution, is right opposite to upstream of water flow, so that carbonic acid solution in diffuser is reversely sprayed out from passage for releasing carbonic acid solution opposite to water flow and a vortex reinforced mixing effect is generated. Alternatively, diffuser is placed in basin, pond or tank to be treated, where no water flows, and on both side walls or on entire cylindrical side wall of diffuser, several rows of channels are opened for releasing carbonic acid solution. In step (2), carbon dioxide gas kept at a preset temperature and pressure, is conveyed to reaction tank through carbon dioxide gas pipeline. In step (3), carbon dioxide gas with preset temperature and pressure, enters tank top of reaction tank through gas pipeline, meanwhile, reaction water is pressurized to preset pressure through water pump and then sent into reaction tank from tank top in a high-pressure fog shape, through water pipeline. The pressure in tank is kept at preset pressure and in reaction tank, carbon dioxide gas is mixed with pressurized reaction water to form gas-liquid mixture with preset pressure. As the contact area of water mist and carbon dioxide gas is large, carbon dioxide gas is quickly dissolved in water and reacts to generate carbonic acid solution in a set pressure environment. The carbon dioxide gas is continuously fed in, or water mist with certain pressure continuously enters tank, pressure in tank is raised to set pressure, and when it reaches a preset value, carbonic acid solution in the reaction tank is converted into saturated carbonic acid solution. The saturated carbonic acid solution is pumped to control valve or flow-limiting orifice plate or an orifice hole which can accurately control flow and pressure through pipeline at bottom of reaction tank, control valve or flow-limiting orifice plate or orifice hole seals saturated carbonic acid solution between reaction tank and control valve or flow-limiting orifice plate or orifice hole. The saturated carbonic acid solution becomes supersaturated carbonic acid solution due to continuous pressure in reaction tank and dissolution of carbon dioxide gas. The supersaturated carbonic acid solution is conveyed to diffuser through pipeline through valve core or flow-limiting orifice plate or throttling hole in opened control valve. The supersaturated carbonic acid solution forms mixed solution of high-concentration carbonic acid solution and small amount of carbon dioxide microbubbles through channel (namely small hole and/or narrow gap) designed on diffuser and used for releasing carbonic acid solution, mixed solution is reversely sprayed into water, carbonic acid solution meets alkaline substances in water and starts to perform acid-base neutralization reaction, or carbonic acid solution meets calcium ions or calcium carbonate in water and reacts, small amount of carbon dioxide microbubbles are absorbed by water flow. In step (4), a water quality on-line detector is arranged at downstream of water flow in raw water or water pipeline or at water outlet of pool, pond or sewage tank. The detector is connected to signal receiver connected to programmable logic controller (PLC). PN CN112903755-A
TI Carbon dioxide sensor for carbon dioxide detecting            circuit, has silicon substrate layer and epitaxial            wafer deposited on silicon substrate layer, top            portions of aluminum electrode and platinum electrode            are coated with passivation layer.
AU HAN D
   LIU L
   SANG S
   ZHUO K
   JI J
   ZHANG W
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 202166992Q
AB    NOVELTY - The sensor has an epitaxial wafer deposited on                a silicon substrate layer. An end of the epitaxial                wafer is etched and deposited with an aluminum                electrode (9) by magnetron sputtering. Another end                of the epitaxial wafer is formed with a platinum                electrode (10) by photoetching. A top portions of                the epitaxial wafer, the aluminum electrode and the                platinum electrode are coated with polyethylene                imine and starch mixture. Top portions of the                aluminum electrode and the platinum electrode are                coated with a passivation layer.
   USE - Carbon dioxide sensor used in a carbon dioxide                detection circuit.
   ADVANTAGE - The sensor has wide detection range, better                stability, high material utilization rate, wide                detection range, low price, easy detection,                repeatable use and other beneficial effects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing carbon dioxide sensor, which                involves:
   (A) growing gallium nitride or aluminum                gallium nitride epitaxial wafer with high crystal                quality on the silicon substrate layer;
   (B) adopting molten sodium hydroxide or                potassium hydroxide to etch the epitaxial                wafer;
   (C) forming ohmic contact between the aluminum                electrode (9) and the epitaxial wafer, where                surface layer of the other end of the epitaxial                wafer is made of platinum electrode with thickness                of 20nm-50nm by photoetching technology;
   (D) forming Schottky diode by platinum                electrode and the aluminum gallium nitride                layer;
   (E) depositing a passivation layer on the                surface of the aluminum electrode and the platinum                electrode;
   (F) mixing the polyethylene imine and starch                according to the proportion of 2: 1;
   (G) using the magnetic stirrer to mix                uniformly;
   (H) coating the uniformly mixed                solution;
   (I) using drying box to evaporate solvent on                the epitaxial wafer; and
   (J) finishing the manufacturing of the whole                carbon dioxide sensor device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                carbon dioxide sensor.

PN CN110846011-A
TI Gel solution comprises methylidene polyacrylamide, phenolic crosslinker, foaming agent, thiourea structure-containing phenolic organics, additives, and water.
AU WANG Y
   LAN J
   MENG L
   JIANG B
   XU N
   LIANG L
   LIU B
   TANG L
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
GA 2020199420
AB    NOVELTY - Gel solution comprises 0.2-0.8 wt.% methylidene polyacrylamide, 0.2-1.4 wt.% phenolic crosslinker, 0.3-1 wt.% foaming agent, 0.2-0.5 wt.% thiourea structure-containing phenolic organics, 0.2-0.5 wt.% additives, and water (remaining amount). The thiourea-containing phenolic organics are prepared by mixing organic compounds with benzene ring and thiourea structures with liquid bromine under the conditions of catalysis and heating, it causes the substitution of bromine atom on the benzene ring to replace the hydrogen atom, mixing the resulting product with a sodium carbonate solution to obtain an organic compound containing sodium phenoxide and thiourea structures, mixing with strong acid. The compound with a benzene ring and a thiourea structure comprises N-phenylthiourea, 4-pyridylthiourea, 1-phenethyl-3-phenyl-2-thiourea, 4-fluorobenzene thiourea and/or N-(2-pyridyl) thiourea. The strong acid comprises nitric acid, sulfuric acid and/or hydrochloric acid.
   USE - The thiourea-containing carbon dioxide gel foam is useful for water blocking, acidification, fracturing or profile control in oil fields, preferably for selective water blocking in acid formations (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide gel foam: has strong water blocking selectivity; does not block oil flow channels; does not affect oil flow; and improves crude oil recovery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) carbon dioxide gel foam containing a thiourea structure prepared by passing carbon dioxide gas into the gel solution, where, the gas-liquid volume ratio of the carbon dioxide to the gel solution is 100:1-2; and
   (2) preparing the carbon dioxide gel foam containing a thiourea structure comprising (a) preparing thiourea-containing phenolic organic compound, (b) thoroughly mixing the gel solution components and placing in an environment of 20-25 degrees C for 8-12 hours to obtain gel solution, and (c) passing carbon dioxide gas into the gel solution at a gas-liquid volume ratio of 100:1-2, and reacting at 60-75 degrees C for 40-50 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The phenolic cross-linking agent is 0.1-0.7 wt.%, preferably 0.2-0.5 wt.% phenol or hydroquinone, and 0.1-0.7 wt.%, preferably 0.2-0.5 wt.% formaldehyde, paraformaldehyde or hexamethylenetetramine. The degree of hydrolysis of the methylidene polyacrylamide is 10-20%, and the molecular weight is between 10-18 million. The methylidene polyacrylamide has a degree of hydrolysis of 12-15% and molecular weight between 13-16 million. The auxiliary agent is sodium sulfite, potassium sulfite and/or sodium thiosulfate The foaming agent comprises octylphenol polyoxyethylene ether, nonylphenol polyoxyethylene ether, lauryl alcohol and/or fatty acid polyoxyethylene ester. Preferred Method: The phenolic organic compounds containing thiourea structure is prepared by (i) in the presence of an iron halide catalyst, mixing an organic substance having a benzene ring and a thiourea structure with liquid bromine, and performing reaction of replacing a hydrogen atom with a bromine atom on a benzene ring under heating at 40-60 degrees C, taking compounds with phenyl ring and thiourea structure, N-phenylthiourea, 4-pyridylthiourea, 1-phenethyl-3-phenyl-2-thiourea, 4-fluorophenylthiourea or N -(2-pyridyl) thiourea, (ii) mixing the product obtained in the step (i) with sodium carbonate solution at 120-140 degrees C to obtain organic compound containing sodium phenolate and thiourea structure, and mixing with small amount of strong acid to obtain phenolic organic compound containing a thiourea structure, (iii) mixing the phenolic organic compound containing the thiourea structure with specific amount of weak base to remove excess hydrogen ions, and crystallizing and cooling. The reaction of the synthetic step (i) of the phenolic organic compound containing a thiourea structure includes (a) iron halide comprises iron bromide or iron chloride, (a2) the temperature of the heating condition is 50-55 degrees C, (a3) the molar ratio of the organic compounds of the benzene ring and thiourea structure to liquid bromine is 1:(2-2.5), preferably, the molar ratio of the organic compounds of the benzene ring and thiourea structure to liquid bromine is 1:( 2.1-2.4). In the step (ii), the reaction of the synthesis of thiourea-containing phenolic organic compounds includes (b1) the high temperature condition is 130 degrees C, (b2) the strong acid comprises nitric acid, sulfuric acid, and/or hydrochloric acid, (b3) the molar ratio of the organic substance containing sodium phenoxide and thiourea structure to strong acid is 1:(0.5-1.5), the molar ratio of the organic substance containing sodium phenoxide and thiourea structure to strong acid is 1:(0.7-1.2). In the step (iii), (c1) weak base is sodium carbonate and/or ammonia, (c2) the reaction is carried out at 50-100 degrees C, and (c3) the molar ratio of the thiourea-containing phenolic organics to the weak base is 1:(0.1-0.7), preferably, the molar ratio of the thiourea-containing phenolic organics to the weak base is 1:(0.2-0.4).

AU OKANO H
   HIROSHI O
AE NISHIBU GIKEN KK (NISH-Non-standard)
   SEIBU GIKEN KK (SEIB-Non-standard)
   SEIBU GIKEN KK (SEIB-Non-standard)
GA 202005784B
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon-dioxide concentration apparatus                comprises non-air permeable sheet in which solid                adsorbent particles are two-dimensionally adhered                and fixed so that packing density is less than or                equal to 55 vol.%, is wound around to form a                cylindrical adsorbent, or is laminated comprising                adsorbent so that solid adsorbent particle come to                contact with sheet or another solid adsorbent                particle different from sheet at one point, process                zone and desorption zone which are provided in                adsorbent. The adsorbent is made wet with condensed                water to adsorb carbon dioxide when passing the                processing gas in process zone. Evaporative cooling                of condensed water is made to advance                simultaneously, and the desorption zone is passed                through a saturated vapor mixture of carbon dioxide                and water vapor.
   USE - A carbon-dioxide concentration                apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus has increased adsorption                performance by evaporative cooling effect of water                and desorbs by circulating saturated steam mixed                with carbon dioxide and water vapor. The                energy-saving apparatus uses low temperature                exhaust heat of combustion exhaust gas. The                apparatus eliminates the fault of filled layers,                such as fixed floor, moving bed and fluid bed                system. The apparatus has high recovery of carbon                dioxide gases, high concentration and high                durability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for adsorbent                comprising non-air permeable sheet in which solid                adsorbent particles are two-dimensionally adhered                and fixed so that packing density is less than or                equal to 55 vol.% is wound or laminated on greater                than or equal to 1 single surface so that solid                adsorbent particle come to contact with sheet or                another solid adsorbent particle different from                sheet at one point.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                apparatus which adhesive-fixes adsorbent particles                continuously on single surface of the sheet.                (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
   Pressing roller (1)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The solid adsorbents are                  arranged in a linear or staggered arrangement in                  a gas flow, and a planar sheet having a gas                  introduction passage formed between each row is                  wound or laminated. The solid adsorbent meanders                  in zigzag direction along the gas flow, and the                  planar sheet adhered and fixed to form a gas                  introduction path between each row is wound or                  laminated on greater than or equal to 1 side. The                  planar sheets in which the solid adsorbent is                  simultaneously adhered to both sides of the sheet                  are alternately bent and laminated on both sides                  so that the solid adsorbents come into contact                  with each other. Preferred Components: The solid                  adsorbent is porous adsorbent which carries amine                  compound. The solid adsorbent is macromolecular                  system adsorbent which takes in amine group as                  fixed ion.

PT P
PN CN112142087-A
TI Preparing spherical calcium carbonate, by adding organic solvent as carbon dioxide dissolving agent and calcium carbonate crystal stabilizer to ammonia solution, mixing inorganic polymer morphology control agent, carbonizing and drying.
AU WANG Q
   HUANG J
   WANG Z
AE WANG Q (WANG-Individual)
GA 2021031782
AB    NOVELTY - Process for preparing spherical calcium carbonate, involves adding organic solvent as carbon dioxide dissolving agent and calcium carbonate crystal stabilizer to aqueous ammonia solution, adding inorganic polymer morphology control agent to prepared calcium chloride and aqueous ammonia solution, thoroughly mixing, passing carbon dioxide to carbonize until pH value is less than 8, dehydrating calcium carbonate slurry, washing, and drying, preferably (i) adding calcium chloride aqueous solution with aqueous ammonia or calcium hydroxide with ammonium chloride to prepare calcium chloride and aqueous ammonia solution, adding organic solvent and uniformly stirring, (ii) adding inorganic polymer to calcium chloride and ammonia solution as a crystal morphology control agent, (iii) introducing carbon dioxide gas to carry out carbonization reaction, and (iv) dehydrating calcium carbonate slurry, drying, crushing and sieving.
   USE - The process is useful for preparing spherical calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is simple to operate, has short carbonization time and low production cost, and controls the particle diameter of the spherical calcium carbonate within 0.1-5 mu m. The spherical calcium carbonate does not require surface treatment, and has regular spherical shape and excellent dispersibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Process for preparing spherical calcium carbonate by carbonization of calcium chloride and aqueous ammonia solution, involves adding organic solvent as carbon dioxide dissolving agent and calcium carbonate crystal stabilizer to aqueous ammonia solution, adding inorganic polymer morphology control agent to the prepared calcium chloride and aqueous ammonia solution, thoroughly mixing, passing carbon dioxide to carbonize until the pH value is less than 8, dehydrating the calcium carbonate slurry, washing, and drying to obtain spherical calcium carbonate, preferably (i) adding calcium chloride aqueous solution with aqueous ammonia or calcium hydroxide with ammonium chloride to prepare calcium chloride and aqueous ammonia solution, adding organic solvent and uniformly stirring, (ii) adding inorganic polymer to the prepared calcium chloride and ammonia solution as a crystal morphology control agent, (iii) introducing carbon dioxide gas to the calcium chloride and ammonia solution to carry out carbonization reaction, when the pH is less than 8, the reaction is determined to be completed, and stopping the introduction of carbon dioxide gas, and (iv) after the carbonization reaction is completed, dehydrating the calcium carbonate slurry, drying, crushing and sieving to obtain spherical calcium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The crystal form is a mixed crystal form of vaterite crystal form and calcite crystal form, and the crystal morphology is spherical calcium carbonate. Preferred Composition: In the mixed crystal form, the vaterite crystal form is more than 50%, the calcite crystal form is less than 50%, and the spherical calcium carbonate has a particle size of 0.1-5 mu m. The content of calcium chloride in calcium chloride and ammonia solution in step (i) is 2-20%. The molar ratio of ammonia to calcium chloride in step (i) is 2:1, and ammonia can be slightly excessive. Preferred Method: The method forms regular, complete and uniform spherical calcium carbonate, or irregular, uneven or spherical calcium carbonate that contains partially broken spherical calcium due to improper control of reaction conditions and process parameters, and spherical calcium carbonate containing a certain amount of cubic crystal particles. The step (ii) involves adding an inorganic polymer as a crystal morphology control agent to the prepared calcium chloride and ammonia solution, which is polyaluminum chloride and/or polyaluminum sulfate, where the amount of crystal morphology control agent added is 0-8% of the solid content of calcium chloride. The step (iii) involves adding calcium chloride and ammonia solution to the crystal morphology control agent in the carbonization device with stirring, uniformly stirring, adjusting the initial carbonization temperature to 2-50 degrees C, when the stirring is turned on, introducing a gas containing carbon dioxide concentration of 15-100% for carbonization reaction until the pH is less than 8, and stopping the gas to complete the carbonization. The method involves filtering and dehydrating the obtained calcium carbonate slurry, washing, drying, crushing and sieving to obtain spherical calcium carbonate. The step (iv) involves performing precipitation, filtration, and clarification of the filtrate obtained from the dehydration of the calcium carbonate slurry, recycling and reusing, and using for the preparation of the calcium hydroxide slurry into calcium chloride and ammonia solution to achieve recycling. Preferred Conditions: In step (iii), there is no special requirement for the carbonization reactor, and both common carbonization reaction devices and special reaction devices are suitable for the process.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent in step (i) is methanol, ethanol, methyl acetate, ether and/or acetone. Preferred Composition: The amount of organic solvent in step (i) is 10-70% of calcium chloride and ammonia solution.

PT P
PN CN108858957-A; CN108858957-B
TI Foaming supercritical carbon dioxide fluid comprises sealing and opening the airtight cover, placing the product in center of the support at the bottom of the autoclave, closing the airtight cover, placing the autoclave on a curved tray.
AU YAN L
   ZHONG Y
   YUAN Y
AE UNIV HANGZHOU DIANZI (UYHH-C)
GA 2018956046
AB    NOVELTY - Foaming supercritical carbon dioxide fluid comprises: (i) sealing and opening the airtight cover, placing the product in center of the support at the bottom of the autoclave, closing the airtight cover, placing the autoclave on a curved tray in center of the sealed cylinder; (ii) blowing and opening the cylinder, passing carbon dioxide gas from the inlet line into autoclave through vertical line, blowing to replace air in the pipeline and the autoclave; (iii) heating, energizing the heating tube to heat the heating medium in the sealed cylinder, where the heating medium and the heating tube heats the autoclave to a temperature slightly lower than the experimental temperature in the autoclave, stirring the autoclave by stirring device during the heating process; (iv) maintaining constant temperature and constant pressure; and (v) controlling the pressure relief time, reducing the pressure in the autoclave to atmospheric pressure, then taking out the sample.
   USE - The method is useful for foaming supercritical carbon dioxide fluid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the foaming quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Foaming supercritical carbon dioxide fluid comprises: (i) sealing and opening the airtight cover, placing the product in center of the support at the bottom of the autoclave, closing the airtight cover, placing the autoclave on a curved tray in center of the sealed cylinder i.e. the product is sealed in the autoclave; (ii) blowing and opening the cylinder, passing carbon dioxide gas from the inlet line into autoclave through vertical line, blowing for 2-3 minutes to replace air in the pipeline and the autoclave; (iii) heating, energizing the heating tube to heat the heating medium in the sealed cylinder, where the heating medium and the heating tube heats the autoclave to a temperature slightly lower than the experimental temperature in the autoclave, simultaneously the compressor is used to pump high-pressure carbon dioxide gas into the autoclave, and the temperature in the autoclave rises with the pressure of the high pressure carbon dioxide gas to reach the experimental temperature, stirring the autoclave by stirring device during the heating process; (iv) maintaining constant temperature and constant pressure; and (v) controlling the pressure relief time, reducing the pressure in the autoclave to atmospheric pressure, then taking out the sample.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), setting the pressure and temperature of the autoclave to 50 MPa and 250 degrees C respectively, and the inner volume of the autoclave is 600 ml.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the center of the upper surface of the support is provided with a product placement plane, a circular slope is circumferentially connected to the product placement plane, and the annular ramp is inclined from outside to inside. In the step (iii), a cantilevered support tube is connected to the inner wall of the sealed cylinder, a horizontally arranged curved tray is connected to the suspended end of the cantilevered support tube; an autoclave is disposed on the curved tray; and a sealing cavity is formed between the upper surface of the curved tray and the bottom of the autoclave; the upper end of the autoclave is passed up to the top of the sealed cylinder and connected to the pressurizing device. The suspended end of the cantilevered support tube and stirring device are located below the curved tray. The stirring device is connected to a power supply line that is passed through the cantilevered support tube. The upper surface of the curved tray has an arcuate recess. The outer diameter of the bottom of the autoclave is smaller than the open diameter of the curved recess. The bottom of the autoclave is placed at the opening of the arcuate recess. The constant temperature is controlled by a heating tube and/or a heating medium. In the step (iv), maintaining constant pressure using the compressor, an outlet line is connected to the top of the autoclave for control, a ball valve is connected to the outlet line. The bottom of the autoclave is provided with an arcuate convex surface that coincides with the arcuate recess. The heating tube is distributed in the circumferential direction of the autoclave.

PT P
PN RU2660265-C1
TI Method and unit for co2-extract production for obtaining CO2-extract from a plant raw material includes grinding of raw materials.
AU EROFEEVA E G
   TARASOV S V
   TARASOV V E
   CHUMAK A A
   TARANETS O V
AE UNIV KUBAN STATE TECHNOLOGY (UYKU-Non-standard)
GA 201860502B
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to essential oil industry. Method for obtaining CO2-extract from a plant raw material includes grinding of raw materials, loading into the extractor, introducing of gaseous carbon dioxide into the lower part of the extractor, extraction with liquid carbon dioxide in the extractor at equilibrium pressure by repeated circulation, draining the miscella, followed by distillation and condensation of carbon dioxide. In this case, before extraction, the air is removed from the layer of crushed vegetable material by passing carbon dioxide gas at a pressure of 6.76 MPa, until a pressure of 3.3 MPa is reached in the extractor, the extraction is carried out by the method of irrigation with the supply of pure solvent to each stage, number of which is determined by the achievement of exhaustive extraction, followed by holding for 15-20 minutes, the final discharge of the miscella and the removal of gaseous carbon dioxide by heating the oil meal to a temperature of 34-40 degrees C. A unit for producing CO2-extract from plant material includes a cascade-mounted condenser, storage tank of liquid carbon dioxide, heated evaporator, two parallelly installed extractors connected by pipelines to each other and to a evaporator which is connected by a pipeline to a shut-off valve with a condenser and equipped with a pipeline with a stop valve for discharging the finished CO2-extract. Storage tank of liquid carbon dioxide is connected by a pipeline with a shut-off valve with extractors, while the lower parts of the extractors are connected by means of a common pipeline with a three-way tap for draining the miscella with a heated evaporator in connection with the finished CO2-extract, by means of a common pipeline with a three-way stop valve for draining the miscella, the upper parts of the extractors on one side are connected by a pipeline to a valve with a heated evaporator, on the other hand they are equipped with a pipeline with a shut-off valve to exit to the gas holder and a shut-off valve for the discharge of gaseous carbon dioxide into the atmosphere.
   USE - Essential oils industry.
   ADVANTAGE - Invention makes it possible to increase the extraction rate and the amount of extractive substances by organizing extraction process according to the cross-current flow of a solvent with respect to materials.
3 cl, 2 dwg, 1 tbl

GA 2022345422
AB    NOVELTY - Production of synthesis gas involves splitting a hydrocarbon fluid into hydrogen and carbon by plasma energy input, separating at least portion of hydrogen from carbon, and mixing the hydrogen with carbon dioxide at process parameters that allow a conversion of hydrogen and carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide and water. PN CN113730434-A
TI Surgical washing solution used for providing antibacterial effect, comprises sodium chloride, distilled water, hypochlorous acid and medical carbon dioxide.
AU TAO G
AE YUNNAN MIEBA TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (YUNN-Non-standard)
GA 2021F0479F
AB    NOVELTY - Surgical washing solution comprises sodium chloride, distilled water, hypochlorous acid and medical carbon dioxide. The surgical washing solution contains 0.7-1.1 %mass sodium chloride and 36-45 parts per million (ppm) hypochlorous acid. The pH value of the surgical washing solution is adjusted to 5.6-5.8 with medical carbon dioxide.
   USE - The surgical washing liquid is used for providing antibacterial effect.
   ADVANTAGE - The surgical washing liquid has excellent stability and no irritation to the skin and mucous membranes while ensuring its bactericidal function.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for preparing the surgical washing solution, involving (i) adding the weighed sodium chloride to distilled water and dissolving, (ii) adding hypochlorous acid in the dissolved solution to obtain a mixed solution, and (iii) adding medical carbon dioxide to the mixed solution to adjust the pH until the pH is stabilized to be 5.6-5.8.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The surgical washing solution contains 0.9 %mass sodium chloride and 40 ppm hypochlorous acid.
   ACTIVITY - Antibacterial.

PT P
PN CN112980016-A; CN112980016-B
TI Preparation of water-soluble chitosan/noble metal nanocomposite material involves adding chitosan to solvent, introducing carbon dioxide gas to prepare mother liquor, adding metal salt, stirring, and standing.
AU ZHOU Q
   ZHAO B
   LOU C
   JIN X
AE UNIV NINGBO TECHNOLOGY (UNGC-C)
GA 202171922B
AB    NOVELTY - A water-soluble chitosan/noble metal nanocomposite material is prepared by adding chitosan to solvent, introducing carbon dioxide gas at flow rate of 0.1-10 mL/minute for 1 minute to 12 hours to prepare mother liquor, adding metal salt, stirring for 0.5-72 hours, standing for 4-8 hours to obtain chitosan/metal compound/carbon dioxide mixed solution, placing onto film-forming plate, and volatilizing solvent.
   USE - The method for preparing water-soluble chitosan/noble metal nanocomposite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The introduction of carbon dioxide reacts with the amino groups on the chitosan to generate carbamate, reduces the generation of hydrogen bonds, increases the chelating sites of metal particles, strengthens the reduction effect of metal ions, and obtains uniform dispersion. The method is green, environment-friendly and economical utilizing greenhouse gases.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal particles are zero valence, and has average particle size of less than 12 nm, and includes metal salts of silver, gold, copper, palladium and/or platinum.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is formic acid, anhydrous acetic acid and/or anhydrous lactic acid. The mass ratio of chitosan to solvent in the mother liquor is 0.01-1 g/mL. The chitosan/noble metal nanocomposite material can be dissolved in water, and the mass ratio of chitosan to water is 0.01-1 g/mL. The degree of deacetylation of chitosan is 60-10 0%, and the molecular weight is 1000-100000 g/mol. The weight ratio of the metal salt to chitosan is 0.1-20%.

PT P
PN KR2021054775-A
TI Producing double metal catalyst structure for electrochemical carbon-dioxide reduction, comprises e.g. electrospinning spinning solution containing transition metal precursor and carbon nanofiber precursor or nitrogen precursor.
AU JOO Y C
   LEE J
AE UNIV SEOUL NAT R & DB FOUND (USEO-C)
GA 202153493A
AB    NOVELTY - Producing double metal catalyst structure comprises (a) electrospinning a spinning solution containing a transition metal precursor and a carbon nanofiber precursor, or a spinning solution containing a transition metal precursor, a carbon nanofiber precursor, and a nitrogen precursor to obtain a core-shell carbon nanofiber structure; and (b) mixing copper precursor and palladium precursor with carbon nanofiber structure; and (c) heat-treating the mixture through an electric shock.
   USE - The double metal catalyst structure is useful for electrochemical carbon-dioxide reduction.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst structure increases the conversion rate upon conversion to a carbon-dioxide or higher hydrocarbon compound.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for double metal catalyst structure for electrochemical carbon-dioxide reduction comprising nitrogen-doped core-shell carbon nanofibers and copper-palladium double metal particles distributed on the core-shell carbon nanofibers.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises heat-treating the electrospun carbon nanofibers at 750-850 degrees C at a partial pressure of 0.1-0.2 torr oxygen. In the step (c), electric shock is applied for a time of 100-500 ms with a power of 10 W or more. In the step (c), the heat treatment process further comprises an aging after quenching, where the arrangement of copper-palladium double metal particles is aligned through the aging (ordered). Preferred Components: In the step (a), a nitrogen-doped core-shell carbon nanofiber structure is formed. The carbon nanofiber precursor is polyacrylonitrile (PAN), polyimide (PI), polyaniline (PANI) and polypyrrole (PPy). The copper precursor comprises at least one of copper (II) acetate, copper (II) acetylacetonate and copper (II) nitrate. The palladium precursor comprises at least one of palladium acetate, palladium acetylacetonate, palladium nitrate and palladic chloride. The molar ratio of copper precursor and palladium precursor is 5:5-7:3. The copper particles and palladium particles are in the range of 20-50%, based on the total weight of the catalyst respectively obtained. The double metal catalyst structure is -1.3V vs. in an alkaline condition to electrochemically reduce carbon dioxide at the potential of RHE. The carbon nanofibers have a diameter in the range of about 100-200 nm. The average particle diameter of the copper-palladium double metal particles is 10-70 nm.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); F01 (Threads and fibres - natural or artificial; spinning - including the production of mineral and carbon fibres (D01).)

PN CN108690310-A
TI Preparation of foamed grafted olefin polymer used for e.g. food and medical packaging involves molding grafted olefin polymer to obtain crude product, heating, and extracting crude product under supercritical carbon dioxide environment.
AU LI Z
   ZHANG F
   ZHANG X
   JIA J
AE NINGBO NENGZHIGUANG NEW MATERIAL TECHNOL (NING-Non-standard)
GA 201886265M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of foamed grafted olefin polymer comprises molding grafted olefin polymer to obtain crude product; putting crude product into entrainer and soaking for 1-8 hours; putting soaked crude product in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle; injecting carbon dioxide and pressurizing to supercritical state; sealing; heating and extracting; slowly removing carbon dioxide pressure for 10-20 minutes to obtain foamed grafted olefin polymer with low volatile organic compound (VOC).
   USE - Preparation of foamed grafted olefin polymer used for food and medical packaging and other areas of high hygiene standards.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple and environment-friendly process. It produces foamed grafted olefin polymer with extremely low VOC content, no irritating odor, no black and yellow spots, has high foaming ratio and good material strength.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The entrainer comprises acetone, water, dichloromethane, trichloromethane, methanol, ethanol, pyridine, and/or tetrahydrofuran.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The sealing and heating is performed at temperature below melting point and above glass transition temperature of polymer. The supercritical extraction is performed under pressure of 8-45 MPa for 10-60 minutes. The pressure relief is performed for 1-5 seconds. The molding step involves injection molding, extrusion molding, or blow molding. It involves rapidly removing carbon dioxide pressure, re-injecting carbon dioxide, pressurizing to supercritical state, sealing and heating, extracting, and repeating process for 1-5 times. The method involves injecting entrainer while injecting carbon dioxide. Preferred Component: The grafted olefin polymer is polypropylene-grafter-maleic anhydride (PP-g-MAH), polypropylene-grafted-glycidyl methacrylate (PP-g-GMA), polyethylene-grafted-maleic anhydride (PE-g-MAH), polyethylene-grafted-glycidyl methacrylate, ethylene-octane-copolymer-grafted maleic anhydride (POE-g-MAH), or ethylene-octane-copolymer-grafted-glycidyl methacrylate (POE-g-GMA).
DC A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A17 (Polymers of unsubstituted aliphatic monoolefins; including polyethylene.); A92 (Packaging and containers - including ropes and nets.)
MC A04-G01E; A08-B04B; A11-B06A; A12-P01; A12-S04A2; A12-V00V; A12-W09
IP C08J-009/12; C08L-051/06

PI CN10378448    25 Apr 2018

PN CN108529649-A
TI Producing ammonia synthesis gas from sodium cyanide tail gas comprises e.g. pre-purifying sodium cyanide tail gas, carrying out deamination and deacidification, converting carbon monoxide, deoxidizing and mixing high-purity nitrogen.
AU WANG W
   YANG H
   ZHONG Z
AE YANG H (YANG-Individual)
GA 2018748532
AB    NOVELTY - Producing ammonia synthesis gas from sodium cyanide tail gas comprises pre-purifying sodium cyanide tail gas and carrying out deamination and deacidification and removing all sulfur, where the gas composition is more than 90% hydrogen, less than 8% nitrogen, 0.3-0.5% oxygen, 0.3-0.5% carbon monoxide and less than 0.1% carbon dioxide, catalytically converting carbon monoxide into carbon dioxide, converting carbon monoxide from 0.3-0.5% to carbon dioxide, where the residual concentration of carbon monoxide is less than 1 ppm, finely deoxidizing the conversion gas, sending pressure swing adsorption and removing carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide to below 10 ppm, and mixing high-purity nitrogen.
   USE - The method is useful for producing ammonia synthesis gas from sodium cyanide tail gas.
DC E35; H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)
MC E11-W; E32-A01; H04-E; H04-F02E

PT P
PN WO2018141649-A1; CN110234428-A; EP3576871-A1; US2019381486-A1; JP2020505227-W; US10888846-B2; JP6830543-B2; EP3576871-B1
TI Catalyst useful for methanation of carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide with gaseous hydrogen, comprises aluminum oxide, a nickel active mass and manganese.
AU KOEHLER K
   THOMYS O
   HINRICHSEN K
   KOSCHANY F
   BURGER T
AE CLARIANT PROD DEUT GMBH (CLRN-C)
   WACKER CHEM AG (WACK-C)
   UNIV TECH MUENCHEN (MUEN-C)
   CLARIANT GMBH (CLRN-C)
   CLARIANT PROD DEUT GMBH (CLRN-C)
   WACKER CHEM AG (WACK-C)
   CLARIANT PROD DEUT GMBH (CLRN-C)
   CLARIANT PROD DEUT GMBH (CLRN-C)
   WACKER CHEM AG (WACK-C)
   UNIV TECH MUENCHEN (MUEN-C)
GA 201861432A
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises aluminum oxide, a nickel active mass and manganese. The molar ratio of nickel and manganese in the catalyst is 3-10, preferably 5.5-6.5.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for methanation of carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide with gaseous hydrogen (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high selectivity and stability, and improved activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing catalyst, comprising (a) co-precipitating a solution containing aluminum, nickel and manganese in dissolved form to obtain a precipitate, (b) isolating the precipitate from the step (a), (c) drying the isolated precipitate from the step (b), and (d) calcining the dried precipitate from the step (c); and
   (2) methanation of carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide, comprising contacting a gas containing carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide with the above mentioned catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nickel active mass has crystallite with a diameter of less than 20 nm, preferably less than 10 nm. The catalyst has a carbon dioxide-absorption capacity at 35 degrees C of greater than 200 mu mol, preferably 200-300 mu mol/g. The solution from the step (a) is an aqueous solution. In the method: nickel, aluminum and manganese are present in dissolved form as ionic compounds in the solution and ionic compounds have the same anion; and manganese present in the solution of the step (a) is in oxidation state (II) or (III). The anion is a nitrate, sulfate, a halide, chloride or acetate. Preferred Method: The precipitate is aged in the solution for at least 30 minutes. The isolated precipitate from the step (b) is washed. The calcination of the dried precipitate is carried out at 300-600 degrees C in air. In the methanation method, the gas is contacted with the catalyst at above 200 degrees C.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)
MC E10-J02D1; E11-D02; H04-E; H04-F02E; H04-F05; H04-F06; N02-C01; N03-E; N06-E01; N06-F; N07-B02

TI Low toxicity non-flammable gas mist cleaning agent comprises ethanol, carbon dioxide, 1,1,1,2-tetrafluoroethane, 1,1-dichloro-1-fluoroethane and CnH2(n+2).
AU SHANG L
   ZHONG L
   XIAO Y
   ZHOU B
   ZUO Z
   GOU P
AE SICHUAN DONGSHU NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (DFEC-C)
GA 201853344A
AB    NOVELTY - Low toxicity non-flammable gas mist cleaning agent comprises ethanol, carbon dioxide, 1,1,1,2-tetrafluoroethane, 1,1-dichloro-1-fluoroethane, and CnH2(n+2).
   USE - Used as low toxicity non-flammable gas mist cleaning agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The cleaning agent: reduces the flammability and toxicity of the aerosol; and ensures and improves cleaning effect of the cleaning aerosol.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Low toxicity non-flammable gas mist cleaning agent comprises ethanol, carbon dioxide, 1,1,1,2-tetrafluoroethane, 1,1-dichloro-1-fluoroethane, and CnH2(n+2).
   n = 9-13.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of cleaning agent comprising mixing ethanol, 1,1,1,2-tetrafluoroethane, 1,1-dichloro-1-fluoroethane, and CnH2(n+2), encapsulating into an aerosol bottle, and packaging carbon dioxide into an aerosol bottle to obtain final product..
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The calorific value of combustion after mixing each substance is 3-30 KJ/g. The heat of combustion after mixing each substance is 5-20 KJ/g. Preferred Composition: The low toxicity non-flammable gas mist cleaning agent comprises 1-5 wt.% ethanol, 10-30 wt.% carbon dioxide, 20-55 wt.% 1,1,1,2-tetrafluoroethane, 8-25 wt.% 1,1-dichloro-1-fluoroethane, and 5-44 wt.% CnH2(n+2). The internal pressure of the aerosol bottle at 0 degrees C is 0.35-45 MPa (after the carbon dioxide is encapsulated in the aerosol bottle).

PN CN107941097-A
TI Liquid carbon dioxide hydro-fracturing rock            processing method, involves connecting cracking pipe to            blaster by conductor in series, connecting crystalline            converter with blaster, where igniting voltage of            cracking pipe is in specific range.
AU YU X
AE CHINA RAILWAY TEN BUREAU GROUP EIGHTH (CREN-C)
GA 2018339941
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves detecting a rock level                blasting object. Inner diameter of a cracking pipe                and liquid carbon dioxide filling amount are                selected according to different levels of a rock.                An air-facing plane is formed. Drilled holes                cleaning process is performed. Multiple cracking                pipes are assembled on a liquid carbon dioxide                filling machine. Each cracking hole is sealed. Each                cracking pipe is connected to a high-energy pulse                blaster by a conductor in series. A crystalline                converter is connected with the blaster, where                igniting voltage of the cracking pipe is about                1800V.
   USE - Liquid carbon dioxide hydro-fracturing rock                processing method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reducing construction                period, rock blasting vibration and rock blasting                noise, increasing blasting quantity, and avoiding                blasting influence to surrounding buildings and                people.
DC Q79 (Weapons, ammunition, blasting (F41, 2))

PT P
PN CN113842744-A
TI Method for adsorption, purification and recycling of perfluoroisobutyronitrile/carbon dioxide recovery insulating gas, involves introducing recycled insulating gas into column with adsorbent to remove organic impurities, and recycling.
AU QUAN H
   LIU D
   QING F
   GUO Q
   DONG L
AE BEIJING YUJI SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
   CHEM & CHEM ENG GUANGDONG LAB (CHCH-Non-standard)
GA 2022098143
AB    NOVELTY - Method for adsorption, purification and recycling of perfluoroisobutyronitrile/carbon dioxide recovery insulating gas, involves performing adsorption-purification by introducing the recovery insulating gas into fixed-bed adsorption column filled with an adsorbent to remove most of organic impurities, water and hydrofluoric acid, and performing gas redistribution by measuring volume content ratio of perfluoroisobutyronitrile/carbon dioxide in obtained mixed gas, re-mixing the mixed gas to original insulating gas and directly recycling. The adsorbent is composed of an active component and a carrier. The active component comprises transition metal compounds and alkali metal and/or alkaline earth metal compounds. The alkali metal is selected from e.g. lithium and cesium. The alkaline earth metal is selected from e.g. beryllium and barium. The transition metal is selected from e.g. copper and zinc. The carrier is e.g. SBA-15-type molecular sieve and HZSM-5-type molecular sieve.
   USE - Method for adsorption, purification and recycling of perfluoroisobutyronitrile/carbon dioxide recovery insulating gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent can effectively absorb the impurity in perfluoroisobutyronitrile recycling gas with adsorption rate of 99.1%, and has excellent removing effect for water and hydrofluoric acid. The recycled insulating gas after primary adsorption can satisfy lowest limit requirement of impurity. The method is simple and practical, and greatly reduces recycling cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for adsorption, purification and recycling of perfluoroisobutyronitrile/carbon dioxide recovery insulating gas, involves performing adsorption-purification by introducing the recovery insulating gas into fixed-bed adsorption column filled with an adsorbent to remove most of organic impurities, water and hydrofluoric acid, and performing gas redistribution by measuring volume content ratio of perfluoroisobutyronitrile/carbon dioxide in obtained mixed gas, re-mixing the mixed gas to original insulating gas or the volume of perfluoroisobutyronitrile/carbon dioxide in the required insulating gas and directly recycling after comparison. The adsorbent is composed of an active component and a carrier. The active component comprises one or more of transition metal compounds and one or more of alkali metal and/or alkaline earth metal compounds. The alkali metal is selected from lithium, sodium, potassium, rubidium and cesium. The alkaline earth metal is selected from beryllium, magnesium, calcium, strontium and barium. The transition metal is selected from copper, silver, iron, cobalt, nickel, palladium, platinum, tantalum, lanthanum, cerium, promethium, neodymium, yttrium, scandium and zinc. The carrier is one or more of X-type molecular sieve, Y-type molecular sieve, SBA-15-type molecular sieve and HZSM-5-type molecular sieve, and the metal element loading in the active component is 1-25%. The perfluoroisobutyronitrile/carbon dioxide recovery insulating gas is insulating gas of perfluoroisobutyronitrile/carbon dioxide insulating gas through discharging or thermal decomposition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The active component is loaded on the carrier, by at least one of ion exchange method, mechanical mixing method and dipping method. The ion exchange method is carried out by mixing and modifying the carrier and the metal salt ion solution of the active component precursor, leaving still for cleaning, drying at 80-150degrees Celsius and calcining at 200-400degrees Celsius to obtain the modified adsorbent, where the concentration of the metal salt ion solution is 0.01-5 mol/L and the solid-liquid ratio of the carrier to the metal salt ion solution is 1/1-1/50. The mechanical mixing method is carried out by mixing and roasting the carrier and the metal salt of the active component precursor, where the mass ratio of the carrier to the metal salt is 1/0.1-1/5. The impregnation method is carried out by mixing and modifying the carrier and the metal salt ion solution of the active component precursor, leaving still for cleaning, drying at 80-150degrees Celsius and calcining at 200-400degrees Celsius to obtain the modified adsorbent, where the solution concentration is 0.01-5 mol/L.Preferred Composition: The solid-liquid ratio of the carrier to the metal salt ion solution is 1/1-1/20. The loading amount of metal element is 1-25%. The total loading amount of alkali metals and/or alkaline earth metals in the active components is 0.1-20%. The loading amount of transition metals is 0.1-10%.Preferred Components: The metal salt is a mixture of one or more of transition metal chloride, nitrate or carbonate and one or more of alkali metal and/or alkaline earth metal chloride, nitrate or carbonate. The precursor of the active component is nitrates and carbonates. The alkali metal is preferably sodium or potassium. The alkaline earth metal is preferably magnesium or calcium. The transition metal is preferably cobalt, lanthanum, cerium or zinc. The carrier is X-type molecular sieve or SBA-15-type molecular sieve.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The recovery insulating gas contains impurities oxalonitrile, trifluoroacetonitrile, pentafluoropropionitrile, hexafluoropropylene, hydrofluoric acid, carbon monoxide and water.Preferred Composition: The volume ratio of perfluoroisobutyronitrile/carbon dioxide in the original insulating gas is 1:4.
DC E16 (Aliphatics - containing N and/or halogen.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC E31-N05C; E11-Q02C; J01-E02B1; J01-D; J04-X
IP B01D-053/04; B01J-020/04; B01J-020/28; B01J-020/30; C07C-253/34; C07C-255/10
