PT P
PN US2021094893-A1; US11208364-B2
TI Producing hydrocarbon compounds including two            carbon atoms, comprises performing oxidative coupling            of methane (OCM) reaction, separating OCM effluent            stream, separating second stream and directing OCM            reactor feed to OCM reactor.
AU RAFIQUE H A
   VUDDAGIRI S
   RADAELLI G
   SCHER E C
   MCCORMICK J
   CIZERON J
AE LUMMUS TECHNOLOGY LLC (CBRI-C)
GA 2021342248
AB    NOVELTY - Producing (M1) hydrocarbon compounds including                at least two carbon atoms (C2+ compounds),                comprises: (a) performing an oxidative coupling of                methane (OCM) reaction in an OCM reactor to produce                an OCM effluent stream comprising carbon dioxide,                carbon monoxide, hydrogen, at least one C2+                compounds, and methane; (b) separating the OCM                effluent stream into a first stream comprising at                least some of the C2+ compound and a second stream                comprising carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide,                hydrogen, and methane; (c) separating the second                stream in a pressure swing adsorption (PSA) unit to                produce an OCM reactor feed comprising methane and                a third stream comprising hydrogen and carbon                monoxide and/or carbon dioxide; and (d) directing                the OCM reactor feed to the OCM reactor.
   USE - The methods are useful for producing                hydrocarbon compounds including at least two C2+                compounds.
   ADVANTAGE - The methods: provide OCM systems for small                scale and world scale production of olefins; and                utilize methanation subsystem downstream and                fluidically coupled to the OCM subsystem can be                used to react hydrogen with carbon monoxide and/or                carbon dioxide in the non-C2+ impurities that                generate methane, which can be recycled to the OCM                subsystem.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) producing (M2) hydrocarbon compounds                including at least two C2+ compounds, comprising                (a1) performing OCM reaction in an OCM reactor to                produce an OCM effluent stream comprising carbon                dioxide, carbon monoxide, hydrogen, at least one                C2+ compound, and methane, (b1) separating the OCM                effluent stream in PSA unit into a first stream                comprising at least some of the C2+ compounds and                CL and a second stream comprising carbon dioxide,                carbon monoxide and hydrogen, (c1) separating the                first stream in a demethanizer unit to produce an                OCM reactor feed comprising methane and a third                stream comprising C2+ compounds and (d1) directing                the OCM reactor feed to the OCM reactor; and
   (2) producing (M3) hydrocarbon compounds                including at least two C2+ compounds, comprising                (a2) performing OCM reaction in an OCM reactor to                produce an OCM effluent stream comprising carbon                dioxide, carbon monoxide, hydrogen, at least one                C2+ compound, and methane, (b2) separating the OCM                effluent stream in PSA unit into a first stream                comprising methane and a second stream comprising                at least some of the C2+ compounds, carbon dioxide,                carbon monoxide and hydrogen, (c2) separating the                second stream to produce a third stream comprising                C2+ compounds and a fourth stream comprising carbon                monoxide carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and                hydrogen, and (d2) directing the first stream to                the OCM reactor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows methanation systems that can                be used with systems.
   Methanation system (600)
   First reactor (601)
   Second reactor (602)
   Heat exchanger (603)
   Recycle stream (604)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method (M1) further                  comprises: prior to step (b), directing the OCM                  effluent stream to a post-bed cracking unit to                  generate olefins from alkanes in the OCM                  effluent; directing a fourth stream comprising                  ethane to the post-bed cracking unit; directing                  the third stream to a methanation reactor to                  produce a second OCM reactor feed comprising                  methane formed from the hydrogen and carbon                  monoxide and/or carbon dioxide in the third                  stream, and directing the second OCM reactor feed                  to the OCM reactor; directing the OCM effluent                  stream and a natural gas feed stream comprising                  methane to a process gas compressor unit that is                  downstream of the OCM reactor; directing the                  natural gas feed stream to a sulfur removal unit                  prior to directing the natural gas feed stream to                  the process gas compressor; and directing an                  ethane feed stream and an oxidant feed stream to                  the OCM reactor. The method (M2) further                  comprises: prior to step (b1), directing the OCM                  effluent stream to a post-bed cracking unit to                  generate olefins from alkanes in the OCM                  effluent; directing a fourth stream comprising                  ethane to the post-bed cracking unit; directing                  the second stream to a methanation reactor to                  produce a second OCM reactor feed comprising                  methane formed from the carbon dioxide, carbon                  monoxide and hydrogen in the second stream, and                  directing the second OCM reactor feed to the OCM                  reactor; directing the OCM effluent stream and a                  natural gas feed stream comprising methane to a                  process gas compressor unit that is downstream of                  the OCM reactor and upstream of the PSA unit; and                  directing an ethane feed stream and an oxidant                  feed stream to the OCM reactor. The method (M3)                  further comprises: prior to step (b2), directing                  the OCM effluent stream to a post-bed cracking                  unit to generate olefins from alkanes in the OCM                  effluent; directing a fifth stream comprising                  ethane to the post-bed cracking unit; directing                  the fourth stream to a methanation reactor to                  produce a second OCM reactor feed comprising                  methane formed from the carbon dioxide, carbon                  monoxide and hydrogen in the fourth stream, and                  directing the second OCM reactor feed to the OCM                  reactor; directing the OCM effluent stream and a                  natural gas feed stream comprising methane to a                  process gas compressor unit that is downstream of                  the OCM reactor and upstream of the PSA unit; and                  directing an ethane feed stream and an oxidant                  feed stream to the OCM reactor.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Air conditioner has store that is provided with gas supply apparatus to change into multiple stages of gas supply volume supplied in store of storage, and target oxygen concentration changes gas supply volume into gas supply apparatus.
AU FUJIMOTO Y
   KAMEI N
   YUUSUKE F
   NORITAKA K
AE DAIKIN KOGYO KK (DAIK-C)
   DAIKIN IND LTD (DAIK-C)
   DAIKIN IND LTD (DAIK-C)
   DAIKIN IND LTD (DAIK-C)
GA 201975639K
AB    NOVELTY - The air conditioner has a store that is provided with a gas supply apparatus to change into multiple stages of the gas supply volume supplied in the store of a storage. A controller is provided. The carbon dioxide levels of the air in a store turn into a concentration within a predetermined reference concentration range, when the oxygen concentration of the air in the said store is higher than target oxygen concentration. The carbon-dioxide-levels adjustment operation to which a gas supply operation is carried out and the oxygen concentration of the air in the store is reduced to the target oxygen concentration that is performed, changing the gas supply volume into the gas supply apparatus according to the carbon dioxide levels of the air in the store.
   USE - Air conditioner.
   ADVANTAGE - The internal air can be adjusted to a desired composition, even when the carbon dioxide concentration in the internal air is low at the start of operation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The gas supply apparatus which performs gas supply operation which supplies the low oxygen concentration air whose oxygen concentration generated by removing oxygen from external air is lower than the external air in the store of storage for accommodating a plant (15) which breathes. The controller controls operation of gas supply apparatus so that a composition of the air in a store of storage turns into a desired composition. The store is provided with the gas supply apparatus to change into multiple stages the gas supply volume supplied in the store of said storage. The reference concentration range is changed according to the initial concentration which is carbon dioxide levels of the air in the store at the time of operation start of the carbon-dioxide-levels adjustment operation. The quantity increase control which raises one step of the gas supply volume of the gas supply apparatus is performed. The carbon dioxide levels of the air in the store are maintained, the quantity reduction control is performed. The operation mode of the air conditioner in the store is switched to the carbon-dioxide-levels adjustment operation from the oxygen concentration reduction. A refrigerant circuit performs a refrigerating cycle. A freezing apparatus for containers adjusts a composition of the air in a store while cooling the air in a store of the container which is the storage.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a control flow diagram of the carbon dioxide concentration adjustment operation. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Step for forming a storage space (S1)
   Step for dividing storage space (S2)
   Step for determining the gas supply apparatus (S3)
   Step for increasing gas supply volume (S4)
   Step for determining whether the quantity reduction conditions are satisfied (S5)

GA 202255221H

   USE - Silver-doped copper vanadate composite photocatalytic material for use in visible light catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The silver-doped copper vanadate composite photocatalytic material has high catalytic activity and stability for reducing carbon dioxide under the irradiation of visible light, can convert carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide with high selectivity, and is simple in preparation process, eco-friendly and beneficial to large-scale production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1)a method for preparing a silver-doped copper vanadate composite photocatalytic material, which involves: performing hydrothermal reaction on the aqueous solution containing the vanadium source, the copper source and the silver source to obtain the vanadium-containing copper-silver composite material; and

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The application method of the silver-doped copper vanadate composite photocatalytic material specifically involves contacting carbon dioxide with silver-doped copper vanadate composite catalyst for reduction reaction under visible light condition and in a saturated water vapor atmosphere, and converting into carbon monoxide. Preferred Conditions: The nanobelt-shaped copper vanadium oxide (CuV2O6) has a length of 10-15 microm, a width of 200-500 nanometer, and a thickness of 10-30 nanometer. The doping amount of the silver ions in the nanobelt-shaped CuV2O6 is 1-10 wt.%. The molar ratio of the silver source to the copper source is 1:100-1:10. The hydrothermal reaction is performed at the pH of 4-6, the temperature is 180-220degrees Celsius, and time is 18-24 hours.

GA 2021703979
AB    NOVELTY - The process involves electrochemically                converting carbon dioxide to formic acid or salt by                applying a voltage to an electrochemical cell                including an indium-containing catalytic electrode                as cathode and an anode. The electrochemical cell                is fed with an electrolyte including carbon                dioxide. The catalytic electrode is regenerated by                lowering the voltage and subsequently washing the                catalytic electrode with an aqueous liquid and                exposing the catalytic electrode to air without                applying voltage. The conversion process and the                regeneration process are optionally repeated.
   USE - Process for electrochemical conversion of                carbon dioxide to formic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The regeneration process provides an improved                faradaic yield of the electrochemical reaction and                an improved lifetime of the electrode.    (1) a use of a regeneration step for improving                the faradaic yield of an electrochemical                process;
   (2) a use of a regeneration step for improving                the lifetime of an indium-containing catalytic                electrode; and
   (3) an electrochemical cell assembly for                reduction of carbon dioxide to formic acid.

PN CN112522737-A
TI Preparing carbon dioxide reduction catalyst of copper-doped nitrogen-rich porous carbon hollow sphere comprises e.g. adding anthracene oil, nano calcium carbonate, dopamine hydrochloride and copper sulfate into tetrahydrofuran.
AU SUN H
AE SUN H (SUNH-Individual)
GA 202131486L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide reduction catalyst of copper-doped nitrogen-rich porous carbon hollow sphere comprises (i) adding anthracene oil fraction, nano calcium carbonate template agent, dopamine hydrochloride and copper sulfate into the tetrahydrofuran, dispersing ultrasonically and uniformly, stirring at constant temperature for 2-4 hours in the 35-45 degrees C water bath, filtering and drying to obtain the precursor, (ii) grinding the precursor fully, placing the obtained powder in the atmosphere tubular furnace, carrying out heat activation process, carbonizing and cooling to obtain the solid product; and (iii) placing the solid product in the water bath device with the hydrochloric acid solution of 70-90 degrees C, stirring at constant temperature for 1-3 hours, filtering, washing and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon dioxide reduction catalyst of copper-doped nitrogen-rich porous carbon hollow sphere.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces carbon hollow sphere which improves the Faraday efficacy of the composite material, accelerates the electron transmission, increases the gas-liquid contact area, exposes more active sites, improves the dynamic speed of the electro-catalytic reduction carbon dioxide, releases carbon monoxide speed, and realizes more excellent electro-catalysis reducing carbon dioxide performance.

PT P
PN CN110396591-A
TI Performing iron ore carburizing method involves constituting portion of iron ore powder to form core, parcel is wrapped outside core to form pellet, and package is composed of another portion of iron ore fines and carbon dioxide absorbent.
AU CHEN D
   GUO H
   YAN B
   ZHANG S
   CHEN S
AE UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
GA 201992713B
AB    NOVELTY - Performing iron ore carburizing method involves constituting a portion of iron ore powder to form a core. The parcel is wrapped outside the core to form a pellet. The package is composed of another portion of iron ore fines and a carbon dioxide absorbent. The carbon dioxide absorber comprises calcium oxide and calcium hydroxide. The pellet is calcined at a temperature of from 900-1050 degrees C. The calcined pellets are subjected to a reduction treatment under a hydrogen atmosphere of 800-1000 degrees C. The reduced pellets are subjected to carburization treatment under a carbon monoxide atmosphere of 550-750 degrees C. The carburized pellets are ball milled and then magnetically selected to obtain an iron carbide concentrate.
   USE - Method for performing iron ore carburizing method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform iron ore carburizing method adopts carbon dioxide absorbent coating and alternating absorption of reducing exhaust gas and carburizing exhaust gas, effectively purifying the carburizing exhaust gas and reducing carbon dioxide emissions, and the carburizing efficiency is high, and the production cost is lowered and the yield is improved.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Conditions: The reducing exhaust gas is introduced into a water vapor removal tank having a temperature of 300-400 degrees C to form a reduced regeneration gas. The reduced regeneration gas is then passed to a reduction processing chamber. The water vapor remover in the water vapor removal tank is calcium oxide particles having a particle size of less than 1 mm. The carbon dioxide remover in the carbon dioxide removal tank is calcium oxide particles having a particle size of less than 1 mm. The diameter of the core is 3-6 mm. The pellets have a diameter of 8-10 mm. The diameter of the core is 3-6 mm. The pellets have a diameter of 8-10 mm. The weight ratio of the iron ore fines to the carbon dioxide absorbent is from 80-95:5-20. The particle size of the carbon dioxide absorber in the package is less than 1 mm. The calcination treatment time is 5-30 minutes. The reduction treatment time is 30-60 minutes. The carburizing treatment time is 30-210 minutes.
DC M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).)
MC M24-A01A
IP C22B-001/00; C22B-001/02

PT P
PN CN209229949-U
TI Person in room-based air conditioner and fresh air system predictive control system, has controller connected with image collector and gas concentration sensor for receiving current indoor image and/or carbon dioxide concentration data.
AU WANG F
   LIN B
   LIU Y
   CHEN Q
   LU W
   DOU Q
AE UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
   BEIJING FUTURE SCI PARK TECHNOLOGY DEV (BEIJ-Non-standard)
   BEIJING PERSAGY ENERGY SAVING TECHNOLOGY (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 201970954M
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims an air conditioner and fresh air system predictive control system of indoor people, comprising: setting the indoor first preset position of at least one image collector; set in the room at the second predetermined position of at least one gas concentration sensor and a controller, wherein the controller is respectively connected with at least one image collector and at least one gas concentration sensor, for receiving current indoor image obtained and/or current indoor carbon dioxide concentration data by data fusion; to identify the current person in the room according to the current indoor carbon dioxide concentration data and present the number of person in the room to obtain the amount of temperature change and carbon dioxide concentration change amount after the first preset time, according to the temperature change amount and the carbon dioxide concentration change amount adjusting air and/or fresh air system. The system effectively improves the accuracy of the number identification so as to improve accuracy of air conditioner and fresh air system predictive control, is simple and easy to implement.
DC Q74 (Heating, ranges, ventilating (F24)); S01 (Electrical Instruments); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation); T01 (Digital Computers); X27 (Domestic Electric Appliances\n)
MC Q74-A02B; Q74-T20; S01-H09; S03-B01E; S03-E04X; S03-F06A; T01-J08A; X27-E01B

PT P
PN CN110057124-A; CN210089172-U
TI Partially overlapping quotient type-B carbon dioxide trans-critical double-stage compression refrigerating system, has subcooled system and trans critical compression refrigeration system connected by cooling evaporator.
AU SUN Z
   LI J
   WANG C
   YANG L
   LIU Y
   XU Y
AE UNIV TIANJIN COMMERCE (UTJC-C)
GA 2019669168
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a compressor, a condenser, a throttle valve and a cooling evaporator refrigerant connected by a pipeline. A low temperature evaporator and a low-temperature compressor are connected with a middle temperature evaporator and the compressor. A medium pressure throttle valve is connected between an intermediate temperature evaporator and an intercooler. A refrigerant outlet of a gas cooler is connected to a refrigerant inlet of the cooling evaporator through the pipeline. A refrigerant outlet of the cooling evaporator is connected to a high pressure throttle valve through the pipeline. A partially cascaded subcooled refrigeration system and a carbon dioxide trans critical two-stage compression refrigeration system are connected by the cooling evaporator.
   USE - Partially overlapping quotient type-B carbon dioxide trans-critical double-stage compression refrigerating system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can realize combination of various cooling and heating requirements of commercial supermarkets, avoid low coefficient of performance of carbon dioxide refrigerant for commercial super-cooling system, ensure safety performance of the system and effectively improve energy utilization efficiency.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a pneumatic circuit diagram of a partially overlapping quotient type-b carbon dioxide trans-critical double-stage compression refrigerating system. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'
DC Q74 (Heating, ranges, ventilating (F24)); Q75 (Refrigeration, liquefaction (F25)); X27 (Domestic Electric Appliances\n)
MC Q74-A02A; Q74-A02B; Q75-A02A; Q75-A02H; X27-F02A; X27-F02C
IP F24F-005/00; F24H-004/02; F25B-001/10; F25B-029/00

PT P
PN WO2019103235-A1; KR1981457-B1
TI Treatment of gas used as coke oven gas, involves preparing flue gas, separating exhaust gas and hydrogen sulfide in assist gas having lower carbon dioxide content than exhaust gas, and separating exhaust gas from ammonia in assist gas.
AU LEE S J
   CHO M K
   KIM B I
   CHOI J W
   PARK K T
   JAE L S
   KYUNG C M
   IL K B
   WON C J
   TAE P K
AE POSCO (POSC-C)
GA 201948401V
AB    NOVELTY - Treatment of gas involves preparing a flue gas, providing an assist gas having a lower carbon dioxide content than the exhaust gas, separating the exhaust gas and hydrogen sulfide contained in the assist gas, and separating the exhaust gas from ammonia contained in the assist gas.
   USE - Treatment of gas used as coke oven gas for fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method treats gas by efficiently separating hydrogen sulfide from the exhaust gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for gas-treatment apparatus, which has a hydrogen sulfide collector for separating hydrogen sulfide from the exhaust gas discharged from the reactor, an ammonia collector for separating ammonia from the exhaust gas passed through the hydrogen sulfide collector, a regenerator for treating a solution (S1) discharged from the hydrogen sulfide collector, a distiller for treating a solution (S2) discharged from the ammonia collector, a carbon dioxide separator for separating carbon dioxide from a mixed gas of an acid gas generated in the regenerator and ammonia gas generated in the ammonia collector, and introducing a mixed gas in which carbon dioxide is separated into the exhaust gas, and a gas-liquid separator.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The hydrogen sulfide is separated by gas treatment using an amine aqueous solution containing monoethanolamine, diethanolamine and methyl diethanolamine. Treatment of gas further involves treating solution (S1) by heating to separate an acid gas containing hydrogen sulfide into an amine aqueous solution and separating the hydrogen sulfide in which the gas is recycled, and treating solution (S2) by heating solution (S2) to separate into water and ammonia gas, and producing sulfur by heat-treating a portion of the separated ammonia gas and an acid gas generated in treatment of solution (S1), and supplying separated carbon dioxide to a reactor for treating the raw material and reducing the carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide. The assist gas is produced by separating carbon dioxide from a mixed gas containing a process gas generated in process of generating sulfur and a portion of the separated ammonia gas. The process gas is separated from the assist gas. Separation of carbon dioxide involves cooling the process gas and a mixed gas obtained by mixing a portion of the separated ammonia gas to produce carbon dioxide contained in the mixed gas as an ammonium carbonate, and dissolving the ammonium carbonate salt in steam to produce an aqueous ammonium hydrogen carbonate solution.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. PN CN108793128-A
TI Preparing multilayer graphene by pouring magnesium particles into corundum ark, then introducing into self-propagating synthesis furnace, sealing, introducing carbon dioxide gas, and igniting magnesium powder to react with gas.
AU CHENG J
   YANG J
   ZHU S
   TAN H
   WANG S
   LIU W
AE LANZHOU CHEM & PHYSICS INST CHINESE ACAD (CLCP-C)
GA 2018927554
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing multilayer graphene by high temperature self-propagation method involves: (a) pouring magnesium particles with a particle size of 100-200 mesh into a long strip-shaped corundum ark and maintaining the thickness at 3-5 mm; (b) placing the corundum ark carrying magnesium chips in a self-propagating synthesis furnace, embedding a spiral molybdenum wire having a length equivalent to that of the corundum ark in the magnesium chips and connecting the electrodes at both ends; (c) sealing the self-propagating synthesis furnace, introducing carbon dioxide gas, charging and discharging to eliminate the interference of the air and then continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas; and (d) connecting an electric current by an electric welding machine, igniting the magnesium powder to react with carbon dioxide gas, cooling, collecting the target product, ultrasonically cleaning with dilute hydrochloric acid, washing with distilled water, carrying out suction filtration and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing multilayer graphene by high temperature self-propagation method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and economical preparation of the multilayer graphene with high purity and yield and without requiring larger equipment, can effectively use the carbon dioxide greenhouse gas in the atmosphere, and uses wide source of raw materials.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step of washing the target product with distilled water is performed until the pH-value is neutral. Preferred Condition: The pressure of the carbon dioxide gas in the self-propagating synthesis furnace is 0.2-0.5 MPa. The ignition of magnesium powder is controlled at 60-80 A for 5-15 seconds. The ultrasonically cleaning step is carried out for 10-12 hours. Preferred Components: The mass percent of the diluted hydrochloric acid is 5-10%.

PN FR3075660-A1; WO2019122661-A1; FR3075660-B1; CA3085236-A1; CN111447985-A; KR2020097730-A; EP3727648-A1; US2021086129-A1; US11291946-B2; CN111447985-B
TI Producing biomethane by purifying biogas feed            stream, comprises e.g. introducing feed gas stream into            pre-treatment unit, and subjecting gaseous stream            depleted of carbon dioxide to cryogenic separation to            separate nitrogen from gas stream.
AU TERRIEN P
   CHANTANT N
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   CLAUDE METHODS STUDY & DEV GEORGES (CLAU-Non-standard)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 2019539744
AB    NOVELTY - Producing biomethane (40) by purifying biogas                feed stream (1), comprises: (a) introducing feed                gas stream into pre-treatment unit (5); (b)                subjecting gaseous stream (22) from step (a)                depleted of carbon dioxide to cryogenic separation                in distillation column (26) to separate nitrogen                from gas stream; (c) recovering stream enriched in                methane obtained from cryogenic separation by                pumping bottom product from column at pressure P2                of greater than 25 bar absolute, preferably greater                than 25 bar, critical pressure of product, where                the gaseous stream from carbon dioxide-depleted                step (a) is carried out in step (b) having oxygen                concentration equal to C1 and when C1 is strictly                greater than 1 mol% and process is stopped and when                C1 is strictly greater than 0.1 mol%, the gaseous                stream results from carbon dioxide depleted step                (a) implemented in step (b) is introduced into                distillation column at level of between plateau n-4                and plateau n.
   USE - The process and plant are useful for producing                biomethane by purifying biogas feed stream from                non-hazardous waste storage facilities (ISDND)                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process and plant: produce biomethane in                accordance with the injection into a natural gas                system or in local use as a vehicle fuel; reduce                operating costs; reduce further the carbon dioxide                content at the outlet of the membrane step, while                ensuring a sufficient solubility of the carbon                dioxide in the gas to be treated (mainly methane)                in order to avoid a crystallization and that at any                point of the process; and limit the accumulation of                oxygen during the distillation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing biomethane (40) by purifying a                biogas feed stream (1), comprises: (a) introducing                the feed gas stream into a pre-treatment unit (5),                where the gas stream is partially separated from                the carbon dioxide and oxygen contained and is                compressed to a pressure P1 of greater than 10 bar                abs; (b) subjecting the gaseous stream (22) from                step (a) depleted of carbon dioxide to a cryogenic                separation in a distillation column (26) to                separate the nitrogen from the gas stream, where                the column of distillation comprises n trays, n is                an integer of 8-100; (c) recovering a stream                enriched in methane obtained from the cryogenic                separation by pumping the bottom product from the                column at a pressure P2 of greater than 25 bar                absolute, preferably greater than 25 bar, critical                pressure of the product, where the gaseous stream                from the carbon dioxide-depleted step (a) is                carried out in step (b) having an oxygen                concentration equal to C1 and when C1 is strictly                greater than 1 mol% and the process is stopped and                when C1 is strictly greater than 0.1 mol%, the                gaseous stream results from the carbon dioxide                depleted step (a) implemented in step (b) is                introduced into the distillation column at a level                of between plateau n-4 and plateau n, and plateau n                is the highest plateau in the column. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a plant                comprising successively a source of biogas, a                pre-treatment unit for removing all or part of the                volatile organic compounds, water, sulfur compounds                from the gas stream to be treated, at least two                stages of separating membranes capable of partially                separating the carbon dioxide and the oxygen from                the gaseous flow, a compressor capable of                compressing the gas stream at a pressure of 25-100                bar, a heat exchanger adapted to cool the depleted                carbon dioxide gas stream, a distillation column,                where the distillation column comprises n trays and                the level of introduction of the stream to be                treated in the column depends on the oxygen                concentration of the stream to be treated and n is                8-100.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of the                plant.
   Biogas feed stream (1)
   Pre-treatment unit (5)
   Gaseous stream (22)
   Distillation column (26)
   Biomethane (40)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: When C1 is strictly 0.5-1                  mol%, the gaseous stream results from step (a)                  depleted of carbon dioxide used in step (b) is                  introduced into the distillation column at the                  plateau n, where the plateau n is the highest                  plateau in the column. n is 15-100 and when C1 is                  less than or equal to 0.1 mol%, the gaseous                  stream resulting from step (a) is depleted. The                  carbon dioxide used in step (b) is introduced                  into the distillation column at a level between                  the n-10 plateau and the n-5 plateau. P1 is                  greater than 50 bar absolute. The step (a)                  further comprises a step of water purification of                  the gaseous stream (8) compressed at the pressure                  P1. During step (a), the separation of the carbon                  dioxide and oxygen from the feed gas stream is                  carried out by a unit comprising at least two                  stages of separating membranes. The pressure P2                  of step (c) is greater than 40 bar abs. During                  step (b), the gaseous stream depleted of carbon                  dioxide from step (a) undergoes expansion to a                  pressure P3 of 15-40 bar abs before entering the                  distillation column. Prior to the expansion, the                  gaseous stream depleted of carbon dioxide from                  step (a) is at least partially condensed in a                  heat exchanger. The carbon dioxide-depleted gas                  stream from step (a) is at least partially                  condensed in a heat exchanger countercurrent to                  the enriched stream of 1-14C from step (c) and at                  least a portion of the nitrogen stream separated                  in step (b).
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. PN US2020080013-A1; US11261390-B2
TI Apparatus of preparing synthetic fuel using natural gas comprises liquid separator which separates condensate and oil from natural gas, desulfurizer, pre-reformer which converts higher hydrocarbons into methane, and reforming reactor.
AU MOON D J
   SHIN S
   YANG E H
   NOH Y
   HONG G
   PARK J I
   JUNG J S
AE KOREA INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (KSCT-C)
GA 202020716J
AB    NOVELTY - An apparatus of preparing synthetic fuel using natural gas, comprises a liquid separator (20) which separates greater than or equal to 5-carbon-containing ( greater than or equal to 5C) condensate and oil from natural gas supplied from a standard gas field (10); a desulfurizer (30) which removes sulfur from 1-4C gas obtained by separating greater than or equal to 5C condensate and oil from natural gas by liquid separator; a pre-reformer (40) which converts higher hydrocarbons having greater than or equal to 2 carbon atoms in desulfurized gas into methane; a reforming reactor (50) which generates synthetic gas comprising hydrogen (H2) and carbon monoxide (CO) through a Steam Carbon dioxide Reforming (SCR) reaction to reform methane and carbon dioxide into steam; a Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reactor (70) which generates synthetic fuel from synthetic gas generated by reforming reactor through Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction; and gas purification unit (80) which controls H2/CO molar ratio of synthetic gas and concentration of carbon dioxide in synthetic gas.
   USE - An apparatus of preparing synthetic fuel using natural gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus prepares synthetic fuel using natural gas of standard gas field on land or at sea, which may recover and recycle residual carbon dioxide (CO2) in synthetic gas as reaction raw material to prepare the synthetic gas and thus maximally prevent and suppress energy loss and greenhouse gas emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An apparatus of preparing synthetic fuel using natural gas, comprises a liquid separator (20) which separates greater than or equal to 5-carbon-containing ( greater than or equal to 5C) condensate and oil from natural gas supplied from a standard gas field (10); a desulfurizer (30) which removes sulfur from 1-4C gas obtained by separating greater than or equal to 5C condensate and oil from natural gas by liquid separator; a pre-reformer (40) which converts higher hydrocarbons having greater than or equal to 2 carbon atoms in desulfurized gas into methane; a reforming reactor (50) which generates synthetic gas comprising hydrogen (H2) and carbon monoxide (CO) through a Steam Carbon dioxide Reforming (SCR) reaction to reform methane and carbon dioxide into steam, after a pre-reforming process executed by pre-reformer; a Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reactor (70) which generates synthetic fuel from synthetic gas generated by reforming reactor through Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction; and gas purification unit (80) which controls H2/CO molar ratio of synthetic gas and concentration of carbon dioxide in synthetic gas, and comprises carbon dioxide (CO2) separation device which separates and removes carbon dioxide from synthetic gas and bypass unit which diverts a portion of synthetic gas so as not to pass through CO2 separation device, where CO2 separation device and bypass unit are provided in parallel between reforming reactor and Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reactor. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparing synthetic fuel using natural gas which involves executing a gas-liquid separation process of separating greater than or equal to 5C condensate and oil from the natural gas supplied from a standard gas field; executing a desulfurization process of removing sulfur from 1-4C gas obtained by separating greater than or equal to 5C condensate and oil from the gas in gas-liquid separation; executing a pre-reforming process of converting higher hydrocarbons having greater than or equal to 2 carbon atoms in desulfurized gas, obtained in the desulfurization process, into methane; executing a reforming reaction process of generating synthetic gas comprising hydrogen (H2) and carbon monoxide (CO) through a Steam Carbon dioxide Reforming (SCR) reaction to reform methane and carbon dioxide into steam, after the pre-reforming process; executing a Fischer-Tropsch synthesis process of generating the synthetic fuel from the synthetic gas, generated in the reforming reaction process, through a Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction; and executing a carbon dioxide adjustment and separation/purification process of controlling a H2/CO molar ratio of the synthetic gas supplied from reforming reaction process to Fischer-Tropsch synthesis process and concentration of carbon dioxide in synthetic gas, using a gas purification unit comprising a CO2 separation device and bypass unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the apparatus of preparing synthetic fuel using a compact gas-to-liquid (GTL) process.
   Standard gas field (10)
   Liquid separator (20)
   Desulfurizer (30)
   Pre-reformer (40)
   Reforming reactor (50)
   Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reactor (70)
   Gas purification unit (80)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Devices: In gas purification unit, the CO2 separation device transmits the synthetic gas supplied by reforming reactor through 1st supply line, selectively separates and removes carbon dioxide from transmitted synthetic gas, and then supplies the synthetic gas comprising an adjusted amount carbon dioxide to Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reactor through a second supply line; and the bypass unit comprises a bypass line branching off from 1st supply line and connected to 2nd supply line and diverts a portion of synthetic gas through the bypass line not to pass through the CO2 separation device so that the diverted portion of synthetic gas is supplied to the Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reactor through the 2nd supply line. The CO2 separation device is a pressure swing adsorption (PSA) device configured to separate and remove carbon dioxide from the synthetic gas by selectively adsorbing carbon dioxide. Adsorbent beds of PSA device have an emission line to emit carbon dioxide desorbed from adsorbent to recover carbon dioxide. Gas purification unit further comprises a flow control unit to control flow rates of parts of synthetic gas respectively distributed to the CO2 separation device and the bypass unit. The CO2 separation device of gas purification unit is a CO2 separation device executing an amine process to separate and remove carbon dioxide from the synthetic gas by selectively adsorbing carbon dioxide. The CO2 separation device of gas purification unit is a CO2 separation device selectively separating and removing carbon dioxide from synthetic gas using a membrane. The gas purification unit adjusts the H2/CO molar ratio of synthetic gas supplied from reforming reactor to Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reactor to 1.8-2.2. Preferred Process: In carbon dioxide adjustment and separation/purification process, the H2/CO molar ratio of synthetic gas supplied to Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction is adjusted to 1.8-2.2, preferably 2-2.05 by the gas purification unit. In carbon dioxide adjustment and separation/purification process, the concentration of carbon dioxide in synthetic gas supplied to the Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction is adjusted to 0.1-15 mol.%, preferably 2-12 mol.% by the gas purification unit.
DC H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

GA 2022C2030H
AB    NOVELTY - Removing residual normal hexane in tea oil,                comprises using subcritical carbon dioxide                extraction to remove residual n-hexane in the tea                oil, the subcritical carbon dioxide extraction                temperature is 24.5&#8451; and pressure                5.3MPa.
   USE - The method is useful for removing residual                normal hexane in tea oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does no need to introduce steam to                heat at high temperature for a long time, and the                active ingredients in the camellia oil can be                avoided from being damaged and lost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The subcritical carbon                  dioxide extraction, cycle extraction time 10-30                  minutes. The method comprises placing the tea oil                  leached with n-hexane into the extraction kettle,                  and sealing it with a cover, starting the                  refrigeration and heating of the device, and the                  temperature of the extraction kettle is                  24.5&#8451;, reaching the set value, the                  carbon dioxide cooled to liquid state is                  compressed by the high-pressure pump and                  preheated by the preheater, and then passed into                  the extraction kettle from the bottom of the                  extraction kettle to a predetermined pressure of                  5.3MPa, the pressure of the separation tank is                  2.5MPa, and the temperature is 24&#8451;,                  then the extraction is circulated for 10-30                  minutes, after the extraction, the pressure is                  released, the extract containing n-hexane is                  released from the separation kettle, and the                  extracted tea oil is released from the bottom of                  the extraction kettle.

PN US2019321269-A1
TI Construction of bioactive compositions of elastic niosome vesicle epidermal delivery vehicles without use of supercritical carbon dioxide and in continuously process, comprise e.g. forming solution or mixture of benevolent phospholipids.
AU RICHARD D A
AE PPP & C INC DBA ROBIN MCGRAW REVELATION (PPPC-Non-standard)
GA 201988047M
AB    NOVELTY - Construction of bioactive compositions including elastic niosome vesicle epidermal delivery vehicles without use of supercritical carbon dioxide and in a continuously operating process, comprises: forming a solution or mixture of benevolent phospholipids, where the solution is hydrophobic or hydrophilic with an aqueous phase; removing any constituent attributes of water-insolubility from the solution, while operating under conditions to preserve activity of labile biomolecules; loading the solution and desired hydrophobic bio-actives into an organic solvent residing in a pressure reactor, where the pressure reactor has been previously driven to a pre-determined working temperature; pressurizing the reactor with compressed carbon dioxide until reaching a pre-determined working pressure to produce a resulting carbon dioxide-expanded solution; and depressurizing the resulting carbon dioxide-expanded solution over an aqueous phase to form vesicular conjugates.
   USE - The method is useful for construction of bioactive compositions.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: increases stratum corneum permeability that generally not effective enough to make the stratum corneum so permeable that the barrier posed by the viable epidermis mattered; provides the viable epidermis that exhibits significant permeability barrier to both small molecules and macromolecules that becomes the rate limiting step; produces liposomes and niosomes containing hydrophobic and hydrophilic ingredients to be beneficial to the repair and rejuvenation to the stratum corneum and underlying epidermis with the ability to effect non-systemic drug absorption and transportation; is large scale production of pharmaceutical grade liposomes which are sterile of a predetermined size, and is free of organic solvents; and provides conjugation of bio-beneficial ingredients to nano carriers that exhibit protection from premature degradation, higher stability, enhanced permeability through biological membranes, higher control of the pharmacokinetics, better ingredient tissue distribution profile, and improvement of intracellular, intercellular and intra-follicular penetration and ability to control whether the nano-carrier goes systemic or non-systemic.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Construction of bioactive compositions including elastic niosome vesicle epidermal delivery vehicles without use of supercritical carbon dioxide and in a continuously operating process, comprises: forming a solution or mixture of benevolent phospholipids, where the solution is hydrophobic or hydrophilic with an aqueous phase; removing any constituent attributes of water-insolubility from the solution, while operating under conditions to preserve activity of labile biomolecules; loading the solution and desired hydrophobic bio-actives into an organic solvent residing in a pressure reactor, where the pressure reactor has been previously driven to a pre-determined working temperature; pressurizing the reactor with compressed carbon dioxide until reaching a pre-determined working pressure to produce a resulting carbon dioxide-expanded solution; and depressurizing the resulting carbon dioxide-expanded solution over an aqueous phase to form vesicular conjugates, where the resulting solution comprises water soluble cationic or non-ionic surfactants and hydrophilic or hydrophobic bio-actives; and forming nano and macro carriers as either uni-molecular or multi-molecular carriers of beneficial ingredients.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The pre-determined working temperature is less than 31.1 degrees C and the pre-determined working pressure is less than 73.8 bar. The method comprises: using a neutral lipid species comprising phosphatidylethanolamine, phosphatidylcholine, sphingomyelin or cholesterol; forming nano and macro carriers as uni-molecular carriers, having dendrimers, carbon nanotubes, polymer-conjugate drug/protein or multi-molecular carriers; forming an agent preparation, incorporated into particles having a diameter of 0.5-100 mu m, where the preparation comprises a core, the core comprises at least one member of a hydrophilic material or a hydrophobic material and at least first and second layers, each member comprising at least one member of hydrophilic material or hydrophobic material, where an interface between the core and the first layer and between adjacent layers is an interface between hydrophobic and hydrophilic material, introducing an active agent into a cell of a plant or animal, where the method further comprises preparing lipid vesicles comprising a cationic, anionic, or non-ionic lipid and containing the active agent and contacting the cell with the elastic lipid vesicles, where the active agent is taken up into the cell; introducing an active agent into a cell of a plant or animal, where the method further comprises preparing lipid vesicles comprising a cationic, anionic, or non-ionic lipid and containing the active agent, and contacting the cell with a bioactive agent in the presence of the lipid vesicles, where the bioactive agent is taken up into the cell. A final arrangement of the liposome solution or mixture is governed by the nature of the initial components and the methodology used in their preparation; forming a combination of the liposome solution or mixture, a hydrophobic or hydrophilic cosmeceutical ingredient, and an aqueous phase and a low-pressure fluid, where the combination is decompressed to separate the low pressure, critical fluid, from the phospholipid and aqueous medium, to form at least one liposome or elastic vesicle and the rate of depressurization influences the size of the liposomes formed; loading the liposome solution and desired hydrophobic bio-actives in an organic solvent into the high-pressure reactor; and forming unilamellar liposome or niosome vesicles, where the size of the liposome or niosome is determined by the rate of decompression. The nano and macro carriers enable the incorporation of cosmeceutical essential oils into carrier materials by facilitating the diffusion of the essential oil due to the swelling and opening of the pores of carrier material particles. Preferred Components: The nano and macro carriers are based on molecular self-assemblies having nanoshells, vesicles, micelles, elastic vesicles, liquid crystal phases, and Langmuir monolayers by surfactant molecules in aggregate structures having a translation aggregate, a glide aggregate and a screw aggregate. The nano and macro carriers have an effective diameter of 30-1500 nm, 150-4000 nm, 50-380 nm or 1-35 mu m. The nano and macro carriers have a polydispersity of 0.001-1, 0.01-0.9, or 0.1-0.6. The nano and macro carriers have an ingredient lipid of 25-200 wt.%, 50-100 wt.%, or 60-90 wt.%. The nano and macro carriers have residual solvent levels of 0.01-10 vol.%, 0.1-6 vol.% or 0.3-5 vol.%. At least one of the layers is an emulsion. The core comprises an emulsion of a hydrophobic material and a hydrophilic material as an oil-in-water emulsion. The core comprises an emulsion of a hydrophilic material and a hydrophobic material as a water-in-oil emulsion.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN WO2020254053-A1; EP3986836-A1; US2022219997-A1
TI New RHO-type zeolite comprising cesium and metal, used as sorbent for carbon dioxide for separation of carbon dioxide from methane or separation of carbon dioxide from inert gas e.g. nitrogen, helium and/or argon.
AU BARRIER N
   BOULLAY P
   DEBOST M
   GILSON J
   GRAND J
   MINOUX D
   MINTOVA S
   NESTERENKO N
AE TOTAL SE (PETF-C)
   TOTAL SE (PETF-C)
   TOTAL SE (PETF-C)
GA 2020C96032
AB    NOVELTY - RHO-type zeolite comprising cesium and metal (M1) is new, where M1 is sodium and/or lithium, the RHO-type zeolite has a silicon/aluminum molar ratio of 1.2-3 as determined by 29-silicon magic angle spinning nuclear magnetic resonance, the RHO-type zeolite has a specific surface area of 40-250 m2g-1 as determined by nitrogen adsorption measurements, the RHO-type zeolite is in the form of at least one nanoparticle, the nanoparticles have an average crystal size of 10-400 nm as determined by Schemer equation, and the nanoparticles form monodispersed nanocrystals or form aggregates of nanocrystals having an average size of 100-500 nm, as determined by scanning electron microscopy.
   USE - The zeolite is useful as: a sorbent for carbon dioxide for separation of carbon dioxide from methane or separation of carbon dioxide from an inert gas e.g. nitrogen, helium and/or argon (claimed); and catalyst in a chemical process e.g. conversion of methyl halides to olefins, conversion of sulfurized hydrocarbons to olefins, partial oxidation of methane, oligomerizing of alkenes, carbonylation of dimethyl ether with carbon monoxide, methylation of amines, a cracking process, a dehydrogenating process, isomerization of olefins, or reforming process.
   ADVANTAGE - The zeolite is capable of adsorbing carbon dioxide (having a diameter of 3.3 degrees A) selectively over methane (having a diameter of 3.8 degrees A).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing the zeolite; and
   (2) amorphous precursor for preparation of the zeolite, where the amorphous precursor of zeolite has a molar composition comprising 10 silica: a alumina: b M12O: c cesium oxide: d water, where a, b, c, and d are coefficients, the coefficient a is at least 0.6 to at most 1.2, the coefficient b is at least 5.3 to at most 9, the coefficient c is at least 0.25 to at most 0.7 and the coefficient d is at least 70 to at most 300.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): Preparation of the zeolite comprises (a) providing an aluminate precursors aqueous suspension, (b) providing a silicate precursors aqueous suspension, (c) adding at least one cesium precursor and at least one additional precursor having at least one sodium precursor and/or at least one lithium precursor, in the aluminate precursors aqueous suspension to form a first aqueous suspension and/or in the silicate precursors aqueous suspension to form a second aqueous suspension, (d) forming an amorphous precursor of zeolite by adding dropwise the aluminate precursors aqueous suspension into the second aqueous suspension or by adding dropwise the silicate precursors aqueous suspension into the first aqueous suspension, or by adding dropwise the first or the second aqueous suspension into the second or the first aqueous suspension, where the first aqueous suspension and the second suspension are organic structure-directing agent-free. Preferred Components: The zeolite has a silicon/aluminum molar ratio of at most 2.4, preferably 1.35-2 as determined by 29-silicon magic angle spinning nuclear magnetic resonance. The zeolite has a cesium/aluminum molar ratio of 0.1-0.5, preferably 0.19-0.38 as determined by inductively coupled plasma optical emission spectrometry. The zeolite has a pore volume of 0.06-0.4 cm3g-1, as determined by analysis of nitrogen sorption isotherms. The zeolite has an M1/aluminum molar ratio of 0.6-0.9, preferably 0.65-0.8 as determined by inductively coupled plasma optical emission spectrometry. The zeolite has an M1/cesium molar ratio of 1.5-5, preferably 2.5-4.5 as determined by inductively coupled plasma optical emission spectrometry. The M1 is sodium. The nanoparticles are nanocrystals with a hexagonal shape, as determined by transmission electron microscopy. The aggregates have an average size of 150-450 nm, preferably 275-300 nm, as determined by scanning electron microscopy. The nanoparticles have an average crystal size of at least 40 nm, preferably at least 60 nm as determined by Schemer equation. The nanoparticles have an average crystal size of at most 350 nm, preferably at most 250 nm as determined by Schemer equation. The amorphous precursor of zeolite has a pH of 12-14. The coefficient a is at least 0.8 to at most 1 and/or the coefficient b is at least 5.5 to at most 8.5. The coefficient c is at least 0.29 to at most 0.6 and/or the coefficient d is at least 80 to at most 250; preferably at most 190. The (M12O+cesium oxide)/silica ratio is at least 0.6, preferably 0.65-0.9. The ratio (M12O/water) is greater than or equal to 0.03, preferably greater than or equal to 0.07. The ratio (M12O/alumina) is greater than or equal to 7, preferably greater than or equal to 8. The ratio (cesium oxide/alumina) is less than or equal to 0.8, preferably less than or equal to 0.6. The sodium precursor comprises sodium hydroxide and/or the cesium precursor comprises cesium hydroxide and/or the aluminate precursor comprises sodium aluminate, aluminum sulfate, hydrated alumina, aluminum powder, aluminum chloride, aluminum hydroxide, kaolin clays and a mixture thereof, preferably sodium aluminate and/or the silicate precursor comprises colloidal silica, silica oxyhydroxide species, silica hydrogel, silicic acid, fumed silica, tetraalkyl orthosilicates, silica hydroxides, precipitated silica and/or clays, preferably colloidal silica. Preferred Method: The step (c) is the step of adding the cesium precursor and additional precursor in the aluminate precursors aqueous suspension to form a first aqueous suspension and the step (d) is the step of adding dropwise the silicate precursors aqueous suspension on the first aqueous suspension. The method comprises further comprises (e) mixing the amorphous precursor at 20-30 degrees C, (f) heating the amorphous precursor at 50-140 degrees C during a time of 0.5-90 hours, to form at least one crystal of zeolite, (g) optionally, recovering the crystal of zeolite. The step (e) is carried out for at least 8 hours to at most 32 hours and/or carried out in a sealed environment and/or carried out under stirring, where the stirring comprises magnetic stirring or mechanical stirring or a first type of stirring during a first period of at least 2 hours and a second type of stirring after the first period for a second period of at least 6 hours, preferably first type of stirring is magnetic stirring or mechanical stirring, the second type of stirring is orbital stirring or shaking. The step (f) is conducted for a time of at most 48 hours, preferably at most 6 hours. The step (f) is conducted at a temperature of at least 70 degrees C to at most 120 degrees C. The step (g) is performed, where the step (g) comprises the adding water, and separating the crystal of RHO-type zeolite, by filtration and/or by centrifugation and/or by dialysis and/or adding flocculating agents followed by filtration.
   EXAMPLE - First aqueous suspension comprising aluminate was prepared by mixing 516 mg sodium aluminate in 3 g of double-distilled water. This suspension was clear. Second aqueous suspension comprising silicate was prepared by mixing 5 g LUDOX A540 (RTM: Colloidal silica) with 1.82 g sodium hydroxide and 0.588 g cesium hydroxide. The reaction was a gel. After vigorous shaking by hand, the reaction turned into a clear suspension to its exothermic character. The second aqueous suspension was stirred at room temperature (25 degrees C). The first aqueous suspension was added dropwise to the second aqueous suspension. During the addition, the second aqueous suspension was maintained at room temperature while being vigorously stirred. A clear aqueous suspension was obtained. The resulting amorphous precursor in the clear aqueous suspension had molar composition of 10 silica: 0.8 alumina: 8 sodium oxide: 0.58 cesium oxide: 100 water. The pH of the clear aqueous suspension was 12, and water clear suspension was obtained. The resulting clear aqueous suspension was then aged by magnetic stirring for 14 hours at room temperature. Then, the hydrothermal crystallization was conducted during 1 hour at 100 degrees C to obtain a solid comprising nanocrystals of synthetic zeolite material RHO-1, dispersed in the mother liquor. The solid was then separated and recovered by high-speed centrifugation and several washes with hot double distilled water (heated at 100 degrees C for 30 minutes) were performed until the pH of the remaining water was 7.5 to obtain nanocrystals of zeolite material RHO-1.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)
MC E10-B04C; E10-G02E; E10-J02C1; E10-J02C3; E10-J02D; E11-E02; E11-E03; E11-F01; E11-F02B; E11-F07A; E11-G02; E11-G04; E11-G08; E11-Q02C; E12-A01; E31-N05C; E31-P02A; J01-D; J01-E02B; J01-E02D; J01-E03C1; J01-E03F; J04-E04A; J04-E04B; N06-A; N06-C09; N06-E01; N07-C02; N07-C03; N07-D01; N07-D02B; N07-D08A; N07-E02; N07-F02; N07-F03; N07-F11; N07-L01E; S03-E06B1

PN US2019010118-A1; US10633332-B2
TI Removing oxyanion from aqueous source containing            oxyanion comprises dissolving oxyanion precipitating            compound into aqueous source to result in precipitation            of oxyanion salt of oxyanion precipitating compound,            and removing oxyanion salt.
AU CUSTELCEAN R
   SEIPP C A
   WILLIAMS N J
AE UT-BATTELLE LLC (UTBA-C)
GA 2019034878
AB    NOVELTY - Removing (M1) an oxyanion from an aqueous                source containing the oxyanion, comprises: (a)                dissolving an oxyanion precipitating compound (Ia)                into the aqueous source to result in precipitation                of an oxyanion salt of the oxyanion precipitating                compound; and (b) removing the oxyanion salt from                the water containing the oxyanion to result in                water reduced in concentration of the                oxyanion.
   USE - The methods are useful for removing oxyanion                from aqueous source containing the oxyanion, and                removing carbon dioxide from a gaseous source (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Removing (M1) an oxyanion from an aqueous                source containing the oxyanion, comprises: (a)                dissolving an oxyanion precipitating compound of                formula (Ia) into the aqueous source to result in                precipitation of an oxyanion salt of the oxyanion                precipitating compound; and (b) removing the                oxyanion salt from the water containing the                oxyanion to result in water reduced in                concentration of the oxyanion.
   A = ring-containing moiety, where at least one                of H in (Ia) may be replaced with at least one                methyl;
   X(m-) = anionic species with a magnitude of                charge m; and
   m, n = at least 1.
   Provided that, where: X(m-) is an anionic                species exchangeable with the oxyanion in the                aqueous source before the oxyanion precipitating                compound contacts the oxyanion in step (a), and                X(m-) is the oxyanion in the oxyanion salt of the                oxyanion precipitating compound; and nx m is 2. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for removing                (M2) carbon dioxide from a gaseous source,                comprising (a1) contacting the gaseous source with                an aqueous solution containing a carbon dioxide                complexing compound of formula (Ib) to result in                precipitation of a carbonate or bicarbonate salt of                the carbon dioxide complexing compound, and (b1)                removing the carbonate or bicarbonate salt from the                aqueous solution.
   A1 = A;
   X(m1-) = anionic species with a magnitude of                charge m1, where X(m1-) is hydroxide in the carbon                dioxide complexing compound before the carbon                dioxide complexing compound is contacted with                carbon dioxide in step (a1), and X(m-) is carbonate                or bicarbonate in the carbonate or bicarbonate                salt; and
   n1, m1 = n.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxyanion includes                  sulfate (preferred), nitrate, chromate, selenate,                  phosphate, arsenate, carbonate, bicarbonate or                  perchlorate.
   Preferred Definitions:
   A = monocyclic ring (including 5-7 membered                ring). unsaturated carbocyclic ring or ring system,                benzene ring, unsaturated heterocyclic ring or ring                system (containing at least one nitrogen ring atom)                or pyridine ring.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN107774114-A; CN107774114-B
TI Capturing carbon dioxide by using phosphogypsum            decomposition slag comprises e.g. pretreating            phosphogypsum and coal respectively for air-drying,            crushing, screening and grinding to obtain            phosphogypsum powder and pulverizing coal.
AU MA L
   ZHAO S
   YANG J
   WANG D
   PENG Y
   XIANG H
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
   UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 201821022U
AB    NOVELTY - Capturing carbon dioxide by using                phosphogypsum decomposition slag comprises e.g.                pretreating phosphogypsum and coal respectively for                air-drying, crushing, screening and grinding to                obtain phosphogypsum powder and pulverizing coal,                uniformly mixing pre-treated phosphogypsum powder                and pulverized coal in a high-temperature tube                furnace, dephosphorizing, calcining in a nitrogen                atmosphere to obtain phosphogypsum decomposition                slag, deoxidizing and calcining in room temperature                and placing phosphogypsum decomposition slag and                ultrapure water in a three-phase fluidized                bed.
   USE - The method is useful for capturing carbon                dioxide by using phosphogypsum decomposition                slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and easy to implement,                enhances the utilization of phosphorus gypsum to                capture the greenhouse gas carbon dioxide and is                environment-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Capturing carbon dioxide by using                phosphogypsum decomposition slag comprises (i)                pretreating phosphogypsum and coal respectively for                air-drying, crushing, screening and grinding to                obtain phosphogypsum powder and pulverizing coal                with a particle size of 100-120 meshes, placing at                100-120 degrees C, heating and drying for 1-3                hours; (ii) uniformly mixing pre-treated                phosphogypsum powder and pulverized coal in a                high-temperature tube furnace, dephosphorizing and                calcining in a nitrogen atmosphere to obtain                phosphogypsum decomposition slag, where the mass                ratio of phosphogypsum powder and pulverized coal                is 4:1, nitrogen flow rate is 50-100 ml/minute,                deoxidizing and calcining at a heating rate of 5-10                degrees C/minute in room temperature to 900-1000                degrees C and maintaining for 0.5-1.5 hours,                heating at 1100-1200 degrees C at a rate of 5-10                degrees C/minutes and insulating for 5-1.5 hours;                (iii) placing phosphogypsum decomposition slag and                ultrapure water obtained in step (ii) in a                three-phase fluidized bed and into the                non-oxidizing gas mixture, carrying out gas carbon                dioxide capture reaction and phosphogypsum                decomposition slag mineralization reaction to                obtain solid calcium carbonate and hydrogen sulfide                mixed gas, where the volume fraction of carbon                dioxide in non-oxidizing gas mixture not greater                than 20%; and (iv) transferring hydrogen                sulfide-containing mixed gas obtained in step (iii)                into the high-temperature tube furnace in step                (ii), and taking hydrogen sulfide as a reducing                agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the                  solid-liquid ratio of phosphogypsum-decomposing                  slag and ultrapure water is 1:(6-16) (g:ml). In                  the step (ii), the ventilation volume of the                  mixed gas containing hydrogen sulfide is a molar                  ratio of hydrogen sulfide in the mixed gas                  containing hydrogen sulfide to caclcium sulfate                  (CaSO4) in phosphogypsum is (0.8-2):3.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN IN202141016852-A; WO2022214945-A1
TI System for generating mechanical power using super            critical carbon dioxide (SCO2), has heat exchanger that            is configured to heat super critical carbon dioxide            received from outlet port of compression cylinder and            supply to inlet port of expansion cylinder.
AU KUMAR P
   KARMARKAR S
AE INDIAN INST SCI (INDF-C)
GA 2022938742
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an expansion cylinder (5) that                defines a first internal volume, and the expansion                cylinder houses a first piston (5a) connected to a                crankshaft (7) through a first connecting rod (5c).                A first heat exchanger (C) is connected to the                inlet port (6i) of the compression cylinder (6) and                the outlet port (6o) of the expansion cylinder. A                second heat exchanger (H) is connected to the                outlet port of the compression cylinder and the                inlet port (5i) of the expansion cylinder. The                first heat exchanger is configured to cool the                carbon dioxide received from the outlet port (5o)                of the expansion cylinder. The second heat                exchanger is configured to heat the super critical                carbon dioxide received from the outlet port of the                compression cylinder and supply to the inlet port                of the expansion cylinder and the high temperature                and high-pressure CO2 drives the first piston                housed inside the expansion cylinder downwards to                generate mechanical energy in the expansion                cylinder.
   USE - System for generating mechanical power using                super critical carbon dioxide (SCO2) used in steam                power plants for electricity generation.
   ADVANTAGE - The system has high thermal efficiencies in                excess of 30% even at a lower source temperature.                The system is compact, and has single-phase                operation, and is attractive to a variety of heat                sources.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                of assembling a system for generating mechanical                power using super critical carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of the                system for generating mechanical power.

   Outlet port (6o)

TI Ultra-critical carbon dioxide Brayton cycle pre-cooling system for use in photo-thermal field, has high-temperature section pre-cooler whose cold outlet is connected to water cooling device through low-temperature medium outlet pipeline.

A hot side inlet of the high temperature section pre-cooler is connected with a high temperature medium inlet pipeline (1). A hot side outlet of the low temperature section pre-cooler is connected with a target temperature medium pipeline (5). A cold side outlet of the high-temperature section pre-cooler is connected to a circulating water cooling device through a low-temperature medium outlet pipeline (15). First and second boosting devices (6,12) and a total boosting device are respectively provided with first and second fans and a main fan.

PT P
PN DE102019119328-A1; EP3693344-A1
TI Method of controlling ambient air supply to aeration tank of wastewater treatment plant, involves increasing supplied amount of ambient air, if proportion falls between referred difference and changed proportionate of carbon dioxide content.
AU HALLAS A
   HALLAS E
AE HALLAS A (HALL-Individual)
   HALLAS A (HALL-Individual)
GA 201982090S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves arranging a collecting device (9) on the aeration tank (6) at a predefined point of the surface of the aeration tank from which the gas leaving the aeration tank is captured, and analyzing the discharged gas, according to the oxygen content and the carbon dioxide content at the surface of the aeration tank. The difference between the sum of oxygen content and the carbon dioxide content between the ambient air supplied and the sum of oxygen content and the carbon dioxide content of gas collected at the surface of aeration tank is determined. The quantity of ambient air supplied is reduced in preset increments, as long as the proportion between the referred difference and the proportionate carbon dioxide content remains the same. If the proportion falls between the referred difference and the changed proportionate of carbon dioxide content, the amount of ambient air supplied by the reduced air volume is increased.
   USE - Method for controlling supply of ambient air to aeration tank of wastewater treatment plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The supply of ambient air into the activated sludge tank is regulated on the basis of exiting gases on the surface of aeration tank and the necessary for the debris removal process supply of ambient air is reduced, so that considerable energy saving in the operation of wastewater treatment plant is achieved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for controlling supply of ambient air to aeration tank of wastewater treatment plant.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of the device for controlling supply of ambient air to aeration tank of wastewater treatment plant.
   Wastewater supply (1)
   Feed tank (2)
   Segregation device (3)
   Aeration tank (6)
   Collecting device (9)
