PT P
PN WO2018215943-A1
TI Mixed metal oxide catalyst used to produce methanol from carbon dioxide source, comprises copper, zinc and zirconium.
AU MAMEDOV A
   SHAIKH S
   REA C
   ZHANG X
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 201894880E
AB    NOVELTY - Mixed metal oxide catalyst comprises copper, zinc and zirconium, where the catalyst comprises a catalytic precursor precipitated from an aqueous solution comprising cesium nitrate and sodium carbonate, the cesium nitrate and sodium carbonate establish a buffer medium that facilitates homogeneous distribution of copper, zinc and zirconium components in the precipitate, and the formation of a stable, active mass of the catalyst oxide components, and the catalyst has a catalytic activity for production of methanol from a carbon dioxide source that is greater than a catalyst comprising a catalytic precursor precipitated from an aqueous solution in the absence of cesium nitrate.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for producing methanol from a carbon dioxide source (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst exhibits significantly higher methanol selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) making (M1) the mixed metal oxide catalyst in a content of at least 1 kg for producing methanol from a carbon dioxide source, comprising obtaining an aqueous solution comprising a copper salt, a zinc salt, a zirconium salt and a cesium salt, precipitating a catalytic precursor with an alkaline salt, separating at least part of the catalytic precursor from the aqueous mixture, and calcining the catalytic precursor to form the mixed metal oxide catalyst in a content of at least 1 kg; and
   (2) producing (M2) methanol from a carbon dioxide source, comprising obtaining a mixed metal oxide catalyst comprising copper, zinc and zirconium, where the catalyst comprises catalytic material precipitated from an aqueous solution comprising cesium nitrate, and exposing a carbon dioxide source to the mixed metal oxide catalyst under conditions to produce methanol, where the catalyst has a catalytic activity that is greater than a catalyst comprising a catalytic precursor precipitated from an aqueous solution in the absence of cesium nitrate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The copper, zinc and zirconium comprise 30-70 wt.% copper oxide, 15-40 wt.% zinc oxide, and 10-40 wt.% zirconium oxide. In the method (M1): the copper salt is copper nitrate, the zinc salt is zinc nitrate, the zirconium salt is zirconium oxynitrate, and the cesium salt is cesium nitrate; the mixed metal oxide catalyst comprises less than a catalytic amount of cesium; the alkaline salt is sodium carbonate; and the cesium salt and the sodium carbonate establish a buffer medium that facilitates homogeneous distribution of copper, zinc and zirconium components in the precipitate. In the method (M2): the carbon dioxide source comprises syngas; the catalyst comprises 30-70 wt.% copper oxide, 15-40 wt.% zinc oxide, and 10-40 wt.% zirconium oxide; the catalytic activity comprises a methanol selectivity of greater than 20%; the catalytic activity comprises a methanol selectivity of greater than 30% after 100 days of time of stream; the catalytic activity comprises a methanol selectivity that increases of 50-150 days; the catalytic activity comprises a carbon dioxide conversion to methanol of greater than 12%; and the catalytic activity comprises a carbon monoxide selectivity of less than 85%. Preferred Method: The method (M1) further comprises heating the aqueous solution at a temperature of at least 80 degrees C before precipitating a catalytic precursor with an alkaline salt.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN113684134-A
TI Preserving algae living body during field sampling involves collecting sample of water body including the living algae, determining initial pH value of water sample, passing carbon dioxide into water sample, adjusting pH value to initial pH value and preserving living algae.
AU PANG W
   WANG Q
   HU Y
   LI T
   PENG J
AE UNIV SHANGHAI NORMAL (USNO-C)
GA 2021E12360
AB    NOVELTY - Preserving algae living body during field sampling involves collecting a sample of the water body including the living algae, determining the initial pH value of the water sample, passing carbon dioxide into the water sample, adjusting the pH value to the initial pH value and preserving living algae.
   USE - Method for preserving algae living body during field sampling.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to preserve algae living body during field sampling, which quickly and efficiently kills the zooplankton in the water samples without affecting the algae vitality, and improves the quality and quantity of the algae species collected from the wild on the way back to the laboratory, has simple operation method, low requirements on equipment, and can adapt to compact field sampling work to meet the needs of researchers who collect algal species from the field, the species is basically the same as the initial situation, and are brought back to the laboratory in high quantities for separation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device, which comprises an interconnected carbon dioxide storage device and a sampling bottle for collecting water samples of the living algae, carbon dioxide storage device is used to store the carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The algae include one or more of green algae, diatoms, cyanobacteria, cryptoalgae, and Euglena. The collected water samples are dispensed into sampling bottles. The inflow flow of the carbon dioxide is 30mL/min, and the inflow time is 7min based on each 150ml of the water sample. After the carbon dioxide is passed into the water body sample, it is left for 30min to adjust the pH value, based on each 150ml of the water body sample. The sampling bottle is filled with the stock solution, which is obtained by filtering the water sample through 0.2 mum. The sampling bottle is replenished with stock solution after pH adjustment. During the preservation of the living algae, the sampling bottle is shaken 2-5 times a day to ensure that the living algae and the supernatant are mixed. The device further includes a flowmeter, and both ends of the flowmeter are respectively connected to the carbon dioxide storage device and the sampling bottle, for detecting the incoming flow of carbon dioxide.

PN CN111097403-A
TI High-valent-metal-ion-doped titanium-based nanomaterial for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide for preparing acetaldehyde, is titanium dioxide based nanomaterial doped with high-valent metal ions.
AU LI Q
   YANG W
   QIAN X
AE INST METAL RES CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAMA-C)
GA 202040431K
AB    NOVELTY - A high-valent-metal-ion-doped titanium-based nanomaterial is a titanium dioxide based nanomaterial doped with high-valent metal ions.
   USE - High-valent-metal-ion-doped titanium-based nanomaterial is used for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide for preparing acetaldehyde (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nanomaterial is doped with high-valent metal ions, which gives the surface the ability to activate carbon dioxide and enrich photogenerated electrons, and improve the efficiency of photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide and product selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of the nanomaterial, which involves smelting and mixing pure metal raw material of the high-valent metal ion and metallic titanium 3 or more times at 2000-4000 degrees C to obtain an alloy ingot, heating the alloy ingot to 1000-1500 degrees C at 5-10 degrees C/minute, carrying out homogenization annealing, heat-preserving for 3-10 hours, carrying out homogenization annealing after direct quenching to room temperature to obtain a single-phase alloy, carrying out oxide etching of the single-phase alloy to obtain an amorphous titanium-based nanomaterial, and crystallizing the nanomaterial by heating to 400-550 degrees C at 2 degrees C/minute and heat-preserving for 2-6 hours; and
   (2) use of the nanomaterial for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide for preparing acetaldehyde.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The high-valent metal is niobium, tantalum, vanadium, tungsten and/or molybdenum, and is doped in the titanium dioxide lattice at the substituted position of titanium ion. The nanomaterial contains 0.1-10 atomic% high-valent metal ion. The crystal phase of the nanomaterial is anatase titanium ore phase. Preferred Property: The nanomaterial has a size of less than 1 microns. Preferred Composition: The etching liquid is 0.1-2 wt.% ammonium fluoride solution containing ammonium fluoride, and a mixed solvent of ethylene glycol and water in a weight ratio of 99:1. Preferred Method: Carbon dioxide reduction involves placing an aqueous solution containing carbon dioxide under non-illumination condition for 30 minutes for 1-24 hours, to reduce carbon dioxide to acetaldehyde. The usage amount of nanomaterial is greater than 1 g/L. The conversion selectivity of acetaldehyde is more than 99%.

TI Emulsion spray-drying method for preparing free flowing spray-dried fat powder used as e.g. creamer, involves mixing spray-dried fat powder with solid carbon dioxide by injecting controlled amounts of solid carbon dioxide through fat powder.
AU HIEMSTRA M
   VAN KONINGSVELD G A
AE DP SUPPLY BV (DPSU-Non-standard)
GA 201942241G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves mixing water, emulsifiers, fats and optional constituents to form an emulsion, and performing spray-drying of the emulsion to generate a flow of droplets and drying the droplets with hot air to form a spray-dried fat powder. The spray-dried fat powder is collected and separated from the hot air. The spray-dried fat powder is mixed with solid carbon dioxide and the spray-dried fat powder is cooled to below the glass transition temperature of the powder, and preferably below the melting/crystallization temperature of the employed fat. The mixing of the spray-dried fat powder is performed by injecting controlled amounts of solid carbon dioxide homogeneously through the spray-dried fat powder with the use of 2 or more carbon dioxide inlets during transport of the powder.
   USE - Emulsion spray-drying method for preparing a free flowing spray-dried fat powder used as food product e.g. fat powder, creamer or whitener, milk powder, protein powder, colorant, antibiotics and blood products, and/or medicament.

PN CN108774099-A
TI Producing liquid methane by utilization of exhaust gas carbon dioxide e.g. comprises under the catalysis of carbon dioxide and hydrogen (nickel, reacting methane, separating, purifying, and deeply-cooling to form liquid methane.
AU YANG Q
   YANG X
   SHI Z
   LI S
   LIU P
   XIE F
   HAN H
AE HENAN GUANGSHUO CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 2018910119
AB    NOVELTY - Producing liquid methane by utilization of exhaust gas carbon dioxidecomprises under the catalysis of carbon dioxide (CO2) and hydrogen (H2) nickel, reacting methane, separating, purifying, and deeply-cooling to form liquid methane (LNG), where the specific method is: the mass purity of 3.8-4.2 MPa is 90% of CO2 and pressure of 3.4-3.6 MPa is 98% of H2 at mass ratio of 1:3 at 350-370 degrees C, catalyzing by nickel to form methane and water, cooling to 190-210 degrees C, sending to waste heat boiler, cooling to 120 degrees C, feeding into cooler, cooling to below 40 degrees C, feeding into water separator, adsorbing by membrane separator, separating H2 by membrane separator and returning to heat exchanger, realizing comprehensive recycling of hydrogen, sending methane separating H2 to cold box, under the protection of liquid nitrogen, chilling at - 170 degrees C, carrying out liquefaction into liquid methane (LNG), sending into storage tank, where the nitrogen (N2) retracts compression, and recycling.
   USE - The method is useful for producing liquid methane by utilization of exhaust gas carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes waste gas, saves energy, protects the environment, has low production cost and good product quality, and is a major progress in liquid methane production, and is economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method, mixing a mass ratio of 3.8-4.2 MPa with a purity of 90% CO2 and a pressure of 3.4-3.6 MPa with a mass concentration of 98% H2 at a mass ratio of 1:3, sending to the heat exchanger 1 via the node A (pipe, the same below), preheating to 255-265 degrees C, feeding into the electric heater 2 via the node B, preheating to 275-285 degrees C, then the reaction vessel 3 containing the nickel catalyst is fed through the node C at 350-370 degrees C, reacting CO2 and H2 to form methane and water, where the weight of methane is 45-50% water, unreacting hydrogen to 5%, then the reactor 3 is taken out and sent back to the heat exchanger 1 via the node D, cool down to 190-210 degrees C, then sent to the waste heat boiler 4 via the node E, cooling down to below 120 degrees C with water, passing to the cooler 5 (condenser) via node F, cooling down to less than 40 degrees C, and sent to the water separator 6 via the node G, the water is separated, discharged and collected, and sending the remaining methane and H2 to the membrane separator 7 through the node H for adsorption, separating methane and H2, and separating the H2 by membrane separator and returning to the exchange, where the heat exchanger 1 realizes the comprehensive recycling of hydrogen, and the methane is separated from H2 and sent to the cold box 8 through the node I, and under the protection of liquid nitrogen cryogenically cooling at - 170 degrees C, liquefying into liquid methane (LNG), and sending through the node J, placing the product in storage tank, and retracting the N2 and recycling. The method comprises mixing 245 MPa of 15 kg CO2 with a mass purity of 90% and 45 kg H2 with a mass concentration of 98% at a pressure of 3.4 MPa, sending to the heat exchanger 1 via the node A (pipe, the same below), preheating to 255 degrees C, feeding into the electric heater 2 via the node B, preheating to 275 degrees C, then the reaction vessel 3 containing the nickel catalyst is fed through the node C at 350 degrees C, reacting CO2 and H2 to form methane and water, where the weight of methane is 30 kg, the water is 27 kg, the unreacted hydrogen is 3 kg, and then the reactor 3 is sent back to the heat exchanger 1 through the node D, and cooling to 190 degrees C, and then passing the node E to the waste heat boiler 4, cooling to 120 degrees C with water, sending to the cooler 5 (condenser) via the node F, cooling to 40 degrees C, and sending to the water separator 6 via the node G, separating water, discharging and collecting, and sending the remaining methane and H2 to the membrane separator 7 through the node H for adsorption, separating the methane and the H2, and separating the H2 by the membrane separator and returning to the heat exchanger 1 to realize the comprehensive recycling of hydrogen, separating the methane from H2 and sending to the cold box 8 through the node I under the protection of liquid nitrogen, cryogenically cooling at - 170 degrees C, liquefying into liquid methane (LNG), sending to the storage tank through the node J, retracting N2 compression and recycling.

AU ASEFA T
   ZHANG T
   MIKMEKOVA E M
   TYRYSHKIN A M
AE UNIV RUTGERS STATE NEW JERSEY (RUTF-C)
   INST SCI INSTR CZECH ACAD SCI VVI (SCIN-Non-standard)
GA 2021D06587
AB    NOVELTY - A material comprises titanium oxide where the material has a light absorption onset of 400-510 nm.
   USE - A material used for reducing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material has improved optical absorption properties and long-lived charge separation capability, and is proficient in photo-catalyzing various reactions. The photocatalytic activity of the material is better than other notable photocatalytic materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A material comprises titanium oxide of formula TiO2-x, where the material has a light absorption onset of 400-510 nm.
   x=0.001-0.1.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) making a titanium oxide particle which involves contacting a titanium-containing compound with greater than or equal to 1 polymer-derived mesoporous carbon (PDMC) material; and heating the titanium-containing compound and the PDMC at 500-1200 degrees C in an inert atmosphere to form titanium oxide particles; and
   (2) reducing carbon dioxide which involves contacting a gas comprising carbon dioxide (CO2) with the material in the presence of light; and reducing CO2 to provide methane (CH4).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The TiO2-x is in an anatase phase. The material does not aggregate with equivalent particles. The material comprises greater than or equal to 1 of optically-active mid-gap states related to oxygen vacancies or interstitial Ti(III) species. The interstitial Ti(III) species comprises under-coordinated oxygen ion groups. Preferred Parameters: The material has an average pore size of 2-12 nm. The material has an average pore size of 12-40 nm. The material has a Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) surface area of 70-110 m2/g. The material has a pore volume of 0.1-0.3 cm3/g. The titanium oxide material has a light absorption onset of 400-510 nm. The methane production rate is 16-35 mu mol/hour. The light comprises UV radiation. Preferred Process: Heating step comprises forming TiO2-x nanoparticles. The method further comprises calcining TiO2-x nanoparticles and PDMC in air. Calcining is at a temperature of 450-650 degrees C. Calcining removes the PDMC and provides a titanium oxide material.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The titanium-containing compound comprises titanium glycolate. The PDMC material comprises polymerized aniline.

PN CN113651727-A
TI Preparing dicyandiamide by mixing ammonia and carbon dioxide, comprises e.g. reacting preheated ammonia with gas from trap in reactor, filtering cyanamide gas, obtaining dicyandiamide gas by polymerization, and performing gas-solid separation.
AU LI G
   KONG D
   QIN Y
   LEI L
   SONG G
   TANG Y
AE SICHUAN GOLDEN-ELEPHANT SINCERITY CHEM (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2021D8991J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing dicyandiamide by mixing ammonia and carbon dioxide, comprises reacting the preheated ammonia with the gas from the trap in the reactor to obtain the cyanamide gas, and filtering the cyanamide gas, obtaining dicyandiamide gas by polymerization in a polymerization reactor, filtering dicyandiamide gas, entering the crystallizer with carbon dioxide as the process cold air, cooling and crystallizing, and the gas brings the dicyandiamide crystals into the trap to complete the gas-solid separation, and collecting the dicyandiamide solid, returning part of the gas separated by the trap to the reactor, and reusing the other part as process tail gas treatment.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing dicyandiamide by mixing ammonia and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures high product quality and high purity, realizes simple process flow, green environmental protection, high raw material utilization rate, steam by-product, low energy consumption, and cost saving.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The method comprises reacting the preheated carbon dioxide gas with the gas from the trap to obtain cyanamide gas, filtering the cyanamide gas, polymerizing in a polymerization reactor to obtain dicyandiamide gas, filtering the dicyandiamide gas, cooling and crystallizing in a crystallizer using ammonia as the process cold air, where the gas brings the dicyandiamide crystals into the trap to complete the gas-solid separation, and collecting the dicyandiamide solid, a part of the gas separated by the trap is returned to the reactor, and the other part is used as process tail gas for treatment and reuse. The temperature after ammonia preheating and the temperature after carbon dioxide preheating are both 0-140degrees Celsius, preferably 25-80degrees Celsius. The polymerization temperature in the polymerization reactor is 200-400degrees Celsius. The temperature of the reactor is controlled at 650-900degrees Celsius. The temperature of the process cold air carbon dioxide is 0-100degrees Celsius, preferably 25-60degrees Celsius.Preferred Components: The mass ratio of ammonia to carbon dioxide is 1:1-1.8. The production system used in the method for preparing dicyandiamide by mixing ammonia and carbon dioxide, and the system includes a preheater, a reactor, a filter one, a polymerization reactor, a filter two, a crystallizer and a trap. The preheater is connected to the reactor, filter one, polymerization reactor, filter two, crystallizer and trap in sequence through pipelines. The trap is connected to the reactor through the first pipeline and the compressor, and the trap is connected to the tail gas absorption device through the second pipeline. The polymerization reactor is respectively provided with a water inlet and a steam outlet; Impurity discharge ports are provided on both the filter one and the filter two; A process cold air inlet is provided on the crystallizer. The reactor is a fluidized bed reactor or a fixed bed reactor, the reactor is connected to the flue gas system through a pipe, and the high temperature flue gas is used to provide heat. The heat in the polymerization reaction in the polymerization reactor can be absorbed by water, and the water becomes steam to transfer the heat.

PT P
PN WO2021226172-A1; TW202200265-A
TI Catalyst useful for converting hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide into other materials comprises molybdenum, first element including group metal, second element including e.g. sulfur, carbon, and oxygen and optionally least one group IA metals.
AU SHEEHAN S W
   CHEN C
AE AIR HOLDINGS CO INC (AIRH-Non-standard)
GA 2021C5902J
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises: 10-50 wt.% molybdenum; at least one first element including a group V, VI, VII, VIII, IX, X, or XI metal (e.g., silver, cobalt, nickel, copper, rhodium, ruthenium, iridium, palladium, niobium, and manganese); at least one second element including sulfur, carbon, oxygen, phosphorus, nitrogen, and selenium; and optionally, at least one group IA metals.
   USE - The catalyst are useful for converting hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide into other materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) catalytic composition, comprising the catalyst, and a support;
   (2) preparing (M1) the catalyst comprising coprecipitation, wet impregnation, or ball milling; and
   (3) hydrogenating (M2) carbon dioxide, comprising contacting the catalyst with a feed mixture comprising carbon dioxide and a reductant gas at a reduction temperature and a reduction pressure, thus providing a liquid product mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises: the first element at a molar ratio of 0.15-2, relative to the molybdenum; cobalt at a molar ratio of 0.15-2, preferably 0.29 relative to the molybdenum; nickel at a molar ratio of 0.15-2, preferably 0.36 relative to the molybdenum; and silver at a molar ratio of 0.15-2, preferably 1 relative to the molybdenum. The group IA metals comprise potassium, sodium, or cesium. The catalyst comprises: group IA metals at a molar ratio of 0.1-0.5 relative to molybdenum; and group IA metals to molybdenum at a molar ratio of 0.44 relative to molybdenum. The catalyst comprises: second element at a molar ratio of 0.3-3.25, preferably 2.5-3.25 relative to molybdenum; and sulfur at a molar ratio of 3.25 relative to molybdenum. The catalyst comprises molybdenum, silver at a molar ratio of 1 relative to the molybdenum, sulfur at a molar ratio of 3 relative to the molybdenum, and the group IA at a molar ratio of 0.4 relative to the molybdenum. The catalyst catalyst comprises, molybdenum, nickel at a molar ratio of 0.36 relative to the molybdenum, cobalt at a molar ratio of 0.29 relative to the molybdenum ,sulfur at a molar ratio of 3.25 relative to the molybdenum, and the group IA at a molar ratio of 0.44 relative to the molybdenum. The catalyst comprises niobium at a molar ratio of 0.12 relative to the molybdenum, cobalt at a molar ratio of 0.6 relative to the molybdenum, sulfur at a molar ratio of 3.25 relative to the molybdenum, and the group IA at a molar ratio of 0.4 relative to the molybdenum. The support comprises: at least one material including an oxide, nitride, fluoride, or silicate of an element from aluminum, silicon, titanium, zirconium, cerium, magnesium, yttrium, lanthanum, zinc, or tin; gamma -alumina; and carbon-based material including activated carbon, carbon nanotubes, graphene, or graphene oxide. The support is a mesoporous material. The support has: a mesopore volume of 0.01-3.0 cc/g; and surface area of 10-1000 m2/g. The reductant gas is hydrogen or hydrocarbon, e.g. methane, ethane, propane, or butane. The reductant gas is, or is derived from, flare gas, waste gas, or natural gas. The reduction temperature is 100-600 degrees C. The reduction pressure is 500-3000 psi . The molar ratio of reductant gas:carbon dioxide in the feed mixture is 10:1-1:10, preferably 5:1-0.5:1. The liquid product mixture comprises methanol, at least 10 wt.% ethanol, or n-propanol. The molar ratio of ethanol to the total amount of methanol and n-propanol in the liquid product mixture is 1:5-1:10. The amount of formic acid in the liquid product mixture is less than 10 ppm. The amount of isopropanol in the liquid product mixture is less than 10 ppm. The carburization gas mixture comprises methane and hydrogen or carbon monoxide and hydrogen. Preferred Composition: The catalytic composition: comprises 5-70 wt.% catalyst; and is in a form of particles having an average size of 20 nm to 5 mu m, preferably 50-300 nm. Preferred Method: The method (M1) comprises: (i) providing a first solution comprising a source of the at least one second element, and combining the first solution with a molybdenum source, thus providing a first reaction mixture, heating the first reaction mixture to a first temperature for a first period of time, providing a second solution comprising an acid, and adding a support to the second solution, thus providing a first suspension, heating the first suspension to a second temperature for a second period of time, providing a third solution comprising a source of at least one first element, and adding the first reaction mixture and the third solution to the first suspension, thus providing the second reaction mixture, heating the second reaction mixture to a third temperature for a third period of time, and isolating a solid material from the second reaction mixture; (ii) providing a first solution comprising a molybdenum source, a source of the at least one first element and a source of the at least one second elements in water, and adding a support to thus provide a first suspension, heating the first suspension to a first temperature for a first period of time, and isolating a solid material from the first suspension; and (iii) mixing a molybdenum source and a support in a mill jar to provide a first mixture, ball milling the first mixture for 2 hours to 2 weeks, thus provide a first precipitate, filtering the first precipitate and heating to a first temperature to provide a ball milled molybdenum source, mixing the ball milled molybdenum source with a source of the at least one first elements and a source of the at least one second elements to provide a second mixture, and isolating a solid material from the second mixture. In method (M1): the second element comprise carbon, comprises providing an oxide catalyst precursor, carburizing the oxide catalyst precursor with a carburization gas mixture at a carburization temperature for a carburization period of time; and providing the oxide catalyst precursor comprises providing a mixture comprising a source of the at least one first elements, a molybdenum source, and an acid, combining the mixture with a slurry comprising a support and water, thus providing a first suspension, heating the first suspension to a first temperature for a first period of time, isolating a solid material from the first suspension, and heating the solid material at a second temperature for a second period of time, thus providing an oxide. The method (M1), comprises: providing a mixture comprising a source of the at least one first elements, a molybdenum source, and an acid; combining the mixture with a slurry comprising a support and water, thus providing a first suspension; heating the first suspension to a first temperature for a first period of time; isolating a solid material from the first suspension; and heating the solid material at a second temperature for a second period of time. The method (M1), further comprises combining the solid material with a source of the at least one group IA metals. The method (M2): comprises contacting the catalyst with the feed mixture for 24 hours, preferably 168 hours; and further comprises reacting the catalyst or catalytic composition with the reductant gas before reacting with the feed mixture.
   EXAMPLE - 85.5 mg Ammonium heptamolybdate was added to an 20 wt.% aqueous ammonium sulfide solution and the mixture was heated at 60 degrees C for 1 hour to form a molybdenum solution. 30 g Cobalt acetate and 30 g nickel acetate were dissolved in 1.1 l deionized water to form a metal solution. 675 ml Glacial acetic acid was diluted with 1.5 l deionized water to form an acetic acid solution, to which high surface area 29.3 g gamma alumina was added to form an acidic alumina slurry, and heated to 50 degrees C. The metal solution and molybdenum solution were added simultaneously to the acidic alumina slurry, which formed a black precipitate. The resulting mixture was heated at 60 degrees C for 1 hour and then cooled down to room temperature. The solid was filtered and dried in a fume bonnet for 2 days to form a highly viscous and moist catalyst paste. 12.6 g Solid potassium carbonate was added to the paste and mixed well with pestle and mortar. The catalyst was dried in oven at 125 degrees C for 3 hours and calcined at 500 degrees C for 1 hour with argon flushing during the entire process, and the resulting catalyst was ground to a fine powder with a mortar and pestle.

AU WU J
AE SUZHOU SIYIWEI AUTOMOBILE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2021A6251B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves reducing pressure in a closed forming device as a first pressure value. Constant current charging and discharging circulation is performed between charging cut-off voltage and discharging cut-off voltage. The constant current charging and discharging circulation is performed between the charging cut-off voltage and the discharging cut-off voltage. Carbon dioxide is filled to normal pressure in the closed forming device. The carbon dioxide is heated to 50-60 degrees C. Sealing process is performed to obtain a secondary battery.
   USE - Method for forming a secondary battery (claimed).

PN US2021261451-A1; WO2021168433-A1
TI Storing carbon used in oil and gas production operations, involves infusing fluid stream with carbon dioxide in form of nanobubbles, and storing infused fluid stream in storage tank, fluid treatment pit, or injection well.
AU PATTON M
AE HYDROZONIX LLC (HYDR-Non-standard)
   HYDROZONIX LLC (HYDR-Non-standard)
GA 2021987248
AB    NOVELTY - Storing carbon involves infusing a fluid stream with carbon dioxide in the form of nanobubbles, and storing the infused fluid stream in one or more of a storage tank, a fluid treatment pit, or an underground formation or formations.
   USE - Method for storing carbon used in oil and gas production operations to produced water or water disposal wells, waterflood wells, oil recovery operations, enhanced oil recovery (EOR) operations, hydraulic fracturing operations.
   ADVANTAGE - The oxygen bubbles provide lift, floats lighter solids, and improves the oil/water separation process. The higher temperature of produced water from the wellhead can be reduced through the process of aeration. The reduction in temperature allows more carbon dioxide to be absorbed by the produced water. This absorption process can be accelerated by adding carbon dioxide in nanobubble form to the airstream used in the aeration process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The infused fluid stream is stored in an underground formation or formations by a well completion process. The carbon dioxide is obtained from flaring of hydrocarbon gas in oilfield operations. The fluid stream is produced water from oilfield operations. The ozone injection system injects the ozone gas or ozone-oxygen mixture gas upstream of the one or more fluid treatment tanks. The dose rate varies dynamically as the quality of the fluid stream changes based upon continuous monitoring of the fluid stream quality. The ozone gas or an ozone-oxygen mixture gas is injected as nano-bubbles or micro-bubbles. The reject gas is directed to the at least one separator as blanket gas. The ozone injection system is contained in whole or in portion in a moveable container. The fluid stream is produced water from oil or gas wells. The fluid stream is fracturing fluid for a hydrocarbon fracturing operation. The system further comprises nitrogen nano-bubble delivery system, configured to inject nitrogen or nitrogen-rich gas into the fluid stream. The nitrogen or nitrogen-rich gas is injected downstream of the one or more fluid treatment tanks. The nitrogen nano-bubble delivery system comprises a manifold with one or more strainers and a mixer.

PN CN112973755-A
TI Preparing graphite phase carbon nitride-based two-dimensional composite photocatalytic material useful in catalytic field, comprises e.g. performing thermal reduction treatment of carbon dioxide and graphene oxide/graphitic carbon nitride.
AU XIONG Y
   JIANG J
   GAO C
AE UNIV CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UCST-C)
GA 202176171X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing graphite phase carbon nitride-based two-dimensional composite photocatalytic material comprises (1) mixing the first precursor mixture carbon dioxide, graphene oxide and graphitic carbon nitride dispersion to obtain second precursor mixture, (2) drying the second precursor mixture to obtain carbon dioxide and graphene oxide/graphitic carbon nitride solid, and (3) performing thermal reduction treatment of carbon dioxide and graphene oxide/graphitic carbon nitride in an inert atmosphere to obtain carbon monooxide-reduced graphene oxide/graphitic carbon nitride catalyst, i.e. the graphite phase carbon nitride-based two-dimensional composite photocatalytic material.
   USE - The composite photocatalytic material is useful in the catalytic field (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and economical; enhances the visible light catalytic activity of the catalyst; and adjusts the ratio of synthesis gas in the catalytic product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for graphite phase carbon nitride-based two-dimensional composite photocatalytic material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (1), the second precursor mixture is 0.0015-0.15 mg/ml graphene oxide. In step (1), the second precursor mixture includes 5-30 mg/ml graphitic carbon nitride. The mass ratio of graphene oxide to graphitic carbon nitride in the second precursor mixture is 0.0625-1:100. The first precursor mixture is 0.01-0.2 mu mol/ml carbon dioxide. The first precursor mixture is 0.003-0.3 mg/ml graphene oxide. In step (3), the inert atmosphere includes either argon or nitrogen. Preferred Method: In step (1), the method for preparing the first precursor mixture carbon dioxide and graphene oxide includes mixing the carbon dioxide aqueous solution with graphene oxide dispersion to obtain the first precursor mixture carbon dioxide and graphene oxide coordinated by carbon dioxide and graphene oxide. In step (1), the preparation method of graphitic carbon nitride includes heat-treating the precursor of graphitic carbon nitride, where the precursor of graphitic carbon nitride includes urea, melamine, dicyandiamine, cyanamide, and/or thiourea. The temperature of the heat treatment is 400-600 degrees C and the time of the heat treatment is 2-4 hours. In step (2), the method for drying the second precursor mixture includes freeze-drying. The freeze-drying time is 24-48 hours. In step (3), the temperature of thermal reduction treatment is 500-600 degrees C. The time of thermal reduction treatment is 3-5 hours.

The extracting is performed at a pressure of less than or equal to 4200 psi, preferably greater than or equal to 5000 psi for selectively extracting THC over CBD from the plant material. The extracting is performed at a temperature of less than 65 degrees C, preferably 40-60 degrees C for selectively extracting CBD over THC from the plant material. The extracting is performed at a pressure of greater than or equal to 4600 psi for selectively extracting CBD over THC from the plant material. The extracting is performed at a pressure of 2000-4600 psi for selectively extracting CBD over THC from the plant material. In the process (P2) extracting is performed at a temperature of 70-90 degrees C. The extracting is performed at a pressure of greater than or equal to 4200 psi, preferably 5000 psi.

TI Method for predicting wellbore temperature during            carbon dioxide fracturing process, involves calculating            relaxation distance parameter, and determining specific            heat capacity of carbon-di-oxide fracturing fluid.
AU WANG L
   LI F
   MA X
   SHI A
   LV X
   ZHOU T
   ZHANG S
   MU J
   ZOU Y
   WANG F
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM BEIJING (UPEB-C)
   SINOPEC EXPLORATION & PRODN RES INST (SNPC-C)
GA 202012164D
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining parameters. A                wellbore is calculated during carbon-di-oxide                fracturing process based on the obtained                parameters. A predicted position is determine. A                specific temperature at a to-be-predicted time is                determined so as to realize the prediction of the                wellbore temperature during the carbon dioxide                fracturing process. A difference between a surface                temperature and an injection temperature of the                carbon-di-oxide fracturing fluid is determined. A                relaxation distance parameter is calculated. A                specific heat capacity of the carbon-di-oxide                fracturing fluid is determined.
   USE - Method for predicting a wellbore temperature                during a carbon dioxide fracturing process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables realizing estimating                carbon-di-oxide fracturing well temperature field,                and improving estimation result efficiency.

AU FENG C
AE JILIN JINYUAN NORTH TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LT (JILI-Non-standard)
GA 201942315D
AB    NOVELTY - Pavement regenerant comprises 50-100 pts. wt. Carbon dioxide, 10-20 pts. wt. copolymer fiber material and 1-5 pts. wt. nano-filler.
   USE - Pavement regenerant.
   ADVANTAGE - The pavement regenerant has large engineering quantity, long construction period and cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for following:
   (1) a method of producing a pavement regenerant, which involves pulverizing the carbon dioxide copolymer and dissolving in a solvent to form a first solution, where solvent is ethyl acetate or dimethyl carbonate; and
   (2) mixing the fiber material and the nano filler, adding first solution and co-stirring to form second mixture; and
   (3) a method for regenerating a diseased pavement by using the pavement regenerant, which involves cutting and breaking the pavement material around the damaged road surface into particles, stirring the particles, adding the solution, stirring together, then adding the mixture, and continuing to stir to obtain a paving material and spreading the paving material on the disease, smoothing and compacting.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide copolymer comprises carbon dioxide copolymer left overs of medical dressing. The fibrous material is a natural fiber material. The natural fiber material is selected from one of basalt fiber, carbon fiber, straw straw fiber, and corn stover fiber. The fiber material is a mixture of basalt fiber, carbon fiber, straw straw fiber and corn stover fiber. Preferred Conditions: The carbon dioxide copolymer has anumber average molecular weight is 3-16. The weight ratio of each component in the mixture is basalt fiber, carbon fiber, straw straw fiber and corn stalk fiber is (1-3):(0.5-2):(4-6):(4-6). The nano filler is nanometre silicon dioxide. The diseased road surface is wetted with first solution and the first solution is sprayed again.

AB    NOVELTY - An aragonite type light calcium carbonate                comprises slaked lime and carbon dioxide in which                aragonite content rate is greater than or equal to                95%, pore volume by gas absorption method is                greater than or equal to 0.15 cm3/g and BET                specific surface area of greater than or equal to                32 m2/g.
   USE - An aragonite type light calcium carbonate used                as filler, coating pigment or carrier for paper                material, ink, coating material, plastics, adhesive                agent foodstuff, pharmaceuticals and film (all                claimed).

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                manufacturing aragonite type light calcium                carbonate which involves blowing in carbon-dioxide                containing gas in slaked lime slurry, reacting                slaked lime and carbon dioxide, adding strontium                compound and organic acid which discharges                strontium ion in slaked lime slurry and advancing                the reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Aragonite type light                  calcium carbonate contains greater than or equal                  to 0.005 mol strontium with respect to 1 mol                  calcium. Aragonite type light calcium carbonate                  consists of acicular particles which do not                  contain the particles with particle diameter of                  greater than or equal to 1 mu m by less than or                  equal to 60 nm shorter axes. Amount of strontium                  compound is 10-20 wt.%. Amount of organic acid in                  slaked lime slurry is 0.1-0.4 wt.%. The amount of                  gas blowing of 1st step is less than the amount                  of gas blowing of subsequent step including two                  reaction steps from which the reaction step                  differs in the blowing amount of carbon-dioxide                  containing gas by less than or equal to 0.2                  L/minute. Preferred Properties: Aragonite type                  light calcium carbonate has Brunauer Emmett                  Teller (BET) specific surface area of greater                  than or equal to 30 m2/g. Preferred Process:                  Dispersing agent is added and is dispersed to                  bead mill after completing the reaction.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Organic acid is chosen                  from citric acid, tartaric acid and phthalic                  acid.

The light intensity of illumination is 5-20 mW/cm2, and the temperature during the illumination is controlled at 30-40 degrees C.

   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce stable isotope                labeled carbon monoxide that has high yield and                high arbitrary value.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The ratio (V1/V2)                  between a flow rate V1 of the water vapor and a                  flow rate V2 of the carbon monoxide is 1-100. The                  volume ratio (V3/V4) between a flow rate V3 of                  the hydrogen and a flow rate V4 of the stable                  isotope labeled carbon dioxide is set to 1-20.                  Preferred Process: The abundance ratio of stable                  carbon isotope in the carbon monoxide is                  controlled to be an arbitrary value in advance.                  The abundance ratio of stable oxygen isotope in                  the water vapor is controlled to be an arbitrary                  value in advance. The water vapor contains 95                  atom % or more of H218O with respect to a total                  of 100 atom % of H216O, H217O, and H218O. The                  stable oxygen isotope in the stable isotope                  selectively contained in the carbon monoxide and                  oxygen isotope in the stable isotope selectively                  contained in the water vapor are different from                  each other. The water vapor selectively contains                  one or both of H216O and H217O.

PT P
PN EP3431441-A1
TI Producing potassium carbonate by washing carbon dioxide-containing exhaust gas, comprises gas scrubbing an aqueous solution of potassium hydroxide or potassium salt, which is recovered from industrial wastewater.
AU LENGHEIM H
AE LENGHEIM H (LENG-Individual)
GA 2019074404
AB    NOVELTY - Producing potassium carbonate by washing carbon dioxide-containing exhaust gas, comprises gas scrubbing an aqueous solution of potassium hydroxide or potassium salt, which is recovered from industrial wastewater.
   USE - The aqueous potassium carbonate solution is useful as a liquid de-icing agent for producing potassium carbonate granules which is used as a solid scattering agent (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method favors waste disposal logistics of industrial wastewater and reduces carbon dioxide pollution in the exhaust gas of industrial plants.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises purifying the optionally concentrated industrial wastewater and/or recovering potassium hydroxide or a potassium salt from the purified wastewater, utilizing an aqueous solution of the recovered potassium hydroxide or potassium salt and/or optionally concentrated industrial wastewater as a washing solution in the gas scrubbing, and separating the potassium carbonate formed from the washing solution during gas scrubbing. The recovery of potassium hydroxide from the purified wastewater is carried out by electrolysis, where the energy contained in the carbon dioxide-containing exhaust gas is used for the operation of an electrolysis plant for the production of potassium hydroxide. The recovery of potassium chloride from the purified wastewater is carried out in electrolysis by flotation, hot dissolving method, electronic system for travel authorization method, cold pre- and post-decomposition or fractional crystallization, preferably fractional crystallization. The reaction temperature when carbonating the potassium hydroxide solution or potassium-containing washing solution is not greater than 100 degrees C, preferably not greater than 60 degrees C. Preferred Components: The industrial wastewater is a wastewater generated during potash mining. The industrial generated wastewater is a wastewater produced during biodiesel production. The carbon dioxide-containing exhaust gas is a combustion- or exhaust gas from large industrial plants e.g. fossil-fired power plants, blast furnaces for production and refinement of metals, natural gas treatment plants, ammonia synthesis plants, cement plants or lime kilns, preferably lime kilns. The electrolysis is chloralkali electrolysis. The aqueous solution produced during chloralkali electrolysis has a concentration of at least 20%, preferably at least 45% potassium hydroxide. The aqueous potassium carbonate solution is concentrated to 10-60%, preferably 40-50% before or after separation by evaporation.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PN WO2022019451-A1; US2022026144-A1; KR2317832-B1; CN114514067-A; JP2022545310-W
TI Apparatus for manufacturing dry ice nuggets used            as coolant in distribution process for e.g. food            delivery, has main portion that manufactures dry ice            nuggets by injecting liquefied carbon dioxide and            pressurizing liquefied carbon dioxide in snow state            which changes phase.
AU CHOI J H
   HEUNG C J
   CHOI J
AE VICTEX CO LTD (VICT-Non-standard)
   VICTEX CO LTD (VICT-Non-standard)
GA 202216397U
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has main portion that                manufactures dry ice nuggets (D11) by injecting                liquefied carbon dioxide and pressurizing the                liquefied carbon dioxide in a snow state which                changes phase. The support base comprises a support                plate facing upward at the upper end. A first case                is configured for reciprocating along the extension                surface of the support base in a direction in which                the support base extends. A second case in which                the first case is in contact with each other when                moving upward to form a compressed space in which                airtightness is maintained is provided. A second                case in which a supply hole connected to a supply                line is formed to provide liquid carbon dioxide to                the compressed space. A pressure piston penetrates                the second case and is configured for reciprocating                the compression space, where the pressure piston                descends so that the compression space is                determined within a pressure range of 16 bar to 20                bar.
   USE - Apparatus for manufacturing dry ice nuggets                used as coolant in distribution process for food                delivery or drug delivery.
   ADVANTAGE - The configuration and process of the apparatus                are simpler, thus realizing an efficient                manufacturing of dry ice. The groove is formed in a                respective shape to prevent separation of the                sealing portion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The apparatus has a pressure piston that                approaches the support plate in the compression                space and the liquid carbon dioxide is pressurized.                The second case and the support base are fixed and                driven by reciprocating motions of the first case                and the pressure piston. The first case includes a                groove formed on a surface in contact with the                second case, and includes a sealing portion                arranged in the groove. The second case and a                sealing portion on a surface in contact with the                first case are provided. A protrusion is made a                metal material having a corresponding shape. The                rod extends from the outside to the inside of the                cylinder. A pressing plate is coupled to one end of                the rod positioned inside the cylinder for pressing                in a pressing direction formed from a first                pressing section formed in the cylinder to a second                pressing section, and extends to contact the inner                wall of the cylinder. The pressure plate, and                distributed pressure portion are formed in a                convexly curved shape toward the pressing                direction. A pressing end is formed in a plane                opposite to the pressing direction and in contact                with the inner wall of the cylinder at the end side                of the distributed pressing unit. The distributed                pressing unit is pressed through contact between                the pressing plate and snow for dispersing                non-uniformly accumulated snow in the second                pressing section in a form corresponding to the                molded shape before pressing. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM                is included for a method for producing dry ice                nugget, involves moving the first case in contact                with the second case by moving the first case                upward along the extension direction of the support                base fixed and supported on the ground. A                compressed space in which the first case and the                second case communicate with each other in an                airtight state is formed by the movement of the                first case. A liquid carbon dioxide is injected                into the compressed space formed in the compression                space forming step. The liquid carbon dioxide                injected in the liquid carbon dioxide injection                step is moved in a pressurizing direction, and a                pressurizing piston is connected to the second case                to pressurize the liquid carbon dioxide. The                pressure piston and the first case are returned                after the pressing step. The dry ice in which the                state of the pressurized liquid carbon dioxide is                changed after the return step is discharged. The                pressure piston approaches the support plate in the                compression space and the liquid carbon dioxide is                pressurized, and the compression space in which the                pressure piston descends so as to be determined                within a pressure range.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a dry                ice nugget manufacturing apparatus.
   Dry ice nugget (D11)
   Curved portion (D11a)
   Flat portion (D11b)
   Central portion (D11c)
   Peripheral portion (D11d)

PN CN114082415-A
TI Preparation of bismuth-tin bimetallic catalyst            used in electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide,            involves performing chemical reduction reaction of            bismuth salt and tin salt.
AU GU D
   SONG Z
   YAN X
   ZHANG C
   WANG M
   ZHU X
   KANG H
   MAO Y
   JIAO X
   ZHANG X
AE UNIV HENAN URBAN CONSTR (UHUC-C)
GA 2022356836
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of bismuth-tin bimetallic catalyst                involves performing chemical reduction reaction of                bismuth salt and tin salt.
   USE - Preparation of bismuth-tin bimetallic catalyst                used in electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The application method is a solution chemical                reduction method, which can effectively regulate                the catalyst synthesis conditions. The preparation                method of the catalyst is simple and easy under                mild reaction conditions, and has high yield and                strong controllability. The synthesized bismuth-tin                bimetallic catalyst has high purity and                uniformity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                application of the bismuth-tin bimetallic catalyst                in electrochemical reduction of carbon                dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method preferably                  involves dissolving bismuth salt and surfactant                  in organic solvent, sealing, stirring and mixing,                  adding tin salt, stirring and mixing to obtain                  catalyst precursor solution, where the ratio of                  the amount of surfactant to sum of the amount of                  metal ions in the bismuth salt and tin salt is                  (1-5):(1-10), dissolving reducing agent solution                  in an organic solvent to obtain a reducing                  solution, and adding the reducing solution to                  catalyst precursor solution for chemical                  reaction, centrifuging obtained suspension,                  washing obtained solid, centrifuging and                  vacuum-drying, where the ratio of the amount of                  the reducing agent in the reducing solution to                  the sum of the amount of metal ions in the                  bismuth salt and tin salt is (3-10):1. The                  application method involves dispersing the                  bismuth-tin bimetallic catalyst into isopropanol                  to obtain a catalyst slurry, adding Nafion (RTM:                  sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based                  fluoropolymer-copolymer) solution and mixing to                  obtain a mixed solution, and coating the mixed                  solution on working electrode component and                  drying. Preferred Component: The organic solvent                  is N,N-dimethylformamide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The bismuth salt is                  bismuth nitrate pentahydrate or bismuth chloride.                  The tin salt is stannous chloride or anhydrous                  tin tetrachloride. The working electrode                  component is carbon paper. Preferred Composition:                  The ratio of the amount of bismuth salt to tin                  salt is (1-4):(0-4).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The surfactant is                  polyvinylpyrrolidone.

   CHAI Q

AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 202113463S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of cluster bismuth selenide comprises mixing bismuth salt and selenium dioxide with water, adding reducing agent solution and heating at 120-200 degrees C for hydrothermal reaction. The molar ratio of bismuth salt and selenium dioxide is 1:1-3. The reducing agent is hydrazine hydrate and/or sodium borohydride.
   USE - The preparation of cluster bismuth selenide is used for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The cluster bismuth selenide has excellent electrocatalytic reduction performance of carbon dioxide, higher Faraday efficiency and current density of formate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The bismuth salt is bismuth nitrate pentahydrate, bismuth chloride and/or bismuth carbonate. The molar volume ratio of bismuth salt to water is 1 mmol: 10-100 ml. The concentration of reducing agent is 0.5-1.5 mol/l. The molar volume ratio of bismuth salt to reducing agent solution is 1 mmol: 1-10 ml. The molar ratio of bismuth salt and selenium dioxide is 1:1-2.

TI Producing 2,5-furan dicarboxylic acid, comprises forming a mixture by mixing catalyst containing triazole ligand and 2-furoic acid, and injecting carbon dioxide into the mixture to produce a reactant.
AU KIM Y J
   CHO J K
   LEE H J
   CHIEN T C
   KIMYONGJIN
   TRUONGCONGCHIEN
AE KOREA INST IND TECHNOLOGY (KTEC-C)
GA 2018783679
AB    NOVELTY - Producing 2,5-furan dicarboxylic acid, comprises (a) forming a mixture by mixing catalyst containing triazole ligand and 2-furoic acid, and (b) injecting carbon dioxide into the mixture to produce a reactant.
   USE - The method is useful for producing 2,5-furan dicarboxylic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, environmentally friendly and economical, and produces compound without using toxic reactants.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for catalyst comprising M-triazolide, where M is alkali metal for synthesizing 2,5-furan dicarboxylic acid from pentose and carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is M-triazole catalyst, preferably lithium(triazollide), sodium(triazollide), potasium(triazollide), rubidium(triazollide), caesium(triazollide), caesium(Benzotriazolide) or caesium(amino-triazollide). The amount of 2-furoic acid is 0.5-0.7 mmol in 1 mmol catalyst.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) is carried out by performing solventless reaction. The step (a) is carried out at 100-300 degrees C. The step (b) comprises injecting carbon dioxide into the mixture at first pressure, stirring the carbon dioxide injected mixture with the first pressure, preferably 10-90 psi and injecting carbon dioxide into the mixture at second pressure, preferably 200-600 psi.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN107651704-A; CN107651704-B
TI Hierarchical structure cadmium sulfide nanopattern used for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide comprises ultra-thin cadmium sulfide nanosheets with exposed crystal face.
AU ZHAO C
   CHEN F
   ZHAN X
   YI L
   LI J
   JIAO P
   LUO H
AE UNIV XIANGTAN (UNXT-C)
   UNIV XIANGTAN (UNXT-C)
GA 201811506C
AB    NOVELTY - Hierarchical structure cadmium sulfide nanopattern comprises ultra-thin cadmium sulfide nanosheets with exposed crystal face having 2-4 nm thickness.
   USE - Hierarchical structure cadmium sulfide nanopattern used for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The yield of carbon monoxide during photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide under visible light is 1.558 mu mol/g/hour, which is about twice as that of nanoparticles.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of hierarchical structure cadmium sulfide nanopattern comprising mixing inorganic cadmium salt, deionized water, diethylenetriamine and sulfur powder uniformly, subjecting mixture to solvothermal reaction, washing product, and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The crystal of cadmium sulfide is wurtzite. The ratio of sulfur powder and inorganic cadmium salt is 4-8:1. The ratio of deionized water and inorganic cadmium salt is 0.3-0.7:1. The ratio of inorganic cadmium salt and diethylenetriamine is 200-500:1. The inorganic cadmium salt is cadmium nitrate, cadmium chloride, cadmium acetate, cadmium nitrate tetrahydrate, cadmium chloride pentahydrate, and/or cadmium acetate dihydrate. Preferred Method: The solvothermal reaction is carried out at 60-100 degrees C for 24-72 hours.

PN US2018045405-A1; WO2018031117-A1; US10113740-B2; AU2017310229-A1; KR2019035893-A; BR112019002879-A2; CN109804142-A; IN201927005450-A; JP2019525118-W; PL430832-A1; IN375140-B; KR2336903-B1; CN109804142-B
TI Combusting carbonaceous fuel involves combusting the fuel and oxygen-containing in a fluidized bed reactor to produce carbon dioxide and steam, and recycling produced carbon dioxide and steam as oxygen-containing gas to the reactor.
AU FITZSIMMONS M
AE GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
   GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
   GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
   GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
GA 201813707D
AB    NOVELTY - Combusting a carbonaceous fuel, comprises: introducing the fuel and oxygen-containing gas into fluidized bed reactor at location in sufficiently close proximity to each other to avoid producing reducing atmosphere; combusting the fuel and oxygen-containing gas to produce carbon dioxide and steam; and recycling produced carbon dioxide and steam as oxygen-containing gas to the reactor so that the total quantity of oxygen supplied to the reactor is greater than 20% but the quantity of oxygen supplied in each stage is no more than 20%.
   USE - The method is useful for combusting a carbonaceous fuel in oxy-combustion reactor.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: achieves desired mixing of the carbonaceous fuel and oxygen; avoids the production of reducing atmosphere at the location; allows to recycle at least a portion of the produced carbon dioxide and steam to the fluidized bed reactor as a portion of the oxygen-containing gas so that the total quantity of oxygen supplied to the reactor is greater than 20% but the quantity of oxygen supplied in each stage is no more than 20%; improves or optimize carbon dioxide capture at relatively low cost and permits attainment of high efficiency, at least in part through the use of staged combustion; improves or increases fuel flexibility, higher turndown capability, and range of coolant temperature variation when compared to an oxy-combustion system that is designed to introduce all oxygen at once and does not stage the combustion; and utilizes the processing system that has compact size and enables the use of densely packed heat exchanger surfaces so as to advance at least one of complete combustion, moderated bed temperatures, reduced capital costs and reduced waste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Combusting a carbonaceous fuel, comprises: introducing the carbonaceous fuel and an oxygen-containing gas into a staged fluidized bed reactor, in which the carbonaceous fuel and the oxygen-containing gas are eluted through a fluidized bed comprising an inert material and/or dolomite, where the oxygen-containing gas comprises a mixture of oxygen, recycled carbon dioxide and steam having sufficient oxygen added to the recycled carbon dioxide and steam so that the mixture in each stage contains 7-20 mole% oxygen, and the carbonaceous fuel and the oxygen-containing gas are introduced into the fluidized bed at a location in sufficiently close proximity to each other to avoid producing a reducing atmosphere at the location; combusting the carbonaceous fuel and with at least a portion of the oxygen-containing gas to produce at least carbon dioxide and steam; and recycling to the fluidized bed reactor at least a portion of the produced carbon dioxide and steam as a portion of the oxygen-containing gas so that the total quantity of oxygen supplied to the reactor is greater than 20% but the quantity of oxygen supplied in each stage is no more than 20%. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for
   (1) combusting a carbonaceous fuel in an oxy-combustion reactor (16'), comprising controlling local oxygen content within the oxy-combustion reactor to less than 20 mole%; and
   (2) a processing system (10') comprising a staged fluidized bed reactor where a carbonaceous fuel (12') and an oxygen-containing gas (14') are eluted through the fluidized bed, and the carbonaceous fuel and the oxygen-containing are injected into the fluidized bed at a location in sufficiently close proximity to each other to avoid producing a reducing atmosphere at the location.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows schematic view of multi-stage processing system.
   Processing system (10')
   Carbonaceous fuel line (12')
   Oxygen line (14')
   Oxy-combustion reactor system (16')
   Product gases line (22')
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The fluidized bed reactor comprises: at least one in-bed heat exchanger, or many in-bed heat exchangers occupying 20-35 vol.% of the fluidized bed reactor; and many vertically separated stages, where at least one pair of adjacent vertically separated stages are separated by internal heat exchanger surfaces.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step of introducing of the carbonaceous fuel and the oxygen-containing gas into the fluidized bed reactor: achieves desired mixing of the carbonaceous fuel and oxygen; and comprises introducing the carbonaceous fuel and the oxygen-containing gas into the fluidized bed reactor via an unlike doublet in which a fuel injector is paired with an oxygen-containing gas injector, where the fuel injector and the oxygen-containing gas injector produce plumes of matched momentums and the plumes interact to form a sheet of uniform mixing, or comprises introducing the carbonaceous fuel and the oxygen-containing gas into the fluidized bed reactor via a coaxial splash plate injector where the carbonaceous fuel is fed through a central tube to impact a first splash plate and to be redirected to initially flow outwardly generally perpendicular to the central tube and the oxygen-containing gas is fed through an annular flow path disposed around the central tube to impact a second splash plate and to be redirected to initially flow outwardly generally perpendicular to the central tube and parallel the redirected initial flow of the carbonaceous fuel, or comprises introducing the carbonaceous fuel and the oxygen-containing gas into the fluidized bed reactor via a coaxial arrangement where the carbonaceous fuel is fed through a central tube and the oxygen containing gas is fed through an annular flow path disposed around the central tube, where the coaxial arrangement further comprises many side discharge outlets that centrally side discharge a portion of the carbonaceous fuel with an annular plume of the oxygen-containing gas, or introducing the carbonaceous fuel and the oxygen-containing gas into the fluidized bed reactor in parallel and vertically spaced apart. Preferred Components: The carbonaceous fuel comprises: a solid comprising coal, petroleum coke and/or biomass; or natural gas. Each stage has a beginning having an oxygen mole percent content of at least 7-20% and an end, with the end of each stage prior to the last stage having an end oxygen content of no more than 3 mole % and the end of the last stage having an oxygen content of no more than 0.7-1.0 mole%.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); Q73 (Combustion equipment/processes (F23))

GA 2022B0925Q
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a biomass gasification furnace                for accessing water vapor, high-temperature air,                carbon dioxide, nitrogen and volatile organic                compounds (VOCs) organic waste gas. A hydrogen                separating device separates biomass fuel gas from                hydrogen and carbon dioxide. A carbon separator                separates the carbon dioxide from the biomass gas.                The carbon dioxide is separated by the CO separator                into an incinerator. A portion of the mixed gas is                desorbed by an active carbon desorption device to                burn and release heat. A flue gas discharged by the                incinerator enters a steam boiler to generate                steam. A part of the flue air enters into an air                chamber gasifying furnace to be used as a                gasification agent.
   USE - The active carbon VOCs gas high temperature                desorption and coupling processing system is useful                in the technical field of gas treatment,                particularly for the treatment of hazardous                waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The active carbon VOCs gas high temperature                desorption and coupling processing system: utilizes                500-800?? C high temperature smoke and carbon                monoxide-rich air as gasification agent; has high                gasification efficiency, high biomass gas CO                content, high heat value and high hydrogen content;                and improves the energy comprehensive utilization                efficiency of the whole system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of an                active carbon volatile organic compound gas high                temperature desorption and coupling processing                system (Drawing includes non-English language                text).

TI System for maintaining carbon dioxide            concentration of beer in draft beer keg, has correction            unit that processes order through tablet provided at            each table in store and corrects optimum correction            coefficient according to optimum pressure value.
AU KIM J H
AE YELLOW OCEAN CO LTD (YELL-Non-standard)
GA 2022A38256
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a control device (200) for                measuring a pressure value in a draft beer keg                (500), generating a corrected pressure value in                consideration of a temperature change, and                comparing the corrected pressure value with a                preset pressure value to control an injection or                discharge operation of carbon dioxide. A server                (100) transmits the preset pressure value to a                control device, receives control information, and                transmits to an user terminal (300). A                communication unit is provided for receiving the                preset pressure value and the correction                coefficient from the server and transmitting the                control information to the server. A correction                unit processes an order through a tablet provided                at each table in the store and corrects an optimum                correction coefficient according to the optimum                pressure value and temperature for each type of                beer through a questionnaire function on beer                taste.
   USE - System for maintaining carbon dioxide                concentration of beer in draft beer keg.
   ADVANTAGE - The concentration of carbon dioxide in the                beer is maintained uniformly even with changes in                the internal pressure of the draft beer keg or                environmental changes such as ambient                temperature.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                system for maintaining the carbon dioxide                concentration of beer in the draft beer keg.                (Drawing includes non-English language text)

PN CN114420787-A
TI Method of annealing solar cell, involves introducing supercritical carbon dioxide into substrate in hydrogen atmosphere for first process time, and performing light injection on substrate in second process time.
AU WU S
AE XIAN LONGI SOLAR TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202264108G
AB    NOVELTY - The method of annealing a solar cell involves (101) introducing supercritical carbon dioxide into a substrate in a hydrogen atmosphere for a first process time, and (!02) performing light injection on the substrate in a second process time. The first process time is 5-35 minutes. The second process time is 5-10 minutes.
   USE - Method of annealing a solar cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The supercritical carbon dioxide is introduced into a substrate in a hydrogen atmosphere within a first process time, and then light injection is carried out on the substrate within a second process time. The supercritical carbon dioxide is high in density and high in solubility to hydrogen, and hydrogen can be better transferred to the substrate, so that the problem of hydrogen overflow is solved. The hydrogen passivation effect is enhanced, after hydrogen excitation is carried out through the light injection. The defects of the substrate can be fully saturated, the recombination loss is reduced, the passivation effect is improved, and the conversion efficiency and the stability of a cell are improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an annealing device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating the steps of a method of annealing a solar cell. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Introducing supercritical carbon dioxide into a substrate in a hydrogen atmosphere for a first process time (101)
   Performing light injection on the substrate in a second process time (102)

AE UNIV ANHUI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYLG-C)
GA 202259986H
AB    NOVELTY - The device has an air inlet compressor (3) whose tail end is orderly connected with a gas check valve and a gas conveying pipe. The gas conveying pipe is formed with an exhaust hole.
