PT P
PN EP3842140-A1; KR2021082923-A; KR2305272-B1
TI Catalyst used for converting carbon dioxide in selective production of specified liquid hydrocarbons and linear alpha-olefins to make e.g. transportation fuels, synthetic lubricants and surfactants, comprises iron-aluminum oxide compound.
AU KIM J H
   MUHAMMAD K K
   MUHAMMAD I
   JO H T
   JO H
AE UNIV SUNGKYUNKWAN RES & BUSINESS FOUND (USKK-C)
   UNIV SUNGKYUNKWAN RES & BUSINESS FOUND (USKK-C)
GA 2021743936
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises iron-aluminum oxide compound (I).
   USE - The catalyst is used for converting carbon dioxide in selective production of liquid hydrocarbons having more than 5 carbons and linear alpha -olefins having more than 5 carbons (all claimed) for preparing transportation fuels, synthetic lubricants, plasticizers, polyethylene comonomers, low molecular weight fatty acids, dielectric chemical materials, and surfactants.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: is capable of inhibiting methane formation to produce the liquid hydrocarbons at a high yield and inhibiting excessive hydrogenation to enhance the carbon-carbon coupling reaction remains; promotes reverse-water-gas-shift (RWGS) reaction and the subsequent carbon-carbon coupling reaction to produce the liquid hydrocarbons; has high selectivity to the linear alpha -olefins without a carbon-carbon coupling catalyst e.g. zeolite; exhibits very high stability under stream for up to 450 hours while causing negligible change in the product composition; and increases carbon dioxide conversion percentage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Catalyst comprises iron-aluminum oxide compound of formula (FexAlyOz) (I).
   x = 0.1-0.8;
   y = 0.2-0.9; and
   z = 0.5-3.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for converting carbon dioxide using the catalyst, comprising reacting the catalyst with hydrogen gas to adsorb hydrogen in it, injecting a gas mixture of carbon dioxide and hydrogen so that the gas mixture reacts with the hydrogen-adsorbed catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is composed of iron and aluminum in an at.% ratio of 10:90-80:20, preferably 50:50. The catalyst contains crystallized ferric oxide phase. The catalyst further comprises an alkali metal including sodium, potassium, rubidium and/or cesium. The catalyst comprises 0.5-10 wt.% alkali metal. The catalyst contains an amorphous aluminum oxide. The catalyst contains ferric oxide phase and aluminum oxide, where the ferric oxide phase and the aluminum oxide are adjacent to each other. The catalyst is combined with zeolite to form a composite. The gas mixture is composed of carbon dioxide and hydrogen in a mixing ratio of 3:1-1:3. Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide is adsorbed to the ferric oxide phase during carbon dioxide conversion. The ferric oxide phase is fired at 400-1000 degrees C to act as basic site. The hydrogen desorption occurs on the aluminum oxide. The aluminum oxide is fired at 400-1000 degrees C to act as an acidic site. The gas mixture is injected at a space velocity of 2000-10000 ml/g.hour. The reaction pressure by the gas mixture is 1.5-3.5 MPa. The carbon dioxide conversion method is carried out at 300-500 degrees C.
   EXAMPLE - 10 g Iron(III) nitrate nonahydrate and 10 g aluminum(III) nitrate nonahydrate were dissolved in 150 ml distilled and deionized (DDI) water to obtain solution 1. 11 g Sodium carbonate was dissolved in 53 ml DDI water to obtain aqueous sodium carbonate solution as precipitant, and then aqueous sodium carbonate solution was added dropwise to the solution 1 while stirring at 80 degrees C, where a dark brown precipitate was formed during the dropwise addition of the precipitant, then solution mixture was stirred continuously for 12 hours with a bar magnet while maintaining pH at 8, the mixture was aged at room temperature for 6 hours, the precipitate was filtered using filter paper when most of the precipitate was formed, the precipitate remaining on the filter paper was washed with 300 ml DDI water, the filtered substance was placed in drying oven at 80 degrees C for 12 hours and then calcined at 600 degrees C for 6 hours in static air condition to obtain the catalyst.
DC A60 (Additives and Compounding Agents If the usage is very restricted it may be classified under the individual polymer or process involved.); A17 (Polymers of unsubstituted aliphatic monoolefins; including polyethylene.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).);

PN CN112898205-A; CN112898205-B
TI Synthesis of benzimidazole, involves reacting            o-phenylenediamine compound and carbon dioxide using            amine-functionalized ordered mesoporous phenolic resin            polymer as catalyst and dimethylaminoborane as reducing            hydrogen reagent.
AU XUE T
   HUANG X
AE UNIV EAST CHINA NORMAL (UYEN-C)
GA 2021681186
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis of benzimidazole (I), involves                reacting an o-phenylenediamine compound (II) and                carbon dioxide using an amine-functionalized                ordered mesoporous phenolic resin polymer as                catalyst and dimethylaminoborane as reducing                hydrogen reagent in N-methylpyrrolidone solvent.                The usage amount of catalyst is 0.01-1 mol% with                respect to the nitrogen content of compound (II).                The filling pressure of carbon dioxide is 0.1-2                MPa. The reaction temperature is 60-180 degrees C                and time is 6-48 hours. The molar ratio of                N-methylpyrrolidone and catalyst is 50-100:1.
   USE - Synthesis of benzimidazole.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses a catalyst, which can be                prepared by a simple method and has excellent                catalytic activity, and enables synthesis of                benzimidazole under mild reaction conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesis of benzimidazole of formula (I),                involves reacting an o-phenylenediamine compound of                formula (II) and carbon dioxide using an                amine-functionalized ordered mesoporous phenolic                resin polymer as catalyst and dimethylaminoborane                as reducing hydrogen reagent in N-methylpyrrolidone                solvent. The usage amount of catalyst is 0.01-1                mol% with respect to the nitrogen content of                compound (II). The filling pressure of carbon                dioxide is 0.1-2 MPa. The reaction temperature is                60-180 degrees C and time is 6-48 hours. The molar                ratio of N-methylpyrrolidone and catalyst is                50-100:1.
   R = H, CH3, CH3O, Br, Cl or NO2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The                  amino-functionalized ordered mesoporous phenolic                  resin polymer is dimethylamino-functionalized                  ordered mesoporous polymer, amino-functionalized                  ordered mesoporous polymer, or                  methylamino-functionalized ordered polymer,                  preferably dimethylamino-functionalized ordered                  mesoporous polymer. Compound (II) is                  o-phenylenediamine (derivative).

PT P
PN CN112691670-A
TI Zirconium-modified nickel-based double-pore structure catalyst used for methane carbon dioxide reforming reaction comprises carrier i.e. gamma alumina, active component i.e. nickel oxide and auxiliary agent i.e. zirconium dioxide.
AU MA Q
   HAN Y
   LV L
   ZHAO T
   ZHANG J
   GAO X
   FAN S
   LI P
AE UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)
GA 202144791A
AB    NOVELTY - Zirconium (Zr)-modified nickel-based double-pore structure catalyst comprises carrier, active component and auxiliary agent. The carrier is gamma -alumina (Al2O3). The active component is nickel oxide (NiO). The auxiliary agent is zirconium dioxide (ZrO2). The active component and auxiliary agent are dispersed in the carrier framework. The catalyst is provided with mesopores and macropores at the same time.
   USE - The Zr-modified nickel-based double-pore structure catalyst is used for methane carbon dioxide reforming reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The Lewis base increases the adsorption amount of carbon dioxide by the catalyst. The addition of Zr can improve the stability of the catalyst pore structure, increasing the dispersion degree of Ni metal, so as to further improve the catalytic activity and anti-carbon performance of the catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparation of the catalyst comprising mixing template agent, nickel source, aluminum source, zirconium source, acid and polystyrene microsphere in organic solvent to obtain mixed material liquid, and drying and roasting the mixed material liquid in sequence; and
   (2) application of the catalyst in methane carbon dioxide reforming reaction comprising performing reduction treatment to the catalyst to obtain activated Zr-modified nickel-based double-pore structure catalyst; and introducing methane and carbon dioxide for reforming reaction in the presence of the activated catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The aperture of the mesopores is 2-20 nm, and the pore diameter of the macropores is 50-100 nm. The content of Ni in the catalyst is 1-20 wt.%, and the content of Zr is 0.1-30 wt.%. Preferred Method: The nickel source comprises nickel nitrate or nickel oxide. The aluminum source comprises aluminum isopropoxide or aluminum nitrate. The zirconium source comprises zirconium nitrate or zirconium oxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The acid comprises concentrated nitric acid or citric acid. The organic solvent comprises ethanol or isopropanol. The volume ratio of the acid and organic solvent is 1-6:3-18. The concentration of the acid is 13-16 mol/L.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The template agent comprises P123 or F127. The mass ratio of template agent, nickel source, aluminum source, zirconium source and polystyrene microsphere is 10-40:1-10:2-80:1-10:10-40. The concentration of the template agent in the organic solvent is 5x 10-3-10x 10-3 mol/L. The particle diameter of the polystyrene microsphere is 50-200 nm. The roasting is carried out under air atmosphere at 500-900 degrees C for 2-4 hours.

PN IN202141017649-A
TI Automatic mini goli soda filling machine has soda solenoid valve and carbon dioxide solenoid valve that shuts off and programmable logic controller (PLC) sends signals to bottle lifting cylinder to turn bottle upside down.
AU 
AE SODAHUB IND AUTOMATION (SODA-Non-standard)
GA 2021452866
AB    NOVELTY - The bottle filling machine has a high pressure pump that pumps water from water can to a carbonator. A carbon dioxide cylinder is connected by a pipe to a regulator. A pressure transducer senses the pressure inside the carbonator by a carbon dioxide solenoid valve to maintain the desired pressure inside the carbonator accurately. A bottle is placed in a filling box and soda flows into the bottle through soda solenoid valve and the level of soda in the bottle is adjusted, by changing the bottle filling angle. The soda solenoid valve and carbon dioxide solenoid valve shuts off and programmable logic controller (PLC) sends signals to bottle lifting cylinder to turn the bottle upside down to bring the goli into the locking position and the PLC sends signal to release the pressure from neck of the bottle by first cylinder solenoid valve to create pressure difference on the goli for locking the goli at the goli seal position.
   USE - Automatic mini goli soda filling machine.
   ADVANTAGE - The bottle filling machine fills the beverage automatically into goli soda bottle so that easily maintains the same level of beverage in all the bottles, maintains high level of carbonation in the bottle using accurate temperature and accurate carbon dioxide pressure maintained by PLC.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the automatic mini goli soda machine.

TI Method for controlling electronic expansion valve of carbon dioxide heat pump by computer device, involves determining proportional link value according to temperature difference range, and determining differential link value.
AU YE J
   LEI P
   ZHANG L
   LIU Z
AE GUANGDONG PHNIX ENERGY SAVING EQUIP CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202127113S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining a proportional link value according to the temperature difference range corresponding to ambient temperature and exhaust deviation. The difference between actual exhaust temperature and target exhaust temperature is determined. A differential link value is determined according to the deviation range corresponding to the deviation difference. An expansion valve opening degree of an electronic expansion valve is determined based on a proportional-integral-derivative (PID) algorithm.
   USE - Method for controlling the electronic expansion valve of a carbon dioxide heat pump by using computer device.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables adjusting and controlling the electronic expansion valve according to the expansion valve opening so that all environmental temperature working conditions in the set operation range can be adapted. The method enables effectively reducing the condition that the exhaust temperature or exhaust pressure of the machine set is too high.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) an electronic expansion valve control device for a carbon dioxide heat pump;
   (2) a computer device; and
   (3) a storage medium for containing computer-executable instructions that are used to execute the electronic expansion valve control method of the carbon dioxide heat pump.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of a method for controlling the electronic expansion valve of a carbon dioxide heat pump by using a computer device. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

TI Regeneration method of ammonia water, involves providing ammonia water regenerating system, and performing first flashing step, first compressing step, stripping step, second flashing step, and second compressing step.
AU WONG S
   NGUYEN H L Q
   WONG S H
   NGUYEN L Q H
AE UNIV NAT TSING HUA (UNTH-C)
   UNIV NAT TSING HUA (UNTH-C)
   UNIV NAT TSING HUA (UNTH-C)
GA 2020B0196G
AB    NOVELTY - The regeneration method (100) involves providing (110) an ammonia water regenerating system, performing a first flashing step (120), performing a first compressing step (130), in which the first steam is compressed by the first compressor and transferred to the first gas inlet of the stripper, performing a stripping step (140), in which the first flash liquid is stripped by the first steam in the stripper to generate a carbon dioxide stripping gas and the lean solvent, performing a second flashing step (150), in which the lean solvent is flashed in the second flash drum to form a second steam and a second flash liquid, and performing a second compressing step (160), in which the second steam is compressed by the second compressor and transferred to the second gas inlet of the stripper.
   USE - Regeneration method of ammonia water.
   ADVANTAGE - Reduces the energy consumption and achieves the purpose of reducing carbon dioxide emissions and capturing costs since formed ammonia water regenerating system is collocated with the carbon dioxide absorption tower.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a capturing method of carbon dioxide with ammonia water.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the flowchart of the method for regenerating ammonia water after capturing carbon dioxide with ammonia water.
   Regeneration method (100)
   Provide ammonia water regenerating system (110)
   First flashing step (120)
   First compressing step (130)
   Stripping step (140)

PN CN111013496-A
TI Packed tower reactor useful for producing cyclic carbonate, comprises e.g. reaction tower provided with carbon dioxide, alkylene oxide and catalyst inlet respectively at bottom and at least one discharge port provided on side wall of tower.
AU ZHU J
   LIU Z
   DONG Z
   SHI D
   GU X
   YU H
AE JIANGSU OXIRAN CHEM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020340789
AB    NOVELTY - Packed tower reactor comprises a reaction tower provided with a carbon dioxide inlet, an alkylene oxide inlet, and a catalyst inlet respectively at the bottom and at least one discharge port is provided on the side wall of the reaction tower. A heat exchanger is arranged inside the reaction tower and filling random packings in the voids inside the reaction tower except the heat exchanger.
   USE - The packed tower reactor is useful for producing cyclic carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The reactor: can arrange the heat exchanger between random packings; improves the heat exchange efficacy; provides uniform gas-liquid distribution and rapid reaction heat removal; and is simple and easy to operate and suitable for large-scale industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing cyclic carbonate, comprising (i) introducing carbon dioxide, alkylene oxide and catalyst into the reaction tower through the carbon dioxide inlet, the alkylene oxide inlet and the catalyst inlet respectively; and (ii) passing the alkylene oxide through the random packing and the heat exchanger of the reaction tower from bottom to top, contacting with the carbon dioxide under the action of the catalyst to obtain the cyclic carbonate and discharging reacted material through the discharge port.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic representation of packed tower reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The heat exchanger is a tube-and-tube heat exchanger comprising a multiple of heat exchange tubes evenly distributed, where the random packing is uniformly filled between the multiple of heat exchange tubes and there is gap between the side wall of the reaction tower and the heat exchange tubes. The bottom of the reaction tower is provided with a gas distributor and a liquid distributor, the liquid distributor is arranged above the gas distributor, and the bulk packing is arranged above the liquid distributor. The bulk packing comprising at least one of metal triangular packing, step ring packing, Bauer ring packing or rectangular saddle ring packing. A jacket heat exchanger is also provided outside the reaction tower. The diameter of the reaction tower is 2-3 m, and the height of the reaction tower is 10-20 m. The number of the outlets is 1-3. The reactor comprises a circulation pipe, where one end of the circulation pipe is connected to at least one of the discharge ports, and the other end is connected to the catalyst inlet. Preferred Method: The method comprises passing the reacted material into the reaction tower through the catalyst inlet through a circulation pipe to perform a circulation reaction.

PN CN110947392-A; CN110947392-B
TI New catalyst chemical formula of            tin-copper-oxygen, useful for electrochemical reduction            of carbon dioxide to formic acid.
AU WU J
   LIU G
   GUO X
   LI B
   TAN Y
   XIE H
   ZI Z
AE QINGDAO INST BIOENERGY & BIOPROCESS TECH (CAQD-C)
   INST COAL CHEM CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAXI-C)
GA 202028414N
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst has chemical formula of                tin-copper-oxygen (I), is new.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for electrochemical                reduction of carbon dioxide to formic acid                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high catalyst activity, and                good selectivity of formic acid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Catalyst has chemical formula of                (tin-copper-oxygen) SnxCuyO (I). INDEPENDENT CLAIMs                are also included for:
   (1) preparing catalyst; and
   (2) preparing formic acid by electrochemical                reduction of carbon dioxide comprising separately                taking membrane solution and isopropyl alcohol,                mixing membrane solution, isopropanol and catalyst,                placing in a microwave environment for more than 30                minutes to obtain catalyst dispersion, applying the                catalyst dispersion uniformly to the surface of the                carbon cloth to prepare catalytic carbon electrode,                selecting two-chamber electrolytic cell that                includes a cathode chamber and an anode chamber,                separating the cathode chamber and anode chamber by                a proton exchange membrane, injecting 0.5 M                potassium bicarbonate solution into the                electrolytic cell as an electrolyte, using the                catalytic carbon electrode as a working electrode,                selecting silver/silver chloride saturated                electrode as reference electrode, using platinum                electrode as the counter electrode to form a                three-electrode working system, purging nitrogen                gas into the cathode chamber, continuously passing                carbon dioxide into the cathode chamber for 30                minutes, starting the three-electrode working                system, ensuring the working voltage from -2.5 V to                5 V, passing carbon dioxide at a flow rate of 30                ml/minute for constant potential reduction to                prepare a gas product whose main component is                formic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method comprises                  taking copper nitrate, dissolving in water to                  obtain copper nitrate solution, taking                  bis(trichloromethyl)carbonate (BTC), dissolving                  in ethanol to obtain                  bis(trichloromethyl)carbonate solution, adding                  copper nitrate solution to BTC solution dropwise                  according to the molar ratio of copper and BTC of                  3:2, mixing uniformly, adding into a reaction                  kettle to crystallize for 8-24 hours to obtain                  crystallized product, cooling the crystallized                  product and performing suction filtration to                  obtain crystallized solid product, washing the                  crystallized solid product with an ethanol                  solution and drying to obtain copper-BTC, taking                  stannous oxalate, mixing stannous oxalate with                  copper-BTC according to the Cu-BTC amount of 5-80                  wt.%, placing in a muffle furnace for 2-8 hours,                  and cooling to obtain catalyst SnxCuyO, where x+y                  is 1 and x is greater than 0. The method                  comprises crystallizing at 100-250 degrees C. The                  method comprises calcining in the muffle furnace                  by using a programmed temperature calcination                  process, heating the muffle furnace at 400-600                  degrees C at a rate of 1-20 degrees C/minute, and                  calcining for 2-8 hours. The method comprises                  taking 10 mg catalyst, using a pipette to measure                  20 mu l of membrane solution and 250 mu l of                  isopropyl alcohol, mixing the membrane solution,                  isopropanol and catalyst, and placing in a                  microwave environment for 45 minutes to obtain                  catalyst dispersion. Preferred Components: The                  water is deionized water or high-purity                  water.

TI Electrocatalyzing reduction of carbondixoide            comprises e.g. taking monoatomic iron-loaded            nitrogen-doped carbon material as catalyst and            dispersing monovalent iron-supported nitrogen-doped            carbon electrocatalytic materials.
AU ZHANG L
   QUAN F
   JIA F
AE UNIV CENT CHINA NORMAL (UYNZ-C)
GA 201954914G
AB    NOVELTY - Electrocatalyzing reduction of carbondixoide                comprises (i) taking monoatomic iron-loaded                nitrogen-doped carbon material as a catalyst, where                monoatomic iron-supported nitrogen-doped carbon has                a microtopography of a polyhedron having a length,                a width and a height on a nanometer scale; and (ii)                dispersing monovalent iron-supported nitrogen-doped                carbon electrocatalytic materials with supported                metal as a single atom.
   USE - The method is useful for electrocatalyzing                reduction of carbondixoide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces carbon dioxide to carbon                monoxide with high rate as 99.63% good stability is                good; produces hypochlorite at rate of 99.47%; and                maintains high Faraday effect for a long                time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                anodic oxidation with hypochlorite producing                cathodic reduction of carbon dioxide with the                coupling method comprises (1) separating proton                exchange membrane in electrolytic cell into an                anode tank and a cathode tank; (2) taking catalyst                electrode prepared by the monoatomic iron-supported                nitrogen-doped carbon electrocatalyst as working                electrode and serves as a cathode and taking                ruthenium titanium electrode is an auxiliary                electrode and serves as an anode and introducing                electrolyte solution respectively in the                electrolytic cells of the anode tank and the                cathode tank; and (3) passing carbon dioxide into                the cathode tank to saturation, introducing carbon                dioxide continuously, where tank pressure is 1-3 V                cathode to reduce carbon dioxide and anode produces                hypochlorite.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The polyhedrons on the                  nanometer scale are between 50 and 200 nm in                  length, width and height. The loading of metal                  iron is from 0.1-2.16 wt.% preferably 0.5-1.08                  wt.%. The solution of iron acetylacetonate in the                  step (a) is a solution of iron acetylacetonate in                  tetrahydrofuran, performing ultrasonic conditions                  for 1-2 hours, drying under vacuum in the step                  (a) for 1-2 hours and calcining in step (a) is                  carried out for 2-4 hours and preparation of the                  ZIF-8 comprises dissolving 0.5-0.6 g zinc nitrate                  hexahydrate in 10-20 ml methanol to obtain a                  liquid A, dissolving 0.55-0.65 g                  dimethylimidazole in 10-20 ml methanol to obtain                  a liquid B, adding liquid A into the solution B,                  reacting under ultrasonic conditions at 30-40                  degrees C for 10-15 hours, and treating. The                  supported amount of the catalyst in the                  monoatomic iron-supported nitrogen-doped carbon                  catalyst electrode is 1-2 mg/cm2 . Preferred                  Method: The preparation of monoatomic                  iron-supported nitrogen-doped carbon material                  comprises (i) adding ZIF-8 into a solution of                  iron acetylacetonate, mixing uniformly under                  ultrasonic conditions and drying under vacuum to                  obtain a solid powder; and (b) calcining the                  above powder at 900-1000 degrees C for 2-4 hours                  in an inert atmosphere, cooling to room                  temperature naturally and collecting reaction                  product. The specific method comprises applying                  cell pressure to 1.5-2.5V, and the anode and                  cathode electrolyte solutions are 0.05-0.2 M                  sodium chloride solution. The preparation method                  of the monoatomic iron-supported nitrogen-doped                  carbon catalyst electrode comprises taking                  above-mentioned monoatomic iron-loaded                  nitrogen-doped carbon material, dispersing                  ultrasonically into a mixture of Nafion and                  isopropanol, suspending the monoatomic                  iron-loaded nitrogen-doped carbon on the surface                  of the glassy carbon electrode and drying under                  infrared light.

AB    NOVELTY - The boiler has a breeches-leg type single hearth connected with two sides of a separator. An upper exhaust port of the separator is filled with flue. A return section is provided with an external heat exchanger. The breeches-leg type single hearth is arranged with a cooling wall. The breeches-leg type single hearth is provided with a collecting box that is connected with a cooling wall. An outer side of the breeches-leg type single hearth is provided with a low- temperature re-heater, an upper coal saver, a lower coal economizer and an air pre-heater. The external heat exchanger is provided with a high temperature re-heater and a container. The container is connected with the high temperature re-heater that is connected with the low temperature re-heater. A boiler main body is filled with supercritical carbon dioxide.

PN CN110552671-A; CN110552671-B
TI Achieving efficient development of heavy oil            reservoirs by using dimethyl ether assisted            carbon-dioxide flooding involves selecting reservoir as            a mining object and drilling two vertical wells in            reservoir.
AU LIU Y
   HOU J
   LIU W
   YU H
   ZHAO X
   ZHANG F
   WANG X
   RUI Z
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
GA 2019A5762M
AB    NOVELTY - Achieving efficient development of heavy oil                reservoirs by using dimethyl ether assisted                carbon-dioxide flooding involves selecting                reservoir as a mining object. The depth of the                reservoir is 600-4000m, the thickness is 5-60m, the                average permeability is 5-2000md, the viscosity of                formation crude oil is greater than 1000 mega                Pascalasterisksecond, and the density of crude oil                is greater than 0.9 gram per cubic centimetre. The                two vertical wells are drilled in the reservoir,                and the formations are all fired. The well spacing                is 150-350m and the other is a production well. The                formation crude oil is collected in the reservoir,                and determined the minimum miscibility pressure of                dimethyl ether (5-50 mol.%)/carbon di-oxide mixture                and formation crude oil under formation conditions                by laboratory experiments. The carbon-dioxide and                dimethyl ether are mixed in the surface gas                injection device according to a certain                ratio.
   USE - Method for achieving efficient development of                heavy oil reservoirs by using dimethyl ether                assisted carbon-dioxide flooding.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to achieve efficient                development of heavy oil reservoirs by using                dimethyl ether assisted carbon-dioxide flooding is                effectively reduce the carbon-dioxide                driverimplementation process in fracturing caused                byformation of potential risk.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Achieving efficient development of heavy oil                reservoirs by using dimethyl ether assisted CO2                flooding involves selecting reservoir as a mining                object. The depth of the reservoir is 600-4000m,                the thickness is 5-60m, the average permeability is                5-2000md, the viscosity of formation crude oil is                greater than 1000 mega Pascalasterisksecond, and                the density of crude oil is greater than 0.9 gram                per cubic centimetre. The two vertical wells are                drilled in the reservoir, and the formations are                all fired. The well spacing is 150-350m and the                other is a production well. The formation crude oil                is collected in the reservoir, and determined the                minimum miscibility pressure of dimethyl ether                (5-50 mol.%)/carbon di-oxide mixture and formation                crude oil under formation conditions by laboratory                experiments. The carbon-dioxide and dimethyl ether                are mixed in the surface gas injection device                according to a certain ratio, and the molar                fraction of the dimethyl ether after mixing is 5-50                mol.%. The mixed fluid is injected into the heavy                oil through the wellbore in the oil layer, a total                of 0.5-1.0PV of mixed fluid is injected, and the                injection rate is 10-25 t/d. The production well is                opened an order to ensure that the injected mixed                fluid is fully miscible with the formation heavy                oil. The bottomhole pressure of the production well                is controlled to be greater than the minimum                miscible pressure measured in laboratory                experiments 1.0-5.0 mega Pascal, but at the same                time lower than the minimum fracture pressure of                the formation. The carbon-dioxide and dimethyl                ether in the recovered liquid are separated by a                ground separation device, and the recovered                separated carbon-dioxide and dimethyl ether are                mixed in a certain proportion and then injected                back to the specified heavy oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The The minimum                  miscible pressure measured by laboratory                  experiments of 1.0-5.0 mega Pascal. The carbon                  di-oxide and dimethyl ether in the produced                  liquid are separated by a surface separation                  device, and then injected back into the                  formation.

   CHINA ELECTRIC POWER RES INST (SGCC-C)
   STATE GRID SHAANXI ELECTRIC POWER CO ELE (SGCC-C)
   UNIV SHAANXI NORMAL (UNSN-C)
GA 2019562119
AB    NOVELTY - A trace carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide quantitative analyzing method involves placing sample in ten-way valve (V1) in second position and ten-way valve (V2) in first position, such that the sample gas enters loop through the ten-way valve (V1) for sample collection and carrier gas (A) and carrier gas (B) provide baseline signals for thermal conductivity detector and flame ionization detector respectively followed by collecting sample, switching the ten-way valve (V1) to 2 digits, collecting to-be-analyzed gas sample into sample loop, obtaining retention time and peak area by the flame ionization detector to determine content of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, switching the ten-way valve (V2) to the first position at beginning of time period, such that high-concentration gas is discharged through the second channel of discharge detection system of the ten-way valve (V2) and switching the ten-way valve (V2) to the second position at end of time period.
   USE - Method for quantitatively analyzing trace carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide in high concentration gas e.g. hydrogen chloride, chlorine or sulfur hexafluoride (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables quantitatively analyzing trace carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide at large scale.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A trace carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide quantitative analyzing method involves placing sample in ten-way valve (V1) in second position and ten-way valve (V2) in first position, such that the sample gas enters loop through the ten-way valve (V1) for sample collection and carrier gas (A) and carrier gas (B) provide baseline signals for thermal conductivity detector and flame ionization detector respectively, collecting sample, after a time period of obtaining high-concentration gas flowing out of column A, placing the ten-way valve (V1) and ten-way valve (V2) in one position, such that carrier gas (A) pushes sample gas into the column A and the thermal conductivity detector, obtaining a period of time during which the high concentration gas flows out of the column A through the thermal conductivity detector, discharging the sample gas through second channels of the ten-way valve (V2), converting carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide to methane, placing the ten-way valve(V2) at first position, switching the ten-way valve (V1) to 2 digits, collecting to-be-analyzed gas sample into sample loop, switching ten-way valve (V1) to one position and ten-way valve (V2) to 2 digits, recording time, such that carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide flowing out of column A earlier than high-concentration gas entering into column B, methane reformer and flame ionization detector, obtaining retention time and peak area by the flame ionization detector to determine content of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, such that high-concentration gas is accurately cut out from detection system according to time period during which high concentration gas flows out of the column A, switching the ten-way valve (V2) to the first position at beginning of time period, such that high-concentration gas is discharged through the second channel of discharge detection system of the ten-way valve (V2) and switching the ten-way valve (V2) to the second position at end of time period.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The volume concentration of the high concentration gas is more than 99%. The volume concentration of trace amounts of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide is 1-1000 mu l/l. Preferred Components: The carrier gas (A) and the carrier gas (B) are helium.

PT P
PN CN110743326-A
TI High efficiency and energy saving non-aqueous absorbent useful for capturing carbon dioxide.
AU LIU A
   LV X
   LI J
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 202011770Y
AB    NOVELTY - High efficiency and energy saving non-aqueous absorbent for capturing carbon dioxide, is claimed. The non-aqueous absorbent is alkoxy-functional fatty diamine (A) preferably N1-(2-methoxyethyl)-N1,N2-dimethylethane-1,2-diamine (I), N1-(2-ethoxyethyl)-N1,N2-dimethylethane-1,2-diamine (II), N1,N2-dimethyl-N1-(2-propoxyethyl)ethane-1,2-diamine (III), N1-(2-(hexyloxy)ethyl)-N1,N2-dimethylethane-1,2-diamine (IV), N1-(2-(2-methoxyethoxy)ethyl)-N1,N2-dimethylethane-1,2-diamine (V), N1-(2-(2-(2-methoxyethoxy)ethoxy)ethyl)-N1,N2-dimethylethane-1,2-diamine (VI), N1-(2-methoxyethyl)-N1,N2,N2-trimethylethane-1,2-diamine (VII), N1-ethyl-N2-(2-methoxyethyl)-N2-methylethane-1,2-diamine (VIII) or N1-(2-methoxyethyl)-N1,N3-dimethylpropane-1,3-diamine (IX).
   USE - The non-aqueous absorbent for capturing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - This method: increases the molecular volume of the absorbent by introducing a flexible alkoxy functional group, and thus reduces the viscosity increase during the absorption reaction; reduces the hydrogen bonding between the molecules of the absorbent; improves the flow and mass transfer capabilities of fatty diamine non-aqueous absorption system through a low-viscosity functional treatment at the molecular design level; and ensures low energy consumption and high-capacity capture of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - High efficiency and energy saving non-aqueous absorbent for capturing carbon dioxide, is claimed. The non-aqueous absorbent is alkoxy-functional fatty diamine (A) preferably N1-(2-methoxyethyl)-N1,N2-dimethylethane-1,2-diamine of formula (I), N1-(2-ethoxyethyl)-N1,N2-dimethylethane-1,2-diamine of formula (II), N1,N2-dimethyl-N1-(2-propoxyethyl)ethane-1,2-diamine of formula (III), N1-(2-(hexyloxy)ethyl)-N1,N2-dimethylethane-1,2-diamine of formula (IV), N1-(2-(2-methoxyethoxy)ethyl)-N1,N2-dimethylethane-1,2-diamine of formula (V), N1-(2-(2-(2-methoxyethoxy)ethoxy)ethyl)-N1,N2-dimethylethane-1,2-diamine of formula (VI), N1-(2-methoxyethyl)-N1,N2,N2-trimethylethane-1,2-diamine of formula (VII), N1-ethyl-N2-(2-methoxyethyl)-N2-methylethane-1,2-diamine of formula (VIII) or N1-(2-methoxyethyl)-N1,N3-dimethylpropane-1,3-diamine of formula (IX). AU SUN X

   MA L
   TIAN S
   ZHANG W
   DAI Q
   LI K
   WANG F
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 2021926172
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hydrogen sulfide by decomposing non-ferrous smelting flue gas desulfurization gypsum comprises (i) drying the desulfurized gypsum at 100-110 degrees C and allowing to stand; (ii) placing the dried desulfurized gypsum and carbon reducing agent in protective atmosphere at 700-800 degrees C for reducing reaction to obtain reduced calcium sulfide product; and (iii) adding calcium sulfide and water with different liquid-solid ratio and injecting carbon dioxide into the gas-liquid-solid three-phase fluidized bed for carbon dioxide absorbing and mineralizing.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing hydrogen sulfide by decomposing non-ferrous smelting flue gas desulfurization gypsum.

The inlet protection gas is 50 ml/minute nitrogen. Preferred Method: The roasting temperature for decomposing desulfurized gypsum is 700-800 degrees C, and the reaction time is 0.5-1.5 hours. The CO2 absorption and mineralization reaction is carried out in a gas-liquid-solid three-phase fluidized bed, where the liquid-solid ratio of calcium sulfide slag material to water is 4-8 ml:1 g, and the volume of CO2 is 75-85 vol.%, and the reaction time is 0.5-1 hour. The 5% hydrogen sulfide gas produced by the decomposition of desulfurized gypsum is returned to the treatment of non-ferrous smelting acid.

PT P
PN CN113117712-A; CN113117712-B
TI Dry reforming reaction of methane involves            carrying out catalytic reforming of methane and carbon            dioxide using catalyst including composite metal oxides            and silicon carbide under microwave conditions to            generate synthesis gas comprising hydrogen and carbon            monoxide.
AU XU W
   DENG J
   ZHOU J
   LI R
AE UNIV XIANGTAN (UNXT-C)
GA 202184011M
AB    NOVELTY - Dry reforming reaction of methane involves                carrying out catalytic reforming of methane and                carbon dioxide using a catalyst including composite                metal oxides and silicon carbide under microwave                conditions to generate synthesis gas comprising                hydrogen and carbon monoxide as main                products.
   USE - Dry reforming reaction of methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The reforming reaction has low reaction                temperature, low energy consumption and high                conversion rate of methane and carbon dioxide. The                catalyst has excellent activity for prolonged                period of time during use and stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Dry reforming reaction of methane under                microwave conditions involves carrying out                catalytic reforming of methane and carbon dioxide                using a catalyst including composite metal oxides                of formula: M1/CaZrO3 and silicon carbide under                microwave conditions to generate synthesis gas                comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide as main                products.
   M1 = Ni or Co.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                catalyst for methane dry reforming reaction                comprising the composite metal oxide and silicon                carbide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The composite metal                  oxide is nickel-cobalt/calcium zirconate.                  Preferred Method: The catalyst is prepared by                  (a1) dissolving metal nitrate in water, stirring                  uniformly, adding a precipitation agent to adjust                  to a pH of 10-12, aging for 1-4 hours to obtain                  composite metal oxide, filtering, calcining at                  500-800 degrees C, preferably 650-750 degrees C,                  and (a2) mixing the composite metal oxide and                  silicon carbide, and activating to obtain the                  catalyst, where the metal nitrate comprises                  zirconium nitrate, calcium nitrate, nickel                  nitrate and/or cobalt nitrate. The step of                  activating involves heating the mixed composite                  metal oxide and silicon carbide at 400-800                  degrees C, and reducing the mixed gas of hydrogen                  and argon for 0.5-2 hours, where the volume flow                  rate ratio of hydrogen and argon gas is 1:(1-10),                  preferably 1:(2-5). The step of methane dry                  reforming reaction is carried out at a bed                  temperature of 400-800 degrees C, preferably                  650-800 degrees C for 0.5-10 seconds. The step of                  microwave treatment is carried out at a microwave                  frequency of 2.5 GHz, and a microwave power of                  1-1400 W, preferably 500-900 W. Preferred                  Composition: The mass ratio of the composite                  metal oxide and silicon carbide is 1:(2-10). The                  atomic ratio of zirconium and calcium in the                  metal nitrate is 1:(0.2-20), preferably 1:(2-6).                  The atomic ratio of nickel element in nickel                  nitrate to zirconium element in zirconium nitrate                  when the prepared composite metal oxide is                  nickel/calcium zirconate is 1:(0.2-20),                  preferably 1:(2-6). The atomic ratio of cobalt                  element in cobalt nitrate to zirconium element in                  zirconium nitrate when the prepared composite                  metal oxide is cobalt/calcium zirconate is                  1:(0.2-20), preferably 1:(2-6). The atomic ratio                  of nickel element in nickel nitrate to zirconium                  element in zirconium nitrate when the prepared                  composite metal oxide is nickel-cobalt/calcium                  zirconate is 1:(0.2-20), preferably 1:(2-6). The                  atomic ratio of cobalt element in cobalt nitrate                  and nickel element in nickel nitrate is                  1:(0.2-10), preferably 1:(0.2-6). The volume                  ratio of methane and carbon dioxide is 1:(0.1-5),                  preferably 1:(0.5-1.2).

PT P
PN CN113121329-A; CN113121329-B
TI Coupling and extracting lactic acid from hydrous raw material of lactate involves mixing aqueous raw material of lactate with solid adsorbent, introducing carbon dioxide, and eluting solid adsorbent with water to obtain lactic acid.
AU ZHU H
   GAO D
   SUN Q
   WANG P
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC DALIAN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEMICAL (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP DALIAN RES (SNPC-C)
GA 202186239L
AB    NOVELTY - Coupling and extracting lactic acid from hydrous raw material of lactate involves mixing aqueous raw material of lactate with solid adsorbent, introducing carbon dioxide continuously, maintaining pressure not less than 5 megapascal (MPa), reacting at 5-30 degrees C, separating solid absorbent and material liquid, and eluting solid adsorbent with water to obtain lactic acid. The solid adsorbent is capable of selectively absorbing organic acid and not absorbing inorganic ion, and resin is de-adsorbed after heating.
   USE - Method for coupling and extracting lactic acid from hydrous raw material of lactate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to select combination of solid adsorbent capable of absorbing lactic acid and high-pressure carbon dioxide treatment, which can simultaneously transform and extract lactic acid from water-containing raw materials, compared with the traditional calcium salt sulfuric acid acidification method, carbon dioxide is used instead of sulfuric acid as acidifying agent, does not produce calcium sulfate solid waste, and is green and pollution-free separation method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The solid adsorbent is a thermally regenerated resin. The solid adsorbent is selected from polyvinyl pyridine resin, tertiary amine macro-porous styrene-divinyl benzene copolymer resin and lysine-chloromethyl polystyrene resins. The salt ions in aqueous raw material of lactate is ions with high water solubility of carbonate. Preferred Conditions: The solid adsorbent is calculated on dry basis and added amount is not less than 3-4 times mass of lactate in material liquid. The mass fraction of lactate in aqueous raw material of lactate is 4-20% based on weight of lactate. The pressure is maintained at 5-10 MPa after carbon dioxide is introduced. The reaction time is not less than 1 hour after introduction of carbon dioxide. The elution water is greater than or equal to 3 times the mass of the material liquid when elution is performed with water at 80-90 degrees C. Preferred Process: The method for separating solid absorbent and solution involves discharging solution to reaction system after reaction is finished, and releasing carbon dioxide and recycling to obtain solid absorbent. The elution is performed by first washing solid adsorbent with water at 1-10 degrees C, discarding liquid, and eluting with water at 80-90 degrees C, and collecting eluted to obtain crude lactic acid solution.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PT P
PN CN112263890-A
TI Carbon capture method using flue gas waste heat by deeply dewatering flue gas, absorbing and capturing carbon dioxide by adsorbent, heating adsorbent by hot flue gas, and speeding up desorption and regeneration under negative pressure.
AU CHANG C
   YANG Y
   GAO T
   XU D
   SUN Z
   XU Y
AE GUODIAN NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY RES INST (CGUO-C)
GA 2021123104
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon capture method using flue gas waste heat comprises deeply dewatering flue gas, absorbing carbon dioxide and capturing by adsorbent until saturated, heating adsorbent by hot flue gas, speeding up desorption and regeneration under negative pressure to release carbon dioxide gas, cooling gas obtained by desorption of adsorbent is cooled, purifying, and collecting as carbon dioxide product gas after compression. The cooling is done using cooling medium.
   USE - The method is for carbon capture using flue gas waste heat.
   ADVANTAGE - The method greatly reduces water content in flue gas entering the carbon capture stage, improves efficiency of adsorbent regeneration while ensuring cycle life of adsorbent in the subsequent carbon capture stage, and effectively reduces cost of adsorbent regeneration and regeneration energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a flue gas waste heat utilization type carbon capture system comprising dewatering device, adsorption and regeneration device internally filled adsorbent, purifying device, and cooling device.

PN CN111425180-A; CN111425180-B
TI Prediction method of underground coal gasification coupled with carbon dioxide storage (UCG-CCS) overlying crack development height, involves completing prediction of development height of overlying rock fractures through model analysis.
AU XU Y
   GUO G
   LI H
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
GA 2020693777
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves constructing the isolated coal pillar secondary stripping model that is takes into account the high temperature effect during the gasification process and the long-term stability of the coal pillar. The stress distribution of underground coal gasification is analyzed. A mechanical model of the isolated coal pillar is established after the carbon dioxide storage. The analytic hierarchy process is provided to determine the weight of factors that is affected the development height of overlying rock fractures. The weights are provided to build a model of the development height of overburden fissures after carbon dioxide storage. The prediction of the development height of the overlying rock fractures is completed through the model analysis.
   USE - Prediction method of UCG-CCS overlying crack development height.
   ADVANTAGE - The complexity of the method is simplified and is effectively achieved the purpose of predicting the development height of overburden cracks after carbon dioxide storage.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the prediction method. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

PN CN210786860-U
TI Tail gas absorbing device for recycling carbon dioxide, has reflux pipeline mixer and circulation cooler that are provided with sodium carbonate solution outlet, where sodium carbonate solution outlet is connected with storage tank.
AU LIU Z
   LIU M
   CHEN G
   YANG J
   LI Z
   LIU X
AE YINGKOU YINGXIN CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (YING-Non-standard)
GA 202058426R
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a tail gas absorbing device for recycling carbon dioxide, belonging to the recovery and utilization field of tail gas. the device comprises a tail gas absorption tower, a reflux pipeline and storage tank. the tail gas absorbing tower is provided with two height is 3 meters of filling layer, so as to make carbon dioxide and sodium hydroxide solution to fully contact so as to reach ideal absorbing effect; the circulation cooler configuration can timely take away the reaction heat, so that carbon dioxide and the sodium hydroxide solution fully contact, improve the reaction efficiency, furthermore, by adding dilution water can ensure the reaction continuously, which avoids the generation of sodium carbonate precipitation caused by blockage of filling layer gap. The utility model can obviously reduce the energy consumption and improve the finished product yield; besides, the whole device has simple structure, low failure rate, energy saving and environmental protection.

PN KR2020043648-A; KR2117542-B1
TI Preparing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid involves preparing an ionic liquid by acid-base reaction of 2-furoic acid with an organic base, and then reacting an ionic liquid, a catalyst, and carbon dioxide in a reactor.
AU KIMYONGJIN
   TRUONGCONGCHIEN
   LEE H J
   CHO J K
AE KOREA INST IND TECHNOLOGY (KTEC-C)
GA 202035982B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid involves preparing an ionic liquid represented by (I) by acid-base reaction of 2-furoic acid with an organic base, and preparing a 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid represented by (II) by reacting an ionic liquid represented by (I), a catalyst, and carbon dioxide in a reactor.
   USE - Method for preparing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid, that directly reacts ionic liquid and carbon dioxide generated by an acid-base reaction of an organic base and 2-furic acid in a single container in a simple process, resulting in a high yield of 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid involves preparing an ionic liquid represented by formula (I) by acid-base reaction of 2-furoic acid with an organic base, and preparing a 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid represented by formula (II) by reacting an ionic liquid represented by formula (I), a catalyst, and carbon dioxide in a reactor.
   M=proton or alkali metal ion.
   .
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The catalyst is represented by linear formula of M12CO3 or M2Y', where M1 and M2 are the same or different from each other, and each independently an alkali metal, and Y' is pyrazolid ((Pyr)-), imidazolide ((Im)-), 1,2,4-triazolide ((4Triaz)-), 1,2,3-triazolide (( 3-Triaz)-), tetrazolide ((Tet)-), indolide ((Ind)-), benzotriazole ((Bentri)-) and 3-amino-1,2,4-triazole Lead ((3-AT)-). The method for producing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid is carried out in a solventless condition. The catalyst is 1.0-2.0 mmol based on 1 mmol of the ionic liquid. The reaction is performed at a temperature of 100-300 degrees C. The carbon dioxide is supplied at a pressure of 50-1,000 pound-force per square inch (psi). The stirring speed is 300-1,000 revolutions/minute. Preferred Components: The organic base is 1-alkylimidazole (1-alkylimidazole), choline (choline), 1,8-diazabicyclo (5,4,0) undec-7-ene (1,8)-diazabicyclo (5.4.0) undec-7-ene, trialkylpyridine or trialkylphosphine. The alkali metal is any one selected from lithium, sodium, potassium, rubidium and cesium.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI New spinel catalyst useful in preparing ethylene            by dehydrogenation of ethane in carbon dioxide            atmosphere.
AU XIE Q
   YUE Y
   GUO H
   HUA W
   GAO Z
AE UNIV FUDAN (UYFU-C)
GA 202034076D
AB    NOVELTY - Spinel catalyst i.e. magnesium aluminum spinel                doped with gallium oxide (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful in preparing ethylene by                dehydrogenation of ethane in carbon dioxide                atmosphere (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I): has excellent stability, high conversion                of ethane and excellent selectivity of ethylene, is                slow deactivate and easy to regenerate. The method:                is simple in process and utilizes easily available                raw materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Spinel catalyst i.e. magnesium aluminum spinel                doped with gallium oxide of formula                (MgGaxAl2-xO4)(I) is new.
   x = 0.5-2.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparation of (I); and
   (2) use of (I) in preparing ethylene by                dehydrogenation of ethane in carbon dioxide                atmosphere comprising (a) activating (I) in                nitrogen atmosphere at 500-700 degrees C for 1-3                hours, and (b) placing 1-5 mol.% ethane, 3-30 mol.%                carbon dioxide and nitrogen (balance amount) in a                fixed-bed reactor under normal pressure, reacting                in presence of activated (I) at 550-750 degrees C,                where the total flow rate of the raw material gas                is 10-50 ml/minute.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of                  (I), comprises (i) dissolving soluble inorganic                  salt as precursor in water or methanol, adding                  urea as precipitating agent, and stirring at room                  temperature for 30-150 minutes, (ii) placing the                  above solution reaction into a hydrothermal                  reactor and performing solvothermal                  co-precipitation treatment to obtain white solid,                  and (iii) washing, drying, and calcining in air                  atmosphere to obtain final product. Preferred                  Components: The soluble inorganic salt in step                  (i) is magnesium salt, gallium salt or aluminum                  salt. The magnesium salt is magnesium nitrate                  hexahydrate or magnesium acetate. The gallium                  salt is gallium nitrate or gallium chloride. The                  aluminum salt is aluminum nitrate nonahydrate or                  aluminum chloride. Preferred Method: The                  temperature of solvothermal co-precipitation                  treatment in step (ii) is 140-180 degrees C, and                  the solvothermal co-precipitation time is 8-15                  hours. In step (iii), the calcination is                  performed at 600-800 degrees C for 4-8                  hours.

PN CN110947384-A
TI Preparing copper-iron-based catalyst for            synthesizing low-carbon alcohol by carbon dioxide            hydrogenation of metal organic framework material as            precursor, by dissolving copper salt in methanol,            adding iron-based metal material, and drying.
AU WEN Y
   ZHANG W
   WANG B
   SHI J
   SONG R
   HUANG W
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 2020285905
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing copper-iron-based                catalyst for synthesizing low-carbon alcohol using                carbon dioxide hydrogenation of metal organic                framework material as precursor, involves (a)                dissolving soluble copper salt in methanol solvent                to obtain a solution (A), (b) adding iron-based                metal framework material to obtained solution (A),                uniformly stirring and dispersing to obtain a                suspension, and (d) stirring and steaming the                stirred suspension, drying and roasting the dried                solid to obtain the catalyst.
   USE - The method is used for preparing copper                iron-based catalyst for synthesizing low-carbon                alcohol using carbon dioxide hydrogenation of metal                organic framework material as precursor useful in                continuous fixed bed carbon dioxide hydrogenation                to prepare low-carbon alcohol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and economical, and the                prepared catalyst ensures 43.49% selectivity of                carbon ion alcohol in product and more than 95%                distribution of carbon ion alcohol in total                alcohol, and has excellent stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The soluble copper                  salt in step (a) comprises copper (II) nitrate                  trihydrate, copper (II) sulfate pentahydrate,                  copper (II) acetate monohydrate and/or copper(II)                  chloride dihydrate, where the concentration of                  copper in soluble copper salt is 5-100 %mass                  based on copper-iron-based catalyst. The                  iron-based metal framework material in step (b)                  comprises iron compound of formula: C24H4O13Fe3,                  C8H4O5Fe and C18H8O16Fe3. Preferred Conditions:                  The step (b) involves stirring at 15-35 degrees C                  for 12-48 hours. The step (c) involves stirring                  and drying at 70-90 degrees C for 1-1.5 hours,                  drying at 60-80 degrees C, and roasting at                  350-750 degrees C for 3-4 hours, where the                  roasting atmosphere is air or nitrogen.

AB    NOVELTY - Co-producing cyanamide solution and sodium carbonate comprises (i) calcining industrial sodium cyanide at high temperature under nitrogen flow, completing the reaction, cooling the mixture to room temperature under a nitrogen atmosphere, grinding the obtained sodium cyanamide into a powder, and collecting the generated carbon dioxide, and (ii) adding equivalent water into ethanol, passing through the collected carbon dioxide, adding the prepared sodium cyanamide in small amount, stirring the reaction, controlling the process temperature to 19-25 degrees C, the pH is less than 7.5, filtering cyanamide solution and sodium carbonate product, where the molar ratio of water, carbon dioxide and sodium cyanamide is 1-5:1:1.

PN CN109772129-A
TI Method for increasing carbon dioxide gas fertilizer, involves pumping filtered exhaust gas into purification device by using air pump, and exposing gas to microbubble through built-in aeration head to fully react with absorption liquid.
AU ZHU G
AE ZHU G (ZHUG-Individual)
GA 201947031Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves connecting industrial waste gas to the air inlet of the purification device, and installing the filter mesh in the air intake pipe. The circulating cold water pipe is arranged around the inlet pipe, and the exhaust gas is cooled by cold water to filter out solid dust. The filtered exhaust gas is pumped into the purification device by using an air pump, and the gas is exposed to the microbubble through the built-in aeration head to fully react with the absorption liquid. The obtained carbon dioxide gas fertilizer is exported from the gas outlet through the gas outlet into the gas delivery system for ransportation. The absorbing liquid is a solution with urea, sodium sulphate and calcium hydroxide.
   USE - Method for increasing carbon dioxide gas fertilizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can reduce the emission of industrial waste gas, and reuse the purified exhaust gas as carbon dioxide gas fertilizer, which is a very environmentally friendly projected.

PT P
PN CN109399610-A
TI Preparing biomass carbon quantum dots comprises selecting biomass materials, cutting, pyrolyzing, mixing biomass charcoal, performing ultrasonic treatment, injecting carbon dioxide, pressing, centrifuging, filtering and drying.
AU FENG Z
   WU T
   XIA N
   YAN Y
   PENG Y
AE NINGBO NOTTINGHAM NEW MATERIAL INST CO (NING-Non-standard)
   UNIV NOTTINGHAM NINGBO (UYNO-Non-standard)
GA 2019227052
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing biomass carbon quantum dots comprises (i) selecting biomass materials, cutting and grinding into biomass particles; (ii) obtaining elemental composition and pyrolysis characteristic parameters of the biomass particles; (iii) pyrolyzing the biomass particles according to pyrolysis characteristic parameters to obtain biomass charcoal; (iv) mixing biomass charcoal and polar solvent in an ultrasonic bath and performing ultrasonic treatment to obtain mixed solution; (v) injecting carbon dioxide into the reactor for emptying, preheating the reactor to a set temperature, introducing the ultrasonic treated mixed solution into the reactor, pressing carbon dioxide into the reactor to pressurize to a set pressure, set temperature and set pressure cause carbon dioxide to reach a supercritical state and opening the ultrasonic generator of the reactor for reaction; and (vi) collecting the residual carbon residue, centrifuging, collecting the supernatant, filtering and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing biomass carbon quantum dots.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is environment-friendly and economic; and avoids the use of expensive precursor, harsh chemicals and extreme operating conditions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The biomass raw material comprises angiosperm, gymnosperm, monocotyledon, true leaf plant, hardwood or softwood. In the step (iv), the polar solvent is an ethanol solution having a volume fraction of 40-60%. The concentration of biomass carbon in the mixed solution is 3-8 g/l. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), performing elemental analysis on the biomass particles to obtain elemental component information and performing thermogravimetric analysis on the biomass particles to obtain pyrolysis characteristic parameters. In the step (iii), the reaction temperature of the pyrolysis is 900-1200 degrees C. In the step (v), the set temperature of the reactor is 40-80 degrees C, the set pressure is 20-30 MPa, the power of the ultrasonic generator is 1.5-2.5 kw and the reaction is carried out in the reactor for 1.5-3 hours. In the step (vi) the rotation speed of the centrifugal treatment is 10000-20000 rotations/minute, centrifugal time is 1-2 hours and drying using a vacuum dryer at 50-80 degrees C for 6-12 hours.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN108853034-A
TI Orally disintegrating tablet comprises pioglitazone hydrochloride, microcrystalline cellulose, lactose, crospovidone, citric acid, and superfine silica gel powder, where pioglitazone is micronized by supercritical fluid crystallization.
AU HONG J
   LING R
   LIU G
AE HONZ PHARM CO LTD (HONZ-Non-standard)
GA 2018957409
AB    NOVELTY - Orally disintegrating tablet comprises 10 pts. wt. pioglitazone hydrochloride, 25-35 pts. wt. microcrystalline cellulose, 55-65 pts. wt. lactose, 5-15 pts. wt. crospovidone, 1-5 pts. wt. citric acid, and 0.5-3 pts. wt. superfine silica gel powder, where grain size of pioglitazone hydrochloride is less than 10 micrometers, and its preparation method adopts direct tabletting of dry powder.
   USE - Orally disintegrating tablet.
   ADVANTAGE - The orally disintegrating tablet has short disintegration time, accelerated dissolution speed, and high biological solubility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for carrying out supercritical fluid recrystallization micronization using pharmaceutical apparatus, which involves:
   (A) pumping carbon-dioxide through pipeline heater by pump;
   (B) heating to make it become supercritical carbon-dioxide to crystallization kettle;
   (C) using pump to input pioglitazone hydrochloride solution to above crystallizing kettle through pipeline;
   (D) mixing supercritical carbon-dioxide and pioglitazone hydrochloride solution in nozzle;
   (E) precipitating pioglitazone hydrochloride crystal;
   (F) stopping input of pioglitazone hydrochloride solution; and
   (G) continuing to wash and dry crystal with supercritical carbon-dioxide.

PN CN112191190-A
TI Supercritical fluid granulation processing of plant polyphenols involves adjusting extraction solution containing target polyphenol, filtering, performing supercritical fluid chromatography, introducing frozen carbon dioxide and settling.
AU HU Y
AE HU Y (HUYY-Individual)
GA 2021069225
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical fluid granulation processing of plant polyphenols involves adjusting the extraction solution containing target polyphenol to solution of 1-50 wt.% with a developing solvent, pouring the mixture into a separation membrane, filtering to remove insoluble impurities to obtain a filtrate containing desired polyphenol, separating and purifying filtrate by carbon dioxide supercritical fluid chromatography, dissolving resulting solution in anti-solvent to prepare a nearly saturated polyphenol solution, introducing frozen carbon dioxide using a high-pressure pump into a settling vessel at a constant flow rate, maintaining at 30-45 degrees C and 85-125 bar, setting carbon dioxide flow rate to 15-25 g/minutes, setting injection flow rate of polyphenol solution to 0.5-1.5 mL/minute, setting concentration of the polyphenol solution to 0.5-3.5 mg/mL, feeding the polyphenol solution to settling tank through the combined nano-granulation nozzle, and producing target polyphenol particles.
   USE - Supercritical fluid granulation processing of plant polyphenols.
   ADVANTAGE - The polyphenol product having uniform particle size and high purity can be produced. The clogging of the nozzle can be prevented.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Supercritical fluid granulation processing of plant polyphenols involves adjusting the extraction solution containing target polyphenol to a preparation solution of 1-50 wt.% with a developing solvent to obtain a polyphenol/developing solvent mixture, pouring the mixture into a separation membrane, filtering to remove insoluble impurities to obtain a filtrate containing desired polyphenol, separating and purifying filtrate by carbon dioxide supercritical fluid chromatography to obtain a high-purity target polyphenol purified solution, dissolving resulting solution in anti-solvent to prepare a nearly saturated polyphenol solution such that ratio of phospholipid, cholesterol and Tween 80 (RTM: nonionic surfactant) is 25:4:18, and ratio of solvent and lecithin is 50:46, introducing frozen carbon dioxide using a high-pressure pump into a settling vessel at a constant flow rate, maintaining at 30-45 degrees C and 85-125 bar, setting carbon dioxide flow rate to 15-25 g/minutes, setting injection flow rate of polyphenol solution to 0.5-1.5 mL/minute, setting concentration of the polyphenol solution to 0.5-3.5 mg/mL, feeding the polyphenol solution to settling tank through the combined nano-granulation nozzle, and producing target polyphenol particles in the settling container.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The developing solvent is ethanol or methanol. The molecular weight and/or polarity of phenol and other polyphenols are different. The target polyphenols have molecular weight of less than 6000. The large pore size separation membrane has molecular weight of more than 6000, and the small pore size separation membrane has molecular weight of less than 500. The chromatography is carried out using carbon dioxide/isopropanol in mass ratio of 85:15. The wall material is egg yolk lecithin. The anti-solvent is ethanol/dichloromethane, ethanol/dimethylsulfoxide or ethanol. The volume ratio of dichloromethane to ethanol is 1:3.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing calcium fluoride and potassium bicarbonate using carbon dioxide, comprises e.g. (i) reacting potassium fluoride compound with potassium-containing solution, carrying out solid-liquid separation, combining the filtrate and washing solution obtained by washing the filter cake to obtain crude potassium fluoride solution, and adding potassium-containing compound is potassium carbonate obtained in step (v) and additionally potassium hydroxide or potassium carbonate, (ii) adding fluorosilicic acid into step (i) product to adjust the pH to 7-8, filtering and separating to obtain potassium fluoride solution and silicon dioxide-fluorosilicate solid, and returning silicon dioxide-potassium fluosilicate solid to step (i), (iii) acidifying calcium hydroxide emulsion with carbon dioxide, and filtering to obtain calcium bicarbonate solution, (iv) reacting potassium fluoride solution obtained in step (ii) with calcium bicarbonate solution obtained in step (iii), and post processing.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing calcium fluoride and potassium bicarbonate using carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing calcium fluoride and potassium bicarbonate using carbon dioxide, comprises (i) reacting potassium fluoride compound (produced as by-product of wet phosphoric acid processing) with potassium-containing solution, carrying out solid-liquid separation, combining the filtrate and washing solution obtained by washing the filter cake to obtain crude potassium fluoride solution, and adding potassium-containing compound i.e. potassium carbonate obtained in step (v) and additionally potassium hydroxide or potassium carbonate, where the molar ratio between potassium-containing compound and potassium fluorosilicate in potassium fluoride compound is 2-2.1:1, (ii) adding fluorosilicic acid into step (i) product to adjust the pH to 7-8, filtering and separating to obtain potassium fluoride solution and silicon dioxide-fluorosilicate solid, and returning silicon dioxide-potassium fluosilicate solid to step (i), (iii) acidifying calcium hydroxide emulsion with carbon dioxide, and filtering to obtain calcium bicarbonate solution, (iv) reacting potassium fluoride solution obtained in step (ii) with calcium bicarbonate solution obtained in step (iii), carrying out solid-liquid separation, washing the filter cake, and drying to obtain calcium fluoride product, concentrating the filtrate, crystallizing, and drying to obtain potassium bicarbonate product, where the molar ratio between calcium bicarbonate and potassium fluoride is 1:1.8-2.2, and (v) evaporating the potassium bicarbonate mother liquor obtained in step (iv), calcining to obtain potassium carbonate, and returning to step (i).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i): the potassium fluoride compound is potassium fluorosilicate produced by by-product fluorosilicic acid, deposited in phosphoric acid storage tank, deposits in phosphoric acid transport pipeline are scaled to obtain defluoridation products produced by defluorination of dilute phosphoric acid and crystals in backwater pipe of phosphogypsum reservoir. In step (i), the reaction temperature is 20-95 degrees C, and the reaction time is 30-120 minutes. In step (ii), the reaction temperature is 10-40 degrees C. In step (iv), the reaction temperature is 10-50 degrees C, and the reaction time is 10-60 minutes. Preferred Components: In step (i), the amount of potassium compound-containing solution is 20-70 wt.%. In step (ii), the fluorosilicic acid is produced as by-product of phosphorus chemical industry. In step (iii): the mass concentration of calcium bicarbonate solution is 5-14%; and the carbon dioxide is synthetic ammonia industrial discharge of carbon dioxide.
