TI Producing a iron-manganese-platinum metal powder involves heating iron-manganese-platinum metal material and melting in a vacuum atomizing device to form a metal droplet, heating carbon dioxide gas in the heating tank by an electric hea.
AU CHEN J
   WU S
   LI X
   CHEN M
AE SHENZHEN JINGLAI NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2019016493
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a iron-manganese-platinum metal powder involves heating iron-manganese-platinum metal material and melting in a vacuum atomizing device to form a metal droplet, and iron-manganese-platinum metal material comprises 18-25 wt.% manganese, 0.5-4 wt.% aluminum, 0.6-1.5 wt.% carbon, 1-1.5 wt.% platinum, and iron. The carbon dioxide gas is heated in the heating tank by an electric heater, the carbon dioxide gas outlet temperature is between 100 and 400 degrees C, and the carbon dioxide gas is heated and homogenized in the gas mixing tank. The carbon dioxide gas from the gas mixing tank is pressurized to 4.5 to 6 MPa by a pressure pump. The inner diameter of the pipe that leans into the nozzle is reduced through the conversion head to a size that matches the nozzle. The carbon dioxide gas is sprayed through the head to crush the metal droplets of the iron-manganese-platinum metal material in the vacuum atomization device to obtain a metal powder.
   USE - Method for producing a iron-manganese-platinum metal powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce a iron-manganese-platinum metal powder, where nitrogen content of the iron-manganese-platinum metal powder produced is greatly reduced, and the strength and shape of the three dimensional printed portion are improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device producing iron-manganese-platinum metal powder, which comprises a vacuum atomizing device and a heating tank, where the heating tank has a built-in resistance wire, and the vacuum atomizing device and the heating tank further comprise a gas mixing tank, gas mixing tank is connected to the heating tank through a second pipe, and the gas mixing tank is connected to the vacuum atomizing device through a third pipe, pressure pump is disposed on the third pipe, the third pipe extends into the vacuum atomization device, and a nozzle is disposed at a third pipe port located in the vacuum atomization device, spray head is connected to the third pipe through a conversion head, and the heating tank is further connected to the carbon dioxide gas passage device through the first pipe, and the first pipe is provided with a valve.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Composition: The iron-manganese-platinum metal material comprises 19 wt.% manganese, 1 wt.% aluminum, 1.5 wt.% carbon, 1.2 wt.% platinum, and iron. Preferred Condition: The slab of the iron-manganese-platinum metal material having a cross-sectional area of 70x 120 mm is formed into a 20x 30x 30 mm square by sawing. The carbon dioxide gas is from a limestone calcination rotary kiln, and the exhaust gas in the rotary kiln is purified to obtain carbon dioxide gas having a purity of 99 wt.% or more. The electric heater is a resistance wire whose temperature is controlled at 400 degrees C. The metal powder has a particle size of less than 20 mu m. The metal powder has an average nitrogen content of 0.008 wt.%.

An air pump (51) is located inside the housing and turned on or off in response to power application. An inlet pipe (52) is connected to both the inlet port and the gas sensing module to guide the air. A joint pipe (53) is connected to both the gas sensing module and the air pump to guide the air having passed through the gas sensing module to the air pump side. It is possible to easily replace only one filter member in a state after a certain period of time has elapsed after use. Since the inflow port and the inflow port provided at one side of the housing are directed to the bottom, water is not directly introduced into the filter member through the inflow port and the outflow port, the carbon dioxide and the temperature and humidity of the mushroom cultivation site can be accurately measured and thus improving the mushroom cultivation environment.

TI Concrete element obtained by mixing portland cement clinker containing cement made without adding sulfate carrier with water to form wet mixture, and carbonation hardening of green element to provide concrete element.
AU BOLTE G
   ZAJAC M
   SKOCEK J
   BEN HAHA M
AE HEIDELBERGCEMENT AG (HEID-Non-standard)
GA 2022634967
AB    NOVELTY - Concrete element is obtained from a portland cement clinker containing cement made without adding a sulfate carrier by mixing the cement with water to form a wet mixture without adding sulfate, hydration of the wet mixture until at least 5 wt.% of calcium aluminate, calcium ferrite and calcium aluminated ferrite phases contained in the cement are hydrated to provide a green element, and carbonation hardening of the green element to provide the concrete element. The cement is composite cement containing at least one supplementary cementitious material and/or limestone. The cement contains 6-35 wt.% limestone. The retarder comprises phosphates, borates, salts of lead, zinc, copper, arsenic, antimony, lignosulfonates, hydroxycarboxylic acid and their salts, phosphonates, an/or sugars (saccharides).
   USE - The concrete element is useful for sequestering carbon dioxide, where the carbon dioxide is carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide oxide, carbon sulfide oxide, and carbon dioxide particulate solvents.
   ADVANTAGE - The concrete elements have high strength and better surface properties compared to elements made by hydration hardening, and are obtainable by mixing a portland cement clinker containing cement made without adding sulfate carrier with water to form a wet mixture without adding a sulfate, hydration of the wet mixture until at least 5 wt.% of calcium aluminate, calcium ferrite and calcium alumination ferrite phases contained in the cement are hydrated to provide a green element, and carbonation hardening of the green element to provide the concrete element. The method provides the concrete elements with reduced environmental footprint, especially carbon dioxide emission.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) method for manufacturing concrete elements comprising manufacturing a portland cement clinker containing cement without added sulfate carrier; and
   (2) use of a portland cement clinker containing cement made without a sulfate carrier for sequestering carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows the production method of a concrete element in the precast concrete plant that is hardened by carbonation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cement further contains other mixtures than retarders, preferably water reducing agents, plasticizers, super plasticizers, air entraining agents, and/or hydrophobic agents, and/or additives, preferably fillers, pigments, polymers, and/or fibers. The wet mixture has a water:cement weight ratio of 0.1-1, preferably 0.2-0.35, where a contained supplementary cementitious material and/or filler is included in the cement weight for calculation. The amount of carbonates, preferably calcites, from carbonation in the concrete element is 5-100 wt.%, preferably 40-60 wt.%. Preferred Method: The hydration proceeds until at least 10 wt.%, preferably at least 15 wt.% calcium aluminate, calcium ferrite and calcium aluminate ferrite phases are hydrated, typically for 60-300 minutes, preferably 10-120 minutes and/or until a strength of 0.5 MPa, preferably 2 MPa is achieved.

PN CN114353279-A
TI Method for controlling fresh air-type air conditioner, involves judging whether carbon dioxide concentration is greater than or equal to first threshold value, and maintaining current running state of air-conditioner if not.
AU LU J
   ZHANG S
   LIU H
   LI J
   WANG Y
AE GREE ELECTRIC APPLIANCES INC (GREZ-C)
GA 202258056R
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining carbon dioxide concentration and carbon dioxide change rate at a current time under an air conditioner opening temperature adjusting mode. Determination is made to check whether a change rate of the carbon dioxide is greater than or equal to a rate change threshold. The first concentration threshold value is determined to be greater than a second concentration threshold. A fresh air mode is started to supply air by a second air amount. A current running state of the air conditioning mode is maintained. The current operating state is maintained, where the first air amount is larger than the second air volume.
   USE - Method for controlling a fresh air conditioner (Claimed) i.e. fresh air-type air-conditioner, in a room.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables effectively preventing the working of the temperature adjusting mode affecting the fresh air of the air conditioner, thus reducing the power consumption of the conditioner. The method allows the air amount to reach the second air supply time, thus maintaining the current running state of the control device, and hence reducing the energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a temperature control device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of fresh air conditioner control method. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Judging whether the carbon dioxide concentration is greater than or equal to the first concentration threshold value (300)
   Opening the fresh air mode to the first air quantity to supply air, and closing the temperature adjusting mode (310)
   Opening the fresh air mode to the first air quantity to supply air, at the same time, keeping the temperature adjusting mode. (320)
   Judging whether the carbon dioxide concentration is greater than or equal to the second concentration threshold value (400)
   Opening the fresh air mode to the second air quantity to supply air (410)
   Maintaining the current running state of the air conditioner (420)

PN US2022077336-A1
TI Device used to convert carbon dioxide into valuable hydrocarbon products, comprises photovoltaic device capable of generating current at potentials, first catalyst in electronic communication with photovoltaic device and second catalyst.
AU KONG C J
   WARREN E L
   AGER J W
   TAMBOLI A C
AE UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
   ALLIANCE SUSTAINABLE ENERGY LLC (ALLU-C)
GA 202234713F
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises: a photovoltaic device (100) capable of generating current at many potentials; a first catalyst (125) in electronic communication with the photovoltaic device; and a second catalyst in electronic communication with the photovoltaic device. The photovoltaic device comprises a photoelectrode and a transparent conductive encapsulant (TCE) layer which further comprises a polymer, many microspheres of the first catalyst and many microspheres of the second catalyst. The first catalyst comprises silver or gold. The second catalyst comprises copper. The first reaction is the reduction of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide or a first hydrocarbon and the second reaction (135) is the reduction of carbon monoxide to a second hydrocarbon. The first hydrocarbon or the second hydrocarbon comprises ethylene. The device further comprises many additional catalysts, where the photovoltaic device is capable of generating a current at a potential for each catalyst within the device.
   USE - The device is useful for converting carbon dioxide into valuable hydrocarbon products (claimed) and promoting carbon dioxide reduction reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: improves the selectivity of solar-driven carbon dioxide reduction; are tunable and may be used in conjunction with different reactants and products; utilizes light that allows use of solar irradiation, but also may offer advantages if sequences of monochromatic light can be used to sequentially activate different reaction steps; are intended to be light-driven, and while the light source could be the sun, there may be advantages to using LEDs or other tunable light sources to get the ideal spectral conditions to drive reactions at the same rates; and utilizes single catalyst type and a single voltage that are used to drive a reaction, which limits product selectivity and reaction efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for converting carbon dioxide into valuable hydrocarbon products.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure illustrates two junction three terminal solar cell structure of the device.
   Photovoltaic device (100)
   Catalyst (120)
   First reaction (125)
   Second reaction (135)

An anode catalyst layer is fixed between an anode chamber plate (9) and an anode current collection plate (10) corresponding to the desulphurization wastewater reaction chamber. A light source and a double-light pole photo-electrochemical battery main case are arranged opposite to each other. The cathode catalytic layer is located on the carbon-dioxide inlet side for hydrophobic treatment, so that the cabon-dioxide gas is transferred to the catalyst/solution interface to react, while keeping good sealing. The external input energy is reduced by using semiconductor photocatalyst to capture solar energy, and electro-catalysis.

PT P
PN US2021095385-A1; WO2021067123-A1
TI Fabricating working electrode for reduction of carbon dioxide, involves depositing gold film over shrinkable polymer to create layered structure, and heating layered structure at temperature sufficient to cause shrinking of polymer.
AU GEREIGE I
   JUNG H
   CHO K M
   JUNG W
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
   KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 202134165X
AB    NOVELTY - Fabricating working electrode involves depositing gold film (Au) over shrinkable polymer to create layered structure, heating layered structure at temperature sufficient to cause shrinking of polymer, and removing polymer layer after shrinking. The heating creates contracted wrinkled Au film surface owing to difference in thermal coefficient between Au film and underlaying polymer prior to removal of polymer. The wrinkled film contains multiple c-shaped wrinkles having confined spaces adapted to provide elevated localized pH level.
   USE - Method for fabricating working electrode used for reduction of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to enhance selectivity and Faradaic efficiency, improves catalytic activity and selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   1. a method for reducing carbon dioxide, which involves:
   a. constructing electrochemical cell containing electrolyte, counter electrode and working electrode formed as wrinkled Au film containing multiple c-shaped wrinkles in c-shaped wrinkles containing confined spaces adapted to provide elevated localized elevated pH level;
   b. applying potential difference across working electrode and counter electrode ranging between -0.25-(-)0.65 Volts, where potential difference induces carbon dioxide reduction reaction at working electrode and to cause pH level within confined spaces of multiple c-wrinkles to become elevated with respect to surrounding pH level;
   2. a system for reducing carbon dioxide, which comprises electrolyte, counter electrode in contact with electrolyte, working electrode also in contact with electrolyte, working electrode formed as wrinkled Au film containing multiple c-shaped wrinkles in c-shaped wrinkles containing confined spaces adapted to provide elevated localized pH level, and voltage source coupled to counter electrode and working electrode and adapted to generate potential difference ranging between -0.25-(-)0.65 volts, which induces carbon dioxide reduction reaction at working electrode and to cause pH level within confined spaces of multiple c-wrinkles to become elevated with respect to surrounding pH level.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The period of heating is controlled to tune areal strain (epsilon=(A0-Af)A0)), where A0 is total area of Au film prior to wrinkling and Af is total area of Au film after contraction. The Au film is formed over polymer at thickness level to set desired average depth of multiple c-shaped wrinkles. The Au film is formed at thickness ranging from 75-100 nanometer. The needle nanostructures are formed from Au on and within multiple c-shaped wrinkles of Au film. The additional sacrificial layer is formed to increase depths of confined spaces in multiple c-shaped wrinkles, and removed sacrificial layer.Preferred Conditions: The heating is set to produce areal strain of 0.5-0.75. The additional sacrificial is formed at 4-8 wt.% of layered structure. The multiple c-shaped wrinkles have average depth range from 1.8-4.2 mum. The working electrode has Faraday efficiency for reducing carbon dioxide of 65 percent. The working electrode induces current density for carbon dioxide reduction reaction of 0.05 mA/cm2. The Au film of working electrode further includes multiple needle nanostructures. The multiple nanostructure has length ranging from 700-900 nanometer. The working electrode with added needle nanostructures induces current density of carbon dioxide reduction reaction of 0.45 mA/cm2.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN112502803-A; CN214616695-U
TI Method of fully using renewable energy for achieving zero carbon emission power generation comprises e.g. converting renewable energy power into liquid oxygen storage during the low period of renewable energy.
AU XIANG W
   JIA Y
   HU Q
AE CHONGQING FURAN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 202128558G
AB    NOVELTY - Method of fully using renewable energy for achieving zero carbon emission power generation comprises e.g. converting renewable energy power into liquid oxygen storage during the low period of renewable energy, using renewable energy power to obtain cryogenic liquid oxygen, providing the insufficient power of the overall grid by thermal power generation, converting the stored liquid oxygen into gaseous oxygen, mixing gaseous oxygen and carbon dioxide, carrying out combustion with fossil or biomass fuels for energy conversion and power generation to obtain high concentration of carbon dioxide flue gas, and directly collecting, storing and using high-concentration carbon dioxide flue gas, realizing zero-carbon emission power generation in the entire thermal power generation field, reducing or shutting down thermal power generation during the peak and valley period of renewable energy, and full using renewable energy power through thermal power generation peak shaving.
   USE - The method is useful for method of fully using renewable energy for achieving zero carbon emission power generation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieves zero carbon emissions in the power generation sector.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method of fully using renewable energy for achieving zero carbon emission power generation comprises converting renewable energy power into liquid oxygen storage during the low period of renewable energy, using renewable energy power to obtain cryogenic liquid oxygen, providing the insufficient power of the overall grid by thermal power generation, converting the stored liquid oxygen into gaseous oxygen, mixing gaseous oxygen and carbon dioxide, carrying out combustion with fossil or biomass fuels for energy conversion and power generation to obtain high concentration of carbon dioxide flue gas, and directly collecting, storing and using high-concentration carbon dioxide flue gas, realizing zero-carbon emission power generation in the entire thermal power generation field, reducing or shutting down thermal power generation during the peak and valley period of renewable energy, full using renewable energy power through thermal power generation peak shaving, and achieving zero carbon emissions in the power generation sector.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises using renewable energy power for producing and storing cryogenic liquid oxygen, using liquid oxygen for thermal combustion and wide-load power generation, efficiently consuming electricity from renewable energy sources, and solding to other areas for use. In the process of thermal combustion power generation, where the mixing ratio of carbon dioxide and gas oxygen generally corresponds to the mixing ratio of nitrogen and oxygen in the air, mixing fossil or biomass fuels with air for energy conversion and power generation, making the flue gas produced in thermal combustion power generation high-concentration carbon dioxide flue gas, and achieving the purpose of directly collecting and storing carbon dioxide without capturing. The carbon dioxide mixed with gaseous oxygen comes from part of the high-concentration carbon dioxide generated by thermal combustion power generation, and the other part of the stored high-concentration carbon dioxide can be sold and used in other fields.

GA 201933083F
AB    NOVELTY - The fiber diffuser module e.g. separation film diffuser module (700) has multiple fiber yarns on which microorganisms are adhered and grown. An inlet is provided to supply a gas to one side of the fiber yarns. The gas is comprised of oxygen (121) and carbon dioxide (122), and the nitrite is produced by the oxygen. The oxygen concentration of the gas is controlled by the oxygen and the carbon dioxide.
   USE - Fiber diffuser module of reduction nitrogen removal apparatus (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The 100% of the supplied oxygen is utilized to drastically reduce the blowing energy required for oxygen supply by applying biofilm module to simultaneously perform biofilm formation and oxygen supply of the nitrite microorganisms. The removal rate of pollutants in the exhaust gas is maintained at a high level by appropriately mixing air, oxygen, and carbon dioxide to adjust the amount of gas required to mix oxygen to supply oxygen required for nitrification and the amount of gas required for mixing. The amount of gas required to maintain dissolved oxygen concentration is ensured by growing carbon dioxide, and the biofilm is efficiently removed without increasing the amount of oxygen, so that the dissolved oxygen concentration in the reaction tank is not increased. The nitrogen removal reaction is stably maintained. The nitrification is stably maintained by using nitrite-oxidizing microorganism inhibiting system. The maintenance cost is reduced by controlling the ratio of nitrogen dioxide and ammonia introduced into Anamox reaction tank.

PN WO2018180708-A1; JP2019509351-X; CN110461397-A; KR2019130136-A; EP3603719-A1; EP3603719-A4; US2021100967-A1; JP6891267-B2; CN110461397-B
TI Oxygen inhalation therapy system for treating e.g. chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, has display unit that is provided to display ratio of time for blood oxygen levels or arterial carbon dioxide pressure (PaCO2) of user.
AU RYO K
   XIAO Q
   KOIZUMI R
AE TEIJIN PHARMA LTD (TEIJ-C)
   TEIJIN PHARMA CO LTD (TEIJ-C)
   TEIJIN PHARMA LTD (TEIJ-C)
   TEIJIN PHARMA LTD (TEIJ-C)
   TEIJIN PHARMA LTD (TEIJ-C)
GA 201877604G
AB    NOVELTY - The oxygen inhalation therapy system acquires flow rate value of oxygen gas with respect to elapsed time and the blood-oxygen-levels value or the arterial-carbon-dioxide-pressure (PaCO2) value of user from an oxygen supply apparatus which controls the flow volume of oxygen gas for inhalation based on user's blood oxygen levels or arterial carbon dioxide pressure (PaCO2) falls in prescribed range. A storage unit (31) is provided to receive and store the data of the blood-oxygen-levels value of user, or an arterial-carbon-dioxide-pressure (PaCO2) value. A calculating unit (32) is provided to compute the ratio of time for the blood oxygen levels or arterial carbon dioxide pressure (PaCO2) of user to exist in the prescribed range for every flow volume of the oxygen gas about the data of predetermined time. A display unit is provided to display the ratio of the time computed by the calculating unit.
   USE - Oxygen inhalation therapy system for treating chronic respiratory disease such as chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, pulmonary-tuberculosis aftereffect, and pulmonary fibrosis.
   ADVANTAGE - Since the effect for every flow volume of oxygen gas is displayed using the data in which the flow volume of oxygen gas is fluctuated, the flow volume of the oxygen gas of the patient who receives a domiciliary oxygen therapy is prescribed efficiently. The biometric information such as blood oxygen levels of the patient who receives the domiciliary oxygen therapy, and arterial carbon dioxide pressure is acquired. The information regarding the effect for every flow volume of oxygen gas is presented easily.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the oxygen-therapy system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Communication network (1)
   Oxygen supply apparatus (2)
   Data server (3)
   Storage unit (31)
   Calculating unit (32)
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); P34 (Sterilising, syringes, electrotherapy (A61L, M, N).); S05 (Electrical Medical Equipment)

PT P
PN CN108554411-A; CN108554411-B
TI Composite carrier-supported nickel-based catalyst            for reforming methane to synthesis gas by synthesizing            carbon dioxide comprises silicon dioxide, aluminum            oxide and titanium dioxide.
AU LIU Z
   XIAO Y
   SHI X
   LI T
   SONG Y
AE UNIV SHAANXI NORMAL (UNSN-C)
GA 2018780652
AB    NOVELTY - Composite carrier-supported nickel-based                catalyst for reforming methane to synthesis gas by                synthesizing carbon dioxide comprises silicon                dioxide, aluminum oxide and titanium dioxide, where                the active component is nickel, nickel-iron or                nickel-cobalt, the content of the active component                is 5-20% based on 100% by mass of the catalyst and                carrier.
   USE - Used as composite carrier-supported                nickel-based catalyst for reforming methane to                synthesis gas by synthesizing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high methane and carbon                dioxide conversion to carbon dioxide reformed                methane under pressurized condition and exhibits                high activity, high stability and high carbon and                anti-sintering ability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Composite carrier-supported nickel-based                catalyst for reforming methane to syngas by                synthesizing carbon dioxide comprises silicon                dioxide, aluminum oxide and titanium dioxide, where                the active component is nickel, nickel-iron or                nickel-cobalt, the content of the active component                is 5-20% based on 100% by mass of the catalyst and                carrier (remaining amount), the catalyst is                prepared by dissolving the active component                precursor and the carrier precursor in ethanol                according to the composition of the catalyst and                adding an aqueous solution of the                coordination-combustion agent, stirring at room                temperature for 2-4 hours and evaporating the                solvent to obtain viscous liquid and heating and                burning, uniformly polishing solid powder obtained                by the combustion and calcining in an air                atmosphere at 550-800 degrees C for 3-6 hours,                naturally cooling to room temperature, tableting,                granulating and passed through 40-60 meshes sieve                to obtain a catalyst, the coordination-combustion                agent is glycine, alanine, threonine, serine,                ethylenediamine, citric acid, urea, trimesic acid,                nicotinic acid and/or oxalic acid, the amount is                0.5-2 times the total molar amount of the metal                element in the precursor of the active component                and the metal element and the metalloid element in                the carrier precursor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amount of active                  component is 10% based on 100% by mass of the                  catalyst. The coordination-combustion agent is                  glycine, alanine and/or threonine. The amount of                  coordination oxidant is 1.0 to 1.5 times the                  total amount of metal elements and metalloid                  elements in the precursor of the active component                  and the carrier precursor. When the active                  component is nickel, the precursor of the active                  component is nickel nitrate or nickel oxalate,                  when the active component is nickel-iron, the                  active component precursor is nickel nitrate and                  nickel oxalate and/or ferric nitrate, iron                  oxalate and ammonium ferric citrate, when the                  active component is nickel-cobalt, the precursor                  of the active component is nickel nitrate and/or                  nickel oxalate with cobalt nitrate, cobalt                  acetylacetonate or oxalic acid cobalt. The                  carrier precursor is silicon dioxide precursor,                  an aluminum oxide precursor, and titaniu dioxide                  precursor, where the silicon dioxide precursor is                  methyl silicate, ethyl orthosilicate, propyl                  silicate and/or butyl silicate, in case the                  precursor of aluminum oxide is aluminum                  isopropoxide, aluminum nitrate and/or                  pseudoboehmite, and the precursor of titanium                  dioxide is tetrabutyl titanate or titanium                  isopropoxide. The calcined in an air atmosphere                  temperature is 650-750 degrees C for 3-6                  hours.

PN CN108311154-A
TI Nickel-based polymetallic catalyst useful for e.g.            carbon dioxide methanation and in producing ammonia,            diving instrument comprises a carrier, an active            component and an auxiliary agent.
AU LIANG Z
   HU X
   JIA P
AE UNIV JINAN SHANDONG (UNJN-C)
GA 2018598788
AB    NOVELTY - Nickel-based polymetallic catalyst comprises a                carrier, an active component and an auxiliary                agent, the catalyst is prepared by equal volume                impregnation, the carrier is alumina, the active                component is a transition metal nickel, the                auxiliary agent is alkali metal, an alkaline earth                metal, a transition metal, and/or a rare earth                element.
   USE - The nickel-based catalyst is useful for carbon                dioxide methanation (claimed), and in producing                ammonia, diving instrument, spacecraft indoor                carbon dioxide removing and oxygen regenerating                process.
   ADVANTAGE - The nickel-based catalyst: realizes energy                conservation; and is environmentally                friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing carbon-based                methanation-modified nickel-based catalyst                comprising (i) providing raw material, dissolving                auxiliary material precursor in deionized water,                stirring using magnetic stirrer until precursor is                completely dissolved, adding alumina, soaking,                stirring, allowing to stand at room temperature for                12 hours, (ii) drying catalyst semi-finished                product in a drying box at 120 degrees C for 12                hours, placing into the muffle furnace, heating at                20 degrees C/minute speed to 600 degrees C for 4                hours in air atmosphere, (iii) dissolving active                component of nickel nitrate in deionized water,                stirring using magnetic stirrer until the nitrate                is completely dissolved, adding sample obtained in                the step (ii), immersing, stirring, allowing to                stand at room temperature for 12 hours, and                repeating step (ii) to obtain catalyst; and
   (2) use method of a catalyst for carbon                dioxide methanation comprising providing 0.5 g                nickel-X-aluminum oxide (X is auxiliary agent)                catalyst, placing in fixed bed reactor, passing                hydrogen at 60 ml/minute flow rate, reducing at 600                degrees C for 1 hour, cooling to room temperature,                feeding raw material gas of (hydrogen/carbon                dioxide = 4 (Vol./Vol.)) at 16000/hour airspeed,                and performing hydrogenation of carbon dioxide                methanation at 300-600 degrees C to obtain methane                as reaction product and carbon monoxide as                by-product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises                  a20 wt.% active component, 5 wt.% auxiliary agent                  and carrier (balance). The active component                  precursor is nickel nitrate. The auxiliary agent                  is cobalt, chromium, vanadium, niobium, tantalum                  and/or niobium. The active component and the                  auxiliary agent of the catalyst are supported on                  granulated gamma -aluminum oxide carrier having                  large specific surface area and mechanical                  strength.

TI Improving straw biogas output for improving biogas            production and biogas rate from straw, involves soaking            pretreatment with dry yellow straw as raw material,            producing biogas by anaerobic digestion, using carbon            dioxide micro-nano bubble water for soaking            pretreatment, and anaerobic digestion.
AU DONG R
   LI X
   SUN H
   WANG E
   GUO J
AE UNIV CHINA AGRICULTURAL (UCAG-C)
GA 2022C49342
AB    NOVELTY - Improving straw biogas output involves soaking                pretreatment with dry yellow straw as raw material,                producing biogas by anaerobic digestion. Carbon                dioxide micro-nano bubbled water is used as the                additive for soaking pretreatment and anaerobic                digestion.
   USE - Method for improving straw biogas output for                improving biogas production and biogas rate from                straw.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables using carbon dioxide                micro-nano bubble water soaking pretreatment and                anaerobic digestion regulation combined treatment,                the increase rate of unit cumulative methane                production is more than 21.4%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step of soaking                  pretreatment involves cutting dry yellow straw                  into pieces, mixing and sealing the cut straw                  with carbon dioxide micro-nano bubble water,                  soaking and pretreatment under anaerobic                  conditions to obtain a straw soaking liquid. The                  step of anaerobic digestion involves transferring                  the whole amount of the soaking liquid of the                  straw after the soaking treatment into an                  anaerobic digestion reactor, adding inoculum and                  carbon dioxide micro-nano bubble water, and                  sealing the product.Preferred Condition: The mass ratio of                  sheared straw and carbon dioxide micro-nano                  bubble water is 1:(5-20). The soaking                  pretreatment is 20-40 &#8451;, the soaking                  treatment time is 3-5 days. The length of the                  chopped straw is 1-3 cm. The ratio of the                  inoculum to the dry yellow straw is 1:2-2:1 in                  terms of volatile solid mass. The anaerobic                  digestion is mesophilic fermentation or high                  temperature fermentation.

AU DIORIO C
   SCHAIBLE D
AE PHARMACANNIS LABS LLC (PHAR-Non-standard)
GA 2022312715
AB    NOVELTY - Self-emulsifying drug delivery system (SEDDS)                in the form of a free-flowing, compressible                effervescent powder, comprises a                cannabinoid-containing oil and a surfactant                adsorbed onto an acid source and a carbon dioxide                source, where the acid source and carbon dioxide                source are in powder form.
   USE - Self-emulsifying drug delivery system in form                of free-flowing, compressible effervescent                powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The self-emulsifying drug delivery system has                enhanced bioavailability with reduced or no                psychotropic side effects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an                effervescent tablet which comprises the SEDDS                powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The surfactant is                  Vitamin E d-alpha-Tocopheryl polyethylene glycol                  succinate (TPGS) and where the SEDDS powder does                  not comprise any other surfactants. The acid                  source is selected from citric acid, tartaric                  acid and ascorbic acid citric acid; and where the                  carbon dioxide source is selected from sodium                  bicarbonate, sodium carbonate, potassium                  carbonate, potassium carbonate and calcium                  carbonate. The SEDDS powder further comprises                  colloidal silicon dioxide, a compressible sugar.                  The compressible sugar is compressible sucrose.                  The SEDDS powder further comprises one or more of                  a flavoring agent and lubricant. The flavoring                  agent is lemon or orange oil and where the                  lubricant is sodium stearyl fumarate. The                  cannabinoid-containing oil comprises cannabinoid                  selected from tetrahydrocannabinol (THC),                  cannabidiol (CBD), cannabigerol (CBG),                  cannabichromene (CBC),                  Delta9-tetrahydrocannabivarin (Delta9-THCV),                  cannabidivarin (CBDV),                  Delta9-tetrahydrocannabinolic acid (Delta9-THCA),                  and cannabidiolic acid (CBDA). The cannabinoid is                  one or more of THC and CBD. The acid source                  comprises citric acid.The carbon dioxide source                  comprises sodium bicarbonate. The THC is present                  in the tablet in an amount 1-100 mg, preferably                  25 mg. The CBD is present in the tablet at a                  concentration of 1-100 mg, preferably 25 mg. The                  effervescent tablet comprises 15-25 wt.%                  compressible sucrose, 0.15 0.35 wt.% colloidal                  silicon dioxide, 30- 50 wt.% sodium                  bicarbonate/carbonate, 25-35 wt.% citric acid,                  0.15-0.5 wt.% Vitamin E TPGS and 0.75-2.25 wt.%                  sodium stearyl fumarate. The effervescent tablet                  comprises 0.1-0.5% flavoring agent. The                  effervescent tablet comprises 5 mg THC and 5 mg                  CBD.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of chemical or                biological entities or preparations that do not                support the claims.

TI Preparing supercritical carbon dioxide thickening            agent useful in oil field fracturing fluid, comprises            polymerizing hydrogen cyanide ester, acrylate compound            and styrene as polymerization monomers under action of            initiator and emulsifying agent.
AU LI Y
   OU Y
   LIU M
   ZHANG Y
AE SHAANXI BANGXI CHEM CO LTD (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 2021B8839A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing supercritical carbon dioxide                thickening agent comprises polymerizing hydrogen                cyanide ester, acrylate compound and styrene as                polymerization monomers under the action of an                initiator and an emulsifying agent. The content of                the hydrogen fluoride cyanate is 55-65% of the                total molar amount of polymerized monomers, the                acrylate compound is 8-12%, and styrene is                25-30%.
   USE - The agent is useful in oil field fracturing                fluid (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The agent has good thickening effect on                supercritical carbon dioxide, and has good                temperature and pressure resistance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The fluorinated                  hydrogen cyanate is perfluorotrimethylene                  diisocyanate, and the perfluorotrimethylene                  diisocyanate is prepared by the degradation                  reaction of perfluoroglutaryl chloride azide. The                  acrylate compound is 2-(dimethylamino)ethyl                  methacrylate, ethylene glycol dimethacrylate,                  bisphenol A acrylate, 2-hydroxyethyl acrylate                  and/or 2-hydroxypropyl acrylate. The initiator is                  azodiisobutyronitrile, azo-bis-iso-heptonitrile,                  dibenzoylperoxide, potassium persulfate and/or                  ammoniumpersulfate. The emulsifying agent is                  sodium dodecyl sulfate, stearate, oleate, Span,                  Tween, sucrose stearate and/or benzze.Preferred Method: The method includes                  reacting the components under stirring                  conditions, and the reaction conditions include:                  a temperature of 80-100degreesC and a time of 4-8                  hours.

PN WO2021177722-A1; KR2021112652-A
TI Air purifier useful in photosynthesis of microalgae for reducing carbon dioxide and generating oxygen, comprises porous beads containing microalgae, water tank accommodating beads and water, pump for supplying external air to water tank, and light emitting device irradiating light to water tank.
AU PARK S
   SON J
   PARK S Y
AE LG ELECTRONICS INC (GLDS-C)
GA 2021A3729B
AB    NOVELTY - Air purifier comprises porous beads containing microalgae, a water tank accommodating the beads and water, a pump for supplying external air to the water tank, and a light emitting device irradiating light to the water tank.
   USE - The air purifier is useful in photosynthesis of microalgae for reducing carbon dioxide and generating oxygen.
   ADVANTAGE - The air purifier reduces concentration of carbon dioxide, increases concentration of oxygen, and does not allow microalgae to float in water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Device: The apparatus further comprises a porous diffuser arranged inside the water tank and supplying the air discharged from the pump to the water tank. The water tank comprises a cylinder forming a side wall of the water tank, and a connector coupled to the cylinder at the lower side of the cylinder, where the connector comprises an inlet port connected to the pump, an inlet hole contacting with the inlet port, and at least one portion of the diffuser being inserted into the inlet hole. The diffuser comprises a neck inserted into the inlet hole, and a porous head arranged on the upper side of the neck. The water tank further comprises a sealant sealing between the cylinder and the connector. The apparatus comprises several water tanks arranged outside the light emitting device. The apparatus further comprises a distributor for guiding the air discharged from the pump to several water tanks. The apparatus further comprises a base arranged below the water tank. The pump is arranged inside the base, and the base comprises a suction grill through which air is introduced. The water tank comprises an inlet hole formed in the bottom of the water tank, and an inlet port extending downward from the periphery of the inlet hole, where the base comprises a hole through which the inlet port is inserted on an upper surface of the base. The base comprises a top plate forming the upper surface, and a seating surface recessed downward from the top plate to support a lower portion of the water tank. The apparatus further comprises a cover arranged on an upper side of the water tank, where the water tank has an upper side opened, and the cover comprises a discharge grill arranged at a position corresponding to the water tank. The apparatus further comprises a check valve arranged between the pump and the water tank, where the check valve is opened when the pump is operated and closed when the pump is stopped. The apparatus further comprises a control unit for controlling operation of the pump and lighting of the light emitting device, where the control unit turns on the light emitting device when the pump is operated. Preferred Components: The bead has a diameter of 1-5 mm.

The seeds are crushed to fraction of 0.25-0.45 millimeter which are subjected to extraction grapes under pressure of carbon dioxide of 350 atm, temperature of 40-50 degrees C, fluid flow rate is 50g/minute and extraction duration is 60 minutes.

AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide external preparation comprises                (a) a paste base, (b) an alcohol, (c) a carbonate                and/or bicarbonate, and (d) an acid and/or a                substance which produces an acid by                hydrolysis.
   USE - The carbon dioxide external preparation is                useful for providing vasodilation, antitumor and                muscle strengthening effect, promoting muscle                fatigue recovery, fracture healing, wound healing,                lipid metabolism and skin care effects, improving                pigmentation, and preventing or treating                hemorrhoids and constipation.
   ADVANTAGE - The external preparation is convenient to                carry and easy to use, hence it is applied to the                skin in a simple manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Preparation: The external                  preparation comprises (a) 0.1-10 %mass,                  preferably 0.2-5 %mass carboxyvinyl polymer                  and/or an alkyl-modified carboxyvinyl polymer as                  a paste base, (b) alcohol which is a polyhydric                  alcohol chosen from one or more of 40-95 %mass,                  preferably 70-90 %mass 1,3-butylene glycol,                  propylene glycol, glycerin and polyethylene                  glycol liquid at room temperature, (c) 1-20 %mass                  carbonate, preferably 1-10 %mass sodium hydrogen                  carbonate, and (d) 0.1-20 %mass substance,                  preferably 1-10 %mass malic acid and sodium                  dihydrogen phosphate. The external preparation                  further comprises (e) a thickener different from                  the above-mentioned paste base, (f) a desiccant,                  (g) a water-soluble excipient, and (h) an                  exothermic agent.

   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - Components comprising 28 pts. wt. anhydrous                sodium sulfate, 4 pts. wt. malic acid, 31 pts. wt.                potassium dihydrogen phosphate, 375 pts. wt.                1,3-butylene glycol, 1 pts. wt. carboxyvinyl                polymer, 10 pts. wt. hydroxypropyl methylcellulose                and 30 pts. wt. propylene glycol were mixed to                obtain the external preparation.

TI Method of fabricating magnesium-iron composite oxide with carbon dioxide separated and purified.
AU SU Y
   HUANG W
   KUO Y
   YU J
   HUANG C
   CHYOU Y
AE INST NUCLEAR ENERGY RES ATOMIC ENERGY CO (ATEN-C)
GA 2018687237
AB    NOVELTY - A method is provided for making a magnesium iron composite oxide. Therein, carbon dioxide is separated and purified. The method solves the problem relating to using iron ore in chemical combustion loop for producing magnesium iron composite oxide. The present invention comprises the following steps: At first, iron ore and magnesium nitrate (Mg(NO3)2asterisk6H2O) are impregnated for reaction. After sieving particles within a fixed range of size, calcination is processed to obtain the magnesium iron composite oxide. Not only the problem relating to using iron ore in chemical combustion loop for producing magnesium iron composite oxide is effectively solved; but also the whole procedure can be looped for a long time with a high conversion rate of carbon dioxide.

The composite                material compared with the existing copper-based                catalytic material has more excellent catalytic                reduction carbon dioxide capability. It can realize                continuous and stable catalyst conversion under the                photoelectric condition; can effectively match the                existing industrial power system, using alternating                current power supply; and ensures that it has                excellent selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                foam CuOx-TNTs composite material prepared by the                method.

A second extraction line is branched from the end of the beer line and connected to the second valve. A pedestal is mounted on wall of the case and capable of storing beer.    USE - Cooler for cooling beer.

PN CN113856617-A
TI Preparing calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises e.g. mixing calcium salt and citric acid, stirring and reacting, adding metakaolin mineral into mixed solution, continuously stirring, grinding dried gel and calcining in the muffle furnace.
AU DI Z
   ZHANG H
   CHENG F
   GENG Y
   GUO Y
AE UNIV SHANXI (UYSX-C)
GA 202211151B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises (i) mixing calcium salt and citric acid according to mass ratio of 1:0.5-1:1.5 in de-ionized water, continuously stirring and reacting for 2-6 hours at 60-100degreesC to obtain the mixed solution A, (ii) adding metakaolin mineral into the mixed solution A, continuously stirring for 1-4 hours at 60-100degreesC, mixing uniformly to obtain the mixed solution B, where the mass ratio of metakaolin mineral and calcium salt calculated by calcium oxide is 0.5:10-2:10, (iii) allowing stand the mixed solution B at room temperature for 12-24 hours, (iv) taking the mixed solution B after allowing to stand at 60-100degreesC, slowly stirring, stirring until the water is gradually evaporated to obtain metakaolin mineral dispersed uniformly wet gel, (v) placing wet gel in 105-125degreesC oven to dry for 18-25 hours to obtain the dried gel, and (vi) grinding the dried gel, calcining for 1-4 hours at 750-900degreesC in the muffle furnace.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has low cost, is simple and feasible, alleviates the sintering problem that calcium oxide particles tend to agglomerate and stick in the process of cyclically capturing carbon dioxide by the calcium-based adsorbent, and improves the adsorption performance and cyclicstability performance of the calcium oxide adsorbent to carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the calcium salt comprises calcium nitratetetrahydrate or calcium acetate. In the step (i), the mass ratio of calcium salt and citric acid is 1:1-1:1.1.Preferred Method: In the step (i), the calcium salt includes a soluble calcium salt whose decomposition temperature is lower than 850degreesC. In the step (iv), the rotating speed is 90-110 revolutions/minute

PN KR2021097297-A; KR2338183-B1
TI Producing adsorbent containing polyvinylidene fluoride-based activated carbon useful for adsorbing carbon dioxide and/or methane comprises filling boat with polyvinylidene fluoride-based polymer and placing into a kiln and forming film.
AU PARK J H
   CHO D W
   JUNG T S
   LEE C H
   PARKHEYYOUNG
   CHANG H S
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 202193081G
AB    NOVELTY - Producing adsorbent containing polyvinylidene fluoride-based activated carbon comprises (a) filling boat with polyvinylidene fluoride-based polymer and placing into a kiln, (b) forming film having thickness of 0.03-0.10 cm in which the polyvinylidene fluoride-based polymer is melted by heating the filled boat in carbon dioxide flow atmosphere, (c) maintaining formed polyvinylidene fluoride based polymer film at reaction temperature of 600-900 degrees C for 1-3 hours to activate, and (d) recovering the activated carbon by lowering the temperature at a rate of greater than 1-5 degrees C/minute.
   USE - The adsorbent is useful for adsorbing carbon dioxide and/or methane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: does not required additional thermal energy; is easy to operate; reduces operation cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) adsorbent comprising activated carbon based on polyvinylidene fluoride specific surface area of 1250-2000 m2/g, total pore volume of 0.6-0.9 cm3/g, micropore volume of 0.6-0.8 cm3/g, and micropore volume of 0.30-0.35 cm3/g;
   (2) method for adsorption of methane comprising performing adsorption reaction using the adsorbent in an atmospheric pressure (760 mmHg) gas atmosphere containing less than or equal to 100 vol.% methane and nitrogen; and
   (3) carbon dioxide adsorption method comprising performing adsorption reaction using adsorbent in an atmospheric pressure (760 mmHg) gas atmosphere containing less than or equal to 100 vol.% carbon dioxide and nitrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polyvinylidene fluoride-based polymer contains 78 mol.% vinylidene fluoride monomer based on the total number of moles of the monomer. The polyvinylidene fluoride based polymer is poly(vinylidene fluoride), poly(vinylidene fluoride-co-hexafluoropropylene), poly(vinylidene fluoride-co-tetrafluoroethylene), poly(vinylidenefluoride-co-trifluoroethylene), and perfluoropolymers. The adsorbent is polyvinylidene having an element F content of less than or equal to 10 atomic% by X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS). Preferred Method: In the step (b), increasing temperature from room temperature to 600-900 degrees C at rate of 1-10 degrees C/minute. The micropores meets requirement of equation (V1-V2)/V2 = greater than or equal to 0.1, where V1 is the micropore volume (cm3/g) of an adsorbent containing activated carbon obtained by forming a film having a thickness of 0.03-0.10 cm in which the PVdF-based polymer is melted, and V2 is micropore volume (cm3/g) of an adsorbent containing activated carbon obtained by forming a film having a thickness of greater than or equal to 0.12 cm in which the PVdF-based polymer is melted, or V1 is the micropore volume (cm3/g) of the adsorbent containing activated carbon obtained by setting the heating rate of the kiln to more than 1 degrees C and less than 5 degrees C/min, and V2 is micropore volume (cm3/g) of the adsorbent containing activated carbon obtained by setting the heating rate of the kiln to greater than or equal to 5 degrees C/minute. The carbon dioxide adsorption performance meets requirement of equation (A1-A2)/A2 = greater than or equal to 0.15, where A1 is the oxygen dioxide adsorption performance (mmol/g, 25 degrees C, atmospheric pressure) of an adsorbent containing activated carbon obtained by forming a film having a thickness of 0.03-0.10 cm in which the PVdF-based polymer is melted, A2 is oxygen dioxide adsorption performance (mmol/g, 25 degrees C, atmospheric pressure) of an adsorbent containing activated carbon obtained by forming a film having a thickness of greater than or equal to 0.12 cm in which the PVdF-based polymer is melted, or A1 is the oxygen dioxide adsorption performance (mmol/g, 25 degrees C, atmospheric pressure) of the adsorbent containing activated carbon obtained by setting the heating rate of the kiln to more than 1 degrees C and less than 5 degrees C/minute, and A2 is oxygen dioxide adsorption performance (mmol/g, 25 degrees C, atmospheric pressure) of an adsorbent containing activated carbon obtained by setting the heating rate of the kiln to greater than or equal to 5 degrees C/minute. The adsorption reaction is performed at 0-30 degrees C.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of preparation of activated carbon that do not support the claims.

An adjusting device main body is provided with a locating piece e.g. steel bar, steel pipe or steel wire rope. The linear connecting section is used for connecting the adjacent arc-shaped portions. The locating piece is used for connecting the arc-shaped portions at intervals.
   USE - Ultra-critical carbon dioxide turbine adjusting device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device realizes flow of dry ice close to an inner wall surface of the bending pipe under action of centrifugal force so as to melt the dry ice by the heating element with low power consumption, thus temperature of ultra-critical carbon dioxide fluid far away from the inner wall surface of the bending pipe, and hence preventing the ultra-critical carbon dioxide fluid is prevented from generated phase changing under a large temperature changing condition, avoiding entry of the dry ice in a solid form and reducing downstream system pipeline and equipment damage.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an ultra-critical carbon dioxide turbine adjusting device.

PN WO2020098897-A1; CA3117369-A1; AU2019382115-A1; IN202127024792-A; EP3880375-A1; BR112021008674-A2; US2022001428-A1; EP3880375-A4
TI Method for removing matter from lumen of pipe by            back-pulse flushing procedure, involves maintaining            carbon dioxide in liquid state (LC02) or supercritical            state (scC02) by maintaining pipe in pressurized state            for time for LC02 diffusion.
AU THOMSEN J P H
   AMUNDSEN E K
AE OCEAN TEAM GROUP AS (OCEA-Non-standard)
   OCEAN TEAM GROUP AS (OCEA-Non-standard)
   OCEAN TEAM GROUP AS (OCEA-Non-standard)
   OCEAN TEAM GROUP AS (OCEA-Non-standard)
GA 202043992G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves pressurizing the pipe (2)                to a pressure by adding pressurized carbon dioxide                into the pipe at a first end of the pipe. The                pressurized carbon dioxide is added at a                temperature, which is provided at the pressure is                in a LC02 or in a scC02. The carbon dioxide in a                liquid state or in a supercritical state is                maintained, respectively, by maintaining the pipe                in the pressurized state for a time for diffusion                of the LC02 or scC02 through the matter during the                time t and for accumulating the LC02 or scC02                inside the matter or on the opposite side of the                matter or both. The pipe is depressurized at the                first end to a lower pressure level after the time.                The matter through the pipe is pressed to the first                end of the pipe under turbulent conditions.
   USE - Method for removing matter from lumen of pipe                by back-pulse flushing procedure e.g. hydraulic                control pipe system for controlling subsea valves                in offshore installation and especially in oil and                gas industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method creates turbulence for the carbon                dioxide (C02), as this turbulence cleans the pipe                walls when filling C02 into the pipe. The cleaning                from one end only in offshore installation for oil                and gas recovery in that the operation of the oil                or gas plant is not necessary to stop, which saves                high costs.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                offshore installation.
   Atmospheric pressure (1)
   Pipe (2)
   Tube (3)
   Valve (6)
   Well (7)

AB    NOVELTY - A polyurethane foam material based on supercritical carbon dioxide comprises 50-80 pts. wt. polyester polyol, 60-100 pts. wt. polyisocyanate, 5-20 pts. wt. polydimethylsiloxane, 8-16 pts. wt. chain extender, 5-12 pts. wt. coupling agent, 1-5 pts. wt. porous graphene, 1-6.5 pts. wt. catalyst, 0.5-2.5 pts. wt. light stabilizer and 1.2-3.8 pts. wt. antioxidant.
   USE - A polyurethane foam material based on supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The polyurethane foam material has good insulation performance, excellent light stability, heat resistant performance and improved strength after foaming.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparing a polyurethane foam material based on supercritical carbon dioxide which involves weighing separately polyester polyol, polyisocyanate, polydimethylsiloxane, chain extender, coupling agent, porous graphene, catalyst, light stabilizer, and antioxidant, mixing the components, heating to 75-80 degrees C under nitrogen (N2) environment for 1.5-2 hours, cooling to 60-65 degrees C, adding chain extender and catalyst, heat preservation reaction for 3.5-4 hours, adding light stabilizer and antioxidant, stirring uniformly to obtain the polyurethane prepolymer, adding coupling agent to polyurethane prepolymer, stirring at 110-115 degrees C for 25-30 minutes to obtain polyurethane base, placing the obtained polyurethane base in a sealed mold, quickly heating the to 130-135 degrees C, introducing supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) fluid into the mold to control the pressure in the mold at 15.5-18.5 MPa for 3-6 hours, and reducing temperature to 100-110 degrees C and quickly releasing the pressure to obtain polyurethane foam material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Chain extender is one of triethanolamine, dimethylethanolamine, and t-BuDEAt. Coupling agent is silane coupling agent or titanate coupling agent. Catalyst is greater than or equal to 1 of dibutyltin dilaurate, dibutyltin diacetate and chloromonobutylstannic acid. Light stabilizer is benzotriazole and benzophenone; and the antioxidant is 3,5-di-tert.-isooctyl butyl-4-hydroxyphenylpropionate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Polyurethane foam material based on supercritical carbon dioxide comprises 60 pts. wt. polyester polyol, 75 pts. wt. polyisocyanate, 10 pts. wt. polydimethylsiloxane, 8 pts. wt. chain extender, 6 pts. wt. coupling agent, 3 pts. wt. porous graphene, 3.8 pts. wt. catalyst, 1.5 pts. wt. light stabilizer and 2.2 pts. wt. antioxidant. Dicarboxylic acid is selected from dimethyl terephthalate, phthalic anhydride, terephthalic acid, isophthalic acid, polyethylene terephthalate and adipic acid; and the polyol is selected from ethylene glycol, propylene glycol, polypropylene glycol, polyethylene glycol, neopentyl glycol, glycerin and triethanolamine. Polyisocyanate is diphenylmethane diisocyanate or toluene diisocyanate. Preferred Process: Polyester polyol is obtained by reacting dicarboxylic acid and polyol in mass ratio of 1:(1-1.5).

PN WO2019239324-A2; WO2019239324-A3; KR2021024529-A; EP3801831-A2; US2021252448-A1; JP2021527557-W
TI Capture of chemical species used for detecting analytes, involves contacting metal-organic framework comprising square grid comprising pyrazine-2-amidoxime nickel complex and inorganic building block containing niobium-oxide fluoride anion.
AU TCHALALA M R
   NANAIAH K
   ADIL K
   BELMABKHOUT Y
   EDDAOUDI M
   SALAMA K N
AE UNIV KING ABDULLAH SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIK-C)
   UNIV KING ABDULLAH SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIK-C)
   UNIV KING ABDULLAH SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIK-C)
   UNIV KING ABDULLAH SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIK-C)
GA 2019A6147P
AB    NOVELTY - Capture of chemical species (100) involves contacting a metal-organic framework with a fluid composition including one or more sulfur dioxide, carbon dioxide and water (101), and sorbing one or more sulfur dioxide, carbon dioxide and water from the fluid composition on the metal-organic framework (102). The metal-organic framework comprises a square grid pillared by an inorganic building block. The square grid comprises a pyrazine-2-amidoxime nickel (II) complex and the inorganic building block comprising niobium-oxide fluoride anion (NbOF5)2- or aluminum-penta-fluoro hydride anions (AlF5(H2O))2-.
   USE - Capture of chemical species used for detecting analytes (claimed) using sensor.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides metal-organic frameworks exhibit high removal efficiency and/or high uptake, even at low concentrations of sulfur oxide, and having excellent stability and selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for detection of analytes, which involves exposing sensor to environment containing one or more sulfur dioxide, carbon dioxide and water in which the sensor including layer of metal-organic framework as sensing layer, and detecting presence of one or more sulfur dioxide, carbon dioxide and water in the environment using the sensor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of capturing chemical species.
   Capture of chemical species (100)
   Contacting metal-organic framework with fluid composition (101)
   Sorbing one or more sulfur dioxide, carbon dioxide and water from the fluid composition on the metal-organic framework (102)
   Regenerating the metal organic framework (103)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The fluid composition comprises 25-500 ppm sulfur dioxide, and 400-5000 ppm carbon dioxide, where the sulfur dioxide is preferentially sorbed over carbon dioxide on the metal-organic framework, sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide both are sorbed on the metal-organic framework, and sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide are sorbed simultaneously on the metal-organic framework. The fluid composition includes carbon dioxide and water, where carbon dioxide is preferentially sorbed over water on the metal-organic framework, carbon dioxide and water both are sorbed on the metal-organic framework, and carbon dioxide and water are sorbed simultaneously on the metal-organic framework. The sensor is capacitive sensor comprising interdigitated electrode, in which the sensing layer is deposited on the interdigitated electrode of the capacitive sensor, in the presence of one or more of sulfur dioxide, carbon dioxide and water is detected by measuring change in capacitance in the sensing layer. The sensor is quartz crystal microbalance (QCM) sensor comprising electrode, in which the sensing layer is deposited on the electrode of the QCM, in presence of one or more of sulfur dioxide, carbon dioxide and water is detected by measuring change in resonance frequency in the sensing layer. Preferred Condition: The sorbing and detecting method is carried out at room temperature. Preferred Process: The detecting method involves detecting 25-500 ppm sulfur dioxide optionally in the presence of water in the environment, detecting 400-5000 ppm carbon dioxide optionally in the presence of water in the environment, and detecting water optionally in the presence of carbon dioxide, and having relative humidity of less than 40-more than 60%.
DC E12 (Organometallics - ie containing other than H, C, N, O, S, halogens, Si and P.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)

TI Converting method of high ash Indian coal into methanol, involves passing of conditioned syngas to methanol loop and reaction occurs in reactor in which formed methanol leaves reactor and subjected to condensation.
AU CHINTALA T N
   ITTAGUNTA N
   KOTDWALA R
   ARORAM G
   NAKA S K
   DAGANI S R
   PANDEY A K
   TIRUPATHI N C
   NAGAMOHAN I
AE BHARAT HEAVY ELECTRICALS LTD (BHAR-C)
   BHARAT HEAVY ELECTRICALS LTD (BHAR-C)
GA 201999224T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves feeding of coal through stream (3a) using carbon dioxide as a transporting medium (4) and combination of steam, carbon dioxide and oxygen through a stream (8) to the fluidized bed gasifier (9), gasifying high ash Indian coal at a particular gasification temperature, cleaning of the syngas, passing of syngas into a heat exchanger (14), passing of syngas to scrubber (20a), introduction of syngas from scrubber to another hear exchanger (22), transferring of syngas into hydrogen sulfide removal unit, introduction of syngas to compressor (37a) for compression, passing of conditioned syngas into packed column for removal of carbon dioxide, utilization of separated carbon dioxide as fluidizing medium, and passing of the conditioned syngas to a methanol loop and reaction occurs in the reactor in which the formed methanol leaves the reactor and subjected to condensation.
   USE - Converting method of high ash Indian coal into methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - Transfers high ash Indian coal to methanol through oxy-blown fluidized bed gasification. The method is simple yet rapid, and feeds coal and sorbent to the gasifier to maintain the operating temperature and to control the temperature. Uses obtained syngas from gasification to generate steam that in-turn used as gasifying medium and for the regeneration process of amine solvents used in the hydrogen sulfide removal process and carbon dioxide removal process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic block diagram of the process scheme to convert coal to methanol.
   Coal through stream (3a)
   Transporting medium (4)
   Stream (8)
   Fluidized bed gasifier (9)
   Heat exchanger (14)
   Scrubber (20a)
   Another hear exchanger (22)
   Compressor (37a)

Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

TI Enthalpy carbon dioxide three-return heat cooling heat pump/refrigerating system, has first and second heat regenerators and divider connected with each other, and divider and gas-liquid separator formed with enthalpy increasing loop.
AU LI M
   LIU X
   WANG P
   LI Z
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 2019583727
AB    NOVELTY - The system has first, second and third heat regenerators (4-7) provided with an evaporation side inlet, an evaporation-side outlet, a cooling side inlet and a cooling side outlet. A divider (9) comprises an inlet and first and second outlets. An inlet of a compressor (2) is connected with a first gas-liquid separator (12). The compressor is connected with an oil separator (13). An outlet of the oil separator is connected with a port of a first four-way valve (10). An air outlet of the compressor is connected with a second gas-liquid separator (14). A second four-way valve (11), the first and second heat regenerators and the divider are connected with each other. The first and second heat regenerators, the divider and the second gas-liquid separator are formed with an enthalpy increasing loop.
   USE - Enthalpy carbon dioxide three-return heat cooling heat pump/refrigerating system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system realizes carbon dioxide heating and refrigerating function, and increases air displacement of compressor so as to achieve heat increasing effect under low ambient temperature, so that carbon dioxide can be heated in a rapid manner. The system can reheating of a re-heater by enthalpy, so that the system can be operated under high gas cooler and exhaust gas temperature.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an enthalpy carbon dioxide three-return heat cooling heat pump/refrigerating system.
   Compressor (2)
   Heat regenerators (4-7)
   Divider (9)

   Gas-liquid separators (12,14)
   Oil separator (13)

TI Flowmeter-free indirect calorimetry system for            determining the oxygen consumption, carbon dioxide            production, and energy expenditure of a mammal,            comprises a tank that is configured to promote            homogeneous mixing of the samples.
AU PARKER B E
   EHLMANN K M
   GRESSICK W T
AE BARRON ASSOC INC (BARR-Non-standard)
GA 2019372350
AB    NOVELTY - The flowmeter-free indirect calorimetry system                comprises a vacuum pump that induces the flow of                the exhaled gasses to the tank (105) through the                flow tube (101). A flow tube is configured to route                each of the group of samples toward the mixing                tank. A conduit (102) is configured to reduce the                water vapor pressure of the exhaled gasses within                the conduit to approximately a water vapor pressure                in air outside the conduit. An one-way inlet valve                (104) prevents the mixture from escaping from the                tank through the conduit. A battery (109) is                configured to power the sensors (114) and the                wireless transceiver. A tank is configured to                promote homogeneous mixing of the samples. A                barometric pressure sensor (116) is integrated with                the oxygen sensor, where temperature sensor (117)                is integrated with the carbon dioxide sensor                (115).
   USE - Flowmeter-free indirect calorimetry system for                determining the oxygen consumption, carbon dioxide                production, respiratory exchange ratio, and energy                expenditure of a mammal, e.g. humans.
   ADVANTAGE - Ensuring to provide accurate and reliable                measurement of metabolic gas exchange, where tank                is designed to take advantage of the momentum of                exhalations to establish homogenous mixing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                flowmeter-free indirect calorimetry method for                determining the oxygen consumption, carbon dioxide                production, respiratory exchange ratio, and energy                expenditure of a mammal.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of a flowmeter-free indirect calorimetry                system.
   Flow tube (101)
   Conduit (102)
   One-way inlet valve (104)
   Tank (105)
   Battery (109)
   Sensors (114)
   Carbon dioxide sensor (115)
   Barometric pressure sensor (116)
   Temperature sensor (117)

TI Enriching acid gas stream in acid gas enrichment system for sulfur recovery, involves feeding acid gas stream to contactor, acid gas stream comprises hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide, and hydrocarbons.
AU AL-QAHTANI N A
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 201916871L
AB    NOVELTY - Enriching acid gas stream in acid gas enrichment system involves feeding acid gas stream to contactor. The acid gas stream comprises hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide, and hydrocarbons. The acid gas stream is separated in contactor to create carbon dioxide rich stream and purified acid gas stream. The purified acid gas stream is fed to regenerator fluidly which is connected to contactor. The purified acid gas stream is separated in regenerator to create hydrogen sulfide rich stream and hydrogen sulfide lean stream. The hydrogen sulfide rich stream has concentration of hydrogen sulfide and periodically feeding portion of hydrogen sulfide rich stream exiting regenerator to acid gas stream entering contactor.
   USE - Method for enriching acid gas stream in acid gas enrichment system for sulfur recovery (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to enrich acid gas stream in acid gas enrichment system that improves catalyst performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system, which comprises:
   (A) configuring contactor to separate acid gas stream into carbon dioxide rich stream and purified acid gas stream, acid gas stream comprising hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide , and hydrocarbons;
   (B) communicating regenerator in fluid with contactor such that regenerator is configuring to separate purified acid gas stream to create hydrogen sulfide rich stream and hydrogen sulfide lean stream, hydrogen sulfide rich stream has concentration of hydrogen sulfide;
   (C) coupling recycle stream conduit fluidly between regenerator to contactor; and
   (D) configuring to supply portion of hydrogen sulfide rich stream from regenerator to contactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves feeding periodically includes adjusting recycle valve disposed fluid pathway that directs hydrogen sulfide rich stream exiting regenerator to acid gas stream entering contactor. The method involves adjusting recycle valve includes opening or closing recycle valve based on hydrogen sulfide content in hydrogen sulfide rich stream is less than 35 wt.%. The adjusting recycle valve involves closing recycle valve when on hydrogen sulfide content in carbon dioxide rich stream is 500 parts per million. The method further involves removing portion of hydrogen sulfide rich stream exiting from regenerator from acid gas enrichment system. The system comprises acid gas recycle valve coupled to recycle stream conduit that opens and closes fluid pathway from regenerator to contactor. Preferred Components: The system comprises single contactor. The system is exclusive of vacuum spool or compressor for contactor.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves introducing solid fuel                into a pressurized fluidized bed combustor (12) to                produce steam, first quantity of electrical power,                and flue gas comprising carbon dioxide and                nitrogen. Air, natural gas, a portion of the steam                and a portion of the flue gas comprising carbon                dioxide are introduced to a molten carbonate fuel                cell to produce second quantity of electrical power                and an output stream comprising primarily carbon                dioxide, where the pressurized fluidized bed                combustor is air-fired, the solid fuel introduced                into the pressurized fluidized bed combustor is in                a finely pulverized form, and the solid fuel is                selected from a group consisting of coal, petcoke                and biomass.
