PT P
PN DE102017111637-B3
TI Batch process for concentrating ingredients of carbon dioxide-containing liquids, comprises loading tank container with liquids, and performing treatment phase of the liquid, in which partial volume of liquid is conducted under cooling.
AU FRISCHMANN P T
AE ALBERT FREY DIENSTLEISTUNGS AG (ALBE-Non-standard)
GA 201872857F
AB    NOVELTY - Batch process for concentrating ingredients of carbon dioxide-containing liquids, comprises: (a) loading a tank container (1) with carbon dioxide-containing liquid; (b) performing a treatment phase of the liquid, in which a partial volume of the liquid obtained from the tank container, connected to a heat exchanger by pipelines, is conducted under cooling and ice separation to the heat exchanger, where the resulting ice in the heat exchanger is replaced and returned together with the sub-volume in the tank and the ice entrained in the sub-volume is separated by floating an ice layer in the tank container, until a desired concentration of the ingredients is reached; (c) removing the concentrated liquid from the tank container; and (d) thawing removal of leftover ice.
   USE - The method is useful for batch process for concentrating ingredients of carbon dioxide-containing liquids.
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieves simple construction of the batch device for removing ice from the heat exchangers, and safely receives the fluid to be treated.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for batch device for concentrating ingredients of carbon dioxide-containing liquids, comprising at least one first tank container with a tank volume (5), a liquid inlet (6) and a liquid outlet, at least one first heat exchanger (3a) having at least one ice scraper and a pipeline (2) connecting the tank container and the first heat exchanger to a circuit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the batch device.
   Tank container (1)
   Pipeline (2)
   First heat exchanger (3a)
   Tank volume (5)
   Liquid inlet (6)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The detachment in step (b) is carried out by scrapers, preferably rotary scrapers. The step (d) is performed cached heat removed by the step (b). The intermediate storage of the heat takes place by means of a second heat exchanger operatively connected to the first heat exchanger and the tank container. The tank volume is designed to be much larger than the volume of liquid to be accommodated in the tank container such that the ice formed in the tank container from the liquid under operating conditions of the batch device is accommodated. The liquid inlet is arranged in the region below an upper third of the tank.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN108511724-A; CN108511724-B
TI Preparing positive electrode material lithium manganese iron phosphate by sol-gel assisted supercritical carbon dioxide drying involves removing air in reaction vessel, transferring complexing agent solution to reaction vessel.
AU YANG W
   LIU Q
   ZHENG W
   ZHU J
   QIU X
   FANG K
AE UNIV GUANGZHOU (UNGZ-C)
GA 2018722046
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing positive electrode material lithium manganese iron phosphate by sol-gel assisted supercritical carbon dioxide drying involves removing air in the reaction vessel, transferring complexing agent solution to the reaction vessel, and stirring uniform, supplyig high-purity inert atmosphere during the stirring to prevent oxidation of the low-valent metal ions, dissolving the lithium salt, phosphate, manganese salt and ferrous salt in the complexing agent solution in sequence, stirring to fully dissolving inorganic salt, heating and stirring until forming gel, aging, pumping, using absolute ethanol displacing water in the gel to obtain an alcohol gel. The supercritical carbon dioxide fluid is continuously passed through the alcohol gel, carbon ioxide containing ethanol is depressurized into the separator.
   USE - Method for preparing positive electrode material lithium manganese iron phosphate by sol-gel assisted supercritical carbon dioxide drying.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare positive electrode material lithium manganese iron phosphate by sol-gel assisted supercritical carbon dioxide drying is easy to realize, and fast, has high efficiency, avoids material shrinkage and fragmentation in the drying process, so as to keep the original structure and state of the material, improves the production efficiency, and saves the material production cost and easy to realize and has low energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The ethanol is precipitated and recovered when the separator is no longer precipitated with ethanol, and separator is decompressed to obtain a white powder, dried prepared lithium manganese phosphate powder, added external carbon source to form final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The lithium salt, the phosphate salt, the manganese salt and the ferrous salt mass ratio are 1:1: (1-x): x, which is sequentially dissolved in the complexing agent solution, where x is 0-0.5. The method for preparing positive electrode material lithium manganese iron phosphate by sol-gel assisted supercritical carbon dioxide drying is performed at a temperature of 32-72 degrees C, and a pressure of 9-35 MPa. The critical carbon dioxide fluid continuously passes through the alcohol gel, and carbon dioxide containing ethanol is depressurized into a separator having a temperature of 32-82 degrees C and a pressure of 6-9 MPa, ethanol is precipitated and recovered, and carbon dioxide is recycled. The method for preparing positive electrode material lithium manganese iron phosphate by sol-gel assisted supercritical carbon dioxide drying involves heating the powder from room temperature to 400-800 degrees C, and heating up temperature is 5 degrees C/minute, and the temperature is kept for 5-10 hours under an inert gas atmosphere, and cooled to room temperature to obtain final product. Preferred Components: The lithium salt is lithium nitrate, lithium carbonate, lithium chloride or lithium acetate, lithium citrate or lithium oxalate. The phosphate is phosphoric acid, ammonium dihydrogen phosphate, triammonium phosphate, hydrogen phosphate diammonium or sodium dihydrogen phosphate. The manganese salt is manganese nitrate, manganese chloride, manganese carbonate, manganese sulfate, manganese acetate or manganese oxalate. The ferrous salt is ferrous sulfate, ferrous nitrate or ferrous oxalate or ferrous chloride. The complexing agent is citric acid, ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid, acetylacetone or polyacrylic acid. The inert atmosphere is nitrogen, argon, argon-hydrogen mixed gas or nitrogen gas, hydrogen gas mixture.

Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. TI Carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises a porous            support, and an amine adsorbent formed inside the pores            of the porous support.
AU SUNG H K
   SUNBIN J
   HYUNCHUL J
   DONG H J
   HYUN K S
   HYUN J D
AE UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
GA 201849810F
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises a porous                support, and an amine adsorbent formed inside the                pores of the porous support. The amine adsorbent:                includes mixture of amine compound and metal salt                additive; or a core layer formed along the pore                inner wall surface and a shell layer formed on the                core layer. The core layer is mixture of amine                compound and metal salt additive. The shell layer                is formed by crosslinking the amine compound with                crosslinking agent.
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent has excellent impregnation rate,                and maintains high carbon dioxide adsorption                ability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                producing carbon dioxide adsorbent, comprising                either: (a) injecting mixed solution containing                amine compound, metal salt additive and inorganic                solvent into pores of a porous support, and (b)                selectively evaporating the inorganic solvent in                the mixed solution to impregnate a mixture of the                amine compound and the metal salt additive in the                porous support, or (i) injecting mixed solution                containing amine compound, metal salt additive and                inorganic solvent into pores of a porous support,                (ii) selectively evaporating inorganic solvent in                the mixed solution to form a core layer impregnated                with amine compound and metal salt additive in a                porous support along a pore wall, (iii) injecting                mixed solution containing amine compound,                crosslinking agent and inorganic solvent into the                pores of the porous support in which the core layer                is formed, (iv) injecting mixed solution including                crosslinking agent and amine compound into pores of                the porous support having the core layer, and                reacting to form an amine-linkage, and (v)                selectively evaporating the inorganic solvent to                form amine compound crosslinked with the                crosslinking agent on the core layer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The porous support                  includes carbon body, graphite, graphene, carbon                  nanotube, zeolite, metal-organic frameworks,                  porous silica, porous polymer, clay and/or porous                  titania. The metal salt additive includes                  carbonate, nitrate, phosphate, sulfate and/or                  hydrate of metal, preferably lithium carbonate,                  sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, rubidium                  carbonate, cesium carbonate, francium carbonate,                  beryllium carbonate, magnesium carbonate, calcium                  carbonate, strontium carbonate, barium carbonate,                  radium carbonate, lithium nitrate, sodium                  nitrate, potassium nitrate, rubidium nitrate,                  cesium nitrate, francium nitrate, beryllium                  nitrate, magnesium nitrate, calcium nitrate,                  strontium nitrate, barium nitrate, radium                  nitrate, lithium sulfite, sodium sulfite,                  potassium sulfite, rubidium sulfite, secium                  sulfite, francum sulfite, beryllium sulfite,                  magnesium sulfite, calcium sulfite, strontium                  sulfite, barium sulfite, radium sulfite, lithium                  phosphate, sodium phosphate, potassium phosphate,                  rubidium phosphate, cesium phosphate, francium                  phosphate, beryllium phosphate, magnesium                  phosphate, calcium phosphate, strontium                  phosphate, barium phosphate and/or radium                  phosphate. The content of the metal salt additive                  is 5-100 wt.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amine compound                  includes tris(2-aminoethyl)amine,                  1,2-ethanediamine, diethylenetriamine,                  triethylene tetramine, tetraethylene pentaamine,                  pentaethylene hexaamine,                  3,6,9,12,15-pentaazaheptadecane-1,17-diamine,                  polyethylene imine, 2-(diethylamino)ethanol,                  2-dimethylaminoethanol, N-methyldiethanolamine,                  2- (methylamino) ethanol,                  1,2-bis((2-hydroxyethyl)amino)ethane,                  1,3-bis(tris(hydroxymethyl)methylamino)propane,                  (tetrahydroxyethyl)ethylenediamine,                  diethanolamine, 4-ethylamino-1-butanol,                  2-diethylaminoethanol, 2-isopropylaminoethanol,                  2-piperidinemethanol,                  2,2'-(ethyleneimino)diethanol,                  2-(2-aminoethoxy)ethylamine,                  1,7-diaza-12-crown-4, 1,3-phenylenediamine,                  4-aminopentylamine, 3-(2-aminoethyl)aniline,                  3-(aminomethyl)aniline,                  2-amino-1-(3-aminophenyl)ethan-1-ol,                  2,3-dihydro-1H-indene-2,5-diamine,                  1,2,3,4-tetrahydroisoquinolin-7-amine,                  monoethanolamine, diethanolamine,                  triethanolamine, 2,2'-(ethylenediimino)diethanol                  and/or 3-(diethylamino)-1,2-propanediol. the                  amine compound is modified with modifying                  material. The modifying material includes                  ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, 1,2-epoxybutene,                  1,2-epoxypentane, 1,2-epoxyheptane,                  1,2-epoxycycloheptane, glycidol, styrene oxide,                  styrene, vinyl pyridine and/or acrylonitrile. The                  crosslinking agent includes 1,3-butadiene                  diepoxide, glycerol diglycidyl ether, ethylene                  glycol diglycidyl ether, diepoxy octane,                  bisphenol-modified glycidyl ether,                  N,N,N',N'-tetraglycidyl-4,4'-methylenedianiline,                  3,4-epoxycyclohexylmethyl-3',4'-epoxycyclohexanecarboxylate,                  p-glycidyloxystyrene, glycidyl methacrylate,                  triglycidyl glycerol, oxalic acid, malonic acid,                  succinic acid, glutaric acid, adipic acid,                  carbosuccinic acid, dichloromethane,                  dichloroethane and/or epichlorohydrin. The amount                  of the crosslinking agent is 5-90 wt.%.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN107556512-A; CN107556512-B
TI Preparation of microporous poly(arylene ether nitrile)resin foamed material involves melt-extruding poly(arylene ether nitrile)resin, introducing supercritical carbon dioxide, carrying out saturation adsorption, foaming, and drying.
AU WANG H
   SHE G
   GOU K
   XIAO Z
   ZHOU J
   WEN S
AE YIBIN TIANYUAN GROUP CO LTD (YIBI-Non-standard)
GA 2018048019
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of microporous poly(arylene ether nitrile)resin foamed material involves melt-drying poly(arylene ether nitrile)resin in a twin-screw extruder, melt-extruding into film material, quenching to 50 degrees C or less, adding to a reactor, washing with carbon dioxide gas, introducing supercritical carbon dioxide, carrying out saturation adsorption of the film material at 1-40 MPa and 0-45 degrees C for 1-24 hours, foaming in oil bath at 150-350 degrees C for 1-2 minute, quenching with distilled water at room temperature, immersing in ethanol, and vacuum-drying.
   USE - Preparation of microporous poly(arylene ether nitrile)resin foamed material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of lightweight microporous poly(arylene ether nitrile)resin foamed material having excellent strength, impact resistance, flame retardance, dimensional stability, sound insulation effect, filtration effect and adsorption performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of microporous poly(arylene ether nitrile)resin foamed material involves melt-drying poly(arylene ether nitrile)resin in a twin-screw extruder, melt-extruding into film material having size of 0.1-2 mm using nitrogen as protective gas, quenching to 50 degrees C or less, adding to a reactor, washing with carbon dioxide gas, introducing supercritical carbon dioxide, carrying out saturation adsorption of the film material at 1-40 MPa and 0-45 degrees C for 1-24 hours, foaming in oil bath at 150-350 degrees C for 1-2 minute, quenching with distilled water at room temperature, immersing in ethanol, and vacuum-drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The resin has a density of 1.3 g/cm3 and a glass transition temperature of 151 degrees C.
DC A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.); A17 (Polymers of unsubstituted aliphatic monoolefins; including polyethylene.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.)

PT P
PN CN113151908-A
TI Degumming flax fiber supercritical microemulsion by dissolving solid pectinase, xylanase and laccase buffer solution with disodium hydrogen phosphate and citric acid, adding mixed enzyme solution to cysteine to supercritical carbon dioxide pipeline through high-pressure pump, and drying.
AU WANG C
   GAO S
   ZHENG H
   WEI A
   ZHENG L
AE YIXING SUNSHINE LINEN TEXTILE CO LTD (YIXI-Non-standard)
GA 202191502K
AB    NOVELTY - Method for degumming flax fiber supercritical microemulsion involves (a) dissolving solid pectinase, xylanase and laccase in first buffer solution by dissolving the solid pectinase, xylanase and laccase in the first buffer solution with disodium hydrogen phosphate and citric acid, storing in refrigerator, (b) adding the prepared mixed enzyme solution to 2-6 wt.% urea and 0.5-1 wt.% cysteine to form a mixed solution No.(I), adding 1-3 wt.% No.(I) mixture to the supercritical carbon dioxide pipeline through a high-pressure pump, (c) using disodium hydrogen phosphate and citric acid to prepare No.(II) buffer, and dissolving 6-10 wt.% solid catalase in No.(II) buffer, (d) adding 1-2 wt.% fatty acid sorbitan to the enzyme solution to form the No.(II) mixture, reacting in the degumming reactor, and (e) taking out the flax fiber in the degumming reactor and drying to obtain the finished product.
   USE - Method used for degumming flax fiber supercritical microemulsion.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes the degumming of the supercritical carbon dioxidemicroemulsion of the flax fiber and complete clean production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for degumming flax fiber supercritical microemulsion involves (a) dissolving solid pectinase, xylanase and laccase in first buffer solution by dissolving the solid pectinase, xylanase and laccase in the first buffer solution with disodium hydrogen phosphate and citric acid., where the mixed enzyme solution comprises 1-3 wt.% solid pectinase, xylanase and laccase, storing in the refrigerator at 4-8 degrees C for later use, (b) adding the prepared mixed enzyme solution to 2-6 wt.% urea and 0.5-1 wt.% cysteine to form a mixed solution No.(I), adding 1-3 wt.% No.(I) mixture to the supercritical carbon dioxide pipeline through a high-pressure pump, mixing evenly with the supercritical carbon dioxide, and injecting into the degumming reactor, placing flax fiber in the degumming reactor with supercritical carbon dioxide with a flow rate of 4-7 g/minute for every 1 g of flax, (c) using disodium hydrogen phosphate and citric acid to prepare No.(II) buffer with a pH of 4-6, and dissolving 6-10 wt.% solid catalase in No.(II) buffer to prepare an enzyme solution, (d) adding 1-2 wt.% fatty acid sorbitan to the enzyme solution to form the No.(II) mixture, reacting in the degumming reactor in step (b) for 30-60 minutes, adding the No.(II) mixture to the supercritical carbon dioxide pipeline through the high-pressure pump and mixing evenly with the supercritical carbon dioxide, injecting into the degumming reactor and continuously reacting for 30-60 minutes, and (e) taking out the flax fiber in the degumming reactor in step (d) and drying naturally to obtain the finished product.
DC F01 (Threads and fibres - natural or artificial; spinning - including the production of mineral and carbon fibres (D01).)
MC F01-B02
IP D01C-001/02

PN US2021379538-A1
TI Preparation of thermally-rearranged polymer material used as asymmetric modified carbon molecular sieve hollow fiber membrane, involves contacting polymer precursor material with modifying agent, contacting with moisture-containing atmosphere, and heating to form polymer with improved gas permeance.
AU WILLIAMS P J
   KOROS W J
   BHUWANIA N
AE AIR LIQUIDE ADVANCED TECHNOLOGIES US LLC (AIRL-C)
   GEORGIA TECH RES CORP (GTEK-C)
GA 2021E3608W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of thermally-rearranged polymer material involves (i) providing a polymer precursor material, (ii) contacting the polymer precursor material with a modifying agent, (iii) contacting the polymer precursor material with a moisture-containing atmosphere to form a treated polymer precursor material, and (iv) heating the treated polymer precursor material to form a thermally-rearranged polymer having an improved gas permeance.
   USE - Preparation of thermally-rearranged polymer material used as asymmetric modified carbon molecular sieve hollow fiber membrane in separation of gases chosen from carbon dioxide and methane, hydrogen sulfide and methane, carbon dioxide/hydrogen sulfide and methane, carbon dioxide and nitrogen, oxygen and nitrogen, nitrogen and methane, helium and methane, hydrogen and methane, hydrogen and ethylene, ethylene and ethane, propylene and propane, and ethane/propane and ethylene/propylene, separation of acid gases from natural gas stream, and separation of carbon dioxide from hydrocarbon-containing gas stream (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of thermally-rearranged polymer material having improved gas separation performance, strength and flexibility, and high selectivity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The modifying agent is present in the form of solution comprising 1-95 wt.%, preferably 5-25 wt.% modifying agent. The modifying agent is an organosilicon compound of formula: R1R2R3R4Si (I) or an organometallic compound of formula: R1R2R3R4M (II). The compound (I) or (II) comprises at least one 1-6C alkyl or alkenyl substituent and at least one alkoxy or halogen substituent. The compound (I) is preferably vinyltrimethoxysilane. The solution further comprises 5C or more linear or branched aliphatic hydrocarbon, preferably n-hexane.R1-R4 = 1-6C alkyl or alkenyl, alkoxy, or halo; and M = metal, preferably Ge, B, Al, Ti, V and/or Fe.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The thermally-rearranged polymer comprises polybenzoxazole, polybenzothiazole and/or polybenzimidazole. The polymer precursor comprises a polyimide with an ortho-positioned functional group.Preferred Process: The treated polymer precursor material is heated at 250-500degrees Celsius, preferably 300-450degrees Celsius under an inert atmosphere. The concentration of the modifying agent in the solution is selected to obtain thermally-rearranged polymer having gas permeance property that is at least 300% increase over an equivalent thermally-rearranged polymer that is not subjected to the processes (ii) and (iii). The concentration of the modifying agent in the solution is selected to obtain thermally-rearranged polymer having selectivity that is at least 85% of the selectivity of an equivalent thermally rearranged polymer that is not subjected to the processes (ii) and (iii). The concentration of the modifying agent in the solution is selected to obtain thermally-rearranged polymer that is useful in separation of gases chosen from carbon dioxide and methane, hydrogen sulfide and methane, carbon dioxide/hydrogen sulfide and methane, carbon dioxide and nitrogen, oxygen and nitrogen, nitrogen and methane, helium and methane, hydrogen and methane, hydrogen and ethylene, ethylene and ethane, propylene and propane, and ethane/propane and ethylene/propylene. The concentration of the modifying agent in the solution is selected to obtain thermally-rearranged polymer that is useful in separation of acid gases from natural gas stream. The concentration of the modifying agent in the solution is selected to obtain thermally-rearranged polymer that is useful for in separation of carbon dioxide from hydrocarbon-containing gas stream.

TI Mining natural gas hydrate involves, involves            extracting free gas in natural gas hydrate mine,            injecting carbon dioxide into mine, reducing hydrate            mineral deposit temperature followed by injecting            carbon dioxide into mineral deposit.
AU SUN C
   REN L
   CHEN G
   WANG L
   ZHU Y
   YANG L
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM BEIJING (UPEB-C)
GA 2021815821
AB    NOVELTY - Mining natural gas hydrate involves extracting                free gas in a natural gas hydrate mine by                decompression mining until the pressure of the                natural gas hydrate deposit drops to a preset                percentage above the equilibrium pressure                corresponding to the temperature of the hydrate                deposit, and the value range of the preset                percentage is 5-10%. The hydrate mineral deposit                temperature is reduced and maintained under a                freezing point so that the free water in the                mineral deposit is frozen, after the pressure and                temperature of the deposit have stabilized, natural                gas hydrate is exploited followed by injecting                carbon dioxide into mineral deposit, and raising                temperature of the mine above the freezing point                when stopping injecting carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for mining natural gas hydrate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reducing the energy needed                by mining, enhancing the stability of the strata,                effectively avoiding the possible formation                collapse in the conventional mining process,                undersea landslide and other geological                disasters.

AE HAMILTON SUNDSTRAND CORP (SUNH-C)
GA 2019832372
AB    NOVELTY - The lattice (15) has a metal lattice body defining a set of intersecting ligaments, where nodes are formed at intersections of the set of intersecting ligaments. A liner extends through the metal lattice body and defines a hollow-type channel. The hollow-type channel is fluidly communicated with the metal lattice body through a set of apertures, where the metal lattice is formed by additive manufacturing process comprising one of powder bed fusion process, powder-feed directed energy deposition process, and wire-feed directed energy deposition process. The metal lattice body and the liner are manufactured during single additive manufacturing process.

PN CN110209145-A; CN110209145-B
TI Approximated core matrix based carbon dioxide absorbing tower fault diagnosis method, involves obtaining diagnostic data set for calculating label data matrix, and obtaining fault diagnosis result according to weight in diagnostic data set.
AU SONG Z
   YU J
   YE L
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2019790886
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing training sample with a process variable data set and quality variable data set. A total process variable and a total quality variable are determined. Normalization pre-process is performed to obtain a pre-processed training sample set. Core matrix is calculated by using canonical correlation analysis. A canonical correlation analysis model is established. Data is collected in a carbon dioxide absorption tower. Normalization pre-process is performed to obtain a test sample set. A fault sample set is obtained. Normal samples are taken from the training sample set. A diagnostic data set is obtained for calculating label data matrix. Fault diagnosis result is obtained according to weight in the diagnostic data set.
   USE - Approximated core matrix based carbon dioxide absorbing tower fault diagnosis method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reducing load computational problem, and improving fault detection and fault diagnosis efficiency and accuracy, processing safety and industry reliability, product quality and comprehensive economic benefits.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating an approximated core matrix based carbon dioxide absorbing tower fault diagnosis method. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'

PN CN109821027-A
TI Preparing hesperetin or hydroxypropyl-beta-cyclodextrin inclusion, comprises e.g. adding hesperetin bulk drug and the water-soluble carrier in an organic solvent and introducing carbon dioxide into the crystallizer and collecting product.
AU WANG Z
   WANG C
   ZHAN J
   YAO Y
   YANG Q
   ZHU M
   CHEN X
AE UNIV CHINA PHARM (UYCP-C)
GA 2019501325
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hesperetin or hydroxypropyl- beta -cyclodextrin inclusion, comprises (i) adding hesperetin bulk drug and the water-soluble carrier in an organic solvent, to obtain hesperetin-carrier mixed solution, where organic solvent is ethanol, the water-soluble carrier is hydroxypropyl- beta -cyclodextrin, and molar ratio of the hesperetin drug substance to carrier is 1:1; (ii) introducing carbon dioxide into the crystallizer at a certain flow rate to adjust temperature and pressure in the crystallizer; (iii) continuously introducing the carbon dioxide to maintain temperature and pressure in the crystallizer, and preparing the hesperetin prepared in step (i) simultaneously, spraying the carrier mixed solution from the top of the crystallizer through the nozzle into interior of the crystallizer by a high pressure infusion pump; and (iv) completing injection, continuously introducing the carbon dioxide for a certain period of time, releasing pressure, opening the crystallizer, collecting product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing hesperetin or hydroxypropyl-beta-cyclodextrin inclusion.
   ADVANTAGE - The hesperetin or hydroxypropyl-beta-cyclodextrin inclusion has high dissolution rate, bioavailability and stability. The method is easy and environmentally-friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concentration of the mixed solution in the step (i) is 5-7 mg/ml. The carbon dioxide flow rate in step (ii) is 3.5-4 l/minute. In step (ii) the pressure in the crystallizer is 11-15 MPa. Preferred Method: In the step (ii) the temperature in the crystallizer is 40-48 degrees C. The volume flow rate of the solution in the step (iii) is 0.8-1.2 ml/minute. In step (iv) continuously introducing carbon dioxide for 45 minutes.

TI Supported gallium nitride catalyst for            dehydrogenation of propane with carbon dioxide, is            prepared by mixing gallium source, nitrogen source and            carrier, adding to solvent, ultrasonically dispersing,            drying, calcining, and decarburizing.
AU LIU Z
   ZHANG L
   CHEN J
   SONG J
   WANG K
   HE Z
AE UNIV SHAANXI NORMAL (UNSN-C)
   UNIV SHAANXI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYSK-C)
   UNIV SHAANXI NORMAL (UNSN-C)
GA 2019049508
AB    NOVELTY - A supported gallium nitride catalyst comprises                gallium nitride as an active component, and                compound (Q-3, Q-6, Q-15, Q-30 or Q-50) as a                carrier. The catalyst contains 1-30% gallium                nitride. The catalyst is prepared by mixing a solid                gallium source, a solid nitrogen source and a                carrier in molar ratio of gallium element and solid                nitrogen source is 1:1-4, adding to solvent,                ultrasonically dispersing for 20-60 minutes, drying                at 40-120 degrees C for 2-4 hours, calcining in                inert atmosphere at 600-900 degrees C for 1-4                hours, decarburizing in air atmosphere at 400-600                degrees C for 1-3 hours, tableting, granulating,                and sieving.
   USE - Supported gallium nitride catalyst is used for                dehydrogenation of propane with carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The supported gallium nitride catalyst can be                prepared by simple, environmentally-friendly and                economical process using easily available raw                materials, and enables dehydrogenation of propane                with carbon dioxide with selectivity and conversion                rate of propane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of                the catalyst in dehydrogenation of propane with                carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of                  gallium element and solid nitrogen source is                  1:2-3. The solid gallium source is gallium                  trichloride, gallium oxide, gallium carbonate,                  gallium sulfate or gallium acetate. The solid                  nitrogen source is ammonium chloride, ammonium                  carbonate, ammonium sulfate, ammonium nitrate,                  ammonium acetate or urea. The catalyst contains                  3-10% gallium nitride. The inert atmosphere is                  nitrogen, argon or helium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The solvent is water,                  methanol, ethanol or acetone. The volume ratio of                  propane, carbon dioxide and nitrogen is                  3:n:(27-n), where n is 0-12. Preferred Process:                  The dehydrogenation of propane with carbon                  dioxide involves diluting catalyst in quartz                  sand, adding to fixed bed reactor, heating, and                  cooling.

AU KARPOV V A
   TEREKHOVA V A
   UCHANOV P V
AE RUSSIAN FEDERATION MIN DEFENCE (RUDE-C)
GA 201895198J
AB    NOVELTY - Method for determining the aggressiveness of microorganisms-degraders of polymeric materials, consisting in the gas chromatographic determination of the metabolic activity of microorganisms for the emission of carbon dioxide. Calculate specific carbon dioxide emission (mcmol/cm2/h) in the experimental Ve and the control Vc sample according to the formula Ve or Vc=(Se or Scasterisk0.002124asteriskMrasteriskv)/(tasterisks), where Se or Sc the average peak area of the test and control samples on a chromatograph, Mr is the molar mass of carbon dioxide, v is the volume of an aliquot of a gas sample from a vial (cm3), t is the exposure time of a sample incubation in a closed vial (h), s is the surface area of the test or control sample (cm2). Then, the ratio of the specific carbon dioxide emission in the test sample to the control sample in % (v/v) is calculated from the formula k=Ve/Vc and the absence or presence of the microorganism aggressiveness and destruction of the prototype is determined.
   USE - Biotechnology.
   ADVANTAGE - Method provides an expansion of the arsenal of technical means for assessing the aggressiveness of microorganisms-degraders of polymeric materials and increasing the accuracy of determination.

PN CN108562585-A
TI Method for determining mass transfer coefficient between carbon dioxide and oil phase, involves using temperature controller, discharging oil phase from liquid inlet inject, cutting oil circuit, calculating and recording oil droplet volume.
AU DU D
   ZHENG L
   ZHANG D
   LI Y
AE UNIV QINGDAO SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYQS-C)
GA 201877895T
AB    NOVELTY - A mass transfer coefficient determining method involves using the temperature controller to adjust the temperature and the gas booster pump is used to pressurize the carbon dioxide gas pressure to the minimum miscible pressure of the system corresponding to the experimental temperature, discharging the oil phase from the liquid inlet inject and suspending at the end of the capillary dropper and stabilizing, cutting the oil circuit, so that the oil droplets form an isolated system with the carbon dioxide environment and observing the interphase mass transfer, calculating and recording the oil droplet volume and surface area parameters captured by the CCD camera at the same time interval and establishing the coordinate system by using the time as the abscissa and the volume and surface area as the ordinate curve, processing the change curve of the hanging drop volume and surface area with time by using the formula represented by the expression as given in the specification.
   USE - Method for determining mass transfer coefficient between carbon dioxide and oil phase.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables determining mass transfer coefficient with high oil phase.

PN CN107754568-A; CN107754568-B
TI Low-energy consumption recycling carbon dioxide flue gas collecting device, has tower gas heat recycling device connected with solution pump, and concentration adjusting device connected between heat recycling device and lean pipe.
AU SHEN J
AE SHEN J (SHEN-Individual)
GA 2018200891
AB    NOVELTY - The device has an absorption tower whose side surface is provided with a flue gas inlet pipe and top end is connected with an emptying flue gas pipe. A gas desorption device is provided with a stripper and a steam pipe. The steam pipe is connected to a lower side of a desorption tower. The desorption tower is connected with a desorption tower gas heat recycling device that is connected with a solution pump. An absorber concentration adjusting device is connected between the desorption tower gas heat recycling device and a lean pipe.
   USE - Low-energy consumption recycling carbon dioxide flue gas collecting device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device has high heat utilization rate, and realizes desorber heat barren liquor flash evaporation process in an effective manner, and reduces number of a heat exchanger and equipment investment cost, and avoids frequent cleaning blockage problem.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a low-energy consumption recycling carbon dioxide flue gas collecting method.

PN RU2641270-C1
TI Method for solid fuels gasification in gas generator and reactor device for method implementation.
AU 
AE BLAGODAROV YU P (BLAG-Individual)
GA 201806859W
AB    NOVELTY - Method for solid fuels gasification is described, consisting in moving fuel for gasification in a vertically oriented hollow reactor, upwards from the bottom, forming a combustion site in the reactor due to the oxidative reaction of the oxygen compound (O2) of air with the carbon (C) of the fuel, at which carbon dioxide (CO2) is emitted, carrying out of a reductive reaction of a carbon dioxide compound (CO2) with the carbon (C) of the fuel according to the formula CO2+C=2CO in the fuel layer, at which carbon monoxide (CO) - a combustible gas is produced. Behind the combustion site, coal and ash are moved upwards in the vertically oriented hollow reactor, carbon dioxide (CO2) is passed through the coal and ash along the direction of movement to carry out a reductive reaction of the carbon dioxide compound (CO2) with the carbon (C) of the fuel and produce a combustible gas - carbon monoxide (CO), combustible gas is brought up the reactor and beyond. A reactor for of solid fuels gasification is also described.
   USE - Chemistry.
   ADVANTAGE - Method for gasification of solid fuels is developed, which has effective conditions for oxidation-reduction reactions.
3 cl, 5 dwg

PN CN113184874-A; CN113184874-B
TI Preparation of multi-stage hole LSX zeolite            molecular sieve used e.g. in carbon dioxide            hydrogenation to synthesize methanol, by mixing sources            of aluminum, sodium and silicon, adding            trimethoxysilane quat. ammonium salt and reacting.
AU TONG L
   XIANG M
   WU Z
   JI X
   LI D
   LU J
   YANG Q
AE CHANGZHOU INST TECHNOLOGY (UYCZ-C)
GA 202190143T
AB    NOVELTY - A multi-stage hole LSX zeolite molecular sieve                is prepared by uniformly stirring aluminum source,                sodium source and silicon source, adding                trimethoxysilane quat. ammonium salt, uniformly                mixing, putting in reaction kettle, heating and                reacting at 130-160 degrees C for 3-4 hours,                washing, drying and calcining.
   USE - The method is used for preparing multi-stage                hole LSX zeolite molecular sieve as hydrogenation                reaction catalyst carrier in carbon dioxide                hydrogenation to synthesize methanol (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalytic hydrogenation of carbon dioxide                to produce methanol has catalytic effect of more                than 50%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sodium source is                  sodium aluminate, sodium silicate and/or sodium                  chloride. The aluminum source is sodium                  aluminate. The silicon source is sodium silicate.                  The molar amount of sodium element in sodium                  source is calculated as molar amount of sodium                  oxide. The molar amount of aluminum element in                  aluminum source is measured by molar amount of                  alumina. The total molar amount of silicon in                  silicon source is measured as molar amount of                  silica. The dosage of aluminum source, sodium                  source and silicon source is in accordance to                  molar ratio of sodium                  oxide:alumina:silica=5.5-6.25:1.0:2.0-2.2. The                  trimethoxysilane quat. ammonium salt is                  N,N-dimethyl-N-octadecyl-N-(3-triethoxysilyl)ammonium                  bromide. The dosage of trimethoxysilane quat.                  ammonium salt is sodium source. The total mass of                  aluminum source and silicon source is 1-5%. The                  sieve has micro-pore structure and mesoporous                  structure, and crystallinity of more than 80%.                  Preferred Method: Heating and reacting is done at                  150 degrees C for 3 hours. Calcining is done at                  450 degrees C for 4 hours.

PT P
PN CN112604705-A
TI Preparing NPC-molybdenum disulfide/Bi4O5Br2 composite material used for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to produce carbon monoxide and methane under UV light comprises accelerating photo-generated electron transmission rate.
AU YIN X
   LI X
   MU M
AE UNIV TIANJIN TECHNOLOGY (UYTT-C)
GA 202138556U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing NPC-molybdenum disulfide/Bi4O5Br2 composite material comprises promoting the separation of photo-generated electron and hole by heterogeneous structure of the composite material, accelerating the photo-generated electron transmission rate by graphite carbon, and improving the photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
   USE - The NPC-MoS2/Bi4O5Br2 composite material is useful for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to produce carbon monoxide and methane under ultraviolet light (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the performance of the photocatalytic CO2; is simple; has high stability; combines sheet-shaped bismuth oxyhalide with the common molybdenum-based material; is environmentally friendly; saves energy; has economic benefit and application prospect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises using CVD growth system to synthesize NPC-molybdenum disulfide containing graphite carbon by oil bath heating method to firstly prepare precursor Mo-MOF of NPC-molybdenum disulfide, specifically taking MoO3 and imidazole in a molar ratio of 1:1-1:2 into 100 ml de-ionized water and uniformly stirring, then refluxing the mixture at 110-130 degrees C for 12 hours, filtering and collecting the precipitate, using de-ionized water to wash for three times, placing the collected solid in 60 degrees C oven drying overnight, finally obtaining Mo-MOF, preparing the MoS2 containing graphite carbon by CVD growth method, named as NPC-molybdenum disulfide, adding the prepared 0.2-0.4 g Mo-MOF in the center of a tubular furnace, placing 1.6-3.2 g thiourea in the center of the tubular furnace, where the placing order is Mo-MOF before and thiourea is in the back, firstly using N2 to blow the tubular furnace reaction tube for a certain time to remove residual oxygen in the tube, then the thiourea in the Mo-MOF and tube in N2 atmosphere, heating to 900 degrees C at a rate of 5 degrees C/minute, insulating for 3 hours, then cooling the sample in the tubular furnace to room temperature at a rate of 10 degrees C/min, obtaining rod NPC-molybdenum disulfide, using solvent thermal method to synthesize NPC-molybdenum disulfide /Bi4O5Br2 composite material, dispersing a certain amount of NPC-molybdenum disulfide in the 40 ml glycol, ultrasonically processing for 20 minutes, adding 1.5 mmol bismuth(III) nitrate pentahydrate into the suspension, marking the solution as A, dissolving 1.0 mmol potassium bromide in 40 ml glycol, and stirring for 20 minutes, slowly dropping to the solution A, using 2 mol/l sodium hydroxide solution to adjust the pH of the mixed solution to 11, transferring the mixed solution to the stainless steel hydrothermal kettle of 100 ml polytetrafluoroethylene lining, adding in the oven to heat to 160 degrees C, insulating for 12 hours, naturally cooling, centrifugally collecting the product, using de-ionized water and absolute ethyl alcohol to wash for multiple times, collecting the obtained solid in 60 degrees C oven, and drying overnight.

PN CN112452336-A
TI Preparing catalyst useful for oxidizing propylene to acrolein comprises using carbon dioxide-based polyurea as pore-forming agent, when preparing catalyst.
AU YIN Z
   ZHONG D
   FU G
   TAN D
   MENG Y
   SUN D
   WANG S
   XIAO M
   CHEN X
   WEN L
   MA Y
   CHEN D
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
   UNIV GUANGDONG PETROCHEMICAL TECHNOLOG (UNGP-C)
GA 202126225V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing catalyst comprises using carbon dioxide-based polyurea as pore-forming agent, when preparing the catalyst.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for oxidizing propylene to acrolein (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide-based polyurea: is hydrophilic; has excellent acid and alkali resistance, and stable chemical properties; uniformly disperses in the catalyst slurry; removes by high-temperature calcination in the later stage; does not remain in the catalyst; increases the number of mesopores inside the catalyst by gas produced by decomposition; improves the catalytic activity and catalytic life of the catalyst; and improves the selectivity and yield of acrolein.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for the catalyst prepared by the above mentioned method
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide-based polyurea is directly copolymerizing by diethylene glycol di(3-aminopropyl) ether and carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide-based polyurea is completely removing by calcining. The catalyst is prepared by adding carrier powder to the aqueous solution of molybdenum complex and carbon dioxide-based polyurea, uniformly stirring to obtain slurry A, dissolving the bismuth complex, iron complex, cobalt complex and nickel complex in water to obtain slurry B, dissolving at least one of X complex, Y complex, and Z complex in water to obtain slurry C, adding the slurry C to the slurry B, completely stirring to obtain slurry D, dropping the slurry D into the slurry A, uniformly mixing, adding alkali to adjust the pH value to obtain catalyst slurry, removing part of the water from the catalyst slurry, extruding and molding to obtain the catalyst particles, and calcining the catalyst particles. Preferred Components: The molecular weight of the carbon dioxide-based polyurea is 1,000-3,000. The amount of the carbon dioxide-based polyurea is 5-10 wt.% of the catalyst. The catalyst comprises catalytically active component and a carrier. The catalytically active component has the following general formula: molybdenum12-bismuth a-ironb-cobalt c-nickeld-Xf-Yg-Zh-Ox, where the X comprising potassium, manganese, magnesium, sodium, vanadium, chromium, and/or aluminum, the Y comprising tellurium, tin, thallium, platinum, and/or strontium, the comprising antimony, caesium, arsenic, and/or rubidium, a = 0.2-6.3, b = 0.6-4.8, c = 1-10, d = 0.08-10, f = 0-0.5, g = 0-0.3, h = 0-0.2, f, g, and h at least one is not 0. The f=0.02-0.5, the g=0.02-0.3, and the h=0.02-0.2. The carrier comprises silicon dioxide, titanium dioxide, and/or aluminum oxide. The weight of the carrier is 10-50 wt.% of the catalyst. The bismuth complex comprises bismuth nitrate. The iron complex comprises iron nitrate. The cobalt complex comprises cobalt nitrate. The nickel complex comprises nickel nitrate. The alkali comprises ammonia water. The pH value adjustment range is 6-6.4.

TI Perovskite catalyst used for carbon dioxide hydrogenation to prepare ethylene, propylene and butane, comprises core of catalyst comprising lanthanum-iron-manganese-ozone components.
AU ZHANG J
   MA L
   GUO Q
   ZHAO T
   GAO X
   MA Q
AE UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)
GA 2021013366
AB    NOVELTY - Perovskite catalyst comprises core of the catalyst comprising lanthanum-iron-manganese-ozone components, and is prepared by gel forming the catalyst using nitrate solution of lanthanum, iron, and manganese and citric acid in a water bath at 80 degrees C, drying the gel at 105-120 degrees C, and calcining at 600-900 degrees C to obtain the target catalyst, lowering the catalyst to 400-650 degrees C and applying to carbon dioxide hydrogenation to prepare low-carbon olefins.
   USE - The perovskite catalyst is used for carbon dioxide hydrogenation to prepare ethylene, propylene and butane.
   ADVANTAGE - The perovskite catalyst achieves excellent selectivity for low-carbon olefins, and has certain industrial value.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The applied reaction condition of hydrogen/carbon dioxide is 1-4, the temperature is 150-300 degrees C, the pressure is 2-8 MPa, and the time is 1000-4000 hours-1. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio in the catalyst is lanthanum and iron is 0.3-0.6:1, and iron-manganese is 1-2:1. Preferred Components: The optimization of the catalyst is modified by impregnation comprising magnesium, sodium, zinc, and potassium.

PN CN110496933-A; CN110496933-B
TI Water glass carbon dioxide hardened sand used for casting alkaline manganese steel casting material includes calcium oxide, magnesia olivine sand, silica sand, zircon sand, water glass solution and chromium oxide.
AU ZHAO S
   LIAO Z
   KANG J
   TIAN H
   ZHOU Z
AE GUANGXI CHANGCHENG MACHINERY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGXI CHANGCHENG MACHINERY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019A3967M
AB    NOVELTY - Water glass carbon dioxide hardened sand comprises 56-80 pts. wt. calcium oxide, 8-12 pts. wt. magnesia olivine sand, 2-4 pts. wt. silica sand, 3-6 pts. wt. zircon sand, 4-7 pts. wt. water glass solution, 1-2 pts. wt. poly-fluorine ethylene propylene, 2-4 pts. wt. paraffin-base petroleum calcium sulfonate fiber and 0.3-0.8 pt. wt. chromium oxide.
   USE - Water glass carbon dioxide hardened sand used for casting alkaline manganese steel casting material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material has low thermal expansion coefficient, high dispersing property, no chemical reaction and low cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Components: The water glass carbon dioxide hardened sand comprises 70 pts. wt. calcium oxide, 10 pts. wt. magnesia olivine sand, 3 pts. wt. silica sand, 5 pts. wt. zircon sand, 6 pts. wt. water glass solution, 1.6 pt. wt. poly-fluorine ethylene propylene, 3 pts. wt. paraffin-base petroleum calcium sulfonate fiber and 0.6 pt. wt. chromium oxide. The magnesium oxide in magnesium olivine sand is 46.13-58.61% with 20-100 meshes granularity. The water glass solution comprises 2.0-3.0% modulus.

PT P
PN CN110255534-A
TI Preparation of doped three-dimensional porous graphene aerogel used for fuel cell involves dispersing e.g. graphene oxide, solvothermal reacting, adding heteroprecursor solution, supercritical carbon dioxide reacting, and pyrolyzing.
AU CHENG C
   GAO S
   ZHAO N
   YANG J
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 201991733P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of doped three-dimensional porous graphene aerogel comprises uniformly dispersing graphene oxide, reducing agent, and deionized water, carrying out solvent thermal reaction to obtain graphene oxide hydrogel, dialyzing, and freeze-drying; placing in supercritical reactor, adding heteroprecursor solution, introducing carbon dioxide, heating, stirring, impregnating with supercritical carbon dioxide, dispersing, washing with ethanol, and drying at low temperature; and pyrolyzing under protection of inert gas.
   USE - The method is used for preparing doped three-dimensional porous graphene aerogel for fuel cell (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The aerogel has a three-dimensional porous structure, high heteroatom incorporation amount, good oxygen reduction performance, and better methanol resistance and stability than commercial platinum/carbon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The first step is done by adding 3-4 mg/mL reducing agent of ascorbic acid or sodium borohydride to graphene oxide, solvothermal reacting for 10-24 hours at 85-95 degrees C, and dialyzing for 1-2 days. The second step is done by using heteroatom precursor of urea, ammonium dihydrogen phosphate, diammonium hydrogen phosphate, ammonium fluoride, and/or ammonium hydrogen fluoride and performing supercritical reaction at 60-80 degrees C and 10-20 MPa for 6-10 hours. The pyrolysis is done at 900-1100 degrees C for 2 hours.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

TI Method for preparing traditional Chinese medicine active portion with anti-HBV effect involves taking sophora alopecuroide as raw material, using supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain medicine.
AU WANG G
   LI Y
   ZHANG Y
   ZHANG X
   YANG L
   CHEN N
   WU Z
AE UNIV JINAN GUANGDONG (UJIN-C)
   UNIV JINAN (UJIN-C)
GA 201966215P
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing traditional Chinese medicine active portion with anti-HBV effect involves taking sophora alopecuroide as raw material, using supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain the specific condition of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is that extraction pressure is 10-30 megapascal, at 30-40 degrees C and a flow speed of 5-15 per hour, where the extracting time is 2-3 hours.
   USE - Method for preparing traditional Chinese medicine for treating or preventing hepatitis(all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare traditional Chinese medicine has obvious anti-HBV effect, simple preparation, low preparation and labor cost and wide application prospect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Conditions: The extracting is carried out at 35 degrees C with a flow speed of 10 per hour for 2.5 hours. The preparation method further involves using 70-80 wt.% methanol of the ODS column chromatography on ODS column the sophora alopecuroide extract, firstly is 45% by volume fraction of rinsing to remove impurities, eluting with methanol then the volume fraction of 75% is collecting, condensing and drying to obtain the traditional Chinese medicine active portion with anti-HBV effect.

PT P
PN CN109772420-A; CN109772420-B
TI Carbon-supported indium nitride catalyst for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to formate, comprises carbon support and indium nitride that is supported on carbon support in particulate form.
AU HOU P
   KANG P
AE TECH INST PHYSICS & CHEM CHINESE ACAD (CAPC-C)
GA 201947024V
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon-supported indium nitride catalyst comprises a carbon support and indium nitride. The indium nitride is supported on the carbon support in a particulate form.
   USE - Carbon-supported indium nitride catalyst for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to formate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high selectivity to formate in the reaction of electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of carbon-supported indium nitride catalyst which involves (a) uniformly mixing the indium salt and the carbon carrier in water, and removing water in the obtained solution to obtain a precursor, (b) making the precursor to react under an ammonia gas atmosphere to obtain the carbon-supported indium nitride catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The loading amount of indium nitride is 4-15%. The mass ratio of indium salt to the carbon support is 1:5-2:1. Preferred Components: The carbon carrier is selected from conductive carbon black, carbon nanotubes, and graphene. The indium salt is selected from the group consisting of indium nitrate hexahydrate, indium chloride, indium acetylacetonate, and indium sulfate. The carbon support is selected from the group consisting of conductive carbon black, carbon nanotubes, and graphene. Preferred properties: The indium nitride has a particle diameter of 10-50 nm. The electrocatalyzed reduction of carbon dioxide to the formate is applied at a potential of -0.6 V (vs. RHE) to -1.0 V (vs. RHE). Preferred Conditions: The temperature is raised to 873-973 K at a rate of 2-5 K/minutes, and reaction in step b is performed at temperature for 3-12 hours, and cooled to room temperature. The temperature is first lowered to 573 K at a rate of 2-5 K/minutes, and naturally cooled to room temperature. Preferred Process: The reaction is carried out under normal pressure. The electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide is carried out in the aqueous phase.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)

PN CN109467113-A
TI Producing calcium carbonate powder comprises crushing marble to obtain marble powder, calcining marble powder, adding water, reacting and filtering, placing filtrate in reaction tank, filling carbon dioxide, carbonizing and dehydrating.
AU GONG S
AE LIANPING XINFU BUILDING MATERIALS CO LTD (LIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019279736
AB    NOVELTY - Producing calcium carbonate powder comprises (i) crushing marble to obtain marble powder, (ii) calcining marble powder, (iii) placing the calcined marble powder in a first reaction tank, adding water, reacting and filtering to remove impurity and placing the filtrate in a second reaction tank, (iv) filling carbon dioxide into the second reaction tank and carbonizing, and (v) centrifuging, filtering, dehydrating, drying, crushing and packaging.
   USE - The method is useful for producing calcium carbonate powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves purity of the product, reduces residual amount of calcium hydroxide, is environmentally friendly and increases utilization rate of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The marble is crushed into particle diameter of 0.5-1 microns. The mass ratio water and calcined marble powder is 1:6-8.Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the calcining is performed at 900 degrees C for 80-100 minutes, 850 degrees C for 60-100 minutes, 1000 degrees C for 120-200 minutes and 1100 degrees C for 100-120 minutes. In the step (iv), the carbon dioxide is introduced at flow rate of 300-400 ml/minute and each pulse rate of 3 seconds until the pH of the slurry is 7.

PN JP2019019066-A; JP6964867-B2
TI Manufacture of 2-oxazolidinone used as pharmaceutical intermediate, involves carrying out cyclization reaction by reacting propargylamine and carbon dioxide in presence of quaternary ammonium salt.
AU FUJITA K
   FUJII A
   CHOE J C
AE NAT INST ADVANCED IND SCI & TECHNOLOGY (NIIT-C)
GA 201913687H
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of 2-oxazolidinone (II) involves carrying out cyclization reaction by reacting propargylamine (I) and carbon dioxide in the presence of a quaternary ammonium salt.
   USE - Manufacture of 2-oxazolidinone used as pharmaceutical and agrochemical intermediate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical and can produce non-poisonous 2-oxazolidinone.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacture of 2-oxazolidinone of formula (II) involves carrying out cyclization reaction by reacting propargylamine of formula (I) and carbon dioxide in the presence of a quaternary ammonium salt of formula: R1R2R3R4N+X-.
   R1-R4 = 1-20C hydrocarbyl optionally containing N, S, O and/or halo;
   X- = anion;
   R5-R8 = H or 1-20C hydrocarbyl optionally containing N, S, O and/or halo;and
   R6+R7 = ring.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The cyclization reaction is carried out in an alcohol. The initial partial pressure of carbon dioxide in the reaction system in the cyclization reaction is 0.01-1.0 MPa.
   Preferred Definitions:
   X- = halide ion, hydroxide ion, hexafluorophosphate ion or acetate ion.

   USE - A carbonated alcoholic beverage.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacture of carbonated alcoholic beverage which involves dissolving alcohol, fermented milk, and water-soluble constituent in drinking water, dissolving carbon dioxide gas in primary raw material liquid to obtain secondary raw material liquid, injecting aloe mesophyll which has hexahedral shape to drink container, and filling secondary raw material liquid into drink container containing aloe mesophyll.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Compositions: Carbonated alcoholic beverage contains saccharides, acesulfame potassium, and sucralose as a sweetener. Saccharides are selected from sucrose, glucose, and/or high-fructose corn syrup. Alcohol content is 10-100 ml/L. The carbonated alcoholic beverage contains citric acid and/or sodium citrate as a sour agent. Preferred Condition: Carbon dioxide gas pressure is 0.07-0.4 MPa.

PT P
PN CN112143904-A
TI Recycling blast furnace molten hot slag and hot molten salt involves ammonium chloride process and/or ammonium sulfate process, and generating blast furnace molten hot slag, carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide.
AU HU C
   GONG J
   LU W
AE QINGDAO HESHENG INTELLIGENT ENVIRONMENTA (QING-Non-standard)
GA 2021030641
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling blast furnace molten hot slag and hot molten salt involves an ammonium chloride process and/or an ammonium sulfate process. The blast furnace molten hot slag and under oxygen or coal gas conditions in the hot molten slag quenching and tempering tank high-temperature pyrometallurgical precipitation and recovery of iron at the same time high-temperature desulfurization, generating blast furnace molten hot slag, carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide, sulfur dioxide through the ammonia scrubber, after washing, liquid ammonium sulfate is generated, and the carbon dioxide enters the carbonization tower after being washed and pressurized. The blast furnace molten hot slag and the excess saturated ammonium chloride solution are undergo a hot molten salt reaction in the leaching tank to generate liquid chlorate, solid acid insolubles and ammonia, and then entered in carbonization tower to generate ammonium bicarbonate with carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for recycling blast furnace molten hot slag and hot molten salt.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to recycle blast furnace molten hot slag and hot molten salt does not use hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid and alkali, saves the cost of acid and alkali, and effectively solves the anti-corrosion problem of the equipment. It uses the enthalpy of the hot slag while precipitation and recovery of iron in the blast furnace molten hot slag at high temperature. It can also extract and separate elements such as calcium, magnesium, aluminum, titanium, silicon, manganese and fluorine, and make full use of the thermal energy of the blast furnace molten hot slag, and realize the economical and reasonable recycling of each element in the blast furnace molten hot slag without pollution, and also utilized the energy and resources of blast furnace molten hot slag.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recycling blast furnace molten hot slag and hot molten salt involves an ammonium chloride process and/or an ammonium sulfate process. The blast furnace molten hot slag and under oxygen or coal gas conditions in the hot molten slag quenching and tempering tank high-temperature pyrometallurgical precipitation and recovery of iron at the same time high-temperature desulfurization, generating blast furnace molten hot slag, carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide, sulfur dioxide through the ammonia scrubber, after washing, liquid ammonium sulfate is generated, and the carbon dioxide enters the carbonization tower after being washed and pressurized. The blast furnace molten hot slag and the excess saturated ammonium chloride solution are undergo a hot molten salt reaction in the leaching tank to generate liquid chlorate, solid acid insolubles and ammonia, and then entered in carbonization tower to generate ammonium bicarbonate with carbon dioxide. The solid-liquid separation is carried out on the product to obtain solid acid-insoluble matter and liquid chlorate. The remaining liquid chlorate reacts with manganese carbonate to obtain liquid manganese chloride, solid calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate. The liquid manganese chloride is reacted with ammonium bicarbonate, and the product is separated into solid and liquid to obtain solid manganese carbonate and liquid ammonium chloride. The generated blast furnace molten hot slag or solid acid-insoluble matter is subjected to high temperature desulfurization by high-temperature fire precipitation and recovery of iron in the hot molten slag desulfurization and tempering tank under oxygen or coal gas conditions to produce hot blast furnace molten hot slag or acid insoluble matter, sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide. The hot blast furnace molten hot slag or acid-insoluble matter reacts with excess saturated ammonium sulfate solution in the leaching tank for the second time hot molten salt reaction, solid-liquid separation obtains solid calcium sulfate and acid-insoluble matter, and liquid sulfate and silicon fluoride gas. The liquid sulfate is adsorbed and extracted by resin to extract the rare elements, silicon fluoride gas enters the ammonia scrubber to react with ammonia water, and the product is separated from solid and liquid to obtain solid white carbon black and liquid ammonium bifluoride. The remaining liquid sulfate is heated to undergo a hydrolysis reaction, and the product is separated from the solid and liquid to obtain solid titanium dioxide and liquid aluminum ammonium sulfate. The liquid ammonium aluminum sulfate is reacted with ammonium bicarbonate, and the product is separated from solid and liquid to obtain solid ammonium aluminum carbonate and liquid ammonium sulfate. The solid ammonium aluminum carbonate is decomposed at high temperature to produce solid alumina, carbon dioxide and ammonia, and carbon dioxide and ammonia are absorbed by water to form ammonia and carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The calcium fluoride is added to the hot molten slag quenching and tempering pool or the hot molten slag desulfurization and tempering pool. The solid calcium sulfate after solid-liquid separation is reacted with ammonium bicarbonate, and the product is gravity-sorted to separate acid-insoluble matter and then subjected to solid-liquid separation to obtain liquid ammonium sulfate and solid calcium carbonate. The sulfur dioxide is washed by the ammonia washing tower to generate liquid ammonium sulfate, and the carbon dioxide enters the carbonization tower after being washed and pressurized. The liquid ammonium chloride is returned to the leaching tank for recycling, and the liquid ammonium sulfate after solid-liquid separation is returned to the leaching tank for recycling. The waste heat of the hot molten slag quenching and tempering pool and the hot molten slag desulfurization and tempering pool are recycled and utilized. The electric heating is used to heat hot molten slag quenching and tempering tank and the hot molten slag desulfurization and tempering tank respectively. The solid ammonium bifluoride is obtained by vacuum concentration and evaporation of the liquid ammonium bifluoride is returned to the leaching tank for recycling to participate in the secondary hot molten salt reaction.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).); M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).); Q77 (Furnaces, kilns, ovens, retorts (F27))

PN CN110568163-A
TI High-precision calculation of ship fuel sulfur content based on ship exhaust gas monitoring data comprises e.g. measuring ship tail gas data including carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide concentrations calculating and comparing result.
AU ZHOU F
   GU J
   CHEN W
   NI X
   AN B
AE SHANGHAI ANXIN INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   PRC PUDONG MARITIME SAFETY ADMINISTRATIO (PRCP-Non-standard)
GA 2019A8658T
AB    NOVELTY - High-precision calculation of ship fuel sulfur content based on ship exhaust gas monitoring data comprises e.g. measuring ship tail gas data including carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide concentrations in the atmosphere as background values, measuring concentrations of carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide concentrations in the plume of the ship's exhaust gas as measured values to obtain two sets of carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide concentration data, recoring as NBG and NTEST, combining the two sets according to the order of background and measured values to form a set of time series data, calculating average of new set of time series data after every 10 numbers and calculating average of few according to the actual data number to form a new set of average time series data numbers, providing minimum value of sulfur dioxide in the background data of the first NBG averages, recording it as MINSO2BG, calculating and comparing the calculation result.
   USE - The method is useful for high-precision calculation of ship fuel sulfur content based on ship exhaust gas monitoring data.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and accurate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - High-precision calculation of ship fuel sulfur content based on ship exhaust gas monitoring data comprises (i) measuring ship tail gas data including carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide concentrations in the atmosphere as background values, measuring concentrations of carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide concentrations in the plume of the ship's exhaust gas as measured values to obtain two sets of carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide concentration data, recoring as NBG and NTEST, combining the two sets according to the order of background and measured values to form a set of time series data, calculating average of new set of time series data after every 10 numbers and calculating average of few according to the actual data number to form a new set of average time series data numbers, (ii) providing minimum value of sulfur dioxide in the background data of the first NBG averages, recording it as MINSO2BG, returning value position NSO2BG, in (NSO2BG-10, NSO2BG + 10) (0 less than NSO2BG10 is less than NSO2BG + 10 is less than NBG + 1, if NSO2BG-10 is less than 1, the taking 1 on the left side of the interval, if NSO2BG + 10 is greater than NBG, then taking NBG on the right side of the interval), taking the minimum value of carbon dioxide in the data in the interval position and recording as MINCO2BG, (iii) taking the maximum value of sulfur dioxide in (NBG + 1, NTOTAL) average measurement data, recording it as MAXSO2, returning the value position NSO2, in (NSO2-10, NSO2 + 10) (NBG is less than NSO2-10 is less than NSO2 + 10 is less than NTEST + 1, if NSO2-10 is less than NBG + 1, then taking NBG + 1 at the left side of the interval, if NSO2 + 10 is greater than NTEST, then taking NTEST on the right side of the interval), and (iv) substituting values MAXSO2, MINCO2BG, MAXSO2, and MAXCO2 into the calculation equation of the ship's fuel sulfur content, and comparing the calculation result with the standard threshold to estimate whether the ship's fuel sulfur content exceeds the standard, taking the maximum value of carbon dioxide from the data in the interval position and recording as MAXCO2, where calculation equation for the sulfur content of fuel in ships is FSC (% m/m) = (( Delta SO2 concentration (ppm)x 32)/( Delta CO2 concentration (ppm)x 12/0.87))x 100 = ( Delta SO2 concentration (ppm)/ Delta CO2 concentration (ppm)x 0.232), where Delta SO2 concentration is the difference between the sulfur dioxide test concentration value and the sulfur dioxide background concentration value, that is MAXSO2-MAXSO2, the unit is ppm, Delta CO2 concentration is the difference between the carbon dioxide test concentration value and the carbon dioxide background concentration value i.e. MAXCO2-MINCO2BG, the unit is ppm, the molecular weight of sulfur (S) is 32, the molecular weight of carbon (C) is 12, and the carbon content of ship fuel with different sulfur content is not much different, and is about 87%.

PN WO2022212650-A1; US2022317080-A1
TI Deriving alkalinity measurement, comprises e.g.            introducing fluid sample comprising inorganic carbon in            reaction chamber of analyzer, and measuring phosphate            amount of fluid sample using colorimetric reagent.

AE HACH CO (DANH-C)
   HACH CO (DANH-C)
GA 2022C7550L
AB    NOVELTY - A method for deriving an alkalinity                measurement involves introducing a fluid sample                comprising inorganic carbon into a reaction chamber                of an analyzer, measuring a phosphate amount of the                fluid sample, measuring a pH of the fluid sample                which correlates to a hydroxide amount of the fluid                sample using a pH sensor of the analyzer,                introducing an acid reagent to convert the                inorganic carbon to carbon dioxide, applying a                positive potential, introducing at least one acid                reagent comprising a metallic catalyst that                converts the carbonate and the partially oxidized                species to carbon dioxide, determining total                organic carbon by detecting an amount of carbon                dioxide produced by the oxidation, and determining                a derived alkalinity. The reaction chamber includes                an electrochemical cell comprising the sp3                substituted solid carbon electrode doped with a                conductivity elevating composition. The inorganic                carbon comprises carbonate and bicarbonate.
   USE - Method for deriving alkalinity measurement for                ensuring water purity in drinking water,                pharmaceuticals, chemicals and other manufacturing                applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables accurate determination of                alkalinity in aqueous sample with high                sensitivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A method for deriving an alkalinity                measurement involves introducing a fluid sample                comprising inorganic carbon into a reaction chamber                of an analyzer, measuring a phosphate amount of the                fluid sample using a colorimetric reagent in a                phosphate analyzer of the analyzer, measuring a pH                of the fluid sample which correlates to a hydroxide                amount of the fluid sample using a pH sensor of the                analyzer, introducing an acid reagent to convert                the inorganic carbon to carbon dioxide, applying a                positive potential sufficient to oxidize organics                in the fluid sample to produce carbonate and                partially oxidized organics to sp3 substituted                carbon electrode using a generator of the analyzer,                introducing at least one acid reagent comprising a                metallic catalyst that converts the carbonate and                the partially oxidized species to carbon dioxide                before or simultaneously during the application of                the positive potential to the sp3 substituted                carbon electrode in the reaction chamber,                determining total organic carbon by detecting an                amount of carbon dioxide produced by the oxidation                using at least one detector of the analyzer, and                determining a derived alkalinity based upon the                phosphate amount, the hydroxide amount and the                amount of carbon dioxide generated from the                inorganic carbon. The reaction chamber includes an                electrochemical cell comprising the sp3 substituted                solid carbon electrode doped with a conductivity                elevating composition. The inorganic carbon                comprises carbonate and bicarbonate. INDEPENDENT                CLAIMS are included for the following:
   device for deriving an alkalinity measurement;                and
   product for measuring alkalinity in a                sample.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of derived                alkalinity method and system.
