PN CN112138654-A
TI Preparing carbon dioxide hydrogenation methanation reaction catalyst comprises e.g. preparing liquid phase titanium salt precursor, mixed aqueous solution of zirconium metal salt and magnesium metal salt and adding alkali liquid.
AU YANG Z
   XING Z
   XING L
AE YANG Z (YANG-Individual)
   XING Z (XING-Individual)
   XING L (XING-Individual)
GA 202103130Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide hydrogenation methanation reaction catalyst comprises e.g. (i) preparing liquid phase titanium salt precursor, mixed aqueous solution of zirconium metal salt and magnesium metal salt and adding alkali liquid into the prepared mixed solution to obtain suspension; (ii) centrifuging the precipitate in the suspension obtained in the step (i), adding metal powder into the precipitate, mixing uniformly, molding, drying and roasting to obtain the catalyst carrier, the metal powder comprises at least one of zinc powder, aluminum powder or tin powder; (iii) loading the active component metal element and auxiliary agent element on carrier obtained in step (ii), drying and roasting to obtain the catalyst precursor, where the active component comprise at least one of platinum, ruthenium, nickel or cobalt auxiliary agent element comprises rhenium, iridium, boron or phosphorus; and (iv) treating the catalyst precursor obtained in the step (iii) by sodium hydroxide solution.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon dioxide hydrogenation methanation reaction catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing carbon dioxide hydrogenation methanation reaction catalyst comprises (i) preparing liquid phase titanium salt precursor, mixed aqueous solution of zirconium metal salt and magnesium metal salt and adding alkali liquid into the prepared mixed solution to obtain suspension; (ii) centrifuging the precipitate in the suspension obtained in the step (i), adding metal powder into the precipitate, mixing uniformly, molding, drying and roasting to obtain the catalyst carrier, the metal powder comprises at least one of zinc powder, aluminum powder or tin powder, the obtained catalyst carrier comprises titanium dioxide, zirconium dioxide, magnesium oxide and metal powder at the mass ratio of 50-80:5-30:1-10:1-10:1-10; (iii) loading the active component metal element and auxiliary agent element on the carrier obtained in step (ii), drying and roasting to obtain the catalyst precursor, where the active component comprise at least one of platinum, ruthenium, nickel or cobalt the auxiliary agent element comprises rhenium, iridium, boron or phosphorus; and (iv) treating the catalyst precursor obtained in the step (iii) by sodium hydroxide solution. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for application the catalyst for the hydromethanation of carbon dioxide, comprising (a) using the gas regeneration reaction in the enclosed space of the underwater vehicle and the gas regeneration reaction of the environmental control life support system; and (b) hydrogenating carbon dioxide in the purification of synthetic ammonia feed gas or hydromethanation of carbon dioxide enriched in the purification process of biogas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the method comprises dropping the liquid titanium salt precursor into deionized water under ice-water bath conditions to form a transparent solution and dissolving zirconium-containing metal salt and magnesium-containing metal salt in a transparent solution to obtain a mixed aqueous solution. In the step (ii), the calcination temperature is 200-600 degrees C. In the step (iii), the method comprises using an equal volume impregnation method, an excess impregnation method or an alkali precipitation method to load the active component metal elements and the auxiliary metal elements on the solid solution carrier obtained in step (ii). Preferred Components: The liquid phase titanium salt precursor is titanium tetrachloride, tetrabutyl titanate, tetraethyl titanate or tetraisopropyl titanate, the zirconium metal salt is zirconyl chloride or zirconyl chloride hydrate and the magnesium metal salt is magnesium nitrate or magnesium chloride. The alkali solution comprises sodium aqueous solution, potassium aqueous solution or ammonia aqueous solution, and adding alkali solution for precipitation until pH=12-14. In the step (iv), the mass concentration of the sodium chloride solution is 8-20 wt.%. The loading amount of the active component metal element is 0.5-10 wt.% of the catalyst carrier and the total loading of the auxiliary element is 0.5-5 wt.% the catalyst carrier.

PN JP2019156761-A; WO2019176745-A1; DE112019001261-T5; JP7061484-B2
TI Apparatus for producing methane from source gas, has controller to operate flow path switching device for connecting generation gas line and off-gas line, when detected methane concentration is less than preset concentration.
AU IWAKI M
   NISHIYAMA A
   WARAYA Y
   MASAMOTO M
   WARATANI Y
AE KAWASAKI HEAVY IND LTD (KAWJ-C)
   KAWASAKI JUKOGYO KK (KAWJ-C)
   KAWASAKI HEAVY IND LTD (KAWJ-C)
GA 2019806132
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (100) has a reactor (R1,R2) for accommodating a methanation catalyst. A source gas supply line (2) supplies a source gas to the reactor. A generation gas line (30) connects the reactor and a quality control apparatus (9) and sends a generated gas to the quality control apparatus. A product gas tank (4) stores the product gas. A product gas line (40) connects the quality control apparatus and the product gas tank, and sends the product gas from the quality control apparatus to the product gas tank. An off-gas line (80) is connected with the quality control apparatus. The generation gas line and the product gas line are connected. A controller for operating a flow path switching device is provided so that the generation gas line and the off-gas line are connected, when the detected methane concentration is less than the predetermined concentration.
   USE - Apparatus for producing methane from source gas containing carbon-dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The methane concentration of product gas is controllable even when the apparatus into which methane is isolated from generated gas is excluded.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The quality control apparatus includes a methane concentration meter which detects the methane concentration of the generated gas, and the flow-path switching device which switches a flow path connected with the generation gas line between the product gas line and the off-gas line. A downstream end portion of the off-gas line is connected with the source gas supply line. The quality control apparatus is equipped with several off-gas containers parallelly connected to the generation gas line. A gas having concentration less than the predetermined concentration is retained in the off-gas containers. A hydrogen separator is provided in the product gas line. The hydrogen separator includes a hydrogen-permeable membrane, a methane flow path along which the methane provided in the one side passes through the hydrogen-permeable membrane, a carbon-dioxide flow path along which the carbon dioxide before mixing with the hydrogen provided in the other side passes through the hydrogen-permeable membrane. A temperature sensor (T1,T2) detects the temperature of the catalyst of the reactor. A hydrogen supply line (24) is provided in the source gas supply line and which supplies the hydrogen to a buffer tank (21). A carbon-dioxide supply line (25) supplies the carbon dioxide to the buffer tank. The discharge valve is provided in the carbon-dioxide supply line. A portion of the carbon dioxide passing through the carbon-dioxide supply line is discharged to system outside based on the temperature of the detected catalyst, when the temperature of the catalyst is more than predetermined temperature. A discharge-valve control apparatus (27) operates a discharge valve (26) so that the discharge valve is closed, when the temperature of the catalyst is less than predetermined temperature. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a methane production method involves sending the generated gas to the off-gas line by mixing the generated gas in the source gas, and sending the generated gas to the off-gas line by sending the generated gas to several gas bottles. A hydrogen partial pressure difference with the carbon dioxide before the process of sending the generated gas to the product gas tank is mixed with the methane separated from the generated gas and the hydrogen is utilized.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view illustrating the entire structure of the methane production apparatus.

   Carbon-dioxide supply line (25)
   Discharge valve (26)
   Discharge-valve control apparatus (27)
   Generation gas line (30)
   Product gas line (40)
   Off-gas line (80)
   Methane production apparatus (100)
   Reactors (R1,R2)
   Temperature sensors (T1,T2)

PT P
PN CN110746284-A
TI Extracting and separating beta-acid homologues from hop comprises e.g. mixing ethanol and hop carbon dioxide extract, separating, introducing carbon dioxide gas, adjusting pH, filtering, collecting separated solid, washing and drying.
AU CHEN P
   LIU Z
   LIU Y
   DU W
   FAN X
   SHI M
   YANG J
AE UNIV XINJIANG (UXIJ-C)
GA 202011701F
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting and separating b~-acid homologues from hop comprises (i) adding 0.5-1 wt.% ethanol into hop carbon dioxide extract, mixing uniformly in water bath at 50-60 degrees C, adding distilled water into hop carbon dioxide extract, allowing to stand, filtering, separating and removing insoluble oil to obtain clear filtrate, slowly introducing carbon dioxide gas into the filtrate while stirring and adjusting pH to 8-8.5, continuously stirring, filtering, collecting the separated solid, washing with distilled water and drying the separated solid to obtain b~-acid extract; (ii) placing b~-acid extract in a clean beaker on a water bath and dropping aqueous methanol solution until b~-acid extract dissolves, filtering quickly, separating the precipitate and filtering to obtain crystal and crystallizing twice to obtain b~-acid homolog I; and (iii) taking b~-acid homolog I, dropping aqueous methanol solution and dissolving completely, separating the precipitate, filtering and crystallizing twice.
   USE - The homologues are useful as bacteriostat for inhibiting food spoiling bacteria (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, economical and easy, and is suitable for large-scale.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting and separating b~-acid homologues from hop comprises (i) adding 0.5-1 wt.% ethanol into hop carbon dioxide extract, placing in water bath at 50-60 degrees C and uniformly mixing, adding 5-25 wt.% alkaline aqueous solution and adjusting the pH to 12-14, adding 5-20 wt.% distilled water into hop carbon dioxide extract, adding 5-25 wt.% alkaline aqueous solution and adjusting the pH to 12-14, allowing to stand for 20-40 minutes, filtering, separating and removing insoluble oil to obtain clear filtrate, slowly introducing carbon dioxide gas into the filtrate while stirring and adjusting pH to 8-8.5, stopping aeration when the filtrate appears turbid and a small amount of precipitate appears, continuously stirring for 20-40 minutes, allowing to stand for 30 minutes, filtering, collecting the separated solid, washing with distilled water until the filtrate is neutral and drying the separated solid to obtain b~-acid extract; (ii) taking b~-acid extract in a clean beaker, placing in water bath at 50 degrees C and dropping 90 wt.% aqueous methanol solution until b~-acid extract dissolves, filtering quickly, allowing to stand at 4 degrees C for 1-2 hours, separating the precipitate and filtering to obtain crystal, and repeating crystallizing step twice to obtain b~-acid homolog I; (iii) taking b~-acid homolog I, dropping 90 wt.% aqueous methanol solution at 50 degrees C and dissolving completely, allowing to stand at room temperature for 1-2 hours, separating the precipitate, filtering to obtain crystal, and repeating crystallizing step twice to obtain b~-acid homolog II; (iv) taking beta -acid homolog I, dripping 90 wt.% aqueous methanol solution at 50 degrees C and dissolving completely, introducing carbon dioxide gas and adjusting the pH to 7-7.5, stopping aeration when precipitate appears, stirring for 10-30 minutes, allowing to stand for 30 minutes, filtering to obtain crystal, washing crystal with distilled water to neutral and drying to obtain b~-acid homolog III and (v) taking b~-acid crude extract obtained in the step (i), dropping hexane solution at 50 degrees C until b~-acid dissolves completely, filtering quickly, allowing to stand at 4 degrees C for 1-2 hours, separating the precipitate, filtering to obtain crystal and repeating crystallizing step twice to obtain the b~-acid homolog IV.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the b~-acid extract is not less than 80 wt.%. In the step (ii), the b~-acid homolog I is not less than 98% and the mass ratio of homolog co-lupulone and n + ad-lupulone is 1:1. In the step (iii), the b~-acid homolog II is not less than 98% and the mass ratio of homolog co-lupulone and n + ad-lupulone is 10:1. In the step (iv), the b~-acid homolog III is not less than 98% and the mass ratio of homolog co-lupulone and n + ad-lupulone is 1:4. In the step (v), the b~-acid homolog IV is not less than 98% and the mass ratio of homolog co-lupulone and n + ad-lupulone is 20:1. In the step (i), the alkaline aqueous solution is aqueous solution of sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide, preferably aqueous solution of potassium hydroxide and the concentration is 10-20 wt.%. Preferred Method: In the step (i) and step (iv), the flow rate of carbon dioxide gas is 0.1-1 ml/min.
   ACTIVITY - Antibacterial.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)
MC D03-A; D03-H02

AB    NOVELTY - The device has an electric heating furnace                (1), a bubbling pipe (2), a feed inlet (3) and a                reaction kettle (10). The                electric heating furnace is arranged around the                reaction kettle to heat the solid halide raw                material added into the reaction kettle to form                liquid molten salt, and carbon dioxide from a                carbon dioxide gas source is introduced below a                liquid surface of the liquid molten salt through                the bubbling pipe to carry out a high-temperature                gas-solid phase decarburization reaction. An argon                from an argon gas source is introduced below the                liquid surface of the liquid molten salt through                the bubbling pipe to remove the carbon dioxide                after the high-temperature gas-solid phase                decarburization reaction to realize decarburization                purification.

The CO2 is introduced below the                liquid surface of the liquid molten salt through                the bubbling pipe below to remove the carbon CO2                after the high temperature gas-solid phase                decarbonation reaction, thus realizing the                decarberization purification.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                chemical decarburization method of halide molten                salt.

PT P
PN CN111848829-A
TI Seaweed chemical calcification process, by monitoring sodium alginate, adding mixture of calcium chloride and calcium hydroxide, transporting carbon dioxide, centrifuging, adding calcium hydroxide, and filtering to obtain clear liquid.
AU ZHANG Y
AE ZHANG Y (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 2020A93776
AB    NOVELTY - Seaweed chemical calcification process, involves (i) monitoring the content of sodium alginate in a diluted solution (A), adding a mixture of calcium chloride and calcium hydroxide, stirring, and centrifuging to obtain calcium alginate sediment and calcification wastewater (A), (ii) monitoring the concentration and conductivity of calcium(2+) ions (Ca2+) in the wastewater (A), transporting carbon dioxide to make calcium hydroxide completely react to produce calcium carbonate precipitate and water, and filtering to obtain calcium carbonate and clear liquid, (iii) monitoring the content of sodium alginate in a diluted solution (B), adding calcium carbonate, carbon dioxide, and calcium chloride solutions, and centrifuging to obtain calcium alginate sediment and calcified wastewater (B), and (iv) monitoring the concentration and conductivity of Ca2+ in the wastewater (B), quantitatively adding calcium hydroxide, and filtering to obtain calcium carbonate and clear liquid.
   USE - Seaweed chemical calcification process.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is green and environmentally-friendly, improves on the traditional technology, ensures the complete calcification and the concentration of Ca2+ in the water body, reduces the consumption of calcium chloride, reduces the production cost, realizes the recycling of calcium carbonate, clear liquid and carbon dioxide, ensures sufficient purification and effectively reduces the processing cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Seaweed chemical calcification process, involves (i) monitoring the content of sodium alginate in a diluted solution (A), quantitatively adding a mixture of calcium chloride and calcium hydroxide, stirring, mixing, and centrifuging to obtain calcium alginate sediment and calcification wastewater (A), (ii) monitoring the concentration and conductivity of calcium(2+) ions (Ca2+) in the wastewater (A), quantitatively transporting carbon dioxide to make calcium hydroxide completely react to produce calcium carbonate precipitate and water, and filtering to obtain calcium carbonate and clear liquid, (iii) monitoring the content of sodium alginate in a diluted solution (B), sequentially adding calcium carbonate, carbon dioxide, and calcium chloride solutions to it, stirring, mixing and centrifuging to obtain calcium alginate sediment and calcified wastewater (B), (iv) monitoring the concentration and conductivity of Ca2+ in the wastewater (B), quantitatively adding calcium hydroxide, and the reaction produces calcium carbonate precipitate and water, and filtering to obtain calcium carbonate and clear liquid, and (v) interspersing and using steps (i)-(ii) and (iii)-(iv).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: The calcium carbonate and clear liquid in steps (ii) and (iv) are used as additives and dilution water for recycling. Preferred Conditions: In step (i), the ratio of the total mass of sodium alginate to the total added amount of the mixture is 1:2, and the mass ratio of calcium chloride to calcium hydroxide in the mixture is 1:4. In the steps (i) and (iii), the stirring step is carried out at 50 rpm for 20 minutes, and the centrifugation is carried out at 500 rpm for 30 minutes. The ratio of the total mass of sodium alginate to the total amount of calcium carbonate added in step (iii) is 1:1.5. In step (iii), the ratio of the total mass of sodium alginate to the total added amount of the calcium chloride solution is 4:1, and the concentration of the calcium chloride solution is 10%.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The porous material is                  carbon material, silica gel and/or polyester                  material porous molecular sieve, preferably                  selected carbon nano tube, activated carbon,                  silica gel, MCM molecular sieve series, ZSM                  molecular sieve series, SBA molecular sieve                  series and/or KIT molecular sieve series. The                  nitrogen content of the amino-enriched asphalt is                  10-25%. The asphalt containing polycyclic                  aromatic hydrocarbon is petroleum asphalt, coal                  tar asphalt and/or asphalt natural. The                  polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon is 9-30C fused                  aromatic hydrocarbon, preferably naphthalene,                  acenaphthylene, acenaphthylene, fluorene,                  phenanthrene, anthracene, pyrene, benzo (a)                  anthracene, benzo (b) fluoranthene, benzo (k)                  fluoranthene, benzo (a) pyrene, indeno (1,2,3-cd)                  pyrene, dibenzo (a, h) anthracene, benzo (g,h,i)                  perylene, 1-methyl naphthalene and/or 2-methyl                  naphthalene. The organic polycyclic amine                  compound is 4-10C organic polycyclic amine                  compound, preferably hexamethylene tetramine,                  triethylene diamine, diethylenetriamine and/or                  cyclohexane. The acidic organic solvent system                  comprises a organic solvent and an organic                  carboxylic acid. The organic solvent is polar                  solvent, preferably N-methyl pyrrolidone, N,                  N-dimethyl formamide and/or N, N-dimethyl                  acetamide. The organic carboxylic acid is 1-6C                  organic carboxylic acid, preferably glacial                  acetic acid, glycolic acid, citric acid and/or                  formic acid. The mass ratio of the polycyclic                  aromatic hydrocarbon and/or the polycyclic                  aromatic hydrocarbon-containing asphalt and                  organic polycyclic amine compound is 1:(0.01-5),                  preferably 1:(0.5-3); the mass ratio of the                  polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon and/or the                  polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon-containing                  asphalt to the organic carboxylic acid is                  1:(0.001-0.5), preferably 1:(0.01-0.2). The mass                  ratio of the material porous the amino-enriched                  asphalt is 1:(1-10), preferably 1:(1-5).Preferred Method: The preparation of the                  amino-enriched asphalt comprises reacting the                  polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon and/or the                  asphalt containing the polycyclic aromatic                  hydrocarbon with the organic polycyclic amine                  compound in the acidic organic solvent.

PT P
PN CN112625231-A; CN112625231-B
TI Preparing catalyst useful for preparing polyols, and copolymerizing carbon dioxide and epoxide comprises carrying out polymerization reaction under the action of first initiator, double metal cyanide and epoxide.
AU QIAO L
   FU S
   WANG X
   MIAO Y
   WANG F
AE CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
GA 2021401134
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing catalyst comprises carrying out polymerization reaction under the action of first initiator, double metal cyanide and epoxide, where the first initiator comprises active hydrogen-containing compound, and/or active hydrogen-containing polymer.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for preparing polyols, and copolymerizing carbon dioxide and epoxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has carbonate units content in the obtained copolymerization product is relatively high; and has relatively less cyclic carbonate content.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) catalyst prepared by the above method; and
   (2) preparing poly(carbonate-ether) polyol comprising polymerizing carbon dioxide and epoxide under the action of catalyst and second initiator to obtain poly(carbonate-ether) polyol, where the second initiator comprises active hydrogen-containing compounds and/or active hydrogen-containing polymers.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first initiator comprises at least one of 1-10C alcohol compounds, carboxylic acid compounds, and hydroxy-containing polymers with a molecular weight of less than 2000 g/mol. The 1-10C alcohol compound comprises ethylene glycol, 1,2-propanediol, 1,3-propanediol, 1,4-butanediol, 1,2-butanediol, 1,3-butanediol, 1,5-pentanediol, 1,5-hexanediol, 1,6-hexanediol, 1,7-heptanediol, 1,8-octanediol, decanediol, 1,3-cyclopentanediol, 1,2-cyclohexanediol, 1,3-cyclohexanediol, 1,4-cyclohexanediol, bisphenol A, neopentyl glycol, 1,2-cyclohexane dimethanol, diethylene glycol, triethylene glycol, tetraethylene glycol, dipropylene glycol, tripropylene glycol, glycerol, trimethylolpropane, pentaerythritol, sorbitol, sucrose, starch, and/or cellulose. The carboxylic acid compound comprises oxalic acid, malonate, succinic acid, glutaric acid, adipic acid, pimelic acid, suberic acid, azelaic acid, sebacic acid, dodecanedioic acid, 1,3-pyruvate, malic acid, citric acid, maleic acid, fumaric acid, itaconic acid, gamma -hydroxybutyric acid, gamma -aminobutyric acid, ricinoleic acid, salicylic acid, hippuric acid, aconitic acid, transaconitic acid, 1,2-cyclohexanedicarboxylic acid, 1,3-cyclohexanedicarboxylic acid, 1,4-cyclohexanedicarboxylic acid, phthalic acid, isophthalic acid, terephthalic acid, m-phthalic acid, terephthalic acid, trimesic acid, and/or pyromellitic acid. The functionality of hydroxy-containing polymers with a molecular weight of less than 2000 g/mol is 2-8. The epoxide comprises ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, butylene oxide, cyclohexane oxide, epichlorohydrin and/or styrene oxide. The mass ratio of double metal cyanide to first initiator is 1:0.1-50. The mass ratio of epoxide to first initiator is 0.5-20:1-3. The second initiator comprises 1-10 C alcohol compounds, carboxylic acid compounds, and hydroxy-containing polymers with a molecular weight of less than 2000 g/mol. Preferred Method: The double metal cyanide compound is prepared by (i) mixing tert-butanol, water and zinc salt compound to obtain solution a, and mixing soluble potassium salt with deionized water to obtain solution b, and (ii) adding solution b to solution a, stirring, reacting, carrying out solid-liquid separation, and drying to obtain double metal cyanide based on zinc cobalt cyanide complex (Zn3(Co(CN)6)2). The polymerization reaction is carried out under protective gas conditions, or oxygen-free conditions at 0-150 degrees C, where the protective gas is an inert gas and/or carbon dioxide. The polymerization reaction is carried out at 5-120 degrees C for 1-48 hours, where the carbon dioxide pressure of polymerization reaction is 0-12 MPa.
DC A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.)
MC A02-A06; A05-E06A; A05-E09; A10-D; A10-D03

AU ZHAO J
   BAO F
   WANG W

AE CHINESE ACAD SCI CHEM INST (CSCI-C)
GA 201962867K
AB    NOVELTY - Copper and nitrogen doped carbon-based catalyst comprises hetero element doping, where hetero element includes copper and nitrogen elements.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to syngas (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing copper and nitrogen doped carbon-based catalyst comprising calcining copper-zeolitic imidazolate framework-8 at 1000 degrees C for 2-4 hours;
   (2) copper and nitrogen doped carbon-based catalyst obtained by above method; and
   (3) method of electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to syngas, comprising (i) coating catalyst according on glassy carbon electrode as a working electrode, and (ii) taking hydrogen-type electrolytic cell to perform constant voltage electrolysis in closed three-electrode system, where working electrode and reference electrode are at one end and counter electrode is at the other end.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst does not have copper particles when observed under transmission electron microscope. The specific surface area copper and nitrogen-doped carbon-based catalyst is 600-1000 m2/g, 832 m2/g; content of copper in catalyst is 5-10 wt.%; and content of nitrogen in catalyst is 2-10 wt.%. The volume ratio between carbon dioxide and syngas is 0.25-2:1. Preferred Method: The method for calcining copper-zeolitic imidazolate framework-8 specifically comprises heating copper-zeolitic imidazolate framework-8 at 1000 degrees C at a heating rate of 5-10 degrees C/minute for 2-4 hours, naturally cooling to room temperature obtain final product, where method for preparing copper-zeolitic imidazolate framework-8 comprises (i) dissolving 10.8 g dimethylimidazole, 4.7 g zinc acetate dihydrate and 1.2 g copper nitrate in 800 ml methanol, ultrasonic mixing for 5 minutes, heating and stirring at 50 degrees C for 1 hours to obtain crude product, and (ii) washing crude product for 3 times with methanol and drying in oven at 70 degrees C for 8 hours. The method comprises heating at a rate of 5-10 degrees C/minute, and calcining at 1000 degrees C for 2-4 hours.

PT P
PN CN109651150-A; CN109651150-B
TI Preparing vitamin A acetate comprises mixing triphenylphosphine and acid in supercritical carbon dioxide, contacting the mixture with vinyl-beta-ionol to form salt reaction to obtain C15 phosphonium salt, and adding the solvent water.
AU LIN L
   LIANG H
   ZHANG T
   LV Y
   LI Y
   ZHANG X
   CHENG X
   WANG Y
   ZHU L
AE WANHUA CHEM GROUP CO LTD (WHCH-C)
GA 201939383B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing vitamin A acetate comprises (i) mixing triphenylphosphine and acid in supercritical carbon dioxide, contacting the resulting mixture with vinyl beta -ionol to form salt reaction to obtain C15 phosphonium salt, (ii) adding the solvent water, ionic liquid and C5 aldehyde to the system, releasing the carbon dioxide for solvent replacement, and uniformly mixing the materials, (iii) adding the extractant to the system after the solvent replacement in the step (ii), adding alkali solution to carrying out the wittig reaction.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing vitamin A acetate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is safe and easy to control, has mild reaction, high purity and product yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acid is hydrochloric acid and hydrobromic acid. In the step (i), the molar ratio of the vinyl beta -ionol and acid is 1:1 to 1:1.1, preferably 1:1.0-1 to 1.06, where the molar ratio of the vinyl beta -ileol and triphenylphosphine is 1:1 to 1:1.1, preferably 1:1.0 to 1:1.06. The ionic liquid is 1-butyl-3-methyl imidazole ((Bmim)Im), hydroxy 1-butyl-3-methylimidazole, 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium carbonate ((Bmim)2CO3), and/or 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium acetate ((Bmim)(CH3COO)). In the step (ii), the solvent water is used in an amount of 2-10 times, preferably 4-8 times the mass of triphenylphosphine and/or the molar ratio of the C5 aldehyde and vinyl-beta ionol is 1.05:1-1.2:1, preferably 1.1:1-1.15:1, and/or, the ionic liquid is used in an amount of 1-12 wt.%, preferably1.5-6 wt.%, based on the amount of the solvent water. The extracting agent is n-hexane, petroleum ether, n-heptane, n-pentane, methylcyclohexane and cyclohexane, and/or the volume ratio of the extracting agent and solvent water is 1:4-2:1, preferably 1:3-1:1. The alkali liquid is aqueous potassium hydroxide solution, aqueous sodium hydroxide solution, aqueous lithium hydroxide solution, aqueous ammonium carbonate solution, aqueous sodium carbonate solution, aqueous potassium carbonate solution, and/or aqueous ammonia solution, preferably aqueous potassium hydroxide solution or hydrogen, aqueous solution of sodium oxide, an aqueous solution of sodium carbonate, and/or aqueous solution of potassium carbonate, where the molar ratio of the solute base and vinyl beta ionol in the lye is 1.05:1-2:1, preferably 1.1/1-1.6/1. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the reaction pressure of the salt formation reaction is 12-30 MPa, preferably 14 to 25 MPa, where the reaction temperature at 35-70 degrees C, preferably 45-60 degrees C, and the reaction time is 0.5-2.5 hours, preferably 1-2 hours. In the step (iii), the reaction temperature of the wittig reaction is 25-70 degrees C, preferably 35-50 degrees C, and the reaction time is 1-3 hours, preferably 1.5-2.5 hours. In the step (iii), the reaction product is subjected to phase separation, and the solid phase and filtrate phase are separated, the solid phase is washed and separated by the extracting agent, the obtained washing liquid is mixed with the filtrate and subjected to phase separation to obtain an extractant solution phase containing vitamin A acetate and solvent aqueous phase containing the ionic liquid, preferably, the aqueous phase of the ionic liquid-containing solvent is recycled to the wittig reaction.
DC E11 (Containing P and/or Si.)

   (B) stirring and reacting for 100-400                revolution per minute for 5-60 minutes, after                finishing reaction, filtering;

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aliphatic                  polyamine is aliphatic amine or carbon-containing                  number is six or less of aliphatic diamine. The aliphatic tertiary amine is                  aliphatic hydrocarbontertiary amine. The                  aliphatic alkanediamine is ethylenediamine,                  1,6-diaminehexane or 1,10-diamine decane, and                  aliphatic ether diamine is 2,2'-oxybisethylamine                  , 2,2'-1,2-ethanebisoxybis-ethylamine,                  2,2'-oxybis2,1-ethanediyloxybis-ethylamine or                  1-2-aminoethoxypropan-2-amine. The                  oligomeric polyol is one or more of polyester                  polyol, polyether polyol, polycarbonate polyol,                  and polymer polyol, and isocyanate is toluene                  diisocyanate and diphenylmethane one or more of                  isocyanate, 1,5-naphthalene diisocyanate and                  p-phenylene diisocyanate. The oligomeric polyol                  is dehydrated at vacuum of 50-500 pascal at 130                  degrees C. The conditions of uniform mixing,                  stirred at 100-300 revolution per minute for                  10-30 minutes. Preferred Conditions: The flow                  rate of carbon dioxide is 1-20 milliliter/minute.                  The reaction temperature is 0-10 degrees C. The                  aliphatic polyamine is aliphatic alkane diamine,                  stirring at speed of 100-300 revolution per                  minute, reacting 5-30 minutes, aliphatic                  polyamine is aliphatic etherdiamine, aliphatic                  diamine ester or aliphatic tertiaryamine,                  stirring speed of 100-400 revolution per minute,                  reacting for 20-60 minutes. Preferred Process:                  The method involves adding oligomeric polyalcohol                  into reaction container, after dehydration under                  vacuum, under protection of inert gas, adding                  diisocyanate and heatingreaction at 70-130                  degrees C, to obtain desired NCO content is 3-9%                  of polyurethane prepolymer, introducing carbon                  dioxide reversible chain-linking of protection                  are dispersed in organic solvent. The                  polyurethane pre-polymer is added, stirred and                  mixed uniformly, cold trap and vacuum degree is                  50-500 pascal for 5-30 minutes to obtain mixture.                  The mixture obtained transferred on vulcanized in                  mould, open mould, solidify and form to obtain                  polyurethane elastomer.

AU YAN Q
   XIAO S
AE HUNAN KEYKING RECYCLING TECHNOLOGY CO (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2018934074
AB    NOVELTY - A battery grade lithium carbonate preparing method involves heating waste lithium ion battery cathode material powder to 800-1100 degrees C under hydrogen atmosphere for 0.5-1 hour, adding water to the solid-liquid and filtering to obtain a lithium hydroxide solution and a filter residue containing nickel, cobalt and manganese, passing carbon dioxide into lithium hydroxide solution, starting to precipitate, continuing to pass carbon dioxide, when precipitate completely dissolved, stopping carbon dioxide, filtering solution, heating filtrate to 90-100 degrees C and drying filtrate to obtain the finished product.
   USE - Method for preparing battery grade lithium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing battery grade lithium carbonate high leaching rate.

PT P
PN CN108217659-A
TI Utilizing fly ash involves mixing fly ash and carbon reducing agent, introducing chlorine for chlorination reaction to obtain first gas phase mixture slurry which comprises carbon tetrachloride, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide.
AU SUN Y
   FAN X
   YIN B
   WU Z
   WANG R
AE XINJIANG ZHIXIN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XINJ-Non-standard)
GA 201854029D
AB    NOVELTY - Utilizing fly ash involves mixing fly ash and carbon reducing agent, introducing chlorine for chlorination reaction to obtain first gas phase mixture slurry which comprises carbon tetrachloride, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, aluminum chloride and iron chloride, cooling to obtain second gas mixture which comprises aluminum chloride and iron chloride and second gas phase mixture comprises carbon tetrachloride, chlorine, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, condensing mixture of second gas-phase and liquid-phase silicon tetrachloride for wet washing to obtain third liquid phase mixture which includes carbon monoxide, silicon dioxide and chlorine gas, and third liquid phase mixture comprises silicon tetrachloride.
   USE - Method for utilizing fly ash.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The mass ratio of fly ash and carbon reducing agent is 20:1-10. The mass ratio of the fly ash and the silicon carbide is 20:1-10. Preferred Components: The carbon reducing agent comprises active carbon, petroleum coke and fine coal. The chlorination reaction further comprises silicon carbide. Preferred Conditions: The chlorination reaction temperature is 600-1200 degrees C and pressure is 0.1-2 megaPascal. Preferred Process: The coal ash is utilized by using strong alkali solution and mixing in second solid, generating meta aluminate and ferric hydroxide precipitate, filtering and separating to obtain meta aluminate and ferric hydroxide. The coal ash is utilized by washing third gas phase mixture with water washing to obtain fourth gas phase mixture which comprises carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, washing with water and desorb the chlorine. The third liquid phase mixture is rectified and purified by using silicon tetrachloride to obtain organic silicon. The coal ash is utilized by washing, filtering slurry by water to obtain washing liquid, recrystallized, filtered and separated by using chloride of alkali metal chloride and/or alkali earth metal.

TI Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction system has carbon dioxide low temperature storage tank, carbon dioxide delivery pump, condenser, carbon dioxide storage tank, carbon dioxide high pressure pump, drying heater and extraction kettle.
AU GAO X
AE GAO X (GAOX-Individual)
GA 202242003Q
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction system has a carbon dioxide low temperature storage tank 1, a carbon dioxide delivery pump to deliver the liquid carbon dioxide in carbon dioxide cryogenic storage tank to the carbon dioxide storage tank, a condenser 3, a carbon dioxide storage tank for storing liquid carbon dioxide, a carbon dioxide high pressure pump, a drying heater, an extraction kettle 6, a separation heater and a separator 8, where the carbon dioxide delivery pump is used to deliver the liquid carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide cryogenic storage tank to the carbon dioxide storage tank and to provide circulating power for the liquid carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide storage tank, the carbon dioxide high-pressure pump is used to transport the carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide storage tank to the drying heater and the drying heater is used to convert the liquid carbon dioxide transferred from the carbon dioxide storage tank into supercritical carbon dioxide.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction system.
   ADVANTAGE - The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction system provides rapid and efficient extraction of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction system has a carbon dioxide low temperature storage tank, a carbon dioxide delivery pump to deliver the liquid carbon dioxide in carbon dioxide cryogenic storage tank to the carbon dioxide storage tank, a condenser, a carbon dioxide storage tank for storing liquid carbon dioxide, a carbon dioxide high pressure pump, a drying heater, an extraction kettle, a separation heater and a separator, where the carbon dioxide delivery pump is used to deliver the liquid carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide cryogenic storage tank to the carbon dioxide storage tank and to provide circulating power for the liquid carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide storage tank, the carbon dioxide high-pressure pump is used to transport the carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide storage tank to the drying heater, the drying heater is used to convert the liquid carbon dioxide transferred from the carbon dioxide storage tank into supercritical carbon dioxide, the extraction kettle is used to place the product to be extracted, and the separation heater is used to heat the supercritical carbon dioxide carrying dissolved matter so that the supercritical carbon dioxide is converted into gaseous carbon dioxide.

   Conveying pump (2)

PN DE102020106111-A1; WO2021175595-A1; EP3898081-A1
TI Blasting dry ice pellet to impulse clean object by gas stream carrying dry ice pellet comprises producing dry ice pellet by compressing carbon dioxide snow and introducing into gas stream and accelerated and gas flow carrying dry ice pellet.
AU JAGER F
   HAAS A
   GUDE J
   JAEGER F
AE ACP SYSTEMS AG (ACPS-Non-standard)
   ACP SYSTEMS AG (ACPS-Non-standard)
GA 2021A3493T
AB    NOVELTY - Blasting dry ice pellet, comprises producing dry ice pellets by compressing carbon dioxide snow, and introducing into gas stream and accelerated and gas flow carrying dry ice pellets is output via a jet outlet nozzle and thus object to be cleaned is acted upon. The dry ice pellets are formed from carbon dioxide snow in a compression device (16) by intermeshing compression wheels (18) with an axial passage opening in at least one compression wheel by outwardly protruding projections (20) of a compressor wheel with compression depressions in other compressor wheel which are complementary to this and which are essentially radially inward opening into through-opening, are brought into engagement and disengagement from one another when compressor wheels roll, so that carbon dioxide snow introduced into a gap of intermeshing compressor wheels is compressed into dry ice and dry ice is pressed radially inward into relevant passage opening and broken into dry ice pellet.
   USE - The dry ice pellet is useful for impulse cleaning of objects by gas stream carrying dry ice pellets (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method prevents contamination of dry ice pellets.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for cleaning device for the impulse-acting cleaning of objects by a gas stream carrying dry ice pellets, performing a blasting method, with a connection or a storage device (8) for liquid carbon dioxide, comprising an expansion device (12) for generating carbon dioxide snow from liquid carbon dioxide, a compression device with intermeshing compressor wheels with an axial passage opening in at least one compressor wheel, with at least one compressor wheel being provided with outwardly protruding projections (20) which are complementary thereto compression depressions formed essentially radially inward into the through opening in the other compressor wheel are brought into engagement and disengagement from one another as the compressor wheels roll, so that the intermeshing compressor wheels, introduced carbon dioxide snow is compressed into dry ice and the dry ice is pressed radially inward into the relevant through opening and broken into dry ice pellets, a pressurized gas connection or with a device for generating a pressurized gas stream, and with pressurized gas line unit for guiding the gas stream, for receiving dry ice pellets and for forwarding the gas stream that carries the dry ice pellets to a jet nozzle through which the gas flow entrained by the dry ice pellets is output during operation of the cleaning device and directed at the object to be cleaned. The axial passage opening in at least one compressor wheel is part of the compressed gas line unit, i.e. the gas flow is passed through the passage opening in the compressor wheel during operation of the cleaning device and dry ice pellets can be fed to the gas stream.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the cleaning device.
   Storage device (8)
   Expansion device (12)
   Compression device (16)
   Compression wheels (18)
   Projections (20)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The dry ice pellets are formed by two intermeshing compressor wheels each with an axial through opening made of carbon dioxide snow, and that each compressor wheel has projections and compression depressions which engage with one another and are disengageable so that dry ice pellets are pressed into the through opening of each of the two intermeshing compressor wheels, and that a gas stream is guided through both through openings. The both gas streams are merged downstream in order to form a single gas stream to act on the object to be cleaned. The compressor wheels are driven continuously and not clocked during the execution of the blasting process. The compressor wheels are driven individually and synchronously with one another and that the compressor wheels mesh with one another without contact. The dry ice pressed radially inward into the relevant passage opening is broken into dry ice pellets by a shearing device provided there. An axial through opening is formed in each of the two intermeshing compressor wheels, which is part of the compressed gas line unit, and that each compressor wheel has projections and compression depressions which engage with one another and can be disengaged so that dry ice pellets are pressed into the through opening of each of the two intermeshing compressor wheels. Both axial through-openings and thus both gas flows are brought together downstream in order to form a single gas flow in order to act on the object to be cleaned. A drive device for the compressor wheels designed in such a way that both compressor wheels are driven individually and synchronously with one another during the execution of the blasting process. The compressor wheels can be driven continuously and not in a clocked manner. The compressor wheels mesh with one another without contact. A shear device is provided in a respective axial through opening of a compressor wheel, by which dry ice pressed radially inward into the relevant through opening can be sheared into dry ice pellets. The outwardly protruding projections are designed in the shape of a plunger, pin or dome. The compression depressions arranged next to one another are delimited from one another, so that each compression depression can interact with a projection assigned to it for compression of the carbon dioxide snow. The compression depressions, starting from a surface level of the compressor wheel from radially outside to radially inside, have a first compression area which tapers radially inwards from radially outside to radially inside, preferably conically has a second transport area with a constant, preferably cylindrical, opening cross-section. The first compression area is delimited by a wall with an inclination angle to the radial direction of 5-35 degrees . The compression depressions have a third transport region which adjoins the second transport region radially inward and has a larger opening cross-section than the second transport region. The projections, starting from a surface level of the compressor wheel, have a radially outwardly rising tappet-shaped area for engagement in a relevant compression depression. The tappet-shaped region has an outer circumferential contour that is essentially constant in the radial direction, preferably a cylindrical shape. The ram-shaped area has a stamp surface essentially orthogonal to the radial direction.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gas stream is kept at a pressure of 3-20 bar, preferably 3-10 bar. The gas stream is operated at a speed of 4-60 m/s. No pneumatic or hydraulic components are used for the production of carbon dioxide snow and carbon dioxide dry ice pellets.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PN CN110026071-A
TI Carbon circulation system based on ammonia decarburization and electrocatalytic reduction utilization useful in various types of thermal power units comprises ammonia decarburization system and reheating control system.
AU GAO J
   LI H
   DU Q
   WANG Z
   WU S
   QIN Y
AE HARBIN INST TECHNOLOGY (HAIT-C)
GA 201965182D
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon circulation system based on ammonia decarburization and electrocatalytic reduction utilization comprises an ammonia decarburization system, an electrocatalytic reduction system, a clean energy recovery system, and a reheating control system. The ammonia decarburization system includes a cooler, a carbon dioxide absorption tower, a dissolution analysis enhanced crystallization system, and a solid-liquid separator. The boiler flue gas is cooled by the cooler and then enters the carbon dioxide absorption tower. The carbon dioxide is removed by the dissolution crystallization system to form ammonium bicarbonate crystals and carbonized liquid. The products are separated by a solid-liquid separator and sent to the electrocatalytic reduction system, electrocatalytic system 1 and electrocatalytic system 2. The remaining flue gas is discharged from the flue gas outlet of the carbon dioxide absorption tower.
   USE - The carbon circulation system is useful in various types of thermal power units.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon circulation system: solves the problem of overcapacity caused by the clean energy entering the network of the power plant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon circulation system based on ammonia decarburization and electrocatalytic reduction utilization comprises an ammonia decarburization system, an electrocatalytic reduction system, a clean energy recovery system, and a reheating control system. The ammonia decarburization system includes a cooler, a carbon dioxide absorption tower, a dissolution analysis enhanced crystallization system, and a solid-liquid separator. The boiler flue gas is cooled by the cooler and then enters the carbon dioxide absorption tower. The carbon dioxide is removed by the dissolution crystallization system to form ammonium bicarbonate crystals and carbonized liquid. The products are separated by a solid-liquid separator and sent to the electrocatalytic reduction system, electrocatalytic system 1 and electrocatalytic system 2. The remaining flue gas is discharged from the flue gas outlet of the carbon dioxide absorption tower. The electrocatalytic reduction system respectively performs electrocatalytic reduction of the treated ammonium bicarbonate crystals and the carbonized liquid as a consumed raw material to form a clean energy carbon monoxide or formic acid. The required electric energy is provided after being judged by the returning furnace control system. The clean energy recovery system processes the clean energy source at the outlet of the electrocatalytic reduction system. The part is supplied to the furnace control system, and the other part is stored in the industrial storage device for industrial application. The back-furnace control system is used for judging the operating state of the boiler and whether it supplies electric energy to the electrocatalytic system, and whether the clean energy is returned to the furnace. During the low-load operation of the thermal power unit, the system controls the amount of electricity supplied to the electrocatalytic system for electrocatalytic reduction. During high-load operation, the clean energy carbon monoxide recovered from the clean energy recovery system is returned to the furnace to assist combustion and suppress NOx formation. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for carrying out carbon cycle method based on ammonia decarburization and electrocatalytic reduction utilization.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the carbon circulation system based on ammonia decarburization and electrocatalytic reduction utilization.

   USE - Composite particles capable of adsorbing                carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite particles have improved particle                strength and ensure low-cost capture of industrial                source flue gas carbon dioxide. Since fly ash,                bottom slag and desulfurized gypsum stacked in a                large area in a coal-fired power plant are used as                raw materials, the prepared composite particles can                replace portion of the mineral raw materials such                as quartz, clay, sand, and thus ensuring pollution                reduction with waste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of composite particles, which involves                (S1) placing fly ash, bottom slag and desulfurized                gypsum into a ball mill for ball milling, (S2)                placing the ball milled mixture and Portland                cement, alkali activator and binder into stirrer,                stirring to obtain uniformly mixed powdery mixture,                (S3) placing the mixture into granulator for                spheronization or extrusion granulation, and (S4)                performing flue gas curing and natural                curing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The alkaline activator                  is one of sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide                  and calcium oxide.Preferred Properties: In step (S1), the                  particle size of ball milled particles is 50-100                  &#181;m. In step (S3), the formed particles                  are spherical or ellipsoidal. The particle size                  of formed particles is 5-35 mm.Preferred Process: In step (S4), the flue                  gas curing is performed in industrial source                  carbon dioxide flue gas atmosphere.Preferred Composition: The curing flue gas                  contains 10-50% carbon dioxide and 5-25% water                  vapor.Preferred Condition: In step (S4), the flue                  gas curing is carried out at 60-200&#8451;                  for 1-12 hours under a pressure of 0.5-2.0 MPa.                  The natural curing process is performed at normal                  temperature and normal pressure for 7-30                  days.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Component: The binder is one of                  kaolin, refractory soil and water glass.

AE BAIBEIYUN ZHEJIANG IOT TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BAIB-Non-standard)
GA 202296391F
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves acquiring current                information, where the current information                comprises current illumination intensity and                acquisition time, and judging whether the current                illumination intensity meets the lowest                illumination intensity, if the current illumination                intensity meets the minimum illumination intensity.                The preset adjusting scheme in the adjusting data                is determined according to the current illumination                intensity, conventional adjustment is performed                according to the preset adjusting scheme, and the                completion rate and total completion rate are                calculated.
   USE - Method for adjusting carbon dioxide                concentration and illumination in a                greenhouse.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables determining the adjusting                preset adjusting solution under different                environment conditions according to the                light-combining action habit of the crop according                to real-time illumination intensity to match the                carbon dioxide (CO2) interval of the preset                adjusting scheme, so that the CO2 concentration                adjusting method adopts the solution with minimum                energy consumption to perform compensation control.                The method allows the crop to be always in the                growing environment with the best photosynthesis                effect when the real time illumination intensity is                not enough.

The low pH environment makes Dendrobium polysaccharides and other nutrients more stable, inhibits the growth of aerobic microorganisms, significantly extends the shelf life, and not only can have a rich bubble taste when drinking, absorbs effective active ingredients of dendrobium, honey and other fresh juices.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The filtration treatment and the centrifugal treatment of Dendrobium described in step (I) are performed at least twice. In the step (ii), the filtration treatment and the centrifugal treatment of fruit are performed at least twice. In the sterilization process, (iii), the first adopts an ultra-high temperature sterilization process and ultraviolet sterilization process. The other raw materials in the step (iii) are preferably prickly pear stock solution, natural honey, 0 calorie sugar and purified water. In the step (iv), the blending ratio is based on the sequence of Dendrobium juice first, prickly pear stock solution, natural honey, 0-calorie sugar, fruit juice, and finally purified water. When the juice is sweeter, 2:2 is preferred: The blending ratio of 1:3:3.3:28, when the juice is slightly acidic, a blending ratio of 4:5:4:4:6.6:56 or 4:5:4:4:7:56 is preferably used.Preferred Method: In the step (iv), a high-pressure homogenizer is preferably used for homogenization treatment, the homogenization pressure is 30-70 MPa, and the temperature is 50-60degreesC. The sterilization in the step(iv)preferably adopts a vacuum microwave sterilization process.

PN CN113318218-A
TI Preparing octreotide acetate injection by mixing mannitol and water for injection, adding water for injection and lactic acid, adding sodium bicarbonate to medicinal solution, adding octreotide acetate, passing carbon dioxide, controlling dissolved oxygen content, filtering, and sterilizing.
AU WANG S
   LI W
   HU Y
   ZHAO C
AE SINOPHARM A-THINK PHARM CO LTD (CNPG-C)
GA 2021A4114D
AB    NOVELTY - Process of preparing an octreotide acetate injection, comprises: mixing and stirring mannitol and first water for injection, and sequentially adding second water for injection and lactic acid to obtain a first medicinal solution; adding sodium bicarbonate to the first medicinal solution to adjust the pH to 3.9-4.3, adding octreotide acetate, passing carbon dioxide below the liquid level, and controlling the dissolved oxygen content to be less than or equal to 0.5 mg/l to obtain a second medical solution; and filtering the second medical solution under the protection of carbon dioxide, encapsulating and sterilizing to obtain the product.
   USE - The process is useful for preparing an octreotide acetate injection (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation process uses carbon dioxide as a protective gas to prepare octreotide acetate injection, which improves the stability of octreotide acetate injection to a certain extent, while increasing the osmotic pressure of the octreotide acetate injection, so that the osmotic pressure of the octreotide acetate injection is basically the same as the reference preparation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the octreotide acetate injection prepared by the preparation process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sodium bicarbonate is 0.5 mol/l sodium bicarbonate solution.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The sterilization temperature is 121 degrees C and the sterilization time is 12-15 minutes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: Each 1 l octreotide acetate injection comprises water for injection, sodium bicarbonate, 0.1 g octreotide acetate, 45 g mannitol and 3.4 g lactic acid, where the amount of sodium bicarbonate is adjusted to pH 3.9-4.3. The first water is water for injection saturated with carbon dioxide. The volume ratio of the first water for injection and the second water for injection is 1:2-2:1. Preferred Properties: The octreotide acetate injection has a residual oxygen content of 3.6%, and an osmolality of 315-350 mOsmol/kg.

   KIMHAKJU
   KIM H
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 202113080V
AB    NOVELTY - A system for pyrolyzing biomethane using solar energy consists a supply unit for supplying biogas, purification unit for receiving biogas and purifying the biogas, oxygen removal unit for receiving biogas and removing oxygen from the biogas, carbon dioxide gas removal unit for receiving the biogas and removing carbon dioxide gas from the biogas, condensing unit for condensing sunlight, a reactor for receiving biogas and generating hydrogen and carbon black by pyrolyzing the biogas using sunlight collected by the condensing unit, and a separation unit for receiving hydrogen and carbon black from the reactor and separating hydrogen and carbon black. The reactor and a main portion are installed in a housing, which is rotated with respect to the housing, and generates hydrogen and carbon black by thermally decomposing the biogas using sunlight collected by the condensing unit.
   USE - System for pyrolyzing biomethane.
   ADVANTAGE - The system produces hydrogen through an environmentally-friendly method. The system increases the reaction efficiency by preventing the reactor from being locally overheated and heating the reactor to a uniform temperature as a whole. The method does not generate carbon dioxide, produces hydrogen in large quantities and utilizes high-purity carbon discharged as a by-product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - MECHANICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The purification unit consists a purification unit (P1) for removing hydrogen sulfide and ammonia from the biogas through an iron-based adsorbent, a moisture removal unit for removing moisture from the biogas by a heat exchanger, and a purification unit (P2) for removing siloxane from the biogas using an adsorbent. The system further consists of a light condensing unit (heliostat) to send sunlight in a predetermined direction by reflecting sunlight by the reflector, and a solar collector configured to receive sunlight from the heliostat and to reflect the light in the direction of the reactor to collect sunlight at a predetermined focus. The reactor consists of an injection unit for injecting water with biogas and steam droplets to the main portion. The main portion has a porous reaction layer (bed) containing pores through which a fluid can pass and a catalyst (optionally). Preferred Process: The steam droplet generates additional hydrogen by generating a steam reforming side reaction using the sunlight collected in the porous reaction layer.

PN EP3750619-A1; WO2020249444-A1; AU2020291602-A1; CA3138976-A1; EP3750619-B1; CN113950365-A; US2022152554-A1; ES2909489-T3; AU2020291602-B2
TI Scrubbing an exhaust gas from carbon dioxide            involves providing a waste material rich in            carbonatable calcium and/or magnesium phases and            injecting waste material into the exhaust gas for            reaction with carbon dioxide.
AU SKOCEK J
   ZAJAC M
   BEN HAHA M
   FEDERHEN S
   BEN H M
AE HEIDELBERGCEMENT AG (HEID-Non-standard)
   HEIDELBERGCEMENT AG (HEID-Non-standard)
   HCONNECT 2 GMBH (HCON-Non-standard)
   HEIDELBERGCEMENT AG (HEID-Non-standard)
   HCONNECT 2 GMBH (HCON-Non-standard)
GA 2020C5283T
AB    NOVELTY - Scrubbing an exhaust gas from carbon dioxide                (CO2) and SOx with x from 1-3 involves providing a                waste material rich in carbonatable calcium (Ca)                and/or magnesium (Mg) phases and with d90 less than                or equal to 1000 mm and a Rosin-Rammler slope (n)                from 0.6-1.4 in dry form or as a slurry. The waste                material is injected into the exhaust gas for                reaction with CO2 and SOx to provide in the exhaust                gas stream an amount of dry waste material ranging                from 5-30 kg/m3. The relative humidity is adjusted                from 50-100% and a temperature from 40-130 degrees                C. A partly carbonated and/or sulphurized waste                material and cleaned exhaust gas are withdrawn, and                recycled a portion of the partly carbonated and                sulphurized waste material while the remainder is                discharged.
   USE - Method for scrubbing an exhaust gas from CO2                and SOx.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to scrubbed an exhaust gas                from CO2 and SOx, where costs can be reduced, due                to the simplicity of the method and direct use of                existing scrubbing devices, which allows low                investment costs, and absorption of CO2 and/or SOx                without associated carbon dioxide emission can be                increased.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The waste material is                  added as a slurry containing from 4-28 wt.%. The                  12.5 wt.% of the waste material calculated as                  oxides is calcium oxide (CaO) and magnesium oxide                  (MgO) and at least 80 wt.% of the CaO and MgO are                  in carbonatable phases before carbonation and/or                  CaO and MgO constitute at least 20 wt.%. The                  exhaust gas is taken from a plant burning fuel                  like coal, oil, gas, and particularly fuels                  containing sulphur, to generate heat for a                  material or energy production process,                  especiallyfrom a coal fired power plant, a                  ceramics manufacturing plant, the magnesia                  industry, a lime kiln, or a cement plant. The                  slurry of a waste material rich in carbonatable                  Ca and/or Mg phases and with d90 less than or                  equal to 1000 mm for cleaning exhaust gas from                  CO2 and/or SOx. The waste material is injected                  into the exhaust gas as dry material together                  withwater or with a premixed slurry of waste                  material and water. A scrubber reactor is                  integrated into an existing cement plant orcoal                  fired power plant for cleaning the exhaust gas.                  Preferred Components: The waste material is                  selected from recycled concrete fines, recycled                  concrete aggregates, fly ashes, slags, and                  mixtures.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).)

PN WO2020060498-A1; KR2021057803-A; CN112912176-A; US2021205798-A1; EP3852920-A1; US2021346877-A1; JP2022501426-W; EP3852920-B1
TI Catalyst system useful for producing cyclic            carbonates from carbon dioxide and epoxide-based            compounds, comprises pre-catalyst compound and            co-catalyst including ammonium salt or phosphonium            salt.
AU PIROMCHART T
   DELIA V
   D ELIA V
AE PTT EXPLORATION & PRODN PUBLIC CO LTD (PTTE-Non-standard)
   PTT EXPLORATION & PRODN CO LTD (PTTE-Non-standard)
   PTT EXPLORATION & PRODN PUBLIC CO LTD (PTTE-Non-standard)
   PTT EXPLORATION & PRODN PUBLIC CO LTD (PTTE-Non-standard)
GA 202023684D
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst system comprises: a pre-catalyst                compound (I); and a co-catalyst including ammonium                salt (Ia) or phosphonium salt (Ib).
   USE - The catalyst system is for useful for                producing cyclic carbonates from carbon dioxide and                epoxide-based compounds (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst system: has high catalytic                activity and high selectivity; and                cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Catalyst system comprises: a pre-catalyst                compound of formula (Bi(X)z) (I); and a co-catalyst                including ammonium salt of formula                ((R1)(R2)(R3)(R4)(N)+(Y1)-) (Ia) or phosphonium                salt of formula ((R1)(R2)(R3)(R4)(P)+(Y1)-)                (Ib).
   X, Y1 = halo, where X is Cl;
   z = 1-3; and
   R1-R4 = cycloalkyl, aryl or heteroaryl (all                optionally substituted) or linear or branched 1-8C                alkyl.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                producing cyclic carbonates, comprising reacting                epoxide-based compounds with carbon dioxide in the                presence of the catalyst system, where the method                is perforated using a mole ratio of the                pre-catalyst: the co-catalyst: the epoxide-based                compound of 1:1:10-1:3:100, at a pressure of carbon                dioxide of 1-100 bar, a temperature of 10-200                degrees C, and a reaction time of 1-8 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The pre-catalyst is                  bismuth trichloride. The co-catalyst comprises                  tetxa-n-butylammonium bromide,                  tetra-n-butylammonium iodide,                  tetra-n-butylphosphonium bromide and/or                  tetra-n-butylphosphonium iodide. The mole ratio                  of the pre-catalyst and the co-catalyst is 1:1 to                  1:3, preferably 1:2. The mole ratio of the                  pre-catalyst: the co-catalyst: the epoxide-based                  compound is 1:2:50. The method is performed using                  the pressure of carbon dioxide of 1-50 bar,                  preferably 1-10 bar. The temperature is 25-120                  degrees C. The reaction time is 2-4 hours.
   Preferred Definitions:
   X = Cl; and
   Y1 = Br or I.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN110741852-A; CN211020264-U
TI Fully automatic plant breeding monitoring system comprises transparent box body whose bottom part is plant sample fixing frame and upper part is set with carbon dioxide gas control and oxygen detection unit, and lighting control device.
AU XU T
AE JIANGSU RUIMING BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020118034
AB    NOVELTY - Fully automatic plant breeding monitoring system comprises a transparent box body (3) provided with a lighting control device (2). Preferred Method: In the step (iv), the extraction solution is further added with 1-3 pts. wt. acidity regulator (malic acid and/or citric acid). In the step (i), the number of meshes classified by sieving is 120-200 meshes sieve. In the step (ii), the temperature of ultrasonic treatment is 50-55 degrees C. In the step (iii), the extraction temperature is 50-55 degrees C.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing modified magnesium oxide-based carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises (i) taking inorganic magnesium source precursor, and the alkali metal nitrate, placing in a container containing deionized water, adding organic carbon source, stirring at a constant temperature in a water bath at 80 degrees C to obtain pale yellow colloid, placing the pale yellow colloid in an oven at 70 degrees C, and drying overnight to obtain a yellow colloid, (ii) placing the dried yellow colloid in a tube furnace, and carbonizing at a constant temperature under an inert atmosphere to obtain grey brown fluffy solid powder having a carbon template, (iii) placing the carbonized solid powder in an air atmosphere at a constant temperature, and calcining at 450 degrees C for 2 hours, (4) naturally cooling to room temperature, taking the sample out, grinding in a mortar to obtain the finished product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing modified magnesium oxide-based carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is convenient; has rich nano-particles, rich porosity, uniform morphology and structure, and large adsorption capacity is large.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for modified magnesium oxide-based carbon dioxide adsorbent prepared by the above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i) the inorganic magnesium source precursor is magnesium nitrate. The alkali metal nitrate is sodium nitrate. The mass ratio of sodium nitrate amount added to the amount of magnesium oxide is 3/17-1/3. The organic carbon source is D-gluconic acid. The molar ratio of D-gluconic acid amount added to the magnesium ion in the inorganic magnesium source precursor is 1/4-1/3. The adsorbent has an adsorption temperature of 300-340 degrees C. Preferred Method: In the step (ii) carbonizing for 350-450 degrees C for 2 hours.

AU YU G
   DENG Y
   CHEN X
   DING L
AE JIHUA GROUP JILIN TIANLONG CATALYST CO (JIHU-Non-standard)
GA 201917412M
AB    NOVELTY - Loaded metallocene catalyst comprises carrier, metallocene compound loaded on the carrier and cocatalyst, where carrier is polymer microsphere; cocatalyst is aluminum alkyl; zirconium loading content in the catalyst is 0.1-5 wt.%, aluminum content is 10-36 wt.%, and content of carrier is 59-89.9 wt.%; and loaded metallocene catalyst is prepared by adding aromatic hydrocarbon solution of the metallocene compound into polymer microspheres during loading, swelling the polymer microsphere with supercritical carbon dioxide, then adding alkyl aluminoxane for catalyst activation, drying and molding.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for ethylene polymerization (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has high polymerization activity, stable polymerization reaction kinetics and controllable molecular weight.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing loaded metallocene catalyst comprising (i) adding aromatic hydrocarbon solution of the metallocene compound into polymer carrier, placing in a high pressure reactor with carbon dioxide, heating and pressurizing until the carbon dioxide reaches a supercritical state, and swelling the polymer carrier using supercritical carbon dioxide, (ii) relieving the pressure after swelling and 5-10 times of nitrogen replacement, and then adding cocatalyst into swelled catalyst intermediate for catalyst activation, and (iii) adding hexane solution into reactor, repeatedly washing for 5-10 times, removing the solvent from steps (i) and (ii), and drying to obtain highly active loaded metallocene catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The polymer microspheres are homopolymer and copolymer microspheres of styrene, divinylbenzene, acrylonitrile or acrylamide monomers. The metallocene compound is ferrocene compound of formula (I). The aluminum alkyl is trimethylaluminum, triethyl aluminum, triisopropyl aluminum, triisobutylaluminum, tri-n-hexyl aluminum, dichloroaluminum and/or methyl aluminoxane. In step (i), aromatic hydrocarbon solution is benzene, toluene and/or xylene. In step (ii), cocatalyst is aromatic aluminum solution of aluminum alkyl; temperature of activation reaction is 10-100 degrees C, and time of activation reaction is 1-4 hours. The molar ratio of the amount of aluminum alkyl and metallocene compound is 20-1000. Preferred Method: In step (i), swelling permeation temperature in the supercritical carbon dioxide swelling process is 31-60 degrees C, swelling pressure is 7.5-12 MPa, and swelling time is 0.5-10 hours. In step (ii), activation and dry forming operation in step (iii) is carried out under anhydrous and anaerobic conditions.R1-R5 and R1'-R5' = H or 1-5C alkyl;M = metal zirconium; andX = halogen.

TI Producing thermochemical hydrogen cycle involves using uranium oxide, carbonate and water as raw material, carrying out hydrogen production reaction to generate diuranate and/or uranate, carbon dioxide and hydrogen and reacting.
AU LIU Y

   LIU C
   LI Z
   CHEN A
   CHEN M
   ZHENG X
AE SHANGHAI INST APPLIED PHYSICS CHINESE AC (CAYW-C)
GA 201868754T
AB    NOVELTY - Producing thermochemical hydrogen cycle involves using uranium oxide, carbonate and water as raw material, carrying out hydrogen production reaction, generating diuranate and/or uranate, carbon dioxide and hydrogen, where reaction temperature of hydrogen production is 300-1100 degrees C and molar ratio of uranium oxide and carbonate is 1:1-1:8, and molar ratio of uranium oxide and water is less than or equal to 1:1, reacting diuranate and/or uranate, carbon dioxide and ammonium carbonate and/or bicarbonate solution at 0-150 degrees C to obtain ammonium uranyl tricarbonate, separating, precipitating carbonate solution, adding ammonium carbonate solid then separating solid and liquid, adding ammonium carbonate solid or solution so that concentration of ammonium carbonate in system reaches to 3-5 moles/L followed by pyrolytic reaction, pyrolyzing ammonium uranyl carbonate to obtain uranium oxide and producing ammonia, carbon dioxide, water and oxygen, recycling uranium oxide and pyrolyzing to 100-950 degrees C.
   USE - Method for producing thermochemical hydrogen cycle.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce thermochemical hydrogen cycle in simple and cost-effective manner which has low operating temperature, low-radiation, and does not contain toxic, and is recyclable.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The temperature of hydrogen production reaction is 400-850 degrees C, preferably 550 degrees C, carbonate salt comprises lithium carbonate, sodium carbonate and potassium carbonate. The uranium oxide is uranium oxide and/or uranium dioxide. The water is used as water vapor or liquid water form. The reaction temperature is 10-60 degrees C. The thermochemical cycle hydrogen production method, where oxygen is generated by pyrolysis of uranyl tricarbonate from reaction system and recycled raw material by dissolving into diuranic acid and pyrolyzing at 300-450 degrees C. Preferred Compositions: The molar ratio of uranium oxide and carbonate is 1:1.5-1:3. The precipitation is performed at 0-110 degrees C. The reaction temperature is 0-100 degrees C, and molar ratio of ammonium carbonate is not less than 1:8, preferably 1:4.
