PT P
PN WO2022099251-A1
TI Method for controlling ratio of carbon dioxide to oxygen gasses in grow space, involves removing air from grow space by removing air including carbon dioxide and oxygen, and returning air to grow space after removing air.
AU STOREY N R
AE MJNN LLC (MJNN-Non-standard)
GA 202263760S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves Controlling a ratio of carbon dioxide to oxygen gasses in a grow space for plant and removes 02 from the grow space. The supplemental CO2 to the space to achieve a desired ratio of CO 2 to 02 in the space. The step of removing 02 comprises filtering out oxygen or reacting 02 with a reactant to produce 02-reduced air. The water vapor in the reduced air is reduced. The method further comprises removing air from the space, where the air includes C02, nitrogen and the 02. The air is returned after removing the 02, and nitrogen is removed from the removed air.
   USE - Method for controlling a ratio of carbon dioxide to oxygen in a grow space i.e. grow room, of a controlled agricultural environment (CAE) for plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides alternative approaches to accelerate plant growth and improve yield as a function of energy inputs by optimizing the concentrations of atmospheric gasses. With the same amount of energy, a system can obtain more efficient growing and more biomass production per unit of energy if the system manages 02 in relation to CO2. It is well documented that increasing the level of CO2 above the ambient level improves photosynthetic efficiency of the plant (absolute ratio). As 02 levels rise the improvements made by increasing CO2 levels are compromised. Thus, artificially removing 02 from the atmosphere of the growing environment to reduce 02 concentration relative to CO 2 enhances the efficiency of photosynthesis in the plant.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram to control the gas mixture in a grow space.
   System for controlling a ratio of carbon dioxide to oxygen in a grow space (2400)
   Conditioning System (2401)
   Nitrogen Concentrator (2402)
   Co2 Concentrator (2404)
   Vent (2406)

PT P
PN CN113653510-A; CN113653510-B
TI Blocking infiltration of rock formation micro-cracks by carbon dioxide mineralized nano-silica gel grouting material, comprises sampling and testing aquifer water, calculating maximum pore pressure, reacting carbon dioxide gas with nanofluid and forming in situ nano-silica gel.
AU WANG Y
   DI J
   WU Y
   MA L
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
GA 2021D6129R
AB    NOVELTY - Method for blocking infiltration of rock formation micro-cracks by carbon dioxide mineralized nano-silica gel grouting material, comprises sampling and testing the aquifer water in the mining area to be constructed, determining the concentrations of sodium, calcium, bicarbonate, magnesium and sulfate ions in aquifer water, calculating and determining the development height of upward micro-cracks and the development depth of downward micro-cracks caused by the disturbance of surrounding rock by mining activities, determining the construction parameters e.g. the angle, depth, aperture, and spacing of the grouting drilling holes, calculating the maximum pore pressure according to the working face mining depth and pore pressure gradient, mixing the silicon-based material and water, and stirring to obtain the base liquid, mixing the nanoparticles with the base liquid, using ultrasonic dispersion and stirring to obtain nanofluid.
   USE - Method for blocking infiltration of rock formation micro-cracks by carbon dioxide mineralized nano-silica gel grouting material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple steps, convenient use and wide practicability, and effectively blocks micro-fissure penetration of rock stratum.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a detection method of a method for blocking the infiltration of rock formation microcracks by using the carbon dioxide mineralized nano silica gel grouting material.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the mineralized nano silica gel grouting system.
   High pressure pump station (1)
   Tank detection method (2)
   Nanofluid (3)
   Valve (4)
   Monitoring device (5)
   Grouting pipeline (6)
   Mining area (7)
   Upward micro-crack breeding area (10)
   Descending large crack breeding area (11)
   Downward micro-crack breeding area (12)
   Working surface propulsion direction (13)
   Grouting drilling hole (14)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises constructing a grouting drilling hole (14), using the mineralized nano silica gel grouting system, injecting a nanofluid (3) into an upward micro-crack breeding area (10) and the downward micro-crack breeding area (12) respectively through a grouting pipeline (6) and the grouting drilling hole, where the injection pressure does not exceed the maximum pore pressure, and stopping the injection when the pressure no longer changes, using the mineralized nano silica gel grouting system to inject carbon dioxide gas into the upward micro-crack breeding area and the downward micro-crack breeding area through the grouting drilling hole on the roof and floor, respectively, stopping the injection when the pressure no longer changes, reacting the carbon dioxide gas with the nanofluid injected into the microcracks in the microcrack development zone, forming in situ nano-silica gel, thus closing the microcracks.
DC Q49 (Mining (E21)); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)
MC Q49-C; S03-F06B; S03-E14B
IP E21D-011/10; E21D-011/38; G01N-015/08

PT P
PN CN113278680-A
TI Preparation of serum carbon dioxide determination reagent ball, by mixing buffer, phosphoenolpyruvate, reduced coenzyme I, malate dehydrogenase, phosphoenolpyruvate carboxylase, magnesium salt, stabilizer, excipient and water, adjusting pH and freeze-drying ice ball.
AU MU J
   WANG C
   ZHOU H
   CHEN M
AE SHENZHEN JINRUI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202199589N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparation of serum carbon dioxide determination reagent ball, involves mixing buffer, phosphoenolpyruvate, reduced coenzyme I, malate dehydrogenase, phosphoenolpyruvate carboxylase, magnesium salt, stabilizer, excipient and water to obtain mixed solution, adjusting pH of the mixed liquid to a preset value, dropping the droplets of the mixed liquid in liquid nitrogen, thus droplets form ice balls, and freeze-drying the ice ball to prepare the serum carbon dioxide measuring reagent ball. The mixed solution comprises 5-150 mmol/l buffer, 5-20 g/l phosphoenolpyruvate, 0.1-10 g/l reduced coenzyme I, 5-20 KU/l malate dehydrogenase, 0.1-5 KU/l phosphoenolpyruvate carboxylase, 0.1-10 g/l stabilizer and 10-100 g/l excipient. The stabilizer is used to maintain the phosphoenol for the activity of pyruvate carboxylase and the malate dehydrogenase, and the stabilizer is a protein or disaccharide stabilizer.
   USE - The method is useful for preparation of serum carbon dioxide determination reagent ball (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures morphology and re-thawing solubility of the reagent ball, protects activity of the reactant, and provides high morphology and remelting solubility, high stability and high precision.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) reagent ball, prepared by the preparation method of the serum carbon dioxide determination reagent ball;

The excipients include trehalose, mannitol, inositol, water-soluble starch, dextran 10000 and dextran 40000 glycans. The stabilizer comprises serine, glycine, sucrose, bovine serum albumin, and EDTA. The buffer comprises tris(hydroxymethyl)aminomethane (Tris) buffer, glycine-sodium hydroxide buffer, and 4-(2-hydroxyethyl)-1-piperazineethanesulfonic acid (HEPES) buffer. The magnesium salt comprises magnesium sulfate or magnesium acetate. The volume of the ice ball is 2.5-3.5 mu l.

TI Facility used to treat feed gas stream to make methane-enriched gas stream, comprises oil-lubricated or water-lubricated vane-type compressor and membrane separation unit to receive compressed stream and separate methane from carbon dioxide.
AU BARRAUD F
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   CLAUDE METHODS STUDY & DEV GEORGES (CLAU-Non-standard)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 2021009280
AB    NOVELTY - Facility comprises: an oil-lubricated or water-lubricated vane-type compressor adapted and configured to compress the feed gas stream; and a membrane separation unit in fluid communication with the compressor adapted and configured to receive the compressed gas stream and separate the methane from the carbon dioxide.
   USE - The facility and process are useful for treatment of a feed gas stream comprising methane and carbon dioxide to produce the methane-enriched gas stream (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The facility and process utilize the vane-type compressor suctions the gas stream via an intake opening and direct it to a housing which experiences a reduction in its volume due to the rotation of the blades, thus increasing the pressure of this gas stream.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for treating a feed gas stream comprising at least methane and carbon dioxide in order to produce a methane-enriched gas stream, using a facility, comprising (a) compressing the feed gas stream to a pressure of 6-13 barg by means of the oil-lubricated or water-lubricated vane-type compressor, (b) eliminating and filtering, from the compressed feed gas stream, at least one of impurities, water vapor or oil vapor, (c) separating, in the membrane separation unit, the carbon dioxide and methane in the compressed feed gas stream from which the impurities, water vapor or oil vapor have been eliminated and filtered, (d) measuring a pressure of the feed gas stream at the inlet of the membrane separation unit, and (e) comparing the measurement taken in step (d) with a target value, and in the event of a difference between the measurement taken and the target value, modifying the compression of the feed gas stream within the compressor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The facility further comprises at least a pressure sensor adapted and configured to measure a pressure of the feed gas stream at an inlet of the membrane separation unit and a controller adapted and configured to compare a pressure measured by the pressure sensor with a target value, and adjust compression of the feed gas stream within the vane-type compressor. The lubricated vane-type compressor is adapted and configured to increase the pressure of the feed gas stream to a pressure of 6-13 barg. The membrane separation unit comprises a first membrane separation subunit adapted and configured to receive the gas stream leaving the adsorbers and to produce a first carbon dioxide-enriched permeate and a first methane-enriched retentate, a second membrane separation subunit adapted and configured to receive the first retentate and to produce a second carbon dioxide-enriched permeate and a second methane-enriched retentate, and a third membrane separation subunit adapted and configured to receive the first permeate and to produce a third methane-enriched retentate and a third carbon dioxide-enriched permeate. Preferred Process: The step (e) is carried out automatically by a data transmission and data processing means. The steps (a)-(e) are carried out continuously. The feed gas stream is biogas.

GA 202063328J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves removing the inorganic carbon from an inorganic carbon removal tank (1) from the sample to be measured. The measurement target sample from which the inorganic carbon is removed is aspirated into a syringe pump (2). A portion of the sample to be measured which is sucked into the syringe pump is discharged. The sample to be measured is injected into the pipe that is connected to an oxidation reactor (6). The samples to be measured that remain in syringe pump are discharged. The carrier gas is sucked into the syringe pump. A sample to be measured that is filled in a pipe connected to an oxidation reactor is injected into an oxidation reactor using a carrier gas drawn in a syringe pump. The measurement target sample injected into the oxidation reactor is oxidized to generate carbon dioxide. The generated carbon dioxide is transferred to an NDIR sensor (8) through a cooling unit (7) by a carrier gas. The amount of carbon dioxide transferred by an NDIR sensor is measured.
   USE - Method for measuring total organic carbon of sample by combustion oxidation.
   ADVANTAGE - The precision of the measurement of the total organic carbon is increased. The dual-cooling process removes moisture from the mixed gas with high efficiency, makes maintenance easy and reduces the failure factor of the measuring device, thus, the cost of maintenance and repair is reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for measuring total organic carbon of sample by combustion oxidation.

PN EP3511442-A1; WO2019137827-A1; DE102018000213-A1
TI Producing gas product containing carbon monoxide, comprises forming electrolysis insert containing carbon dioxide, water and hydrogen and feeding to cathode side of high-temperature electrolysis cell, and removing electrolysis crude product.
AU PESCHEL A
   HENTSCHEL B
AE LINDE AG (LINM-C)
   LINDE AG (LINM-C)
GA 201962185W
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a gas product containing at least carbon monoxide, comprises forming an electrolysis insert containing at least carbon dioxide, water and hydrogen and feeding to a cathode side of a high-temperature electrolysis cell, and removing an electrolysis crude product containing at least water, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and hydrogen from the cathode side of the high-temperature electrolysis cell. At least carbon monoxide-containing fraction is separated off from at least part of the original electrolysis product or from a gas mixture formed using at least part of the electrolysis product, where the carbon monoxide contained in the carbon monoxide fraction is at least partially subjected to a water gas shift reaction with water to form hydrogen, and at least part of the hydrogen formed in the water gas shift reaction is used in the formation of the electrolysis insert.
   USE - The method is useful for producing a gas product containing at least carbon monoxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is carried out in simple and cost efficient manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for plant for producing a gas product containing at least carbon monoxide, comprising a high-temperature electrolysis cell, means adapted to form an electrolysis insert containing at least carbon dioxide, water and hydrogen and to supply a cathode side to a high-temperature electrolysis cell, and means adapted to discharge from the cathode side of the high-temperature electrolysis cell an electrolysis crude product containing at least water, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and hydrogen. The means are provided which are adapted to separate from at least a part of the electrolysis raw product or from a gas mixture formed using at least part of the electrolysis raw product a fraction containing at least carbon monoxide.
TF EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)

PN EP3485961-A1; WO2019096441-A1; CN111246928-A; US2021170326-A1; US11285433-B2; CN111246928-B
TI Wash column for separating solid particles and            residues of absorbent from carbon dioxide-containing            off gas stream of gasification plant for production of            synthesis gas, has outlet which is arranged at top of            jacket.
AU CORBET S
   FREY C
   LEICHNER H
   SCHMIDT S
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   CLAUDE METHODS STUDY & DEV GEORGES (CLAU-Non-standard)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 201945577B
AB    NOVELTY - The wash column (1) has a shell which extends                along a longitudinal axis extending parallel to the                vertical and which encloses an interior which is                subdivided into an upper and a lower region. A                structured packing (4) is arranged in the upper                region of the inner space for separating off                residues of the first absorbent from the second                waste gas partial stream by absorption with a                second absorbent. An inlet for the second                absorbent, is arranged above the structured                packing. The inlet is arranged below the structured                packing for the second partial exhaust gas flow. A                packing is set in the lower part of the interior                space and below the inlet for the second partial                exhaust gas stream. An outlet is arranged at the                top of the jacket for the carbon dioxide-containing                offgas stream freed of residues of the first                absorbent and of solid particles.
   USE - Wash column for partially separating solid                particles and residues of absorbent from carbon                dioxide-containing off gas stream of gasification                plant for production of synthesis gas from                carbonaceous feedstocks and downstream gasification                plant for separation of carbon dioxide and                sulfur-containing associated gases from the                synthesis gas (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The residual methanol content from                particle-laden gas streams is reduced and dust                deposits are removed with less effort. The                consumption of absorbent is reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for performing partial separation of solid                particles and residues of first absorbent from                carbon dioxide-containing waste gas stream.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of wash                column.
   Wash column (1)
   Upper wash zone (2)
   Lower wash zone (3)
   Structured packing (4)
   Packed packing (5)

PT P
PN CN107857887-A
TI Device used for extracting lignin in straw, comprises vertically-placed heating tank, multilayer filter screen, carbon dioxide generator, horizontally-placed electric rotating shaft, and open flame heating furnace.
AU CHEN Z
AE CHENGDU XIANSHI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2018264169
AB    NOVELTY - A device comprises a vertically-placed heating tank, a multilayer filter screen, which is installed inside the heating tank, a carbon dioxide generator, which is connected to the top of the heating tank, a horizontally-placed electric rotating shaft, which is connected to both sides of the heating tank, and an open flame heating furnace. The screen filters are arranged in parallel in sequence along the height of the heating tank. The carbon dioxide generator is connected with the heating tank through an air outlet pipe. One end of the electric rotating shaft is fixed with the heating tank.
   USE - The device is useful for extracting lignin in straw.
   ADVANTAGE - The device extracts lignin in straw with high quality in a rapid manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A device comprises a vertically-placed heating tank, a multilayer filter screen, which is installed inside the heating tank, a carbon dioxide generator, which is connected to the top of the heating tank, a horizontally-placed electric rotating shaft, which is connected to both sides of the heating tank, and an open flame heating furnace. The screen filters are arranged in parallel in sequence along the height of the heating tank. The carbon dioxide generator is connected with the heating tank through an air outlet pipe. One end of the electric rotating shaft is fixed with the heating tank, and the other end of the electric rotating shaft is fixed on a bracket. The bracket is fixedly connected to a base below the heating tank. The open flame heating furnace is arranged on the base, and is located between the heating tank and the base.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: A telescopic column is connected between the open flame heating furnace and the base. The air outlet pipe is connected with a branch pipe. One end of the branch pipe is connected with the heating tank, and the other end of the branch pipe is connected with the air outlet pipe through a hose. Valves are installed on the air outlet pipe and branch pipe. An aperture of the filter screen in the heating tank is sequentially reduced from bottom to top.
DC A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.)
MC A03-C02; A10-A
IP C08H-007/00

TI Unmanned underwater vehicle power system for deep            sea detection, life saving, removing water mine and            high risk underwater operation, comprises high-pressure            carbon dioxide liquid storage tank whose outlet is            connected with outlet of vortex tube through            pipeline.
AU HE W
   QIN Y
   WANG J
   GUO M
   PU W
   XIONG C
AE UNIV NANJING AERONAUTICS & ASTRONAUTICS (UNUA-C)
GA 2022A2161G
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a high-pressure carbon dioxide                liquid storage tank whose outlet is connected with                an outlet of a vortex tube through a pipeline. An                outlet of the vortex tube is connected to an outlet                pipeline of a first throttle valve. A carbon                dioxide heat pump compressor (12)and a second inlet                of a low pressure carbon dioxide storage tank are                connected by the pipeline. Another end of a cold                side channel of a fourth heat exchanger is                connected through the pipeline with a                low-temperature gas outlet of vortex tube. Two ends                of a high temperature water storage tank (23), a                hot-side channel of an evaporator (24) are                connected with a hot side channel outside a                condenser. An inlet end of the evaporator is                provided with a first valve (26), a second valve                (27) and a third valve (28). The first valve and                the third valve are connected together by a                pipe.
   USE - Unmanned underwater vehicle power system based                on compressed carbon dioxide energy storage for                deep sea detection, life saving, removing water                mine and high risk underwater operation.
   ADVANTAGE - The power system has no corrosive working                substance, good safety, and can be switched under                multiple working conditions. The system has high                energy storage density, high energy utilization                rate, flexible characteristics and can effectively                improve the cruising ability of the submersible.                When the external water pressure is high, using                water pressure to push the piston to do work,                keeping the working medium pressure output in the                high pressure storage tank, effectively using the                environment characteristics improving the energy                utilization rates, and improving the utilization                rate of the energy and the energy storage                densities. The working substance involved in the                system is low in cost, good in safety, friendly to                the environment, and is switched under a number of                working conditions, compared with the storage                battery, the cost is lower, and the service life is                longer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for the                working method of underwater unmanned underwater                vehicle power system based on carbon dioxide energy                storage.

PT P
PN CN114097879-A
TI Storage method of rice comprises removing impurity of fresh harvested rice, drying, adding into hulling machine for removing shell, processing the rice, spraying preservative, and repellent agent on the rice surface, packaging in bag, and filling carbon dioxide into packaging bag.
AU SANG S
AE YANGZHOU XINGHANG RICE IND CO LTD (YANG-Non-standard)
GA 202241754D
AB    NOVELTY - Storage method of rice comprises removing the impurity of the fresh harvested rice, and then drying until the water content is 10-12%, adding the processed rice into the hulling machine for removing shell, controlling the gap of the hulling machine is 1.5-2.5 mm, sending the rice to be stored into the vibration machine filter, removing the crushed particles, withered leaves and grass seed impurities, and processing the filter, spraying the preservative, and repellent agent on the rice surface, packaging in bag, then filling carbon dioxide into the packaging bag according to the proportion of each 1 million kilograms of rice filled with 8-12 kilograms of carbon dioxide, then sealing the packaging bag.
   USE - The method is useful for storage method of rice.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: uses preservative, and insect repellent, which can inhibits the occurrence of mildew, reduces rate of rice water loss, and does not affect the taste quality of the rice itself; makes the inside of the packaging bag form a vacuum environment, so as to prolong the time of the rice; will not generate pollution and improve the quality of the rice.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Storage method of rice comprises (i) removing the impurity of the fresh harvested rice, and then drying until the water content is 10-12 %, (ii) adding the processed rice into hulling machine for removing shell, controlling the gap of the hulling machine at 1.5-2.5 mm, (iii) conveying the rice to be stored into the vibration machine filter, removing the crushed particles, withered leaves and grass seed impurities, and processing the filter, where the processing of rice comprises controlling the water content and sterilizing of rice, selecting the rice meeting the quality of the specified standard, and maturity of rice, (iv) placing the rice in a sealed container, injecting carbon dioxide into the container, and making the concentration of carbon dioxide in the container is 90-95%, at the same time spraying the preservative, and repellent agent on the rice surface, (v) taking the processed rice, filtering out mildew, insect spot rice, adding the rice into drying room, drying till the water content of rice is less than 5-9%, stop drying, using ventilate hemp bag to package the rice, and (vi) extracting the air in each of the packaging bag, then filling carbon dioxide into the packaging bag according to the proportion of each 1 million kilograms of rice filled with 8-12 kilograms of carbon dioxide, then sealing the packaging bag.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The preservative is composed of 5-9 pts. wt. pepper leaves, 4-6 pts. wt. mountain pepper oil, 3-5 pts. wt. chitosan, 0.5-1 pts. wt. tea polyphenols, 1-5 pts. wt. rattan tea extract, 0.05-0.1 pts. wt. epsilon-polylysine, 0.1-0.2 pts. wt. beta-cyclodextrin, 5-10 pts. wt. sucrose, and 500-1000 pts. wt. deionized water. The insect repellent agent is obtained by acidifying and drying 10-30 pts. wt. attapulgite clay, and 20-50 pts. wt. bentonite, mixing obtained mixture with 2-8 pts. wt. ginger, 1-6 pts. wt. garlic, 3-10 pts. wt. orange peel, 6-22 pts. wt. red pepper, 10-30 pts. wt. pepper and 3-8 pts. wt. common fennel, grinding together, granulating and drying to obtain the product. The attapulgite clay and bentonite are naturally weathered for 20-60 days and then sun-dried, and the moisture content should be less than or equal to 13%..Preferred Method: In the step (iii), fumigating the rice after sterilizing for 6-8 days, and ventilating for 2-4 days, where fumigant adopts aluminum phosphide. In the step (iii), sterilizing the rice by microwave sterilizing machine and ultraviolet lamp, where the microwave power of the microwave sterilizing machine is 200-400 W, and the processing time is 2-4 minutes.

PN CN113150871-A
TI Extracting Osmanthus fragrans extract comprises dehydrating fresh Osmanthus fragrans with air, irradiating dehydrated Osmanthus fragrans with electron accelerator and subjecting material to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction.
AU SHI Y
   CHEN H
   ZHANG X
AE UNIV HUBEI SCI & TECHNOLGY (UHST-C)
GA 2021916440
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting Osmanthus fragrans extract comprises (i) dehydrating fresh Osmanthus fragrans with air; (ii) irradiating the dehydrated Osmanthus fragrans with an electron accelerator; and (iii) subjecting the material to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting Osmanthus fragrans extract.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces extract in high yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), when the irradiation dose of the electron accelerator is 10-20 kGy, preferably 15 kGy the total yield of Osmanthus fragrans extract can be improved. In the step (ii), by adjusting the irradiation dose of the electron accelerator to be between 12kGy and 17kGy, preferably 15 kGy, the content of aldehydes in the Osmanthus fragrans extract is increased, and the content of alcohols and their oxides is reduced. In the step (ii), by adjusting the radiation dose of the electron accelerator between 18kGy and 22kGy, preferably 20 kGy, the content of alcohols and their oxides in the Osmanthus fragrans extract is increased, and the content of aldehydes and ketones is reduced. In the step (ii), by adjusting the radiation dose of the electron accelerator to be between 23kGy and 28kGy, preferably 25 kGy, the content of ketones or esters in Osmanthus fragrans extract is the largest, followed by the content of alcohols and their oxides.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the specific method of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction comprises the extraction pressure is 16 MPa, the extraction temperature is 45 degrees C, the separation I temperature is 60 degrees C, the separation I pressure is 8MPa, the separation II temperature is 35 degrees C, the separation II pressure is the same as the tank pressure, the flow rate is 3 liter/h, and the extraction time is 2-2.8 hours.

TI Mine carcass composition comprises cement, fly ash of circulating fluidized bed boiler, and bottom ash.
AU LEE H
   SEO S
   KIM C
   NAM S
   CHO Y
   WOO L H
   SEOK S S
   SIK K C
   YOUNG N S
   KWANG C Y
AE HANIL CEMENT MFG CO LTD (HANI-Non-standard)
   KOREA SOUTH POWER CO LTD (KOSP-C)
GA 201868397P
AB    NOVELTY - Mine carcass composition comprises cement, fly ash of circulating fluidized bed boiler, and bottom ash. The fly ash and the bottom ash are fixed with carbon dioxide.
   USE - Used as mine carcass composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition reduces carbon dioxide in environment, improves workability by reinforcing the lowered strength due to higher unit quantity, and performs mining liquor at very low cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composition: comprises 5-30 pts. wt. fly ash or 5-30 pts. wt. bottom ash, and 100 pts. wt. green cement; and further comprises either: 60-80 pts. wt. slag, 0.1-1 pts. wt. fluidizing agent, and 200-400 pts. wt. aggregate based on 100 pts. wt. cement; or 60-80 pts. wt. slag, 0.1-1 pts. wt. fluidizing agent, and 200-400 pts. wt. aggregate based on 100 pts. wt. green cement. Preferred Components: The cement is Portland cement or green cement. The fly ash has powdery degree of 5000-10000 cm2/g. The bottom ash has particle size of 80-5000 mu m. The green cement comprises calcium sulfoaluminate-based expanding agent. The fly ash and bottom ash are carbon dioxide-immobilized through an accelerated carbonation process which converts calcium oxide contained in fly ash and bottom ash as calcium carbonate.
   EXAMPLE - 21 kg Green cement containing calcium sulfoaluminate-based expansion agent having powdery degree of 3400 cm2/g, 15 kg slag generated from blast furnace steelworks, 0.1 kg naphthalene-based fluidizing agent, and 63.9 kg aggregate produced by sand extraction from the coastal area were mixed to prepare mine carcass composition.

TI Carbon dioxide fertilizer used as fertilizer for            crops, for capturing carbon dioxide, or for regulating            and controlling photosynthesis of plant, which is            prepared by absorbing carbon dioxide from absorbent            solution.

   LI C
   FANG T
   FENG X
   SUN D
   LI P
   ZHU Y
   JIA X
AE UNIV SHIHEZI (USHZ-C)
GA 202285406S
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide fertilizer, which is prepared                by absorbing carbon dioxide from an absorbent                solution; the carbon dioxide fertilizer is an                emulsion; the carbon dioxide fertilizer comprises                an oil-in-water emulsion structure.
   USE - Carbon dioxide fertilizer used as fertilizer                for crops or for capturing carbon dioxide                (claimed), for regulating and controlling                photosynthesis of plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide fertilizer is convenient                for road and pipeline transportation, it is applied                to greenhouse, improving photosynthesis of                plant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   1. a preparation method for carbon dioxide                fertilizer, which involves:
   a. mixing the absorbent and water preparation                absorbent solution;
   b. introducing the gas containing carbon                dioxide into the absorbent solution to obtain the                carbon dioxide fertilizer;
   2. a capture method for carbon dioxide, which                involves:
   a. introducing the gas containing carbon                dioxide into the carbon dioxide fertilizer, the                carbon dioxide fertilizer absorbing carbon                dioxide;
   b. capturing carbon dioxide, before                introducing the carbon dioxide-containing gas, the                carbon dioxide fertilizer also needs to release                CO2, the carbon dioxide-containing gas is                introduced into the carbon dioxide fertilizer at                normal pressure, the amount of absorbed carbon                dioxide is 0-8 mg/g, preferably 8 mg/g, the carbon                dioxide fertilizer release CO2 at normal pressure                can be recycled.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide                  fertilizer is a white emulsion or a translucent                  white emulsion, the absorbent solution is an                  aqueous solution of absorbent, sodium fatty acid,                  for example, selected from sodium stearate,                  sodium palmitate, sodium laurate and sodium                  ricinoleate, sodium ricinoleate (NaRcn).The carbon dioxide-containing gas is carbon                  dioxide-containing industrial waste gas.                  Preferably, the industrial waste gas is power                  plant flue gas, refinery exhaust gas, steel                  making exhaust gas, cement plant exhaust gas,                  chemical plant exhaust gas, water gas, biogas,                  natural gas and carbonate ore decomposition gas.                  Preferably, the carbon dioxide-containing gas is                  selected from flue gas, where the volume                  concentration of carbon dioxide is 20%. The                  carbon dioxide fertilizer absorbs carbon dioxide                  reversibly.

   ADVANTAGE - The pipe adopts the constant pressure safety                valve to replace the splinter type safety valve,                when there is over-pressure, only the exhaust                pressure is regulated, and it is not completely                exhausted, it will not cause the failure of the                whole mounted rock breaking pipe. Adopts the                waterproof heat insulation measures, effectively                reducing the water passing through the steel                rock-breaking pipe to the liquid carbon dioxide in                the rock- breaking pipe transfer heat quantity is                too fast, causing liquid carbon-di-oxide heating,                gasification, pressure stabilizing pressure                limiting valve automatically discharges part of the                gas, and the stability of the pressure in the pipe                is kept.

AB    NOVELTY - A collaborative traceability method involves                (i) performing online data preprocessing based on                collaborative traceability technology by                eliminating outliers and unified units, (ii)                setting pollutant and carbon ratio based on                collaborative traceability technology by modeling                base settings and source spectral feature                constraints, (iii) establishing technical                parameters for coordinated traceability of various                atmospheric pollutants and greenhouse gases by                determining the concentration thresholds, (iv)                identifying and quantifying pollution sources and                source impacts of single pollutants using novel                receptor models by incorporating particulate                material (PM) 2.5, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide,                sulfur dioxide, nitrogen oxide (I), volatile                organic compounds (VOCs) gaseous pollutants into                the PMF (positive matrix factorization)/ME2-SR                (multilinear engine 2-species ratios) model, (v)                constructing collaborative traceability                technology.
   USE - Collaborative traceability method for multiple                atmospheric pollutants and greenhouse gases used in                applications of atmospheric particulate material,                gaseous pollutant and greenhouse gas.

and carbon ratio based on                collaborative traceability technology by modeling                base settings and source spectral feature                constraints, (iii) establishing technical                parameters for coordinated traceability of various                atmospheric pollutants and greenhouse gases by                determining the concentration thresholds, carbon monoxide, sulfur dioxide, nitrogen                oxide of formula: NO_x (I), volatile organic                compounds (VOCs) gaseous pollutants into the PMF                (positive matrix factorization)/ME2-SR (multilinear                engine 2-species ratios) model, and calculating the                source and impact of each substance in turn, (v)                constructing collaborative traceability technology                for multiple atmospheric pollutants and greenhouse                gases by proposing formula for calculating the                influence of synergistic sources, identifying the                synergistic sources of various gaseous pollutants                sulfur dioxide-nitrogen oxide (I)-VOCs and                PM2.5-carbon dioxide, and TI Use of methanol produced using carbon dioxide,            hydrogen and electrical energy obtained from solar            energy, as fuel for operating internal combustion            engine used for operating truck and bus with preset            permissible total mass.
AU OBRIST F
AE OBRIST TECHNOLOGIES GMBH (OBRI-Non-standard)
GA 202289994S
AB    NOVELTY - Use of methanol as fuel for internal                combustion engine of truck or bus with a                permissible total mass of at least 3.5 tons, is                claimed. The methanol is produced by splitting                water into hydrogen and oxygen in a                hydrogen-producing electrolysis unit (11),                extracting carbon dioxide from ambient air using a                carbon dioxide sorption unit (12), and supplying                hydrogen and carbon dioxide into a methanol                synthesis unit (34) and producing methanol. A                photovoltaic unit (24) absorbs solar energy and                converts into electrical energy. The electrolysis                unit, the carbon dioxide sorption unit and the                methanol synthesis unit are operated by the                electrical energy generated in the photovoltaic                unit.
   USE - Use of methanol as fuel for operating internal                combustion engine e.g. two-cylinder reciprocating                piston engine used for operating truck and bus (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The use of methanol operates the truck or bus                in an environmentally-friendly manner even for long                journeys, ensures climate-neutral mobility and                climate-neutral goods transport, reduces the costs                for complex charging stations, and eliminates the                complex storage of hydrogen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) method for operating internal combustion                engine of truck or bus, which involves transporting                the produced methanol by a distribution system to                at least one tank of the truck or bus, and feeding                (if required) to the internal combustion engine                from the tank and burning to generate mechanical                energy;
   (2) truck or bus, which comprises a drive                system containing a power unit, at least one drive                battery and electric motor(s) for drawing                electrical energy from the drive battery. The power                unit contains a two-cylinder reciprocating piston                engine containing two cylinder piston units in a                tandem arrangement, and at least one generator for                generating electrical energy. Each piston unit                contains a crankshaft. The crankshafts of both                piston units are mechanically coupled to each                other. At least one crankshaft, preferably both                crankshafts are mechanically connected to the                generator; and
   (3) method for operating truck or bus, which                involves operating the two-cylinder reciprocating                piston engine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of plant                for production of methanol.
   Hydrogen-producing electrolysis unit                (11)
   Carbon dioxide sorption unit (12)
   Photovoltaic unit (24)
   Seawater desalination unit (27)
   Methanol synthesis unit (34)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Before feeding water into                  the electrolysis unit, the water is desalinated                  in a seawater desalination unit (27) which is                  operated completely by the electrical energy                  generated in the photovoltaic unit. A portion of                  methanol in a splitting unit arranged between a                  tank and the reciprocating engine splits into a                  synthesis gas that comprises hydrogen and carbon                  monoxide. The synthesis gas alone or together                  with the methanol is passed into the                  reciprocating engine.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - MECHANICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Process: The reciprocating piston                  engine is operated with a compression ratio of at                  least 14:1, preferably at least 20:1. The                  electric motor acts through a gearbox or directly                  on a wheel axle. Preferred Components: The tank                  of the truck or bus is fluidly connected to the                  reciprocating engine and at least partially,                  preferably completely filled with regeneratively                  produced methanol.

TI Residual gas volume measurement device for            measuring volume of residual gas in to-be-inspected            container, has pump which delivers water for            pressurization in second pipe to airtight container            side after residual gas is introduced from container to            be inspected to airtight container.
AU SHUJI N
   TAKASHI M
   NOBUAKI W
AE KYOTO ELECTRONICS MFG CO LTD (KYOT-Non-standard)
GA 202242927X
AB    NOVELTY - The device (1) has a second pipe (50) that                allows communication between an airtight container                (10) filled with a basic carbon dioxide absorbing                liquid and a second storage unit (40) for storing                water for pressurization. The second pipe has a                section on the airtight container side filled with                the carbon dioxide absorbing liquid and has a                section on the second storage unit side filled with                the water for pressurization. A pump (P1) is                arranged in the section of the second pipe filled                with the water for pressurization. The pump                delivers the water for pressurization in the second                pipe to the second storage unit side before a                residual gas is introduced from a container (B) to                be inspected to the airtight container, and                delivers the water for pressurization in the second                pipe to the airtight container side after the                residual gas is introduced from the container to be                inspected to the airtight container.
   USE - Residual gas volume measurement device for                measuring volume of residual gas in to-be-inspected                container filled with carbonated beverage, beer and                sparkling wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The pump can pressurize the airtight container                filled with the carbon dioxide absorbing liquid                only by feeding the pressurized water without                contacting the carbon dioxide absorbing liquid, so                as to prevent the pump from being exposed to the                strongly basic state, to suppress the                deterioration, and to prevent the solute of the                carbon dioxide absorption liquid from being                deposited on the pump even if the pump is used for                a long period of time. The volume of the residual                gas can be measured with high accuracy on the basis                of the volume change amount of the residual gas due                to the pressurization and the pressure in the                airtight container before and after the                pressurization, without being affected by the                change in the partial molar volume when the                pressurized water and the carbon dioxide absorbent                are mixed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                residual gas volume measurement method for                measuring volume of residual gas in container to be                inspected.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                residual gas volume measurement device. (Drawing                includes non-English language text)

TI Catalyst useful for catalyzing hydrogenation reduction of carbon dioxide in cooperation with non-thermal plasma, comprises active component (comprised of palladium and boron) and carrier (comprised of zirconium dioxide).
AU SUN Y
   WU K
   WU W
   JIANG B
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG GONGSHANG (UYZG-C)
GA 202179922L
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises an active component comprising palladium Pd and component (B) comprising an oxide of zinc, indium and/or cerium loaded on a carrier comprising zirconium dioxide (ZrO2).
   USE - The catalyst is useful for catalyzing hydrogenation reduction of carbon dioxide in cooperation with non-thermal plasma (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: can realize carbon dioxide low temperature, atmospheric pressure, efficient and stable catalytic conversion, and carbon monoxide with higher selectivity through the synergy of non-thermal plasma; has high conversion rate of carbon dioxide and high energy efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Catalyst comprises an active component comprising palladium Pd and component (B) comprising an oxide of zinc, indium and/or cerium loaded on a carrier comprising zirconium dioxide (ZrO2). The catalyst comprises PdxAy/ZrO2.
   y = 0-2, and
   A = at least one of zinc, indium and cerium.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) use method of catalyst for catalyzing the hydrogenation reduction of carbon dioxide in cooperation with non-thermal plasma, comprising (i) building a DBD reactor, combining the PdxAy/ZrO2 catalyst, filling the DBD reactor, and (ii) passing hydrogen/argon gas into the DBD reactor to increase the temperature to activate the PdxAy/ZrO2 catalyst. After the activation is completed, the PdxAy/ZrO2 catalyst is lowered to room temperature and switched to the reaction atmosphere of H2 and CO2, when the gas path is stabilized, turning on plasma power source for carbon dioxide hydrogenation reduction reaction; and
   (2) preparing catalyst, comprising (i) adding 2 g commercial ZrO2 in a 500 ml three-hole flask, (ii) dissolving 5.04 g sodium bicarbonate in 150 ml deionized water, stirring to dissolve, adding to the three-hole flask, stirring for 10 minutes at 80 degrees C, (iii) according to mol ratio of y/x, adding the zinc nitrate and palladium nitrate into the 100 ml water, stirring to dissolve to prepare precursor solution, (iv) adding the precursor solution to the three-hole flask by peristaltic pump at 80 degrees C and continuously stirring, after finishing dripping, stirring and aging for 2 hours at 80 degrees C, (v) using de-ionized water to centrifugally wash to neutral, drying at 80 degrees C for 12 hours to obtain the yellow brown powder, and (vi) placing the obtained yellow brown powder in a tubular furnace, roasting for 4 hours at 350 degrees C and drying under air condition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The content of the carrier ZrO2 is 95-95 wt.%, and the content of the active component Pd is 2 wt.%, the content of the active component (B) is 0-2.93 wt.%. The PdxZny/ZrO2 is Pd1Zn0.3/ZrO2.Preferred Method: The method comprises discharging DBD reactor through the dielectric barrier comprising a high voltage electrode. a ground electrode and a quartz tube, where the ground electrode is covered on the outer surface of the quartz tube, the high voltage electrode is set in the quartz tube, connected with the plasma power supply, the two ends of the quartz tube are provided with an air inlet and an air outlet, the gap part of the quartz tube is completely or partially filled in the PdxAy/ZrO2 catalyst; the air inlet is used for introducing the mixed gas of the reaction gas H2 and CO2 and the gas outlet is connected with the gas chromatograph for detecting the composition of the gas after reaction. The PdxAy/ZrO2 catalyst is PdxZny/ZrO2 catalyst, and the DBD reactor is first used 10 vol.% H2/Ar is activated at a temperature rising rate of 10 degrees C/minute to 400 degrees C for 4 hours. The PdxAy/ZrO2 catalyst is PdxZny/ZrO2 catalyst and the reaction gas ratio CO2 : H2 is 1:1-1:4. The PdxAy/ZrO2 catalyst is a PdxZny/ZrO2 catalyst, and the reaction gas flow rate is 20-80 ml/minute. The power of the plasma power supply is 10-20 W.

TI Evaluating the equivalent permeability of cement in the vertical corrosion direction after carbon dioxide corrosion, involves cementing cement parameters corresponding to the cementing stone of the corroded oil or water well.
AU FENG F
   CONG Z
   HAN X
   YAN M
   LIU S
   HU X
AE UNIV NORTHEAST PETROLEUM (UYPT-C)
GA 202028182L
AB    NOVELTY - Evaluating the equivalent permeability of cement in the vertical corrosion direction after carbon dioxide corrosion, involves cementing cement parameters corresponding to the cementing stone of the corroded oil or water well according to the actual formation temperature and pressure conditions, carbon dioxide content and ion content. The simulation results of the thickness of each corroded area is calculated in cement cement stone at different times and the thickness of uncorroded area, h1 is the amorphous silicom dioxide enriched area. The volume fraction of each mineral in each corrosion area of cementing cement stone at different times is calculated. The porosity of each corrosion zone of cementing cement is calculated. The permeability of each corrosion area of cementing cement stone is calculated. The vertical corrosion direction of the cement in different corrosion stages is calculated.
   USE - Method for evaluating the equivalent permeability of cement in the vertical corrosion direction after carbon dioxide corrosion.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to evaluate the equivalent permeability of cement that considers the effect of the size of the cement stone test piece on the equivalent permeability in the vertical corrosion direction, and can truly evaluate the degree of the effect of CO2 corrosion on the equivalent permeability in the vertical corrosion direction of the annulus cement stone of different sizes underground.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Evaluating the equivalent permeability of cement in the vertical corrosion direction after carbon dioxide corrosion, involves cementing cement parameters corresponding to the cementing stone of the corroded oil or water well according to the actual formation temperature and pressure conditions, carbon dioxide content and ion content. The simulation results of the thickness of each corroded area is calculated in cement cement stone at different times and the thickness of uncorroded area h4, i is the number of corroded area, i is 1, 2, 3, h1 is the amorphous silicom dioxide enriched area, h2 is the calcium oxide deposition area, h3 is the calcium hydroxide consumption area. The volume fraction of each mineral in each corrosion area of cementing cement stone at different times is calculated. The porosity of each corrosion zone of cementing cement is calculated. The permeability of each corrosion area of cementing cement stone is calculated. The vertical corrosion direction of the cement in different corrosion stages is calculated.

PT P
PN CN110791595-A; CN110791595-B
TI Regenerating waste biomass using acidic electrolytic water comprises e.g. pulverizing waste biomass, adding to hydrogen peroxide solution, and performing irradiation treatment, filtering, and drying to obtain pretreated waste biomass.
AU CHEN K
   XIAO W
   LI X
   SHEN M
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (USWS-C)
GA 2020154027
AB    NOVELTY - Regenerating waste biomass using acidic electrolytic water comprises e.g. (i) pulverizing waste biomass, adding to hydrogen peroxide solution, and performing irradiation treatment, filtering, and drying to obtain pretreated waste biomass, and (ii) adding pretreated waste biomass and acidic electrolytic water A to the high-pressure reactor, injecting high-pressure carbon dioxide into the reactor, and maintaining at 50-65 degrees C and a pressure of 10-20 MPa for 90-120 minutes, releasing pressure, and adding Lewis acid and acidic electrolytic water B, and again injecting high-pressure carbon dioxide into the reactor, and maintaining at 80-100 degrees C and a pressure of 12-22 MPa for 30-60 minutes, completing supercritical carbon dioxide treatment, releasing pressure by opening valve, after releasing pressure, subjecting reaction material to solid-liquid separation, liquid recovery, and solid drying to obtain the product, and using sodium chloride as an electrolyte.
   USE - The method is useful for regenerating waste biomass using acidic electrolytic water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: increases utilization of raw materials, and improves degradation of lignin and hemicellulose.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Regenerating waste biomass using acidic electrolytic water comprises (i) pulverizing waste biomass, adding to hydrogen peroxide solution, and performing irradiation treatment, filtering, and drying to obtain pretreated waste biomass, and (ii) adding pretreated waste biomass and acidic electrolytic water A to the high-pressure reactor, injecting high-pressure carbon dioxide into the reactor, and maintaining at 50-65 degrees C and a pressure of 10-20 MPa for 90-120 minutes, releasing pressure, and adding Lewis acid and acidic electrolytic water B, and again injecting high-pressure carbon dioxide into the reactor, and maintaining at 80-100 degrees C and a pressure of 12-22 MPa for 30-60 minutes, completing supercritical carbon dioxide treatment, releasing pressure by opening valve, after releasing pressure, subjecting reaction material to solid-liquid separation, liquid recovery, and solid drying to obtain the product, where, using sodium chloride as an electrolyte to obtain an acidic stock solution by electrolysis, and diluting acidic stock solution 10-15 times to obtain acidic electrolytic water A, diluting acidic stock solution 30-45 times to obtain acidic electrolytic water B, the redox potential ORP of the acidic stock solution is 900-1250 mv, and the pH of acidic stock solution is 1.3-2.5.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the process of using sodium chloride as an electrolyte to obtain an acidic stock solution by electrolysis, the concentration of the sodium chloride solution used is 0.05-0.15 kg/l, and the flow rate of the sodium chloride solution into the electrolytic cell is 5-10 l/minutes, and the electrolysis current is 5-10 A, and the voltage is 10-18 V. In the step (i), the mass fraction of the hydrogen peroxide solution is 15-30%, the mass ratio of the waste biomass to the hydrogen peroxide solution is 1:5-8, and the irradiation treatment uses gamma rays with an irradiation dose of 5-15 kGy for 30-60 minutes. In the step (ii), the weight ratio of the pretreated waste biomass to the acidic electrolytic water A is 1:8-12, and the weight ratio of the pretreated waste biomass to the acidic electrolytic water B is 1:3-5. In the method, before solid-liquid separation, adding acidic electrolytic water C to the reaction material, and adding vacuum packaging bag for vacuum packaging, and controling vacuum degree to 0.1 MPa, placing vacuum packaging bag into the high static pressure processing equipment, sealing pressurizing cavity, and pressurizing for high static pressure processing. The parameters of the high static pressure treatment are heating to 500-600 MPa at a pressure increasing speed of 4-6 MPa/seconds, and maintaining pressure for 10-20 minutes at room temperature. The acidic electrolytic water C is obtaining by diluting an acidic stock solution 20-25 times, and the mass ratio of the reaction material to the acidic electrolytic water C is 1:8-12. Preferred Components: The Lewis acid is aluminum trichloride, magnesium dichloride, zirconium chloride, zinc dichloride, zinc acetate, antimony trioxide, stannous octoate, tin chloride or chromium oxide, and the weight ratio of the Lewis acid to the acidic electrolytic water B is 1:15-20. The waste biomass is straw, forest product processing residues, or garden greening waste. The straw is corn straw, wheat straw, sorghum straw, or bamboo straw.

PN GB2574436-A; WO2019234202-A1; GB2588038-A
TI Liquid processing system for diffusing carbon dioxide into flow of beverage, has pressure control device that maintains pressure of gas within gas plenum lower than of flow of liquid at temporal commencement and cessation of flow of liquid.
AU COLBY E G
AE NARINO LTD (NARI-Non-standard)
GA 2019A3742N
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has a diffusion chamber (102) through which a flow of liquid occurs. A carbon dioxide (CO2) gas reservoir (108) is coupled to a gas exchange membrane arrangement of the chamber. A liquid container (114) is coupled to the diffusion chamber through a liquid inlet port (116). A pressure control device (130) is provided between the reservoir and a CO2 gas inlet port (110), and is comprised of two CO2 gas flow paths arranged in parallel. One CO2 gas flow path operates to limit a pressure drop to maximum pressure drop, and other CO2 gas flow path is formed as a bleed flow path having resistance to CO2 gas flow. The pressure control device maintains a pressure of CO2 gas within a CO2 gas plenum (106) lower than the pressure of flow of liquid at temporal commencement and cessation of flow of liquid through the diffusion chamber. The pressure of flow of liquid above the pressure of CO2 gas is limited within maximum differential pressure.
   USE - Liquid processing system for diffusing carbon dioxide (CO2) into flow of beverage such as beer, wine and ale, used in compact public house and bar.
   ADVANTAGE - The pressure control device provides a practical and cost-effective control of pressure transients which potentially cause auxiliary-optimal reliability of the liquid processing apparatus. The range of height and diameter enables the liquid processing system to have a spatial compact form factor, thus improving the portability and reducing the installation space requirement. The components connectable by pipes can allow the liquid processing system to be arranged as per the convenience of the user, thus enabling the liquid processing system to be configured to have a compact and customizable form factor. The detachable components of the liquid processing system enables improved portability of the liquid processing system and also makes maintenance more convenient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of operating a liquid processing system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the liquid processing system.
   Liquid processing system (100)
   Diffusion chamber (102)
   Carbon dioxide gas plenum (106)
   Carbon dioxide gas reservoir (108)
   Carbon dioxide gas inlet port (110)
   Liquid container (114)
   Liquid inlet port (116)
   Pressure control device (130)

PN CN110451567-A; CN110451567-B
TI Removing phosphorus, arsenic and silicon impurities, extracting tungsten and recovering alkali from crude sodium tungstate solution, by mixing calcium tungstate, passing carbon dioxide, extracting and heating sodium bicarbonate solution.
AU CHEN X
   ZHAO Z
   LIU X
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 201998422T
AB    NOVELTY - Method for removing phosphorus, arsenic and silicon impurities, extracting tungsten and recovering alkali from a crude sodium tungstate solution, involves (a) mixing calcium tungstate with a crude sodium tungstate solution for reaction, so that phosphorus ions, arsenic ions, and silicon ions in the crude sodium tungstate solution all form calcium precipitates, and filtering to obtain a sodium tungstate solution, (b) passing carbon dioxide into the sodium tungstate solution obtained in step (a) until the solution pH reaches 3-6.5, (c) extracting with a weakly alkaline extractant in step (b) the tungsten in the same sodium polytungstate solution, (d) heating the sodium bicarbonate solution obtained in step (c) to obtain a sodium carbonate solution and a carbon dioxide gas, and the sodium carbonate solution is returned for decomposing tungsten ore to prepare the crude sodium tungstate solution.
   USE - The method is used for removing phosphorus, arsenic and silicon impurities, extracting tungsten and recovering alkali from a crude sodium tungstate solution.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently removes the impurities, extracts and recovers the alkali from crude sodium tungstate solution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for removing phosphorus, arsenic and silicon impurities, extracting tungsten and recovering alkali from a crude sodium tungstate solution, involves (a) mixing calcium tungstate with a crude sodium tungstate solution for reaction, so that phosphorus ions, arsenic ions, and silicon ions in the crude sodium tungstate solution all form calcium precipitates, and filtering to obtain a sodium tungstate solution, (b) passing carbon dioxide into the sodium tungstate solution obtained in step (a) until the solution pH reaches 3-6.5, so that the tungsten in the solution exists as a polymer ion to obtain the same sodium polytungstate solution, (c) adsorbing with a weakly basic anion exchange resin or extracting the tungsten in the same sodium polytungstate solution with a weakly alkaline extractant in step (b), where the raffinate solution or the post-cross solution is a sodium bicarbonate solution, (d) heating the sodium bicarbonate solution obtained in step (c) to obtain a sodium carbonate solution and a carbon dioxide gas, respectively, returning the sodium carbonate solution for decomposing tungsten ore to prepare the crude sodium tungstate solution, and returning the carbon dioxide gas to step (b) for decomposing the sodium tungstate solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (a), the calcium tungstate is artificial calcium tungstate. In step (a), dividing the crude sodium tungstate solution into two parts, and adding calcium to one of the crude sodium tungstate solutions to form calcium tungstate, and mixing with the other crude sodium tungstate solution for reaction. Preferred Property: In step (b), the pressure of carbon dioxide is controlled to be 0.1-10 MPa. Preferred Conditions: In step (a), the reaction temperature is 20-150 degrees C, and the reaction time is 0.5-6 hours. In step (b), the reaction temperature is 20-80 degrees C, and the reaction time is 0.5-4 hours. In step (d), the sodium bicarbonate solution is heated at 60-100 degrees C.

PN CN109724974-A
TI Transformer online monitoring system based on laser detection technology, comprises laser detection sensing system comprising component and content for detecting faulty gas dissolved in transformer oil.
AU WEI Z
   LI C
   FAN M
   ZHOU X
   YU J
   SHANG X
AE JIAXING POWER SUPPLY CO STATE GRID ZHEJI (SGCC-C)
GA 2019427905
AB    NOVELTY - A transformer online monitoring system comprises laser detection sensing system comprising component and content for detecting faulty gas dissolved in transformer oil, where fault indication of partial discharge of-paper insulation is issued, when contents of ethane and carbon dioxide reaches preset value, fault indication of spark discharge in the oil is issued, when the laser detection sensing system detects hydrogen and acetylene and contents of hydrogen and acetylene reaches preset value, fault indication of arc in the oil is issued, when the laser detection sensing system detects hydrogen, acetylene, methane, ethylene and ethane and contents of hydrogen, acetylene, methane, ethylene and ethane reaches preset value and fault indication of the arc in oil and paper is issued, when the laser detection sensing system detects hydrogen, acetylene, methane, ethylene, ethane and carbon monoxide and contents of carbon dioxide and hydrogen, acetylene, methane, ethylene and ethane.
   USE - Transformer online monitoring system based on laser detection technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The transformer online monitoring system monitors composition and content of fault gases dissolved in transformer oil, predicts early failure of transformer, such that repairing transformer in time, extends life of transformer and prevents catastrophic accidents.

TI Method for predicting hypotension in patient using deep learning-based non-invasive data, involves inputting preprocessed PPG data, electrocardiogram data and carbon dioxide partial pressure data into machine-learning deep neural network.
AU KO S
   LEE S
   YI H J
AE UNIV YONSEI WONJU IND-ACAD COOP FOUND (UYIA-C)
GA 202193076G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves acquiring polyphenylene glycol (PPG) data, electrocardiogram data and carbon dioxide capnography data of a patient, and preprocessing the PPG data, the electrocardiogram (ECG) data, and the carbon dioxide partial pressure data. The pre-processed data is input into a machine-learning deep neural network, and a hypotension prediction value of the patient is calculated through the deep network. The data is acquired based on the patient's biosignals acquired during the operation of the patient. A mean arterial blood pressure is smaller than a reference blood pressure, and is classified into hypotension data and normotensive data as correct answer data.
   USE - Method for predicting hypotension of a patient during surgery using non-invasive data based on deep learning.
   ADVANTAGE - The accuracy and reliability of hypotension prediction is improved by using not only PPG data but also electrocardiogram data and carbon dioxide partial pressure data as input values. The deterioration of patient's condition due to prolonged hypotension is minimized.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a a patient's hypotension prediction program using deep learning-based non-invasive data for functioning of a computer to execute the method.

TI Determining krypton, xenon and methane in air            separation oxygen involves carrying out catalytic            dealkylation and catalytic deoxygenation to standard            gas.
AU LUO X

   YU G
AE ZHONGHAO GUANGMING RES & DESIGN INST CHE (CNCC-C)
GA 2021621176
AB    NOVELTY - Determining krypton, xenon and methane in air                separation oxygen involves carrying out catalytic                dealkylation and catalytic deoxygenation to a                standard gas. A gas chromatography test is carried                out. A peak area of krypton, xenon and carbon                dioxide is obtained. Volume content of krypton,                xenon and methane in the standard gas is marked as                C mark 1, C mark 2 and C mark 3. The catalytic                dealkylation, catalytic deoxygenation and gas phase                chromatography test process of a to-be-tested air                separation oxygen are repeated. A volume content of                carbon dioxide background in the to-be-detected air                separation oxygen is obtained.
   USE - Method for determining krypton, xenon and                methane in air separation oxygen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables removing the methane,                avoiding the detection interference of the methane                to krypton, converting the methane into carbon                dioxide by testing the carbon dioxide so as to                realize the purpose of accurately measuring the                content of methane, avoiding the oxygen peak                covering krypton peak, preventing the damage of the                detector caused by oxygen and improving the                detection precision of krypton, xenon and                methane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device                for measuring krypton, xenon and methane in air                separation oxygen, which comprises a catalytic                dehydrocarbon device, a sampling valve, a catalytic                deoxygenation device and a gas chromatographic test                device arranged in sequence.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dehydrocarbon                  catalyst used in the catalytic                  dehydrocarbonization is a precious metal                  catalyst. The particle size of the noble metal                  catalyst is 15-25 mu m. The deoxygenation                  catalyst used in the catalytic deoxygenation is a                  palladium catalyst and/or a zeolite catalyst. The                  particle diameters of the palladium-based                  catalyst and the zeolite catalyst are                  independently 15-25 mu m. The chromatographic                  column used in the gas chromatographic test is a                  GDX-104 packed column with an outer diameter of 3                  mm and a length of 3 m. The carrier gas is                  hydrogen.

PN WO2021011111-A1; AU2020313849-A1; CN114096592-A; KR2022034201-A; EP3999227-A1; US2022266219-A1; JP2022541479-W
TI Framework for metal-organic framework system,            comprises disalicylate linkers comprising aromatic            ring(s) comprising carboxylate and alcohol functional            groups adjacent to one another, modulators and            localized defects formed by modulator.
AU ABNEY C W
   FALKOWSKI J M
   WESTON S C
   IVASHKO A C
   ABNICA-TER-W
   PALKOVSKY J M
   IBASHKOANNA
   FAILKOWSKI J M
AE EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
GA 2021085144
AB    NOVELTY - A framework comprises disalicylate linkers,                modulators and localized defects formed by the                modulator. The linker comprises at least one of                aromatic ring comprising a carboxylate functional                group and an alcohol functional group. The                carboxylate functional group and the alcohol                functional group are adjacent to one another on                each aromatic ring. Each aromatic ring is                positioned at a greatest distance from the other.                The framework has one or more distinct metallic                elements bridged by a linker.
   USE - Framework for metal-organic framework system                used for particles and adsorbent material (all                claimed) for separation of gases e.g. individual                gases from stream of combined gases, such as carbon                dioxide/nitrogen, carbon dioxide/hydrogen, carbon                dioxide/methane, carbon dioxide/oxygen, carbon                monoxide/nitrogen, carbon monoxide/methane, carbon                monoxide/hydrogen, hydrogen sulfide/methane and                hydrogen sulfide/nitrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The framework increases diffusion of gases,                and efficiently separates gases.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) metal-organic framework system, which                comprises metal-organic framework and a ligand (A)                comprising an amine; and
   (2) synthesis of the metal-organic framework,                which involves contacting a solution comprising one                or more sources of distinct metallic elements and                an organic linker capable of bridging metal chains,                and a modulator, and heating.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal-organic                  framework is of formula: M1xM2(2-x)(A), where M1                  and M2 are different metal cations, preferably                  divalent cations of calcium (Ca2+), magnesium                  (Mg2+), iron (Fe2+), chromium (Cr2+), vanadium                  (V2+), manganese (Mn2+), cobalt (Co2+), nickel                  (Ni2+), zinc (Zn2+) and copper (Cu2+), A is                  disalicylate organic linker and modulator, and x                  is 0.0-2.0. The metallic elements are chosen from                  calcium, magnesium, iron, chromium, vanadium,                  manganese, cobalt, nickel, zinc and copper. The                  solution comprises an elemental metal or a salt                  of the metal in which the counter anion comprises                  a nitrate, acetate, carbonate, oxide, hydroxide,                  fluoride, chloride, bromide, iodide, phosphate or                  acetylacetonate. Preferred Property: The                  metal-organic framework provides an X-ray                  diffraction pattern which is indexed to a                  hexagonal unit cell. Preferred Process: The                  method further involves contacting the framework                  with a ligand (B) in gas or liquid medium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The modulator                  comprises at least partially deprotonated beta                  -hydroxy carboxylic acid. The beta -hydroxy                  carboxylic acid is chosen from 3-hydroxypropionic                  acid, salicylic acid, and/or its functionalized                  derivative. The ligand (A) preferably comprises                  cyclic diamine or tetramine. The ligand (B) is an                  amine-containing molecule, preferably diamine,                  triamine or tetramine. The disalicylate linkers                  are of formulae (I)-(V). The cyclic diamine is                  preferably dimethylethylenediamine (mmen) of                  formula (VI), or 2-(aminomethyl)piperidine                  (2-ampd) of formula (VII). The tetramine is                  preferably 3,4,3-tetramine (spermine) of formula                  (VIII) or 2,2,2-tetramine of formula (IX).R1-R10 = H, halo, hydroxy, or                  (halo)methyl;andR7' = aryl or heteroaryl (both optionally                  substituted), vinyl, alkynyl, divinyl benzene, or                  diacetyl benzene.
DC E19 (Other organic compounds general - organic compounds of unknown or indefinite structure; general mixtures of many types; organic reactions (e.g. nitration, resolution) when applied generally.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC E05-B01; E05-E01B; E05-F02A; E05-K; E05-L02; E05-L03; J01-E02B2; J01-E03C2; J01-G03
