TI Carbon dioxide absorber for environmental protection treatment, comprises device main body, air pump, liquid level sensor, pH sensor, fixing plate, filler absorbing layer and air feeding pipe, where middle of one side of device main body is provided with liquid inlet.
AU FU Z
   WEI H
   QU W
AE JILIN JITAI ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (JILI-Non-standard)
GA 202085327R
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a carbon dioxide absorber technology field, specifically is a carbon dioxide absorber for environment-friendly treatment, comprising: a device main body, an air pump, a liquid level sensor, a pH sensor, a fixing plate, a filler absorbing layer and an air feeding pipe; one side of the device main body is provided with a liquid inlet; one side bottom end of the device main body is provided with a liquid outlet; the liquid outlet is fixedly installed with a drain valve; one side of the device main body is fixedly installed with a control panel; a display screen and a controller, the control panel is electrically connected with the controller; the controller is electrically connected with the drain valve and the display screen; one side of the device main body is fixedly installed with the air pump. The carbon dioxide absorber for environment-friendly treatment not only can real time monitor the amount and pH value of the internal alkali liquid, convenient for adding and changing at any time, can always keep high absorption efficiency, and at the same time removing the acidic and alkaline gas, the absorption effect is better.

PN CN211088735-U
TI High voltage carbon dioxide laser tube insulating structure, has insulating sleeve whose outer wall is provided with multiple axial convex ribs, where outer wall of sealed end of radiator is contacted with inner wall of insulating sleeve.
AU ZHAN W
AE NANTONG ZHUORUI LASER TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANT-Non-standard)
GA 202071901G
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a high voltage insulating structure of carbon dioxide laser tube, belonging to the field of carbon dioxide laser tube, comprising a radiator made of metal material and an insulating sleeve made of ceramic material, one end of the radiator is open, the other end is sealed, one end of the radiator opening is sleeved on the end part of the carbon dioxide laser tube; one end of the inner wall of the radiator is sealed is equipped with a lens; the insulating sleeve is one end is open, the other end is sealed cylindrical cylindrical; the inner wall of the insulating sleeve is provided with an inner thread; the outer wall of the radiator is provided with an outer thread matched with the inner thread; the outer wall of the insulating sleeve is provided with several axial convex ribs; several axial convex ribs are uniformly distributed in the circumferential direction; the outer wall of the sealed end of the radiator is contacted with the inner wall of the sealed end of the insulating sleeve. The utility model has simple structure, reasonable design, which can effectively ensure the laser tube insulation treatment, improve the heat conducting ability, and also has the fireproof capability.

TI Supercritical extraction device for peppermint oil, has stirring blade whose outer side is sleeved with separating box, and kettle body whose outer side is provided with jacket, where upper and lower parts of jacket are provided with water inlet and water outlet.
AU LIU R
   ZHENG J
   HE L
AE ANHUI EVER-DEV PERFUME CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202072422Y
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a supercritical extraction device of peppermint oil, comprising a carbon dioxide separating kettle, wherein the carbon dioxide separating kettle is composed of a kettle cover and a kettle body bolt; the kettle cover is provided with an exhaust pipe and a liquid inlet pipe; the middle part of the kettle cover is provided with a stirring driving mechanism; the output end of the stirring driving mechanism is fixedly connected with the stirring shaft; the stirring shaft is provided with multiple groups of stirring blades from top to bottom; the outer side of each group of the stirring blade is sleeved with a separating box; the outer side of the kettle body is provided with a jacket; and the upper and lower two parts of the jacket are respectively provided with a water inlet and a water outlet. The device, for the mixed solution after extracting into the separating kettle, layering and extruding and crushing, so as to accelerate the carbon dioxide in the mixed liquid to release, so as to ensure the efficiency and effect of the carbon dioxide separation, ensuring the product quality.

PT P
PN MY175835-A
TI Nanocomposite membrane for selective removal of            fluid, e.g. for removal of carbon dioxide from syngas,            natural gas and flue gas, has layer of thin polyamide            film which is formed by interfacial polymerization of            ethylene oxide monomer containing polyfunctional amine            and polyfunctional acyl chloride.
AU SEAN G P
   ISMAIL A F
   CHUN W K
   CHEER N B
AE UNIV MALAYSIA TEKNOLOGI (UYMA-Non-standard)
GA 202290144A
AB    NOVELTY - The membrane has a polysulfone membrane which                is coated with a first layer of siloxane-based                compound, and a second layer of a thin polyamide                film which is formed by interfacial polymerization                of an ethylene oxide monomer containing                polyfunctional amine and a polyfunctional acyl                chloride. The first and second layers comprise                multiple multi-walled carbon nanotubes within the                matrix. The multi-walled carbon nanotubes are                acid-oxidized with carboxylic acid group. The                multi-walled carbon nanotubes are functionalized                with methyl methacrylate. The siloxane-based                compound is polydimethylsiloxane. The ethylene                oxide monomer-containing polyfunctional amine is                diethylene glycol bis(3-aminopropyl) ether or                diaminopolyethylene glycol.
   USE - Nanocomposite membrane for selective removal                of fluid, e.g. for removal of carbon dioxide from                syngas, natural gas and flue gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The multi-layer nanocomposite membrane                incorporating multiple multi-walled carbon                nanotubes is provided. The dense and compact                nanocomposite membrane is provided by virtue of                incorporating size reduced MWCNTs. The                nanocomposite membrane is provided with improved                carbon dioxide permeance and selectivity, over                nitrogen and methane. The presence of moisture                effectively reduces emission of carbon dioxide into                the atmosphere.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross sectional view of                the forming mechanism of nanocomposite                membrane.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Beverage carbon dioxide tester, has cabinet body hinged with cabinet door that is formed with groove, cup cover connected with measuring needle, and fixing plate located on upper surface of telescopic rod that is fixedly connected with operation panel.
AU HAN L
   FAN J

PN CN110878077-A
TI Continuously preparing ethylene carbonate comprises e.g. ethylene carbonate with a catalyst and water, introducing carbon dioxide into the reaction system, introducing the reaction solution into a flash tank and rectifying the crude product.
AU DING Y
   LI L
   WANG Q
   AI N
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 202022207Y
AB    NOVELTY - Continuously preparing ethylene carbonate comprises (1) mixing ethylene carbonate with a catalyst and water at a set ratio to obtain a catalyst solution A, (2) introducing carbon dioxide into the reaction system according to the set flow rate, and using the back pressure valve to back pressure the system to the required pressure, (3) introducing catalyst solution A and ethylene oxide into the micro-mixer a at a set ratio, quickly mixing uniformly to obtain a catalyst-dissolved ethylene oxide mixed solution B, and (4) continuously introducing the mixed liquid B and the carbon dioxide gas into the micro-mixer B according to the set flow ratio to obtain a gas-liquid mixture C, and introducing the tubular reactor for reaction, (5) introducing the reaction solution into a flash tank with a set volume, and flashing out unreacted ethylene oxide, carbon dioxide and water to obtain a preliminary product, and (6) rectifying the crude product obtained to obtain the product of ethylene carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for continuously preparing ethylene carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is safe and efficient; has high reaction efficiency and ethylene oxide conversion rate of up to 100% in a short reaction residence time; and shortens the reaction residence time.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the steps (3) and (4), the mixing temperature of the micro-mixer is 34-60 degrees C, and the mixing time is 0.1-1 second. In the steps (3), the reaction temperature in the tubular micro-reactor is 100-200 degrees C, and the residence time of the reaction solution C is 2-3 minutes. Preferred Components: In the step (1), the molar flow ratio of the ethylene carbonate to ethylene oxide is 1:10. The mass flow ratio of catalyst to ethylene oxide is 5-10%: 1. The mass flow ratio of water to ethylene oxide is 0.1-3%:1. In the step (2), the molar flow ratio of the carbon dioxide flow rate to the ethylene oxide flow in the step (3) is 1-2:1, and the pressure range of the back pressure is 1-5 MPa. In the step (3), the flow rate range of the ethylene oxide is 0.5-5 ml/minute. In the step (4), the molar flow ratio of the carbon dioxide flow rate to the ethylene oxide flow in the step (3) is 1-2:1. In the step (3) and (4), the micro-mixer is a micro-channel reactor, a membrane dispersion micro-reactor or a micro-sieve reactor.The catalyst includes a quaternary ammonium salt having a formula R1R2R3R4N+ X- (I).R1R2R3R4 = aliphatic or aryl groups CxHy;x = 1-10;y = 2-21;X = chlorine, bromine or iodine, metal halide e.g. zinc + X-, magnesium + X-, aluminum + X-; andX = chlorine, bromine, or bromine or an ionic liquid.

PT P
PN RD662077-A
TI Method for detecting high-power carbon dioxide laser beam in EUV system, involves evaluating laser beam misalignment in respect of drifting direction and drifting distance, and dividing safety aperture into quadrants.
AU 
AE ANONYMOUS (ANON-C)
GA 2019509956
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves evaluating laser beam misalignment in respect of drifting direction and drifting distance. A safety aperture is divided into quadrants or octants, where each quadrant or octant is sufficiently thermally isolated. Temperature of thermally isolated octant is raised when the laser beam approaches an inner surface of an octant. Laser beam drifting direction and drifting distance are identified by monitoring and evaluating temperature change of each octant of the aperture. Heat sensing protrusions are added to each quadrant of an aperture for measuring temperature of quadrant.
   USE - Method for detecting high-power carbon dioxide laser beam in a EUV system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables accurately detecting misalignment of a high power carbon dioxide laser beam in a EUV system and preventing safety apertures from being damaged by carbon dioxide laser beam. The method enables identifying the laser beam drifting direction and the drifting distance by monitoring and evaluating temperature change of each octant of the aperture for correcting alignment of downstream optical elements such that efficiency of EUV generation can be subsequently improved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of safety apertures divided into thermally isolated octants and thermometers added to the quadrants to allow estimation of a laser beam position.
DC G07 (Photo-mechanical production of printing surfaces (G03F).); P84 (Other photographic (G03D-H).)

The stirring reaction time in step (i) is 45 minutes. In the step (ii), the microwave irradiation time is 25 minutes. In the step (iii), the collected solid product is to clean by using the deionized water and ethanol.

   YANG L
   LOU Y
   PAN C

AE UNIV JIANGNAN (UYJN-C)

AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of copper-metal organic framework material involves dissolving 3,4,9,10-perylene tetracarboxylic dianhydride in N, N-dimethylformamide as solvent, reacting with 1- (3-aminopropyl) imidazole to obtain perylene tetracarboxylic acid bis (propyl) imidazole, washing, drying, dissolving in chloroform solution, filtering to obtain PDI chloroform saturated solution, placing PDI chloroform saturated solution and methanol solution of copper chloride in a sealed container, heating at 40-80 degrees C, reacting for 2-6 hours, separating solid and liquid to obtain precipitate, washing, and drying.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing composite catalyst comprises (1)                dissolving ferric nitrate, nitrate, in alkaline                solution, stirring and mixing for 1-3 hours,                placing into hydrothermal synthesis kettle, under                the condition of 120-200 degrees C, performing heat                synthesis for 5-15 hours, washing and drying to                obtain the modified iron-based catalyst, (2)                pre-treating the molecular sieve, (3) physically                coating coupling modified iron-based catalyst and                pre-processing molecular sieve to obtain the                finished product composite catalyst.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nitrate is copper                  nitrate, manganese nitrate, zinc nitrate, nickel                  nitrate, and/or magnesium nitrate. The molar                  ratio of nitrate to ferric nitrate is 2:1. The                  aqueous metallic nitric acid solution is                  potassium ion, magnesium ion, copper ion, sodium                  ion, lithium ion, zinc ion, nickel ion, and                  calcium ion, The alkaline solution is sodium                  hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, or                  tetraethylammonium hydroxide. The mass ratio                  between the pre-treatment molecular sieve and the                  modified iron-based catalyst is 0.05-1. Preferred                  Method: In the step (2), the pretreatment of the                  molecular sieve includes two treatment methods,                  (a) adding molecular sieve directly to the                  0.05-0.5 mol/l metal nitric acid aqueous solution                  for ion exchange to control the acidity of the                  molecular sieve, (b) adding the molecular sieve                  directly to the 0.05-0.5 mol/l alkaline solution                  to control the pore structure of the molecular                  sieve.    LIU M
   TIE Y
AE UNIV GUANGXI (UYGU-C)
GA 2020787044
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of vaterite calcium carbonate                microspheres involve adding pre-prepared 5-30 %mass                calcium acetate solution to carbonization tower,                adjusting pH to 10-12 with aqueous ammonia, adding                0.5-5 %mass lactic acid, passing carbon dioxide                gas, using pH meter to monitor pH value of reaction                system online, stopping carbon dioxide gas passage                when pH value drops to 7 to obtain milky white                suspension, transferring milky white suspension to                stirred reactor, adding 1 %mass sodium alginate                solution, stirring for 0.5-3 hours, filtering,                washing filter cake 3 times with water, and drying                in blower drying oven at 105 degrees C.

PN CN110407639-A
TI Preparation of nano ionized water for agriculture,            involves mixing carbon dioxide nanoparticles, zinc            oxide nanoparticles, copper oxide nanoparticles, methyl            nanoparticles and amino acid with solvent and diluting            base solution.
AU HE J
   ZHANG L
AE SHENZHEN TIANJIXIN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2019948354
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of nano ionized water involves                mixing carbon dioxide nanoparticles, zinc oxide                nanoparticles, copper oxide nanoparticles, methyl                nanoparticles and amino acid with an aqueous                solvent, ultrasonically processing to prepare nano                ion water base liquid and diluting nanoparticle                base solution.
   USE - Preparation of nano ionized water used for                agricultural plant cultivation (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process produces nano ionized water which                can promote absorption of essential nutrients such                as carbon dioxide needed for plant growth. The                prepared water has good permeability, absorption                conductivity and deeply activates the growth                vitality of crops and helps crops to grow stronger.                The nano-ion water is environmentally-friendly,                does not consume soil fertility, helps to clean up                chemical residues in the soil and can effectively                reduce the use of chemical fertilizers and                pesticides.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The base solution                  comprises 25-35 pts. wt. carbon dioxide                  nanoparticles, 8-12 pts. wt. zinc oxide                  nanoparticles, 0.05-0.5 pt. wt. copper oxide                  nanoparticles, 25-35 pts. wt. methyl                  nanoparticles, 1-5 pts. wt. amino acid and 15-25                  pts. wt. aqueous solvent. Preferred Process: The                  preparation of carbon dioxide nanoparticles                  involves providing copper coil for spray-drying                  to form nano-coating film copper coil,                  electrically connecting one end of the film with                  zinc sheet, inserting the connected sheet into                  the electrolyte and introducing air into the                  electrolyte. The preparation of copper oxide                  nanoparticles involves providing a copper coil                  for spray-drying to form nano-coating film copper                  coil, electrically connecting one end of the                  nano-coated film copper coil with the copper                  plate, inserting the connected sheet into the                  electrolyte and introducing air into the                  electrolyte. The zinc oxide nanoparticles are                  prepared by providing a zinc coil for                  spray-drying to form a nano-coating on the                  surface of the zinc coil, electrically connecting                  one end of the nano-coated film zinc coil with                  the zinc sheet, and the other ends of the                  nano-coated film zinc coil with zinc sheet,                  inserting the connected sheet into the                  electrolyte and introducing air into the                  electrolyte. The spray-drying process involves                  forming a nano-coating on the surface of the                  copper coil, spraying the inner copper coil in                  clockwise direction at 30-800 degrees C and outer                  copper coil in counter-clockwise direction and                  subjecting to blasting treatment. Preferred                  Component: The copper coil comprises an inner                  layer copper coil and outer layer copper                  coil.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The methyl nanoparticles                  are prepared by providing a copper coil for                  spray-drying to form a nano-coating film copper                  coil, electrically connecting one end of the                  nano-coated film copper coil with the galvanized                  iron plate, inserting the connected sheet into                  the electrolyte and introducing air into the                  electrolyte.

GA 201985268R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of catalyst involves dispersing                carbon material in deionized water, adding metal                salt solution and hydrogen peroxide and then                stirring, mixing at room temperature, soaking                melamine aerogel in mixed solution, drying to                obtain carbon aerogel precursor, pyrolyzing                catalytic material precursor at 700-1100 degrees C                under inert gas atmosphere and obtaining metal                atom-anchored single-atom three-dimensional porous                catalytic material.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The usage amount of                  carbon material is 20 mg, deionized water is 5-20                  ml, preferably 10 ml, metal salt solution is 20                  mg, 10-500 mu l, preferably 280 mu l or                  0.005-0.015 mM, preferably 0.012 mM, melamine                  aerogel is 20 mg or 30-120 mg, preferably 60 mg,                  and hydrogen peroxide is 20 mg, 10-30 mu l or                  28-32 wt.%. Carbon                  material is graphene with particle size of 1-2 mu                  m or carbon nanotube with diameter of 10-20 nm                  and length of 10-30 mu m.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The application method                  involves: placing catalyst in gas diffusion                  electrode including carbon paper, carbon cloth                  and carbon felt and catalytically reacting; and                  grinding 0.1-3 mg/cm2, preferably 0.5-1 mg/cm2                  catalyst, dispersing in isopropanol solution,                  adding 5-30 wt.% Nafion (RTM: Perfluorosulfonic                  acid resin), stirring to obtain mixed solution,                  applying the mixed solution to gas diffusion                  electrode and drying. Preferred Components: The                  ratio of catalyst and isopropanol is 0.1-10 mg:1                  ml, preferably 9 mg:1 ml. The volume ratio of                  Nafion (RTM: Perfluorosulfonic acid resin) and                  isopropanol is 1:5-1:10, preferably 1:9.                  Preferred Property: Melamine aerogel has density                  of 4-12 mg/cm2 and pore size distribution of                  100-200 mu m.

PN CN109437632-A
TI Preparation of cement based material with fixed Carbon dioxide function involves mixing fly ash and sodium hydroxide, adding 3-(2-(2-amino ethylamino)ethylamino)propyl-trimethoxy silane, dry milling, curing and demolding product.
AU LIU P
   WANG F
   ZHANG F
   QU M
   GAO Y
AE UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
GA 2019249071
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of cement based material with fixed carbon dioxide function involves (1) mixing fly ash and sodium hydroxide, heating to 80-90 degrees C for 7-9 hours obtaining alkali-activated fly ash, (2) mixing the obtained alkali-activated fly ash with 3-(2-(2-amino ethylamino)ethylamino)propyl-trimethoxy silane at room temperature under dry milling for 7-9 hours to obtain modified fly ash, (3) adding modified fly ash to cement substrate forming multifunctional concrete slurry, curing at 20 plus minus 2 degrees C and 95 plus minus 5 degrees C under relative humidity for 7-days and demolding the product.
   USE - Preparation of cement based material with fixed carbon dioxide function with respect to organic cement based material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of sodium hydroxide is 5 mol/L. The mass ratio of alkali-activated fly ash and 3-(2-(2-amino ethylamino)ethylamino)propyl-trimethoxy silane is 9:1. The water-cement ratio of modified fly ash added to cement-based material is 0.4. The mass ratio of modified fly ash concrete is 10%.

PT P
PN CN109320401-A; CN109320401-B
TI Catalytic hydrogenation to synthesize ethanol using straw gasification coupled with carbon dioxide, comprises adding ferric oxide with oxygen into a gasification reaction furnace, adding bifunctional absorbent and catalyst and processing.
AU SA H
   HUANG J
   LI J
   GUO Y
   ZHAO C
AE UNIV NANJING NORMAL (UNNO-C)
GA 201917283D
AB    NOVELTY - Catalytic hydrogenation to synthesize ethanol using straw gasification coupled with carbon dioxide, comprises orderly connecting crusher, gasification reaction furnace, a first cyclone separator, a dust remover, an absorbing reaction furnace, a second cyclone separator, a regeneration reaction furnace and synthesizing reaction furnace, then adding ferric oxide with oxygen into a gasification reaction furnace, and placing in the absorption reactor is equipped with a bifunctional absorbent magnesium oxide (MgO)-magnesium (Mg2)-nickel, passing composite catalyst Pd-Cu in the synthetic reaction furnace, and processing.
   USE - The method is useful for catalytic hydrogenation to synthesize ethanol using straw gasification coupled with carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: solves environmental problem of forestry waste incineration; produces value-added products; and realizes resource utilization of the waste.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (i) crushing biomass straw, then adding into the gasification reaction furnace, passing crushed biomass straw oxygen through a gasification reactor catalytic cracking of iron oxide in iron oxide, then adding first mixed component to first cyclone separator, then gas-solid separating to obtain first mixed gas, where the first mixed gas is composed of carbon dioxide, hydrogen gas and carbon monoxide, and (ii) passing the first mixed gas separated by the first cyclone separator through dust collector and enters the absorption reaction furnace, then performing gas separation under action of a dual-function absorbent (MgO-Mg2 Ni), then adding produced second mixed component into the second cyclone separator, then performing gas-solid separation to obtain second mixed gas and saturated dual absorber, where the second mixed gas is composed of carbon monoxide, the saturated functional absorbent is composed of (MgCO3-Mg2NiH4), and (iii) adding saturated-absorbent into the regeneration reaction furnace, regenerating the third mixing gas and passing into synthesis reaction furnace, and then generating ethanol under action of composite catalyst Pd-Cu, where the third mixed gas is composed of hydrogen gas and carbon monoxide. The reaction temperature of the gasification reactor is 400-450 degrees C. The reaction temperature of the absorption reactor is 200-250 degrees C. The reaction temperature of the reaction furnace is 350-400 degrees C. The reaction temperature of the synthesis reactor is 200-300 degrees C. The ferrous oxide is oxidized by performing gas-solid separation by activating the first cyclone by high-temperature steam, then passing into the oxygen-supporting iron oxide and in the oxidation reaction tube, and adding to the gasification reactor and recycling, the reaction temperature of oxidation reaction pipe is 100-200 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The method further comprises a solid output end of coal and oxidation reaction tube are connected, first cyclone separator is connected with the oxidation reaction pipe, output end of oxidation reaction pipe and the gasification reaction furnace. The solid output end of the regeneration reaction furnace is connected with the absorbing reaction furnace.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

PT P
PN EP3431442-A1; EP3431442-B1
TI Production of potassium carbonate used e.g. as liquid thawing agent, involves washing carbon dioxide-containing exhaust gases by using aqueous solution of potassium hydroxide and/or potassium salt, obtained from dump wastewater.
AU LENGHEIM H
AE LENGHEIM H (LENG-Individual)
GA 2019074170
AB    NOVELTY - Production of potassium carbonate involves washing carbon dioxide-containing exhaust gases by using an aqueous solution of potassium hydroxide and/or potassium salt, obtained from dump wastewater generated during potash mining.
   USE - Production of potassium carbonate in the form of aqueous solution used as liquid thawing agent and for producing potassium carbonate granules used as solid scattering agent (all claimed). The liquid thawing agent is used in road applications. Can also be used in fertilizer, glass, food industry, soap and enamel production, and as starting material for producing other potassium compounds.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical since it uses dump wastewater generated during potash mining, and efficiently provides potassium carbonate with high yield and high quality. The method reduces carbon dioxide load generated during combustion or exhaust gas of industrial plants.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide-containing exhaust gas is combustion or exhaust gas obtained from industrial large-scale plants, such as fossil-fired power plants, blast furnaces for production and refinement of metals, natural gas processing plants, ammonia synthesis plants, cement plants or lime distilleries, preferably lime distilleries. Preferred Process: The method involves purifying the concentrated dump wastewater if necessary, recovering potassium hydroxide or potassium salt from the purified wastewater, using the aqueous solution of the recovered potassium hydroxide or potassium salt as a washing solution, and separating the potassium carbonate formed during gas scrubbing or washing, from the washing solution. The potassium hydroxide is recovered by electrolysis, preferably by chloralkali electrolysis, where the energy contained in the carbon dioxide-containing exhaust gas is used for operation of electrolysis plant for producing potassium hydroxide. The potassium chloride from the wastewater for use in electrolysis is recovered by flotation, hot dissolving process, electrostatic separation, cold pre- and post-decomposition, or fractional crystallization, preferably by fractional crystallization. Preferred Composition: The aqueous solution comprises at least 20%, preferably at least 45% potassium hydroxide. The aqueous potassium carbonate solution is concentrated to an amount of 10-60%, preferably 40-50% before or after separation by evaporation. Preferred Conditions: The carbonation of the potassium hydroxide or potassium-containing solution is carried out at a temperature of 100 degrees C or less, preferably 60 degrees C or less.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); ; G04 (Miscellaneous compositions - including luminescent and tenebrescent materials, de-icing/de-misting compositions, mastics, heat transfer compositions and aerosol-can filling mixtures (C09H).)

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The device further                  comprises a processor. The processor is connected                  to the oxygen concentration detecting device,                  carbon dioxide concentration detection device,                  blower and exhaust fan. The one-way valve                  comprises valve body. An air inlet hole is                  disposed at the upper end of the valve body. An                  air outlet hole is disposed at the lower end of                  the valve body. The working hole connects the air                  inlet hole and the air outlet hole. A piston is                  disposed in the working hole and a driving device                  is for driving the piston to move up and down.                  The upper end of the piston is frustum-shaped.                  The air inlet hole and the working hole are                  provided with a chamfer angle. The chamfer angle                  is adapted to the upper end of the piston. An                  upper sealing rubber is provided on the chamfer                  angle. A lower sealing rubber is provided at a                  frustum-shaped portion of the upper end of the                  piston. The multiple sealing rings are matched                  with the outer sidewalls of the piston and                  disposed on the inner wall of the working hole. A                  groove is corresponded to the sealing ring, which                  is provided on an outer sidewall of the piston.                  The driving device comprises a connecting plate                  disposed on the outer side wall of the lower end                  of the piston. A pin is provided on the                  connecting plate. An arc plate is disposed                  outside the pin and connecting plate, which is                  disposed on the arc plate for cooperating with                  the pin. The arc plate is rotated about a                  rotation shaft . A handle is attached to the arc                  plate. A hook is provided on the handle. An outer                  wall of the valve body is provided with a fixing                  rod for connecting with the hook. The filter                  comprises three filtration membranes arranged on                  one side of the gas flow direction. The pore size                  of first filter membrane is 0.5-1 mu . The pore                  size of second filter membrane is 0.1-0.5 mu .                  The pore size of third filter membrane is 0.01 mu                  .

TI Method for capturing carbon dioxide from flue gas, involves purifying flue gas containing carbon dioxide followed by preparing carbon dioxide lean liquid, introducing into absorption tower and collecting and storing carbon dioxide.
AU QIAN X
   LIU M
   ZHANG H
   ZHAO L
   LI J
AE UNIV SHANDONG NORMAL (UYSN-C)
GA 2018A17595
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide capturing method involves purifying flue gas containing carbon dioxide, adjusting temperature of the purified flue gas to not greater than 50 degrees C, preparing carbon dioxide lean liquid, introducing the carbon dioxide lean liquid and the purified flue gas into the absorption tower, such that gas-liquid two-phase contact occurs in the absorption tower with the carbon dioxide lean liquid and the carbon dioxide lean liquid absorbs carbon dioxide from the flue gas to form carbon dioxide rich liquid, introducing the carbon dioxide rich liquid into heating device, heating, such that decomposition of potassium bicarbonate in the carbon dioxide rich liquid releases carbon dioxide and the carbon dioxide rich liquid is converted into carbon dioxide lean liquid, cooling, performing absorption steps, absorbing carbon dioxide and collecting and storing the carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for capturing carbon dioxide from flue gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables capturing carbon dioxide with less energy consumption in a simple and easy manner at low cost by utilizing steel mill and power plants for carbon dioxide absorption and not utilizing coal and calcium carbonate, realizing recycling of potassium carbonate solution and utilization of waste and protecting environment.

AB    NOVELTY - The production system (1a) has a solid oxide                type water electrolysis apparatus (30) to generate                oxygen gas containing the oxygen gas for burning                the biomass derived fuel to the anode side, and                supplies the generated oxygen gas to the oxygen                blowing power generation device (20). The power                supply device is set for converting electric power                necessary for electrolysis into direct current (DC)                power and supplying the same to the solid oxide                type water electrolysis device, and a steam reheat                device is configured for reheating the steam                supplied from the steam supply device by heating                energy, and reheating the high temperature water                vapor. The synthetic gas is supplied from the solid                oxide type water electrolysis device. The synthesis                gas is made to react with a catalyst in a                prescribed temperature and pressure environment.                The FT crude oil or the crude methanol is                synthesized in the liquid fuel synthesizing                apparatus (60).
   USE - System for producing carbon neutral liquid                fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The energy efficiency of the production system                is increased. The methane gas from being released                into the atmosphere is prevented. The livestock                excreta, food processed residue and rubbish are                reused efficiently.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The producing system has a biomass-derived                fuel supply device (10) for supplying the                biomass-derived fuel. The biomass derived fuel                supplied from the biomass-derived fuel supply                device to burn the biomass-derived fuel with an                oxygen gas to generate rotational driving force.                The biomass-derived fuel is divided into one                portion and other portion of the carbon dioxide gas                rich exhaust gas which is combusted by burning with                the oxygen gas. The other portion is supplied                together with the oxygen gas to the power generator                (23). The oxygen-blowing power generator is                equipped with a generator driven by the power                generating device, for generating power. A carbon                dioxide gas separated from one portion of the                carbon dioxide gas rich exhaust gas by a carbon                dioxide separator (35) is supplied. A high                temperature steam having a molar ratio of the                carbon dioxide to the carbon dioxide is supplied to                the carbon dioxide gas separator. The carbon                dioxide gas and the high temperature steam are                electrolyzed by direct current (DC) power. A                synthesis gas of the hydrogen gas and the carbon                monoxide gas of about 2: 1 in a molar ratio is                formed on the cathode side and the molar ratio of                the carbon monoxide gas to the carbon monoxide gas                is almost 1.5. The alternating current power                generated by the oxygen-blowing power generation                device and the regenerative energy-derived                alternating current power supplied from the power                grid are interconnected to cover the operating                power necessary for the operation. The                biomass-derived fuel supply device supplies the                biomass-derived fuel with carbon monoxide-rich                gasified gas generated from the biomass generated                from the biomass by the methane fermentation                apparatus (11) or the oxygen-blown biomass                gasification apparatus (15), and the oxygen-blowing                power generation apparatus. The biogas or the                oxygen blowing gas engine device (24) which is                operated by burning the gas with the gaseous oxygen                rich in carbon monoxide with the oxygen gas is                provided as the power generator. The oxygen-blown                gas engine generator is equipped with a generator                driven by the oxygen-blowing gas engine device, for                generating power as the generator.

PN CN111158082-A
TI Preparing forming optical echo wall micro-cavity using the instability of fluid in fiber comprises e.g. preparing fiber preform, preparing optical fibers with different diameters, and connecting optical fiber fixing device.
AU WANG P
   ZONG M
AE UNIV BEIJING TECHNOLOGY (UYBT-C)
GA 202044921U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing forming optical echo wall micro-cavity using the instability of the fluid in the fiber comprises e.g. (i) preparing fiber preform, (ii) preparing optical fibers with different diameters, where the optical fiber diameter range is 20 mu m-2 mm, (iii) severally fixing two ends of semiconductor germanium core phosphate glass cladding optical fiber by optical fiber fixing device, the diameter of the optical fiber is 20 mu m-2 mm, (iv) connecting the optical fiber fixing device to three-dimensional displacement platform, and connecting the X axis of the three-dimensional displacement platform to gear motor, (v) focusing micro-device on fiber core part, and observing the change of the fiber core inside the fiber during heat treatment, and (vi) placing the heat source on the lower surface of the fiber.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing forming optical echo wall micro-cavity using instability of the fluid in the fiber.
   ADVANTAGE - The microsphere cavity has uniform size, smooth surface and adjustable spacing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing forming optical echo wall micro-cavity using the instability of the fluid in the fiber comprises (i) preparing fiber preform, (ii) preparing optical fibers with different diameters, where the optical fiber diameter range is 20 mu m-2 mm, (iii) severally fixing two ends of semiconductor germanium core phosphate glass cladding optical fiber by optical fiber fixing device, the diameter of the optical fiber is 20 mu m-2 mm, (iv) connecting the optical fiber fixing device to three-dimensional displacement platform, and connecting the X axis of the three-dimensional displacement platform to gear motor, (v) focusing micro-device on fiber core part, and observing the change of the fiber core inside the fiber during heat treatment, (vi) placing the heat source on the lower surface of the fiber, where the heat source comprises flame or carbon dioxide laser, and the heating temperature is higher than the melting point of the core material but less than the melting point of the cladding material, (vii) presetting the motion speed v of X-axis deceleration motor to make the fiber X-axis stretch to both ends at the speed v, where the deceleration speed range is 20-400 mu m/seconds, (viii) presetting the spot diameter of the carbon dioxide laser, the initial spot diameter equivalent to the optical fiber core diameter, (ix) presetting the power level of the carbon dioxide laser, the initial power is set to 0 W, (x) turning on the carbon dioxide laser, making the laser heat-treat the bottom of the optical fiber, adjusting the power of the carbon dioxide laser, and observing that the optical fiber core begins to deform in the microscopic device, (xi) turning on the motor speed at both ends of the optical fiber to make optical fiber stretch to both ends at speed v, the start speed is 20 mu m/seconds, (xii) changing the distance between central axis of the three-dimensional displacement platform and carbon dioxide laser, i.e., the area size of the carbon dioxide laser heat treatment fiber is changed, (xiii) increasing the power of the carbon dioxide laser, increasing the heat treatment temperature of the optical fiber, under the heat treatment of the laser and pulling force of the deceleration motors at both ends, series of micro-cavities with uniform size and smooth surface is formed in the optical fiber, (xiv) controlling the power level of carbon dioxide laser and initial diameter of the optical fiber, controlling the size of micro-cavity of the echo wall, (xv) controlling stretching speed of the geared motor to change the spacing between each micro-cavity, (xvi) observing the optical fiber contains series of micro-cavities of semiconductor germanium material with uniform size and smooth surface inside in the microscopic device, and (xvii) placing the optical fiber containing semiconductor germanium micro-cavity in hydrofluoric acid, slowly eroding phosphate glass cladding for promoting the exposure of the micro-cavity of the semiconductor germanium material echo wall.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), preforming at least one core, cores parallel to the longitudinal axis of the fiber, the melting point of the cladding material of the preform is greater than the melting point of core material, the viscosity of the cladding material is greater, the core material is germanium rod having purity of 99.99%, the cladding glass is phosphate glass, placing the germanium rod in phosphate glass tube to obtain fiber preform. In the step (ii), preparing the semiconductor germanium core phosphate glass cladding fiber by the optical fiber fusion core method, the temperature of drawn fiber is less than the melting point of phosphate glass cladding, but needs is higher than the melting point of the semiconductor germanium core, the viscosity of the cladding is large at this time, meeting the continuity conditions, and setting the drawing temperature to 1000 degrees C.

The power cord is                electrically connected to the right side of the                horizontal fermenting tank by means of an embedded                manner. The right end of the power cord is mounted                on the left surface of the electrical control box                and the axis of the power cord is perpendicular to                the left surface of the electrical control box. The                front surface of the electrical control box is                provided with an operation panel and is connected                by means of an embedded manner. The drive motor is                located directly below the power cord and is                mounted on the same horizontal plane. The drive                motor is disposed on the right side of the                horizontal fermenting tank by means of an embedded                method. The bottom end of the horizontal fermenting                tank is mounted on the upper surface of the support                bottom plate by electric welding. The feed sealing                clasp and the horizontal fermenting tank are of a                unitary structure. The carbon dioxide treatment                pressure relief auxiliary device comprises an                auxiliary device main body mechanism, a wireless                receiving control mechanism, an electromagnetic                closing mechanism, an airflow rotating power                guiding mechanism , a sodium peroxide guiding                mechanism, a mixed strong oxidation mechanism, and                a gas powder electrostatic separation mechanism. An                inner side of the auxiliary device main body                mechanism is provided with an electromagnetic                closing mechanism and connected by electric                welding. The wireless receiving control mechanism                is located at the upper left of the electromagnetic                closing mechanism and is electrically connected.                The airflow rotating power guiding mechanism is                installed in an embedded manner at a bottom end of                the sodium peroxide guiding mechanism and is                electrically connected. The hybrid strong oxidizing                mechanism is mounted on the inner side of the                auxiliary device main body mechanism by welding. A                gas powder electrostatic separation mechanism is                disposed to the right of the mixed strong oxidation                mechanism.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic representation                of the horizontal rotary food fermentation                device.

PN JP2021154236-A
TI Carbon dioxide absorbing liquid used in biogas treatment method, comprises carbon dioxide chemically absorbable amine having nitrogen-hydrogen bond, and oxygen atom or nitrogen atom via hydrocarbon group having carbon atoms, and non-aqueous carbon dioxide absorber having tertiary polydentate amine.
AU KANAKUBO M
   MAKINO T
   KONO Y
   SHISHIDA K
   FUJIHIRA H
   FUJIKAWA S
AE NAT INST ADVANCED IND SCI & TECHNOLOGY (NIIT-C)
   TAKUMA KK (TAKU-C)
GA 2021B4961G
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide absorbing liquid comprises a carbon dioxide chemically absorbable amine having a nitrogen-hydrogen bond, and an oxygen atom and/or a nitrogen atom via a hydrocarbon group having two or more carbon atoms in the main chain, and a non-aqueous carbon dioxide absorber containing a tertiary polydentate amine having a total of two or more hydrogen bond accepting oxygen atoms and nitrogen atoms, where the carbon dioxide chemically absorbable amine having a nitrogen-hydrogen bond is a secondary amine having a hydrocarbon group having an ether group and no hydroxyl group.
   USE - The carbon dioxide absorbing liquid is useful for biogas treatment method for concentrating methane gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The absorbing liquid efficiently performs absorption and emission under narrow temperature condition, and performs carbon dioxide separation and recovery with energy saving.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a carbon dioxide separation and recovery method, involving contacting the above-mentioned carbon dioxide absorbing liquid with a mixed gas containing carbon dioxide at 10-40 degrees C, absorbing carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide absorbing liquid to selectively separate the carbon dioxide from the mixed gas, and heating the carbon dioxide absorbing liquid that has absorbed the carbon dioxide to a temperature higher than the absorption temperature to dissipate and recover the absorbed carbon dioxide, and regenerating the carbon dioxide absorbing liquid or a carbon dioxide separation and recovery method, involving contacting the carbon dioxide absorbing carbon dioxide by the carbon dioxide absorbing liquid to selectively separate carbon dioxide from the mixed gas, heating the carbon dioxide absorbing liquid that has absorbed the carbon dioxide at 60-100 degrees C to dissipate and recover the absorbed carbon dioxide, and regenerating the carbon dioxide absorbing liquid; and
   (2) a biogas treatment method for concentrating methane gas in a biogas, involving using the above-mentioned carbon dioxide separation and recovery method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide chemically absorbable amine comprises bis(2-methoxyethyl) amine, bis(2-ethoxyethyl) amine, and/or bis(2-isopropoxyethyl) amine. The tertiary polydentate amine is a tertiary polydentate amine in which two hydrocarbon groups having a hydroxyl group and one hydrocarbon group having no hydroxyl group are bonded to one nitrogen atom, where the tertiary polydentate amine comprises N-methyldiethanolamine, N-ethyldiethanolamine, and/or N-butyldiethanolamine. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the carbon dioxide chemically absorbing amine to the tertiary amine is the ration of carbon dioxide chemically absorbing amine to the carbon dioxide chemically absorbing amine and the tertiary amine solvent is 1/100-50/100.
   EXAMPLE - The example does not provide specific component of the liquid, so they do not support the claims.

TI Methanation of molecular hydrogen and gas chosen            from carbon dioxide or carbon monoxide for producing            biomethane, involves continuously feeding anaerobic            bioreactor with liquid substrate, continuously            filtering reaction medium, injecting gas, hydrogen or            synthesis gas into medium, and recovering.

GA 2021D6908F
AB    NOVELTY - Methanation of molecular hydrogen and gas (7)                chosen from carbon dioxide or carbon monoxide                involves (a) continuously feeding a anaerobic                bioreactor (1) with a liquid substrate, (b)                continuously filtering a reaction medium (2)                through a filtration membrane (4) in order to                retain the active microbial flora and the large                size macromolecules, and discharging a digestate,                (c) injecting at least a gas (g1), hydrogen or                synthesis gas into the medium and, optionally, a                gas (g2), carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide or                biogas, injecting at least one gas into the medium                at a pressure (P1) more than the limit pressure                (PI) for formation of bubbles of the at least one                gas in a liquid, and (d) recovering biomethane or a                biogas rich in methane, produced within the                anaerobic bioreactor (1). The anaerobic bioreactor                comprises a reaction medium (2), containing liquid                substrate with organic material to be degraded, and                a microbial flora, comprising at least                hydrogenotrophic methanogenic microorganisms.
   USE - Methanation of molecular hydrogen and gas                chosen from carbon dioxide or carbon monoxide for                producing biomethane used as automotive fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables methanation of molecular                hydrogen and gas chosen from carbon dioxide or                carbon monoxide with improved the valuation of the                syngas by limiting the investments in purification                and pooling the gas injection stations, and capable                of catalyzing the transformation of hydrogen and                carbon dioxide or of hydrogen and carbon monoxide                into CHU.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                installation, which consists of the anaerobic                bioreactor comprising a reaction medium, containing                at least one liquid substrate with organic material                to be degraded, and a microbial flora comprising at                least hydrogenotrophic methanogenic microorganisms,                and containing feeding unit for supplying the                anaerobic bioreactor with liquid substrate, unit                for extracting biogas rich in methane produced, and                a filtration module (40) of the reaction medium                through a filtration membrane containing module,                the filtration membrane has a cut-off threshold of                0.01-0.2 mu m, capable of retaining a portion of                the reaction medium containing the microbial flora                and macromolecules having a size more than the                cut-off threshold, and allowing a digestate to                pass, the filtration membrane is connected to unit                for evacuating the digestate, and further                containing injection unit (i), within the reaction                medium (2), of at least the gas (g1) comprising                hydrogen or syngas, and unit for injecting the gas                (g2) chosen from carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide                and biogas, the injection unit comprises a membrane                contactor comprising hydrophobic microporous                membrane, on the other hand, unit for controlling                and maintaining the injection pressure (P1) of at                least one gas (g1) within the reaction medium at                the pressure more than the limiting pressure (PI)                of formation bubbles of gas injected into a                liquid.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view                illustrating methanation process.
   Anaerobic reactor (1)
   Reaction medium (2)
   Filtration medium (4)
   Gas (7)
   Filtration module (40)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Process: In the step (b),                  filtering the reaction medium. In the step (c),                  injecting at least one gas into the reaction                  medium by a recirculation loop external to the                  anaerobic bioreactor during step (c) at a                  pressure (P1) of at least 10 mbar, preferably                  10-500 mbar than the limit pressure (PI) for the                  formation of gas bubbles in a liquid. In the step                  (c), injecting the gas comprising hydrogen gas,                  and the gas (g2) comprising biogas, and at least                  4 moles of hydrogen gas for one mole of hydrogen                  and carbon dioxide, and injecting the gas (g1)                  comprising syngas. In the step (a), continuously                  supplying the anaerobic bioreactor with a liquid                  substrate comprising a methanizer effluent, an                  industrial effluent, an agricultural effluent,                  and/or an effluent from the purification of water                  from a purification station.

TI Electrode of chemical cell comprises substrate having surface, array of conductive projections supported by substrate and extending outward from surface of substrate.
AU MI Z
   ZHOU B
   SONG J
   OU P
AE UNIV MICHIGAN (UNMI-C)
GA 202162816R
AB    NOVELTY - Electrode of a chemical cell comprises substrate having a surface, an array of conductive projections supported by the substrate and extending outward from the surface of the substrate, each conductive projection of the array of conductive projections having a semiconductor composition for reduction of carbon dioxide (CO2) in the chemical cell and a catalyst arrangement disposed along each conductive projection of the array of conductive projections. The catalyst arrangement comprises a copper-based catalyst and an iron-based catalyst for the reduction of carbon dioxide (CO2) in the chemical cell.
   USE - Electrode of a chemical cell used in electrochemical system, or photoelectrochemical system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - An electrode of a chemical cell is simultaneously converting renewable solar energy into storable, value-added fuels and other chemicals. The photoelectrochemical (PEC) route to CO2 reduction combines light harvesting photovoltaic and electrochemical components into a monolithically integrated device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for following: an electrochemical system, which comprises a working electrode configured in accordance with the electrode, and further comprises a counter electrode an electrolyte in which the working and counter electrodes are immersed and a voltage source that applies a bias voltage between the working and counter electrodes, where bias voltage establishes a preference for the reduction of carbon dioxide (CO2) at the working electrode toward methane;a photocathode for a photoelectrochemical cell, which comprises a substrate that includes a light absorbing material, the light absorbing material being configured to generate charge carriers upon solar illumination, an array of conductive projections supported by the substrate, each conductive projection of the array of conductive projections being configured to extract the charge carriers from the substrate, multiple catalyst particles disposed across each conductive projection of the array of conductive projections, each catalyst particle of the multiple catalyst particles comprising copper, and a distribution of an iron-based catalyst disposed adjacent to the multiple catalyst particles in a co-catalyst arrangement with the multiple catalyst particles for the reduction of carbon dioxide (CO2) in the chemical cell;a photoelectrochemical system, which comprises a working photocathode configured in accordance with the photocathode, and further comprises a counter electrode, an electrolyte in which the working photocathode and the counter electrode are immersed and a voltage source that applies a bias voltage between the working photocathode and the counter electrode, where bias voltage establishes a preference for the reduction of carbon dioxide (CO2) at the working electrode toward methane;a method for fabricating an electrode of an electrochemical system, which involves growing an array of conductive projections on a semiconductor substrate, each conductive projection of the array of conductive projections having a semiconductor composition for reduction of carbon dioxide (CO2) in the electrochemical system and depositing a catalyst arrangement along each conductive projection of the array of conductive projections, and catalyst arrangement comprises a copper-based catalyst and an iron-based catalyst for the reduction of carbon dioxide (CO2) in the chemical cell.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The copper-based catalyst comprises multiple copper nanoparticles. An iron-based catalyst comprises a distribution of iron oxide disposed in a co-catalyst arrangement with the copper-based catalyst. The copper-based catalyst is provided between the iron-based catalyst and the conductive projection. The copper-based catalyst and the iron-based catalyst are linked by a metallic bond. The substrate comprises a semiconductor material and the semiconductor material is configured to generate charge carriers upon absorption of solar radiation such that the chemical cell is configured as a photoelectrochemical system. An array of conductive projections are configured to extract the charge carriers generated in the substrate. The conductive projection of the array of conductive projections comprises a respective nanowire. The conductive projection of the array of conductive projections comprises a Group III-V semiconductor material. The structure is planar. The semiconductor composition of the array of conductive projections establishes a Schottky junction with the catalyst arrangement. The catalyst arrangement has an iron-to-copper ratio of 6.3-1. The copper-based catalyst may be partially oxidized. The chemical cell is a thermochemical cell.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AB    NOVELTY - Group processing for improving the uniformity                of tobacco leaf structure, comprises (i) sieving                the raw materials of tobacco sheets into large,                medium, small and small pieces according to                effective diameter of large, medium, small and                broken pieces D1, D2, D3 and D4, respectively, and                D1 is not greater than 42 mm, D2 is 6.35-42 mm, D3                is 2-6.35 mm and D4 is not less than 2; and (ii)                cutting width of the large to 0.9 plus minus 0.1                mm, mixing the leaves obtained from the large                shreds, carbon dioxide expansion and drying,                cutting width of the medium to 1 plus minus 0.1 mm,                and mixing the leaf obtained by cutting middle                piece, and then roll-drying, where the condition of                carbon dioxide expansion and drying is: carbon                dioxide immersion time is 40-80 seconds, dry hot                air temperature is 180-260 degrees C, drying time                is 5-8 seconds, the water content of the discharge                is controlled at 12.5-13.5%, the condition of drum                drying is: the wall temperature is 120-150 degrees                C, the drying time is 4-6 minutes and discharging                water content controlled at 12-13%.
   USE - The method is useful for group processing for                improving uniformity of tobacco leaf                structure.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can ensure the leaf silk breaking                rate does not increase after drying, reduces the                filament rate of the leaf after drying, improves                the structural uniformity and filling value of the                leaf.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Group processing for improving the uniformity                of tobacco leaf structure, comprises (i) sieving                the raw materials of tobacco sheets into large,                medium, small and small pieces according to                effective diameter of large, medium, small and                broken pieces D1, D2, D3 and D4, respectively, and                D1 is not greater than 42 mm, D2 is 6.35-42 mm, D3                is 2-6.35 mm and D4 is not less than 2; and (ii)                cutting width of the large to 0.9 plus minus 0.1                mm, where the chips are not shredded, mixing the                leaves obtained from the large shreds, carbon                dioxide expansion and drying, cutting width of the                medium to 1 plus minus 0.1 mm, where the small                piece is not shredded and mixing the leaf obtained                by cutting the middle piece, and then roll-drying,                where the condition of carbon dioxide expansion and                drying is: carbon dioxide immersion time is 40-80                seconds, dry hot air temperature is 180-260 degrees                C, drying time is 5-8 seconds, the water content of                the discharge is controlled at 12.5-13.5%, the                condition of drum drying is: the wall temperature                is 120-150 degrees C, the drying time is 4-6                minutes and discharging water content controlled at                12-13%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method comprises                  before the sieving of the tobacco sheet of the                  step (i), respiratory respirators are used, raw                  materials of the flakes are treated by                  traditional vacuum resurgence and loose                  resurgence, water content of the flakes is 18-21%                  and flake raw materials after resurgence are                  added according to the formula design                  requirements and discharge moisture content is                  18.5-22%. The leaf strands treated in the step                  (ii) are mixed to form a full-form cut                  tobacco.

TI Preparing liquid fuels and chemicals comprises            e.g. catalytically hydrogenating carbon monoxide in            hydrogenation reactor, condensing effluent, feeding            non-condensable component into oligomerization reactor            and condensing discharged effluent.
AU HERSKOWITZ M
   HOS T
AE UNIV BEN-GURION BG NEGEV TECHNOLOGIES (UYNE-C)
   BG NEGEV TECHNOLOGIES & APPL LTD (UYNE-C)
   UNIV BEN-GURION BG NEGEV TECHNOLOGIES (UYNE-C)
   UNIV BEN-GURION BG NEGEV TECHNOLOGIES (UYNE-C)

GA 2018232271
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing (P1) liquid fuels and chemicals,                comprises feeding carbon monoxide and hydrogen to                hydrogenation reactor, catalytically hydrogenating                carbon monoxide, condensing effluent to recover                organic liquid(s) and aqueous solution, feeding                non-condensable component of effluent into                oligomerization reactor, condensing effluent                discharged from oligomerization reactor to give                additional organic liquid and gaseous stream,                separating the organic liquid, and either                combusting the gaseous stream to give heat and                electricity, or processing same to give recyclable                gaseous streams utilizable in process.
   USE - The processes are useful for preparing liquid                fuels and chemicals (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The processes are novel and highly efficient                eco-friendly processes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing (P1) liquid fuels and chemicals,                comprises: feeding carbon monoxide and hydrogen to                a hydrogenation reactor, where the molar ratio                carbon monoxide:hydrogen is of 1:0.5-1:0.9,                catalytically hydrogenating the carbon monoxide in                the hydrogenation reactor, condensing the effluent                of the hydrogenation reactor to recover at least                one organic liquid(s) and an aqueous solution,                feeding a non-condensable component of the effluent                into an oligomerization reactor; and condensing an                effluent discharged from the oligomerization                reactor to obtain an additional organic liquid and                an additional gaseous stream, separating the                additional organic liquid, and either combusting                the additional gaseous stream to produce heat and                electricity, or processing same to obtain                recyclable gaseous streams utilizable in the                process. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing (P2) liquid fuels and chemicals,                comprising dry reforming of natural gas with carbon                dioxide or co-electrolyzing carbon dioxide with                steam to yield hydrogen-lean syngas (0.5-1.5),                optionally separating the excess hydrogen from the                syngas to set it at the optimal value (0.5-0.9),                thus generating a hydrogen stream, adjusting the                feed to the carbon monoxide hydrogenation reactor                by mixing the syngas with recycled carbon monoxide                to set it at its optimal value (0.5-0.9),                converting the hydrogen-lean syngas in the presence                of potassium-promoted (Fe2+( (Fe3+)y(Al3+)1-y)2O4),                silica-containing pellets, to yield higher                hydrocarbons, separating the organic liquid and the                water products by cooling in two stages to 40-70                degrees C, feeding the obtained gaseous product to                the oligomerization catalytic reactor, separating                the organic liquid product by cooling the product                to 40 degrees C, separating the carbon monoxide                from the gaseous product by pressure swing                adsorption or by a membrane, thus generating a                recyclable carbon monoxide stream and carbon                dioxide-rich gas mixture, splitting the carbon                dioxide-rich gas mixture into two subsidiary                streams, feeding one of the subsidiary carbon                dioxide-rich streams to the dry reformer or to the                co-electrolysis unit, feeding the other carbon                dioxide-rich stream and hydrogen to the reverse                water gas shift (RWGS) reactor, and converting                carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide over a catalyst                and feeding it back to the carbon monoxide                separator to supply carbon monoxide for                hydrogenation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The molar ratio carbon                  monoxide:hydrogen is of 1:0.6-1:0.8. The                  catalytic hydrogenation takes place over                  potassium-promoted (Fe2+( (Fe3+)y(Al3+)1-y)2O4),                  silica-containing extrudates. The process                  comprises condensing the effluent of the                  hydrogenation reactor at a first temperature to                  obtain a first organic liquid and a first gaseous                  stream, separating the first organic liquid,                  condensing the first gaseous stream at a second                  temperature to obtain a second liquid consisting                  of an organic phase and an aqueous phase, and a                  second gaseous stream, separating the second                  liquid from the second gaseous stream and                  separating the second liquid into a second                  organic liquid and an aqueous solution. The                  process further comprises: combining the organic                  liquids which are generated separately in the                  carbon monoxide hydrogenation reaction and the                  oligomerization reaction, and hydro-treating the                  combined organic liquid to form fuel materials;                  supplying hydrogen to a reactor where the                  hydro-treatment of the combined organic liquid                  takes place, where the hydrogen being a                  subsidiary hydrogen stream which is separated                  from the carbon monoxide and hydrogen syngas                  mixture prior to feeding to the carbon monoxide                  hydrogenation reaction; and converting into                  olefins oxygenates dissolved in the aqueous                  solution recovered from the hydrogenation                  reaction. The carbon monoxide and hydrogen                  feedstock is supplied by dry reforming carbon                  dioxide with natural gas or co-electrolysis of                  carbon dioxide and steam, where the process                  further comprises splitting the additional                  gaseous component generated in the                  oligomerization reaction into a carbon monoxide                  stream and carbon monoxide- depleted, carbon                  dioxide-rich stream, recycling the carbon                  monoxide stream to the hydrogenation reactor,                  dividing the carbon monoxide-depleted, carbon                  dioxide-rich stream into two subsidiary streams,                  where one subsidiary carbon dioxide-containing                  stream is used to supply carbon dioxide to the                  dry reforming reaction or the co-electrolysis,                  and the other carbon dioxide-containing stream is                  reacted with hydrogen in RWGS reactor to produce                  carbon monoxide and water, following which the                  effluent of the RWGS reactor is separated into                  water and carbon monoxide-containing stream which                  is used to supply carbon monoxide to the                  hydrogenation reaction. The process comprises                  producing a mixture of carbon monoxide and                  hydrogen either by means of dry reforming carbon                  dioxide or co-electrolysis of carbon dioxide and                  steam, separating hydrogen in part from the                  mixture, to form syngas feedstock, feeding the                  syngas to a hydrogenation reactor, catalytically                  hydrogenating the carbon monoxide in the                  hydrogenation reactor, condensing the effluent of                  the hydrogenation reactor at a first temperature                  to obtain a first organic liquid and a first                  gaseous stream, separating the first organic                  liquid and collecting same, condensing the first                  gaseous stream at a second temperature to obtain                  a second liquid, which consists of an organic                  phase and an aqueous phase, and a second gaseous                  stream, separating the second liquid into a                  second organic liquid and an aqueous phase,                  feeding the second gaseous stream into an                  oligomerization reactor, condensing the effluent                  discharged from the oligomerization reactor to                  obtain a third organic liquid and a third gaseous                  stream, separating and collecting third organic                  liquid, splitting the third gaseous component                  into a carbon monoxide stream and carbon                  monoxide-depleted, carbon dioxide-rich stream,                  recycling the carbon monoxide stream to                  hydrogenation reactor, dividing the carbon                  monoxide-depleted, carbon dioxide-rich stream                  into two subsidiary streams, where one subsidiary                  carbon dioxide-containing stream is used to                  supply carbon dioxide to the dry reforming                  reaction or the co-electrolysis, and the other                  carbon dioxide-containing stream is reacted with                  hydrogen in RWGS reactor to produce carbon                  monoxide and water, following which the effluent                  of the RWGS reactor is separated into water and                  carbon monoxide -containing stream which is used                  to supply carbon monoxide to the hydrogenation                  reaction, hydro-treating at least one organic                  products collected in the process to form premium                  liquid fuels, and converting oxygenates in the                  aqueous solution into olefins which are fed to                  the oligomerization reactor. The carbon monoxide                  and hydrogen feedstock is supplied from waste                  gases having hydrogen/carbon monoxide molar ratio                  of less than 0.5, where the process comprises                  subjecting the waste gas to water gas shift (WGS)                  reaction to convert part of the carbon monoxide                  so that the hydrogen/carbon monoxide molar ratio                  increases, and separating excess hydrogen in a                  membrane so that the hydrogen/carbon monoxide                  molar ratio of the stream fed to carbon monoxide                  hydrogenation reactor is adjusted to 0.6-0.8, and                  hydrogenating the product of the WGS reaction.                  The carbon monoxide and hydrogen feedstock is                  supplied from waste gases having hydrogen/carbon                  monoxide molar ratio of greater than 0.9, and the                  process comprises subjecting the waste gas to                  RWGS reaction, condensing the product of the RWGS                  reaction to obtain water and non-condensable                  component, separating water from the gaseous                  component and separating hydrogen from it, and                  directing the gaseous stream to the hydrogenation                  reaction.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. TI Heat exchange functions featured carbon dioxide high-pressure stage middle storing gas-liquid separating device, has gas-liquid separator whose upper part is formed with gas outlet and lower part is formed with liquid outlet.
AU JIECAI H
   QIFENG L
   TINGXUN Z
