AE SHANGHAI GEN FUSHI REFRIGERATION EQUIP (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201809955F
AB    (CN206930026-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a CO2 high-pressure stage middle liquid storage and heat exchange function of the gas-liquid separator device, belonging to the field of refrigeration engineering. a reservoir, the reservoir is connected with the evaporator by the expansion valve, the evaporator and the gas-liquid separator is connected with the inlet, the gas-liquid separator is provided with a gas outlet and a liquid outlet, the liquid outlet is connected with CO2 efficient mapper. The CO2 efficient mapper connected to the reservoir, the air outlet is connected with the compressor, the compressor connected with the air cooler, air cooler connected to the reservoir, a gas-liquid separator is provided with a clapboard, the clapboard is provided with a hole, gas inlet and gas outlet is set at the upper part of the gas-liquid separator. liquid outlet is set at the bottom of the gas-liquid separator. A CO2 high-pressure stage middle liquid storage and heat exchange function of the gas-liquid separator device of the utility model, use of internal heat exchanging type gas-liquid separator, reduces the heat exchanger design and use, reduces the cost and simplifies the structure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a high-pressure stage carbon dioxide middle liquid storage and heat exchange function featured gas-liquid separating device.
   Reservoir (1)
   Expansion valve (2)
   Evaporator (3)
   Gas-liquid separator (4)
   High-efficient carbon dioxide mapper (5)
   Compressor (6)
   Gas cooler (7)
   Liquid inlet (41)
   Gas outlet (42)
   Liquid outlet (43)
EA    NOVELTY - The device has a reservoir (1) connected with an evaporator (3) through an expansion valve (2). The evaporator is connected with a liquid inlet (41). A liquid outlet (43) is connected with a high-efficient carbon dioxide mapper (5). The high-efficient carbon dioxide mapper and the reservoir are connected with a gas outlet (42), which is connected with a compressor (6). The compressor is connected with a gas cooler (7) that is connected with the reservoir. A gas-liquid separator (4) is provided with a baffle plate that is provided with a hole. The liquid inlet and the gas outlet are fixed at an upper part of the gas-liquid separator. The liquid outlet is fixed on a bottom end of the gas-liquid separator.
   USE - High-pressure stage carbon dioxide middle liquid storage and heat exchange function featured gas-liquid separating device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device utilizes the gas-liquid separator so as to reduce heat exchanger design and use, thus reducing operation cost. The device is simple in structure.

The eccentricity of the eccentric                pipe is positively related to a heat load of the                eccentric pipe. The eccentric pipe comprises a thin                wall end at a fire side. The fire side of the                eccentric pipe is far away from an inner wall of a                boiler. The wall optimizes the cooling                wall pipe along the hearth circumference                distribution to match with the non-uniform heat                load distribution, thus, improving the heat                exchange capability.

PN CN112090278-A
TI Preparing high-purity urea solution for vehicles comprises e.g. passing high-purity ammonia and carbon dioxide in sequence in first reactor and second reactor under high temperature and high pressure, collecting and compressing gas.
AU LI F
   SUN C
   SANG S
AE HENAN HONGKANG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 2021019961
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-purity urea solution for vehicles comprises (i) passing high-purity ammonia and carbon dioxide in sequence in the first reactor and second reactor under high temperature and high pressure, collecting and compressing the gas in the second reactor, passing through the first reactor, and collecting the liquid urea melt fluid in the first reactor and second reactor, (ii) flash evaporating the liquid urea melt to remove ammonia and carbon dioxide, adding into high-purity water at 75-80 degrees C, and mixing to obtain saturated urea solution, collecting the flash vaporized gas, and passing into the first reactor, (iii) filtering the urea aqueous solution with filter membrane having pore size of 0.2-0.5 mu m, and (iv) adding the urea saturated solution into multi-stage liquid film crystallizing kettle.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high-purity urea solution for vehicles.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves problems of low production efficiency, low utilization rate and low purity in existing production processes, and has high yield and high purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for high-purity vehicle urea solution system, comprising ammonia storage tank and carbon dioxide storage tank, the ammonia storage tank and carbon dioxide storage tank is orderly connected with the first reactor and second reactor, saturated urea solution storage tank, flash evaporation device and cooling device, membrane filtering device, multistage liquid film crystallizing kettle and urea solution storage tank, the first reactor and second reactor is connected with the distilled water storage tank, the multi-level liquid film crystallizing kettle is provided with stirring device and cooling device, and the multi-stage liquid film crystallizing kettle is connected with the filtrate recycling tank.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating the high-purity vehicle urea solution system (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), decomposing the ammonium carbamate in the liquid urea melt under heating, reduced pressure, and flashing conditions. In the method, the pressure reduction is to reduce the pressure to 0.10-14 MPa. In the step (iv), the multi-stage liquid film crystallizing kettle comprises 4 sections of cooling section with gradually reduced temperature, the temperature difference of each cooling section is at least 10 degrees C, the temperature difference between the urea saturated solution and first cold section is 30-50 degrees C. In the step (iv), separately collecting the filtrate from the crystallization kettle, and introducing into the next stage crystallization kettle.

TI Multilayered membrane used for e.g. gas filter, comprises layer comprising sulfone-containing polymers, and layer comprising polyamides comprising aliphatic diamine and aromatic dicarboxylate, and preset amount of carbon nanoparticles.
AU ALJUNDI I H
   AWAD A M
AE UNIV DHAHRAN KING FAHD PETROLEUM & MINER (UYKF-C)
GA 2020590291
AB    NOVELTY - A multilayered membrane comprises a layer (L1) comprising 50 wt. % or more sulfone-containing polymers, and a layer (L2), comprising 50 wt. % or more polyamides comprising an aliphatic diamine and an aromatic dicarboxylate in condensation polymerized form, and 0.005-1 wt.% at least one carbon (CDC) nanoparticles chosen from silicon carbide, iron carbide, tungsten carbide, titanium silicocarbide, zirconium carbide, boron carbide, tantalum carbide, molybdenum carbide and titanium-derived nanoparticles. The layer (L1) directly contacts the layer (L2).
   USE - Multilayered membrane used for gas filter and gas treatment apparatus for separating gas such as carbon dioxide (all claimed) from natural gas and fermentation gases, for petroleum production and refining applications, chemical companies and industrial gas suppliers. Can also be used for separating hydrogen, nitrogen, argon and ammonia in enhanced oil recovery, olefin/paraffin, iso/normal paraffins separations, vapor separations such as deep desulfurization of gasoline and diesel fuels, ethanol/water separations, pervaporation dehydration of aqueous/organic mixtures, and battery separator.
   ADVANTAGE - The multilayered membrane can separate carbon dioxide from mixture of carbon dioxide and methane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) gas separation, which involves passing a gas mixture comprising carbon dioxide and a further gas, through the membrane, thereby forming an permeate that is enriched in carbon dioxide content; and
   (2) manufacture of mixed matrix membrane, which involves combining a sulfone-containing polymer with a polar aprotic solvent, to obtain mixture (M1), mixing an alcohol with the mixture (M1) to obtain a mixture (M2), casting the mixture (M2) onto a surface to create a layer with a thickness of 100-400 mu m, drying the layer to obtain a dried layer, polymerizing composition comprising an aliphatic diamine and an aromatic dicarboxylate and at least one carbon (CDC) nanoparticles to obtain the mixed matrix membrane comprising the CDC-doped polyamide layer on the sulfone-containing polymer layer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The layer (L2) comprises preferably 0.01-0.9 wt.% carbon (CDC) nanoparticles. The carbon (CDC) nanoparticles comprises preferably titanium-derived nanoparticles. The CDC nanoparticles are prepared by (i) heating titanium carbide and chlorine gas at temperature of 600-1000 degrees C for 2-6 hours, replacing the chlorine gas with hydrogen gas at 100 degrees C of the heating in (i), and replacing the hydrogen gas with inert gas and cooling.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The diamine is a cyclic diamine and comprises a piperazine, 4-aminopiperidine, 3-aminopyrrolidine, 1,4-diaminocyclohexane, 1,4-diaminomethylene-cyclohexane, 1,4-diazacycloheptane, 1,5-diazocane, hexahydropyrrolo(3,4-c)pyrrole, hexahydropyrrolo(3,4-b)pyrrole, 3,7-diaza-bicyclo(3.3.1)nonane, 2,5-diazabicyclo(2.2.2)octane, 3,8-diazabicyclo(3.2.1)octane, 2,5-diazabicyclo(2.2.1)heptane, ethylenediamine, 1,3-diaminopropane, 1,4-butanediamine, and/or 1,5-pentanediamine. The diamine comprises 75 wt.% or more unsubstituted piperazine with respect to the total of diamine. The dicarboxylate comprises a 1,3-benzenedicarboxylate (isophthalate), 1,4-benzenedicarboxylate (terephthalate), 1,2-benzenedicarboxylate (phthalate), 2,6-naphthalenedicarboxylate, 2,3-naphthalenedicarboxylate, 1,4-naphthalenedicarboxylate, 1,5-naphthalenedicarboxylate, 1,7-naphthalenedicarboxylate, 1,2,3,4-tetrahydro-1,4-naphthalenedicarboxylate, 2,6-pyridinedicarboxylate (dipicolinic acid), 2,5-pyridinedicarboxylate (isocinchomeronic acid), and/or 1H-pyrrole-2,4-dicarboxylate. The dicarboxylate comprises 75 wt.% or more 1,3-benzenedicarboxylate does not having further substituents with respect to the total dicarboxylate. The further gas is methane. The polar aprotic solvent comprises dimethyl formamide, dimethyl acetamide, N-methylpyrrolidone, tetrahydrofuran, dimethyl sulfoxide, ethyl acetate, 1,4-dioxane, nitromethane, dichloromethane, chloroform, acetonitrile, and/or acetone. The mixture (M3) comprises from 0.5-5.0 (weight/volume)% piperazine and water. The mixture (M4) comprises 0.05-0.5 (weight/volume)% isophthaloyl chloride or isophthaloyl anhydride and hexane and 0.001-1 wt.% CDC nanoparticles.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The membrane further comprises polyamide-comprising layer comprising the CDC nanoparticles. The sulfone-containing polymer comprises structural unit of formula (a)-(e), preferably of formula (A)-(E), preferably polyether sulfone having molecular weight of 10000-50000.Ar', Ar'', Ar''' = aryl residues;Y', Z' = aliphatic, cycloaliphatic, aryl or heterocyclyl residues;andn = 100-100000.Preferred Process: The manufacture of membrane involves polymerizing by contacting the dried layer with a mixture (M3) comprising the diamine in water, to obtain amine-treated layer; and agitating the amine-treated layer with a mixture (M4) comprising the aromatic dicarboxylate and the CDC nanoparticles in an organic solvent and repeating the polymerization.

A compressor compresses biogas from which hydrogen sulfide and siloxane have been removed. A purification unit supplies compressed biogas to remove carbon dioxide. The purification unit has a linear structure having a relatively larger diameter than the inlet and the outlet, so that the internal pressure rises. A polyimide hollow fiber membrane is filled inside the structure. The system is capable of producing high-quality alcohol by supplying to an alcohol fermentation tank in a state in which the organic acid, which is a raw material for alcohol generation, is separated through an electrodialysis device. After methane fermentation in a biogas fermentation tank, hydrogen sulfide, siloxane, and carbon dioxide are removed through a biogas purification unit to produce high-purity biogas.

PT P
PN RU2665794-C1
TI Method and plant for mechanical and thermal energy generation, in which hot gases from combustion chamber are directed to an inlet to steam gas turbine under pressure necessary to supply combustion chamber.
AU KOSOI A S
   KOSOI A A
   SINKEVICH M V
   ANTIPOV YU A
AE AS RUSSIA HIGH TEMPERATURES JOINT INST (ASHI-Non-standard)
GA 2018819329
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to heat engineering. Method for generating mechanical and thermal energy includes the steps in which hot gases from the combustion chamber are directed to an inlet to a steam-gas turbine, the pressure in the combustion chamber being at least 7.5 MPa. Gases spent in the steam-gas turbine at a pressure of 0.2-0.9 MPa enter the first cooler of the exhaust gases. Exhaust gases from the first coolant are fed to a first contact cooler where they are cooled to the temperature necessary to separate water from the exhaust gases by condensing it, then the condensed water is removed from the first contact cooler. Exhaust gases from the first contact coolant, containing carbon dioxide as the main constituent, are sent to the compressor inlet, which compresses the gases to a pressure of at least 3.5 MPa. Compressed exhaust gas is supplied to the second contact cooler, where they are cooled. From the second contact cooler the cooled exhaust gases enter the second cooler, where the exhaust gases are cooled to the temperature required to condense the carbon dioxide, further, the condensed carbon dioxide is removed from the second coolant. Some of the water withdrawn from the first contact cooler enters the inlet of the water pump-regulator, which pumps it into the combustion chamber. Some of the carbon dioxide condensed in the second cooler enters the carbon dioxide pump-regulator inlet, which pumps it into the combustion chamber. Carbon fuel and oxygen, respectively, are fed into the combustion chamber by a fuel pump-regulator and an oxygen pump-regulator, under the pressure necessary to supply the combustion chamber. Also, an installation for generating mechanical and thermal energy is disclosed.
   USE - Energy.
   ADVANTAGE - Invention makes it possible to increase the efficiency of the installation.
21 cl, 1 dwg
DC X11 (Power Generation and High Power Machines)
MC X11-A09

PT P
PN WO2018111719-A1; TW201840475-A; EP3554675-A1; US2019390901-A1; EP3554675-A4
TI Method of producing ultra high purity carbon            monoxide, involves cooling carbon monoxide stream in            reversing heat exchanger for freezing out removing            carbon dioxide, liquefying carbon monoxide and            separating hydrogen remains in gaseous state.
AU MILNER D
AE LINDE AG (LINM-C)
   MILNER D (MILN-Individual)
   LINDE AG (LINM-C)
   LINDE AG (LINM-C)
   LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
GA 201849457X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves cooling the carbon                monoxide stream in a reversing heat exchanger for                freezing out removing carbon dioxide. The gas                stream is afterwards directed to a snow trap for                removing solid particles. The carbon monoxide is                liquefied and hydrogen remains in a gaseous state                and is separated. The cooling agent in the                reversing heat exchanger, in the snow trap and in                the liquefier and hydrogen removal vessel liquid                nitrogen is used. The liquid ultra high purity                carbon monoxide is compressed by a diaphragm                compressor or a cryogenic liquid pump and is stored                in gas cylinders. The content of carbon dioxide and                hydrogen in the ultra high purity carbon monoxide                is 0.1 ppm or less.
   USE - Method of producing ultra high purity carbon                monoxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The production of ultra high purity carbon                monoxide having a carbon dioxide content of 0.1 ppm                or less is achieved by treating the carbon monoxide                stream from the primary carbon monoxide production                process using a reversing heat exchanger to freeze                the carbon dioxide out of the source stream. The                cost-effective method for purification of carbon                monoxide to obtain ultra high purity carbon                monoxide needed by the electronics industry is                achieved. The production of ultra high purity                carbon monoxide is less expensive both in capital                and operation costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                apparatus for producing ultra high purity carbon                monoxide, has a heat exchanger, which is connected                to a snow trap, which is also connected to a                liquefier and hydrogen removal vessel. The heat                exchanger, the snow trap, the liquefier and                hydrogen removal vessel and the respective                connecting lines are placed in vacuum insulated                cold box. The liquefier and hydrogen removal vessel                contains inserts. The purified carbon monoxide                stream is compressed by a diaphragm compressor or a                cryogenic liquid pump.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                process system for the production of ultra high                purity carbon monoxide.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

GA 2018178229
AB    NOVELTY - Recovery of hydrogen involves performing                primary purification process for purifying the                pyrolysis gas (b) by removing gas (e) containing                carbon dioxide as main component from the pyrolysis                gas under pressurization by absorption, carrying                out secondary purification process for purifying                the purified gas (c) obtained by the primary                purification process for recovering a gas (d)                containing hydrogen as main component, and                supplying the gas containing hydrogen to a                container filled with a hydrogen storage                alloy.
   USE - Recovery of hydrogen used as fuel of fuel cell                for motor vehicle, backup power supply, wireless                machine, mobile telephone, unmanned aircraft, and                domestic-use thermoelectric supply system (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient recovery of                hydrogen with high recovery rate and reduced                electric power consumption at low operation                pressure, without using a special apparatus or                substance such as adsorption agent and separation                membrane, by economical and                environmentally-friendly process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recovery of hydrogen involves recovering                hydrogen from pyrolysis gas obtained by                heat-processing biomass (a) by performing primary                purification process for purifying the pyrolysis                gas by removing gas containing carbon dioxide as                main component from the pyrolysis gas under                pressurization by absorption, carrying out                secondary purification process for purifying the                purified gas obtained by the primary purification                process at a pressure lower than the pressure in                the primary purification process by removing gas                containing carbon dioxide as main component from                the purified gas under pressurization by absorption                for recovering a gas containing hydrogen as main                component, and performing hydrogen storage process                by supplying the gas containing hydrogen as main                component recovered by the secondary purification                process to a container filled with a hydrogen                storage alloy and storing high-purity hydrogen in                the container.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the flowchart for recovery                of hydrogen.
   Biomass (a)
   Pyrolysis gas (b)
   Purified gas (c)
   Gas containing hydrogen (d)
   Gas containing carbon dioxide (e)

PT P
PN WO2018002802-A1; US2019330059-A1
TI Supported cobalt catalyst used for carbon dioxide reforming of lower alkanes to synthesis gas, contains cobalt catalyst, and is modified with boron precursor comprising sodium borohydride, ammonium borohydride and/or boric acid.
AU TAKANABE K
   BASSET J
   PARK J
   SAMAL A
   ALSABBAN B
AE UNIV KING ABDULLAH SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIK-C)
   UNIV KING ABDULLAH SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIK-C)
GA 201801202S
AB    NOVELTY - A supported cobalt catalyst comprises 0.4-20 wt.% cobalt catalyst, based on the weight of the catalyst, and an oxide support. The oxide support comprises zirconium oxide, cerium oxide, aluminum oxide, magnesium oxide, hydrotalcite and/or titanium oxide. The supported cobalt catalyst is modified with a boron precursor comprising sodium borohydride, ammonium borohydride and/or boric acid. The supported cobalt catalyst comprises 0.001-1.0 wt.% boron, based on the weight of the catalyst.
   USE - Supported cobalt catalyst used for carbon dioxide reforming of lower alkanes e.g. methane, to synthesis gas (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst is economical, has excellent stability, shelf life and resistance to carbon deposition, and effectively converts methane and carbon dioxide to synthesis gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) carbon dioxide reforming or steam reforming of lower alkanes to synthesis gas, which involves contacting a feed stream comprising gaseous lower alkane and carbon dioxide, with the supported cobalt catalyst at a temperature of 600-950 degrees C and a pressure of 1-30 bar to produce a synthesis gas comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide; and
   (2) preparation of supported cobalt catalyst, which involves providing a support comprising an oxide, impregnating the oxide support with a solution comprising a cobalt salt, drying the cobalt impregnated oxide support at 60-150 degrees C to form a supported cobalt catalyst, treating the supported cobalt catalyst with the boron precursor and stirring at 20-90 degrees C for 1-3 hours to form a boron-treated supported cobalt catalyst, and drying the boron-treated supported cobalt catalyst at 60-150 degrees C.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view explaining the preparation of supported cobalt catalyst.
   Addition of cobalt chloride hexahydrate, 6-aminohexanoic acid and zirconium dioxide (201)
   Heat treatment of mixture (203)
   Addition of sodium borohydride dissolved in deionized water in mixture (204)
   Mixture stirring process (205)
   Product filtering and washing process (206)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxide support preferably comprises zirconium oxide and/or cerium oxide. The solution is an aqueous solution of cobalt nitrate hexahydrate and/or cobalt(II) chloride hexahydrate. The supported cobalt catalyst further comprises cobalt(II) oxide/zirconium dioxide, cobalt(II) oxide/cerium(IV) oxide and/or cobalt(II) oxide/cerium(IV) oxide-zirconium dioxide. Preferred Composition: The catalyst comprises 0.4-20 wt.% cobalt and 0.001-1.0 wt.% boron, based on the weight of the catalyst. Preferred Process: The carbon dioxide reforming or steam reforming of lower alkanes to synthesis gas involves reducing the catalyst by heating the catalyst under argon atmosphere comprising 4-10% hydrogen at 600-900 degrees C for 30 minutes-5 hours, before contacting the feed stream with the catalyst. The production of supported cobalt catalyst further involves calcining the cobalt impregnated oxide support at 450-1000 degrees C, before treating the supported cobalt catalyst with the boron precursor. The carbon dioxide reforming of lower alkanes to synthesis gas further involves collecting the synthesis gas.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The solution further comprises a surface capping agent with the cobalt salt. The surface capping agent comprises 6-aminohexanoic acid and/or cetyltrimethylammonium bromide.

PT P
PN US2022274061-A1; CN115044921-A; JP2022131811-A
TI Carbon dioxide treatment apparatus for producing            carbon compounds, involves capturing device that            captures carbon dioxide, and electrochemical reaction            device that electrochemically reduces carbon dioxide,            where capturing device includes absorption unit.
AU OIKAWA H
   HIROSHI O
AE HONDA MOTOR CO LTD (HOND-C)
   HONDA MOTOR CO LTD (HOND-C)
GA 2022B1674C
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide treatment apparatus (100)                comprises a capturing device (1) that captures                carbon dioxide, and an electrochemical reaction                device (2) that electrochemically reduces carbon                dioxide. The capturing device includes an                absorption unit that brings an electrolytic                solution composed of a strong alkaline aqueous                solution and carbon dioxide gas into contact with                each other to dissolve carbon dioxide in the                electrolytic solution and absorb the carbon                dioxide. The electrochemical reaction device                includes a cathode, an anode, and a liquid flow                path provided between the cathode and the anode                through which the electrolytic solution that has                absorbed carbon dioxide in the absorption unit                flows, and reduces the dissolved carbon dioxide in                the electrolytic solution at the cathode.
   USE - Carbon dioxide treatment apparatus for                producing carbon compounds.
   ADVANTAGE - The energy efficiency of carbon dioxide                capture and reduction is high, and the carbon                dioxide loss is also reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for: a. a                carbon dioxide treatment method, which involves: i.                bringing carbon dioxide gas into contact with an                electrolytic solution composed of a strong alkaline                aqueous solution to dissolve carbon dioxide in the                electrolytic solution and absorb the carbon                dioxide; and ii. supplying the electrolytic                solution between the cathode and the anode and                electrochemically reducing dissolved carbon dioxide                in the electrolytic solution to generate carbon                compounds and hydrogen; and b. a method of                producing carbon compounds for producing carbon                compounds in which carbon dioxide is reduced by                using the carbon dioxide treatment method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a carbon                dioxide treatment apparatus.
   1Capturing device
   2Electrochemical reaction device
   4Homologation reaction device
   5Heat exchanger
   11Concentration unit
   100Carbon dioxide treatment apparatus

Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

PT P
PN IN202241050337-A
TI System for utilizing hydrophobic nanoparticles to            enhance sono-assisted carbon-rich solvent regeneration            in controlled temperature environment, comprises            reactor, ultrasonic tank and high frequency generator,            where chiller inlets and outlets are connected to water            circulation chamber.
AU BALRAJ A
   RAMAMURTHY N
   BABARAO R
   KUMAR M S
   KANNAIYAN S K
AE SRI SIVASUBRAMANIYA NADAR COLLEGE ENG (SRIS-Non-standard)
GA 2022C3545X
AB    NOVELTY - System has a reactor, an ultrasonic tank (105)                and a high frequency generator (101). A carbon-rich                aqueous monoethanolamine is taken and the desired                concentration of the ferric oxide nanoparticles is                dispersed into the solution. The mixture is loaded                into a jacketed glass reactor. A chiller (107)                inlet and outlets are connected to the water                circulation chamber. The sono-assisted carbon                dioxide stripping is carried out. The temperature                of the reaction mixture, water circulation chamber                and the ultrasonic tank water bath are measured.                During regular intervals of sonication, the                carbon-lean solvent (106) is collected, and                filtered. The lowest concentration of nanoparticles                shows maximum enhancement. The carbon dioxide                stripping rate is much higher for with ferric oxide                nanoparticles case. The hydrophobic ferric oxide                nanoparticles in sono-assisted carbon dioxide                stripping/solvent regeneration reduce the solvent                regeneration energy.
   USE - The system is used for utilizing hydrophobic                nanoparticles to enhance sono-assisted carbon-rich                solvent regeneration in controlled temperature                environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The system provides uniform distribution of                the sonication effect hence energy is distributed                throughout the system which leads to the better                performance and attribute to the scalable                potential, diminishes the sonication effect due to                the particle agglomeration and suppresses the                mechanisms of bubble formation, coalescing,                breaking and the shuttle effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The system of utilizing hydrophobic                nanoparticles to enhance the sono-assisted                carbon-rich solvent regeneration in a controlled                temperature environment at 12&#176; C comprises                a reactor, an ultrasonic tank (105) and a high                frequency generator (101), whereas the experiments                are carried out in the reactor for 60 minutes. A                known quantity of carbon-rich 30 wt.% aqueous                monoethanolamine is taken and the desired                concentration of the ferric oxide nanoparticles is                dispersed into the solution. The mixture is loaded                into a jacketed glass reactor, which has the                provision for chill water circulation. A chiller                inlet (104) and outlet (103) are connected to the                water circulation chamber to maintain the desired                regeneration temperature and the whole arrangements                are placed in a sonication bath. The sono-assisted                carbon dioxide stripping is carried out at                12&#176; C and the conditions are regulated by                the chiller (107). During the experiment, the                temperature of the reaction mixture, water                circulation chamber and the ultrasonic tank water                bath are measured using a k-type thermocouple.                During regular intervals of 5, 15, and 60 minutes                of sonication, the carbon-lean solvent (106) is                collected, filtered and the filtrate is subjected                to the carbon loading measurement using Chittick                apparatus. 5 Different nanoparticles concentrations                (0.005, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1 and 0.2 wt.%) are tested,                among five 0.005, 0.01 and 0.05 wt.% of                nanoparticles shows enhancement in carbon dioxide                stripping. Among three, the lowest concentration                (0.005 wt.%) of nanoparticles shows maximum                enhancement of 12.29, 20.93 and 33.62% for the                tested frequencies of 360kHz, 470kHz and 1MHz                respectively. Carbon dioxide stripping rate is much                higher for with ferric oxide nanoparticles case at                lowest concentration than the without                nanoparticles. The hydrophobic ferric oxide                nanoparticles in sono-assisted carbon dioxide                stripping/solvent regeneration reduce the solvent                regeneration energy by 1.26, 2.4 and 2.9 times                lesser than the without nanoparticles case for the                tested frequencies.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                sono-assisted carbon dioxide stripping/solvent                regeneration with hydrophobic nano particle.

AE UNIV HENAN (UYEH-C)
GA 202286146F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing clean syngas by chemical chain                gasification of biomass comprises performing                pyrolysis and gasification reactions on biomass                under heating and oxidation of oxygen carriers in                oxidized state from oxygen carrier material bin in                moving bed fuel reactor, generating gaseous                products, semi-coke and reduced oxygen carriers,                entering gaseous product into moving bed reforming                reactor, heating tar and carbon dioxide in gaseous                product, catalyzing by catalyst from the                regenerator to undergo tar carbon dioxide reforming                reaction, using moving particle bed formed by                catalyst to capture dust in gaseous product,                drawing resulting clean syngas from gas channel,                entering semi-coke and reduced oxygen carrier in                moving bed fuel reactor into combustion section in                lower part of lifting pipe air reactor, oxidizing                reduced oxygen carrier into oxidized oxygen carrier                by incoming hot air, burning semi-coke out by hot                air to generate ash and hot flue gas, and releasing                heat.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing clean                syngas by chemical chain gasification of                biomass.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes double-cycle oxygen                carrier and tar carbon dioxide reforming catalyst;                achieves the in-situ efficient removal of tar,                carbon dioxide and dust from biomass gasification                and their gaseous products; simultaneously converts                tar and carbon dioxide into hydrogen and carbon                monoxide; improves the yield and quality of syngas;                significantly improves the utilization rate of                atoms and energy; and avoids the contact and mutual                pollution between the oxygen carrier and the tar                carbon dioxide reforming catalyst through                independent double-cycle operation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing clean syngas by chemical chain                gasification of biomass comprises performing                pyrolysis and gasification reactions on biomass                under heating and oxidation of oxygen carriers in                oxidized state from oxygen carrier material bin in                moving bed fuel reactor, generating gaseous                products, semi-coke and reduced oxygen carriers,                entering gaseous product into moving bed reforming                reactor, heating tar and carbon dioxide in gaseous                product, catalyzing by catalyst from the                regenerator to undergo tar carbon dioxide reforming                reaction, using moving particle bed formed by                catalyst to capture dust in gaseous product,                drawing resulting clean syngas from gas channel,                entering semi-coke and reduced oxygen carrier in                moving bed fuel reactor into combustion section in                lower part of lifting pipe air reactor, oxidizing                reduced oxygen carrier into oxidized oxygen carrier                by incoming hot air, burning semi-coke out by hot                air to generate ash and hot flue gas, releasing                heat by combustion heats oxidized oxygen carrier,                entering oxidized oxygen carrier and ash into                lifting section in upper part of lifting pipe air                reactor, lifting to gas-solid separator by hot flue                gas and incoming hot air, separating                dust-containing flue gas and oxidized oxygen                carrier in first gas-solid separator, removing dust                and recovering heat, evacuating dust-containing                flue gas, entering oxidized oxygen carrier into                oxygen carrier silo, returning to moving bed fuel                reactor to form first circulation loop, entering                dust-captured carbon deposition catalyst in moving                bed reforming reactor into lifter, lifting by air                to second gas-solid separator, separating                dust-containing tail gas and coke catalyst in                second gas-solid separator, removing dust,                evacuating dust-containing exhaust gas, entering                coke catalyst into regenerator, combusting and                regenerating with incoming hot air and auxiliary                fuel, releasing heat by combustion heats                regenerated catalyst, and returning to reaction                chamber of moving bed reforming reactor to form                second circulation loop.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                device for producing clean syngas by chemical chain                gasification of biomass.

   USE - Carbon dioxide promoting transport                membrane.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The hygroscopic agent                  is porous particles. The hygroscopic agent is                  dispersed in the gel membrane. The gel                  membrane is a hydrogel. The gel membrane is                  supported on a hydrophilic porous membrane.                  Preferred Method: The step of preparing the sol                  solution involves adding the hydrophilic polymer                  and the carbon dioxide carrier to pure water to                  which the hygroscopic agent has been added first,                  and stirring the mixture. Preferred Properties:                  The moisture absorbing agent has a particle size                  of less than or equal to 75 mu m.

AU SI G
   LV X
   WU W
   XIE Y
AE UNIV BEIJING TECHNOLOGY (UYBT-C)
GA 2022832676
AB    NOVELTY - Metal-organic framework material of zirconium                of bifunctional pyridine carboxylic acid porphyrin                ligand, has chemical formula.
   USE - Metal-organic framework material of zirconium                of bifunctional pyridine carboxylic acid porphyrin                ligand used for adsorbing carbon dioxide, or for                preparing carbon monoxide by photocatalytic                reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The metal organic framework material shows                application in selective reduction of                photocatalytic carbon dioxide to carbon                monoxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Metal-organic framework material of zirconium                of bifunctional pyridine carboxylic acid porphyrin                ligand, has chemical formula of [Zr3(DPTC)], where                H4DPTC is an organic ligand 5', 5''-(10, 20-bis                (pyridine-4-yl)-5, 15-diyl) bis ([1, 1': 3',                1''-triphenyl]-4, 4'-dicarboxylic acid)                porphyrin.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing metal-organic framework material of                zirconium of bifunctional pyridine carboxylic acid                porphyrin ligand, which involves: adding                pyridinecarboxylic acid porphyrin (HDPTC) and                zirconium chloride (ZrCl) in the mixed solution of                N,N-dimethylformamide (DMF) and benzoic acid under                the sealing condition to obtain this metal-organic                through solvothermal reaction frame crystals. The                molar ratio of organic ligand pyridinecarboxylate                porphyrin (H4DPTC) to zirconium chloride (ZrCl4) is                1: (1-5), every 0.05mmol of zirconium chloride                corresponded to 1-4 mL dimethylformamide (DMF) ,                0.05-4 gram benzoic acid, the temperature of the                thermal reaction is 80-160&#8451;, and the                reaction time is 12-168 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal-organic                  frame material has a three-dimensional frame                  structure, and from the perspective of frame                  connection and construction. Preferred                  Conditions: The crystal structure of the                  metal-organic frame belongs to the monoclinic                  crystal system, and the space group is P21/m, and                  unit cell parameter is &#945;, &#946; and                  &#947;=90&#176;. The DPTC ligand                  exhibited a tetrahedral configuration in this                  metal-organic framework, and the Zr6 cluster                  coordinates with oxygen from eight different DPTC                  ligands, but the N on the pyridine group does not                  participate in the coordination. The bond lengths                  of the Zr-O bonds are 2.09 &#8491;, and all                  DPTC ligands and Zr6 clusters are connected to                  each other. The corresponding diagonal lengths of                  the hexagonal and rhombic channels formed in the                  a-axis direction are approximately 15.664                  &#8491; and 12.437 &#8491;.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The organic ligand is 5',                  5''-(10, 20-two(pyridine-4-base)-5,15-two base)                  bis([1, 1': 3 ', 1''-triphenyl]-4,                  4'-dicarboxylic acid)porphyrin (H4DPTC), i.e.                  two-end pyridine-tetra-carboxylate porphyrin                  ligand of formula (I). The ligand contains a                  porphyrin ring, two pyridines and four carboxylic                  acid groups. The angle between the two adjacent                  carboxyl groups in the ligand is 119&#176;,                  and the pyridine group is directly connected to                  the porphyrin ring. Preferred Process: The                  synthetic method of H4DPTC ligand, involves                  adding paraformaldehyde and freshly distilled                  pyrrole, and further adding trifluoroacetic acid,                  sealing, protecting with inert gas, adding sodium                  hydroxide, and purifying to obtain                  bis(1H-pyrrole-2-base) methane. The 3,                  5-dibromobenzaldehyde,                  4-methoxycarbonylbenzeneboronic acid, sodium                  carbonate and tetrakis (triphenylphosphine)                  palladium (Pd(PPh3)4) are put in methanol                  solution under inert gas protection to obtain                  desired aldehyde by suction filtration. The                  bis(1H-pyrrole-2-base) methane and 5'-formyl-[1,                  1': 3', 1''-triphenyl]-4, 4'-dicarboxylate are                  dissolved in dichloromethane, added                  trifluoroacetic acid under the protection of                  inert gas, reacted under dark conditions, added                  dichloromethane dicyano benzoquinone to obtain                  product by purification, preferably per13.68 mmol                  5'-formyl-[1, 1': 3', 1''-triphenyl]-4,                  4'-dicarboxylate dimethyl acid corresponded to                  13.68 mmol bis (1H-pyrrole-2-yl)methane, 10.00                  mmol trifluoroacetic acid and 13 .22mmol                  dichlorodicyanobenzoquinone. The 5', 5''-(5                  ,15-diphenyl) bis([1, 1': 3', 1''-triphenyl]-4,                  4'-dicarboxylic acid) tetramethylporphyrin and                  N-bromosuccinimide are dissolved in chloroform                  and reacted at room temperature for four days.                  The 5', 5''-(5, 15-diphenyl) bis([1, 1': 3',                  1''-triphenyl]-4, 4'-dicarboxylic acid)                  tetramethylporphyrin and 4-pyridineboronic acid,                  potassium carbonate and Pd(PPh3)4 are dissolved                  in a mixed solution of dioxane and water under                  the protection of inert gas, and heated at                  100&#8451; for 12 hours. The 5', 5''-(5,                  15-diphenyl) bis([1, 1': 3', 1''-triphenyl]-4,                  4'-dicarboxylic acid) tetramethylporphyrin and                  sodium hydroxide are added in a mixed solution of                  tetrahydrofuran/methanol/water under reflux to                  obtain the final product.

PT P
PN KR2021071474-A; KR2365536-B1
TI Reuse of electronic wastewater used in e.g. semiconductor manufacturing plant, involves adjusting pH of wastewater, removing particulate substances using separation membrane, and removing organic substances using reverse osmosis membrane.
AU HONG S H
   DAE-SOO P
   LEE T J
   KANG S K
   CHUNG J W
   CHOI J H
   DONG H J
   MIN J
   LEE S
AE SAMSUNG ENG CO LTD (SMSU-C)
GA 202167926T
AB    NOVELTY - Reuse of electronic wastewater involves adjusting (i) pH of electronic wastewater to obtain a primary treated water, removing (ii) particulate substances in the primary treated water using a separation membrane to obtain a secondary treated water, and simultaneously removing (iii) remaining organic substances and ionic substances in the secondary treated water using a reverse osmosis membrane to obtain a tertiary treated water.
   USE - Reuse of electronic wastewater e.g. heavy water, scrubber washing water, and utility tubing copper wastewater, in semiconductor manufacturing plant and display manufacturing plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively removes particulate substances, organic substances and ionic substances contained in the electronic wastewater, significantly reduces the risk of field workers by applying non-toxic, low-oxidizing chemicals, such as carbon dioxide to replace toxic chemicals and installing the carbon dioxide leak prevention unit, and reduces costs in terms of installation area and operating cost by minimizing reverse osmosis pretreatment process and improving reverse osmosis chemical cleaning method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an electronic wastewater reuse device, which comprises a pH adjustment unit that adjusts the pH of the electronic wastewater, a separation membrane unit that removes particulate substances in the primary treated water, a reverse osmosis membrane unit that simultaneously removes organic substances and ionic substances in the secondary treated water, and a carbon dioxide injection unit for injecting carbon dioxide into the pH adjustment unit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: The reuse device further comprises a carbon dioxide leak prevention unit that detects whether carbon dioxide leakage occurs from the carbon dioxide injection unit and discharges the leaked carbon dioxide to the outside when there is carbon dioxide leakage, a disinfectant injection unit that independently injects disinfectant into the separation membrane unit and the reverse osmosis membrane unit, an acid detergent injection unit that independently injects acid detergent into the separation membrane unit and the reverse osmosis membrane unit, an alkali detergent injection unit that injects alkali detergent into the reverse osmosis membrane unit, and a scale inhibitor injection unit that injects scale inhibitor into the reverse osmosis membrane unit.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The electronic wastewater has a pH of 7 or more. The primary treated water has a pH of 6.5 or less. Preferred Process: The reuse process does not involve pretreatment process other than the process (i) and process (ii), before the process (iii). The process (i) involves injecting carbon dioxide into the electronic wastewater, detecting whether carbon dioxide leakage occurs during the carbon dioxide injection process and discharging the leaked carbon dioxide to the outside if there is leaked carbon dioxide. The injection amount of carbon dioxide is determined based on the pH and total alkalinity of the electronic wastewater. The injection amount of carbon dioxide is 1000 mg/l or less, when the pH of the electronic wastewater is less than or equal to 9, and the total alkalinity of the electronic wastewater is less than or equal to 1000 mg/L (in terms of calcium carbonate). The process (ii) involves injecting a disinfectant into the primary treated water, and using microfiltration membrane or ultrafiltration membrane as separation membrane. The process (iii) involves injecting a scale inhibitor and/or disinfectant into the secondary treated water, and using reverse osmosis membrane which is a reverse osmosis membrane for brackish water or seawater. The reuse process further involves cleaning (iv) the separation membrane of the process (ii) without using a separate alkali detergent, and cleaning (v) the reverse osmosis membrane of the process (iii). The process (iv) involves repeatedly cleaning the separation membrane using disinfectant and an acid detergent at intervals of a cycle (c1) using a cleaning agent having a concentration (A1), and a cycle (c2) using a cleaning agent having a concentration (A2) which is lower than the concentration (A1). When the disinfectant is injected at a concentration of 10 mg into 1 L electronic wastewater, free residual halogen in 1 L electronic wastewater is detected in an amount of 0.2 mg or less. The process (v) involves repeatedly cleaning the reverse osmosis membrane using an acid detergent and an alkali detergent at intervals of a cycle (c3) using a cleaning agent having a concentration (A3), and a cycle (c4) using a cleaning agent having a concentration (A4) which is lower than the concentration (A3).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The scale inhibitor is polyphosphate, organophosphonate, polycarboxylate and/or polyacrylate, preferably sodium hexametaphosphate, 2-phosphonobutane-1,2,4-tricarboxylic acid, polyacrylic acid, polymethacrylic acid and/or polymaleic acid. The acid detergent is hydroxyacetic acid, acetic acid, sodium ethylenediamine hydroxyethyl triacetate, ammonium citrate, citric acid and/or ammonium dihydrogen citrate. The alkali detergent is sodium hydroxide, EDTA, ammonia, methylamine, ethylamine and/or pyridine. Preferred Properties: The acid detergent has a pH of 1-5. The alkali detergent has a pH of 10-14.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. GA 201952871X
AB    NOVELTY - Method for curing cementitious article (102)                involves providing cementitious article formed from                a mixture of aggregate, water and cement binder                comprising tricalcium silicate, placing                cementitious article in curing chamber (104),                maintaining curing relative humidity between 40%                and 80% and curing temperature between 50 degrees C                and 80 degrees C in curing chamber, and curing                cementitious article by flowing mixture of dry                steam (108) and carbon dioxide of concentration 2.5                and 20.0 vol.% forms calcium hydroxide and further                carbon dioxide reacts with cementitious article to                form calcium carbonate in the cementitious article                by at least one of the reactions. The curing                chamber is maintained with curing relative humidity                and curing temperature for 4 hours and 24 hours.                The cured cementitious article has equal or greater                than 15 wt.% of carbon dioxide.

   (2) preparation of cementitious product.

TI Method for eluting calcium from steel-making slag e.g. converter slag used in cement material, involves making pulverized or crushed steel-making slag to contact with carbon dioxide aqueous solution to elute calcium from steel-making slag.
AU FUKUI Y
   ASABA A
AE NISSHIN STEEL CO LTD (NISI-C)
GA 201950400U
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves grinding steel-making slag in a slurry or grinding the surface of the steel-making slag in the region on a bottom side inside a grinding/settling tank (110) when leaching calcium from steel-making slag inside the grinding and settling tank. The flow of the slurry due to stirring is suppressed to precipitate the steel-making slag in the upper region close to the liquid level inside the crushing and settling tank. The pulverized or crushed steel-making slag is brought into contact with a carbon dioxide aqueous solution to elute calcium from the steel-making slag.
   USE - Method for eluting calcium from steel-making slag such as converter slag, pretreated slag, secondary refining slag and electric furnace slag generated during steel-making process and used in cement material, road base material, civil engineering material and fertilizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The larger amount of calcium is easily eluted from the steel-making slag to an aqueous solution comprising carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for collecting calcium from steel-making slag; and
   (2) an apparatus for eluting calcium from a steel-making slag.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the apparatus for eluting calcium from a steel-making slag.
   Apparatus (100)
   Grinding/settling tank (110)
   Grinding mechanism (120)
   Grinding medium (122)
   Carbon-dioxide introducing portion (130)

GA 201807301W
AB    NOVELTY - The indoor air conditioning unit (100) has an influx unit (110), where the moisture and the carbon dioxide are removed from the supplied fluid. The influx unit is equipped in an inlet line side of a heating ventilation and cooling system, where the fluid is supplied in the heating ventilation and cooling system. An outlet unit (140) is provided, where the fluid from the moisture and a unit of the carbon dioxide are removed. A moist defoger (200) has a separating film module, which selectively absorbs and separates the moisture from the fluid. The fluid is flown.
   USE - Indoor air conditioning unit for a heating ventilation and cooling system (Claimed) for applying a separating film module.
   ADVANTAGE - The influx unit is provided, where the moisture and the carbon dioxide are removed from the supplied fluid, and hence ensures controlling the speed of a moving fan and reduces the cost of establishing and maintenance expense of an indoor air conditioning unit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for operating an indoor air conditioning unit.

PN CN113310063-A; CN215259901-U
TI Device for e.g. purifying carbon dioxide from glass kiln flue gas, has flue gas pre-processing system and carbon dioxide collecting system with desulfurization washing tower which absorbs sulfur dioxide and nitric oxide in carbon dioxide.
AU GAO Y
   LIU X
   JI L
   JIN S
   ZHANG D
   LI J
AE SHANGHAI TRIUMPH ENERGY CONSERVATION ENG (CHNB-C)
GA 2021A2454V
AB    NOVELTY - A glass kiln flue gas carbon dioxide trapping and purifying device has orderly connected carbon dioxide flue gas pre-processing system (1), carbon dioxide collecting system (2) and carbon dioxide refining system (3). The flue gas pre-processing system is connected to glass kiln (4), and is used for pretreatment of denitrification, desulfurization, dust removal and cooling of the flue gas discharged from the glass kiln. The carbon dioxide capturing system has a desulfurization water washing tower (6). The desulfurization water washing tower further absorbs sulfur dioxide and nitric oxide in the pre-processed carbon dioxide, realizes fast cooling and dust removing, and adsorbs and desorbs carbon dioxide into high-concentration carbon dioxide gas by pressure swing adsorption method. The carbon dioxide refining system can compress and dry high-concentration carbon dioxide gas, further remove impurities and prepare food-grade liquid carbon dioxide product.
   USE - Device used for capturing and purifying carbon dioxide from glass kiln flue gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device is capable of reducing emission of carbon dioxide and other pollutants to the atmosphere by capturing and enriching carbon dioxide in the flue gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for capturing and purifying carbon dioxide from glass kiln flue gas, which involves:
   (A) pretreating the flue gas with carbon dioxide produced by glass kiln;
   (B) performing dust removal, desulfurization and denitration treatment;
   (C) washing with water;
   (D) performing desulfurization and denitrification of pretreated flue gas to obtain purified flue gas;
   (E) using pressure swing adsorption to desorb carbon dioxide from the purified flue gas to prepare high-purity carbon dioxide gas;
   (F) compressing high-purity carbon dioxide gas;
   (G) adsorbing;
   (H) refrigerating;
   (I) liquefying; and
   (J) rectifying to obtain food-grade carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the device for capturing and purifying carbon dioxide from glass kiln flue gas.
   Carbon dioxide flue gas pre-processing system (1)
   Carbon dioxide collecting system (2)
   Carbon dioxide refining system (3)
   Glass kiln (4)
   Desulfurization water washing tower (6)
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); Q73 (Combustion equipment/processes (F23))

PN DE102019119194-A1
TI Refrigerant for refrigeration machine with refrigerant circuit comprises first component including carbon dioxide, second component including fluorinated hydrocarbon and third component including hydrocarbon.
AU HESSE U
   GOPFERT T
AE UNIV DRESDEN TECH (UYDR-C)
GA 202108324E
AB    NOVELTY - Refrigerant for a refrigeration machine comprises a mixture of three components, first component including carbon dioxide, second component including a fluorinated hydrocarbon or mixture of fluorinated hydrocarbons and third component including hydrocarbon or mixture of hydrocarbons.
   USE - The refrigerant is useful as a replacement for refrigerant R23 in the refrigeration machine (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The refrigerant is non-flammable and has low global warming potential with favorable oil behavior.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The refrigerant comprises at least 50 wt.% first component, at least 23 wt.% second component and 0-1 wt.% third component. Preferred Components: Third component include alkanes and alkenes, preferably propane and/or propene. Second component includes R14, R23, R32, R41, R125, R134, R134a, R13I1 (CF31), R152a, R410A, R466A , R1234yf or R1234ze. Second component has 6-26 wt.% R41 in pure form or R41 as part of a mixture of fluorinated hydrocarbons. The temperature glide during the evaporation process is less than 20 K, preferably less than 10 K. The mass ratio of the three components is selected such that the GWP of the refrigerant is less than 2500, preferably less than 250.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Use of Zingiber zerumbet extract being extract prepared by extracting with supercritical carbon dioxide or heptane extract for combating and/or preventing formation of dandruff.
AU LUC J
   ROUX I
   FIORINI C
   JOULIA P
   PANEL A
   LUKA J
AE FABRE DERMO-COSMETIQUE PIERRE (FABR-C)
   FABRE DERMO-COSMETIQUE PIERRE (FABR-C)
   FABRE DERMO COSMETIC CO PIERRE (FABR-C)
   FABRE DERMO-COSMETIQUE PIERRE (FABR-C)
GA 202050738A
AB    NOVELTY - Use of Zingiber zerumbet extract being an extract prepared by extracting with supercritical carbon dioxide or a heptane extract, is claimed for combating and/or preventing formation of dandruff.
   USE - The Z.zerumbet extract is useful in dermatological or cosmetic composition for combating and/or preventing formation of dandruff (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for dermatological or cosmetic composition, which comprises at least one Z.zerumbet extract.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The extract is an extract of Z.zerumbet rhizome. The composition comprises 0.05-2 wt.%, preferably 0.2-0.8 wt.% Z.zerumbet extract with respect to dry extract. The composition further comprises at least one anti-dandruff active ingredient such as an antifungal chosen from piroctone olamine, zinc pyrithione, ciclopirox or their mixture. The composition further comprises an extract of celery seeds.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological. Test details are described but no results given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - The cosmetic composition is applied by topical route. No dosage details given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN109851778-A; CN109851778-B
TI Preparing polybutyrolactam by anionic ring-opening polymerization comprises potassium t-butoxide activation step, carbon dioxide initiation step and paraffin oil and sodium lauryl sulfate addition step.
AU JIANG S
   ZHAO P
   ZHAO Y
   WANG L
   WANG D
   ZHENG Z
   LIU Y
AE HI-TECH FIBER GROUP CORP (HITE-Non-standard)
   HENGTIAN BIOLOGIC MATERIALS ENG TECHNOLO (HENG-Non-standard)
   WEIFANG XINLONG BIOLOGICAL MATERIAL CO (WEIF-Non-standard)
GA 201953332T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polybutyrolactam by anionic ring-opening polymerization comprises potassium t-butoxide activation step, carbon dioxide initiation step and paraffin oil and sodium lauryl sulfate addition step.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing polybutyrolactam by anionic ring-opening polymerization.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has slow and controllable polymerization rate and is suitable for large-scale production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The raw materials are 6-15 (preferably 6-9) pts. wt. 2-pyrrolidone, 0.5-6 pts. wt. (preferably 1-1.5) potassium tert-butoxide, 30-50 (preferably 40-50) pts. wt. paraffin oil and 1 pts. wt. sodium lauryl sulfate. Preferred Method: The potassium t-butoxide activation step comprises placing 2-pyrrolidone in the reaction vessel under nitrogen protection, heating and stirring under vacuum at 60-100 degrees C for 0.5-8 hours, turning off the vacuum, adding potassium t-butoxide under nitrogen atmosphere, turning off the nitrogen, stirring under vacuum and incubating at 60-100 degrees C for 2-6 hours to obtain activation solution. The carbon dioxide initiation step comprises adding half of the activation solution into reaction kettle, heating at 15-50 degrees C and passing carbon dioxide at a flow rate of 2-3 l/minute for 5-30 minutes. The sodium lauryl sulfate addition step comprises introducing paraffin oil and sodium lauryl sulfate, and then adding paraffin oil, sodium lauryl sulfate, and the other half of activation solution remaining in the reactor and heating continuously in a closed reactor at 30-70 degrees C for 18-72 hours. The method comprises heating paraffin oil under vacuum at 70-120 degrees C for 1-6 hours. The method further comprises adding n-hexane, where mass ratio between n-hexane and 2-pyrrolidone is 20-45:6-15, stirring for 3-48 hours, suction filtering to obtain polymer, washing the polymer with acetone and water respectively and drying to obtain white solid as final product. Preferred Components: The molecular weight of polybutyrolactam is 20000-40000 or more yield is 79-83%; and maximum thermal decomposition rate temperature is 270 degrees C.
DC A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.)
MC A02-A; A05-F01A; A08-M03B; A08-S01; A10-D03

AU YIN C
   FAN L
   ZHANG J
   WANG Y
   FU A
AE CHINA NAT PETROLEUM CORP (CNPC-C)
   TUBULAR GOODS RES CENT CNPC (CNPC-C)
   CHINA NAT PETROLEUM CORP TUBULALR GOODS (CNPC-C)
GA 201903928H
AB    NOVELTY - Anti-carbon dioxide corrosion inhibitor                comprises 50-60 wt.% bis-imidazoline quaternary                ammonium salt, 10-50 wt.% alkyl/aryl sodium                diethyldithiocarbamate and 10-40 wt.%                solvent.
   USE - The inhibitor is useful for inhibiting                corrosion of oil or gas pipeline (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The inhibitor is economical and suitable for                industrial production; and has good anti-corrosion                effect and low dosage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The inhibitor                  comprises 50-60 wt.% bis-imidazoline quaternary                  ammonium salt, 20-40 alkyl/aryl sodium                  diethyldithiocarbamate and 10-30 wt.% solvent.                  Preferred Components: The solvent is water or                  N,N-dimethylformamide. The mass ratio of                  imidazoline intermediate, sulfuric acid and epoxy                  chloropropane is 4:2:1, and the mass                  concentration of sulfuric acid is 95%. The                  alkyl/aryl sodium diethyldithiocarbamate is                  sodium dimethyldithiocarbamate, sodium                  diethyldithiocarbamate, sodium dibutyl                  diethyldithiocarbamate, phenyl dithiocarbamate or                  benzyl dithiocarbamate. Preferred Method: The                  preparation of bis-imidazoline quaternary                  ammonium salt comprises adding oleic acid and                  diethylenetriamine into a reactor at a mass ratio                  of 1:1, reacting at 140-160 degrees C for 3-5                  hours at a heating rate of 1-2 degrees C/minute,                  vacuum dehydrating at 220-240 degrees C for 6                  hours to obtain imidazoline intermediate, adding                  sulfuric acid and epoxy chloropropane into the                  imidazoline intermediate, and reacting at 105-110                  degrees C for 11-13 hosurs, where the heating is                  carried out at 220-240 degrees C.

PN CN108579210-A
TI Filter material for adsorbing carbon dioxide, comprises raw materials comprising high efficiency particulate air filter, graphene-carbonic anhydrase solution and binder.
AU WANG Q
AE GUANGZHOU KANGLV PURIFICATION TECHNOLOGY (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201879804W
AB    NOVELTY - A filter material comprises raw materials comprising high efficiency particulate air filter, graphene-carbonic anhydrase solution and binder, where the graphene-carbonic anhydrase solution comprises raw materials comprising carbonic anhydrase, modified graphene powder, dilution buffer and crosslinking agent.
   USE - Filter material for adsorbing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The filter material has excellent antibacterial effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for preparing the filter material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The dilution buffer (100 ml) comprises 15-30 mM tris(hydroxymethyl)aminomethane-hydrochloride buffer, 1-5 mM magnesium chloride, 1-5 mM sodium chloride and remaining protein refolding solution.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The graphene-carbonic anhydrase solution comprises raw materials comprising 0.1-1 pts. wt. modified graphene powder, 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. carbonic anhydrase, 0.1-1 pts. wt. crosslinking agent and remaining dilution buffer. Preferred Components: The crosslinking agent is dialdehyde crosslinking agent. PN CN111156725-A; WO2021151392-A1
TI Carbon dioxide phase change cycle refrigeration system comprises e.g. a liquid carbon dioxide storage tank used to store liquid carbon dioxide, the evaporating chamber connected to liquid carbon dioxide storage tank used for depressurizing.
AU YANG J
AE LUOYANG MAITUOFENG TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (LUOY-Non-standard)
   SHANGHAI FULUDI FLUID TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202044620R
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide phase change cycle refrigeration system comprises e.g. a liquid carbon dioxide storage tank (1) is used to store liquid carbon dioxide. A evaporating chamber (3) connected to the liquid carbon dioxide storage tank used for depressurizing and evaporating liquid carbon dioxide into gaseous carbon dioxide, and cooling the circulating ethylene glycol aqueous solution flowing through it. A boosting chamber (4) connected to the gas outlet of the evaporating chamber used to pressurize and cool the high-temperature gaseous carbon dioxide flowing out of the gas outlet of the evaporating chamber. A condensing chamber (5) connected to the boosting chamber and the liquid carbon dioxide storage tank used to reduce the temperature of the high-temperature and high-pressure gas carbon dioxide flowing out of the boosting chamber to liquid carbon dioxide and stored in the liquid carbon dioxide storage tank.
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide phase change cycle refrigeration system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: is economical and environmentally friendly; has high heat exchange, carbon dioxide cycle efficiency and utilizes thermal effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide phase change cycle refrigeration system, comprises a liquid carbon dioxide storage tank (1) is used to store liquid carbon dioxide. A evaporating chamber (3) connected to the liquid carbon dioxide storage tank used for depressurizing and evaporating liquid carbon dioxide into gaseous carbon dioxide, and cooling the circulating ethylene glycol aqueous solution flowing through it. A boosting chamber (4) connected to the gas outlet of the evaporating chamber used to pressurize and cool the high-temperature gaseous carbon dioxide flowing out of the gas outlet of the evaporating chamber. A condensing chamber (5) connected to the boosting chamber and the liquid carbon dioxide storage tank used to reduce the temperature of the high-temperature and high-pressure gas carbon dioxide flowing out of the boosting chamber to liquid carbon dioxide and stored in the liquid carbon dioxide storage tank and a liquid/air heat exchanger connected to the liquid outlet of the evaporating chamber for receiving the low-temperature ethylene glycol aqueous solution flowing out of the liquid outlet of the evaporating chamber and exchange heat with the circulating air flowing through it. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for processing carbon dioxide phase change cycle refrigeration method for system comprising (i) sending the liquid carbon dioxide in liquid carbon dioxide storage tank into the evaporation chamber, evaporating the liquid carbon dioxide in the evaporation chamber and converting into high-temperature gaseous carbon dioxide, when evaporating and absorbing heat from the ambient air, (ii) exchanging the evaporated gaseous carbon dioxide with the ethylene glycol aqueous solution entering the evaporation chamber, entering the cooled ethylene glycol aqueous solution into the liquid/air heat exchanger for convection heat exchange with the circulating air to reducing the air temperature, (iii) flowing out the high-temperature gaseous carbon dioxide after convection heat exchange in the evaporation chamber entering the pressurization chamber to pressurizing, entering the pressurized high-temperature and high-pressure gaseous carbon dioxide to the condensation chamber cooling and converting into liquid carbon dioxide again, (iv) condensing the liquid carbon dioxide in the evaporation chamber an entering the liquid carbon dioxide storage tank for storage and (v) repeating the above steps for circulating cooling.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of sectional view of the.
   Liquid carbon dioxide storage tank (1)
   Evaporating chamber (2)
   Boosting chamber (4)
   Condensing chamber (5)

AU PARK I G
   PARK Y S
AE PYEONG HWA ENG & CONSULTING CO LTD (PYEO-Non-standard)
GA 202002858A
AB    NOVELTY - The carbon dioxide treatment system comprises a blower (100) for supplying indoor air including carbon dioxide to an absorber (200), and an absorption unit in which carbon dioxide is absorbed by the carbon dioxide absorbent. An absorbent discharge line (10) is provided for supplying the absorbent absorbed carbon dioxide to a desorption unit (300). The desorption unit receives the absorbent absorbed carbon dioxide discharged from the absorber to separate the carbon dioxide and the absorbent. A carbon dioxide collection unit (400) is provided for receiving and storing the carbon dioxide separated from the desorption unit. An absorbent regeneration line (20) is provided for refluxing the absorbent separated from the detachable portion to the absorbent portion. A carbon dioxide concentration detector (500) senses the concentration of carbon dioxide in real time.
   USE - Carbon dioxide treatment system for use in industrial facilities, such as liquor factories and food and beverage manufacturers.
   ADVANTAGE - Efficient removal of carbon dioxide is enabled without the inflow of external air to maintain optimal production conditions. Human injury caused by high concentration of carbon dioxide generated in industrial facilities, is minimized.
