PN WO2021041581-A1; US2022305437-A1
TI Carbon-dioxide-selective membrane used for            separating carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide,            comprises selective polymer layer comprising polymer            matrix and amine-containing carrier, and has preset            carbon dioxide/carbon monoxide selectivity.
AU HO W S W
   HAN Y
AE OHIO STATE INNOVATION FOUND (OHIS-C)
   OHIO STATE INNOVATION FOUND (OHIS-C)
GA 202122189L
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon-dioxide-selective membrane (123)                comprises a support layer and a selective polymer                layer comprising a hydrophilic polymer matrix and                an amine-containing carrier dispersed in the                polymer matrix, arranged on the support layer. The                carbon-dioxide-selective membrane has a carbon                dioxide/carbon monoxide selectivity of at least 500                at 100 degrees C and feed pressure of 2 atm.
   USE - Carbon-dioxide-selective membrane is used for                separating carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide in                feed stream e.g. exhaust gas from solid oxide fuel                cell (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon-dioxide-selective membrane has high                carbon dioxide permeability, and comprises the                hydrophobic blocking layer which prevents intrusion                of water into the membrane and protects the                membrane from damage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                separation of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide,                which involves contacting the                carbon-dioxide-selective membrane with a feed gas                stream comprising carbon dioxide and carbon                monoxide under conditions to afford transmembrane                permeation of carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view explaining                separation of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide                using the carbon-dioxide-selective membrane.
   Fuel cell system (100)
   Anode (104)
   Cathode (106)
   Carbon dioxide separator (118)
   Carbon-dioxide-selective membrane                (123)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The base is at least                  one chosen from potassium hydroxide, sodium                  hydroxide, lithium hydroxide, cesium hydroxide,                  and ammonium hydroxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amine-containing                  mobile carrier comprises a salt of a primary                  amine or a salt of a secondary amine, of formula                  (I), preferably an amino acid salt chosen from                  glycinate salt, sarcosinate salt, and                  aminoisobutyrate salt. The salt of                  amine-containing mobile carrier is obtained by                  reaction of an amine or a base with an amino                  acid. The amine is at least one chosen from                  piperazine, 2-(1-piperazinyl)ethylamine,                  ethylenediamine, diethylenetriamine,                  triethylenetetramine, and tetramethylguanidine.                  The amino acid is at least one chosen from                  aminoisobutyric acid, sarcosine, glycine,                  proline, lysine, arginine, histidine, aspartic                  acid, glutamic acid, serine, threonine,                  asparagine, glutamine, cysteine, selenocysteine,                  alanine, valine, isoleucine, leucine, methionine,                  phenylalanine, tyrosine, tryptophan, pipecolinic                  acid, and N,N-dimethylglycine. The selective                  polymer layer further comprises a crosslinking                  agent comprising at least one chosen from                  formaldehyde, glutaraldehyde, maleic anhydride,                  glyoxal, divinylsulfone, toluene diisocyanate,                  trimethylol melamine, terephthalate aldehyde,                  epichlorohydrin, vinyl acrylate, silane, and                  aminosilane.R1-R4 = H or 1-4C hydrocarbyl;n = 0-4;A(power)(m+) = cation having valence of                  1-3;andm = integer equal to valence of                  cation.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The hydrophilic                  polymer is at least one chosen from polyvinyl                  alcohol, polyvinyl acetate, polyethylene oxide,                  polyvinylpyrrolidone, polyacrylamide,                  polysiloxane, and their copolymers. The                  amine-containing carrier comprises at least one                  amine-containing polymer chosen from                  polyvinylamine, polyallylamine,                  polyethyleneimine, poly-N-isopropylallylamine,                  poly-N-tert-butylallylamine,                  poly-N-1,2-dimethylpropylallylamine,                  poly-N-methylallylamine,                  poly-N,N-dimethylallylamine,                  poly-2-vinylpiperidine, poly-4-vinylpiperidine,                  polyaminostyrene, chitosan, and their copolymers,                  preferably polyvinylamine, and an                  amine-containing mobile carrier. The support                  layer comprises at least one gas-permeable                  polymer chosen from polyamide, polyimide,                  polypyrrolone, polyester, sulfone-based polymer,                  nitrile-based polymer, polymeric organosilicone,                  fluorinated polymer, polyolefin, and their                  copolymers, preferably polyethersulfone or                  polysulfone. The gas-permeable polymer is                  arranged on a base which is a nonwoven fabric                  comprising fibers made of a polyester. The                  carbon-dioxide-selective membrane is configured                  in a flat sheet, a spiral-wound, hollow fibers,                  or a plate-and-frame configuration, and further                  comprises a hydrophobic blocking layer arranged                  on the support layer, arranged between the                  support layer and selective polymer layer, and/or                  arranged on the selective polymer layer. The                  membrane is used with a solid oxide fuel cell                  system (100) to covert carbon dioxide to carbon                  monoxide. In the solid oxide fuel cell system, a                  paraffin such as ethane or propane is reacted at                  an anode of the fuel cell and convert to an                  olefin such as ethylene or propylene. Preferred                  Properties: The carbon-dioxide-selective membrane                  preferably has a carbon dioxide/carbon monoxide                  selectivity of 500-3000 or 1000-2000 at 100                  degrees C and feed pressure of 2 atm, and has a                  carbon dioxide/carbon monoxide selectivity of at                  least 100, preferably 100-500 or 100-250 at 170                  degrees C and feed pressure of 2 atm, a carbon                  dioxide permeability of at least 1500 Barrer,                  preferably 1500-10000 Barrer or 2500-10000 at 100                  degrees C and feed pressure of 2 atm, and a                  carbon dioxide permeability of at least 500                  Barrer, preferably 500-7500 Barrer or 500-5000                  Barrer at 170 degrees C and feed pressure of 2                  atm. The amine-containing mobile carrier has a                  molecular weight of less than 1000 Da.

PT P
PN CN109012089-A; CN109012089-B
TI Method for trapping carbon dioxide, involves introducing flue gas from flue gas purification system for purification treatment and cooling down, followed by introducing flue gas absorption tower and absorbing potassium carbonate solution.
AU LI J
   WANG J
   LIU M
   ZHAO L
   ZHANG H
   QIAN X
AE UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
   UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
GA 2018A5401N
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide trapping method involves introducing the flue gas from the flue gas purification system for purification treatment and cooling down, introducing the flue gas an absorption tower, absorbing the potassium carbonate solution to carbon dioxide, converting the potassium carbonate into potassium bicarbonate, reacting the potassium hydroxide solution with the carbon dioxide in the flue gas to form a carbon dioxide lean liquid, evacuating and introducing the carbon dioxide lean liquid to the absorption tower, introducing the carbon dioxide rich liquid in the primary absorption tower to the carbon dioxide desorption system, transferring the flue gas in the heat exchanger, decomposing the potassium hydrogen carbonate and releasing carbon dioxide, collecting carbon dioxide through a carbon dioxide collection system, introducing the regenerated potassium hydroxide solution into the secondary absorption tower to continue to absorb carbon dioxide in the flue gas.
   USE - Method for trapping carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables trapping carbon dioxide in simple and cost effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon dioxide trapping method involves introducing the flue gas from the flue gas passage to the flue gas purification system for purification treatment, cooling down the purified flue gas, after cooling, introducing the flue gas an absorption tower, and the potassium carbonate solution in the absorption tower is contact with the flue gas, absorbing the potassium carbonate solution to carbon dioxide, converting the potassium carbonate into potassium bicarbonate to form a carbon dioxide rich liquid, after complete the absorption of the flue gas from the primary absorption tower into the secondary absorption tower, reacting the potassium hydroxide solution with the remaining carbon dioxide in the flue gas to form a carbon dioxide lean liquid mainly composed of a potassium carbonate solution in the secondary absorption tower, after the secondary absorption flue gas exits the secondary absorption tower, evacuating, and introducing the carbon dioxide lean liquid to the absorption tower from the secondary absorption tower to absorb the carbon dioxide in the flue gas, introducing the carbon dioxide rich liquid in the primary absorption tower to the carbon dioxide desorption system, transferring the waste heat of the flue gas in the heat exchanger to the carbon dioxide rich liquid, raising the temperature of the carbon dioxide rich liquid, and decomposing the potassium hydrogencarbonate in the rich liquid and releasing carbon dioxide and converting the carbon dioxide rich liquid into carbon dioxide lean liquid, collecting carbon dioxide through a carbon dioxide collection system, introducing the carbon dioxide lean liquid into the potassium hydroxide regeneration system to form a potassium hydroxide solution, introducing the regenerated potassium hydroxide solution into the secondary absorption tower to continue to absorb carbon dioxide in the flue gas.

PN CN113717513-A
TI Thermoplastic biodegradable plastic comprises raw material starch, polybutylene adipate terephthalate, propylene oxide, methylene dichloride, hydrochloric acid, methanol solution, maleic anhydride, calcium carbonate, crosslinking agent, and catalyst.
AU LI L
   WU C
AE WU C (WUCC-Individual)
GA 2021E5739D
AB    NOVELTY - A thermoplastic biodegradable plastic comprises raw material starch, polybutylene adipate terephthalate, propylene oxide, methylene dichloride, hydrochloric acid, methanol solution, maleic anhydride, calcium carbonate, crosslinking agent, and catalyst, and is prepared by (1) adding catalyst to a reactor, drying, adding propylene oxide and maleic anhydride, introducing carbon dioxide gas, stirring, reacting, stopping the reaction, and cooling to obtain viscous material, (2) washing with methylene dichloride, hydrochloric acid and distilled water, filtering, dissolving, stirring, filtering, and drying to obtain carbon dioxide/propylene oxide/maleic anhydride copolymer, and (3) adding raw material starch, polybutylene adipate terephthalate, crosslinking agent, calcium carbonate and carbon dioxide/propylene oxide/maleic anhydride copolymer into a mixer for melt-blending, transferring into a flat vulcanizing machine, hot-pressing, keeping the pressure, and cooling.
   USE - Thermoplastic biodegradable plastic.
   ADVANTAGE - The thermoplastic biodegradable plastic has excellent compatibility, plastic biodegradability and thermoplasticity, high mechanical strength, simple production and low production cost, and is environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A thermoplastic biodegradable plastic comprises 20-40 parts mass raw material starch, 20-60 parts mass polybutylene adipate terephthalate, 60-100 parts mass propylene oxide, 30-50 parts mass methylene dichloride, 30-50 parts mass hydrochloric acid, 30-50 parts mass methanol solution, 1-10 parts mass maleic anhydride, 10-20 parts mass calcium carbonate, 5-15 parts mass crosslinking agent, and 1-10 parts mass catalyst, and is prepared by (1) adding catalyst to a stainless steel high pressure reactor, drying at 80-90degreesC for 24 hours, adding propylene oxide and maleic anhydride, introducing carbon dioxide gas to make the pressure reach 4-6 MPa, stirring at speed of 100-300 rpm, reacting at 70-90degreesC for 24 hours, stopping the reaction, and cooling to room temperature to obtain viscous material, (2) washing the viscous material with methylene dichloride, hydrochloric acid and distilled water 2-3 times respectively, filtering, dissolving in methanol solution, stirring for 1-3 hours at a speed of 200-300 rpm, filtering to remove insoluble substances, and drying at 80degreesC to constant weight to obtain carbon dioxide/propylene oxide/maleic anhydride copolymer, and (3) adding raw material starch, polybutylene adipate terephthalate, crosslinking agent, calcium carbonate and carbon dioxide/propylene oxide/maleic anhydride copolymer obtained in the step (2) into a mixer for melt-blending, transferring into a flat vulcanizing machine, hot-pressing at 10 MPa and 160degreesC for 5 minutes, keeping the pressure at 10 MPa, and cooling to room temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The starch is modified thermoplastic starch which is prepared by mixing corn starch and glycerol, adding tartaric acid, magnetically stirring at room temperature for 6-12 hours, transferring to a twin-screw extruder, setting the rotating speed at 60-100 rpm and the machine head temperature at 120-160degreesC, extruding and granulating, where the mass ratio of corn starch, glycerol and tartaric acid is 7:3:(0.1-1). In step (3), the starch and polybutylene adipate terephthalate are dried in a vacuum drying oven at 80degreesC for 6 hours. Preferred Composition: The amount of methanol solution is 50 %mass. Preferred Components: The catalyst is one or more of zinc glutarate, beta-diimide zinc, co-porphyrin, bistriphenylphosphine ammonium chloride, and tricyclohexyl phosphine. The cross-linking agent is one or more of hyperbranched polyester amide, dicumyl peroxide and maleic anhydride graft.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The hydrochloric acid is 5 %mass hydrochloric acid. Preferred Component: The calcium carbonate is nano-calcium carbonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: In step (3), the melt blending is performed in an internal mixer at 160-190degreesC and 60-100 rpm for 10-15 minutes, and the carbon dioxide/propylene oxide/maleic anhydride copolymer is dried in the vacuum drying oven at 30degreesC for 6 hours, and putting into the internal mixer for melt blending.

Thus starch would be excluded, but chemically modified starch included).); A17 (Polymers of unsubstituted aliphatic monoolefins; including polyethylene.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.)

PN CN113353949-A
TI Synthesizing zeolite from fly ash and coal gangue            aluminum slag and its comprehensive utilization            comprises e.g. mixing fly ash clinker and coal gangue            aluminum slag, ultrasonicating mixed solution, and            aging under stirring conditions.
AU WANG R
   WANG L
   HE Y
AE UNIV NORTHEASTERN (UNEN-C)
GA 2021A74614
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing zeolite from fly ash and coal                gangue aluminum slag and its comprehensive                utilization comprises (1) pre-treating fly ash to                obtain fly ash clinker mixed with fly ash and                alkali, (2) mixing fly ash clinker and coal gangue                aluminum slag to obtain raw materials, (3) mixing                the raw materials and water to obtain a mixed                solution, and ultrasonicating the mixed solution                and aging under stirring conditions, (4) performing                hydrothermal crystallization treatment on the mixed                solution to obtain a crystal solution, (5)                filtering out the liquid in the crystal solution to                obtain zeolite, placing the zeolite in an oven and                drying for 6-10 hours at 100-120 degrees C, (6)                using zeolite to absorb carbon dioxide or sulfur                dioxide, (7) reacting the enriched carbon dioxide                with the sodium aluminate solution to generate                sodium carbonate; and (8) using the generated                sodium carbonate as alkali required for the                pretreatment of fly ash.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing zeolite                from fly ash and coal gangue aluminum slag and its                comprehensive utilization.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes fly ash and coal gangue                aluminum slag to synthesize high value-added                zeolite.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesizing zeolite from fly ash and coal                gangue aluminum slag and its comprehensive                utilization comprises (1) pre-treating fly ash to                obtain fly ash clinker mixed with fly ash and                alkali, (2) mixing fly ash clinker and coal gangue                aluminum slag to obtain raw materials, (3) mixing                the raw materials and water to obtain a mixed                solution, and ultrasonicating the mixed solution                and aging under stirring conditions, (4) performing                hydrothermal crystallization treatment on the mixed                solution to obtain a crystal solution, (5)                filtering out the liquid in the crystal solution to                obtain zeolite, placing the zeolite in an oven and                drying for 6-10 hours at 100-120 degrees C, (6)                using zeolite to absorb carbon dioxide or sulfur                dioxide, and then desorbing the zeolite with carbon                dioxide or sulfur dioxide to achieve the enrichment                of carbon dioxide or sulfur dioxide, (7) reacting                the enriched carbon dioxide with the sodium                aluminate solution to generate sodium carbonate; or                converting the enriched sulfur dioxide to sulfuric                acid, and (8) using the generated sodium carbonate                as alkali required for the pretreatment of fly ash,                and as the alkali required for extracting alumina                through the alkali method as coal gangue; or using                the generated sulfuric acid as acid required for                the production of alumina through the acid method                as coal gangue.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The pretreatment of fly                  ash includes mechanically grinding the fly ash                  for 15-45 minutes, so that the fly ash can pass                  through a 325 mesh sieve, then roasting for 1-4                  hours at 700-900 degrees C, grinding and mixing                  fly ash with alkali at ultrasonic power of 500 W                  and 100% amplitude, ultrasonicating for 15-45                  minutes in a working environment with frequency                  of 25 kHz to obtain the first mixture material,                  roasting the first mixture material at 500-800                  degrees C for 4-6 hours to obtain fly ash                  clinker. The sodium aluminate solution is                  solution produced in the process of producing                  aluminum oxide from coal gangue through the                  alkaline method. In the step (3), the aging is                  carried out for 1-3 hours at 60-80 degrees C, and                  the stirring speed is 300-400 revolutions per                  minute. The stirring speed is 350 revolutions per                  minute. In the step (4), the temperature of                  hydrothermal crystallization is 100-120 degrees C                  for 12-48 hours. Preferred Components: In the                  step (2), the mass ratio of fly ash clinker and                  coal gangue aluminum slag is 20:1-1:10. The mass                  ratio of alkali and fly ash in the first mixture                  is 1.5:1-2.5:1, the alkali includes sodium                  carbonate and sodium hydroxide, and the content                  of sodium hydroxide in the alkali is at most                  25-40 wt.%. In the step (3), the mass ratio of                  raw material and water is 1:5-1:10. The coal                  gangue aluminum extraction slag is the waste slag                  after the coal gangue undergoes acid method or                  alkali method to produce aluminum oxide.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN WO2021078635-A1; AU2020369070-A1; CN114585773-A; EP4010514-A1
TI Electrolyzer for carbon dioxide reduction, has two gas diffusion electrodes whose common electrolyte space is provided from cathode gas diffusion electrode, and anode gas diffusion electrode which comprises cation-selective coating.
AU REINISCH D
   MARTIC N
   SCHMID G
AE SIEMENS ENERGY GLOBAL GMBH & CO KG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS ENERGY GLOBAL GMBH & CO KG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS ENERGY GLOBAL GMBH & CO KG (SIEI-C)
GA 2021425407
AB    NOVELTY - The electrolyzer (2) has an electrolysis cell (4) with a cathode gas diffusion electrode (6) and an anode gas diffusion electrode (8). The cathode gas diffusion electrode on a first side (12) flatly adjoins a cathode gas chamber (10). The anode gas diffusion electrode adjoins an anode gas space (14) with a first side (13). The two gas diffusion electrodes common electrolyte space (16) is provided, which from the cathode gas diffusion electrode to extends to the anode gas diffusion electrode and is delimited in sections by the two gas diffusion electrodes with their second side (18, 19) facing away from the respectively assigned gas chambers. The anode gas diffusion electrode has a cation-selective coating. A carbon-di-oxide separator (32) is provided in the electrolyte circuit (26).
   USE - Electrolyzer for carbon dioxide reduction in lignite power plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The electrolysis cell of the electrolyzer has only one electrolyte space, which not separated by a membrane or a diaphragm. The electrolyte located in the electrolyte space and flowing through it is connected to both gas diffusion electrodes. The effective transport of protons into the electrolyte is achieved. The oxygen is released in the electrolyte during the process, diffuses through the anode gas diffusion electrode as molecular oxygen and does not mix with the carbon dioxide that is formed in the electrolyte. The carbon dioxide in the electrolyte is in supersaturated form and is led out of the electrolyte space and is discharged from the electrolyte outside the electrolyte space. The carbon dioxide discharged is fed back into the process as educt gas, which makes the overall process significantly more economical. The requirement of the complex separation of a carbon dioxide-oxygen mixture is avoided, so that the process is made much more efficient. The carbon-di-oxide present as a result of the neutralization does not necessarily have to lead to the formation of gas bubbles in the electrolyte space.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for operating an electrolyzer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view that shows an electrolyzer with the individual process devices.
   Electrolyzer (2)
   Electrolysis cell (4)
   Cathode gas diffusion electrode (6)
   Anode gas diffusion electrode (8)
   Cathode gas chamber (10)
   First side (12)
   First side (13)
   Anode gas space (14)
   Gas diffusion electrodes common electrolyte space (16)
   Side of gas diffusion electrodes (18,19)
   Electrolyte circuit (26)
   Carbon-di-oxide separator (32)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The dissolved salt is not based on a salt of carbonic acid. The carbon-di-oxide on a cathode gas diffusion electrode is partially reduced to carbon mono oxide.

PT P
PN CN111333093-A
TI Producing light calcium carbonate by controlling alkali residue white slurry water content, adding calcium oxide, adding electrolyte solution, separating, combining filter residue, adding water, dispersant and whitening agent, and beating.
AU LI B
   CHEN Z
AE GUANGDONG YUFENG POWDER MATERIAL CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202061167B
AB    NOVELTY - Producing light calcium carbonate, involves (a) controlling alkali residue white slurry water content, adding calcium oxide, adding electrolyte solution, stirring, separating, and collecting filtrate and filter residue (A), (b) introducing the filtrate into an autoclave, which is first supercritical state, heat-preserving, passing carbon dioxide, maintaining the pressure, stirring in the second supercritical state, passing carbon dioxide, maintaining the pressure, stirring in the third supercritical state, passing carbon dioxide, maintaining the pressure, adding the flocculant, separating, collecting the filtrate and residue (B), (c) combining the filter residue (A) and (B), adding water, dispersant, whitening agent and chelating agent, heating, heat-preserving, filtering, using water to beat, filtering, and repeating the beating operation to obtain beaten filter residue, (d) drying the beaten residue to obtain powder, and (e) crushing the powder, packing in barrels, and packaging.
   USE - The method is useful for producing light calcium carbonate using industrial alkali residue white slurry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively removes elements such as chloride ions and sodium ions, and improves the purity of the produced calcium carbonate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing light calcium carbonate using industrial alkali residue white slurry, involves (a) controlling alkali residue white slurry water content to 35-45%, adding calcium oxide, adjusting pH to 9.5-10, adding electrolyte solution, stirring the mixture, leaving still, separating, and collecting filtrate and filter residue (A), (b) introducing the filtrate into an autoclave, which is first supercritical state, heat-preserving at 32-35 degrees C, stirring for 2-3 hours, passing carbon dioxide, maintaining the pressure in the kettle at 7.5-7.8 MPa for 2-3 hours, stirring at 40-45 degrees C for 1 hour in the second supercritical state, passing carbon dioxide, maintaining the pressure in the kettle at 8-10 MPa for 1-2 hours, stirring at 45-50 degrees C for 1 hour in the third supercritical state, passing carbon dioxide, maintaining the pressure in the kettle at 10-11 MPa for 0.5-1 hour, adding the flocculant, stirring, leaving still, separating, collecting the filtrate and residue (B), (c) combining the filter residue (A) and (B), adding 10 wt.% water, dispersant, whitening agent and chelating agent, heating at 50-60 degrees C, heat-preserving, filtering, using 5 wt.% water to beat, filtering, and repeating the beating operation for 3-5 times to obtain beaten filter residue, (d) drying the beaten filter residue to obtain powder, and (e) crushing the powder twice, packing in barrels, and packaging to obtain the light calcium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The calcium oxide has a particle size of 1-3 cm. Preferred Composition: The electrolyte solution comprises 10% calcium acetate solution. Preferred Method: The step (a) involves adding 0.01-0.1 wt.% calcium acetate solution. Preferred Conditions: In step (d), the moisture content less than or equal to 0.5%. In step (c), the heat-preserving time is 2-3 hours. Preferred Components: The dispersant is disodium edetate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The whitening agent is styrene. The chelating agent is an amino acid chelating agent.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The flocculant is polyacrylamide.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)

PT P
PN CN111068745-A
TI Producing alpha-olefin involves contacting methane with steam to perform steam reforming reaction to obtain steam to be reformed into gas, and contacting methane and carbon dioxide to perform dry reforming reaction.
AU JIN C
   WU Y
   ZHANG R
   HOU C
   XIA G
   SUN X
   YAN Z
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC RES INST PETROLEUM PROCESSING (SNPC-C)
GA 202038123D
AB    NOVELTY - Producing alpha-olefin involves contacting methane with steam to perform steam reforming reaction to obtain steam to be reformed into gas. The methane and carbon dioxide are contacted to perform a dry reforming reaction to obtain dry weight integrated gas. The portion of the steam is recombined into gas and mixed portion of the dry weight into gas to prepare a Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction feed. The Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst precursor is reduced and activated to obtain a Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst. The Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction feed is contacted with the Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst to perform a Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction to obtain a Fischer-Tropsch synthesis product stream, and separated alpha-olefin, methane and carbon dioxide from the Fischer-Tropsch synthesis product stream.
   USE - Method for producing alpha-olefin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce alpha-olefin which can improve the utilization rate of two greenhouse gases, such as carbon dioxide and methane, in the production process of alpha-olefin, so as to transform it into a product with high added value, reduces greenhouse gas emissions, reduce system energy consumption, and significantly increases overall process resource and energy utilization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing alpha-olefin involves contacting methane with steam to perform steam reforming reaction to obtain steam to be reformed into gas. The methane and carbon dioxide are contacted to perform a dry reforming reaction to obtain dry weight integrated gas. The portion of the steam is recombined into gas and mixed portion of the dry weight into gas to prepare a Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction feed. The Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst precursor is reduced and activated to obtain a Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst. The Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction feed is contacted with the Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst to perform a Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction to obtain a Fischer-Tropsch synthesis product stream, and separated alpha-olefin, methane and carbon dioxide from the Fischer-Tropsch synthesis product stream. The Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst precursor includes a carrier and active metal oxides and auxiliary metal oxides supported on the carrier. The carrier is a manganese oxide molecular sieve. The active metal in the active metal oxide is a group-VIII metal, and the auxiliary metal in the auxiliary metal oxide is a group-IIB metal, alkaline earth metal, and group-IB metal. The reduction activation includes pre-reducing the Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst precursor in the first gas to obtain a pre-reduction catalyst. The pre-reduction catalyst is re-reduced in the second gas to obtain a Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The active metal is one or more of iron, cobalt, nickel, preferably iron. The auxiliary metal is one or more of zinc, cadmium, copper, silver, preferably zinc and/or copper, more preferably zinc. The second gas is a gaseous hydrocarbon or a mixed gas of a gaseous hydrocarbon and an inert gas. The gaseous hydrocarbons are gaseous alkanes and/or gaseous olefins, preferably one or two or more selected from 1-4C alkanes and/or 2-4C olefins, and further selected from methane, ethane, ethylene, propylene, propane, butane, and butene. Preferred Compositions: The content of the active metal oxide is 3-70 wt.%, preferably 5-50 wt.%, more preferably 8-30 wt.%. The content of the auxiliary metal oxide is 1-60 wt.%, preferably 2-50 wt.%. The content of the carrier is 12-94 wt.%, preferably 35-91 wt.%. The metal molar ratio of the active metal and the auxiliary metal in the impregnating liquid is 1:0.2-5, preferably 1:0.3-3. The molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide is 0.4-3:1, preferably 0.6-2.8: 1, more preferably 0.8-2.6:1. Preferred Process: The catalyst is prepared by loading the active metal and the auxiliary metal on the carrier, and then sequentially drying and calcining to obtain the supported catalyst. The loading is carried out by dipping method or co-precipitation method. The impregnation method is equal volume impregnation method or saturation impregnation method. The impregnation method includes dissolving the soluble salt of the active metal and the auxiliary metal in a solvent to obtain an impregnating solution, and impregnating the carrier in the impregnating solution. The impregnation is one-time impregnation or step impregnation. The production method includes recycling the separated methane for the steam reforming reaction and/or the dry reforming reaction, and recycling the separated carbon dioxide for the dry reforming reaction. The soluble salt of the active metal and the auxiliary metal are nitrate or hydrochloride.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC A01-D13; E10-J02C3; E11-D02; E11-E02; E11-F03; E11-W; E34-A; E34-B01; E34-D01; E34-D03C; E34-D03D; E34-E01; E34-E02; E35; E35-U01; H04-E04; H04-E05; H04-F02E; H04-F03; H04-F05; H04-F06; J04-E01; J04-E03; J04-E04A; J04-E11; N01-B; N02-A01; N02-B01; N02-C01; N02-D01; N02-E; N02-F03; N02-F05; N03-A01; N03-A02; N03-F; N06-E01; N06-F; N06-G; N07-B02; N07-C02; N07-D03; N07-K01

PN CN110616090-A
TI Method for in-situ conversion and disposal of by-product tar, involves obtaining heavy tar and separating by-product tar from gas generator of gas generator, followed by distilling with hot gas in furnace, and generating hot flue gas.
AU YUAN W
   LI J
AE TANGSHAN KEYUAN ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY (TANG-Non-standard)
GA 202003545N
AB    NOVELTY - A by-product tar in-situ conversion and disposal method involves obtaining heavy tar, separating the by-product tar from the gas generator is in the retorting section of the gas generator, distilling it with hot gas in the furnace, going down to the bitumen of the distillation product with the coal material to the gasification reaction section to produce gas with carbon monoxide and hydrogen, forming an other part of the distillation product to light tar, and returning to the tar pool to participate in tar separation, generating the hot flue gas, bringing the physical heat of the hot flue gas and carbon dioxide into the gas with the gasifier in a furnace, introducing the physical heat by the hot flue gas supplements a part of the reaction heat required for the gasification reaction, carrying the carbon dioxide by participating the hot flue gas in the gasification reaction, which is offset a part of the exothermic heat of coal combustion and generating carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for in-situ conversion and disposal of by-product tar.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables in-situ conversion and disposal of by-product tar with saving coal resources.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A by-product tar in-situ conversion and disposal method involves obtaining heavy tar, separating the by-product tar from the gas generator is in the retorting section of the gas generator, distilling it with 500-600 degrees C hot gas in the furnace, going down to the bitumen of the distillation product with the coal material to the gasification reaction section for gasification to produce gas with carbon monoxide and hydrogen as the main combustible components, forming an other part of the distillation product to light tar, and returning to the tar pool to participate in tar separation and further conversion, generating the hot flue gas, after the combustion of light tar and air and water vapor constitute a gasifying agent, which is blasted into the furnace from the bottom of the gas generator to participate in the gasification reaction, bringing the physical heat of the hot flue gas and carbon dioxide into the gas with the gasifier in a furnace, introducing the physical heat by the hot flue gas supplements a part of the reaction heat required for the gasification reaction, carrying the carbon dioxide by participating the hot flue gas in the gasification reaction as a reaction substance, which is offset a part of the exothermic heat of coal combustion and generating carbon dioxide.

Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

PT P
PN CN109750432-A
TI Preparation of viscose-based activated carbon fiber cloth involves taking viscose fiber cloth, washing with distilled water, immersing in acetone, degreasing and dehydrating, drying, and soaking in immersion liquid.
AU GAO J
AE SHAOXING YONGZE TEXTILE CO LTD (SHAO-Non-standard)
GA 2019444615
AB    NOVELTY - A viscose-based activated carbon fiber cloth is prepared by taking viscose fiber cloth, washing with distilled water, immersing in acetone for 3-5 hours, degreasing and dehydrating, drying, soaking in 12-16 wt.% immersion liquid comprising 5-9 wt.% chloride and sulfate or phosphate or nitrate for 5-10 hours, raising temperature to 80-95 degrees C at rate of 1 degrees C/minute for 5-8 hours, taking out, drying at 125-140 degrees C, carbonizing under nitrogen gas atmosphere at rate of 5-8 degrees C/min to 250-300 degrees C for 15 minutes, raising temperature to 500-600 degrees C for 15 minutes at rate of 5 degrees C/minutes, introducing carbon dioxide for 15-20 minutes at 750-850 degrees C, feeding 1:4-1:2 mass ratio of carbon dioxide and water vapor mixture at rate of 8 degrees C/minute to 900-950 degrees C for 20-30 minutes, stopping heating, and carbon dioxide and water vapor mixture inlet, maintaining nitrogen gas, cooling to below 180 degrees C, taking out, air cooling, and drying.
   USE - Method for preparing viscose-based activated carbon fiber cloth.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high product yield with large specific surface area.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A viscose-based activated carbon fiber cloth is prepared by taking viscose fiber cloth, washing with distilled water, immersing in acetone for 3-5 hours, degreasing and dehydrating, drying, soaking in 12-16 wt.% immersion liquid comprising 5-9 wt.% chloride and sulfate or phosphate or nitrate for 5-10 hours, raising temperature to 80-95 degrees C at rate of 1 degrees C/minute for 5-8 hours, taking out, drying at 125-140 degrees C, carbonizing under nitrogen gas atmosphere at rate of 5-8 degrees C/min to 250-300 degrees C for 15 minutes, raising temperature to 500-600 degrees C for 15 minutes at rate of 5 degrees C/minutes, introducing carbon dioxide for 15-20 minutes at 750-850 degrees C, feeding 1:4-1:2 mass ratio of carbon dioxide and water vapor mixture at rate of 8 degrees C/minute to 900-950 degrees C for 20-30 minutes, stopping heating, and carbon dioxide and water vapor mixture inlet, maintaining nitrogen gas, cooling to below 180 degrees C, taking out, air cooling, and drying. The chloride is sodium chloride, zinc chloride or potassium chloride; sulfate is ammonium sulfate; phosphate is ammonium phosphate, ammonium dihydrogen phosphate or diammonium hydrogen phosphate; and nitrate is ammonium nitrate or magnesium nitrate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Components: The viscose fiber cloth is knitted plain or twill fabric woven from filament viscose fiber with weight of 200-700 g/m2.

PN CN208475719-U; CN110470073-A; CN113390139-A; CN110470073-B
TI Carbon dioxide heat pump system, has first branch provided with electromagnetic valve through pipeline, where electromagnetic valve is connected with high pressure oil divider, and second branch fixed on heat exchanger.
AU ZHAO J
   PAN Z
   NI M
   XU D
AE ZHEJIANG DUNAN ELECTRO-MECHANICAL TECHNOL (DUNN-C)
   ZHEJIANG DUNAN ELECTROMECHANICAL TECHNOL (DUNN-C)
   ZHEJIANG DUNAN ELECTRO-MECHANICAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (DUNN-C)
   ZHEJIANG DUNAN ELECTRO-MECHANICAL TECHNO (DUNN-C)
GA 201916221J
AB    (CN208475719-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a carbon dioxide heat pump system, comprising a gas side main loop, said gas side main loop comprises a pipeline connecting the compressor, a high-pressure oil separator, a gas cooler, a heat regenerator, a throttling device, an evaporator, the carbon dioxide heat pump system further comprises a high pressure oil return loop, the high pressure return oil loop comprises divided into two parallel branches, the first branch comprising a fourth electromagnetic valve and the third capillary connecting through the pipeline of the outlet of the third electromagnetic valve is connected with the high pressure oil divider connection, third electromagnetic valve, the second branch comprises a connecting heat exchanger on the second branch pipe, the two parallel branch to the compressor inlet. The utility model uses the high pressure oil separator separating the refrigerant from the refrigeration oil, oil content in the gas cooler is reduced, can improve heat exchange effect, so as to improve the system heating capacity and energy efficiency.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of a carbon dioxide heat pump system.
EA    NOVELTY - The system has a gas side main loop provided with a compressor, a high-pressure oil separator, a gas cooler and a heat regenerator that are connected by a pipeline. The compressor compresses refrigerant gas into high-temperature and high-pressure gas, which is transmitted into a high-pressure oil divider. The high-pressure oil separator separates refrigerant from refrigerated oil such that the refrigerant gas is entered into the gas cooler as low-temperature high pressure gas or liquid after heat exchange. A first electromagnetic valve is divided into two parallel branch circuits. The first branch circuit is provided with a branch pipeline connected with a second electromagnetic valve and a capillary tube. The second branch circuit is provided with a branch pipeline connected to the heat exchanger. The branch circuits are commonly connected to an inlet of the compressor.
   USE - Carbon dioxide heat pump system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system separates the refrigerant from the refrigeration oil by the high-pressure oil separator such that oil content in the gas cooler is reduced, thus improving heat exchange effect, system heating capacity and energy efficiency.

PT P
PN WO2018190737-A1; PL421318-A1; EP3609839-A1; US2020115249-A1; US10919774-B2; EP3609839-B1
TI Management of phosphogypsum involves charging reactor with apatite and/or phosphorite phosphogypsum and with aqueous or ammoniacal solution of ammonium carbonate and/or bicarbonate, stirring entire content and filtering resultant suspension.
AU TOCZEK A
   MERKA W
   GOSCINIAK P
   GOSCINIAK M
AE FERIN SP ZOO (FERI-Non-standard)
   FERIN SP ZOO (FERI-Non-standard)
   FERIN SP ZOO (FERI-Non-standard)
   FERIN SP ZOO (FERI-Non-standard)
   FERIN SP ZOO (FERI-Non-standard)
GA 201881975B
AB    NOVELTY - Management of phosphogypsum, comprises: charging a reactor (2) with apatite and/or phosphorite phosphogypsum and with an aqueous or ammoniacal solution of ammonium carbonate and/or bicarbonate from a pre-reactor (1), at 1:(0.1-4) ratio of phosphogypsum to the aqueous or ammoniacal solution of ammonium carbonate and/or bicarbonate; stirring the entire contents at -10 degrees C to 200 degrees C for at least 2 minutes, carbon dioxide being released in the course of the process is directed to the pre-reactor, and the post-reaction mixture is directed to a filter (3) to obtain an aqueous ammonium sulfate solution; and filtering the resultant suspension through a filter to obtain an aqueous calcium nitrate solution, and carbon dioxide being released in the reactor is recirculated to the pre-reactor where carbon dioxide is reacted with ammonia in an aqueous solution to obtain the aqueous or ammoniacal solution of carbonate and/or bicarbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for management of phosphogypsum.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is efficient, reproducible and cost-effective; and reduces the amounts of this waste material directed to dumping grounds.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Management of phosphogypsum, comprises: charging a reactor (2) with apatite and/or phosphorite phosphogypsum and with an aqueous or ammoniacal solution of ammonium carbonate and/or bicarbonate from a pre-reactor (1), at 1:(0.1-4) ratio of phosphogypsum to the aqueous or ammoniacal solution of ammonium carbonate and/or bicarbonate; stirring the entire contents at -10 degrees C to 200 degrees C for at least 2 minutes, carbon dioxide being released in the course of the process is directed to the pre-reactor, and the post-reaction mixture is directed to a filter (3) to obtain an aqueous ammonium sulfate solution, whereas the precipitate from the filter is heat-treated, followed by dissolving it in nitric acid in a reactor; and filtering the resultant suspension through a filter to obtain an aqueous calcium nitrate solution, and carbon dioxide being released in the reactor is recirculated to the pre-reactor where carbon dioxide is reacted with ammonia in an aqueous solution to obtain the aqueous or ammoniacal solution of carbonate and/or bicarbonate which is directed to the reactor, with the process for obtaining the aqueous or ammoniacal solution of ammonium carbonate and/or bicarbonate being conducted until the pH 7-12 of the solution is reached.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of how to carry out the method.
   Pre-reactor (1)
   Reactor (2)
   Filter (3)
   Oven (4)
   Mixer (7)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method: the reactor is charged with an aqueous or ammoniacal ammonium carbonate and/or bicarbonate solution of the total concentration of 10-50 wt.% from the pre-reactor; the contents of the reactor are sonicated for 1 minute per every 10 minutes of the duration of the process to the continuous sonication; the precipitate from the filter is heat-treated in an oven (4) at 40-800 degrees C; residual gases from the heat treatment of the precipitate from the filter are directed to the pre-reactor; the precipitate from the filter after the heat treatment is mixed with an aqueous solution of calcium nitrate before being dissolved in the reactor; and the filtrate from the filter is directed to a mixer (7) where the pH value of the filtrate is adjusted to 3-7, whereas the residual carbon dioxide being released is introduced to the pre-reactor.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).)

PT P
PN CN108046278-A; CN108046278-B
TI Producing high specific surface area silica involves formulating different concentrations of salt solution and silicon source aqueous solution, adding silicon source solution to salt solution, adjusting temperature of reaction system.
AU GUO Z
   WANG C
   WANG J
AE HEFEI XINGCHAO ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (HEFE-Non-standard)
GA 2018413405
AB    NOVELTY - Producing high specific surface area silica involves formulating different concentrations of salt solution and silicon source aqueous solution, adding silicon source solution to salt solution, where concentration of silicon in the silicon source solution is 0.05-0.30 moles/L, and concentration of the salt solution is 0.1-4.0 moles/L. The temperature of the reaction system is adjusted to 0-90 degrees C. The carbon dioxide gas is adjusted and flow rate of the silicon source solution is to passed the carbon dioxide gas and silicon source liquid simultaneously through the three-way flow of the continuous flow reactor to genrate gas-liquid alternate segment flow. The liquid portion separated by carbon dioxide bubble is called liquid bomb having length of 1-10 millimeter and uniform size, these liquid bullets roll forward in small-scale reaction pipeline of continuous flow reactor, and precipitation increases with the flow of liquid bomb. The precipitate and the aqueous solution are collected.
   USE - Method for producing high specific surface area silica.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce high specific surface area silica which prepares small particles and stable product quality of silica.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing high specific surface area silica involves formulating different concentrations of salt solution and silicon source aqueous solution, adding silicon source solution to salt solution, where concentration of silicon in the silicon source solution is 0.05-0.30 moles/L, and concentration of the salt solution is 0.1-4.0 moles/L. The temperature of the reaction system is adjusted to 0-90 degrees C. The carbon dioxide gas is adjusted and flow rate of the silicon source solution is to passed the carbon dioxide gas and silicon source liquid simultaneously through the three-way flow of the continuous flow reactor to genrate gas-liquid alternate segment flow. The liquid portion separated by the carbon dioxide bubble is called liquid bomb having length of 1-10 millimeter and uniform size, these liquid bullets roll forward in the small-scale reaction pipeline of the continuous flow reactor, and precipitation increases with the flow of the liquid bomb. The precipitate and the aqueous solution are collected at the outlet of the tubular reactor to obtain precipitated silica. Precipitated silica is obtained by filtration or centrifugation, and washed with purified water or dilute hydrochloric acid for 2-4 times. The salt solution can be reused after dilute acid adjustment and distillation. The washed silica is dried at 50-150 degrees C for 8-12 hours and ground to obtain high specific surface area silica.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The salt solution comprises one of an alkali metal salt, alkaline earth metal salt and ammonium salt. The silicon source uses sodium silicate or silica sol or mixture of both. Preferred Condition: The flow rate of carbon dioxide gas and silicon source solution is controlled at 0.1-100 mL/minute. The outlet of the tubular reactor is directly connected with the filtration device. The precipitated silica is obtained by direct online filtration, and the unreacted carbon dioxide is recovered by compression. The specific surface area of silica obtained by the preparation method is in the range of 500 to 800 m2/gram.

PN CN114405215-A
TI Method for capturing carbon dioxide from flue of kiln, involves collecting carbon dioxide in valley, dense forest, depression or underground cave, where carbon dioxide capture site is closer to carbon dioxide storage site.
AU WANG Z
   WANG Y
AE WANG Z (WANG-Individual)
   WANG Y (WANG-Individual)
GA 2022641609
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves collecting carbon dioxide in valley, dense forest, depression or underground cave. Carbon dioxide capture site is closer to a carbon dioxide storage site. The carbon dioxide is extracted by using a movable air pumping device or an air pumping system, which is provided with a fixed air pumping device and a floral tube.
   USE - Method for capturing carbon dioxide from a flue of a kiln.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables avoiding problem in capturing of carbon dioxide at high temperature kiln so as to greatly reduce trapping difficulty of carbon dioxide, thus simplifying trapping process, and hence reducing energy consumption, carbon dioxide emission and collecting cost. The method enables collecting carbon dioxide in a valley, dense forest, lowland or underground cave at higher concentration, thus ensuring that concentration of the captured carbon dioxide.

PN CN114736105-A
TI Catalytic synthesis of anisole by reacting            dimethyl carbonate and phenol under catalysis of            calcium-aluminum composite oxide, which is prepared by            adding calcium hydroxide and aluminum hydroxide to            water, feeding carbon dioxide, reacting, dehydrating            and roasting calcium-aluminum hydrotalcite.
AU ZHU L
   YANG Y
   ZHOU X
AE YANG Y (YANG-Individual)
GA 202295895P
AB    NOVELTY - A method for catalytically synthesizing                anisole using calcium- aluminum composite oxide                comprises reacting dimethyl carbonate and phenol as                raw materials under the catalysis of                calcium-aluminum composite oxide to synthesize                anisole. The preparation of the calcium-aluminum                composite oxide comprises (S1) adding calcium                hydroxide and aluminum hydroxide into a reactor                containing water, stirring and mixing, heating to                60-100&#8451;,feeding carbon dioxide, and                reacting for 0.5-5 hours, (S2) stop feeding carbon                dioxide, continuously reacting at 60-100&#8451;                for 10-30 hours, dehydrating and drying the solid                to obtain calcium-aluminum hydrotalcite, and (S3)                roasting the obtained calcium-aluminum hydrotalcite                for 1-8 hours, and crushing to obtain                calcium-aluminum composite oxide.
   USE - The method is used for synthesis of anisole                using calcium- aluminium composite oxide                catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieves efficient and clean                preparation of anisole.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ratio of dimethyl                  carbonate to phenol is 1.8-3: 1. The mass ratio                  of the calcium- aluminum composite oxide to                  phenol is 1-5: 100.Preferred Method: The catalytic reaction is                  carried out at 190-220&#8451; for 2-6                  hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mol ratio of the                  calcium hydroxide to the aluminum hydroxide in                  step (S1) is 1.7-2.3: 1. The mass ratio of water                  to aluminum hydroxide in step (S1) is 10-60:                  1.Preferred Method: The pressure of carbon                  dioxide in step (S1) is 0.1-3 MPa. The roasting                  process in step (S3) is carried out at                  400-800&#8451;.

AE FENG Y (FENG-Individual)

The ore                material roasted is cooled under an inert gas                condition to 200-400 &#176;C, and the                temperature is kept for 20-40 minutes. PN CN114505055-A
TI Sludge-based biological carbon, obtained by            performing hydrothermal carbonization with mixture of            sludge and rice husk as raw material, and performing            acid washing activation and amino modification.
AU ZHU H
   RAO P
   JU R
   ZHOU H
   WANG X
   ZHANG L
   ZHANG Z
AE UNIV HENAN URBAN CONSTR (UHUC-C)
GA 202272190F
AB    NOVELTY - Sludge-based biological carbon, obtained by                performing hydrothermal carbonization with mixture                of sludge and rice husk as raw material, and                performing acid washing activation and amino                modification.
   USE - The biological carbon is useful in adsorbing                carbon dioxide in flue gas after combustion                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The biological carbon improves the specific                surface area and micropore structure of                sludge-based biological carbon, has a stronger                adsorption capacity of carbon dioxide, and has a                very high adsorption capacity and adsorption                efficiency in the adsorption of carbon dioxide in                the flue gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparing                sludge-based biological carbon, comprising (1)                mixing sludge and rice husk to obtain mixture, and                performing hydrothermal carbonization to obtain                carbonized product, and (2) placing carbonized                product in acid solution for dipping, placing in                organic amine solution for dipping, and vacuum                drying to obtain sludge-based biological                carbon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dry weight of the                  sludge and mixing mass ratio of the rice husk are                  10:2-4. In the step (2), the solute of the acid                  solution is hydrochloric acid, phosphoric acid,                  acetic acid, citric acid, hydrofluoric acid. The                  solvent is deionized water with concentration of                  0.5-2 mol/l. The solute of the organic amine                  solution is ethylene diamine, ethanolamine,                  diethanolamine, triethylamine, ethylamine. The                  solvent is ethanol and organic amine solution                  with concentration of 5-15 wt.%.Preferred Method: In the step (1), the                  hydrothermal carbonization conditions the                  temperature is 120-220&#8451;, and the time                  is 0.5-2 hours. In the step (2), the dipping time                  is 2-6 hours, the temperature is                  40-60&#8451;, and the dipping time is 6-12                  hours. In the step (1), the mixture is subjected                  to pretreatments before hydrothermal                  carbonization: slow stirring and mixing, followed                  by rapid stirring for 3-5 hours, and microwave                  ultrasonic pretreatment for 10-20 minutes. The                  slow stirring speed is 100-150 revolution/minute,                  the fast-stirring speed is 500-600                  revolution/minute, the microwave ultrasonic                  pretreatment temperature is 100-250&#8451;,                  and the ultrasonic frequency is 300-500 Hz. The                  mixture is subjected to pretreatment before                  microwave ultrasonic pretreatment is anaerobic                  fermentation at 40-50&#8451; for 3-5 days,                  slow stirring and mixing, and rapid stirring for                  3-5 hours.

The foaming in step (ii) is carried out at 120-140degrees Celsius for 10-20 seconds. The extruding into strips is carried out at 8-25 Mpa in a screw extruder at rotational speed 80-165 revolutions/minute and preferably the cooling rate of the die head of the screw extruder is 8-16degrees Celsius/minute. The method of preparing foaming material further comprises a step of cooling, cutting, polishing, welding and slicing after extruding into strips.

TI Improving yield of natural gas comprises subjecting coke oven gas to methanation, concentrating gas, collecting by pressure swing adsorption or membrane separation, recycling, supplementing carbon dioxide and circulating.
AU LIU W
   LI N
   ZHANG H
   DU X
   ZHANG L
   LIU J
AE DALIAN CATALYTIC ENG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (DALI-Non-standard)
   CHENGDU TONGCHUANG WEIYE NEW ENERGY TECH (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2021211859
AB    NOVELTY - Improving yield of natural gas comprises subjecting coke oven gas to methanation by three stages/four stages after purifying the coke oven gas, concentrating the gas generated after the last stage dehydration treatment of the methanation treatment, collecting by pressure swing adsorption or membrane separation, recycling the collected hydrogen in the methanation or purifying methanation process, supplementing the carbon dioxide in one stage methanation process, and circulating in the methanation reaction to improve the yield of the natural gas.
   USE - The method is useful for improving yield of natural gas and in the field of energy utilization.
   ADVANTAGE - The method completely uses the hydrogen in the coke oven gas; uses the carbon dioxide outside the boundary region; increases the methane content in the methanation process outlet gas by 2-25%; and ensures the total carbon conversion rate reaches to 99.997 % (residual carbon content is not more than 30ppm).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises subjecting the purified coke oven gas to three-stage methanation treatment, then dehydrating, concentrating and collecting hydrogen by pressure swing adsorption or membrane separation, collecting hydrogen to return to the first-stage methanation reaction, mixing, supplementing carbon dioxide before the first- stage methanation reaction and circulating in the methanation reaction to improve the yield of the natural gas. The method comprises subjecting the purified coke oven gas to three-stage methanation treatment, then dehydrating, concentrating and collecting hydrogen by pressure swing adsorption or membrane separation, collecting hydrogen to return to the first-stage methanation reaction, mixing, supplementing carbon dioxide before and secondary methanation reaction and circulating in the methanation reaction to improve the yield of the natural gas. The method comprises subjecting purified coke oven gas to three-stage methanation treatment, then dehydrating, concentrating and collecting hydrogen by pressure swing adsorption or membrane separation, returning 20-50% hydrogen to the inlet of the first-stage methanation, mixing with the carbon dioxide supplied outside the boundary region, entering into the first- stage methanation section, returning the remaining hydrogen to the inlet of the secondary methanation, mixing with the carbon dioxide supplied by the boundary region and then entering into the secondary methanation section, where the ratio of the flow rate of the carbon dioxide and the return hydrogen flow rate is 0.2-1.5, and circulating in the methanation reaction to improve the yield of the natural gas. Preferred Components: The recycling flow rate of the recycling hydrogen gas is 5-30% of the flow rate of the raw material gas. The volume ratio of the flow rate of the added carbon dioxide and the flow rate of the recycled hydrogen is 0.2-1.5.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nonionic                  surfactant is polysorbate-80 and/or                  polysorbate-20. PN CN110156049-A
TI Preparing calcium cyanamide by adding calcium oxide to high-boiling point solvent, introducing ammonia gas and carbon dioxide while stirring, filtering and roasting filter residues in nitrogen atmosphere under high-temperature condition.
AU CHENG Z
   CHANG Z
   ZHANG J
   MA Y
   LUAN B
AE CHAMBROAD CHEM IND RES INST CO LTD (CHAM-Non-standard)
GA 201977167K
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing calcium cyanamide by solvent method involves using calcium oxide, ammonia gas and carbon dioxide as raw materials and obtaining calcium cyanamide by two-step reaction involving (i) firstly adding calcium oxide to a high-boiling point solvent, introducing ammonia gas and carbon dioxide while stirring and filtering to obtain filter residues after completing the reaction and (ii) roasting the filter residues in a nitrogen atmosphere under the high-temperature condition.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing calcium cyanamide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problem of insufficient contact of gas-solid heterogeneous reaction and non-uniform reaction, reduces reaction temperature, reaction time and roasting temperature of precursor, adopts widely available raw materials, is cost-effective, environmentally-friendly and suitable for industrial promotion and application and improves quality of the calcium cyanamide product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The calcium oxide is chosen from calcium oxide, calcium hydroxide (preferred) and calcium carbonate. Preferred composition: The calcium oxide, ammonia gas and carbon dioxide are in a molar ratio of 1:(2-10):(1-10), preferably 1:2:4. Preferred Method: The step (i) involves reacting at 150-280 degrees C, preferably 250 degrees C for 3-5 hours, preferably 4 hours. The step (ii) involves roasting at 500-650 degrees C, preferably 600 degrees C for 0.5-1.5 hours, preferably 1 hour.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred components: The high boiling point solvent is chosen from N,N-dimethylformamide, dimethyl sulfoxide and glycerin.

PT P
PN CN109701495-A
TI Preparation of mesoporous zinc-zeolitic imidazolate framework material for adsorbing carbon dioxide, involves adding zinc acetate dihydrate and polyvinylpyrrolidone to methanol solution, and dissolving 2-methylimidazole in methanol solution.
AU XIE Y
   ZENG D
   LING Y
AE UNIV NANCHANG HANGKONG (UYNH-C)
GA 2019423916
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of mesoporous zinc-zeolitic imidazolate framework material, involves (1) adding zinc acetate dihydrate and polyvinylpyrrolidone to a methanol solution, and uniformly mixing, (2) dissolving 2-methylimidazole in a methanol solution, (3) mixing the two solutions obtained in the steps (1) and (2), stirring at 50-90 degrees C for a period of time, separating the solid matter, washing and drying, and (4) placing zinc-zeolitic imidazolate framework material in a tube furnace under the protection of an inert gas at a temperature of 500-900 degrees C and calcining in a tube furnace for 5-12 hours.
   USE - Preparation of mesoporous zinc-zeolitic imidazolate framework material for adsorbing carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The mesoporous zinc-zeolitic imidazolate framework material has strong carbon dioxide adsorption capacity at normal temperature, excellent environmental protection and greenhouse effect control, and wide development space in heavy metal adsorption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The grade pure methanol is analyzed. Preferred Composition: The ratio of the amount of the zinc acetate dihydrate to the 2-methylimidazole is 1:1, 1:2, 1:4, 1:6. Preferred Conditions: The stirring time in the step (3) is 24 hours. The temperature increase rate in the step (4) is 5 degrees C/minute.

PT P
PN CN109675526-A; CN109675526-B
TI Ultramicroporous metal organic framework material for separating and purifying carbon dioxide, comprises zinc, oxalic acid, and 3-methyl-1H-1,2,4-triazole.
AU LI Z
   PENG J
   XIAO J
   ZHOU X
   XIA Q
   XI H
   WU Y
   XIAN S
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 2019407904
AB    NOVELTY - A ultramicroporous metal organic framework material comprises zinc, oxalic acid, and 3-methyl-1H-1,2,4-triazole.
   USE - Ultramicroporous metal organic framework material is used for separating and purifying carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The ultramicroporous metal organic framework material has excellent water stability, achieves preferential adsorption of carbon dioxide and has high adsorption amount, and the adsorption of methane, acetylene, nitrogen, ethylene, ethane and other gases is completely eliminated.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A ultramicroporous metal organic framework material is of formula: Zn(ox)0.5(mtz), where ox is oxalic acid, and mtz is 3-methyl-1H-1,2,4-triazole. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of ultramicroporous metal organic framework material, which involves (1) adding oxalic acid, 3-methyl-1H-1,2,4-triazole and zinc metal salts into water or a mixed solvent of water and alcohol, mixing well, adding the mixture into the autoclave for reaction, obtaining crude Zn(ox)0.5(mtz), filtering, and purifying by washing with water and methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The crystal of the material is monoclinic system. The space group is P2/1c, The unit cell parameters a is 8.7482, b is 8.3396, c is 10.2638, alpha and gamma is 90 degrees , and gamma is 100.845. Preferred Component: The zinc metal salt is zinc carbonate or basic zinc carbonate. The alcohol is an alcohol having 1-4C. The carbon dioxide is separated and purified in carbon dioxide and methane mixture, carbon dioxide and acetylene mixture, carbon dioxide and ethylene mixture, and carbon dioxide and ethane mixture. Preferred Condition: The temperature of the reaction is 130-200 degrees C. The reaction time is 24-96 hours. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of zinc metal ion, oxalic acid and 3-methyl-1H-1,2,4-triazole in the zinc metal salt is 1:(0.5-2):(1-10).
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN TW201905393-A
TI Air conditioner capable of lowering carbon dioxide concentration in enclosed space includes an air conditioner having an air blowing channel, and a ventilation device having a functional layer provided with a peroxide metal.
AU TSAUR G
   TSAUR F
   WANG T
   TSAUR N J
AE TSAUR G (TSAU-Individual)
   TSAUR F (TSAU-Individual)
   WANG T (WANG-Individual)
   TSAUR N J (TSAU-Individual)
GA 2019371439
AB    NOVELTY - This invention relates to an air conditioner capable of lowering carbon dioxide concentration in an enclosed space, which includes an air conditioner, the air conditioner has an air blowing channel, and the air conditioner is disposed with a ventilation device inside the air blowing channel; the ventilation device has a functional layer, and the functional layer has functions of lowering humidity and generating oxygen; the functional layer is provided with a peroxide metal. Accordingly, with the ventilation device being disposed with the functional layer capable of lowering the carbon dioxide concentration and generating oxygen, the air quality introduced into the air blowing channel can be improved. The peroxide metal adopted in the functional layer is in a powder or fine-particle state, thereby being able to be coated on a surface of the functional layer.

PN CN109239258-A; CN209148629-U
TI Combustion system for simulating real oxygen-enriched combustion, has gas-supplying device for supplying oxygen and carbon dioxide to combustion device, and steam supply device supplies water vapor to combustion device.
AU ZHANG X
   YANG E
   HAO Y
   WU X
AE CHINA ENFI ENG CORP (CMEG-C)
GA 201913134R
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an air inlet pipeline connected with an air outlet pipeline. A gas-supplying device is provided with an oxygen supply component, a carbon dioxide supply subassembly, a carbon monoxide supply assembly, a nitric oxide supply assembly and a sulfur dioxide supply subassembly. An air supply device is connected on the air inlet pipeline. A steam supply device is connected to a lower part of a combustion device. The gas-supplying device supplies oxygen and carbon dioxide to the combustion device according to predetermined volume through the air inlet pipeline. The steam supply device supplies water vapor to the combustion device.
   USE - Combustion system for simulating real oxygen-enriched combustion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for researching generated change pollutant in an oxygen-enriched combustion process.

PN CN109189128-A; CN209149167-U
TI Automatic environment monitoring and controlling device, has executing unit provided with oxygen concentration adjustor, carbon dioxide concentration adjustor, temperature adjustor and humidity adjustor.
AU WU F
   ZHOU Y
   CHENG Q
   QIN Y
   YANG J
   CAO Z
   YU M
AE AEROMEDICINE INST PLA (AERO-Non-standard)
GA 201909122S
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a gas sampling unit and an executing unit that are connected to a CPU. The gas sampling unit processes and outputs a control signal to the executing unit. The gas sampling unit is provided with an air inlet pipe and an air pump. An air outlet of the air pump is provided with an oxygen concentration sensor, a carbon dioxide concentration sensor, a temperature sensor and a humidity sensor. The air pump controls sample gas flow under air environment.
