PN CN111217332-A; CN211896032-U
TI Pressure swing absorption separation system useful for producing hydrogen phase products by supercritical water gasification, comprises e.g. heat exchanger comprises hot fluid side inlet connected to supercritical water gasification reactor.
AU WANG S
   XU T
   LI Y
   LI J
   ZHANG Y
   JIANG Z
   CUI C
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
   UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 202051606K
AB    NOVELTY - Pressure swing absorption separation system comprises a heat exchanger (2) comprises hot fluid side inlet is connected to the supercritical water gasification reactor (1) outlet. The outlet of the heat fluid side of the heat exchanger is connected to the inlet of the three-phase separator (3). The reaction materials enter the supercritical water gasification reactor along the cold fluid side of the heat exchanger. The liquid phase and solid phase outlets of the three-phase separator are connected to the subsequent units. The gas phase outlet is connected to the inlet of the pressure regulator (13). The outlet of the pressure regulator is connected to the inlet of the high-pressure water absorber one (4). The gas phase outlet of high-pressure water absorber one is divided into two paths. The liquid phase outlet of the high-pressure water absorber one is connected to the inlet of the carbon dioxide analyzer through the liquid pressure reducing valve.
   USE - The pressure swing absorption separation system is useful for producing hydrogen phase products by supercritical water gasification (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system: can realize pressure swing absorption and separation of gas-phase products under high-pressure conditions and improves the energy utilization efficacy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Pressure swing absorption separation system comprises a heat exchanger (2) comprises hot fluid side inlet is connected to the supercritical water gasification reactor (1) outlet. The outlet of the heat fluid side of the heat exchanger is connected to the inlet of the three-phase separator (3). The reaction materials enter the supercritical water gasification reactor along the cold fluid side of the heat exchanger. The liquid phase and solid phase outlets of the three-phase separator are connected to the subsequent units. The gas phase outlet is connected to the inlet of the pressure regulator (13). The outlet of the pressure regulator is connected to the inlet of the high-pressure water absorber one (4). The gas phase outlet of high-pressure water absorber one is divided into two paths through gas path valve one and gas pressure reducing valve in turn, where one path is connected to high-purity hydrogen pipeline through gas path valve two. The other way is connected to the inlet of the depth separator through the gas path valve three. The liquid phase outlet of the high-pressure water absorber one is connected to the inlet of the carbon dioxide analyzer through the liquid pressure reducing valve. The gas-phase outlet of the carbon dioxide analyzer is connected to the gas pipeline mainly composed of carbon dioxide and the liquid-phase outlet of the carbon dioxide analyzer is connected back to the high-pressure water absorber one through the waterway valve one. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for pressure swing absorption separation method of the pressure swing absorption separation system based on the hydrogen phase product produced by supercritical water gasification, comprising (i) preheating the heat exchanger and passing the material into the supercritical water gasification reactor for supercritical water gasification reaction; (ii) passing the fluid coming out of the supercritical water gasification reactor into the heat exchanger to cool down to normal temperature; (iii) passing the fluid cooled by the heat exchanger into the three-phase separator for three-phase separation of gas, liquid and solid and passing the gas phase into the gas separation unit, and the liquid phase and solid phase into the subsequent processing unit; (iv) passing the gas phase separated by the three-phase separator into the series of high-pressure water absorber one for preliminary by-product carbon dioxide separation; (v ) adjusting the water valve one to control the amount of high-pressure water required; (vi) if the gas-phase product from the high-pressure water absorber one needs to be further processed further, it passes through the pressure-reducing valve and passing the deep separator to carry out fine separation of the product to obtain high-purity hydrogen, carbon monoxide and methane mixed combustible gas components, and high-purity carbon dioxide; (vii) Vacuum extracting the liquid phase in the medium and high pressure water absorber one by the pressure reducing valve and passing the carbon dioxide analyzer for gas-liquid separation to obtain a gas mainly composed of carbon dioxide; and (viii) injecting the liquid phase water separated by the carbon dioxide analyzer back into the high-pressure water absorber one to establish the circulation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic representation of pressure swing absorption separation system.
   Supercritical water gasification reactor (1)
   Heat exchanger (2)
   Three-phase separator (3)
   High-pressure water absorber one (4)
   Pressure regulator (Drawing includes non-English language text). (13)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The gas-phase outlet of the high-pressure water absorber one is divided into two paths, where one is connected to the inlet of the gas path valve one and the other is connected to the high-pressure water absorber two through the gas path valve four, the gas phase outlet of the high-pressure water absorber two is connected to the inlet of the gas pressure reducing valve, the liquid phase outlet of the high-pressure water absorber two is connected to the inlet of the carbon dioxide analyzer through the liquid pressure reducing valve and the liquid phase outlet of the carbon dioxide analyzer is connected back to the high pressure through the waterway valve two and water absorber one. The more high-pressure water absorbers are connected in series after the second high-pressure water absorber. A high-pressure water pump is provided at the liquid phase outlet of the carbon dioxide analyzer. The inlet of the high-pressure water absorber one is provided with a gas composition detector one, a gas component detector two is provided at the gas phase outlet, a gas composition detector three is provided at the inlet of the gas pressure reducing valve, determining the gas composition and the pressure of each separator according to each gas composition detector, using the pressure regulator and high-pressure water pump to adjust the pressure of the separator to the required index, and use the waterway valve one, water valve two regulates the amount of high-pressure water required by the system, and uses gas circuit valve one and gas circuit valve four to control the number of times the gas undergoes high-pressure water absorption treatment. The depth separator is a low-pressure gas separator, and the separated gas is divided into three channels which are respectively connected to a high-purity hydrogen pipeline, a carbon monoxide and methane mixing pipeline, and a high-purity carbon dioxide pipeline. The low-pressure gas separator is a pressure swing adsorption separator or a membrane separator. The one of the gas-phase outlets of the high-pressure water absorber one is connected to the inlet of the high-pressure water absorber two through the gas path valve four thus realizes a series of high-pressure water absorber two after high-pressure water absorber one, the liquid phase outlet of the carbon dioxide analyzer is connected back to the high-pressure water absorber two through the waterway valve two, the liquid phase outlet of the carbon dioxide analyzer is provided with a high-pressure water pump to absorb high-pressure water, a gas composition detector one is provided at the inlet of the device one, the gas phase outlet is provided with a gas composition detector two, the gas pressure reducing valve is provided with a gas composition detector three and the gas composition detector one, gas component detector two detects the gas component, and adjusts water channel valve one and water channel valve two to control the amount of high-pressure water required, according to the gas composition detected by the gas component detector two, the number of times the gas passes through the high-pressure water absorption treatment is controlled by controlling the gas circuit valve one and the gas circuit valve four, the hydrogen-rich gas after preliminary separation of the by-product carbon dioxide detects the gas composition through the gas composition detector three and controls the gas path valve two and the gas path valve three to control whether the separated gas needs to enter the depth separator, the liquid water separated by the carbon dioxide analyzer is pressurized by the high-pressure water pump and injected back into the high-pressure water absorber one and the high-pressure water absorber two to establish the circulation.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification.    GU Y
   CUI Q
   LI Y
AE SHENHUA GROUP CORP LTD (SHGR-C)
   NAT INST CLEAN & LOW CARBON ENERGY (CGUO-C)
   CHN ENERGY INVESTMENT GROUP CO LTD (SHGR-C)
GA 2018A26254
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing dialkyl carbonate comprises esterifying carbon dioxide-containing gas with monohydric alcohol in presence of amine absorbent, catalyst and dehydrating agent.
   USE - The dialkyl carbonate is useful in capturing carbon dioxide from gas containing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has improved conversion rate of reactants and selectivity of dialkyl carbonate; avoids carbon dioxide analysis process, and realizes carbon dioxide enrichment and utilization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for method of capturing carbon dioxide from gas containing carbon dioxide comprising fixing carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide-containing gas to the dialkyl carbonate to achieve carbon dioxide capture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The weight ratio between monohydric alcohol and amine absorbent is 100:1-50, preferably 100:5-35. The weight ratio between monohydric alcohol and catalyst is 100:1-20, preferably 100:8-14. The weight ratio between monohydric alcohol and dehydrating agent is 100:1-100, preferably 100:30-70. The carbon dioxide-containing gas is industrial waste gas having carbon dioxide content of less than or equal to 30%. The monohydric alcohol is 1-8C monohydric alcohol, preferably methanol, ethanol, n-propanol, isopropanol and/or n-butanol; amine absorbent is monoethanolamine, diethanolamine, triethanolamine, isopropanolamine, diisopropanolamine, diglycolamine, 1-amino-2-propanol, 3-amino-2-propanol, 2-methylaminoethanol, 2-ethylaminoethanol, 2-propylaminoethanol, 2-butylaminoethanol, N,N-dimethylethanolamine, N,N-diethylethanolamine, 1-dimethylamino-2-propanol, 3-dimethylamino-1-propanol, 2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol, 2-amino-2hydroxymethyl-1,3-propanediol, 2-amino-2-methyl-1,3-propanediol, N-methyldiethanolamine, N-ethyl diethanolamine, N-methyl diallylamine, diethylenetriamine, triethylenetetramine, tetraethylenepentamine, and/or pentaethylenehexamine; catalyst is potassium carbonate, zinc oxide, copper oxide, cerium oxide, zirconium oxide and/or iron oxide; and dehydrating agent is molecular sieve, acetonitrile, diazabicyclo, calcium chloride and/or silica gel. Preferred Method: The conditions of esterification reaction include temperature of 50-250 degrees C and time of 1-24 hours. The method comprises heating reaction system to temperature of the esterification reaction at heating rate of 2-20 degrees C/minute. The method comprises placing amine absorbent, catalyst, dehydrating agent and monohydric alcohol in closed reaction system before introducing the esterification reaction, introducing carbon dioxide-containing gas to system pressure of 0.1-20 MPa, and holding pressure, where conditions for holding the pressure include: temperature of 10-40 degrees C and time of 0.5-24 hours.

TI Inspection apparatus of carbon dioxide sensor, has supply cup which is installed at end of supply rod for covering carbon dioxide sensor so that carbon dioxide from supply rod is supplied to carbon dioxide sensor.
AU SEO D S
   LEE J G
   OH S W
AE LEE J G (LEEJ-Individual)
   FOURPS CO LTD (FOUR-Non-standard)
   SEO D S (SEOD-Individual)
GA 201844177M
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (100) has mass flowmeters (113,123) that regulate supply amount of carbon dioxide and a diluting gas. A first mixing chamber (130) mixes carbon dioxide and diluent gas supplied at a predetermined rate through the flowmeters. A second mixing chamber (140) mixes diluting gas and carbon dioxide to adjust the mixing ratio to the first chamber to supply a uniform concentration of carbon dioxide. A supply nozzle unit (180) supplies carbon dioxide whose concentration adjusted in the second chamber to a carbon dioxide sensor. A supply rod (181) of supply nozzle unit supplies carbon dioxide with adjusted concentration to the second chamber. A supply cup (183) is installed at an end of the supply rod for covering the carbon dioxide sensor so that the carbon dioxide from the supply rod is supplied to the carbon dioxide sensor.
   USE - Inspection apparatus of carbon dioxide sensor for air conditioning system installed at passenger room of railway vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide sensor is inspected effectively, since supply cup is installed at the end of the supply rod for covering the carbon dioxide sensor so that the carbon dioxide from the supply rod is supplied to the carbon dioxide sensor. The operation of carbon dioxide sensor is determined accurately. The emission of carbon dioxide from air conditioning system is reduced. The occurrence of safety accident is prevented. The consumption of diluting gas is reduced.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the inspection apparatus.

   First mixing chamber (130)
   Second mixing chamber (140)
   Supply nozzle unit (180)
   Supply rod (181)

TI Removing hydrogen, methane, carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide from cracked gases leaving cracking furnace comprises passing gases through transfer line having continuous passageway of metal, insert, and port through metal to withdraw gases.
AU BENUM L W
   KOSELEK M E
   SIMANZHENKOV V
   FARAG H I
   MAH E G
   KLUTHE J T
AE NOVA CHEM INT SA (NVCH-C)
GA 2019071187
AB    NOVELTY - Removing at least one of hydrogen, methane, carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide from cracked gases leaving a cracking furnace, comprises passing the gases through a transfer line, where the transfer line is between an outlet of a steam cracking furnace and an inlet to a quench exchanger and comprises: a continuous passageway of a metal having a melting temperature greater than 1000 degrees C having a flange at one end of the passageway adapted to cooperate with the outlet from the steam cracking furnace and a flange at the opposite end of the passageway adapted to cooperate with the inlet to the quench exchanger; at least one inserts in the passageway permitting a flow of gases through the passageway; and at least one port through the metal to withdraw gases from the gas tight chamber.
   USE - The method is useful for removing at least one of hydrogen, methane, carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide from cracked gases leaving a cracking furnace by passing the gases through a transfer line.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: perform the separation using membranes at elevated temperatures; removes the hydrogen at the temperature of reaction (200-700 degrees C) that improve the efficiency of the process in a water-gas-shift reactor; utilizes the robust ceramic membranes that will selectively transport hydrogen at up to 1000 degrees C and higher; and addresses the long-standing issue of ceramic durability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Removing at least one of hydrogen, methane, carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide from cracked gases leaving a cracking furnace, comprises passing the gases through a transfer line, where the transfer line is between an outlet of a steam cracking furnace and an inlet to a quench exchanger and comprises: a continuous passageway of a metal having a melting temperature greater than 1000 degrees C having a flange at one end of the passageway adapted to cooperate with the outlet from the steam cracking furnace and a flange at the opposite end of the passageway adapted to cooperate with the inlet to the quench exchanger; at least one inserts in the passageway permitting a flow of gases through the passageway, where the insert is permeable to at least one of hydrogen, methane, carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide at 500-900 degrees C and spaced from an interior wall of the passageway and sealed to the interior wall of the passageway to provide at least one gas tight chamber; and at least one port through the metal to withdraw gases from the gas tight chamber, where the insert comprises (a) ceramic inserts having a melting point greater than 900 degrees C and a porosity from 5-75% of pores having a size of 0.001-0.5 mu m and fitting within the metal casting and/or (b) metal inserts having a porosity of 5-75% of pores having a size of 0.001-0.5 mu m, and optionally a membrane permeable to at least one of hydrogen, methane, carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide at temperatures 500-900 degrees C on the insert to permit the passage of at least one of hydrogen, methane, carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide through into the gas tight chamber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The insert comprises a porous ceramic formed from oxides, dioxides, nitrides, carbides and phosphates comprising porous silicon dioxide, fused silicon dioxide, porous aluminum oxide, titanium dioxide, zirconium dioxide, thorium dioxide, lanthanum oxide, magnesium oxide, calcium oxide, barium oxide, tin oxide, cerium dioxide, zinc oxide, boron oxide, boron nitride, boron carbide, boron phosphate, zirconium phosphate, yttrium oxide, aluminum silicate, silicon nitride and/or silicon carbide. The insert comprises a metal alloy comprising at least one of iron, nickel, titanium, chromium, aluminum, chromium, nickel or molybdenum. The membrane is present and has a thickness of 0.1-10 mu m. The membrane is present and the membrane comprises at least one metal of palladium, tantalum, vanadium, platinum, niobium or zirconium. The membrane further comprises at least one metal oxide ceramic of aluminum oxide, barium titanate, strontium titanate or zirconium dioxide. The membrane comprises a dense metal oxide membrane. In the membrane the metal comprises palladium. The metal oxide comprises yttria stabilized zirconium dioxide or calcia stabilized zirconium dioxide. The membrane is not less than 95% of theoretical density. The membrane is coated on component (b), where the intermediate the ceramic oxide and component (b) comprise a ceramic comprising at least one particle of tungsten, alumina oxide, zirconia, titania, silicon carbide, chromium oxide or yttrium oxide, having a particles size of 0.01-5 mu m. The monomeric and/or oligomeric silazanes contain heteroatoms of boron, titanium, aluminum and/or phosphorus.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The insert is in the shape of a cone or an island.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The insert is resistant to coking. The membrane is coated on component (a), where the component comprises an alumina ceramic. The membrane is silicon/carbon/nitrogen ceramic is formed by combining a monomeric and/or oligomeric silazane ceramic precursor with a comonomer comprising at least one of the group comprising ene (vinyl) functionalized, oligomeric, inorganic or organic silazanes, difunctional thiols or tetrafunctional thiols, forming the combination as a thin film on a substrate, photopolymerizing the thin film, and pyrolyzing the photopolymerized thin film so as to result in a ceramic membrane that contains no oxide.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

   USE - The functional material product is useful in                the oil and gas energy fracturing mining.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacturing functional material comprises                using a compound comprising an olefin-containing                group and a fluorine-containing group as a raw                material, further comprises using a                silicon-containing group, aldose group-containing                compound as a raw material, (2) selecting raw                materials, 4-20 pts. wt. 3-(trimethoxysilyl)propyl                acrylate, 1-4 pts. wt. hexafluoropropylene, 0-35                pts. wt. L (+)-laural aldose, reacting under                0.01-0.55 pts. wt. catalyst, (3) using carbon                dioxide to remove oxygen and flushing into part of                carbon dioxide, using an organic solvent as the oil                phase, and controlling reaction temperature at 5-85                degrees C, the reaction sequence and parameters                comprises dissolving the 0.5-35 pts. wt.                L(+)-laural aldose in 0.5-2 pts. wt. water, adding                the peroxide catalyst at 5-20 degrees C, adding                4-20 pts. wt. 3-(trimethoxysilyl)propyl acrylate,                dissolving in 1-3 pts. wt. ethyl ether as an oil                phase, adding to the reaction vessel, and 1-4 pts.                wt. hexafluoropropylene, pumping in the azo                initiator, flushing into CO2 and reacting under                pressure and heating when the azo initiator is                added in half and reacted, reducing pressure, and                adding, the auxiliary aqueous phase in the above 1                is emulsified, the initiator is continuously pumped                and reacted for a period of time, and adding                hexafluoropropylene to the CO 2 to be slowly                pressurized and reacted under stirring for 3-10                hours until the reaction is completed and (4)                cooling to a normal temperature to obtain a                functional material product or reverse phase                dehydration drying to obtain a functional material                product. In                  the step (1) further comprises using a polyol,                  and the catalyst, aluminum copper and organic                  amine or ketone ether organic matter. PN WO2018114752-A1; AU2017384130-A1; CA3046440-A1; IN201947022118-A; CN110072837-A; KR2019091458-A; EP3555037-A1; BR112019012126-A2; US2020010400-A1; AU2017384130-B2; US10647656-B2; MX2019006874-A1; ZA201903145-A; EP3555037-B1; IN366826-B; EA38387-B1
TI Preparing ethylene and vinyl acetate involves contacting gas stream in first reaction zone which comprises ethane and ethylene with oxygen-containing gas stream in presence of oxydehydrogenation catalyst under oxidative dehydrogenation.
AU LANGE J P A M
   LANGE J P A M J
   GHISLAIN LANGE J P A M J
   LANGE J P A M J G
   LANGE J P
   ANDRE M
   JOSEPH G
   LANGE JEAN P A M J
AE SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
   SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
   SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
GA 201851003E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ethylene and vinyl acetate (VAM) involves contacting gas stream in first reaction zone which comprises ethane and ethylene with oxygen-containing gas stream in the presence of oxydehydrogenation catalyst under oxidative dehydrogenation conditions to produce first product stream which comprises ethane, ethylene, acetic acid and water, and other compounds. The first product stream is separated in a vapor permeation unit into a second stream which comprises water and third stream which comprises ethane, ethylene, acetic acid, and other gaseous compounds, and contacted in a second reaction zone at portion of third stream with stream which comprises concentrated acetic acid with oxygen-containing gas stream in the presence of VAM catalyst to produce a fourth product stream which comprises vinyl acetate, acetic acid, ethane, ethylene, water, carbon dioxide, and other compounds.
   USE - Method for preparing ethylene and vinyl acetate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare ethylene and vinyl acetate that is selective, and in simple and cost effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The fourth product stream is separated in a vinyl acetate separation zone into fifth stream which comprises vinylacetate, acetic acid, water and other condensable compounds. The sixth stream comprises ethylene, ethane, carbon dioxide and other gaseous compounds. The vinyl acetate is recovered from fifth stream. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a reaction system for oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane and co-production of vinyl acetate, which comprises first reaction zone which comprises inlet for receiving an ethane-containing feed stream, inlet for receiving an oxygen-containing feed stream, and outlet for discharging an effluent from the first reaction zone, vapor permeation unit which comprises inlet for receiving an effluent from the first reaction zone, outlet for discharging a water-depleted effluent stream from the vapor permeation unit, and outlet for discharging water from the vinyl acetate separation zone, second reaction zone which comprises inlet for receiving the water-depleted effluent stream from the vapor permeation unit, inlet for receiving an oxygen-containing feed stream, inlet for receiving a concentrated acetic acid- containing feed stream, and outlet for discharging an effluent from the second reaction zone, vinyl acetate separation zone which comprises inlet for receiving an effluent from the second reaction zone, inlet for receiving a washing liquid stream, outlet for discharging a vinyl acetate-enriched liquid stream from the vinyl acetate separation zone, and outlet for discharging a vinyl acetate-depleted gaseous stream from the vinyl acetate separation zone, a purification zone which comprises inlet for receiving a vinyl acetate-enriched liquid stream from the vinyl acetate separation zone, outlet for discharging a concentrated acetic acid-containing stream from the purification zone, and outlet for discharging a concentrated vinyl acetate-containing stream from the purification zone, and outlets for discharging further compounds, and carbon dioxide removal zone which comprises inlet for receiving a vinyl acetate-depleted gaseous stream from the vinyl acetate separation zone, and an outlet for discharging a carbon dioxide-depleted stream from carbon dioxide removal zone, ethane/ethylene separation unit which comprises inlet for receiving a carbon dioxide-depleted gaseous stream from the carbon dioxide removal zone, an outlet for discharging an ethylene-enriched stream from ethane/ethylene separation unit, and outlet for discharging an ethane-enriched stream from ethane/ethylene separation unit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The vinyl acetate separation zone comprises a condenser. The vinyl acetate separation zone is a washing zone. The fourth product stream is contacted with water or liquid absorbent to produce a fifth stream which comprises vinylacetate, acetic acid, water and/or absorbent and other condensable compounds. The sixth stream comprises ethylene, ethane, carbon dioxide, and other gaseous compounds. The system is used for recirculating the concentrated acetic acid-containing stream from the purification zone to the second reaction zone, and/or recirculating an ethylene-enriched stream from the ethane/ethylene separation unit to the second reaction zone, and/or recirculating an ethane-containing stream from the ethane/ethylene separation unit to the first reaction zone. Preferred Process: The fifth stream is fed to purification zone to produce a seventh stream which comprises concentrated acetic acid. The eighth stream comprises concentrated vinyl acetate, and one or more streams which comprises water and/or absorbent, and other compounds. The portion of the seventh stream which comprises concentrated acetic acid is used as the feed stream which comprises concentrated acetic acid supplied to second reaction zone. The sixth stream comprises ethylene, ethane, carbon dioxide, and other gaseous compounds which is fed to a carbon dioxide removal zone to remove carbon dioxide and light condensable compounds to produce ninth stream enriched in ethane and ethylene. The portion of ninth stream enriched in ethane and ethylene is recycled to the first reaction zone. The ninth stream enriched in ethane and ethylene is separated in a ethane/ethylene separation unit to produce tenth stream enriched in ethane and an eleventh stream enriched in ethylene. The portion of the tenth stream enriched in ethane is recirculated to the first reaction zone. The portion of the eleventh stream enriched in ethylene is recirculated to the second reaction zone.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN JP2019209228-A
TI Container for carbon dioxide absorbent for an electronic apparatus, comprises carbon-dioxide absorber containing water and tetravalent lithium silicate and first moisture-content detection portion which detects moisture content.
AU KOJI H
   NAKA T
   YAMADA H
AE SHARP KK (SHAF-C)
GA 2019A3724A
AB    NOVELTY - Container comprises carbon-dioxide absorber containing water/moisture and tetravalent lithium silicate, first moisture-content detection portion which detects the first moisture content which is the quantity of the water/moisture content discharged/released from the carbon-dioxide absorber.
   USE - Container for carbon dioxide absorbent for an electronic apparatus (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The container comprising carbon-dioxide absorber can detect decrease in the absorption performance of the water-containing carbon-dioxide absorbent material easily and inexpensively at a temperature of about room temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a carbon-dioxide absorber, which comprises carbon dioxide absorbent that absorbs carbon dioxide contained in a gas containing water and carbon dioxide, and contains water, tetravalent lithium silicate, and a color component; and
   (2) a method for absorbing carbon-dioxide, which involves using a carbon dioxide absorbent for detecting the first moisture content which is the quantity of water/moisture content released from the carbon-dioxide absorber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon-dioxide absorber comprises 16-33 wt.% moisture. The second moisture-content detection portion detects the second moisture content which is the quantity of water/moisture content contained in the gas. The container comprises first path through which the gas passes through the carbon dioxide absorbent and is introduced into the first moisture amount detection unit, path switching unit that switches between the second path introduced into the first moisture amount detection unit without passing through the carbon dioxide absorbent and color detection unit for detecting a color of the carbon dioxide absorbing material. Preferred Conditions: The water content of the carbon dioxide absorbent is 16-33 wt.%. The odor detection process detects the density/concentration of the odor component discharge/released from the carbon dioxide absorber. The method for absorbing carbon-dioxide further involves detecting a color of the carbon dioxide absorbing material.

PT P
PN JP2021032174-A; US2021062697-A1; CN112443373-A; DE102020119874-A1; US11473465-B2; JP7160000-B2
TI Vehicle e.g. car has carbon dioxide outlet that            takes out carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide recovery            device, and openable and closable lid which covers both            fuel filler port and carbon dioxide outlet.
AU OTSUKI H
   GOTO H
   KORENAGA S
   SUZUKI T
   YOKOYAMA H
   YOKOYAMA D
   GOTOU H
   DA Y
   KORENGA S
AE TOYOTA JIDOSHA KK (TOYT-C)
   TOYOTA JIDOSHA KK (TOYT-C)
   TOYOTA JIDOSHA KK (TOYT-C)
   TOYOTA MOTOR CORP (TOYT-C)
GA 202120663T
AB    NOVELTY - The vehicle (100) has a fuel tank (2) that                stores fuel. A fuel filler port (3) is provided for                supplying fuel to the fuel tank. A carbon dioxide                recovery device (4) recovers carbon dioxide. A                carbon dioxide outlet (5) takes out carbon dioxide                from the carbon dioxide recovery device. An                openable and closable lid (6) covers both the fuel                filler port and the carbon dioxide outlet. The fuel                filler port and the carbon dioxide outlet are                integrated connection ports in which the refueling                port and the carbon dioxide outlet are integrated.                The integrated connection port is configured, such                that an integrated hose in which a refueling hose                connected to the refueling port and a take-out hose                connected to the carbon dioxide outlet are                integrated is connected. The carbon dioxide                recovery device is configured to be able to recover                carbon dioxide in exhaust gas discharged from                internal combustion engine.
   USE - Vehicle e.g. car.
   ADVANTAGE - Since the refueling port and the carbon                dioxide outlet are covered with one lid, the lid is                opened and closed only once when performing each of                the refueling work and the carbon dioxide take-out                work. The moving distance of the operator when                connecting hoses to the fuel filler port and the                carbon dioxide outlet are suppressed. Therefore,                the burden on the worker when performing each work                can be reduced, and the convenience of the worker                is improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A control device controls the internal                combustion engine, so that high-temperature exhaust                gas is discharged from the internal combustion                engine when carbon dioxide is taken out from the                carbon dioxide recovery device. The carbon dioxide                recovery device is heated by introducing high                temperature exhaust gas into the carbon dioxide                recovery device. An oxidation catalyst is provided                in exhaust passage of the internal combustion                engine. A fuel addition valve is provided in                exhaust passage on the upstream side in exhaust                flow direction of oxidation catalyst to supply fuel                to the oxidation catalyst. The carbon dioxide                recovery device is configured to be able to recover                carbon dioxide in exhaust gas discharged from the                internal combustion engine. The internal combustion                engine is motorized to supply air to the exhaust                passage, and fuel is injected from the fuel                addition valve to supply fuel to the oxidation                catalyst when the control device takes out carbon                dioxide from the carbon dioxide recovery device.                The carbon dioxide recovery device is heated by                introducing air heated by the heat of oxidation                when the fuel is oxidized on the oxidation catalyst                into the carbon dioxide recovery device. A display                unit provides information to workers who perform                refueling work to fuel tank and carbon dioxide                extraction work from the carbon dioxide recovery                device. A communication device is configured to be                able to communicate with a stand for supplying fuel                to the fuel tank and taking out carbon dioxide from                the carbon dioxide recovery device. The control                device displays information on the fuel tank                refueling completion time received from the stand                side through the communication device and the                carbon dioxide removal completion time from the                carbon dioxide recovery device on the display unit.                The control device completes both refueling of the                fuel tank and removal of carbon dioxide from the                carbon dioxide recovery device when the fuel tank                refueling completion time and the carbon dioxide                removal completion time from the carbon dioxide                recovery device are different. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS                are included for the following:
   (1) a terminal associated with the occupant of                the vehicle, has a display unit that displays                information, a communication device configured to                be able to communicate with a stand for supplying                fuel to the fuel tank and taking out carbon dioxide                from the carbon dioxide recovery device, and a                processing device configured to display information                on the fuel tank refueling completion time received                from the stand side through the communication                device and the carbon dioxide removal completion                time from the carbon dioxide recovery device on the                display unit; and
   (2) a stand for supplying fuel to the fuel                tank of the vehicle, has a stand-side communication                device that receives vehicle-side information                including the carbon dioxide recovery amount of the                carbon dioxide recovery device, and the free                capacity of the fuel tank transmitted from the                vehicle-side communication device mounted on the                vehicle. The refueling completion time of the fuel                tank and the removal completion time of carbon                dioxide from the carbon dioxide recovery device are                set to be the same time based on the vehicle side                information. A stand-side control device is                configured to control the amount of fuel supplied                to the fuel tank and the amount of carbon dioxide                taken out from the carbon dioxide recovery                device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram                illustrating the vehicle. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)

   Filler hole (3)

   Vehicle (100)

TI Apparatus for producing high-strength carbon dioxide pellets from carbon dioxide snow, has control device that controls apparatus such that mechanical properties of carbon dioxide pellets to be produced are predetermined.
AU ECKSTEIN L
   AYRER R
   PETZOLD R
AE KAERCHER SE & CO KG ALFRED (KARC-C)
GA 201945765V
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (42) comprises a main compacting device (44) for compacting carbon dioxide snow to form carbon dioxide pellets. A control device controls the apparatus such that mechanical properties of the carbon dioxide pellets to be produced are predetermined.
   USE - Apparatus for producing high-strength carbon dioxide pellets from carbon dioxide snow, used for cleaning device for irradiating surfaces to be treated with mixture flow from compressed gas and carbon dioxide pellets.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide pellets are optimally educable especially for different cleaning purposes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The control device controls the apparatus such that density and/or size, in particular length of carbon dioxide pellets to be produced are predetermined. The control device has an input device for prescribing the mechanical properties of pellets, in particular their density and/or size. A pre-compression device (31) is arranged for pre-compression of carbon dioxide snow produced by expansion of liquid carbon dioxide. The control device with pre-compression device is cooperatively designed to control an amount generated by the pre-compression device. The pre-compression device has an expansion device (34) for generating carbon dioxide snow from liquid or gaseous carbon dioxide. The pre-compaction device has a pre-compression chamber (38) for receiving and pre-compressing the generated carbon dioxide snow and that the expansion device and pre-compression chamber are fluidly connected with each other. The tube is made of pipe material, in particular of a metallic material or plastic. The metal comprises stainless steel or non-alloy steel. The plastic comprises PVC, polyethylene (PE), in particular PE 500, or polymethyl methacrylate (PMMA). An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a cleaning device for irradiating surfaces to be treated with a mixed flow, which comprises a compressed gas and carbon dioxide pellets. The carbon dioxide connection (20) for connection to a liquid carbon dioxide-containing carbon dioxide source (24) or through liquid carbon dioxide containing carbon dioxide source, is formed. A compressed gas connection (52) for connection to a compressed gas generating device (54) or by a compressed gas generating device (56) is formed. A transfer unit (48) is arranged for transferring the carbon dioxide pellets into carbon dioxide pellet accelerating device (58) for accelerating them, which has a compressed gas line (50) set in fluid communication with the compressed gas connection or the compressed gas generating device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the cleaning device for irradiating surfaces to be treated with a mixture stream of a compressed gas and carbon dioxide pellets.

PT P
PN CN113171758-A; WO2022088675-A1
TI Preparing carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises adding nano alumina powder into amine solution, stirring and mixing, immersing, and evaporating at room temperature to obtain evaporated solid, drying the evaporated solid.
AU ZHANG Z
   SHEN X
   YAN F
   LI C
AE UNIV SOUTHERN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (USOU-C)
   UNIV SOUTHERN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (USOU-C)
GA 202188576S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide adsorbent, comprises adding nano alumina powder into amine solution, stirring and mixing, immersing, and evaporating at room temperature for 4-6 hours to obtain evaporated solid, drying the evaporated solid at 55-65 degrees C for 4-6 hours to obtain carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   USE - The carbon dioxide adsorbent is useful in adsorption of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide adsorbent has simple preparation method and low cost, strong adsorption capacity of carbon dioxide, and stable regeneration performance in the carbon dioxide gas flow.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) carbon dioxide adsorbent, comprising substrate and active material supported on substrate, and the substrate is the nano alumina powder, and the active material is amine material; and
   (2) use method of carbon dioxide adsorbent in adsorption of carbon dioxide comprising adsorption of carbon dioxide in carbon dioxide gas stream of 15-100 vol.% at temperature of 30-110 degrees C, and the carbon dioxide adsorption capacity of carbon dioxide adsorbent is greater than or equal to 140 mg/g.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises evaporating at room temperature for 5 hours to obtain the evaporated solid and drying the evaporated solid under vacuum conditions at 60 degrees C for 5 hours to obtain the carbon dioxide adsorbent. The preparation method further includes synthesizing the nano alumina powder before the impregnation step. The method for synthesizing the nano-alumina powder includes the following steps: passing carbon dioxide into the sodium aluminate solution for reaction, and stopping the reaction when the pH of the reaction system is 9.8-10.3, filtering the reaction system that stopped the reaction to obtain a precipitate, and drying the precipitate to obtain a nano alumina precursor, calcining, cooling, and grinding the nano-alumina precursor to obtain the nano-alumina powder. In the method of synthesizing the nano alumina powder, the reaction steps are as follows: feeding a 1-6 wt.% sodium aluminate solution continuously with a concentration of 15-40 vol.% at room temperature, and the reaction is carried out with carbon dioxide with a flow rate of 400-2000 ml/minute per liter of sodium aluminate solution. In the method for synthesizing the nano-alumina powder, baking the nano-alumina precursor at 400-500 degrees C for 3-5 hours. The preparation method further includes preparing a solution of the amine substance before the step of dipping. The step of preparing the solution of the amine substance is: mixing and stirring the amine substance and the organic solvent to obtain the solution of the amine substance. The mass ratio of the amine substance to the organic solvent is 1:10-1:250. The method of use further includes regenerating the carbon dioxide adsorbent after the step of adsorbing carbon dioxide. The regeneration step is: heating and regenerating the carbon dioxide adsorbent in a flow of carbon dioxide at a temperature of 120-165 degrees C. Preferred Components: The loading amount of amine substance in the carbon dioxide adsorbent is 10-70%. The amine substance includes polyethyleneimine, diethylenetriamine, tetraethylenepentamine, and/or pentaethylenehexamine. In step of preparing amine substance solution, the organic solvent comprises methanol, ethanol and/or acetone.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PT P
PN US2018154307-A1; TW201821363-A; TW646050-B1; US10335735-B2
TI Capturing carbon dioxide from its source, comprises contacting carbon dioxide-containing source with reactive solution including absorption agent so that carbon dioxide in carbon dioxide-containing source is absorbed by absorption agent.
AU CHEN C
   WANG C
   SHEN J
AE UNIV NAT CHENG KUNG (UNCK-C)
   UNIV NAT CHENG KUNG (UNCK-C)
   UNIV NAT CHENG KUNG (UNCK-C)
GA 201845001P
AB    NOVELTY - Capturing carbon dioxide from its source, comprises contacting a carbon dioxide-containing source with a reactive solution that includes an absorption agent so that carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide-containing source is absorbed by the absorption agent, where the absorption agent is potassium phosphate, potassium EDTA, and a potassium monocarboxylate having a total of less than or equal to 12C.
   USE - The method is useful for capturing carbon dioxide from its source (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method requires less time to capture carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises: desorbing the absorbed carbon dioxide; and collecting the desorbed carbon dioxide. The content of water added into the reactive solution to desorb the absorbed carbon dioxide is 30-50% of the weight of the reactive solution. The absorbed carbon dioxide is desorbed by heating the reactive solution that contains the carbon dioxide absorbed by the absorption agent. The method of heating is performed at a temperature of 70-100 degrees C. Preferred Components: The absorption agent is potassium acetate, potassium propionate, potassium butyrate and/or tetrapotassium ethylenediamine-tetraacetate. The molality of the absorption agent in the reactive solution is 2-20 m. The reactive solution further includes a solvent in which the absorption agent is dissolved. The solvent is water, dimethyl sulfoxide and/or dimethyl formamide. The absorbing agent is a potassium monocarboxylate having a total of less than or equal to 12C, and the absorbed carbon dioxide is desorbed by addition of water into the reactive solution that contains the carbon dioxide absorbed by the absorption agent.
   EXAMPLE - 39.26 g Potassium acetate serving as an absorption agent was dissolved in 20 g water so as to obtain a reactive solution. The reactive solution was aerated and reacted with 50 ml carbon dioxide for 80 minutes under 1 atm and 29 degrees C, so as to obtain a product which contained carbon dioxide captured by potassium acetate. The product was analyzed by thermogravimetric analysis-fourier transform infrared spectroscopy at the absorption peak of 2400 cm-1 to determine whether carbon dioxide was captured by the absorption agent. The absorbed carbon dioxide amount in the product was determined by a gravimetric method, so as to calculate the absorbed carbon dioxide amount per mole of the absorption agent. Then 25 g water having a temperature of 30 degrees C was added into the product under 1 atm and 29 degrees C to desorb the absorbed carbon dioxide, followed by collecting the desorbed carbon dioxide using a drainage process until no gas bubbles were generated to give carbon dioxide.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

GA 2022A1997E
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing bimetallic catalyst                involves (a) dissolving the metal cyanide complex                with solvent and stirring to obtain solution S1,                (b) using the solvent to dissolve the metal zinc                salt and trace metal salt, and stirring the                solution S2, (c) placing the solution in a carbon                dioxide atmosphere, pressurizing, and injecting the                solution, stirring, centrifuging, pulping and                washing to obtain precipitate, and (d) vacuum                drying the precipitate to constant weight to obtain                a double-metal catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing bimetallic                catalyst, which is useful in preparation of poly                (carbonate-ether) polyol as raw material of                polyurethane synthesis.
   ADVANTAGE - The bimetallic catalyst: has high catalytic                activity and better product selectivity, and the                carbonate unit content is controllable. The                preparation method of poly (carbonate-ether) polyol                can be carried out in different sizes of high                aggregate reaction kettle, the polymerization                reaction is preferably carried out under constant                pressure and continuous stirring, e.g. stirring at                speed of 500 revolutions per minute after finishing                polymerization, cooling to aggregate temperature,                discharging the residual carbon dioxide and the                aggregate obtained by the reaction is washed by                water or alcohol solvent, drying obtain carbon                dioxide base polyol crude product.

   a poly (carbonate-ether) polyol.

PN CN114468040-A
TI Inhibiting self-ripening of fruits and vegetables            comprises e.g. directly or indirectly contacting            antibacterial, ethylene absorption composition and            fruit and vegetable, where ethylene absorption            composition comprises e.g. environmentally friendly            wax, carbon dioxide absorbent, and deionized            water.
AU SUN Y
   ZHENG S
   LIU S
AE BEIJING JIANZHEN PRESERVATION TECHNOLOGY (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202270381S
AB    NOVELTY - Inhibiting the self-ripening of fruits and                vegetables comprises directly or indirectly                contacting antibacterial, ethylene absorption                composition and fruit and vegetable, where the                antibacterial, ethylene absorption composition is                compounded by 30-35 pts. wt. sepiolite, 15-20 pts.                wt. environmentally friendly wax, 10-15 pts. wt.                carbon dioxide absorbent, 7-12 pts. wt. shell                powder and 50-60 pts. wt. deionized water, and the                steps of directly or indirectly contacting the                fruits and vegetables are as follows: (S1)                uniformly placing the picked fruits and vegetables                on the table, (S2) spraying antibacterial and                ethylene absorbing composition on the surface,                allowing to stand for 5 minutes, and drying to form                wax layer covering the surface of fruits and                vegetables, and (S3) packing and sealing the fruits                and vegetables with the wax layer.
   USE - The method is useful for inhibiting the                self-ripening of fruits and vegetables.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, efficient, green and                environmentally friendly, has no pollution, has                bacteriostatic effect, seals environmentally                friendly wax on the fruit and vegetables surface                for reducing the entering of oxygen, absorbs carbon                dioxide generated by self-breathing of fruit or                vegetable by carbon dioxide absorbent, inhibits                aerobic respiration of fruit/vegetable itself,                greatly prolongs shelf life of the fruit/vegetable,                uses beeswax as base to match with preserved fruit,                honeycomb and raw milk, reaches edible standard, is                safe and non-toxic, and ensures edible                safety.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The antibacterial and                  ethylene absorption composition comprises 33-34                  pts. wt. sepiolite, 17-19 pts. wt.                  environmentally friendly wax, 14-15 pts. wt.                  carbon dioxide absorbent, 7-10 pts. wt. shell                  powder, and 55 pts. wt. deionized water.Preferred Method: In the method, the                  particle sizes of the sepiolite and shell powder                  is 400 meshes, and the water content of the                  sepiolite and shell powder is &#8814; 0.3%.                  The preparation of antibacterial and ethylene                  absorption composition further comprises (A1)                  cleaning the sepiolite and shell powder, adding                  into the drying box for drying treatment, where                  the drying temperature is maintained at                  150&#8451; and the drying time is controlled                  at 20 minutes, (A2) adding the dried sepiolite                  and shell powder into the reactor, adding                  deionized water, stirring and mixing thoroughly,                  reacting at 85&#8451; for 4 hours, and                  filtering to obtain reaction solution, and (A3)                  crushing the environmentally friendly wax, adding                  into the reaction solution together with the                  carbon dioxide absorbent, mixing, where the                  reaction temperature is 65&#8451;, the                  reaction time is 2 hours, completing the                  reaction, filtering to obtain antibacterial and                  ethylene absorption composition. The carbon                  dioxide absorbent is composed of 10 pts. wt.                  magnesite and 30 pts. wt. ethanol, where the                  preparation of carbon dioxide absorbent is as                  follows: (B1) adding magnesite and 1/3 ethanol                  into the reactor, stirring and reacting for 12                  hours, and vacuum drying to obtain stirred                  material at stirring speed of 720                  revolutions/minute, and the reaction temperature                  is 45&#8451;, and (B2) mixing the stirring                  material with the remaining ethanol, and                  filtering to obtain emulsion-type carbon dioxide                  absorbent. In the method, the particle size of                  the magnesite is 500 meshes, and the moisture                  content is &#8804; 1%. The concentration of                  the ethanol is 50% and the viscosity at                  20&#8451; is 1.074MPa.seconds. The                  environmentally friendly wax is composed of 40                  pts. wt. beeswax, 10 pts. wt. preserved fruit, 15                  pts. wt. honeycomb, 25 pts. wt. raw milk and 8                  pts. wt. water, where the preparation method is                  as follows: (C1) adding beeswax into the                  container for heating, controlling the                  temperature at 120&#8451;, cooling naturally,                  and melting into liquid state, (C2) cooling to                  80&#8451;, adding 10 pts. wt. preserved                  fruit, 15 pts. wt. honeycomb, 25 pts. wt. raw                  milk and 8 pts. wt. water, stirring, where the                  stirring is divided into two stages, the initial                  stirring speed is 120 revolutions/minute,                  allowing to stand for 5 minutes, controlling                  stirring speed at 720 revolutions/minute for 20                  minutes to make a mixture, and (C3) adding the                  mixture into the mold, and solidifying at                  -15&#8451;, and (C4) cutting into blocks of                  uniform size to obtain environmentally friendly                  wax.

PN CN114232019-A
TI Preparing copper-based nano-array catalyst loaded with silver nano-particles useful in preparing ethanol by electro-catalysis carbon dioxide, comprises e.g. soaking cuprous oxide nano-wire array in the solution, carrying out plasma bombardment under vacuum condition, washing and drying.
AU GUO J
   LUO J
   KANG X
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA (USOA-C)
GA 202248944G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing copper-based nano-array catalyst loaded with silver nano-particles comprises adding silver nitrate solution in the mixed alcohol solution containing ethylene glycol and absolute ethanol, stirring uniformly, soaking cuprous oxide nano-wire array in the solution, carrying out plasma bombardment under vacuum condition, washing and drying to obtain the grey silver-cuprous oxide nano-wire array. The electrolyte is 0.5 M potassium bicarbonate solution.
   USE - The copper-based nano-array catalyst loaded with silver nano-particle is useful in preparing ethanol by electro-catalysis carbon dioxide (claimed), and in electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide to hydrocarbons, namely methane, ethylene and other alkanes, alcohol i.e. methanol, ethanol and aldehydes.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: uses copper-based in-situ growth of cuprous oxide nano-wire as base material, copper- based in situ growth cuprous oxide nanowire length is long, micro-observation prolongs the path of carbon dioxide reduction in the reaction process, provides more rich active sites to electrochemical reduction reaction, and increase of the pores can stabilize the intermediate product of reduction, promoting generation of C2 product, reduce by-product reaction time to inhibit generation of hydrogen gas, loads the noble metal silver on the nano line through the vacuum plasma beam, can form micro-topography of the silver particle coated nano-wires, through copper, and has synergistic effect of silver.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for copper-based nano-array catalyst loaded with silver nano-particle, is prepared by the above method.

PT P
PN WO2022008914-A1
TI Producing a fertilizer composition comprises contacting ash from an organic source with nitric acid, contacting a composition comprising an amino compound with a composition comprising carbon dioxide, and mixing the compositions.
AU HAMMOND P
AE CCM TECHNOLOGIES LTD (CCMT-Non-standard)
GA 202207576K
AB    NOVELTY - Producing (M1) a fertilizer composition comprises: (a) contacting (i) ash from an organic source with (ii) a source of nitric acid; (b) contacting (iii) a composition comprising an amino compound with (iv) a composition comprising carbon dioxide; and (c) mixing the composition obtained in step (a) with the composition obtained in step (b).
   USE - The methods are useful for producing a fertilizer composition and increasing the nutrient content of a plant growing medium (all claimed). Test details are described but no results given.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: seeks to provide the fertilizer composition that is prepared from atleast one waste products; essential to reduce waste that is sent to landfill and to reduce emissions of carbon dioxide; maximizes crop yield and thus providing safe and effective fertilizers is also a necessity; and can use the incinerated waste from a wood burning power station and combine it with waste nitric acid to produce calcium nitrate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for increasing (M2) the nutrient content of a plant growing medium comprising (a1) contacting (i) ash from an organic source with (ii) a source of nitric acid, (b1) contacting (iii) a composition comprising an amino compound with (iv) a composition comprising carbon dioxide; (c1) mixing the composition obtained in step (a1) with the composition obtained in step (b1); (d1) optionally adding a composition comprising cellulosic fibers to the mixture obtained in step (c1); (e1) optionally further processing the composition obtained in step (c1) or step (d1); and (f1) mixing the mixture obtained in step (c1), step (d1) or step (e1) with the plant growing medium.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: The method (M1) further includes: a step (d) of adding a composition comprising cellulosic fibers to the composition obtained in step (c); and a step (e) of further processing the material obtained in step (c) or step (d). The step (e) involves drying, pulverizing and/or granulating the material. Preferred Components: The component (i) comprises wood ash, where the component (i) comprises incinerated waste from a wood burning power station (preferred), waste from a gasification plant or waste from a pyrolysis plant. The component (ii) is an aqueous composition comprising from 15-35 wt.% nitric acid, or aqueous composition comprising 2-10 wt.% nitric acid. The component (iii) comprises 20-50 wt.% ammonia and an anaerobic digestate. The component (ii) comprises ammonical liquor from an anaerobic digestion process. The component (iv) comprises neat carbon dioxide gas and/or a gaseous mixture of carbon dioxide and atleast one further gases, where carbon dioxide is at a range of 5 vol.%. The component (iv) comprises the exhaust gases from the combustion of biogas, or of methane recovered from biogas.
   ACTIVITY - Plant Growth Stimulant; Fertilizer.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - Administration of the composition is via spray in the form of liquid. No dosage details given.
   EXAMPLE - 250 kg 30% Nitric acid was blended with 100 kg wood ash material, the mixture was blended in a ribbon blender until the production of bubbles in the slurry (carbon dioxide) ceased and the temperature of the unit started to fall, where 45 minutes and a maximal temperature of 45 degrees C was achieved in the mixer. A sample of this material was dried and analyzed and contained the following key components 1% phosphorus pentoxide, 51% calcium nitrate, 6% potassium nitrate and 4% magnesium nitrate. Then anhydrous ammonia was dissolved in an anaerobic digestate liquor in a dilution mixer to provide an aqueous composition comprising ammonia, composition X. Hot flue gases from methane combustion in the anaerobic digestion plant was directed through a heat exchanger where energy was recovered to dry digestate in a digestate dryer, where the carbon dioxide from the flue gases were bubbled through composition X to form a paste comprising ammonium nitrate and calcium carbonate. The reaction time depends on the concentration of carbon dioxide in the flue gas, but can vary 10-60 minutes. The reaction with carbon dioxide was exothermic, and heat from this reaction is recovered and used to dry digestate. The 110kg of the material obtained in step A was blended with the paste product obtained in step B. when the production of carbon dioxide from the system had ceased and the temperature of the slurry had stopped falling the mixer was turned off and the material passed to a dewatering belt press before being finished in a dryer at 45 degrees C, finally material was dried to a moisture content of 15% and then pelletized.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.)
MC C04-A08; C04-A09; C04-D02; C05-C01; C05-C02; C05-C04; C05-C06; C14-S18; C14-T03; C14-T04
IP C05C-003/00

TI New phloroglucinol polyether carboxylate compound useful for carbon dioxide flooding in deep low-permeability heavy oil by viscosity reduction and efficiency synergistic method.
AU MA J
   SHEN Z
   LI Y
   WU C
AE SINOPEC SHANGHAI RES INST PETROCHEMICAL (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 202211075K
AB    NOVELTY - Phloroglucinol polyether carboxylate compound (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful for carbon dioxide flooding in deep low-permeability heavy oil by viscosity reduction and efficiency synergistic method (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I): reduces the viscosity of heavy oil and improves the flow performance of heavy oil; and solves the problem of injection in deep low-permeability heavy oil reservoirs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Phloroglucinol polyether carboxylate compound of formula (I) is new.
   R1, R2 and R3 = H or (CH2)eH;
   e = 0-4;
   a1, a2, a3, b1, b2, b3, c1, c2 and c3 = 0-50;
   a1+ a2+a3+b1+b2+ b3+c1+c2+c3 = greater than 0;
   X1, X2, and X3 = H or carboxy group compound of formula (-(C=O)-R4);
   R4 = 8 -50C optionally substituted hydrocarbon group;
   R5 = H or 1-50C hydrocarbon group;
   R6, R7 and R8 = (CH2)jH;
   j = 0-4;
   f, g, and h = 0-50;
   f+g+h = greater than 0;
   Y = H or carboxy group compound of formula (-(C=O)R9); and
   R9 = 6-50C optionally substituted hydrocarbon group.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   1. preparation of (I);
   2. composition containing phloroglucinol polyether carboxylate, comprises (I) and auxiliary agent i.e. aryl compound of formula (II);
   3. preparing composition containing phloroglucinol polyether carboxylate, comprising mixing (I) with an auxiliary agent; and
   4. useful for carbon dioxide flooding in deep low-permeability heavy oil by viscosity reduction and efficiency synergistic method, comprising mixing composition containing (I) with liquid or supercritical carbon dioxide, and then injecting into formation together.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises (i) reacting phloroglucinol with epoxy compound in presence of catalyst A to obtain phloroglucinol polyether compound, (ii) carrying our condensation reaction of phloroglucinol polyether compound obtained in step (i) with carboxylic acid compound (R4COOH) to obtain final product.Preferred Method: In step (i), the reaction is carried out at 100-140degrees Celsius, the catalyst A is sodium carbonate, sodium hydroxide, and/or potassium hydroxide, and the addition amount of catalyst A is 0.5-5% of the mass of phloroglucinol, and/or in step (ii), the reaction is carried out at 180-220degrees Celsius, catalyst B is sodium bicarbonate, sodium carbonate, and/or potassium bicarbonate, and the addition amount of catalyst B is 0.5-3% of the mass of phloroglucinol. The auxiliary agent is prepared by (1) reacting alkylphenol compound of formula (RPhOH) (IV) with epoxy compound in presence of catalyst C to obtain (III), (2) subjecting the polyether compound obtained in step (1) to condensation reaction with carboxylic acid compound of formula (R9COOH) to obtain (III). In step (1), the reaction is carried out at 100-140degrees Celsius, the catalyst C is sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, sodium hydroxide, and/or potassium hydroxide, and the addition amount of catalyst C is 0.5-5 wt.% of mass of alkylphenol compound, in step (ii), the reaction is carried out 180-220degrees Celsius, the catalyst D is sodium bicarbonate, sodium carbonate, and/or potassium bicarbonate, and the addition amount of catalyst D is 0.5-5% of the mass of polyether compound. The method: the addition amount of composition is 0.1-5 wt.% the mass of carbon dioxide quality under injection pressure, preferably 0.5-3 wt.%, the reservoir temperature is 20-120degrees Celsius, the injection temperature is 20-100degrees Celsius, and the viscosity of the thick oil is 500-50000 mPa.s.Preferred Components: The molar ratio between (I) and auxiliary agent is 1:0.01-100, preferably 1:0.1-10.

TI Method for generating thermal energy and basic chemicals, involves performing production of aluminum metal by fused-salt electrolysis in fused-salt electrolysis system, where gaseous carbon dioxide and liquid aluminum metal are provided.
AU DENTLER C
   WINDHAB N
AE DENTLER C (DENT-Individual)
   WINDHAB N (WIND-Individual)
GA 2021C5224N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves performing the production of an aluminum metal by the fused-salt electrolysis in a fused-salt electrolysis system, where the aluminum metal is used to generate the thermal energy of basic chemical substances. The basic chemical substances are selected from a group of carbon monoxide and hydrogen by bringing the carbon dioxide and water into a contact with the aluminum metal. The aluminothermic reaction to form aluminum oxide and carbon monoxide or hydrogen is implemented. The storage or chemical conversion of the carbon monoxide and hydrogen is produced in a process. The gaseous carbon dioxide and liquid aluminum metal are brought into the contact with one another. The aluminothermic reaction is occurred in the absence of oxygen gas.
   USE - Method for generating the thermal energy and basic chemicals.
   ADVANTAGE - The production of an aluminum metal is performed by the fused-salt electrolysis in a fused-salt electrolysis system, where the aluminum metal is used to generate the thermal energy of basic chemical substances, and hence ensures improving the nutrition of the increasing world population by intensifying the existing agricultural areas or by increasing productivity in the production of food with the production of biomass and reduces the electrical power available for the electrolysis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a method for generating thermal energy and carbon monoxide by the aluminothermic reaction of carbon dioxide; and
   (2) a method for generating the thermal energy and hydrogen through the aluminothermic reaction of water vapor.

AU YAGI Y
   IWAMURA K
   TOKI K
   TANAKA A
AE NIPPON STEEL & SUMITOMO METAL CORP (YAWA-C)
   NIPPON STEEL CORP (YAWA-C)
GA 201988352X
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a performance acquisition unit which acquires the performance data at the time of the blow temper in a converter. A parameter extraction unit (202) derives a waste gas correction coefficient and the amount of approach carbon dioxide flows based on the performance data. The performance data are the performance values in each time of the flow volume of the waste gas emitted from a converter at the time of blow temper. The performance value includes the performance value in each time of the flow volume of carbon monoxide gas contained in the waste gas, carbon dioxide gas, and oxygen gas, and the performance value in each time of the flow volume of the carbon dioxide gas contained in the blowing-in gas blown in with respect to the molten steel in the converter at the time of blow temper. The amount of approach carbon flows is the flow volume of carbon which approachs into the waste gas duct along which the waste gas passes from atmosphere.
   USE - Converter parameter extraction apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - The correction coefficient for correcting the measurement error of the flowmeter which measures the flow volume of waste gas is derived accurately.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a converter parameter extraction method; and
   (2) a converter parameter extraction program.

AB    NOVELTY - The system has an evaporator (1). TI System for removing ash deposits within boiler, has soot blower that is operative to inject compressed gas from gas source into boiler to remove ash deposits from surface of boiler.
AU LEVASSEUR A
   ZHANG W
   STALLMANN O
   NIASS T
   YOUNES M
   JAMAL A
AE GENERAL ELECTRIC TECHNOLOGY GMBH (GENE-C)
   GENERAL ELECTRIC TECHNOLOGY GMBH (GENE-C)
GA 201834740Q
AB    NOVELTY - The system (1) has a soot blower (6) that is formed in a boiler (2). A gas supply line (7) is in fluid communication with the soot blower and a gas source. The soot blower is operative to inject compressed gas from the gas source into the boiler to remove the ash deposits from a surface of the boiler. The compressed gas is made of carbon dioxide. A compressor (15,23) is used for compressing a flue gas stream discharged from the boiler. A separator is formed downstream of the compressor that is operative to separate carbon dioxide from the flue gas streamboiler.
   USE - System for removing ash deposits within boiler.
   ADVANTAGE - The system for removing ash deposits within boiler is improved. The soot blower is operative to inject carbon dioxide gas received from the carbon dioxide gas storage vessel into the boiler thus the ash deposits from a surface of the boiler is removed. The steam utilized for soot blowing is often drawn/tapped from a supply of steam used to generate electricity thus the amount of steam available to generate electricity and result in a power production loss in the encompassing power plant can be reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for removing ash deposits within boiler.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a system for removing ash deposits within a boiler.
   System for removing ash deposits within boiler (1)
   Boiler (2)
   Soot blower (6)
   Gas supply line (7)
   Compressors (15,23)

PN WO2018057780-A1; US2018094560-A1; WO2018057780-A4; US11041420-B2; US2021396168-A1
TI Micro carbon capture system has carbon capture            unit that is included with regeneratable physical            adsorbent which is positioned to adsorb carbon dioxide            from exhaust gas in first position, and to release            carbon dioxide in second position.
AU WILLIAMS D
   RODRIGUEZ E
AE WILLIAMS D (WILL-Individual)
   RODRIGUEZ E (RODR-Individual)
   WILLIAMS D (WILL-Individual)
   RODRIGUEZ E (RODR-Individual)
   RODRIGUEZ E (RODR-Individual)
   M-TRIGEN INC (MTRI-Non-standard)
GA 201825641V
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a fossil fuel oxidation unit                that oxidizes fossil fuel to convert fossil fuel                chemical energy into thermal energy, mechanical                energy and electrical energy. A carbon capture unit                (2000) is positioned to receive exhaust gas of the                fossil fuel oxidation unit. A cycling mechanism is                provided to cycle the regeneratable physical                adsorbent between first and second positions. The                physical adsorbent is positioned to adsorb carbon                dioxide from the exhaust gas in the first position,                and the physical adsorbent is positioned to release                the carbon dioxide in the second position.
   USE - Micro carbon capture system.
   ADVANTAGE - The local energy conversion and waste carbon                utilization allow the use of combustion waste heat                to directly drive the carbon capture process, while                avoiding the complexities and cost of carbon                transfer and storage. The fuel efficiency is                greatly increased and the overall costs which                plague centralized energy production are reduced.                Simple and cost effective carbon capture process is                achieved, while eliminating the transport and                sequestration phases and risk of potential leakage                or environmental pollution, while operating at high                energy efficiencies characteristic of local power                generation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method of capturing carbon from fossil                fuel exhaust gases; and
   (2) a method of operating micro carbon capture                system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                micro-carbon capture system.
   Internal combustion engine (100)
   Exhaust pipe (102)
   Air Compressor (107)
   Adsorbent rotor (110)
   Carbon capture unit (2000)
