PT P
PN CN107722252-A; CN107722252-B
TI Preparation of polycarbonate copolymer, involves adding bisphenol, benzenediol and triphosgene to reagent bottle, reacting, adding catalyst, carbon dioxide, epoxide and formaldehyde to reagent bottle, heating reagent bottle, and stirring.
AU GONG W
AE SUZHOU BOYUN PLASTICS CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
   JIANGSU BOILN PLASTICS CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018171595
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of polycarbonate copolymer, involves adding bisphenol and benzenediol to reagent bottle, stirring at 10-15 rpm for 5-10 minutes, adding sodium salt aqueous solution and triphosgene to reagent bottle, carrying out polycondensation reaction, adding double metal cyanide catalyst and carbon dioxide to reagent bottle, stirring at 15-22 rpm for 10-12 minutes, sealing reagent bottle, heating reagent bottle, adding epoxide, aromatic formaldehyde, chain extender and dimethyl ether to reagent bottle, stirring at 25-30 rpm for 20-25 minutes, and leaving still.
   USE - Preparation of polycarbonate copolymer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of polycarbonate copolymer with high stability. The polycarbonate copolymer reduces water absorption rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of polycarbonate copolymer, involves weighing 60-75 pts. wt. triphosgene, 5-10 pts. wt. bisphenol, 8-12 pts. wt. benzenediol, 5-8 pts. wt. double metal cyanide catalyst, 1-5 pts. wt. carbon dioxide, 2-4 pts. wt. epoxides, 2.5-5.5 pts. wt. aromatic formaldehyde, 1.5-3.5 pts. wt. chain extender and 3-5 pts. wt. ethylene glycol dimethyl ether, adding bisphenol and benzenediol to reagent bottle, stirring at 10-15 rpm for 5-10 minutes, adding sodium salt aqueous solution and triphosgene to reagent bottle, carrying out polycondensation reaction for 2-3 hours, adding double metal cyanide catalyst and carbon dioxide to reagent bottle, stirring at 15-22 rpm for 10-12 minutes, sealing reagent bottle, heating reagent bottle at 110-150 degrees C, adding epoxide, aromatic formaldehyde, chain extender and dimethyl ether to reagent bottle, stirring at 25-30 rpm for 20-25 minutes, and leaving still.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The polycarbonate copolymer comprises 65-70 pts. wt. triphosgene, 7-8 pts. wt. bisphenol, 9-10 pts. wt. benzenediol, 6-7 pts. wt. double metal cyanide catalyst, 2-4 pts. wt. carbon dioxide, 2.5-3.5 pts. wt. epoxides, 3-5 pts. wt. aromatic formaldehyde, 2-3 pts. wt. chain extender and 3.5-4.5 pts. wt. ethylene glycol dimethyl ether. Preferred Components: The epoxides are ethylene oxide and propylene oxide. The chain extenders are ethylenediamine and/or epoxypropyl ether.
DC A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.)
MC A02-A00A; A02-B; A05-E06; A10-D03; A10-D05
IP C08G-064/16; C08G-064/26; C08G-064/28; C08G-064/32; C08G-064/34

PT P
PN CN113883686-A
TI Carbon dioxide collecting device for air            conditioner, has control module for determining carbon            dioxide concentration of indoor air reaches preset            concentration threshold, and controlling opening of air            channel to collect carbon dioxide.
AU CHEN G
   ZHU B
AE HISENSE SHANDONG AIR CONDITIONER CO LTD (HISG-C)
GA 202211133L
AB    NOVELTY - The device (1) has a box body (10) provided                with an air channel. A carbon dioxide collecting                module (11) is fixed in the box body. A control                module determines carbon dioxide concentration of                indoor air reaches a first preset concentration                threshold. The control module controls an opening                of the air channel to collect carbon dioxide in the                indoor air flowing through the air channel through                the carbon dioxide collection module. A first wind                screen (14) is movably set relative to a first wind                opening. A second wind screen (15) is movably set                opposite to a second wind opening.
   USE - Carbon dioxide collecting device for use in an                air conditioner (claimed) for collecting carbon                dioxide in indoor air flowing through an air                channel through a carbon dioxide collecting                module.
   ADVANTAGE - The device absorbs carbon dioxide in the                indoor air by the carbon dioxide collecting module                when carbon dioxide concentration of indoor air                exceeds the reasonable range so as to reduce carbon                dioxide, thus realizing adjustment of the                concentration of carbon dioxide and improving                safety of the air conditioner and user comfort. The                device allows a control module to control an                electric heating unit to start and to release the                collected carbon dioxide when the control module                determines that carbon dioxide collection module                reaches a saturated state, so that the comfort of                the user can be improved. The control module                controls the opening of an air channel to collect                carbon dioxide flowing through the air channel                through the module, thus reducing carbon dioxide.                The module is set in the box body, and is located                in the air passage, thus preventing the module from                falling out of a box body.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for: (1) a                carbon dioxide collecting method; (2) a computer                readable storage medium storing a set of                instructions for collecting carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - Carbon dioxide collecting device (1)
   Box body (10)
   Carbon dioxide collecting module (11)
   First tuyere (12)
   Second tuyere (13)
   First wind screeen (14)
   Second wind screeen (15)
   First driving unit (16)
   Second driving unit (17)
   Electric heating unit (18)
   Exhaust port (19)
   Third wind shield (20)
   Third driving unit (21)
   Weight sensor (22)
DC Q74 (Heating, ranges, ventilating (F24)); T01 (Digital Computers); X27 (Domestic Electric Appliances\n)
MC Q74-T20; Q74-A02B; T01-J07A; T01-J08A; T01-S03; X27-E01B

PT P
PN KR2020124930-A; KR2190874-B1
TI Preparing liquid absorbent of carbon dioxide involves selecting an amino acid mimetic containing an amino acid, an amine group and a carboxyl group, or an alkali metal salt, which can chemically absorb carbon dioxide in an aqueous solution.
AU JUNG S
   BAK K T
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 2020A8761Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing liquid absorbent involves selecting an amino acid mimetic containing an amino acid, an amine group and a carboxyl group, or an alkali metal salt, which can chemically absorb carbon dioxide in an aqueous solution and provides an amine group (-NH2) that can be regenerated by heating as a carbon dioxide absorbent; selecting a transition metal oxide containing a transition metal that does not coordinate with the carbon dioxide absorbent and provides an acid point under the condition of an aqueous solution containing the carbon dioxide absorbent as the carbon dioxide absorption catalyst; and preparing a carbon dioxide absorbing liquid by adding the carbon dioxide absorbing catalyst to water.
   USE - Method for preparing liquid absorbent of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - Amino acids are non-toxic and environmentally friendly, and since the vapor pressure is relatively lower than that of alkanolamine absorbents, the volatility is low, so that the loss of the absorbent can be reduced in the stripping reaction that requires a high temperature when applied to industrial facilities. The carbon dioxide absorption rate can be increased due to the metal oxide catalyst, so carbon dioxide can be removed with high efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a composition for absorbing carbon dioxide, which comprises amino acid mimetic containing an amino acid, an amine group and a carboxyl group, or an alkali metal salt, which can chemically absorb carbon dioxide in an aqueous solution and provides an amine group (-NH2) that can be regenerated by heating as a carbon dioxide absorbent, and a transition metal that does not coordinate with a carbon dioxide absorbent and provides an acid point; and
   (2) a method for removing carbon dioxide from a carbon dioxide-containing gas through gas-liquid contact with a carbon dioxide absorbing liquid, which involves preparing a water-based carbon dioxide absorbing liquid containing a transition metal oxide not coordinating therewith and providing an acid point and removing carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide-containing gas through gas-liquid contact with the carbon dioxide absorbing liquid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide absorption catalyst is selected by screening from oxides of transition metals in period 4 of the periodic table. The carbon dioxide absorption catalyst is selected from cupric oxide (CuO), ferric oxide, nickel(II) oxide and manganese dioxide. The composition further comprises an alkali metal hydroxide (MOH) capable of activating -NH3+ of an amino acid or amino acid mimic with -NH2 in an aqueous solution. The amino acid or amino acid mimetic has two or more amine groups. The composition contains lysine as a carbon dioxide absorbent or an alkyl lithium metal salt and CuO as a carbon dioxide absorbing catalyst. Preferred Process: The method for producing a carbon dioxide absorbing liquid involves selecting the concentration of the transition metal oxide by measuring the change in viscosity of the carbon dioxide absorbing liquid according to the concentration of the transition metal oxide. Preferred Conditions: The aqueous solution comprises 0.5-2 M carbon dioxide absorbent and 0.05-0.5 M carbon dioxide absorption catalyst. The composition contains a strong basic aqueous solution having a pH of 10 or more.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN114768479-A
TI Efficient absorbing carbon dioxide gas deep            eutectic solvent that is used for preparing carbon            dioxide absorbent, comprises imidazole compound and            ethanolamine compound, where carbon dioxide gas deep            eutectic solvent by mixing imidazole compound and            ethanolamine compound.
AU LONG Z
   LIU X
   SHI S
   LI S

AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide is processed by creating a carbon dioxide plasma within a liquid medium; and feeding the plasma with carbon dioxide to generate at least carbon monoxide from the carbon dioxide.
   USE - Processing of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - Carbon dioxide plasma is created within a liquid medium and can provide sufficient energy to convert carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide. The liquid medium prevents the process from being affected by potential pollutants, such as nitrogen, and acts to dissipate heat from the recombinants including carbon monoxide. The process to convert carbon dioxide in a cost effective manner uses a plasma within a liquid medium to reduce carbon dioxide and oxidize carbon such that the recombinants create a valuable commodity, syngas, a mixture of carbon monoxide and hydrogen gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an apparatus used for carrying out processing of carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the feed mechanism.
   Stationary carbon block electrode (3)
   Flange (5)
   Lead nut (7)
   Lead screw (8)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Creating the plasma comprises applying an electrical voltage between two spaced electrodes within the liquid medium. Spacing between the electrodes is maintained substantially constant. Carbon dioxide is fed to the plasma through greater than or equal to 1 of the electrodes. The method further comprises feeding the plasma with carbon. The method includes generating hydrogen by the consumption of the carbon dioxide and water by the plasma. The method includes generating oxygen. The liquid medium is under pressure. Preferred Components: The greater than or equal to 1 of electrodes comprises carbon and the electrode is consumed to feed the plasma with carbon. The liquid medium is consumed by the plasma during processing. The liquid medium comprises water.

TI Preparation of            nickel-cobalt/zirconia/magnesium(aluminum) oxide            catalyst for preparing syngas by methane/carbon dioxide            reformation, involves calcining            nickel-cobalt-zirconium-magnesium-aluminum hydrotalcite            precursor.
AU SUN Y

   DU Y

AE SHANGHAI CURUI LOW-CARBON ENERGY TECHNOL (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202140102H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of                nickel-cobalt/zirconia/magnesium(aluminum) oxide                catalyst, involves synthesizing a                nickel-cobalt-zirconium-magnesium-aluminum                hydrotalcite precursor by uniform coprecipitation                using nickel and cobalt as active phase, and                zirconia as additive, and calcining.
   USE - Preparation of                nickel-cobalt/zirconia/magnesium(aluminum) oxide                catalyst for preparing syngas by methane/carbon                dioxide reformation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple preparation of                catalyst having high activity and prolonged service                life.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method preferably                  involves dissolving nickel nitrate hexahydrate,                  cobalt nitrate hexahydrate, zirconium nitrate                  pentahydrate, magnesium nitrate hexahydrate, and                  aluminum nitrate nonahydrate to deionized water                  to obtain a mixed salt solution, adding urea,                  stirring and dissolving, adding ethylenediamine                  to obtain a mixed solution, transferring the                  mixed solution to a PTFE-lined stainless steel                  reaction kettle, sealing, placing in an oven at                  95 degrees C, reacting at constant temperature                  for 24-48 hours, taking out the reaction kettle,                  cooling to room temperature, filtering, washing                  with deionized water until the filtrate is                  neutral, to obtain a filter cake, dispersing the                  filter cake in acetone, repeating the process                  thrice, dispersing, filtering, washing the filter                  cake with ethanol 2-3 times, drying in an oven at                  80 degrees C for 12 hours to obtain a precursor,                  and calcining the precursor in a muffle furnace                  at 700 degrees C for 5 hours. Preferred                  Composition: The molar ratio of trivalent                  aluminum with respect to total content of                  divalent nickel, divalent cobalt, divalent                  magnesium, and trivalent aluminum is 0.2-0.33:1.                  The molar ratio of tetravalent zirconium and                  divalent nickel is 0.003-0.01:1. The molar ratio                  of divalent cobalt and divalent nickel is                  0.005-0.015:1. The molar ratio of urea and                  nitrate radical in the mixed salt solution is                  3-4:1. The addition amount of ethylenediamine is                  1-5% of the volume of mixed solution. The usage                  amount of acetone each time of dispersing the                  filter cake is 10-20 times the mass of filter                  cake. The usage amount of ethanol each time of                  washing the filter cake is 2-5 times the mass of                  filter cake.

AE UNIV CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UCST-C)
GA 2020B27195
AB    NOVELTY - Photoelectric catalytic system comprises anode and cathode. The cathode includes foamed copper and zinc-doped cuprous oxide film which are deposited on the surface of the foamed copper.
   USE - Photoelectric catalytic system is used in photoelectric catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to prepare acetic acid (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photoelectric catalytic system has high catalytic activity and selectivity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The solvent of the electrolyte is water and lactic acid. The anode includes conductive glass. The N-type semiconductor layer are compounded on the surface of conductive glass. The noble metal nanocrystalline layer are compounded on the surface of N-type semiconductor. The N-type semiconductor layer is N-doped titanium dioxide nanosheet layer, N-doped tungsten trioxide nanosheet layer or N-doped zinc oxide nanosheet layer. The precious metal nanocrystalline layer is gold nanocrystalline layer or silver nanocrystalline layer. Preferred Conditions: The mass of the zinc source is 4.5-5.5 wt.% of the solvent in the electrolyte. The mass of the copper source is 6.5-7.5 wt.% of the solvent in the electrolyte. The mass ratio of the water to lactic acid is (60-80):(40-20). The zinc-doped cuprous oxide film is electrodeposited, voltage of the working electrode relative to the reference electrode is (-)0.3-(-)0.4 volt. The thickness of the noble metal nanocrystalline layer is 4-6 nanometer. Preferred Process: The cathode is prepared by adopting three-electrode electrolysis system, using foamed copper as a working electrode, and electrodepositing zinc-doped cuprous oxide film in electrolyte containing zinc source and copper source to obtain cathode. The anode is prepared by forming semiconductor layer on the surface of the conductive glass by a hydrothermal method, and then calcining in ammonia atmosphere to obtain conductive glass compounded with N-type semiconductor layer. The electron beam evaporation is used to deposit precious metal nanocrystals on the surface of the conductive glass compounded with the N-type semiconductor layer to obtain anode.

TI Protective gas for laser welding comprises argon, helium, and carbon dioxide at determined volume ratio.
AU LONG J
   DONG H
   WANG J
   ZHENG S
   WANG G
   TANG J
   SHAO J
AE TIANJIN MASITE CAR BODY EQUIP TECHNOLOGY (TIAN-Non-standard)
   MASTER LIGHTWEIGHT TECHNOLOGY TIANJIN CO LTD (MAST-Non-standard)
GA 2019A4411R
AB    NOVELTY - A protective gas comprises 7.5-15 vol.% argon (Ar), 2.5-7.5 vol.% helium (He), and 80-90 vol.% carbon dioxide (CO2).
   USE - Protective gas for laser welding.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas increases strength of welding line.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a laser welding method, which comprises providing to-be-welded work-piece through clamp and machine tool control, adjusting laser irradiated on to-be-welded spot size on surface of work-piece; and welding at 2-4 m/minute.

PT P
PN CN110128851-A; CN110128851-B
TI Preparing hydrophobic nano calcium carbonate comprises placing calcium hydroxide, modifier and carbon dioxide-containing gas in microreactor for carbonization.
AU LUO G
   HAN C
   WANG K
AE UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
GA 201972736U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hydrophobic nano calcium carbonate comprises placing calcium hydroxide, modifier and carbon dioxide-containing gas in a microreactor for carbonization.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing hydrophobic nano calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: inhibits agglomeration of nanoparticles while forming the nanoparticles, high particle dispersibility and uniformity of modification; prepared hydrophobic nano calcium carbonate has high activation degree, uniform particle morphology and narrow particle size distribution, and production cycle is shortened and is economical and is suitable for industrial applications.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The micro-reactor is micro-screen reactor or membrane-dispersed micro-reactor, where micro-mesh has a diameter of 0.2-1 mm, number of mesh holes is at least 1 and average pore diameter of dispersed film is 0.5-100 mu m.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises mixing calcium hydroxide slurry and modifier uniformly to obtain slurry D and passing slurry D and carbon dioxide-containing gas into the micro reactor to carry out carbonization reaction to obtain hydrophobic nano calcium carbonate. The preparation of slurry D is obtained by mixing slurry A with modifier and emulsification at a high speed to obtain a slurry C, and mixing slurry C and slurry B uniformly. The slurry A and slurry B are each a calcium hydroxide slurry and concentrations of the slurry A and the slurry B are the same or different. The high speed emulsification speed is 5000-30000 revolutions/minute and high speed emulsification time is 5 minutes. Preferred Components: The concentration of slurry A and slurry B are both 5-100 g/l. The volume ratio of slurry A and slurry B with same concentration is 0.1-10. The modifier is at least one of stearic acid, stearate, phosphate, silane coupling agent, titanic acid ester coupling agent or aluminic acid ester coupling agent. The amount of modifier is 0.5-5 wt.% of the theoretical reaction amount of calcium hydroxide. The carbon dioxide-containing gas is a mixture of carbon dioxide and an inert gas and volume fraction of carbon dioxide in the mixed gas is 10-100%. The volume flow ratio of mixed gas to the slurry D is 1-10. The carbonization reaction temperature is 0-50 degrees C. The slurry is continuously circulated until the pH of the reaction solution is 6-8 in the carbonization reaction and aeration is stopped. The method comprises taking precipitate by centrifugation after carbonization reaction is completed, washing and drying to obtain hydrophobic nano calcium carbonate. The drying temperature is 100 degrees C and drying time is 3-12 hours.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); G01 (Inorganic pigments and non-fibrous fillers (C09C).)

PN CN109666138-A; CN109666138-B
TI Preparing aliphatic polycarbonate involves heating carbon dioxide, propylene oxide and comonomer by heterogeneous catalyst to obtain aliphatic polycarbonate, where comonomer comprises styrene, acrylonitrile or vinyl acetate.
AU ZHOU Q
   GUO H
   CAI Y
   WANG X
AE CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
GA 2019399641
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing aliphatic polycarbonate involves heating carbon dioxide, propylene oxide and comonomer by heterogeneous catalyst to obtain aliphatic polycarbonate, where comonomer comprises styrene, acrylonitrile or vinyl acetate with glycidyl methacrylate copolymer or glycidyl methacrylate homopolymer.
   USE - Method for preparing aliphatic polycarbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare aliphatic polycarbonate that reduces byproduct generation, reaction time, investment and production cost, and improves the device utilization rate and reaction rate to 2-4 times.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Condition: The molecule of the comonomer is 3-50 kg/mole. The epoxy equivalent weight of the comonomer is 140-1000 g/equivalents. The ratio of the copolymerized monomer and propylene oxide is 1:(99-999). The ratio of heterogeneous catalyst and propylene oxide is (0.2-2):100. The ratio of carbon dioxide and propylene oxide is (30-50):100. The polymerization temperature is 100-15 degrees C. The polymerization time is 1-5 hours. The polymerization pressure is 3-6 megapascal. The cooling is carried out below 50 degrees C in 30 minutes. Preferred Components: The heterogeneous catalyst is rare earth three-way catalyst or dicarboxylic acid zinc catalyst. The composition of the rare earth three-way catalyst is alkyl zinc, glycerol and metal salts which are classified into rare earth salts and non-rare earth salts. The zinc is ethyl zinc, n-propyl zinc, isopropyl zinc, n-butyl zinc, One of isobutyl zinc, phenyl zinc or benzyl zinc. The structural formula of the rare earth salt is MXnYm, where n, m is positive integer of 0-3, sum of n and m is 3, M is Yttrium, Lanthanum, Cerium, Praseodymium, Neodymium, Samarium, Europium, Gadolinium, Terbium, Dysprosium, Holmium, Erbium, Ytterbium, Lutetium, X is carboxylic acid group or sulfonic acid group with Ka value of 10-3 or more, sulfamic acid group, a trifluoroacetic acid group, a trichloroacetic acid group, a dichloro group a group of an acetate group, a chloroacetic acid group, an o-chlorobenzoic acid group, an alpha-tartaric acid group, a benzenesulfonic acid group or a naphthalene sulfonic acid group, and Y is chloro group. The structural formula of the non-rare earth salt is MXnYm, where M is zinc or aluminum, n, m is positive integer of 0-3, X is citrate group, alpha-tartaric acid group, iminodiacetic acid group, trichloroacetic acid group, dichloroacetic acid group, chloroacetic acid group, sulfamic acid group, o-chlorobenzoic acid group, benzene a sulfonic acid group or a naphthalenesulfonic acid group. Preferred Process: The dicarboxylic acid zinc catalyst is prepared by reacting divalent metal carboxylic acid monovalent metal salt and zinc salt in water, and collecting solid formed by the reaction to obtain dicarboxylic acid zinc catalyst. The initial pH of the reaction value is 5.2-6.3. The end point of the pH is 3.8-4.7. The polymerization further comprises cooling and deliming. The deashing is carried out by removing unreacted propylene oxide and carbon dioxide within 1 hour under conditions of 50 degrees C or more and less than 0.02 megapascal.
DC A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A18 (Addition polymers in general.); A21 (Epoxides; aminoplasts; phenoplasts.)

PN CN109400826-A
TI Preparation of lignin-based acrylate polymer by emulsion polymerization, involves esterifying lignosulfonate with conjugated diene under supercritical carbon dioxide conditions.
AU JIN L
   ZHANG Y
   WANG C
   CHU F
AE INST CHEM IND FOREST PROD CHINESE ACAD (CLYK-C)
GA 201922674C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of lignin-based acrylate polymer by an emulsion polymerization process, involves ultrasonically dissolving lignosulfonate purified by the ultrafiltration membrane in N,N-dimethylacetamide, esterifying lignosulfonate with a conjugated diene under supercritical carbon dioxide conditions, and performing redox-polymerization of the product and the acrylate monomer.
   USE - Preparation of lignin-based acrylate polymer by emulsion polymerization process.
   ADVANTAGE - The lignin-based acrylate polymer is environmentally-friendly, safe and energy saving and is economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxidizing agent is potassium chlorate, hydrogen peroxide, tert-butyl hydroperoxide, potassium persulfate and/or ammonium persulfate. The reducing agent is oxalic acid, sulfite, ferrous sulfate, ferrous chloride, sodium bisulfite and/or thiols.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The lignosulfonate is obtained by fractional purification through an ultrafiltration membrane to obtain a lignosulfonate having a molecular weight of 400-600. Preferred Conditions: The supercritical esterification is carried out at 50-70 degrees C, under pressure of 15-30 MPa for 3-5 hours. Preferred Components: The conjugated dibasic acid is hexadienoic acid, heptadienoic acid, pentadienoic acid, decadienoic acid, and/or octadecadienoic acid. Preferred Compositions: The acrylate monomer comprises 0.5-2.0 parts mass acrylic acid, 20-70 parts mass butyl acrylate and 30-80 parts mass methyl methacrylate. The mass of the redox initiator is 1-3% of the total mass of the monomers. The amount of the modified lignosulfonate is 20-40% of the total amount of the monomers.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A11 (Polysaccharides; natural rubber; other natural polymers (only a restricted range of (modified) natural polymers are included. PN CN108929240-A; CN108929240-B
TI Preparation of 2-hydroxyl-5-aminobenzoic acid            comprises taking p-aminophenol, alkaline catalyst and            alkaline earth metal oxide, carboxylation reactin under            carbon dioxide atmosphere, and acidifying with            inorganic acid.
AU SHENG J
   HUANG F
   CHEN W
   WANG T
   JIA C
AE ZHEJIANG SANMEN HENGKANG PHARM CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 201899496W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of 2-hydroxyl-5-aminobenzoic acid                comprises taking p-aminophenol, alkaline catalyst                and alkaline earth metal oxide, carboxylation                reactin under carbon dioxide atmosphere to obtain                2-carboxyl-5-aminobenzoate; and acidifying with                inorganic acid.
   USE - Method for preparing 2-hydroxyl-5-aminobenzoic                acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids material agglomeration and                side reaction material, improves product yield and                purity, and has simple post-processing                operation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkaline catalyst                  is potassium carbonate, potassium hydroxide, or                  sodium hydroxide. The alkaline earth metal oxide                  is magnesium oxide and/or calcium oxide. The                  inorganic acid is hydrochloric acid, sulfuric                  acid, nitric acid, or phosphoric acid at amount                  of 30-35%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of                  p-aminophenol and alkaline catalyst is 1:1-1:6.                  The mass ratio of p-aminophenol and alkaline                  earth metal oxide is 0.5:1-3:1.5. Preferred                  Method: The carboxylation reaction is done at                  1.0-4.0 MPa and 160-200 degrees C for 3-5 hours.                  The method further comprises mixing with water at                  10 degrees C or less and solid-liquid                  separating.

TI Regenerating organic gas adsorption saturated activated carbon involves initially calcining organic gas adsorption saturated activated carbon to obtain calcined material, and then finally calcining under carbon dioxide atmosphere.
AU LIU S
   LI W
   XU Z
   TONG G
   MA C
   LUO S
AE UNIV NORTHEAST FORESTRY (UNDB-C)
GA 201842364V
AB    NOVELTY - Method of regenerating organic gas adsorption saturated activated carbon involves: (1) initially calcining the organic gas adsorption saturated activated carbon at 200-550 degrees C to obtain a calcined material (A); and (2) finally calcining the calcined material (A) at 580-1000 degrees C under carbon dioxide atmosphere to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful for regenerating organic gas adsorption saturated activated carbon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (1), the activated carbon is chosen from wood activated carbon, coal activated carbon, nut shell activated carbon and/or sludge-based activated carbon. In step (2), the flow rate of carbon dioxide is 0.5-15 l/minute.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (1) is carried out using a protective gas including helium, argon and/or nitrogen, where the protective gas has a flow rate of 2-10 l/minute. Preferred Condition: The calcining step in step (1) is carried out for 0.5-6.5 hours. The calcining step in step (2) is carried out for 1-10 hours. Preferred Components: The organic gas in step (1) is chosen from benzene, toluene, xylene, formaldehyde, acetic acid, acetone, ether and/or methane.

GA 201840479C

   ADVANTAGE - System can continuously provide purified methane at high concentration. Capability of water to release carbon dioxide may be regulated by changing the temperature of water.    DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A system for purifying biogas comprises biogas separating unit (10) which consists of biogas inlet (11) where biogas contains methane and carbon dioxide, and methane outlet (12); separating unit for methane and carbon dioxide (20) which is connected to biogas inlet and methane outlet; and separating unit for carbon dioxide (30) consisting of heater (31) connected to separating unit for methane and carbon dioxide, carbon dioxide filter (32) connected to heater, water tank (33) connected to carbon dioxide filter, dosing device (36) connected to water tank, and vacuum pump (37) connected to water tank where separating unit for methane and carbon dioxide is mounted between biogas inlet and methane outlet, and separating unit for biogas is also mounted between heater and water tank.

   Biogas separating unit (10)
   Biogas inlet (11)
   Methane outlet (12)
   Separating unit for methane and carbon dioxide (20)
   Separating unit for carbon dioxide (30)

   Water tank (33)
   Dosing device (36)
   Vacuum pump (37)

One portion of carbon dioxide membrane is in vacuum condition, and remaining portion of carbon dioxide membrane is filled by solution containing dissolved and adsorbed carbon dioxide. Water-level controller is mounted on top of water tank. Water drainage device is mounted on sidewall of water tank. Separating unit for carbon dioxide further includes carbon dioxide discharger and water supply which are connected to carbon dioxide filter. Degas pump is mounted between carbon dioxide discharger and carbon dioxide filter. Separating unit for carbon dioxide further includes cooler consisting of hot water pipe and cold water pipe, and connects to water tank by hot water pipe and cold water pipe. System further includes 2 degas pumps and hydraulic pump where one of degas pumps is mounted between biogas inlet and separating unit for methane and carbon dioxide, another one of degas pumps is mounted between separating unit for methane and carbon dioxide and methane outlet, hydraulic pump is mounted between water tank and separating unit for methane and carbon dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Carbon dioxide adsorbents chosen from methyldiethanolamine, triethylenetetramine and/or aminoethylethanolamine are stored in dosing device.

PT P
PN CN111239276-A
TI Determining organic matter content of soil and            sludge, comprises determining value of carbon dioxide            produced by reaction of soil samples or sludge samples            with strong oxidizing acids using headspace gas            chromatograph.
AU HU Z
   TU X
   OUYANG X
   ZHAO T
AE UNIV GUANGXI (UYGU-C)
GA 2020517594
AB    NOVELTY - Determining organic matter content of soil and                sludge, comprises determining the value of carbon                dioxide produced by the reaction of soil samples or                sludge samples with strong oxidizing acids using                the headspace gas chromatograph, where the method                for indirect determination of organic matter                comprises (i) taking 0.05-0.2 g soil sample in the                headspace bottle, adding potassium dichromate                solution, (ii) immediately covering the headspace                bottle, (iii) measuring different volumes of                organic matter standard solution, (iv) placing the                headspace in the headspace sampler and performing                headspace gas chromatography analysis, (v) taking                0.05-0.2 g sludge samples in the digestion tube,                (vi) preheating the graphite digestion apparatus to                170-180 degrees C in advance, (vii) measuring                different volumes of organic standard solutions in                the headspace bottle, (viii) placing the digestion                solution into a small test tube after digestion,                and (ix) comparing carbon dioxide signal value with                the standard curve.
   USE - The method is useful for determining organic                matter content of soil and sludge.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has good reproducibility, high                accuracy, the relative standard deviation is less                than 5%, and the recovery rate is between                97.9-106%, and realizes batch analysis of the OM                content of the high-throughput sample.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Determining organic matter content of soil and                sludge, comprises determining the value of carbon                dioxide produced by the reaction of soil samples or                sludge samples with strong oxidizing acids using                the headspace gas chromatograph, where the method                for indirect determination of organic matter                comprises (i) taking 0.05-0.2 g soil sample in the                headspace bottle, adding potassium dichromate                solution, utilizing samples of the same quality                acidified with hydrochloric acid as blank controls,                (ii) immediately covering the headspace bottle,                shaking uniformly, and placing at 120-140 degrees C                for redox reaction, removing the test tube, and                cooling to normal temperature, (iii) measuring                different volumes of organic matter standard                solution in the headspace bottle and preparing a                low-content organic matter standard solution of                0-100 g/kg, placing the headspace vial containing                the standard solution to be tested in the headspace                sampler, setting the operating conditions of the                headspace sampler and the operating conditions of                the gas chromatograph, performing headspace gas                chromatography analysis and detection, obtaining a                standard curve according to the corresponding                relationship between the obtained signal value of                the peak area of the carbon dioxide chromatogram                and an organic standard solution of known                concentration, (iv) placing the headspace in the                headspace sampler and performing headspace gas                chromatography analysis using the same headspace                sampler operating conditions and gas chromatograph                operating conditions, recording the signal value                generated by the carbon dioxide of the sample,                comparing the gas chromatographic signal value with                the standard curve and calculating the content of                organic matter in the soil, (v) taking 0.05-0.2 g                sludge samples in the digestion tube, adding                potassium dichromate solution and equal volume                concentrated sulfuric acid solution, (vi)                preheating the graphite digestion apparatus to                170-180 degrees C in advance, placing the digestion                tube, boiling for 5-10 minutes when the liquid in                the digestion tube boils and bubbles start to                count, removing the digestion tube, and cooling,                (vii) measuring different volumes of organic                standard solutions in the headspace bottle,                preparing into high content organic standard                solution of 0-800 g/kg respectively, placing the                headspace bottle containing the standard solution                to be tested in the headspace sampler, setting the                operating conditions of the headspace sampler and                the operating conditions of the gas chromatograph,                carrying out headspace gas chromatography analysis                and detection, obtaining a standard curve according                to the corresponding relationship between the                obtained signal value of the peak area of the                carbon dioxide spectrum and an organic standard                solution of known concentration, (viii) placing the                digestion solution into a small test tube after                digestion, and placing in the headspace bottle,                slowly adding oxalic acid solution into the                headspace bottle, immediately capping the headspace                bottle, shaking uniformly, analyzing by headspace                gas chromatograph, and recording the signal value                generated by carbon dioxide, and (ix) comparing                carbon dioxide signal value with the standard curve                and calculating the content of organic matter in                the sludge.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  sampling mass of the soil sample is 0.05-0.2 g,                  which is packed in a 20 ml headspace bottle. The                  concentration of potassium dichromate solution is                  0.136 mol/l, the concentration of hydrochloric                  acid is 0.001-0.01 mol/l, and the volume is 1-5                  ml. The soil includes sandy soil, cinnamon soil                  and red soil and the sludge includes residual                  sludge from sewage treatment plants and                  sedimentary sediments from rivers and lakes. The                  calibration curves of soil samples and sludge                  samples are 0-100 g/kg is the linear regression                  equation of the standard curve of low content                  organic matter as given in the specification.                  Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the time of                  the redox reaction is 10-40 minutes. In the step                  (iii), the analysis conditions of the headspace                  gas chromatograph comprises headspace operating                  conditions having furnace temperature 50-100                  degrees C, pipeline temperature 60-110 degrees C,                  transmission line temperature 70-120 degrees C,                  equilibrium time 3-10 minutes, shaking degree is                  strong level, sample interval is 3-15 minutes;                  and gas chromatography operating conditions GS-Q                  capillary chromatography column (30 mx 0.53 mm)                  used in the operating conditions of the gas                  chromatograph, the inlet pressure is 6 kPa, the                  oven temperature is 30-100 degrees C, the running                  time is 2-10 minutes, and the temperature of TCD                  detector is 150-250 degrees C. In the step (vi),                  the amount of the sludge sample used is 0.05-0.02                  g, adding 5-10 ml potassium dichromate solution                  (0.136 mol/l), 5-10 ml concentrated sulfuric acid                  solution (18.4mol/l), cancelling the solution has                  been, placing the digestion solution 0.1-2 ml                  into the headspace bottle, and slowly adding 1-5                  ml 0.5-2 mol/l oxalic acid solution into the                  headspace bottle, immediately capping the                  headspace vial, shaking uniformly, and analyzing                  by headspace gas chromatography. In the step                  (viii), the analysis conditions of the headspace                  gas chromatograph comprises headspace operating                  conditions having furnace temperature 50-100                  degrees C, pipeline temperature 60-110 degrees C,                  transmission line temperature 70-120 degrees C,                  equilibrium time 3-10 minutes, shaking degree is                  strong level, sample interval is 6-12 minutes;                  and gas chromatography operating conditions GS-Q                  capillary chromatography column (30 mx 0.53 mm)                  used in the operating conditions of the gas                  chromatograph, the inlet pressure is 6 kPa, the                  oven temperature is 30-100 degrees C, the running                  time is 2-10 minutes, and the temperature of TCD                  detector is 150-250 degrees C.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)
MC J04-B01C5; J04-C01; J04-C04; S03-E09C1
IP G01N-030/02; G01N-030/06; G01N-030/86

TI Carbon dioxide-based copolymer foam plastic comprises carbon dioxide-propylene oxide copolymer, toughening agent is selected from ethylene-octene copolymer, ethylene-methyl acrylate-glycidyl methacrylate copolymer.
AU GAO F
   DONG Y
   ZHOU Q
   WANG X
AE CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
GA 202103198D
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide-based copolymer foam plastic comprises 100 pts. wt. carbon dioxide-epoxycyclohexane copolymer, 5-30 pts. wt. carbon dioxide-propylene oxide copolymer, 2-20 pts. wt. toughening agent, 0.1-1 pts. wt. transesterifier.
   USE - Carbon dioxide-based copolymer foam plastic.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide-based copolymer foam plastic has good foaming properties, elastic modulus and compressive strength, achieves better interaction, elastic modulus reaches 804 megapascal, and compressive strength (50%) reaches 26 megapascal.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing carbon dioxide-based copolymer foamed plastics, which involves mixing carbon dioxide-epoxycyclohexane copolymer, carbon dioxide-propylene oxide copolymer, toughening agent and transesterification agent, granulating to obtain plastic particles, placing plastic particles obtained in carbon dioxide of 3-8 megapascal, reacting, and then subjecting to pressure relief treatment, and cooling to obtain carbon dioxide-based copolymer plastic foam.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The toughening agent is selected from ethylene-octene copolymer, ethylene-methyl acrylate-glycidyl methacrylate copolymer, methyl methacrylate-butadiene-styrene copolymer, polyester thermoplastic polyurethane elastomers synthesized by polyol and isophorone diisocyanate. The transesterification agent is selected from tetra-n-butyl titanate and/or tetraisopropyl titanate. Preferred Conditions: The number average molecular weight of the carbon dioxide-epoxycyclohexane copolymer is 40-60 kg/mol, and molecular weight distribution is 1.8-3. The number average molecular weight of the carbon dioxide-propylene oxide copolymer is 80-150kg/mol. The molecular weight distribution is 2.2-3.

PN CN114479940-A
TI Converting carbonaceous biomass raw material into            gaseous products, comprises supplying biomass material            to reactor, introducing carbonaceous feedstock, alkali            metal gasification catalyst, and portion of steam into            gasifier.
AU WANG X
   GU S
   XUE D
   SUN Z
AE SINO-INDO ALLWIN BEIJING TRADING CO LTD (SINO-Non-standard)
GA 202276460F
AB    NOVELTY - Converting carbonaceous biomass feedstock into                multiple gaseous products comprises supplying a                biomass feedstock to a reactor, where the biomass                feedstock is in particulate form, or comprises an                aqueous slurry. The small portion combustion of                biomass feedstock in the reactor generates thermal                energy and exhaust gas containing carbon dioxide in                the presence of an oxygen-containing gas at an                appropriate temperature and pressure. A                carbonaceous feedstock is introduced into an alkali                metal gasification catalyst, where the carbonaceous                feedstock in the gasifier is reacted in the                presence of steam and an alkali metal gasification                catalyst at 450-900degrees Celsius and a pressure                of 400-1000 psig to form a carbonaceous feedstock.                The carbonaceous feedstock comprises streams of                gaseous products including methane and hydrogen,                carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide,                ammonia, and other higher hydrocarbons.
   USE - Method for converting carbonaceous biomass                feedstock into multiple gaseous products.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to convert carbonaceous                biomass raw material into gas products in an                integrated catalytic gasification process, where                the carbonaceous material is converted into                combustible gas, e.g. methane, carbon monoxide,                hydrogen and carbon dioxide in a simple and                cost-effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Converting carbonaceous biomass feedstock into                multiple gaseous products comprises supplying a                biomass feedstock to a reactor, where the biomass                feedstock is in particulate form, or comprises an                aqueous slurry. The small portion combustion of                biomass feedstock in the reactor generates thermal                energy and exhaust gas containing carbon dioxide in                the presence of an oxygen-containing gas at an                appropriate temperature and pressure. A                carbonaceous feedstock is introduced into an alkali                metal gasification catalyst, where the carbonaceous                feedstock in the gasifier is reacted in the                presence of steam and an alkali metal gasification                catalyst at 450-900degrees Celsius and a pressure                of 400-1000 psig to form a carbonaceous feedstock.                The carbonaceous feedstock comprises streams of                gaseous products including methane and hydrogen,                carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide,                ammonia, and other higher hydrocarbons. The                multiple gaseous products are partially separated                to produce a product stream comprising a major                amount of one of the gaseous products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The gas stream                  comprises carbon dioxide produced by the                  combustion and catalytic gasification of                  carbonaceous feedstocks. The gas stream comprises                  carbon monoxide and hydrogen. The alkali metal                  gasification catalyst comprises a source of                  alkali metal. The thermal energy is transferred                  to a heat exchanger in contact with a water                  source to generate steam, where the product                  stream comprises a main amount of methane.

Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

PN CN114428123-A
TI Method for analyzing and detecting laser thermal cracking solid liquid by starting double-color infrared thermometer recording software, starting carbon dioxide laser, starting laser thermal cracking, measuring accurate quality by high precision electronic balance, calculating solid product quality.
AU WANG L
AE BEIJING FENGCE BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202271588W
AB    NOVELTY - The method comprises preparation and placement of the sample, non-volatile sample at 105 degrees C temperature dry 2h in the box, placed in the centerposition of the sample plate, using high precision electronic balance with the precision of 0.01mg to measure the accurate quality of the sample, placing the sample plate in the central position of the laser thermal cracking reactor, setting the thermal cracking condition, setting laser irradiation mode and irradiation power on the carbon dioxide laser controller, creating a thermal cracking atmosphere, determining the sweeping speed by gas composition, starting the double-color infrared thermometer recording software, starting the carbon dioxide laser, starting laser thermal cracking, analyzing and detecting the solid gas product, immediately starting gas phase chromatographic analysis detection program after finishing cracking, measuring accurate quality by high precision electronic balance, and calculating the solid product quality.
   USE - Method for analyzing and detecting laser thermal cracking solid liquid used for pyrolysis of carbonaceous material e.g. acetylene. Can also be used in carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: integrates the reaction part and the detection part of the laser and reactor to improve the detection efficiency, sensitivity and accuracy; has high temperature air tightness by reactor; can measure the temperature in the reactor; can measure the accurate quality of the sample and the residue after reaction; is convenient for collecting the remainder for other analysis; can directly enter the detecting part to detect by pyrolysis product; can accurately detect the pyrolysis gas phase product by detecting part; has fast speed, small sample quantity, high precision, high sensitivity and so on; can simulate different pyrolysis conditions of the carbonaceous material, and quantitatively analyzing the pyrolysis property under the condition; can provide the analysis detection basis for pyrolysis process under extreme conditions e.g. plasma cracking.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating the method for analyzing and detecting laser thermal cracking solid liquid (Drawing includes non-English language text).

TI Forming composite material by disposing dried            plant material and nanoparticles in supercritical            fluid, where cellular structure of dried plant material            expands when disposed in supercritical fluid such that            nanoparticles migrate into and are embedded within            expanded cellular structure.
AU MIELEWSKI D F
   LANGHORST A E
AE FORD GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (FORD-C)
   FORD GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (FORD-C)
GA 2021D9171M
AB    NOVELTY - Forming a composite material, involves                disposing dried plant material and nanoparticles in                a supercritical fluid, where a cellular structure                of the dried material expands when disposed in the                supercritical fluid such that the nanoparticles                migrate into and are embedded within the expanded                cellular structure of the disposed dried material,                or method for forming a composite material,                involves disposing dried plant fibers,                nanoparticles and carbon dioxide supercritical                fluid in a vessel, where a cellular structure of                the dried plant fiber expands such that carbon                dioxide supercritical fluid and nanoparticles flow                into the expanded cellular structure and                nanoparticles are embedded within the expanded                cellular structure, reducing a pressure within the                vessel, removing the disposed dried fibers with                embedded nanoparticles from the vessel, mixing the                removed dried fibers with a polymer, forming a                polymer-nanoparticle mixture, and forming a portion                using the polymer-nanoparticle mixture.
   USE - The method is useful for forming composite                material used for forming component used for                forming vehicle (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method forms lightweight, strong, and                sustainable components.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for forming a composite material,                involves disposing dried plant material and                nanoparticles in a supercritical fluid, where a                cellular structure of the dried plant material                expands when disposed in the supercritical fluid                such that the nanoparticles migrate into and are                embedded within the expanded cellular structure of                the disposed dried plant material, or method for                forming a composite material, involves disposing                dried plant fibers, nanoparticles and carbon                dioxide supercritical fluid in a vessel, where a                cellular structure of the dried plant fibers                expands such that the carbon dioxide supercritical                fluid and nanoparticles flow into the expanded                cellular structure and the nanoparticles are                embedded within the expanded cellular structure,                reducing a pressure within the vessel such that the                carbon dioxide supercritical fluid becomes a gas,                removing the disposed dried plant fibers with                embedded nanoparticles from the vessel, mixing the                removed dried plant fibers with embedded                nanoparticles with a polymer and forming a                polymer-nanoparticle mixture. and forming a portion                using the polymer-nanoparticle mixture, wherei the                portion comprises the nanoparticles within a                polymer matrix.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step of disposing the                  dried plant material and nanoparticles in the                  supercritical fluid, involves flowing the                  supercritical fluid into a vessel containing the                  dried plant material and nanoparticles, and                  flowing the supercritical fluid and nanoparticles                  into a vessel containing the dried plant                  material. The method involves introducing a                  chemical additive to the supercritical fluid,                  where the chemical additive removes                  hemicellulose, lignin or pectins from the dried                  plant material comprising a sheet of dried plant                  material or an individual plant cells, removing                  the disposed dried plant material with embedded                  nanoparticles from the supercritical fluid,                  drying the plant material with embedded                  nanoparticles, mixing the plant material with                  embedded nanoparticles within a polymer matrix,                  post-processing the dried plant material with                  embedded nanoparticles prior to mixing within the                  polymer matrix, and applying a magnetic field or                  an electric field to the dried plant material and                  nanoparticles disposed in the supercritical fluid                  such that the nanoparticles migrate into and are                  embedded within the expanded cellular structure                  of the disposed dried plant material. The                  post-processing involves chopping, winding,                  chemical treatment, heat treatment, washing,                  radiation treatment, and/or steam explosion. The                  step of disposing the dried plant fibers,                  nanoparticles and carbon dioxide supercritical                  fluid in the vessel involves placing the dried                  plant material and nanoparticles in the vessel,                  flowing the carbon dioxide supercritical fluid                  into the vessel, placing the dried plant material                  in the vessel, and flowing the carbon dioxide                  supercritical fluid and nanoparticles into the                  vessel. The method involves adding chemical                  additive to carbon dioxide supercritical fluid,                  where the chemical additive removes                  hemicellulose, lignin or pectins from the dried                  plant material, and the chemical additive is                  water.Preferred Components: The nanoparticles                  comprise carbon-based nanoparticles, metals                  and/or metal oxide nanoparticles, polymer                  nanoparticles, inorganic nanoparticles,                  functionalized nanoparticles, carbon coated metal                  nanoparticles, or their combinations. The                  supercritical fluid is supercritical carbon                  dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dried plant                  material comprises zucchini, corn, tomato,                  soybean, bitter melon, rapeseed, radish,                  ryegrass, lettuce, cucumber, cabbage, red                  spinach, faba bean, Arabidopsis, carrot, onion,                  barley, rice, switchgrass, tobacco, wheat, garden                  cress, Sorghum, mustard, alfalfa, Onobrychis,                  pumpkin, garden pea, leek, peppers, flax, Agave,                  cattail, mung bean, cotton, algae, lemna gibba,                  cilantro, squash, bean, grasses, Landoltia                  punctata, Eelsholtzia splendens, Microcystis                  aeruginosa, Elodea densa, bamboo, cane,                  carnation, monocot or dicot, bast fibers, lily,                  sugar cane, monocot, Brassica rapa, hemp, or                  their combinations.

TI Boron-modified pentacoordinate aluminum-rich            aluminum oxide directionally loaded ruthenium-based            catalyst useful in catalyzing methanation reaction of            carbon dioxide comprises ruthenium nano-particles,            boron and pentacoordinate aluminum.
AU LI F
   ZHANG J
   FAN G
   YANG L
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 2021A02922
AB    NOVELTY - Boron-modified pentacoordinate aluminum-rich                aluminum oxide directionally loaded ruthenium-based                catalyst comprises 2-4 wt.% ruthenium                nano-particles of average particle diameter as 1-2                nm, 2-10 wt.% boron, 20-30 wt.% pentacoordinate                aluminum, where the dispersion degree is 90-100%,                the specific surface area of the catalyst is                200-450 m2/g.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in catalyzing                methanation reaction of carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has high dispersion, high                specific surface area and improved stability; and                exhibits good catalytic performance in carbon                dioxide methanation reaction with conversion rate                of carbon dioxide as more than 80% and selectivity                more than 99%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing boron-modified pentacoordinate                aluminum-rich aluminum oxide directionally loaded                ruthenium-based catalyst comprises (a) preparing                aluminum salt solution with concentration of                aluminum (III) ions as 0.05-0.2 mol/l, preparing                borohydride solution with concentration of 1-8                mol/l, adding aluminum salt solution and                borohydride solution at uniform speed in the volume                ratio of 1:2-2:1 into a microliquid membrane                reactor to react for 3-5 minutes, then transferring                the obtained mixture into a hydrothermal reaction                kettle, reacting at 200-250 degrees C for 5-40                hours, cooling, filtering, washing to neutral                state, drying, grinding to obtain alumina carrier                precursor, (b) roasting the precursor in a muffle                furnace at 300-600 degrees C for 3-6 hours to                obtain alumina carrier, (c) dispersing 0.3-1 g                alumina carrier in 50-100 ml deionized water, using                sodium hydroxide solution to adjust pH value to                8-12, dropping 1-20 ml ruthenium chloride solution                of 5-50 mmol/l concentration, stirring for 3-10                hours, centrifuging, washing to neutral state,                drying, grinding to obtain precursor, and (d)                reducing precursor in mixed atmosphere of nitrogen                and hydrogen at 200-450 degrees C for 1-4 hours;                and
   (2) application of the boron-modified                pentacoordinate aluminum-rich aluminum oxide                directionally loaded ruthenium-based catalyst in                catalyzing methanation reaction of carbon                dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The borohydride                  includes at least one of potassium borohydride,                  sodium borohydride and lithium borohydride.                  Preferred Method: The methanation reaction                  includes adding 0.1-0.4 g boron-modified                  pentacoordinate aluminum-rich aluminum oxide                  directionally loaded ruthenium-based catalyst                  into a fixed bed reactor, introducing hydrogen                  and carbon dioxide mixed reaction gas, and                  reacting at 300-400 degrees C.

PN CN110841622-A; CN110841622-B
TI Indium oxide-zinc oxide nano heterojunction            photocatalytic material for catalyzing reduction            reaction of carbon dioxide to produce carbon fuel such            as carbon monoxide, methane, ethane, and ethylene,            comprises hollow rod-shaped indium oxide.
AU LI J
   ZHAO C
   ZHOU A
   XIE Y
AE UNIV BEIJING TECHNOLOGY (UYBT-C)
GA 2020203282
AB    NOVELTY - Indium oxide-zinc oxide nano heterojunction                photocatalytic material comprises hollow rod-shaped                indium oxide, which is externally loaded with a                layer of leaf-like zinc oxide.
   USE - Indium oxide-zinc oxide nano heterojunction                photocatalytic material for catalyzing reduction                reaction of carbon dioxide to produce carbon fuel                such as carbon monoxide, methane, ethane, and                ethylene (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The indium oxide-zinc oxide nano                heterojunction photocatalytic material has a                hexagonal prism-like structure, which facilitates                the diffusion of reaction substrates and products                for catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to carbon                fuel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for                following:
   (1) a method for preparing an indium                oxide-zinc oxide nano heterojunction photocatalytic                material, which involves preparing indium-metal                organic frameworks by dissolving an indium salt                solution and terephthalic acid in N,                N-dimethylformamide, and transferring the mixed                solution to a reaction container after stirring,                where reaction conditions are 90-150 degrees C and                constant temperature is 0.5-12 hours and cooling to                room temperature, centrifuging, washing, and                drying; dissolving indium oxide-metal organic                frameworks-zinc-zeolitic imidazolate framework with                polyvinylpyrrolidone (PVP), and stirring in                methanol for 0.5-24 hours at room temperature, and                separating the solid sample by filtration;adding a                certain amount of zinc salt aqueous solution to the                solid sample and soaking it for 0.5-12 hours, and                then adding the 2-methylimidazole aqueous solution                to form a mixed solution, and stirring for 0.5-12                hours and centrifuging, washing, and drying                product; raising temperature at 400-900 degrees C                for 0.5-4 hours at a temperature increase rate of                1-15 degrees C per minute; and
   (2) a method for using indium oxide-zinc oxide                nano heterojunction photocatalytic material for                catalyzing reduction of carbon dioxide to produce                carbon fuel, which involves weighing catalyst first                with water for surface wetting, and then using                triethylamine for surface wetting, using xenon lamp                as the light source for photocatalytic experiments,                and using carbon dioxide as the reaction gas for                curing and catalysis reaction, and balacing for one                hour in the dark and then illuminate to produce                carbon fuel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The molar ratio of                  terephthalic acid to indium salt is 5:1-1:1,                  preferably 2:1, and the concentration of                  terephthalic acid is 0.01-0.1 mole per litre. The                  mass ratio of indium-metal organic frameworks and                  polyvinylpyrrolidone is 10:1-1:10, preferably                  1:1, and the concentration of                  polyvinylpyrrolidone in methanol is 1-10                  milligram per millilitre. The molar ratio of zinc                  salt and 2-methylimidazole is 5:1-1:1, where                  concentration of 3:1,2-methylimidazole in the                  mixed solution is 0.1-5 millimole per litre. The                  mass ratio of indium-metal organic frameworks and                  2-methylimidazole is 1:1-1:20, preferably                  3:10.

PN KR2021066064-A
TI Composition useful as carbon dioxide absorbent in thermal power plants comprises active substances including potassium carbonate, support i.e. halloysite, titanium dioxide, and zirconium dioxide, and inorganic binder e.g. wollastonite.
AU BAEK J I
   KIM U
   JANG Y
   JO H G
AE KOREA ELECTRIC POWER CORP (KEPC-C)
GA 2021633120
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises active substances including potassium carbonate, support, and inorganic binder. The support is halloysite, alpha -alumina, titanium dioxide, and zirconium dioxide. The inorganic binder is wollastonite and bentonite.
   USE - The composition is useful as carbon dioxide absorbent in thermal power plants for a carbon oxide absorption.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide absorbent has carbon dioxide absorption capacity of greater than or equal to 6 wt.% (6 g carbon dioxide/100 g sorbent); reduces carbon dioxide emission; is reusable and economical; has abrasion resistance, durability, and excellent mechanical strength; prevents elution of active material during regeneration; improves carbon dioxide removal rate. The device: has small installation area and easy operation; is suitable for mass production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for: (1)producing a carbon dioxide absorbent comprising preparing slurry containing composition and solvent, homogenizing the slurry by stirring, spray-drying the homogenized slurry to form solid particles, drying and calcining the solid particles; (2)carbon dioxide absorbent prepared by above mentioned method, the wear index (Attrition Index: AI) of carbon dioxide absorbent is less than or equal to 20% according to equation I i.e. Wear index (AI,%) = (W1(g)/W0(g))x 100, where W1 is a flow rate of 10.0 l/minute (273.15 K, 1 bar) in an abrasion tester according to ASTM D5757-95, the total weight (g) of the absorbent fine powder collected by carrying out an abrasion test under the conditions, and W0 is the initial weight of the absorbent (g, nominal 50 g); (3)carbon dioxide absorption process comprises contacting carbon dioxide absorbent with gas containing carbon dioxide and water vapor to selectively absorb carbon dioxide in the gas, and regenerating the absorbent that has absorbed the carbon dioxide while discharging the carbon dioxide by contacting it with a heat source gas; and (4)carbon dioxide capture device comprising an absorption reactor in which the carbon dioxide absorbent is accommodated in contact with the exhaust gas containing carbon dioxide and water vapor, where the absorbent selectively absorbs heavy carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas, a regeneration reactor for regenerating the absorbent while discharging carbon dioxide by contacting the absorbent that has absorbed the carbon dioxide with a heat source gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating the method for manufacturing a carbon dioxide absorbent (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic binder further comprises a boehmite. The inorganic binder contains pseudo-boehmite, willlastonite and bentonite in a mass ratio of 1:0.5-4:1-20. The specific surface area of alpha -alumina is 5-50 m2/g, titanium dioxide and halloysite is 25-200 m2/g, and the zirconium dioxide is 10-100 m2/g. The solvent comprises water. The slurry comprises 30-60 wt.% composition. The slurry comprises 0.01-5.0 wt.% dispersant, 1.0-5.0 wt.% organic binder and 0.01-0.5 wt.% antifoaming agent. The dispersant is an anionic surfactant, a cationic surfactant, an amphoteric surfactant and a nonionic surfactant. The anti-foaming agent is a silicone-based anti-foaming agent, a metal soap-based anti-foaming agent, amide-based antifoaming agent, polyether-based antifoaming agent, polyester-based antifoaming agent, polyglycol-based antifoaming agent and alcohol-based antifoaming agents. The organic binder is polyvinyl alcohol, polyethylene glycol and methylcellulose. The carbon dioxide absorbent has an average particle size of 60-200 mu m, particle size distribution of 30-400 mu m, and filling density of 0.6-2.0 g/cm3. Preferred Composition: The composition comprises 30-40 wt.% active material, 5-20 wt.% halloysite, 5-30 wt.% alpha -alumina, 5-15 wt.% titanium dioxide, 5-25 wt.% zirconium dioxide, 2.5-15 wt.% wollastonite, 5-10 wt.% bentonite and 0-5 wt.% pseudoboehmite. Preferred Method: The slurry homogenization comprises adding additives of a dispersant, an antifoaming agent, and an organic binder to the slurry, stirring and grinding the slurry. The further comprises after stirring and pulverizing the slurry, removing foreign substances from the slurry. The drying is carried out at 100-150 degrees C for 2-24 hours, the calcination is performed by raising temperature to 500-700 degrees C at rate of 1-5 degrees C/minute in the kiln, and maintaining for 2-10 hours.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
