PT P
PN US2020220189-A1; US10998566-B2
TI Capturing high-purity carbon dioxide in hydrocarbon facility for oil industry by operating hydrogen plant to generate hydrogen stream and carbon dioxide rich stream, reacting carbon dioxide, oxygen and hydrogen and collecting carbon dioxide.
AU AL H A S
   SOUENTIE S
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 2020619932
AB    NOVELTY - Method for capturing high-purity carbon dioxide in hydrocarbon facility, involves operating a hydrogen plant to generate a hydrogen stream and carbon dioxide rich stream with carbon dioxide concentration of above 25%, introducing the hydrogen stream into an anode of a molten carbonate fuel cell, reacting carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide rich stream and oxygen within the cathode of the molten carbonate fuel cell to produce carbonate, reacting carbonate from the cathode of the molten carbonate fuel cell with hydrogen within the anode of the molten carbonate fuel cell to produce an anode exhaust stream, where the anode exhaust stream comprises carbon dioxide and water, separating the carbon dioxide in the anode exhaust stream in one or more separators to form a pure carbon dioxide stream and water stream, where the pure carbon dioxide stream has a purity of 80-100% on a molar basis, and collecting the pure carbon dioxide stream.
   USE - The method is useful for capturing high-purity carbon dioxide in hydrocarbon facility for oil, gas and power industries by reducing carbon dioxide emissions, and generating usable electrical power and useful pure carbon dioxide stream.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for capturing high-purity carbon dioxide in hydrocarbon facility comprising a hydrogen plant to generate a hydrogen stream at a hydrogen stream outlet and carbon dioxide rich stream with carbon dioxide concentration of above 25% at a carbon dioxide rich stream outlet, a molten carbonate fuel cell comprising an anode, a cathode and a molten carbonate electrolyte, and a separator, where the hydrogen stream outlet is operatively connected to the anode of a molten carbonate fuel cell, the carbon dioxide rich stream outlet is operatively connected to the cathode of the molten carbonate fuel cell, an oxygen source stream is operatively connected to the cathode of the molten carbonate fuel cell, the molten carbonate fuel cell is configured for reaction of carbon dioxide from the hydrogen plant and oxygen from the oxygen source stream within the cathode of the molten carbonate fuel cell to produce carbonate, the molten carbonate fuel cell is configured for reaction of the carbonate from the cathode of the molten carbonate fuel cell with hydrogen from the hydrogen plant within the anode of the molten carbonate fuel cell to produce an anode exhaust stream from an anode outlet of the molten carbonate fuel cell, the anode exhaust stream comprising carbon dioxide and water, the separator comprises a separator inlet operatively connected to the anode outlet of the molten carbonate fuel cell, a pure carbon dioxide outlet and a water outlet, the separator configured to separate the anode exhaust stream to form a pure carbon dioxide stream and a water stream, and the pure carbon dioxide stream having a purity of 80-100% on a molar basis.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The hydrogen stream comprises at least 95% hydrogen gas. Preferred Component: The oxygen source stream is atmospheric air. Preferred Method: The method further involves providing the water stream from the separator to a steam reforming system to generate steam for the steam reforming system, boosting the pressure of the water stream from the separator before introduction to the steam reforming system, providing a cathode exhaust stream to the steam reforming system to fuel a burner in the steam reforming system with residual hydrocarbons in the cathode exhaust stream. The steam reforming system generates syngas from methane and water as a feed stream to the hydrogen plant. The separator condenses water from the anode exhaust stream to form the pure carbon dioxide stream and water stream. The method involves oxidizing methane, hydrogen and/or carbon monoxide in the carbon dioxide rich stream in the cathode of the molten carbonate fuel cell to generate heat to raise the temperature of the molten carbonate fuel cell to a molten carbonate fuel cell operating temperature, and introducing oxygen into the cathode of the molten carbonate fuel cell which is provided as an air stream. The steam reforming system is operatively connected to the hydrogen plant to generate syngas from methane and water as a feed stream to the hydrogen plant. Preferred Conditions: The molten carbonate fuel cell operating temperature is maintained at 600-700 degrees C. Preferred Device: The hydrogen plant is a pressure swing adsorption system. The hydrogen plant comprises an absorber and a stripper to separate carbon dioxide from hydrogen. The system further comprises a steam reforming system operatively connected to the water outlet of the separator configured to provide the water stream from the separator to the steam reforming system to generator stream for the steam reforming system, and a pressure boosting unit configured to increase the pressure of the water stream from the separator before introduction to the steam reforming system. The steam reforming system is further operatively connected to a cathode outlet of the molten carbonate fuel cell configured to provide a cathode exhaust stream to the steam reforming system to fuel a burner in the steam reforming system with residual hydrocarbons in the cathode exhaust stream.

PN WO2022025247-A1
TI Zeolite used as adsorbent for separating carbon            dioxide, water, helium, neon, chlorine, ammonia and            hydrochloric acid, comprises carbon atom and has preset            silica-alumina ratio and peak heights in spectrum            obtained by X-ray diffraction.
AU AKAOGI T
   OHKUBO A
   TANAKA S
AE ASAHI KASEI KK (ASAH-C)
GA 202218482H
AB    NOVELTY - A GIS-type zeolite comprises 4 %mass or less                carbon atom and has silica-alumina ratio of 4.23 or                more. When the peak heights near 2( theta ) of                12.45 degrees and around 28.07 degrees are A and B,                respectively, in the spectrum obtained by X-ray                diffraction, the ratio of peak heights (A) and (B)                is 0.62 or more. When the peak heights near 2(                theta ) of 28.07 degrees and around 46.04 degrees                are B' and C, respectively, in the spectrum                obtained by X-ray diffraction, the ratio of peak                heights (B') and (C) is 11.5 or more.
   USE - Zeolite used as adsorbent for separating                carbon dioxide, water, helium, neon, chlorine,                ammonia, and hydrochloric acid from mixture of two                or more gases including one or more hydrogen,                nitrogen, oxygen, argon, carbon monoxide and                hydrocarbons for forming purified gas (all                claimed). Can also be used for desiccants,                detergent additives, ion exchangers, wastewater                treatment agents, fertilizers, food additives and                electrolyte membranes for fuel cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The GIS-type zeolite has large amount of                carbon dioxide adsorbed near room temperature and                small amount of carbon dioxide adsorbed during                heating.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) separation method, which involves                separating one or more of carbon dioxide, water,                helium, neon, chlorine, ammonia, and hydrochloric                acid, from a mixture of two or more gases including                one or more hydrogen, nitrogen, oxygen, argon,                carbon monoxide and hydrocarbons, using an                adsorbent comprising the GIS-type zeolite;
   (2) manufacture of purified gas, which                involves separating one or more of carbon dioxide,                water, helium, neon, chlorine, ammonia, and                hydrochloric acid, from a mixture of two or more                gases including one or more hydrogen, nitrogen,                oxygen, argon, carbon monoxide and hydrocarbons;                and
   (3) manufacture of the GIS-type zeolite, which                involves forming a mixed gel containing a silica                source containing silicon, an aluminum source                containing aluminum, an alkali source containing at                least one alkali metal (M1) and an alkaline earth                metal (M2), a phosphorus source containing                phosphorus, and water and hydrothermally                synthesizing the mixed gel at 80-145 degrees C. In                the mixed gel obtained in the preparation step, the                ratio of silicon dioxide and aluminum oxide is                3.0-70.0, and the ratio of sum of metal (M1) oxide                and metal (M2) oxide is 15.0 or less, and ratio of                water and hydroxide is 95-480. In the GIS-type                zeolite, the carbon atom content is 4 %mass or less                and the silica-alumina ratio is 4.23 or more.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The zeolite contains                  silica-alumina, and hydrogen and an alkali metal                  element as counter cations. Preferred Process:                  The gas is separated by pressure swing type                  adsorption separation method, temperature swing                  type adsorption separation method, or                  pressure/temperature swing type adsorption                  separation method.

PN CN113652395-A
TI Preparing core-shell structure of metalorganic frameworks coated cardiomyocytes comprises discarding supernatant of HL-1 mouse cardiomyocyte culture medium and adding zinc nitrate hexahydrate aqueous solution and 2-methylimidazole aqueous solution.
AU GONG T
   ZHANG L
   CHEN W
   LI X
AE SHENZHEN POLYTECHNIC (SHPN-C)
GA 2021D5920G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing core-shell structure of metalorganic frameworks coated cardiomyocytes comprises (a) discarding supernatant of HL-1 mouse cardiomyocyte culture medium and washing HL-1 mouse cardiomyocytes with phosphate buffer saline or normal saline, (b) adding washed HL-1 mouse cardiomyocytes to cell culture flask or cell culture dish, (c) placing cell culture flask or cell culture dish into carbon dioxide incubator, (d) centrifuging cell suspension and discarding supernatant, (e) using fresh medium to resuspend cell pellet and adding to cell culture flask or cell culture dish, placing cell culture flask or cell culture dish in carbon dioxide incubator, (f) centrifuging suspended cells to collect cell pellets and (g) adding zinc nitrate hexahydrate aqueous solution and 2-methylimidazole aqueous solution to cell culture flask or cell culture dish and transferring cell culture flask or cell culture dish to carbon dioxide incubator
   USE - The method is useful for preparing core-shell structure of metalorganic frameworks coated cardiomyocytes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing core-shell structure of metalorganic frameworks coated cardiomyocytes comprises (a) discarding supernatant of the HL-1 mouse cardiomyocyte culture medium and washing HL-1 mouse cardiomyocytes with phosphate buffer saline or normal saline, (b) adding washed HL-1 mouse cardiomyocytes to the cell culture flask or cell culture dish and adding 0.25 wt.% trypsin phosphate buffer saline solution to obtain trypsin phosphate buffer saline solution with a mass fraction of 0.25% to cover the HL-1 mouse cardiomyocytes, (c) placing cell culture flask or cell culture dish in step (b) into carbon dioxide incubator at 37degreesC for digestion and adding culture medium to terminate the digestion to obtain cell suspension, (d) centrifuging cell suspension and discarding supernatant to obtain a cell pellet, (e) using fresh medium to resuspend the cell pellet and adding to cell culture flask or cell culture dish, placing cell culture flask or cell culture dish in carbon dioxide incubator at 37degreesC for 1-7 days (f) centrifuging suspended cells to collect the cell pellets and using fresh medium to resuspend the cell pellet and transferring to new cell culture flask or cell culture dish for passage, where the passage ratio is 1:2-8, (g) preparing zinc nitrate hexahydrate aqueous solution and 2-methylimidazole aqueous solution respectively having concentrations of 1-10 g/l and 5-20 g/l, (h) adding zinc nitrate hexahydrate aqueous solution and 2-methylimidazole aqueous solution to the cell culture flask or cell culture dish obtained in step (f), transferring cell culture flask or cell culture dish to carbon dioxide incubator with temperature of 37degreesC and culturing for 1-7 days to obtain the core-shell structure of cardiomyocytes coated with metalorganic frameworks.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The volume fraction of carbon dioxide in carbon dioxide incubator described in step (c), (e), (f) and (h) is 5%. The culture medium in the step (c), (e) and (f) comprises 90 wt.% dulbecco's modified eagle medium (DMEM) high glucose, 9 wt.% fetal bovine serum and 1 wt.% double antibody and concentration of the double antibody is 100 U/ml. The volume ratio of the culture medium in the step (c) to the HL-1 mouse cardiomyocytes in the cell culture flask or the cell culture dish is 6-8:1. The volume ratio of zinc nitrate hexahydrate aqueous solution to cell culture medium in step (h) is 6.25x10-5 to 1.67x10-3. The volume ratio of 2-methylimidazole aqueous solution to cell culture medium in the step (h) is 6.25x10-5 to 1.67x10-3.Preferred Method: The number of cleanings in the step (a) is 1-2 times. The digestion time in the step (c) is 0.5-2 minutes. The centrifugation in the step (d) is performed at 500-2000 revolutions/minute for 3-6 minutes.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN108992455-A
TI Application of aucubin for inhibiting release of            inflammation factor involves inoculating the BV2 cells,            preparing aucubin drug, processing drug in normal            cells, using medicine to treat inflammation model cell,            and measuring e.g. TNF-alpha.
AU XUE H
   NIU L
   LIAN X
   MIAO S
   WANG N
   LIU Y
   JIANG L
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2018A36926
AB    NOVELTY - Application of aucubin comprises inoculating                BV2 cell, melting, centrifuging, pouring out                supernatant, adding to sterile Dulbecco's modified                Eagle's medium (DMEM) culture solution with fetal                calf serum, putting carbon dioxide, and culturing;                dissolving aucubin in sterile phosphate-buffered                saline, storing, dissolving lipopolysaccharide in                sterile DMEM culture medium, storing, dissolving                interferon- gamma with sterile DMEM, and storing;                collecting BV2 cells, adding to DMEM containing                fetal bovine serum, diluting, inoculating, setting                experimental and control groups, adding drugs to                each group, placing carbon dioxide, incubating,                detecting cell viability, recording data, and                processing; and collecting BV2 cells, adding to                DMEM containing fetal bovine serum, diluting,                inoculating, setting experimental and control                groups, adding corresponding drugs to each group,                placing carbon dioxide, incubating, measuring e.g.                cell viability, recording data, and                processing.
   USE - Method for applying aucubin used for                inhibiting release of inflammation factor.
   ADVANTAGE - The aucubin has good antiinflammatory effect                and small side effects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Application of aucubin comprises:
   (A) inoculating BV2 cell at 37 degrees C under                water bath, melting, centrifuging at 1000                revolutions/minute (rpm) for 5 minutes, pouring out                supernatant, adding to sterile Dulbecco's modified                Eagle's medium (DMEM) culture solution with 10%                fetal calf serum, putting 5% carbon dioxide, and                culturing at 37 degrees C;
   (B) dissolving aucubin in sterile                phosphate-buffered saline, storing at - 20 degrees                C, dissolving lipopolysaccharide in sterile DMEM                culture medium to 10 mg/mL, storing at - 80 degrees                C, dissolving 105 U/mL interferon- gamma with                sterile DMEM, and storing - 80 degrees C;
   (C) collecting BV2 cells in logarithmic growth                phase, adding to DMEM containing 10% fetal bovine                serum, diluting to 5x 104-105 U/mL, inoculating                cell suspension with 100 mu L LBV2 cells in 96-well                plate, setting 5 groups of experimental groups and                2 groups of control groups of cell-free blank                control group, control group containing BV2 cells,                5 mg/L aucubin, 10 mg/L aucubin, 50 mg/L aucubin,                150 mg/L aucubin, and 200 mg/L aucubin, adding                drugs to each group, placing 5% carbon dioxide,                incubating for 24 hours at 37 degrees C, and                detecting cell viability record data and                processing; and
   (D) collecting BV2 cells in logarithmic growth                phase, adding to DMEM containing 10% fetal bovine                serum, diluting to 5x 104-105 U/mL, inoculating                cell suspension with 100 mu L LBV2 cells in 96-well                plate, setting 7 groups of experimental groups and                2 groups of control groups, adding corresponding                drugs to each group, placing 5% carbon dioxide,                incubating for 24 hours at 37 degrees C, a                measuring cell viability, reactive oxygen species,                nitric oxide, interleukin 1 beta , interleukin 6,                and TNF- alpha levels, recording data, and                processing.
TF ACTIVITY - Antiinflammatory.

PN CN107758700-A
TI Manufacture of calcined soda involves supplying brine into ammonia absorption tower, absorbing ammonia, supplying brine to carbon dioxide absorption tower, carbonizing, forming sodium bicarbonate, and calcining sodium bicarbonate.
AU SHI Y
   SHIQIN B
   YANG N
AE SHANNXI KEYUAN ENERGY SAVING TECHNOLOGY (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHIQIN B (SHIQ-Individual)
GA 2018205207
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of calcined soda involves supplying carbon dioxide into the mother liquor or adding equimolar amount of sodium carbonate the mother liquor supernatant at 20-60 degrees C to form calcium carbonate precipitate, removing calcium ion, carrying out ammoxidation of the supernatant to obtain brine solution containing chloride ion, sodium ion, ammonia and sulfate, supplying brine into the ammonia absorption tower, absorbing ammonia, supplying the brine to carbon dioxide absorption tower, carbonizing to form sodium bicarbonate, and calcining sodium bicarbonate.
   USE - Manufacture of calcined soda.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient manufacture of calcined soda with reduced energy consumption, raw material utilization rate of more than 98%, and suppressed discharge of wastewater.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacture of calcined soda involves heating the mother liquor containing 1.5-1.8 mol/L bicarbonate, 1.4-1.7 mol/L sodium ion, 5.2-5.8 mol/L ammonium ion, 5.2-5.53 mol/L chloride ion to 80-110 degrees C for 60 minutes to obtain a mother liquor containing 5.2-5.53 mol/L chloride ion, 5.2-5.8 mol/L ammonium ion, and 1.4-1.7 mol/L sodium ion, adding calcium oxide powder or lime paste to the mother liquor in molar ratio of 2:1-1.1, stirring for 15-60 minutes, reacting, cooling to 20-60 degrees C to obtain mother liquor supernatant containing 5.2-5.53 mol/L chloride ion, 1.5-2.5 mol/L calcium ion, 5-5.8 mol/L ammonium ion, and 1.4-1.7 mol/L sodium ion, adding sodium sulfate to the supernatant, reacting reaction at 20-60 degrees C, stirring for 15-50 minutes to produce calcium sulfate dihydrate precipitate, filtering calcium sulfate dihydrate, supplying carbon dioxide into the mother liquor or adding equimolar amount of sodium carbonate the mother liquor supernatant at 20-60 degrees C to form calcium carbonate precipitate, removing calcium ion, carrying out ammoxidation of the supernatant to obtain brine solution containing 5.3-5.53 mol/L chloride ion, 5.3-5.53 mol/L sodium ion, 5-5.8 mol/L ammonia and 8 g/L or less sulfate, controlling the temperature of the brine to 20-30 degrees C, supplying brine into the ammonia absorption tower, absorbing ammonia that overflows from the liquid phase and the solid phase, controlling the temperature of brine to less than 40 degrees C, supplying the brine to carbon dioxide absorption tower, carbonizing at 40-70 degrees C to form sodium bicarbonate, and calcining sodium bicarbonate at 170-220 degrees C for 30-60 minutes. The decomposed carbon dioxide gas is washed and cooled by the compressor and then compressed back to the carbonization process.

PN WO2018005577-A1; TW201808609-A; AR108908-A1; CN109362225-A; BR112018074890-A2; EP3475076-A1; US2019126598-A1; IN201917002671-A; JP2019521013-W; VN63875-A; MX2018015077-A1; ID201903632-A; EP3475076-B1; CN109362225-B; ES2812275-T3; IN366655-B; JP6967020-B2; US11260633-B2; TW758301-B1
TI Multilayer film used as package, comprises layer            comprising gas permeation layer and layers comprising            high-density polyethylene having preset density, and            exhibits preset normalized carbon dioxide transmission            rate.
AU LIN Y
   HUANG W
   BENSASON S
AE DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
GA 201801152A
AB    NOVELTY - A multilayer film comprises layers (A), (B)                and (C), arranged in order. The layers (A) and (C)                comprise at least 90 wt.% high-density polyethylene                (HDPE) having a ratio of weight average molecular                weight to number average molecular weight of at                least 6, absolute z-average molecular weight of at                least 500000 g/mol, and a density of 0.957-0.97                g/cm3. The layer (B) comprises a gas permeation                layer. The film exhibits a normalized carbon                dioxide transmission rate of at least 3000                cm3.mil/100 inch2/day, and ratio of the carbon                dioxide transmission rate to oxygen transmission                rate is at least 4.
   USE - Multilayer film is used as package (claimed)                e.g. modified atmosphere packages, pouches and                stand-up pouches which are used for packing fruits                and vegetables.
   ADVANTAGE - The multilayer film exhibits excellent carbon                dioxide/oxygen transmission rates, and has                excellent mechanical strength. The HDPE provides                excellent structural support and porosity to                multilayer film.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A multilayer film comprises layers (A), (B)                and (C), arranged in order. The layers (A) and (C)                comprise at least 90 wt.% high density polyethylene                (HDPE) having a ratio of weight average molecular                weight to number average molecular weight of at                least 6, absolute z-average molecular weight of at                least 500000 g/mol as measured by triple detector                gel permeation chromatography, and a density of                0.957-0.97 g/cm3. The top facial surface of layer                (C) is in adhering contact with the bottom facial                surface of the layer (B). The layer (B) comprises a                gas permeation layer, and the top facial surface of                the layer B is in adhering contact with a bottom                facial surface of layer (A). The film is oriented                in the machine direction and exhibits a normalized                carbon dioxide transmission rate of at least 3000                cm3.mil/100 inch2/day, measured according to ASTM                F-2476 at 23 degrees C, 0% relative humidity, and 1                atm. The film exhibits a ratio of carbon dioxide                transmission rate to an oxygen transmission rate of                at least 4, in which the oxygen transmission rate                is measured according to ASTM D3985 at 23 degrees                C, 0% relative humidity and 1 atm. An INDEPENDENT                CLAIM is included for manufacture of the multilayer                film, which involves providing the film, cold                stretching the film, and hot stretching the film.                The film is cold stretched to 25-150% at 10-50                degrees C, and the film is hot stretched to                520-500% at 90-110 degrees C. The cold stretch% or                hot stretch% of the film is calculated using the                equation: stretch(%)=((total length after                stretching-original film length)/original film                length)x 100 (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The gas permeation                  layer comprises polyether block amide,                  poly(ethylene-vinyl acetate), propylene-based                  olefin block copolymer, polyolefin plastomer,                  and/or polyolefin elastomer. The film is cold                  stretched in the machine direction or traverse                  direction, and hot stretched in the machine                  direction or transverse direction. The film                  further comprises layers (D) and (E). The top                  facial surface of the layer (D) is in adhering                  contact with the bottom facial surface of the                  layer (C), and the top facial surface of the                  layer (E) is in adhering contact with the bottom                  facial surface of the layer (D). The layer (D)                  has the same composition as that of the layer (B)                  and comprises a gas permeation layer (l2), and                  the layer (E) has the same composition as that of                  the layer (A). Preferred Composition: The film                  comprises at least 10 wt.% of layers (A) and (C),                  each. The layers (A) and (C) comprise less than                  10 wt.% calcium carbonate. Preferred Properties:                  The oxygen and carbon dioxide transmission rates                  of the layer (B) are less than the oxygen and                  carbon dioxide transmission rates of the layers                  (A) and (C). The ratio of carbon dioxide                  transmission rate to oxygen transmission rate of                  the film is greater than 5.

PN WO2022107509-A1; JP2022081758-A
TI System used for separating and recovery of carbon dioxide from supply gas containing carbon dioxide and non-carbon dioxide gas, has adsorption unit, supply flow path, storage unit, and adsorption mode for adsorbing carbon dioxide gas.
AU TAKAMASA I
   HIROAKI U
   ITO T
   UMEDA H
AE DENSO CORP (NPDE-C)
GA 2022730640
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide recovery system (10) has a supply flow path (100) through which the supply gas passed, a storage unit (16) for storing carbon dioxide desorbed from the adsorption unit (12), and a gas supply unit (11) for supplying carbon dioxide stored in the storage unit to the adsorption unit. The system has an adsorption mode for adsorbing carbon dioxide contained in the supply gas supplied through the supply flow path, and the carbon dioxide adsorbed by the adsorption unit is desorbed from the adsorption unit, and desorption mode in which the separated carbon dioxide is stored by the storage unit and the carbon dioxide stored in the storage unit by the gas supply unit between the end of the adsorption mode and the start of the desorption mode. The system has desorption preparation mode for supplying to the adsorption mode.
   USE - System used for separating and recovery of carbon dioxide from supply gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide recovery system has a simple structure and ensures improved recovery.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of carbon dioxide recovery system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Carbon dioxide recovery system (10)
   Gas supply units (11,15)
   Adsorption unit (12)
   Storage unit (16)
   Supply flow path (100)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The adsorption unit is constituted to adsorb and desorb carbon dioxide according to the fluctuation of the internal carbon dioxide partial pressure, and has pressure adjusting portions for adjusting the carbon dioxide partial pressure in the adsorption unit. The pressure adjusting portion is provided in the adsorption mode, in order to adsorb carbon dioxide in the adsorption unit, and the carbon dioxide partial pressure in the adsorption portion is set to a prescribed pressure in the desorption mode, in order to desorb carbon dioxide in the adsorption unit. The carbon dioxide partial pressure in the adsorption unit is lower than in the adsorption mode. The desorption preparation mode is provided with an auxiliary storage unit for storing the exhaust gas discharged from the adsorption unit in accordance with the supply of carbon dioxide by the gas supply unit. The exhaust gas stored in the auxiliary storage unit is joined to the supply gas in the adsorption mode and is supplied to the adsorption unit. The several suction units are operated when the specific adsorption unit is operated in the adsorption mode, the other adsorption unit operates in the desorption preparation mode and desorption is performed. The exhaust gas discharged from the adsorption unit operating in the preparation mode as the carbon dioxide is supplied by the gas supply unit from the adsorption unit which is operating in the desorption preparation mode, and is operated in the adsorption mode. In the supply flow path, a check valve provided on the upstream side in the flow direction of the supply gas is from the confluence point where the exhaust gas merges into the supply gas. Gas flows in the direction opposite to the flow direction of the supply gas. In the desorption preparation mode, a gas concentration sensor for detecting the carbon dioxide concentration of the exhaust gas discharged from the adsorption unit as the carbon dioxide is supplied by the gas supply unit is provided, and in the desorption preparation mode. In the desorption preparation mode, when the carbon dioxide concentration detected by the gas concentration sensor becomes a prescribed concentration or more, the mode shifts to the desorption mode. In the desorption preparation mode, a gas concentration sensor for detecting the concentration of the non-carbon dioxide gas of the exhaust gas discharged from the adsorption unit with the supply of carbon dioxide by the gas supply unit. In the desorption preparation mode, when a non-carbon dioxide gas concentration detected by the gas concentration sensor becomes equal to or less than a predetermined concentration, the mode shifts to the desorption mode. The elapsed time from the start of the desorption preparation mode reaches a predetermined time, the system shifts to the desorption mode. The throttle valve provided in the supply flow path has a smaller valve opening degree than the suction mode and the desorption mode in the desorption preparation mode. The execution time of the desorption mode is longer than the execution time of the desorption preparation mode.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN US2020094184-A1; US11478743-B2
TI System e.g. closed loop system, comprises reactor            comprising carbon dioxide sorbent comprising magnesium            oxide, and supercritical carbon dioxide source            containing supercritical carbon dioxide, which is fluid            in communication with reactor.
AU MUTO A J
AE SOUTHERN RES INST (SOUR-C)
GA 202023149E
AB    NOVELTY - A system (100) comprises a reactor (102)                comprising carbon dioxide sorbent comprising                magnesium oxide, and a supercritical carbon dioxide                source (104) comprising supercritical carbon                dioxide and water. The supercritical carbon dioxide                source is in the fluid communication with the                reactor and carbon dioxide sorbent comprising                magnesium oxide to allow flow of the supercritical                carbon dioxide and water between the supercritical                carbon dioxide source and the reactor, thereby                allowing contact of carbon dioxide with the carbon                dioxide sorbent comprising magnesium oxide.
   USE - System e.g. closed loop system used for                storing thermal energy (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system has excellent cyclic durability,                volumetric energy density, and capable of operating                throughout a wide temperature range and provides                the efficient storage of thermal energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for storage                of energy, which involves (a) heating magnesium                carbonate with supercritical carbon dioxide having                temperature of 450 degrees C or more in presence of                water and/or carbonate, thereby promoting an                endothermic chemical reaction to produce the carbon                dioxide and magnesium oxide and (b) separating                carbon dioxide from magnesium oxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                thermochemical storage energy system.
   System (100)
   Reactor (102)
   Source (104)
   Heat source (106)
   Heat exchangers (108)
   Turbine (110)
   Cooling Unit (112)
   Sensible heat storage unit (114)
   Low temperature compressor (116)
   High-temperature compressor (118)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The reactor comprises                  a carbon dioxide sorbent comprising magnesium                  oxide and a liquid carbonate promoter. The system                  further comprises a heat source (106) which is                  configured to be in fluid communication with the                  supercritical carbon dioxide source and the                  reactor and a pump which is configured to pump                  supercritical carbon dioxide from the                  supercritical carbon dioxide source towards the                  heat source and/or reactor, and at least one of                  heat exchangers (108) which is configured to be                  in fluid communication with the supercritical                  carbon dioxide source, the reactor, and the heat                  source, a turbine (110) configured to be in fluid                  communication with an outlet of the reactor, and                  cooling unit (112) which is configured to be in                  fluid communication with an outlet of the                  reactor, heat exchangers and the supercritical                  carbon dioxide source, sensible heat storage unit                  (114) which is configured to be in fluid                  communication with the supercritical carbon                  dioxide source and the heat source and/or                  reactor. The reactor is heat exchanger reactor.                  The heat source is a solar thermal energy                  source.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ratio of water and                  supercritical carbon dioxide in supercritical                  carbon dioxide source is 3.6x 10-5-1 wt.%. The                  ratio of liquid carbonate and magnesium oxide in                  reactor is 1-50 wt.%. The carbonate comprises at                  least one chosen from sodium carbonate, lithium                  carbonate, and potassium carbonate. Preferred                  Processes: The storage method further involves                  transporting at least a portion of the separated                  carbon dioxide to a supercritical carbon dioxide                  source via one or more heat exchangers and a                  cooling unit, combining (c) supercritical carbon                  dioxide having a temperature of 700 degrees C or                  less with the magnesium oxide in the reactor in                  presence of water and/or carbonate, thereby                  promoting an exothermic chemical reaction to                  produce heat and magnesium carbonate, and                  repeating the steps (a)-(c) for 1000 to 20000                  times, and contacting magnesium carbonate with                  supercritical carbon dioxide having a temperature                  of at least 450 degrees C in the presence of                  water and/or a carbonate in the system, to                  thereby store energy. The amount of carbon                  dioxide which can be reacted with the magnesium                  oxide in step (c) is at least 50% of the amount                  of carbon dioxide which could be reacted with the                  magnesium oxide prior to performing the                  method.
DC E37 (Mixtures of many components; inorganic reactions and processes of general applicability.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); J08 (Heat transfer and drying - including direct/ indirect heat exchangers, heat transfer apparatus, drying processes (F26, F28).)
MC E11-Q02C; E31-N05C; E34-B01; J01-E02B; J01-E03C; J04-X03; J08-S02
IP B01D-053/02; B01D-053/62; B01D-053/82; B01D-053/96; B01J-020/04

PN CN110499409-A; CN210560591-U; WO2021057934-A1
TI Device used for recycling carbon dioxide in heat treatment and quenching process by pressure-resistant closed cavity for heating workpiece, carbon dioxide storage tank, carbon dioxide recovery tank, and high-pressure air system.
AU YANG J
AE SHANGHAI YIBO TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANGHAI YIBAI SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019A4126D
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises pressure-resistant closed cavity (10) for heating or heat-treating workpiece (11); carbon dioxide storage tank (20) connected to pressure-resistant closed cavity for delivering liquid or supercritical carbon dioxide to pressure-resistant closed cavity; carbon dioxide recovery tank (30) for recovering gaseous, liquid or supercritical carbon dioxide after being pressurized in pressure-resistant closed cavity; and high-pressure air system (40). The gaseous, liquid or supercritical carbon dioxide in gas provides pressurized, transported and/or phase-transformed high-pressure air. The bottom of carbon dioxide recovery tank is connected with pressure-resistant closed cavity.
   USE - Device is used for recycling carbon dioxide in heat treatment and quenching process.
   ADVANTAGE - The device uses high-pressure air to make supercritical or liquid carbon dioxide realize pressurization and transportation of fluid and phase change of gaseous carbon dioxide to liquid phase. The recovery process is short with high recovery efficiency. The device reduces investment in compressors and heat exchangers, reduces production costs, and reduces use of supercritical carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of device.
   Pressure-resistant closed cavity (10)
   Workpiece (11)
   Carbon dioxide storage tank (20)
   Carbon dioxide recovery tank (30)
   High-pressure air system (40)

PN CN215842414-U
TI Recycling device for recycling carbon dioxide gas, has gas-water separator that is connected with low-pressure gas storage tank, carbon dioxide compressor and high pressure gas tank.
AU SHA Y
   ZHOU B
   ZHANG X
   SU Y
   LI N
   JIANG Y
AE JIANGSU RONGHUI GEN LITHIUM IND CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
   TIBET RONGHUI LITHIUM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TIBE-Non-standard)
GA 202231845R
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a recycling device for recycling carbon dioxide gas, comprising the following steps: cooling the mixed high temperature gas through a heat exchanger, discharging the condensed water after steam condensation by a U-shaped sealing pipeline, preventing the carbon dioxide gas from being discharged through a condensed water pipeline, the carbon dioxide gas further separates the water into the low pressure gas storage tank through the gas-water separator, then the gas in the low pressure tank is compressed by the carbon dioxide compressor and then enters into the high pressure gas storage tank, then the carbon dioxide gas is conveyed to the carbon dioxide gas tank. The invention has automatic drainage function, simple operation, capable of fully recycling carbon dioxide gas generated by the decarburizing device and redundant carbon dioxide gas of carbon dioxide gas device, and can be recycled, effectively reducing the consumption of carbon dioxide in the production.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN US10005021-B1; US2018178160-A1; WO2018114369-A1; EP3558870-A1
TI System used for recovering ammonia from gas stream, comprises regenerator, carbon dioxide wash station, ammonia stripper, carbon dioxide absorber, regenerator, and appendix stripper to remove any ammonia from carbon-dioxide-lean gas stream.
AU DUBE S K
   BEDELL S A
AE GENERAL ELECTRIC TECHNOLOGY GMBH (GENE-C)
   GENERAL ELECTRIC TECHNOLOGY GMBH (GENE-C)
   GENERAL ELECTRIC TECHNOLOGY GMBH (GENE-C)
   GENERAL ELECTRIC TECHNOLOGY GMBH (GENE-C)
GA 201849780G
AB    NOVELTY - System (10) comprises: a regenerator (12) configured to heat a carbon dioxide-rich ammonia-based solution at a low internal pressure to generate a carbon dioxide-rich gas stream comprising residual ammonia; a carbon dioxide wash station (14) configured to receive both the carbon dioxide-rich gas stream and an acidic solution having a dissolved acidic salt and bring them into contact to produce an ammonia-rich solution; an ammonia stripper (16) configured to heat the ammonia-rich solution to produce an ammonia-rich gas stream and the acidic solution; a carbon dioxide absorber (18) configured to receive both the ammonia-rich gas stream and a carbon dioxide-rich flue gas stream to produce the carbon dioxide-rich ammonia-based solution and a carbon-dioxide-lean gas stream; a regenerator configured to receive the carbon dioxide-rich ammonia-based solution from the carbon dioxide absorber; and an appendix stripper configured to remove any ammonia from the carbon-dioxide-lean gas stream.
   USE - The system and method are useful for recovering ammonia from a gas stream (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system: utilizes the carbon dioxide wash station that absorbs the residual ammonia from the carbon dioxide-rich gas stream by contacting the carbon dioxide-rich gas stream with an acidic solution having a dissolved acidic salt to produce an ammonia-rich solution; utilizes the acidic solution in the carbon dioxide wash station and integrating the carbon dioxide wash station with the ammonia stripper and/or the appendix stripper; utilizes the operation of regenerator at low internal pressures, thus allowing for the heating of the carbon dioxide-rich ammonia-based solution via low pressure steam, as low pressure steam is typically less expensive than high-pressure steam and utilize the regenerator that saves up to a 20% in the electrical duty as compared to conventional systems; and provides overall increase in the efficiency of an encompassing power plant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) recovering ammonia from a gas stream, comprising heating a carbon dioxide-rich ammonia-based solution in a regenerator so as to produce a carbon dioxide-rich gas stream, operating the regenerator at a low internal pressure so that the carbon dioxide-rich gas stream comprises residual ammonia, forwarding the carbon dioxide-rich gas stream comprising residual ammonia to a carbon dioxide wash station, contacting the residual ammonia from the carbon dioxide-rich gas stream with an acidic solution having a dissolved acidic salt to produce an ammonia-rich solution, and providing used wash water from an appendix stripper to the carbon dioxide wash station; and
   (2) a carbon dioxide wash station for recovering ammonia from a gas stream, comprising a body having a first opening, a second opening, a third opening, and a fourth opening, where the first and the second openings define a flow path for the gas stream within the body and the first opening is for allowing the gas stream to enter the flow path after having been generated via heating of a carbon dioxide-rich ammonia-based solution within a regenerator that operates at a low internal pressure so that the gas stream includes carbon dioxide and residual ammonia, the third opening is for receiving an acidic solution having a dissolved acidic salt that is contacted with the gas stream along the flow path so as to absorb some of the residual ammonia from the gas stream to produce an ammonia-rich solution and a carbon dioxide-rich gas stream, the second opening is for allowing the carbon dioxide-rich gas stream to exit the body, and the fourth opening is for allowing the ammonia-rich solution to exit the body and the body further has a fifth opening for receiving bottom water from an appendix stripper.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic block diagram of the system.
   System (10)
   Regenerator (12)
   Carbon dioxide wash station (14)
   Ammonia stripper (16)
   Carbon dioxide absorber (18)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The internal pressure is less than or equal to 20 (preferably less than or equal to 5) bara. The carbon dioxide wash station is configured to receive a used wash water stream from the appendix stripper. The carbon dioxide wash station is configured to limit the residual ammonia in the carbon dioxide-rich gas stream that exits the body via the second opening to less than or equal to 10 ppm when the regenerator operates at an internal pressure of less than or equal to 2.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acidic salt is a weak acid. The acidic salt is ammonium acetate or ammonium phosphate. Preferred Method: The method further comprises: forwarding the ammonia-rich solution from the carbon dioxide wash station to an ammonia stripper, and heating the ammonia-rich solution to produce an ammonia-rich gas stream and the acidic solution; providing the ammonia-rich gas stream to a carbon dioxide absorber, contacting the ammonia-rich gas stream with a carbon dioxide-rich flue gas to produce the carbon dioxide-rich ammonia-based solution, and providing the carbon dioxide-rich ammonia-based solution to the regenerator; and forwarding the acidic solution from the ammonia stripper to the carbon dioxide wash station.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN CN114685846-A
TI Preparing supercritical decrosslinking            pre-treatment rubber powder involves mixing rubber            powder, crosslinking agent and dry ice, electrifying            and heating carbon dioxide, and using magnetic sealing            stirring paddle.
AU XU L
   WANG J
   XIAO X
   LI J
   XIAO F
AE UNIV TONGJI (UYTJ-C)
GA 2022896575
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing supercritical decrosslinking                pre-treatment rubber powder involves mixing rubber                powder, crosslinking agent and dry ice uniformly,                placing in a sealed high-pressure reaction kettle.                The carbon dioxide is heated and pressurized in the                high pressure reaction kettle by electric heating                to reach a supercritical state, the magnetic                sealing stirring paddle is used in the                supercritical carbon dioxide environment to make                the rubber powder and the cross linking agent fully                and uniformly react to obtain the final                product.
   USE - Method for preparing supercritical                decrosslinking pre-treatment rubber powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the uniformity and                efficiency of the crosslinking pre-treatment                process of the rubber powder inner part enhances                the compatibility between the waste tire rubber                powder and the matrix petroleum asphalt, so as to                improve the storage stability of rubber powder                modified asphalt, construction and workability and                environmental friendliness. The supercritical fluid                has the permeability of the gas, but also has the                solubility of liquid. Therefore, the method is an                ideal rubber powder cross-linking reaction                medium.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of the                  cross-linking agent to the rubber powder is                  5:100-35:100. ThE reaction kettle is not lower                  than 300 &#8451;, the design pressure and                  working pressure respectively not less than                  15-20MPa, the volume is not less than 1 L. The                  heating speed of the high pressure reaction                  kettle is not lower than 5 &#8451;/min under                  the heating of the external electricity. The                  density of the supercritical carbon dioxide in                  the high-pressure reaction kettle during the                  pre-treatment reaction of the rubber powder is                  not less than 0.16g/cm3, the temperature is not                  lower than 200 &#8451; and the pressure is                  not less than 8Mpa. The preheating temperature of                  the matrix asphalt is 170-175&#8451;, the                  pre-heating time is not less than 2 hours. The                  mass ratio of the supercritical de-crosslinking                  pre-treatment rubber powder to the substrate                  asphalt is 15:100-35:100. The stirring                  temperature is 175-180&#8451;, the stirring                  speed is 1000-1500 revolutions per minute, the                  stirring time is 30-45 minutes.
DC A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.); A93 (Roads, building, construction flooring.); A95 (Transport - including vehicle parts, tyres and armaments.)

PN KR2022065940-A; KR2410340-B1
TI Heat engine comprises combustor for receiving and burning combustible fuel, oxygen, and working fluid circulating in supercritical carbon dioxide cycle and discharging working fluid, turbine, regenerative heat exchanger, and reformer.
AU LEE Y D
   AHN K
   KIM Y
   KANG D
   AN B B A T
AE KOREA INST MACHINERY & MATERIALS (KIMA-C)
GA 202273289C
AB    NOVELTY - A heat engine comprises combustor for receiving and burning combustible fuel, oxygen, and a working fluid circulating in the supercritical carbon dioxide cycle and for discharging the working fluid, a turbine for performing mechanical work by the working fluid discharged from the combustor, a regenerative heat exchanger for exchanging heat between the working fluid discharged from the turbine and the working fluid supplied to the combustor, and reformer (A) arranged between the turbine and the heat exchanger, reforming hydrocarbons into hydrogen by absorbing heat from the working fluid discharged from the turbine, and supply the cooled working fluid to the heat exchanger.
   USE - Heat engine.
   ADVANTAGE - The heat engine has excellent efficiency, compactness, capability of operating at high temperature and pressure, consumes reduced amount of fuel and prevents damage to the heat exchanger by supplying high temperature working fluid to the turbine.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for operating the heat engine based on supercritical carbon dioxide cycle.

PN CN113024456-A; CN113024456-B
TI Preparing 2-chloronicotinic acid comprises reacting 2-hydroxy-pyridine, alkali and carbon dioxide in organic solvent to obtain obtain 2-hydroxynicotinic acid, and reacting it with chlorinating reagent in organic solvent.
AU CHEN Y
   XIAO S
AE JIANGSU XIANDAO PHARM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021767510
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 2-chloronicotinic acid (I) comprises (i) adding 2-hydroxy-pyridine (II), alkali and carbon dioxide into an organic solvent under pressure at certain temperature to obtain 2-hydroxynicotinic acid (III) and (ii) adding (III) and chlorinating reagent into an organic solvent for chlorination reaction at certain temperature to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing 2-chloronicotinic acid (I).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has multiple sources of raw materials and obvious cost advantages; is safer and more convenient; has high yield and low three waste; can avoid the use of phosphine oxychloride or triphosgene, which is beneficial to industrialization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing 2-chloronicotinic acid of formula (I) comprises (i) adding 2-hydroxy-pyridine of formula (II), alkali and carbon dioxide into an organic solvent under pressure at certain temperature to obtain 2-hydroxynicotinic acid of formula (III) and (ii) adding (III) and chlorinating reagent into an organic solvent for chlorination reaction at certain temperature to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (i) adding organic solvent, (II), alkali and carbon dioxide into the reaction vessel in sequence under the protection of nitrogen, uniformly stirring and heating to set temperature and set pressure; maintaining the temperature until the normalized content of (II) in the total material by liquid chromatography is less than 1%, cooling, pickling, crystallizing and drying to obtain (III), (ii) sequentially adding organic solvent, (III) and chlorinating reagent into the reaction vessel under the protection of nitrogen, uniformly stirring and heating to the set temperature, maintaining the temperature until the normalized content of (III) in the total material by liquid chromatography is less than 1%, cooling, alkali washing, crystallizing and drying to obtain (I). Preferred Method: In step (i) and step (ii), the set temperature is 20-200 degrees C. The set temperature is 140-190 degrees C. Preferred Components: In step (i), the molar ratio of the (II) and the alkali is 1:1-10; the molar ratio of the amount of the organic solvent to the (II) is 1-10:1; and the pressure of the carbon dioxide is 0.1-6 MPa. In step (ii): the molar ratio of (III) to the chlorinating reagent is 1:1-10; and the molar ratio of the amount of the organic solvent to (III) is 1-10:1. In step (i) and step (ii), the organic solvent is halogenated hydrocarbon solvents, aromatic solvents, ether solvents, ester solvents, alcohol solvents or heteroatoms containing strong polar solvents. In step (i), the alkali is potassium carbonate, sodium carbonate, potassium phosphate, dipotassium hydrogen phosphate, sodium hydrogen carbonate, sodium acetate, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, sodium hydride, potassium fluoride, pyridine or triethylamine. In step (ii), the chlorinating reagent is chlorine, hydrogen chloride, thionyl chloride, copper chloride, ferric chloride, sodium hypochlorite or sodium chlorite. The halogenated hydrocarbon solvent is dichloromethane, dichloroethane and/or chloroform; the aromatic solvent is chlorobenzene, toluene and/or xylene; the ether solvent is tetrahydrofuran; the ester solvent is methyl acetate and/or ethyl acetate; the alcohol solvent is methanol, ethanol and/or isopropanol; the heteroatom-containing strong polar solvents dimethylformamide, dimethylacetamide (DMA), dimethyl sulfoxide, N-Methylpyrrolidone (NMP), sulfolane and/or 1,3-dimethyl-2-imidazolidinone (DMI).

PN WO2021102503-A1; AU2020390429-A1
TI Module used for producing renewable methanol, consists of water capture generator designed for directly capturing water from atmosphere to provide water in liquid form, electrolyzer and reactor.
AU ROHAN G
AE SOUTHERN GREEN GAS LTD (SGRE-Non-standard)
   SOUTHERN GREEN GAS LTD (SGRE-Non-standard)
GA 202159792C
AB    NOVELTY - A renewable methanol production module (10) consists of a water capture generator (12) designed for directly capturing water from atmosphere to provide water in a liquid form, an electrolyzer (16) operatively coupled to the water capture generator for receiving the liquid water, which is effective in electrolysis of the liquid water to produce hydrogen and a reactor (20) operatively coupled to the electrolyzer for receiving the hydrogen and reacting with carbon dioxide to produce renewable methanol.
   USE - Module used for producing renewable methanol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The module efficiently harnesses waste heat from the exothermic reactor to assist with direct capture of carbon dioxide from atmosphere.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacture of the methanol.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an enlarged plan view of portion of renewable methanol production module.
   Renewable methanol production module (10)
   Water capture generator (12)
   Electrolyzer (16)
   Reactor (20)
   Solar water heater panels (26a, 26b)
   Solar panels (28a, 28b)
   Framework structures (36, 40)
   Carbon dioxide extractor (50)
   Heat exchanger (52)
   Water storage vessel (58)
   Liquid water (60)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The water capture generator includes an adsorbent material designed to be exposed to atmosphere for directly adsorbing water from the atmosphere onto the adsorbent material and a heating unit which is designed to absorb heat from renewable energy source and transfer to the adsorbent material to release the adsorbed water from the adsorbent material to provide the liquid water for the electrolyzer and condensing unit designed to condense the released water by cooling to provide the liquid water. The module comprises a renewable electricity generating assembly powered by a renewable energy source, which is configured to provide electricity for powering the electrolyzer in electrolysis of the atmospheric water in the production of hydrogen. The renewable electricity generating assembly includes a several solar panels (28a, 28b) operatively coupled to the electrolyzer for powering. The solar panels are coupled to the electrolyzer via an inverter and are directly coupled to the electrolyzer. The solar panels are in the form of solar photovoltaic panels arranged in an elongate bank of panels. The solar panels are in the form of printed solar membranes. The group of solar photovoltaic (PV) panels are located in two rows on respective of opposing faces of a solar framework structure (36, 40) which is oriented in a generally magnetic north to south direction. The solar framework structure is in cross-section shaped in the form of an isosceles triangle having each of the two rows of PV panels mounted to respective of leg-sides of the solar framework structure for increased solar exposure for the solar panels. The renewable methanol production module further comprises a carbon dioxide extractor (50) for extracting carbon dioxide from atmosphere, which is configured for directly capturing carbon dioxide from atmosphere using metal-organic framework or other adsorbent structure capable of directly adsorbing carbon dioxide from the atmosphere. The reactor is an exothermic reactor for reacting hydrogen from the electrolyzer with carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide extractor to produce renewable methanol. The exothermic reactor is operatively coupled to a heat exchanger (52) designed to exchange heat derived from the production of renewable methanol with the carbon dioxide extractor to heat the adsorbent structure of the extractor to release the adsorbed carbon dioxide from the adsorbent structure. The carbon dioxide extractor is operatively coupled to the renewable electricity generating assembly or a solar hot water heater for heating of the adsorbent structure to release the adsorbed carbon dioxide. The heat exchanger is operatively coupled to the electrolyzer in which steam produced from the exothermic reactor exchanges heat with the carbon dioxide extractor to promote the release of the adsorbed carbon dioxide and the steam is condensed to provide liquid water to the electrolyzer for the production of hydrogen. The water capture generator includes at least one pair of solar water heater panels (26a, 26b) mounted to a water capture framework structure associated with the solar framework structure. The water capture generator is operatively coupled to the carbon dioxide extractor in which dehumidified air from the water capture generator is received by the carbon dioxide extractor for extracting carbon dioxide from the dehumidified air. The carbon dioxide extractor includes pumps and/or fans powered by electricity supplied from the one or more batteries. The heating unit includes a solar heating unit, and/or heat recovery unit associated with the exothermic reactor for recovering waste heat from the exothermic reaction.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: The manufacture of methanol involves directly capturing water by exposing air to an adsorbent material to adsorb water from the air onto the adsorbent material, releasing the adsorbed water from the adsorbent material by heating solar energy, and/or waste heat from the reaction between hydrogen and carbon dioxide to heat the adsorbent material. and condensing the released water by cooling to provide the liquid water and recirculating of liquid water produced from the reaction between hydrogen and carbon dioxide for electrolysis in the production of hydrogen. The directly capturing of water is performed by refrigerating air to release water from the air to provide the liquid water. The step of reacting the hydrogen with carbon dioxide is performed by extracting carbon dioxide from air or obtaining carbon dioxide from biogas reactor. The extraction of carbon dioxide from air is performed by directly capturing carbon dioxide from air using solar energy and/or waste heat from the reaction between hydrogen and carbon dioxide.

AB    NOVELTY - The compressor design and operation control method involves designing the compressor so that the pressure recovery coefficient of the throttle portion of the compressor is as high as possible. GA 202074007N
AB    NOVELTY - Adsorbent media comprises pellets of adsorbent material, where the pellets of adsorbent material comprise up to 1.5 wt.% organic binder.
   USE - The adsorbent media is used to capture carbon dioxide (claimed). The adsorbent media is used for adsorbing gaseous molecules, including water, carbon dioxide and other small molecules, e.g. methane, acetylene, ethylene, oxygen, nitrogen and argon, and other gaseous molecules including toxic gases, e.g. sulfur dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, carbon monoxide and nitrous oxides.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent media maintains high adsorbance of a target gas while maintaining acceptable mechanical properties for industrial use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of producing the adsorbent media comprising pellets of adsorbent material, comprising (a) mixing an organic binder and a solvent to obtain a solution of organic binder, (b) mixing the solution of organic binder obtained in step (a) with an adsorbent material to provide an adsorbent-binder mixture, where the organic binder and the adsorbent material in the adsorbent-binder mixture are in a weight ratio of 1.5:98.5, and (c) shaping the adsorbent-binder mixture obtained in step (b) to form the pellets of adsorbent material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The adsorbent material is chosen from hybrid ultramicroporous materials, metal-organic framework materials, mesoporous silica, zeolitic imidazolate framework materials, aluminosilicates, carbon-based materials and zeolites. Preferred Method: The method comprises: (d) drying the pellets of adsorbent material obtained in step (c); and (e) removing the adsorbent pellets from the shaping device. The step (c) comprises: arranging the adsorbent-binder mixture onto a shaping device; and arranging the adsorbent-binder mixture in openings of a wire mesh, and the method further comprises (d1) drying the adsorbent-binder mixture in the wire mesh, and (e1) removing the adsorbent pellets from the wire mesh. The step (e) comprises shaking the shaping device or forcing the adsorbent-binder mixture through the shaping device. The method produces the adsorbent pellet. Preferred Properties: The adsorbent media has a largest dimension of 1 mm. The pellets of adsorbent material have a void fraction of less than 0.5.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The organic binder is chosen from a cellulosic material, poly(vinyl) polymers and their derivatives and acrylic acid-derived polymers and their derivatives. The adsorbent-binder mixture obtained in step (b) is a paste. Preferred Device: The shaping device is a mesh.

PT P
PN CN111218337-A
TI Extracting bitter orange essential oil from bitter orange peel by crushing and sieving dried bitter orange peel, putting bitter orange peel powder into supercritical extraction kettle, opening carbon dioxide cylinder, and pressurizing.
AU YANG C
AE NANJING ZELANG MEDICAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NJZL-C)
GA 202051442N
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting bitter orange essential oil from bitter orange peel involves: (1) placing the bitter orange peel in a vacuum drying oven and drying in an environment at 50-60 degrees C for 10 hours; (2) crushing the dried bitter orange peel and passing through a 50-100 mesh sieve to obtain a bitter orange peel powder; (3) putting the crushed bitter orange peel powder into a supercritical extraction kettle, opening a carbon dioxide cylinder, pressurizing by a pressurized system, when the extraction kettle pressure reaches 18.5 MPa, closing the carbon dioxide cylinder, where the extraction temperature is 35 degrees C and the extraction time is 45 minutes, and then analyzing the kettle.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting bitter orange essential oil from bitter orange peel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses supercritical fluid for extraction, and greatly improves the extraction yield of bitter orange essential oil, which is as high as 15% or even more than 20%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The supercritical extraction solvent is a carbon dioxide solution.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The vacuum degree of the vacuum drying box is 0.05 MPa, the vacuum drying temperature is 50-60 degrees C, and the orange peel is dried to a moisture content of less than 10%. The extraction pressure is 18.5 MPa, the extraction temperature is 35 degrees C, and the extraction time is 45 minutes.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)

PN CN110530764-A
TI Method for determining organic carbon and elemental carbon stable isotope in PM2.5, involves utilizing systems, utilizing sample preparation system to perform gradient heating combustion oxidation on particulate sample.
AU HE L
   HUANG X
   DAI J
   ZENG L
   FENG N
AE UNIV PEKING SHENZHEN GRADUATE SCHOOL (UYPK-C)
GA 2019A2893Q
AB    NOVELTY - An organic carbon and elemental carbon stable isotope determining method involves utilizing the systems include sample preparation systems, sample concentration systems and sample measurement systems, utilizing the sample preparation system to perform a gradient heating combustion oxidation on the particulate sample collected on the quartz filter membrane. The oxidation of different carbonaceous substances (OC and EC) is carried out at the rear end of the oxidation furnace into the sample concentration system for concentration purification, respectively, to obtain carbon dioxide gas. The more concentrated carbon dioxide gas is injected into the back-end isotope spectrometer to measure the carbon isotope, after measuring the sample, utilized a standard carbon dioxide gas with a known isotope value to correct the concentration dependence and time drift of the measured sample isotope value. The carbon isotope value is traced back to the international uniform standard.
   USE - Method for determining organic carbon and elemental carbon stable isotope in PM2.5.

AB    NOVELTY - Use of a surface-reacted calcium carbonate as                skin appearance modifier in a cosmetic and/or skin                care composition, is claimed. The calcium carbonate                have a volume median particle size from 0.1-90 mu m                and is a reaction product of natural ground calcium                carbonate or precipitated calcium carbonate with                carbon dioxide and one or more hydronium ion                donors. The carbon dioxide is formed in situ by the                hydronium ion donors treatment and/or is supplied                from an external source.
   USE - Use of surface-reacted calcium carbonate as                skin appearance modifier e.g. covering agent,                mattifying agent and skin color modifier in                cosmetic and skin-care composition e.g. eye makeup                product, facial makeup product, lip-care product                and hand-care product (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The cosmetic composition obtained using the                surface-reacted calcium carbonate provides improved                natural or mattified look, and smooth and fresh                skin feel, and conceals or hides wrinkle and skin                imperfection.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The calcium carbonate                  has a volume median particle size of preferably                  1.5-15 mu m, and specific surface area of 15-200                  m2/g, preferably 25-160 m2/g measured using                  nitrogen and BET method. Preferred                  Composition: The natural ground calcium carbonate                  is chosen from marble, chalk, and/or limestone. The hydronium ion donors are chosen from                  hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, sulfurous acid,                  phosphoric acid, citric acid, oxalic acid, an                  acidic salt, acetic acid and/or formic acid,                  preferably phosphoric acid which is at least                  partially neutralized by cations chosen from                  lithium ions, sodium ions, potassium ions, and/or                  calcium ions.

PN CN109953775-A; CN209153756-U
TI Helicobacter pylori detector sample collecting card, has detecting window whose surface is provided with transparent film, and absorption gas flow space formed between absorption sheet and transparent film.
AU LI M
AE LI M (LIMM-Individual)
GA 201959933N
AB    NOVELTY - The card has an air inlet and an air outlet provided with a cavity. An indicating window is located between the air inlet and an absorbing sheet. The absorbing sheet is provided with a detecting window. A surface of the detecting window is provided with a transparent film. An absorption gas flow space is formed between the absorption sheet and the transparent film. A sample collecting card main body is integrally provided with upper and lower pieces. A groove passage is connected with the air inlet and the air outlet. The groove passage is formed with a ventilating through hole and connected with the connecting pieces.
   USE - Helicobacter pylori detector sample collecting card.
   ADVANTAGE - The card absorbs carbon dioxide gas by making incoming carbon dioxide gas flow through a surface of the absorption sheet, and improves collection efficiency and detection accuracy of the carbon dioxide gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of a helicobacter pylori detector sample collecting card.

PN KR2019057971-A; KR1999448-B1
TI System for generating hybrid electric power by using supercritical carbon dioxide working fluid, has compressor for heat-exchanging and cooling working fluid passed through recuperator and working fluid cooled in condenser.
AU HWANG J H
   LEE H S
   JEONG C R
   HO H J
   SEONG L H
AE DOOSAN HEAVY IND & CONSTR CO LTD (DOOS-C)
GA 201949358X
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a furnace (610) distributed with fuel, and for generating combustion heat. A boiler (600) is equipped with multiple heat exchangers, which heat up working fluid (5) using the combustion heat, and a turbine (400). The boiler heats the working fluid and passes the working fluid through the turbine. A compressor (100) compresses a recuperator (200) and a condenser (500), and heat-exchanges and cools the working fluid passed through the recuperator and the working fluid cooled in the condenser, where the working fluid is selected as supercritical carbon dioxide.
   USE - System for generating hybrid electric power by using supercritical carbon dioxide working fluid.
   ADVANTAGE - The system realizes efficient power generation since the working fluid of a single phase is used and achieves requirements of a pressure pump and a drum.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a system for generating hybrid electric power.
   Working fluids (5, 7)
   Compressor (100)
   Recuperator (200)
   Turbine (400)
   Condenser (500)
   Boiler (600)
   Furnace (610)

PN JP2019052808-A
TI Air conditioner for air conditioning system has second rotor that adsorbs the moisture content in the process target air when process target air is ventilated by the process zone.
AU ITOYAMA K
   BANDO T
   INOUE H
   KURODA A
AE SHIMIZU CONSTR CO LTD (SHMC-C)
   NISHIBU GIKEN KK (NISH-Non-standard)
GA 2019306086
AB    NOVELTY - The air conditioner (20) includes primary rotor (6) whose absorbed carbon dioxide is disconnected with the air for regeneration when the air for regeneration (TA) is ventilated by the regeneration zone (4). A second rotor (8B) adsorbs the moisture content in the process target air when process target air is ventilated by the process zone (2). The moisture content with which the second rotor was adsorbed is removed with the air for regeneration when the air for regeneration is ventilated by the regeneration zone.
   USE - Air conditioner for air conditioning system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The lack of carbon dioxide removals and the lack of dehumidification, or over dehumidification can be suppressed through every year. The carbon dioxide removal and dehumidification of the air conditioning object space can be performed favorably.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an air conditioning system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the air conditioner. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Process zone (2)
   Regeneration zone (4)
   Primary rotor (6)
   Second rotor (8B)
   Air conditioner (20)
   Air for regeneration (TA)

   ZHANG C
   ZHANG Y
   YAO M
AE XIAN THERMAL POWER RES INST CO LTD (CHHN-C)
GA 201880155X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves forming a wing-shaped line in a unit body. A set of wing-shaped unit bodies are formed along a flow direction. Constant angle is formed in an expansion or contraction variable flow area arrangement along the flow direction such that flow speed of each section along the flow direction is consistent. Fluid is heated along the flow direction.    ADVANTAGE - The method enables ensuring constant velocity along the flow direction to reduce flow resistance to reach optimal heat transfer by achieving flow comprehensive performance efficiently.
