PN CN108378283-A
TI Preparation of high quality rice dumpling by grinding glutinous rice flour, washing, soaking to water, grinding, filter pressing, adding, e.g. coarse cereal mixture and drinking water to obtain wrapper dough, and filling with stuffing.
AU GUO S
   ZHU T
AE ANHUI JINTAIYANG FOOD CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201864747R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of high quality rice dumpling comprises grinding glutinous rice flour, washing to removing impurities, soaking to water for 18-24 hours, grinding to obtain rice slurry, and filter pressing to obtain wet filter cake; adding cooked glutinous rice, coarse cereal and drinking water mixture, adding to pressure-resistant stirring barrel, vacuumizing using vacuum pump, suctioning, filling with high density carbon dioxide until pressure reaches 20 MPa for 10-15 minutes, releasing to normal pressure, repeatedly filling high density carbon dioxide and pressure releasing processes for 2-3 times to obtain rice dumpling wrapper dough; and preparing stuffing material, and filling to rice dumpling wrapper dough.
   USE - Method for preparation of high quality rice dumpling.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures retention of fresh and nutrient substance of glutinous rice, uses high density carbon dioxide to sterilize the dumpling wrapper, and avoids using high temperature sterilization process which destroys the nutrient substance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The soaking of glutinous rice to water is done at 22-27 degrees C while changing water once every 6-10 hours. The grinding of glutinous rice is done until particle size is 80 mesh. The filter pressing is done at 15-25 MPa for more than 30 minutes. Preferred Components: The dumpling wrapper comprises 150-200 pts. wt. wet filter cake, 5-10 pts. wt. cooked glutinous rice, 5-10 pts. wt. coarse cereal mixture and 5-20 pts. wt. drinking water.

PN US2018171434-A1; US11118245-B2
TI Recovering method of neodymium and related rare earth elements from variable composition mixture, involves treating alkaline solution with carbon dioxide to form solids concentrated in rare earth elements.
AU BERGGREN M
   ZUBRIN R
   HENRI J T
   NIZAMOV B
AE PIONEER ASTRONAUTICS (PIOE-C)
GA 201848500G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves converting the mixture material to a higher surface area form, treating the mixture with an alkaline solution, isolating the enriched rare earth elements from the alkaline solution, and treating the alkaline solution with carbon dioxide to form solids concentrated in the rare earth elements. The mixture is a permanent neodymium iron boron magnet, and a permanent samarium-cobalt magnet. The alkaline solution is an ammonium hydroxide/ammonium carbonate solution. The ammonium carbonate is recycled as ammonia and carbon dioxide gas after use in the process, and is directly recycled after use in the process.
   USE - Recovering method of neodymium and related rare earth elements from variable composition mixture.
   ADVANTAGE - Avoids the costs and environmental impacts of alternate aqueous treatments using strong sulfuric, hydrochloric, nitric, or hydrofluoric acids, which all produce salts or waste byproducts that must be disposed.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the flow diagram of the recovery of neodymium and related rare earth elements from permanent rare earth magnets.

PT P
PN CN111467940-A; WO2020192204-A1; EP3943473-A1; US2022032230-A1; CN111467940-B
TI Removing carbon dioxide in reaction gas, involves heating up the reaction gas to be removed from carbon dioxide, decarburizing and cooling the gas to obtain a purified gas from which carbon dioxide is removed.
AU WANG X
   ZHANG Y
   XU L
   LI H
   ZHAO Y
   WU X
   YU P
AE SINOPEC NANJING CHEM RES INST CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC NANJING RES INST CHEM IND CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC NANJING RES INST CHEM IND CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC NANJING RES INST CHEM IND CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 202074597H
AB    NOVELTY - Removing carbon dioxide involves heating up the reaction gas to be removed from carbon dioxide, decarburizing and cooling the gas to obtain a purified gas from which carbon dioxide is removed.
   USE - Method for removing carbon dioxide in reaction gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to remove carbon dioxide in reaction gas that reduced the content of carbon dioxide in the gas circulating back to the reactor, and reduces the amount of steam consumed during rich liquid regeneration by more than 30%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a system for removing carbon dioxide in a reaction gas, which comprises a heating zone for heating the reaction gas from which carbon dioxide is to be removed, where heating zone is used for receiving the gas from the heating zone and performing decarburization and cooling treatment of decarbonization zone; and
   (2) a method for using system for removing carbon dioxide in a reaction gas, which involves allowing the reactant gas to be removed of carbon dioxide to enter the heating zone to perform heating treatment, entering gas in decarburization zone for decarburization and cooling treatment to obtain a purified gas from which carbon dioxide is removed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves dividing the reaction gas from which carbon dioxide is to be removed into a gas stream I and a gas stream II. The airflow I and the regeneration gas from the top of the regeneration tower are exchanged for heat exchange and temperature rise to obtain airflow Ia. The air flow II and the washing water is heat up to get the air flow IIa. The air flow Ia and air flow IIa are combined to obtain air flow III. The ratio of the gas flow I to the gas flow II is (30-70): (70-30), preferably (40-60): (60-40). The decarburization liquid is used to decarburize the gas stream III to reduce the concentration of carbon dioxide in the gas stream III. The decarbonization liquid is the lean liquid from the bottom of the regeneration tower, preferably a carbonate solution. The gas stream III is brought into countercurrent contact with the first decarburization liquid to obtain the gas stream IIIa and the first rich liquid;. The gas stream IIIa is contacted with the second decarburization liquid countercurrently to obtain gas stream IV and the second rich liquid. The air flow IV and the washing water are heat-exchanged and cooled to obtain a purified gas for removing carbon dioxide. The washing water is desalinated water, which is first cooled to below 40 degrees C by a cooling device, and then exchanges heat with the airflow IV, and then with The second decarburization liquid exchanges heat, and finally exchanges heat with airflow II, and then returns to the cooling device to cool down and cycle. The first decarburization liquid and the second decarburization liquid are lean liquids from the bottom of the regeneration tower. The first decarburization liquid is directly in countercurrent contact with the gas stream III to obtain the first rich liquid. The second decarburization liquid first exchanges heat and cools with a rich liquid with a lower temperature, then exchanges heat with the washing water, and finally contacts with the gas stream IIIa in countercurrent to obtain a second rich liquid; the first rich liquid And the second rich liquid is returned to the regeneration tower for regeneration.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN WO2021117912-A1
TI Distillation regeneration of amine-based absorbent used for absorbing carbon dioxide, involves adding zirconium oxide catalyst promoting carbamate decomposition in absorbent solution, to absorbent, and removing carbon dioxide from absorbent.
AU PARK S Y
   NAM S C
   BAEK I H
   BHATTI U H
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 202168391D
AB    NOVELTY - Distillation regeneration of amine-based absorbent involves absorbing carbon dioxide into an amine-based carbon dioxide absorbent to obtain an absorbent absorbing carbon dioxide, adding a metal oxide catalyst which promotes decomposition of carbamate in the carbon dioxide absorbent solution to the absorbent which absorbed carbon dioxide, and regenerating absorbent by removing carbon dioxide. The metal oxide catalyst is zirconium oxide catalyst.
   USE - Distillation regeneration of amine-based absorbent used for absorbing exhaust gases e.g. carbon dioxide, sulfur oxide, and nitrogen oxide from industries e.g. thermal power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables distillation regeneration of amine-based absorbent using less absorbent regeneration heat and reduced pressure distillation regeneration tower to reduce energy for carbon dioxide capture, and economically captures carbon dioxide with high efficiency of 75-90%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The absorbent is chosen from monoethanolamine, diethanolamine, triethanolamine, tetraethylenepentamine, triethylenetetramine, N-methyl diethanolamine, 3-isobutoxypropylamine, dimethylaminoethylamine, diglycolamine, hexylamine, 2-amino-2-methylpropanol, hexamethylenediamine, propylamine, dipropylamine, butylamine, isobutylamine, 2-ethylhexylamine, 4-aminobutanol, 3-methoxypropylamine, allylamine, methyldiallylamine, pentylamine, isoamylamine, N-methylethylamine, 2-oxylamine, and 2-hydroxylethylaminopropylamine, piperazine, and triisopropanolamine, preferably monoethanolamine. Preferred Composition: The method is performed using 0.1-20 pts. wt. metal oxide catalyst. Preferred Process: The method further involves reusing the regenerated carbon dioxide absorbent, and recovering the metal oxide catalyst and reusing to regenerate the absorbent. The regeneration of the absorbent is carried out by a reduced pressure distillation method.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a warm air heating system of carbon dioxide conflux. PN KR2020104599-A; KR2170273-B1
TI Carbon dioxide absorbent includes amine having a primary amino group bonded to a tertiary carbon atom and a tertiary amino group bonded to a secondary carbon atom, and as a carbon dioxide absorbent containing an aqueous amine solution.
AU NAMSUNGCHAN
   PARKSUNGYEOL
   YOUNG P
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 202088805Y
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide absorbent includes 10-40 wt.% of an amine having a primary amino group bonded to a tertiary carbon atom and a tertiary amino group bonded to a secondary carbon atom, and 10-40 wt.% of an amine having a primary amino group bonded to a tertiary carbon atom and a secondary amino group bonded to a secondary carbon atom, and as a carbon dioxide absorbent containing an aqueous amine solution, can flexibly cope with fluctuations in the partial pressure of carbon dioxide.
   USE - Carbon dioxide absorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide absorbent flexibly cope with the change in the partial pressure of carbon dioxide, and the carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas by differentiating the carbon dioxide absorbent in the high-pressure region where the partial pressure of carbon dioxide is high and the low-pressure region where the partial pressure of carbon dioxide is relatively low, and an effect of increasing the absorption efficiency while maximizing the amount of absorption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon dioxide capture method, which involves absorbing carbon dioxide from a gas containing carbon dioxide with an aqueous amine solution in which a primary amino group is bonded to a tertiary carbon atom and a tertiary amino group is bonded to a secondary carbon atom, and receiving the gas after the first absorption step and secondly absorbing carbon dioxide with an amine aqueous solution in which a primary amino group is bonded to a tertiary carbon atom and a secondary amino group is bonded to a secondary carbon atom.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The amine having a primary amino group bonded to the tertiary carbon atom and a tertiary amino group bonded to the secondary carbon atom is 3-(dimethylamino)-propylamine. The carbon dioxide absorbent absorbs carbon dioxide in a temperature range of 40-80 degrees C. The partial pressure of carbon dioxide varies in the range of 1x 10-3-1x 10 kg/cm2. The temperature at the time of absorption of the carbon dioxide is in a temperature range of 40-80 degrees C.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN WO2022163209-A1
TI Carbon dioxide absorbent used for directly            absorbing carbon dioxide in air, comprises amine            compound having heterocyclic structure chosen from            oxygen-containing heterocyclic structure and            sulfur-containing heterocyclic structure.
AU KOUNO K
   KAWASHIMA Y
   ASAI R
   KIRINO T
AE MITSUBISHI GAS CHEM CO INC (MITN-C)
GA 202297856W
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide absorbent comprises &#8814;                65 %mass amine compound (A) having a heterocyclic                structure (X) chosen from an oxygen-containing                heterocyclic structure and a sulfur-containing                heterocyclic structure.
   USE - The carbon dioxide absorbent is used for                directly absorbing carbon dioxide in air                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide absorbent is favorable for                absorption amount of carbon dioxide from air and                has excellent desorption property of carbon                dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amine compound (A)                  is chosen from 2,5-bisaminomethylfuran,                  2,5-bis(aminomethyl)tetrahydrofuran,                  furfurylamine, tetrahydrofurfurylamine,                  4-aminomethyltetrahydropyran,                  4-(2-aminoethyl)morpholine, and                  2-thiophenemethylamine and their derivatives. The                  water content is 30 %mass or less. The amine                  compound (A) has 1-4 amino groups. The                  heterocyclic structure (X) is chosen from a                  5-membered ring or a 6-membered ring. Preferred                  Properties: The carbon dioxide absorbent has a                  maximum carbon dioxide dissociation temperature                  of 120&#176; C or less as measured by the                  following method involving heating amine compound                  (A) having carbon dioxide absorbed at a                  temperature-raising rate of 10&#176; C/minute                  from 23-250&#176; C, measuring the                  temperature at which the heat absorption amount                  due to desorption of the carbon dioxide becomes                  maximum, and setting the temperature as the                  carbon dioxide maximum dissociation temperature.                  The amine compound (A) has an acid dissociation                  constant (pKa) of 8-12. The amine compound (A)                  has a molecular weight of 90-1000. The maximum                  endothermic temperature of amine compound (A) is                  120-300&#176; C, measured by the following                  method. The method involves heating amine                  compound (A) of 23-350&#176; C at a rate of                  temperature increase of 10&#176; C/minutes,                  and measuring the temperature at which the amount                  of heat absorbed by volatilization of the amine                  compound (A) becomes maximum, where the                  temperature is defined as the maximum endothermic                  temperature of the amine compound (A). The amine                  compound (A) has an amine value of 400-1500 mg                  potassium hydroxide/g.
   EXAMPLE - 20 g of 2,5-bis(aminomethyl)furan, 5 %mass                rhodium/carbon (8 g) as a catalyst, and 120 ml                tetrahydrofuran as a solvent were introduced into a                pressure-resistant autoclave. The pressure of                hydrogen in the pressure-resistant autoclave was                increased to 6 MPaG, and the reaction was allowed                to continue while the temperature was maintained at                90&#176; C for 1 hour. The catalyst was removed                by filtering the catalyst and the reaction solution                under argon gas flow to obtain a filtrate                containing the product. The filtrate containing the                product was concentrated and purified by vacuum                distillation at a temperature of 120&#176; C                and a pressure of 1 mbar to give                2,5-bis(aminomethyl)tetrahydrofuran.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN JP2019142808-A; JP7052401-B2
TI Apparatus producing methane from carbon dioxide and hydrogen, has heat supply unit that is configured to supply heat which arose with one reactor among first reactor and second reactor to other reactor.
AU SAYAMA S
   YAMAMOTO S
AE TOYOTA CHUO KENKYUSHO KK (TOYW-C)
   TOYOTA CENT R & D LABS INC (TOYW-C)
GA 201974619K
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (1) has a first reactor (10) that accommodates catalyst (11) having methanation catalyst performance and produces a methanation reaction using source gas containing carbon dioxide and hydrogen supplied from the supply source. A second reactor (20) is arranged in a downstream side of the first reactor to produce methanation reaction using the reaction mixed gas containing methane produced in the first reactor. A heat supply unit supplies the heat which arose with one reactor among the first reactor and the second reactor to the other reactor. A control unit (90) controls the reacting weight of the methanation reaction in the second reactor by adjusting the ratio of the carbon dioxide and hydrogen and the supply flow rate with respect to the reaction mixed gas supplied to second reactor.
   USE - Apparatus producing methane from carbon dioxide and hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The conversion ratio of the whole apparatus is improved. Since the reaction is continued even if it supplies the gas of normal temperature to the second reactor, the external energy injected to the second reactor is reduced. Hence the methane production cost is reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A detection unit detects the information relevant to whether or not the active temperature of the catalyst is maintained by the heat supplied by the heat supply unit and the reaction heat of the methanation reaction. The control unit controls the reacting weight of the second reactor according to the information detected by the detection unit. The detection unit detects the temperature peak position which is a position where temperature is relatively high among several positions of the catalyst in the second reactor. The control unit controls the reacting weight of the second reactor according to the change of temperature peak position of the catalyst detected by the detection unit. The detection unit detects composition of the generated gas containing methane produced with the second reactor. The control unit controls the reacting weight of the second reactor according to the change of composition of the generated gas. A reaction mixed gas flow path (60) is formed to supply the reaction mixed gas containing methane produced by the first reactor to the second reactor. A spin-drying unit separates water from the reaction mixed gas. The reaction mixed gas flow path supplies the reaction mixed gas after spin-drying to the second reactor at normal temperature. A source gas flow path (50) is formed to supply the source gas containing carbon dioxide supplied from the supply source and hydrogen to the first reactor at normal temperature. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a methane production method involves supplying the source gas containing carbon dioxide and hydrogen to the first reactor containing catalyst having methanation catalyst performance to produce methanation reaction. The reaction mixed gas containing methane produced with the first reactor is supplied to the second reactor arranged in the downstream side of the first reactor to produce methanation reaction. The heat which arose with one reactor among the first reactor and the second reactor is supplied to other reactor. The source gas containing carbon dioxide and hydrogen is supplied to the first reactor. The reaction mixed gas containing methane produced with the first reactor is supplied to the second reactor arranged in the downstream side of the first reactor to produce methanation reaction. The reacting weight of the methanation reaction in the second reactor is controlled by adjusting the ratio of the carbon dioxide and hydrogen and the supply flow rate with respect to the reaction mixed gas supplied to the second reactor. The detection is made that whether the information relevant to active temperature of the catalyst is maintained. The reacting weight of the second reactor is controlled according to the detected information.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic structure of the methane production apparatus. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Methane production apparatus (1)
   Reactors (10,20)
   Catalyst (11)
   Source gas flow path (50)
   Reaction mixed gas flow path (60)
   Control unit (90)

PN CN109663556-A; CN109663556-B
TI Reaction device useful for disturbing enhanced dielectric barrier discharge-activated carbon dioxide, comprises e.g. a dielectric barrier discharge reactor, a high-voltage pulse power source and an in-line infrared analyzer.
AU ZHANG H
   LI X
   LI L
   YAN J
   CEN K
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2019400190
AB    NOVELTY - Reaction device comprises a dielectric barrier discharge reactor, a high-voltage pulse power source and an in-line infrared analyzer. The dielectric barrier discharge reactor includes a reactor base, a tangential inlet, an inner electrode, an outer electrode, a thermocouple, a quartz tube, and an inner electrode holder. The bottom of the quartz tube is fixed on the base of the reactor, and the top of the quartz tube has a gas outlet. The outer electrode is closely attached to the outer wall of the quartz tube and is connected to the high voltage pulse power source. The inner electrode is placed in the middle and lower position of the quartz tube, and is integrally formed by the lower round table and the upper cone. The cone and the top of the round table are excessively connected by chamfering. The inner electrode is a sleeve type hollow structure, the inner electrode is fixed on the reactor base through the inner electrode support in the sleeve.
   USE - The reaction device is useful for disturbing enhanced dielectric barrier discharge-activated carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device: highly disturbs the reaction zone, fully develops the high-efficiency vibration excitation decomposition; and optimizes the carbon dioxide activation effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Reaction device comprises a dielectric barrier discharge reactor, a high-voltage pulse power source and an in-line infrared analyzer. The dielectric barrier discharge reactor includes a reactor base, a tangential inlet, an inner electrode, an outer electrode, a thermocouple, a quartz tube, and an inner electrode holder. The bottom of the quartz tube is fixed on the base of the reactor, and the top of the quartz tube has a gas outlet. The outer electrode is closely attached to the outer wall of the quartz tube and is connected to the high voltage pulse power source. The inner electrode is placed in the middle and lower position of the quartz tube, and is integrally formed by the lower round table and the upper cone. The cone and the top of the round table are excessively connected by chamfering. The inner electrode is a sleeve type hollow structure, the inner electrode is fixed on the reactor base through the inner electrode support in the sleeve, and the high voltage pulse power source is turned on by the electrode lead piece of the inner electrode support. The bottom of the wall surface of the quartz tube is oppositely designed to face the two-way tangential inlet, and the height is located at the center of the round table below the inner electrode. The gas outlet of the quartz tube is connected with an exhaust pipe, and the outlet of the exhaust pipe serves as a gas outlet, and a sampling pipe is taken out on the exhaust pipe to collect gas into the online infrared analyzer. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for operating method of reaction device for disturbing enhanced dielectric barrier discharge activated carbon dioxide, comprising introducing carbon dioxide into the dielectric barrier discharge reactor from the tangential inlet, turning on the high voltage pulse power source, where the carbon dioxide gas introduced from the tangential inlet forms a spirally rising gas flow between the quartz tube and the inner electrode, sequentially passing the gas flow through the tapered passage, the neck and the divergent passage between the inner electrode and the quartz tube, pushing up the filament discharge spirally and strongly disturbed to form a large-area, disturbance-enhancing filament discharge plasma zone, in the discharge plasma zone, activating and decomposing carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide and oxygen.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic representation of the reaction device for disturbing enhanced dielectric barrier discharge-activated carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The quartz tube has a wall thickness of 2 mm and an inner diameter of 30 mm, and the bottom is fixed on the reactor base through a flange cover. The flange cover and the reactor base are both Teflon materials. The outer electrode has an axial length of 100-120 mm along the quartz tube, and may be metal piece, spiral wire and metal mesh. The round table of the inner electrode has a small circle diameter of 12-16 mm, a large circle diameter of 26 mm, and a height of 15 mm. The bottom of the cone has a diameter of 26 mm and a height of 60 mm. The inner electrode and the inner electrode holder are made of a conductive metal material, and are made of stainless steel, copper or tungsten. The high-voltage pulse power source has a center frequency of 10 kHz and an output voltage of 6-30 kV. The inner electrode holder and the outer electrode are respectively connected through a high-voltage electric wire for generating discharge plasma. A thermocouple is arranged 10 mm above the inner electrode for monitoring the temperature change of the reaction zone, when the temperature is too high, it can be adjusted by lowering the frequency of the high-voltage pulse power source or increasing the intake air flow rate. The flow rate of carbon dioxide introduced into the tangential inlet is adjustable from 2-15 l/minutes. The online infrared analyzer detects the concentration of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide in the product in real time, and then feedbacks and guides the operation parameter setting. When the carbon dioxide concentration is too high, the voltage and frequency of the high-voltage pulse power source are optimized by reducing the intake air flow rate.

   YANG Z
   ZHANG C
AE UNIV HEBEI TECHNOLOGY (UYHT-C)
GA 201892896P
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide normal temperature decomposition method involves installing experimental equipment. Simulated flue gas is prepared followed by adjusting the input voltage of the plasma power source to start discharging the DBD reactor without the filling material, adjusting the power frequency of the plasma power source to be controlled within the numerical range required for the experiment, simulating the flue gas passing through the DBD reactor, the reacted gas flows into the sampling cylinder, and the flue gas analyzer performs component analysis and measures the concentration value of each gas in the sampling cylinder. The flue gas analyzer performs component analysis and measures the concentration value of each gas in the sampling cylinder, when the concentration value no longer changes, the recorded data is exported. The reacted gas is absorbed by the exhaust gas absorption cylinder followed by calculating the removal efficiency of carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for normal temperature decomposition of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables effectively improving the removal efficiency of carbon dioxide with strong reducibility in a low temperature plasma environment, in wide, cheap and easy manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon dioxide normal temperature decomposition method involves installing experimental equipment, which comprises gas cylinder, a rotameter, a gas mixing cylinder, a plasma power source, an oscilloscope, a DBD reactor, a sampling cylinder, a flue gas analyzer, an exhaust gas absorption cylinder, and a stirrer, where rotameter, the gas mixing cylinder, the DBD reactor, the sampling cylinder and the exhaust gas absorption cylinder are sequentially connected by a hard silicone tube, the plasma power source is respectively connected with an oscilloscope and a DBD reactor, and the inlet end of the gas mixing cylinder is opened, hole is used for the entry of air, the flue gas analyzer analyzes the concentration value of each gas in the sampling cylinder, the agitator is located in the exhaust gas absorption cylinder after the connection is completed, the airtightness of the entire experimental equipment is checked, and the rotameter and plasma are turned on. Simulated flue gas is prepared by opening the valve of all cylinders, adjusting the pressure reducing valve on the cylinder to stabilize the indication of the rotameter at the required value, and finely adjusting the gas flow through the rotor flow meter switch, gas flowing into the gas mixing cylinder to prepare a simulated flue gas in the gas mixing cylinder followed by adjusting the input voltage of the plasma power source to start discharging the DBD reactor without the filling material, adjusting the power frequency of the plasma power source to be controlled within the numerical range required for the experiment, simulating the flue gas passing through the DBD reactor, the reacted gas flows into the sampling cylinder, and the flue gas analyzer performs component analysis and measures the concentration value of each gas in the sampling cylinder, when the concentration value no longer changes, the recorded data is the inlet concentration, the reacted gas is absorbed by the exhaust gas. After the cylinder is absorbed, it is discharged into the air duct, loading the filling material in the DBD reactor, adjusting the input voltage of the plasma power source, and starting the discharge of the DBD reactor filled with the filling material, adjusting the power frequency of the plasma power source to control the numerical range required for the experiment after the simulated flue gas passes through the DBD reactor, the reacted gas flows into the sampling cylinder. The flue gas analyzer performs component analysis and measures the concentration value of each gas in the sampling cylinder, when the concentration value no longer changes, the recorded data is exported. The reacted gas is absorbed by the exhaust gas absorption cylinder and discharged into the air passage, followed by calculating the removal efficiency of carbon dioxide.

AE ANHUI JINHE IND CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)

AB    NOVELTY - Separating ammonia-carbon comprises using                devices as a first-stage carbon dioxide absorption                tower is successively connected with a second-stage                carbon dioxide absorption tower, a first-stage                carbon dioxide separation tower, and a second-stage                carbon dioxide separation tower, where involves                sending mixed gas containing ammonia and carbon                dioxide to the bottom of a first-stage carbon                dioxide absorption tower at 73000-78000 L/min, from                the middle part of the first-stage carbon dioxide                absorption tower, the 50- 100L/min ammonia water                with the concentration of 3-5 wt.% is added as the                absorption liquid, circularly absorbing the mixed                gas and the diluted ammonia water in the absorbing                tower by means of counter-current for 20-60                minutes; finishing the circulation, the absorption                liquid in the first-stage carbon dioxide absorption                tower is sent to the middle part of the                second-stage carbon dioxide absorption tower at                50-100 L/min.
   USE - Method for separating ammonia-carbon for                melamine production, where the ammonia is useful in                food processing, food additive.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce steam by the                urea washing tower of the melamine device which is                used for heating the separating tower, reduces the                energy consumption by about 15% and flexibly                selects the actual application, has strong                operability, reduces the steam consumption of the                ammonia-carbon separation system and the ammonia                can be completely separated from the carbon                dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separating ammonia-carbon comprises using                devices as a first-stage carbon dioxide absorption                tower is successively connected with a second-stage                carbon dioxide absorption tower, a first-stage                carbon dioxide separation tower, and a second-stage                carbon dioxide separation tower, where involves                sending mixed gas containing ammonia and carbon                dioxide to the bottom of a first-stage carbon                dioxide absorption tower at 73000-78000 L/min, from                the middle part of the first-stage carbon dioxide                absorption tower, the 50- 100L/min ammonia water                with the concentration of 3-5 wt.% is added as the                absorption liquid, circularly absorbing the mixed                gas and the diluted ammonia water in the absorbing                tower by means of counter-current for 20-60                minutes; finishing the circulation, the absorption                liquid in the first-stage carbon dioxide absorption                tower is sent to the middle part of the                second-stage carbon dioxide absorption tower at                50-100 L/min, the un-absorption gas from the top of                the first-stage carbon dioxide absorption tower is                sent to the bottom of the second-stage carbon                dioxide absorption tower by 50000 L/min, circularly                absorbing for 20-60 min in the absorption tower in                the manner of reverse flow; sending the gas-liquid                mixture in the secondary carbon dioxide absorption                tower into the first-stage carbon dioxide                separation tower to separate from 40000-50000                L/min, after finishing the circulation, controlling                the pressure of the first-stage carbon dioxide                separation tower to be 0.2-0.5 Mpa, the temperature                is 80-100degrees Celsius using the circulating pump                to circulate itself and taking it out to the                secondary carbon dioxide separating tower at 50-                100 L/min, controlling the pressure of the                secondary carbon dioxide separating tower is                0.6-1.2Mpa the temperature is 115-135degrees                Celsius obtaining carbon dioxide with ammonia                content less than 50 g from the top of the tower,                obtaining ammonia/carbon mass ratio of 8-12:1                carbide ammonia water from the bottom of the tower                and then sending the carbonized ammonia water to                decarbonize to obtain liquid ammonia.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The second-stage carbon dioxide absorption                  tower, and the first-stage carbon dioxide                  separation tower all have a circulation system.                  The mixed gas of ammonia and carbon dioxide                  contains 60-64% ammonia (NH3), 35-39 % carbon                  dioxide (CO2). The absorption liquid in the                  first-stage carbon dioxide absorption tower                  contains carbonized ammonia water with an                  ammonia/carbon mass ratio of 3-5:1. The                  unabsorbed gas coming out from the top of the                  first-stage carbon dioxide absorption tower                  contains 33-37% (NH3), 61-65% (CO2). The                  ammonia/carbon mass ratio in the gas-liquid                  mixture in the secondary carbon dioxide                  absorption tower is 5-7:1 carbonized ammonia                  water.

AB    NOVELTY - Ensuring power and carbon dioxide supply comprises using on-site resources methanol and water source as raw materials to obtain mixture of hydrogen and carbon dioxide through methanol-water reforming process, extracting pure hydrogen from mixture by membrane separator, outputting power by hydrogen or air fuel cell to meet power demand of field construction, using carbon dioxide purification process based on pressure swing adsorption technology to extract high-purity carbon dioxide gas, liquefying high-purity carbon dioxide gas into liquid carbon dioxide by pressurization to meet carbon dioxide demand for field construction, extracting pure hydrogen from mixture by membrane separator at 400-900 degrees C, liquefying pressure into liquid carbon dioxide under 3-6.5 MPa at 10-20 degrees C to obtain liquid carbon dioxide, sending to storage tank, supplying power required by by methanol reforming hydrogen production fuel cell and using surplus power output by other construction machines and tools on site.

TI Carbonizing coal using carbon dioxide as gas heat carrier comprises e.g. heating carbon dioxide, dry-distilling, subjecting to purification and separation, heating separated carbon dioxide and returning to the coal dry distillation furnace.
AU ZHANG X
   HAN B
   ZHANG Q
   ZONG L
AE SHANGHAI XINYOU ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANGHAI XINYOU ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201959039P
AB    NOVELTY - Carbonizing coal using carbon dioxide as gas heat carrier comprises heating carbon dioxide by the fuel gas to form high temperature carbon dioxide into the coal retort, dry-distilling the dry coal in the coal pyrolysis furnace generating the dry distillation gas after the dry distillation leaves the coal dry distillation furnace together with the heat carrier carbon dioxide, subjecting to purification and separation, heating the separated carbon dioxide by the fuel gas to form high-temperature carbon dioxide and returning to the coal dry distillation furnace.
   USE - The method is useful for carbonizing coal using carbon dioxide as gas heat carrier.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is stable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for coal carbonization system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The temperature of high temperature carbon dioxide is 600-900 degrees C, preferably 700-850 degrees C. The temperature range of the high-temperature carbon dioxide is a tube furnace heating method, a regener furnace heating method or a direct mixing heating method in which the high-temperature carbon dioxide is formed by heating the carbon dioxide by the fuel gas. The temperature range of the high temperature carbon dioxide is the method of heating the carbon dioxide by the fuel gas to form the high temperature carbon dioxide is preferably a direct mixing heating method. The dry-distilled granular coal is quenched by a combination of a wet quenching method, a gas heat carrier dry quenching method, and/or a water mist dry quenching method. The pelletized coal after dry distillation is quenched by a dry mist quenching method. The purification of the dry distillation gas and the heat carrier carbon dioxide produced after the dry distillation means that the dry distillation gas leaving the coal dry distillation furnace and the heat carrier carbon dioxide are first subjected to gas-liquid separation treatment, and subjecting decarburization and sulfur production to obtain tail gas with carbon dioxide as the main body. The gas-liquid separation treatment of the dry distillation gas produced after the dry distillation and the heat carrier carbon dioxide is to separate particulate matter, water and coal tar from the dry distillation gas leaving the coal retort and the heat carrier carbon dioxide. The separation refers to separating hydrogen, methane, carbon monoxide, diatomic carbon, propylene, and nitrogen from a gas mainly composed of a dry distillation gas, where the separated hydrogen and methane are for external delivery. The method comprises heating f the heat carrier carbon dioxide to obtain high temperature carbon dioxide by the fuel gas, where the oxygen content is controlled to 0-8 wt.%. The separation is carried out by compression, PSA pressure swing adsorption, and freeze separation.

TI Production of aminoacetaldehyde dimethyl acetal comprises e.g. directly mixing chloroacetaldehyde dimethyl acetal and aqueous ammonia solution containing carbon dioxide in proportion, reacting, cooling and performing vacuum distillation.
AU BAI S
   YANG F
   MA H
AE INNER MONGOLIA SHENGSHI CHEM CO LTD (INNE-Non-standard)
GA 2019637716
AB    NOVELTY - Production of aminoacetaldehyde dimethyl acetal comprises directly mixing chloroacetaldehyde dimethyl acetal and aqueous ammonia solution containing carbon dioxide in proportion, reacting, removing ammonia, cooling the system, adding liquid alkali in to the system, adjusting the pH to alkaline, distilling off the product and water, adding polar alcohol to the distilled solution, and performing vacuum distillation to obtain high-purity aminoacetaldehyde dimethyl acetal.
   USE - The method is useful for production of aminoacetaldehyde dimethyl acetal.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The molar ratio of chloroacetaldehyde dimethyl acetal, ammonia, water, carbon dioxide is controlling at 1:25-35:70-90:3-5. The reaction pressure is controlling to 1.0-1.5 MPa and the reaction temperature is 120-150 degrees C. In the process: boiling reaction solution after deamination, refluxing in the kettle for 4-8 hours to obtain hydrochloride of aminoacetaldehyde dimethyl acetal while recovering carbon dioxide, reducing temperature of the system to less than 30 degrees C, and adding liquid alkali to the system. The pH is adjusting to 10-12. The product and water are completely distilling off by continuing to raising the temperature to 130-150 degrees C. The molar ratio of chloroacetaldehyde dimethyl acetal, ammonia, water, carbon dioxide is controlling at 1:30-35:75-85:3.5-4.5. Preferred Components: The strong polar alcohol is glycerin, ethylene glycol or polyethylene glycol.

   HE W
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)

AB    NOVELTY - Ion exchange resin (I) are new.
   USE - (I) are useful in catalyzing the addition reaction of carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Ion exchange resin of formula (I) are new.
   ring p = macroporous nano composite resin matrix;
   M- = anion bicarbonate ion, hydroxide ion, bisulfite ion, carboxylate ion or citrate ion;
   A = cage silsesquioxane unit having the formula (-SiO(1.5))m (POSS);
   m = 6, 8, 10 or 12; and
   R = alkylene group (methylene, ethylene or propylene) or arylene group (phenylene, naphthylene or phenylene) (linking group between the POSS unit and the imidazole cation unit).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The content of the POSS unit in the ion exchange resin catalyst is 5-15 wt.%. The gel-type nanocomposite resin matrix is styrene monomer, co-monomer and nano material obtained by in-situ polymerization of nano gel-type copolymer. The styrenic monomer is styrene, alpha -methylstyrene or 4-butylstyrene; the co-monomer is ethylene glycol dimethacrylate, dipropylene benzene, divinyl phenyl methane and/or divinyl benzene and nanomaterial is single layer graphene, multilayer graphene, graphene oxide or graphene. The styrenic monomer is styrene; and the co-monomer is divinylbenzene; and the porogen is polystyrene. The amount of the styrene monomer is 85-95 pts. wt., the amount of the co-monomer is 2-5 pts. wt., the amount of the nano material is 0.1-3 pts. wt., and the amount of the porogen is 10-100 pts. wt.. The alkylene oxide is of formula (II).R1-R4 = H or 1-6C alkyl group.

PT P
PN CN108440293-A
TI Preparing high-purity chlorogenic acid by subjecting honeysuckle to ultrasonic extraction or supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, extracting extract by ethyl acetate extraction and alkalization precipitation and acidifying.
AU BAI G
   HOU Y
   WANG Y
AE UNIV NANKAI (UNAN-C)
GA 2018682061
AB    NOVELTY - The method for preparing high-purity chlorogenic acid crystal using honeysuckle as raw material involves subjecting honeysuckle to ultrasonic extraction or supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, extracting extract by ethyl acetate extraction and alkalization precipitation, acidifying and carrying out concentrated crystallization.
   USE - Method is useful for preparing high-purity chlorogenic acid crystal , which is useful as intermediate for biomedicine (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple preparation of chlorogenic acid with high purity and yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The honeysuckle plant is an aerial part including flower buds, flowers, leaves and stems of honeysuckle and safflower. Preferred Method: The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method involves using crushed powder of honeysuckle plant as a raw material, using 20-30% ethanol as an entraining agent, carrying out extraction at a pressure of 100-150 bar, a temperature of 35-55 degrees C and a flow rate of 4-12 ml/minute for 0.5-1 hour and concentrating under reduced pressure to obtain chlorogenic acid extract. The ultrasonic extraction method involves using crushed powder of the honeysuckle plant as a raw material, using greater than or equal to 70% ethanol solution as an extraction solvent and concentrating organic solvent under reduced pressure to obtain chlorogenic acid extract. The method involves removing impurities of chlorogenic acid extract, extracting with ethyl acetate to remove soluble impurities, adding alkali for adjusting pH value of 9-11, centrifuging and filtering to obtain a refined extract. Preferred Components: The alkali is sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide or calcium hydroxide.

PN CN108442127-A; CN108442127-B
TI Method for preparing foamed polyester fabric, involves washing and drying polyester fabric in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, foaming, evaporating low surface energy substance PTFE on surface of polyester fabric.
AU DING Z
   ZHU Y
   CAO Y
   REN Y
   DONG Z
   YANG Y
   FAN H
AE UNIV NANTONG (UYNT-C)
GA 201868162Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method for preparing foamed polyester fabric, involves (S1) washing and drying the polyester fabric in a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, foaming, (S2) evaporating the low surface energy substance PTFE on the surface of the polyester fabric.
   USE - Method for preparing foamed polyester fabric.
   ADVANTAGE - The environmentally-friendly method enables simple and economical preparation of lightweight and low surface energy foamed polyester fabric with heat insulation properties, and water and oil repellent properties.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Process: After step (1) includes bias sputtering process which involves bias sputtering foamed polyester fabric in a vacuum evaporation apparatus. The step (1) involves placing the polyester white fabric in hot soapy water at 50-80 degrees C for 10-20 minutes, rinsing the excess foam with water, then ultrasonically washing in an ultrasonic cleaner filled with absolute ethanol for 10-20 minutes, rinsing the excess ethanol with water, finally rinsing with deionized water and drying in an oven at 60-90 degrees C. The specific operation of step (1) is carried out by washing and drying the polyester fabric in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, reacting at 80-200 degrees C, Passing carbon dioxide gas, after the temperature is stable, reacting at pressure of 8-20 MPa, closing the air intake, after voltage regulation, leaving still for 4-48 hours, releasing quick pressure, taking out the polyester fabric out of the kettle within 60 seconds and finishing the foaming of polyester fabric. The foaming agent used in the foaming of the polyester fabric is supercritical carbon dioxide. Preferred Components: The polyester fabric is polyester white fabric. Preferred Properties: The polyester fabric has linear density of 33.3x 33.3 tex, and warp and weft density of 310 pieces/10 cm-230 pieces/10 cm. In step (2), the low surface energy substance PTFE vapor-deposited on the surface of the polyester fabric has a molecular weight of 200000-800000 and purity of 100%. In step (2), the vacuum degree in the vapor deposition chamber is 10-3-10-2 MPa. The evaporation temperature of PTFE is 300-380 degrees C. The thickness of the vapor-deposited PTFE film layer is 300-800 nm. In step (2), the thickness of vapor-deposited PTFE film layer is 500-600 nm.

   SUGIYAMA T
AE OSAKA GAS CO LTD (OSAG-C)
   OSAKA GAS CO LTD (OSAG-C)
GA 201848404Y
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of ionic liquid involves reacting an ionic substance (a1) containing a halogen and a cation chosen from quaternary tetraalkylphosphonium and quaternary tetraalkylammonium, and an ionic substance (a2) containing an alkali metal and an anion chosen from benzimidazole derivative, imidazole derivative, and carbazole derivative.
   USE - Manufacture of ionic liquid used for adsorbing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient manufacture of ionic liquid with improved time efficiency and high yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The halogen is fluorine, chlorine, bromine, or iodine. The alkali metal is chosen from lithium, sodium, and potassium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The reaction between the ionic substance (a1) and the ionic substance (a2) is carried out in the presence of a solvent. The solubility of the solvent in the ionic liquid is more than the solubility in the salt containing the halogen and the alkali metal. PN CN107934996-A
TI Preparing baking soda and ammonium chloride using            tail gas from melamine production comprises e.g.            collecting ammonia and carbon dioxide tail gas,            entering heavy alkali filtrate into deammonizing tower            and subjecting to salt production.
AU GAO C
   YANG F
   LI N
   XU X
   ZHANG Z
   ZHANG S
   NA Z
   GUAN M
   YIN H
   CAO Y
   SHANG J
   ZHANG H
AE ZHONGHAO DALIAN RES & DESIGN INST CHEM (CNCC-C)
   HENAN JINDADI CHEM IND CO LTD (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 2018327905
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing baking soda and ammonium chloride                using tail gas from melamine production comprises                e.g. collecting ammonia and carbon dioxide tail gas                from urea as raw material to produce melamine,                passing tail gas into triamine tail gas ammonia                absorption tower to obtain carbonized ammonia                brine, passing obtained carbonic acid ammonia brine                into upper part of heavy alkali carbonation tower,                subjecting to carbonation in countercurrent flow,                performing solid-liquid separation slurry, entering                obtained heavy alkali filtrate into deammonizing                tower and subjecting to salt production.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing baking soda                and ammonium chloride using tail gas from melamine                production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing baking soda and ammonium chloride                using tail gas from melamine production comprises                (i) collecting ammonia and carbon dioxide tail gas                from urea as raw material to produce melamine, (ii)                passing tail gas obtained in step (i) into triamine                tail gas ammonia absorption tower, absorbing by                dilute ammonia brine, refined brine or brine,                cooling and clarifying to obtain carbonized ammonia                brine, (iii) passing carbonic acid ammonia brine                obtained in step (ii) into upper part of heavy                alkali carbonation tower, then passing carbon                dioxide gas through lower part of tower, subjecting                to carbonation in countercurrent flow, removing                reaction heat, cooling water and removing from                bottom of tower to obtain heavy alkali crystal                slurry, (iv) performing solid-liquid separation of                heavy alkali crystal slurry obtained in step (iii),                (v) adding wet and heavy alkali obtained in step                (iv) into soda filtrate, and then adding water into                mashing drum to obtain heavy alkali slurry, (vi)                passing heavy alkali slurry obtained in step (v)                into wet decomposition tower, decomposing ammonium                bicarbonate, ammonium carbonate and ammonium                hydroxide in heavy alkali slurry, where sodium                bicarbonate decomposition rate reaches more than                65%, then making decomposition of carbon dioxide                and ammonia gas into gas phase, where tower outlet                is carbon dioxide, ammonia and saturated steam,                then cooling, entering net ammonia into carbon                dioxide compressor and returning to step (iii),                tower kettle connected to reboiler, heating                indirectly by low pressure steam at 120-150 degrees                C at a gauge pressure of 0.4-0.8 MPa, removing wet                decomposition solution of tower, heat exchanging                and clarifying with soda filtrate, fine filtering                and placing in baking soda carbonization tower,                (vii) entering baking soda raw material solution                obtained in step (vi) into upper part of baking                soda carbonization tower, performing carbonation                reaction, passing carbon dioxide gas through lower                part of tower, heating, removing alkali bicarbonate                reaction, cooling water and removing resulting                baking soda slurry from bottom of tower, (viii)                performing solid-liquid separation of baking soda                slurry obtained in step (vii), heating isolated                baking soda filtrate with wet decomposition                solution and returning to step (v), (ix) entering                solid-phase wet baking soda obtained in step (viii)                into baking soda dryer, heating and removing water                to obtain baking soda product, (x) entering heavy                alkali filtrate obtained in step (iv) into                deammonizing tower, heat exchanging at 65 degrees                C, decomposing ammonium bicarbonate, ammonium                carbonate and ammonium hydroxide in heavy alkali                filtrate, making decomposition of carbon dioxide                and ammonia into gas phase, where top of tower is                carbon dioxide, ammonia and saturated steam, then                kettle is connected to reboiler and indirectly                heating by low pressure steam at 110-120 degrees C                at pressure of 0.2-0.4 MPa and removing deamination                liquid from tower kettle, (xi) cooling deaminating                tail gas obtained in step (x), then cooling water,                exchanging with heavy alkali filtrate, absorbing                ammonia-removed tail gas using deammonia-exhaust                ammonia absorption tower and returning diluted                aqueous ammonia to ammonia absorption tower of step                (ii), (xii) subjecting deamitic solution containing                ammonium chloride and sodium chloride obtained in                step (x) to salt production at 45-120 degrees C,                saturating under steam pressure using                reverse-material multi-effect evaporation process,                firstly entering deaminating liquid into                salt-effect evaporator, heat exchanging, entering I                effect evaporator, then entering salt I effect                heater under low-pressure steam of 0.2-0.4 MPa (G)                generating secondary steam, entering heater of next                effect evaporator, evacuating and condensing end                effect evaporator secondary vapor, saturating                deaminating liquid sodium chloride generates sodium                chloride crystals during heating and evaporating                and performing solid-liquid separation of salt                crystal slurry to obtain salt filtrate and                by-product wet sodium chloride, (xiii) placing salt                preparation solution obtained in step (xii) in                ammonium flasher, performing multi-effect flash at                120-45 degrees C under flash, cooling, saturating                ammonium chloride to obtain ammonium chloride                crystals, then obtaining solution of ammonium                preparation solution and performing solid-liquid                separation obtaining ammonium chloride crystal                slurry to obtain ammonium filtrate and wet ammonium                chloride, (xiv) returning ammonium-prepared                solution obtained in step (xiii)-(xii) and then                entering salt-effect evaporator with deaminating                solution to obtain closed loop is formed, and (xv)                drying wet ammonium chloride obtained in step                (xiii) to obtain ammonium chloride product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), melamine                  production process using urea as raw material and                  uses high pressure method, low pressure method or                  normal pressure method. In step (ii), degree of                  carbonation of the carbonation ammonia brine is                  not greater than 75-80%. In step (x), deaminating                  solution has free ammonia removal rate of more                  than 99.5% and carbon dioxide is fully removed.                  Preferred Method: In step (vii), temperature of                  soda raw material liquid entering the tower is                  65-90 degrees C, and crystal slurry removal                  temperature is 55-65 degrees C. In step (viii),                  discharging obtained baking soda filtrate to a                  portion of desalted water or brine refining                  process, and then entering sodium chloride                  content of the raw material liquid into baking                  soda tower at less than 4.8 wt%. In steps (xii)                  and (xiii), using downstream evaporation process                  to obtain sodium chloride, where final                  evaporation efficiency of mother liquor is 78-82                  degrees C, then separating sodium chloride,                  cooling evaporation solution and saturating                  ammonium chloride at 30-35 degrees C to obtain                  ammonium chloride crystals, then performing                  solid-liquid separation to obtain ammonium                  finished solution and wet ammonium chloride, then                  returning ammonium conversion solution to the end                  effect evaporator to obtain closed loop. The                  method comprises using sodium chloride raw                  material to prepare baking soda i.e. salt water                  made from dissolved solid crude salt or                  underground brine, and then refining to sodium                  chloride content 4.8-5.35 kmol/m3, sodium sulfate                  less than or equal to 6 g/l, magnesium ion less                  than or equal to 6 mg/l, calcium ion less than or                  equal to 20 mg/l, turbidity less than or equal to                  30 parts per million of refined brine or brine by                  lime-soda method or lime-ammonium carbonate                  method. The method comprises decarbonization gas                  or carbon dioxide produced by pressure swing                  adsorption of boiler flue gas, and provided by                  ammonia plant as a supplemental source of carbon                  dioxide. The low-pressure steam at a gauge                  pressure of 0.1-1 MPa is used as a heat                  source.

PN DE102016220297-A1; WO2018059839-A1; AU2017337208-A1; EP3481974-A1; CN109790632-A; AU2017337208-B2; EP3481974-B1; US2021285111-A1
TI Electrochemical utilization of carbon dioxide involves reducing carbon dioxide to product gas, passing gas comprising carbon dioxide into scrubber, washing gas using adsorbent, and regenerating at least portion of absorbent.
AU FERNANDEZ S E M
   HANEBUTH M
   KRAUSE R
   STARK K
   FERNANDEZ SANCHIS E M
   MELTZER K
   KRAUS R
AE SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
GA 201824131U
AB    NOVELTY - Electrochemical utilization of carbon dioxide involves reducing carbon dioxide to a product gas in an electrolytic cell of an electrolyzer, passing the product gas comprising carbon dioxide into a gas scrubber, washing the product gas using an absorbent in the gas scrubber, regenerating the absorbent in an electrodialysis cell of an electrodialysis unit, at least partially returning the regenerated absorbent in the gas scrubber, and at least partially recycling the released substance during regeneration of carbon dioxide in the electrolyzer.
   USE - Electrochemical utilization of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables energy-efficient and economical electrochemical utilization of carbon dioxide. During the process, the carbon dioxide can be recycled from the electrodialysis unit into the electrolyzer without using any additional equipment. The electrodialysis unit is compact thus minimizing the raw material required for its construction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an apparatus, which consists of the electrolyzer having an electrolytic cell for reduction of carbon dioxide to the product gas, gas scrubber designed to at least partially absorb unreacted carbon dioxide from the product gas into the absorbent, and electrodialysis unit having the electrodialysis cell suitable for regenerating the carbon dioxide-enriched absorbent during absorption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Components: The integrated electrodialysis cell comprises an anode, a cathode, and alternately arranged several bipolar membranes and cation-selective membranes between the electrodes. Two chambers (c1) and (c2) formed by the membranes. The chamber (c1) faces the anode from the bipolar membrane, and the chamber (c2) is delimited towards the anode by the cation-selective membrane. The salt-containing electrolyte and/or the absorbent is passed into the chamber (c1) and then into the chamber (c2). Preferred Condition: The electrolyzer is operated with low carbon dioxide excess. The electrodialysis unit is operated at a pressure higher than the operating pressure of the electrolyzer. The electrodialysis unit and the electrolyzer share a common voltage source. The number of electrolytic cells is greater than the number of electrodialysis cells.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: A salt-containing liquid electrolyte is used in the electrolytic cell, in which carbon dioxide accumulates during the electrolysis and the carbon dioxide enriched salt-containing liquid electrolyte is fed to the electrodialysis cell where the carbon dioxide content in the salt-containing liquid electrolyte is reduced by electrodialysis. The salt-containing liquid electrolyte containing reduced carbon dioxide content is used as the absorbent in the gas scrubber. The salt in the electrolyte is chosen from a potassium salt, preferably potassium bicarbonate and/or potassium sulfate. The product gas comprises carbon dioxide and/or ethene.

TI Method for controlling fresh air system, involves            controlling closing of fresh air unit according to            indoor carbon dioxide concentration and carbon dioxide            concentration change trend after opening fresh air            unit.
AU WEI H
   QIN X
   WU J
   XIAO S
AE NINGBO AUX ELECTRIC CO LTD (AUXS-C)
   AUX AIR CONDITIONING CO LTD (AUXS-C)
   AUX AIR CONDITIONER CO LTD (AUXS-C)
GA 2020A31772
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining (S1) an indoor                carbon dioxide concentration. A fresh air unit is                controlled (S2) to introduce a fresh air into a                room according to the indoor carbon dioxide                concentration. A closing of the fresh air unit is                controlled (S3) according to the indoor carbon                dioxide concentration and a carbon dioxide                concentration change trend after opening the fresh                air unit. The carbon dioxide concentration change                trend is obtained by detecting a derivative of the                carbon dioxide concentration.
   USE - Method for controlling fresh air system used                in (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The indoor carbon dioxide concentration is                detected in real time through the setting of carbon                dioxide concentration sensor and fresh air unit.                The first derivative of carbon dioxide                concentration is accurately calculated. The method                accurately and reliably fed back the change of                indoor carbon dioxide concentration in real time.                The precise control of the fresh air unit to                introduce outdoor fresh air into the room is                achieved. The opening and closing of the fresh air                unit is controlled to avoid misjudgments due to                abnormal signals. The data accuracy and stability                are effectively ensured. The indoor air quality is                improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device                for controlling fresh air system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating                the method for controlling fresh air system.                (Drawing includes non-English language text)

   Step for controlling closing of fresh air                unit according to indoor carbon dioxide                concentration and carbon dioxide concentration                change trend (S3)

AU YUZURIHA A

   FUJIE R
   TANABE T
AE NIPPO CO LTD (NIPP-Non-standard)
GA 2018150912
AB    NOVELTY - The system (20) has an attraction unit is configured to suck air in facility. The piping (P1-P7) are configured to connect carbon dioxide concentration sensor (40), attraction unit, carbon dioxide adsorption portion (50) and first and second flow-path switch units. The piping is connected with facility formed by carbon dioxide concentration sensor and second piping switch unit and multiple piping. The carbon dioxide adsorption portion is configured to block piping for a carbon dioxide concentration calibration and facility is formed by piping.
   USE - Carbon dioxide concentration sensor calibration system for plant cultivation facilities such as mushroom growing facilities.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorption agent is slowly degraded and able to lengthen a replacement interval in order to circulate little air in the closed flow path. The blocked flow path for a carbon dioxide concentration calibration can be switched. The degradation of carbon dioxide adsorption agent lengthens exchange interval and effort of maintenance can be reduced in order to circulate little air. The change of the facility environment by ventilation can be pressed down, since a calibration is performed in a time slot with few ventilation operations.

PN WO2021136744-A1; AU2020417967-A1; CA3165247-A1; NO202200766-A; GB2605518-A; DE112020006424-T5; BR112022012338-A2
TI Solid adsorbent used for capturing carbon dioxide            from gas stream, comprises carbon dioxide, and solid            adsorbent comprising amine covalently bonded to polymer            resin.
AU LEENDERS S H A
   ZEGEREN E B
   OUDWATER R
AE SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
   SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
GA 202177099W
AB    NOVELTY - Solid adsorbent comprises carbon dioxide, and                solid adsorbent comprising an amine covalently                bonded to a polymer resin, where the solid                adsorbent has a carbon dioxide uptake capacity of                greater than about 7 wt.% at 40 degrees C, and the                solid adsorbent has a carbon dioxide uptake                capacity of less than 1.5 wt.% at a temperature of                100 degrees C, as measured when the gas stream                comprises 4 vol.% carbon dioxide.
   USE - The solid adsorbent is useful for capturing                carbon dioxide from gas stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The solid adsorbent has improved temperature                differential between when the solid adsorbent                adsorbs and desorbs carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a process for capturing carbon dioxide                from a gas stream comprising carbon dioxide,                involving (a) contacting the gas stream with a                solid adsorbent in an adsorption zone to form a                CCk-enriched solid adsorbent, where the solid                adsorbent comprises an amine covalently bonded to a                polymer resin, and (b) heating a portion of the                CCk-enriched solid adsorbent in a desorption zone                to a temperature greater than 90 degrees C to                desorb at least a portion of the carbon dioxide                from the carbon dioxide-enriched solid adsorbent to                form a desorbed carbon dioxide and a CCk-depleted                solid adsorbent, where the solid adsorbent has a                carbon dioxide uptake capacity of greater than                about 7 wt.% at a temperature of 40 degrees C, and                the solid adsorbent has a carbon dioxide uptake                capacity of less than about 1.5 wt.% at a                temperature of 100 degrees C, as measured when the                gas stream further comprises a concentration of the                carbon dioxide of 4 vol.%;
   (2) a system for capturing carbon dioxide from                a gas stream comprising carbon dioxide, comprising                an adsorption zone connected to a desorption zone                through a transfer line and a recycle line, where                the adsorption zone comprises a gas stream inlet                for receiving the gas stream, a flue gas outlet for                releasing a gas, and at least one adsorbent bed                comprising a solid adsorbent comprising an amine                covalently bonded to a polystyrene resin, and the                adsorption zone is configured to receive the gas                stream such that the gas stream contacts the solid                adsorbent to form a CC-enriched solid adsorbent,                and the desorption zone is configured to receive at                least a portion of the carbon dioxide-enriched                solid adsorbent from the adsorption zone through                the transfer line and to heat a portion of the                CC-enriched solid adsorbent at a temperature                greater than 90 degrees C to desorb at least a                portion of the carbon dioxide from the CC-enriched                solid adsorbent to form a desorbed carbon dioxide                and a CC-depleted solid adsorbent, and the recycle                line is configured to transfer the CC-depleted                solid adsorbent from the desorption zone to the                adsorption zone, and the solid adsorbent has a                carbon dioxide uptake capacity of greater than                about 7 wt.% at a temperature of 40 degrees C, and                the solid adsorbent has a carbon dioxide uptake                capacity of less than about 1.5 wt.% at a                temperature of 100 degrees C, as measured when the                gas stream further comprises a concentration of the                carbon dioxide of about 4 vol.%;
   (3) a method for preparing the solid adsorbent                for capturing carbon dioxide from a gas stream                comprising carbon dioxide, involving combining an                amine with a chloromethylated polymer resin to form                the solid adsorbent comprising a dry nitrogen                content from 5-10 mol/kg, and the solid adsorbent                has a carbon dioxide uptake capacity of greater                than about 7 wt.% at a temperature of 40 degrees C,                and the solid adsorbent has a carbon dioxide uptake                capacity of less than about 1.5 wt.% at a                temperature of about 100 degrees C, as measured                when the gas stream further comprises a                concentration of the carbon dioxide of about 4                vol.%; and
   (4) use of a polymer resin having an amine                covalently bonded, as a solid adsorbent for                capturing carbon dioxide from a gas stream                comprising carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amine comprises a                  primary amine, a secondary amine, a benzylamine,                  an ethylenediamine, a diethylenetriamine, a                  tetraethylenepentamine, a pentaethylenehexamine,                  a triethylenetetramine, a 1,3-diaminopropane, a                  1,4-diaminobutane, a 1,5-diaminopentane, a                  1,6-diaminohexane, a 1,7-diaminoheptane, a                  1,8-diaminooctane, a 1,9-diaminononane, a                  1,10-diaminodecane, a 1,3-diaminopentane and/or a                  1,2-diaminopropane. The amine is an alkyl                  diamine, where the amine has a chain length, and                  the chain length of the alkyl amine covalently                  bonded to the resin is for at least for 95% the                  same.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The polymer resin has                  a pore volume of 0.001-0.5 cm3/g, and a surface                  area of 1-60 m2/g. Preferred Components: The                  polymer resin comprises polystyrene, where the                  polymer resin has a porosity of 15-60%. The solid                  adsorbent has a dry nitrogen content of 5-10                  mol/kg. Preferred Method: The method involves                  transferring the carbon dioxide-enriched solid                  adsorbent from the adsorption zone to a                  pre-regenerator comprising heating coils, heating                  the CC-enriched solid adsorbent in the                  pre-regenerator to a temperature of greater than                  90 degrees C to desorb an initial portion of the                  carbon dioxide from the CC-enriched solid                  adsorbent to form a partially CC-depleted solid                  adsorbent, transferring the partially CC-depleted                  solid adsorbent to the desorption zone, heating a                  portion of the partially CC-depleted solid                  adsorbent in the desorption zone to a temperature                  of greater than 90 degrees C to desorb an                  additional portion of the carbon dioxide from the                  pre-regenerator treated solid adsorbent to form a                  second portion of the desorbed carbon dioxide and                  a CC-depleted solid adsorbent, recycling the                  CC-depleted solid adsorbent by transferring the                  CC-depleted solid adsorbent from the desorption                  zone to the adsorption zone, and recycling the                  further CC-depleted solid adsorbent by                  transferring the CC-depleted solid adsorbent from                  the desorption zone to the adsorption zone.                  Preferred Device: The adsorption zone comprises                  two or more solid adsorbent beds. The system                  comprises a pre-regenerator comprising heating                  coils, where the pre-regenerator is connected to                  the adsorption zone through a second transfer                  line and configured to receive at least a portion                  of the CCk-enriched solid adsorbent from the                  adsorption zone through the second transfer line,                  and the pre regenerator is configured to heat the                  portion of the CC-enriched solid adsorbent. The                  pre-regenerator is connected to the desorption                  zone through a third transfer line and is                  configured to transfer the partially carbon                  dioxide-depleted solid adsorbent to the                  desorption zone which is configured to heat the                  partially CC-depleted solid adsorbent to form a                  second portion of the desorbed CO2 and a further                  CC-depleted solid adsorbent.
