PN CN111579599-A; CN211785286-U
TI Networked distributed carbon dioxide online            continuous detection system comprises diffusion-type            carbon dioxide detection nodes set on ground or            underground shallow layer, and detection signal            transmitted to node controller via signal line.
AU HE L
   LIU J
   WANG C
AE HANGZHOU CHAOJU TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 202084664V
AB    NOVELTY - Networked distributed carbon dioxide online                continuous detection system comprises multiple                diffusion-type carbon dioxide detection nodes                placed on ground or shallow layer of underground                for detecting concentration of carbon dioxide gas                in the soil. A detection signal is transmitted to a                node controller through a signal line. A node                controller corresponding to the diffusion-type                carbon dioxide detection node and provides power                supply for the diffusion-type carbon dioxide                detection node, and transmits the detection signal                transmitted by the diffusion-type carbon dioxide                detection node to a remote monitoring terminal                through a wireless network. A monitoring terminal                performs wireless communication with the node                controller, receives the data transmitted by the                node controller, displays and stores, and processes                and analyzes the received data to discriminate                whether the carbon dioxide gas concentration                detected by the diffusion-type carbon dioxide                detecting node has abnormal change.
   USE - Used as networked distributed carbon dioxide                online continuous detection system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: continuously monitors carbon                dioxide concentration change in large area in real                time, does not affect original state of measurement                gas, improves the accuracy, and has reliable                analysis result.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                method for detecting networked carbon dioxide                online continuous detection system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the                networked distributed carbon dioxide online                continuous detection system.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for increasing the calorific value of blast furnace gas combustion, which involves performing carbon dioxide pressure swing adsorption separation on the blast furnace gas to obtain carbon dioxide degassing gas and carbon dioxide enriched gas, and performing carbon dioxide membrane separation treatment on the carbon dioxide-rich gas to obtain carbon dioxide product gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The adsorbent is molecular sieve, silica gel, activated carbon and modified adsorbent. AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical extracting Valeriana jatamansi volatile oil and non-volatile components comprises e.g. extracting supercritical carbon dioxide and separation includes (a) removing medicinal material V. jatamansi, drying and crushing to obtain material a for use, (b) placing material in the extraction vessel of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, and to obtain material b, and transferring material b to first-stage separation tank that maintaining supercritical state of carbon dioxide, (c1) separating material b by primary separation tank to obtain supercritical carbon dioxide in which dissolving volatile oil and non-volatile mixture separated from carbon dioxide, and (c2) separating carbon dioxide from resulting supercritical carbon dioxide c in which dissolving volatile oil by secondary separation tank to obtain volatile oil e, while recovering the carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for supercritical extracting Valeriana jatamansi volatile oil and non-volatile components.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has low temperature, short time, is economic, simple operation, has high yield, good separation degree, free from any cosolvent, does not influence the V. jatamansi in any component, can best guarantee medicinal effect, and is easy later further processing products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Supercritical extracting V. jatamansi volatile oil and non-volatile components comprises extracting supercritical carbon dioxide and separation includes (a) removing medicinal material V. jatamansi, drying and crushing to obtain material a for use, (b) placing material in the extraction vessel of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, and extraction temperature is 50-55 degrees C, extraction pressure is 30-35 MPa to obtain material b by extracting 100-150 minutes under the condition of carbon dioxide flow rate is 30-50 Kg/hour, and transferring material b to first-stage separation tank that maintaining supercritical state of carbon dioxide, (c1) separating material b by primary separation tank to obtain supercritical carbon dioxide in which dissolving volatile oil and non-volatile mixture separated from carbon dioxide, and (c2) separating carbon dioxide from resulting supercritical carbon dioxide c in which dissolving volatile oil by secondary separation tank to obtain volatile oil e, while recovering the carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (a), the impurity removal is to remove the dirt and stone impurities in V. jatamansi of medicinal material. In the step (a), the drying is to dry V. jatamansi naturally or to dry at constant temperature. In the step (a), the crushing is the medicine V. jatamansi high speed crusher for crushing. In the step (c1), the separation of primary separation tank is carried out in an environment of maintaining carbon dioxide supercritical state at temperature of 40-45 degrees C and pressure of 30-35 MPa. In the step (c2), the separation of secondary separation tank to change carbon dioxide from supercritical state to gaseous state, allowing to stand and separating, the gaseous carbon dioxide enters the recovery device, and volatile oil remains in secondary separation tank. In the step (c2), the separation of the secondary separation tank is controlled at pressure of 2-5 MPa, and temperature is 20-25 degrees C, carbon dioxide changes from supercritical state to gaseous state, and gaseous carbon dioxide enters the recovery device after being separated and separated, and the volatile oil is left in the secondary separation tank. The static separation time is 1-2 hours.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves judging whether a fresh air module is opened (S202). The carbon dioxide sensor is calibrated (S206) based on the carbon dioxide reference value and the carbon dioxide concentration value. The calibrated carbon dioxide sensor is operated.
   USE - Method for calibrating a carbon dioxide sensor used in an air conditioner (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables improving collecting precision of the carbon dioxide sensor to improve air conditioning effect of the air conditioner and comfort of the user with better utility value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a carbon dioxide sensor calibration device;
   (3) a computer readable storage medium storing instructions for executing a method for calibrating a carbon dioxide sensor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating a carbon dioxide sensor calibration method. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Judging whether fresh air module is opened (S202)
   Obtaining carbon dioxide reference value and carbon dioxide concentration value sent at current time of carbon dioxide sensor (S204)
   Calibrating carbon dioxide sensor based on carbon dioxide reference value and carbon dioxide concentration value (S206)

PN PH12020050201-A1
TI Carbon dioxide capture cartridge used in diesel-fired boiler systems, is provided with nanozeolite sorbent (NZS) that is developed and field-tested for carbon dioxide capture using diesel-fired boilers.
AU BAWAGAN A V
   BALAIS W A
   PREMACIO A R
   DEL RGR O Z O F
   AVILA J E
   AQUINO J P
AE DEPT SCI & TECHNOLOGY IND TECHNOLOGY DEV (SCTE-Non-standard)
GA 202280945C
AB    NOVELTY - The carbon dioxide capture cartridge is provided with nanozeolite sorbent (NZS) that is developed and field-tested for carbon dioxide capture using the diesel-fired boilers. The carbon dioxide adsorption rate of the developed NZS is determined to be at 11-14%, which is higher than the commercially available zeolite at 2%. The carbon dioxide emission reduction for post-combustion is determined to be 5%.
   USE - Carbon dioxide capture cartridge used in diesel-fired boiler systems.
   ADVANTAGE - The environment-friendly cost-effective alternative sorbent is provided for carbon dioxide capture in diesel-fired boiler systems.

PN CN110508098-A; CN110508098-B
TI Multi-energy regulation method of tree-type heterojunction to capture carbon dioxide involves passing industrial flue gas into absorption chamber containing amine solution, absorbing carbon dioxide, and desorbing carbon dioxide.
AU YU Y
   ZHANG C
   DING W
   ZHANG J
   ZHANG Z
   WEI J
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 2019A13617
AB    NOVELTY - Multi-energy regulation method of tree-type heterojunction to capture carbon dioxide involves passing industrial flue gas into an absorption chamber containing an amine solution, absorbing the carbon dioxide, releasing nitrogen, passing the solution after absorbing the flue gas into a hyperboloid tree reactor, and desorbing carbon dioxide under the control of light energy, electrical energy, thermal energy and chemical energy.
   USE - Multi-energy regulation method of tree-type heterojunction to capture carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - Carbon dioxide can be captured economically and efficiently at low temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amine solution is a polyaniline solution. The hyperboloid tree-type reactor includes a shell, and hyperboloid internal components arranged in the shell. The internal components include a substrate made of iron oxyhydroxide, and a solid phase absorbent, which is attached to iron oxyhydroxide. Preferred Composition: The solid-phase absorbent includes polyaniline, pyridine, and guanidine hydrochloride in mass ratio of (2.5-3.5):3:4.
